37KeŇčte mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē baŇč be gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ, baa b…Ē…Ēse gyoo Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄėDe gbeŇčgyoo, worom…Ē te de naa neŇč nyi tanam na ko neŇč te de faa neŇč ween…õ…õ, yaa loŇčboaneŇč na ko neŇč te de faa neŇč loŇč n ny…Ē…Ēw…Ē. 38Worom…Ē te de naa neŇč nyi n y…õ…õ Ňč…Ē…Ēle te de l…õ…õ neŇč gyu da akpaŇčalaŇčse man, yaa n d…ĒŇč…õ…õ diteŇčteli te de faa neŇč ab…Ē…Ē n doow…Ē? 39Worom…Ē te de naa neŇč nyi n na wee, yaa ba te kyaŇč neŇč ket…Ē deni te de kaa doo neŇč n…Ē…Ē?‚Äô 40Bot…Ē…Ē te maŇč gyoo Ňčon maa tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄėAmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyiaa, kpene kamas…õ keŇč …õ be y…õ…õ fa me balaŇč k…õw…Ē man kemaasabii koraŇč na, maŇč yaa …õ y…õ…õw…Ē fa!‚Äô 41Bot…Ē…Ē te maa yako baŇč be kyaa ma demena d…Ē…Ē n…õ nyiaa, ‚Äė∆ź seŇč lee me gyaŇč, …õm…õ…õ baŇč Wurubuar…õ be falaa Ňčon nyi ya gyu ka gyoo boalaŇč degyapaa keŇč …Ē be desina see fa …Ēb…Ēnsam na …Ē kpilala baŇč n…õ man. 42Nawolo nyi tanam koo maŇč na, …õ te fa maŇč ween…õ…õ ya, loŇčboaneŇč koo maŇč na …õ te fa maŇč loŇč ya. 43DebaŇč keŇč me y…õ…õ Ňč…Ē…Ēle e na …õ ta mo maŇč gyu ya akpaŇčalaŇčse man ya. Me t…õ…õw…Ē diteŇčteli na, …õ te fa maŇč kolo maa do ya. Ma weew…Ē m…Ēna …õ te kaa do maŇč n…Ē…Ē ya, te be kyaŇča maŇč ket…Ē deni, m…Ēna …õ te kaa do maŇč n…Ē…Ē ya.‚Äô
44Bot…Ē…Ē te balaŇč baŇč baa b…Ē…Ēse maŇč gyoo Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄėDe gbeŇčgyoo worom…Ē te de naa neŇč nyi tanam na ko neŇč, yaa loŇčboaneŇč na ko neŇč, yaa n y…õ…õ Ňč…Ē…Ēle e yaa n d…ĒŇč diteŇčteli yaa n weew…Ē yaa be kyaŇča neŇč ket…Ē deni te da te ky…Ē neŇč ya?‚Äô 45KeŇčte maa tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ampaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, ‚ÄėKpene keŇč …õ be b…õ…õ …õ te y…õ…õ fa me balaŇč k…õw…Ē man kemaasabii koraŇč ya n…õ, na maŇč yaa …õ b…õ…õw…Ē …õ te y…õ…õ fa ya.‚Äô 46Bot…Ē…Ē te baa mo balaŇč benaŇč gyu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč baa naa diyem kekpaakekpaa n…õ, m…Ēna balaŇč baŇč be gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ d…Ē…Ē, baa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
The Plot To Kill Jesus
(Mark 14.1,2; Luke 22.1,2; John 11.45-53)
1When Jesus had finished teaching, he told his disciples, 2 ‚ÄúYou know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.‚ÄĚ
3At that time the chief priests and the nation's leaders were meeting at the home of Caiaphas the high priest. 4They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death. 5But they said, ‚ÄúWe must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.‚ÄĚ
At Bethany
(Mark 14.3-9; John 12.1-8)
6Jesus was in the town of Bethany, eating at the home of Simon, who had leprosy. 7 A woman came in with a bottle of expensive perfume and poured it on Jesus' head. 8But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, ‚ÄúWhy such a waste? 9We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.‚ÄĚ
10Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said:
Why are you bothering this woman? She has done a beautiful thing for me. 11 You will always have the poor with you, but you won't always have me. 12She has poured perfume on my body to prepare it for burial. 13You may be sure that wherever the good news is told all over the world, people will remember what she has done. And they will tell others.
Judas and the Chief Priests
(Mark 14.10,11; Luke 22.3-6)
14Judas Iscariot was one of the twelve disciples. He went to the chief priests 15 and asked, ‚ÄúHow much will you give me if I help you arrest Jesus?‚ÄĚ They paid Judas 30 silver coins, 16and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus.
Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples
(Mark 14.12-21; Luke 22.7-13; John 13.21-30)
17On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, ‚ÄúWhere do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?‚ÄĚ
18Jesus told them to go to a certain man in the city and tell him, ‚ÄúOur teacher says, ‚ÄėMy time has come! I want to eat the Passover meal with my disciples in your home.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 19They did as Jesus told them and prepared the meal.
20-21When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, ‚ÄúOne of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.‚ÄĚ
22The disciples were very sad, and each one said to Jesus, ‚ÄúLord, you can't mean me!‚ÄĚ
23 He answered, ‚ÄúOne of you men who has eaten with me from this dish will betray me. 24The Son of Man will die, as the Scriptures say. But it's going to be terrible for the one who betrays me! That man would be better off if he had never been born.‚ÄĚ
25Judas said, ‚ÄúTeacher, you surely don't mean me!‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThat's what you say!‚ÄĚ Jesus replied. But later, Judas did betray him.
The Lord's Supper
(Mark 14.22-26; Luke 22.14-23; 1 Corinthians 11.23-25)
26During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, ‚ÄúTake this and eat it. This is my body.‚ÄĚ
27Jesus picked up a cup of wine and gave thanks to God. He then gave it to his disciples and said, ‚ÄúTake this and drink it. 28 This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven. 29From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.‚ÄĚ 30Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives.
Peter's Promise
(Mark 14.27-31; Luke 22.31-34; John 13.36-38)
31 Jesus said to his disciples, ‚ÄúDuring this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say,
‚ÄėI will strike down
the shepherd,
and the sheep
will be scattered.‚Äô
32 But after I am raised to life, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.‚ÄĚ
33Peter spoke up, ‚ÄúEven if all the others reject you, I never will!‚ÄĚ
34Jesus replied, ‚ÄúI promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.‚ÄĚ 35But Peter said, ‚ÄúEven if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.‚ÄĚ
All the others said the same thing.
Jesus Prays
(Mark 14.32-42; Luke 22.39-46)
36Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, ‚ÄúSit here while I go over there and pray.‚ÄĚ
37Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled, 38and he said to them, ‚ÄúI am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.‚ÄĚ
39Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, ‚ÄúMy Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.‚ÄĚ
40He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, ‚ÄúCan't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour? 41Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.‚ÄĚ
42Again Jesus went to pray and said, ‚ÄúMy Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.‚ÄĚ
43Jesus came back and found them sleeping again. They simply could not keep their eyes open. 44He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
45Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, ‚ÄúAre you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners. 46Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is Arrested
(Mark 14.43-50; Luke 22.47-53; John 18.3-12)
47Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders. 48Judas had told them ahead of time, ‚ÄúArrest the man I greet with a kiss.‚ÄĚ
49Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, ‚ÄúHello, teacher.‚ÄĚ Then Judas kissed him.
50Jesus replied, ‚ÄúMy friend, do what you came for.‚ÄĚ
The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him. 51One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
52But Jesus told him, ‚ÄúPut your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting. 53Don't you know that I could ask my Father, and he would at once send me more than twelve armies of angels? 54But then, how could the words of the Scriptures come true, which say this must happen?‚ÄĚ
55 Jesus said to the mob, ‚ÄúWhy do you come with swords and clubs to arrest me like a criminal? Day after day I sat and taught in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. 56But all this happened, so that what the prophets wrote would come true.‚ÄĚ
All Jesus' disciples left him and ran away.
Jesus Is Questioned by the Council
(Mark 14.53-65; Luke 22.54,55,63-71; John 18.13,14,19-24)
57After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there. 58But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
59The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court. 60But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward 61 and said, ‚ÄúThis man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.‚ÄĚ
62The high priest stood up and asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWhy don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?‚ÄĚ 63But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, ‚ÄúWith the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?‚ÄĚ
64 ‚ÄúThat is what you say!‚ÄĚ Jesus answered. ‚ÄúBut I tell all of you,
‚ÄėSoon you will see
the Son of Man
sitting at the right side
of God All-Powerful
and coming on the clouds
of heaven.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
65 The high priest then tore his robe and said, ‚ÄúThis man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said. 66What do you think?‚ÄĚ
They answered, ‚ÄúHe is guilty and deserves to die!‚ÄĚ 67 Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him 68and said, ‚ÄúYou think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!‚ÄĚ
Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus
(Mark 14.66-72; Luke 22.56-62; John 18.15-18,25-27)
69While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, ‚ÄúYou were with Jesus from Galilee.‚ÄĚ
70But in front of everyone Peter said, ‚ÄúThat isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!‚ÄĚ
71When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, ‚ÄúThis man was with Jesus from Nazareth.‚ÄĚ
72Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, ‚ÄúI don't even know that man!‚ÄĚ
73A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, ‚ÄúWe know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.‚ÄĚ
74Peter began to curse and swear, ‚ÄúI don't know that man!‚ÄĚ
Right then a rooster crowed, 75and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, ‚ÄúBefore a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.‚ÄĚ Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.

Bot…Ē…Ē te dek…Ēk…Ēr…Ēk…Ē…Ē kyaŇča e keŇč nyi …Ē b…õ…õ gyeŇč mena keŇč waa y…õ…õ …Ē wose ya. 38KeŇčte …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúDek…Ēk…Ēr…Ēk…Ē…Ē te kyaŇč maŇč gbaa keŇč nyi ne gyae ka ko maŇč. ∆ź kyaa k…õbo na …õ ka deke.‚ÄĚ 39Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu tuuw…Ē gyu siaman kyomii te …Ē tisiw…Ē buŇč wuluŇču …Ē nyee te …Ē fanew…Ē fa Wurubuar…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúTaata, akpaa nyi gyae ke de na, yela mena kayey…õ…õ kei ke laŇč me d…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna na maŇč kegyaebii ya. Y…õ…õ kpene keŇč nyaŇč gbagba ne gyae.‚ÄĚ 40Yesu ka bese k…ĒŇč …Ē kaseela baŇč gyaŇč n…õ, …Ē kaa naaw…Ē nyi ba te doo dosoroŇč. KeŇčte …Ē b…Ē…Ēse Pita nyiaa, ‚ÄúMena d…Ē…Ē …õ te tale deke debaŇč dokoloŇč kooŇč keŇč nyi …õ be doo yee?‚ÄĚ 41KeŇčte …Ē besew…Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź deke na …õ ke fane, na …õ be nyiŇč ka gyoo kek…õ…õsek…õ…õ man ya. Nawolo nyi ma gyeŇč nyi …õ kon…Ē…Ēse man na …õ ne gyae …õ ke y…õ…õ ma kegyaebii, m…Ēna …õ wosenaane betaŇč mena doŇč kenaŇč ya.‚ÄĚ
42KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē lee …Ē kaseela baŇč gyaŇč bolate gyu ke fane nyiaa, ‚ÄúTaata, nyi be gyae ke de nyi mena kayey…õ…õ kei ka lee me d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, kenaŇč na y…õ…õ nyaŇč gbagba n kegyaebii‚ÄĚ 43Yesu ka bese k…ĒŇč n…õ, …Ē kaa naaw…Ē nyi …Ē kaseela baŇč s…õ…õ be dok…õ…õ, nawolo nyi dosoroŇč doo be siaman. 44KeŇčte …Ē besew…Ē lee be gyaŇč gyu …Ē kaa fane botoorote. Te …Ē fanew…Ē yako nombii dokoloŇč kenaŇč ke bela. 45KeŇčte …Ē besew…Ē k…ĒŇč …Ē kaseela baŇč gyaŇč te …Ē kaa b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź s…õ…õ …õ doo …õ ne f…õ…õfo? ∆ź k…õ…õ, debaŇč keŇč te lii. Be ta lese maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon do balaŇč kum…õ…õ nyiŇčmaa man. 46∆ź koro dee gyu, …õ k…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon waa lese maŇč fa n…õ te tuŇč daa taŇč.‚ÄĚ
Ba kekyaŇč Yesu
Maake 14:43-50; Luka 22:47-53; Gy…Ēn 18:3-12
47Yesu ke gyakaa …Ē ne kolosi n…õ, …Ē kaseela kufu bala baŇč maŇč Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Gyudase n…õ kaa leew…Ē bot…Ē…Ē. Ňäon na balaŇč dikpii keŇč b…Ē…Ē lee saese kegyiise na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise gyaŇč n…õ k…ĒŇčala. Na be man baale dana yoosilase na akpokpose. 48Gyudase Ňčon waa kaa lese Yesu fa n…õ taŇč wulaa yako balaŇč baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúWalaŇč Ňčon maa kaa pukaa e n…õ, Ňčon y…õna walaŇč Ňčon …õ ne gyae n…õ. ∆ź kyaŇč e.‚ÄĚ 49Mena d…Ē…Ē Gyudase t…õ…õw…Ē teeŇč gyu Yesu gyaŇč te …Ē baake e nyi ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, te …Ē pukaa e doo e n…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 50KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe gyoo, y…õ…õ kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē n be k…ĒŇč k…õbo n…õ.‚ÄĚ Bot…Ē…Ē te balaŇč baŇč kaa t…Ē…Ē Yesu do te be kyaŇča e. 51Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu kaseela baŇč maŇč Ňčolo lese …Ē yoosila, te …Ē moow…Ē kara saese pou kegyia Ňčon tobaale deŇčele lese ke l…Ē. 52KeŇčte Yesu yako …Ē kaseele Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúMo n yoosila keŇč do ke b…Ē…Ēlee man. Nawolo nyi walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne w…ĒŇč yoosila na, waa yeŇč bo yoosila n…Ē…Ēman. 53N ba gyeŇč nyi maa tale sola me kya na waa yela …Ē kpilala kakpoŇčkakpoŇčse ka lee adido k…ĒŇč debokeiman kaa yoo l…õ…õ maŇč yee? 54M…Ēna akpaa me y…õ…õ mena na, sena te Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan ne yako nyi, kaboena nyi mena nombia k…õŇča d…õ…õk…ĒŇč n…õ, gyae ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč.‚ÄĚ 55KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse balaŇč dikpii keŇč b…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč be kaa kyaŇč e n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe y…õ…õ walaŇčkum e te …õ moo yoosilase na akpokpose …õ ne k…ĒŇč …õ kaa kyaŇč maŇč? Wee kamas…õ na m…õ…õ kyaa Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa man wola Ňčon Wurubuar…õ nombia, m…Ēna …õ te kyaŇč maŇč ya. 56M…Ēna k…õŇča pou k…ĒŇčae mena na nombia Ňčan Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra be Ňčmarase see n…õ ke k…ĒŇč mena.‚ÄĚ Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē kaseela baŇč pou korow…Ē yeu tina e yela.
Be ka mo Yesu gyu kegyiise siaman
Maake 14:53-65; Luka 22:54-55, 63-71; Gy…Ēn 18:13-14, 19-24
57BalaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyaŇč Yesu n…õ moo e gyu saese pou kegyia Kaifase dekpaŇčalaŇč man, bot…Ē…Ē keŇč mmaraa wolala na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise pou be gyaŇčee n…õ. 58Pita sila Yesu w…Ēle w…Ēle men…õ…õ kelii saese kegyia Ňčon dekpaŇčalaŇč man. ∆Ü gyoow…Ē ke man te …Ē gyuuw…Ē ke kyaa tewul…õ…õ baŇč bee gyee dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč n…õ gyaŇč, na waa naa kpene gyae ke k…ĒŇč Yesu d…Ē…Ē. 59Saese kegyiise na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, ne gyae nombii keŇč baa mo gyakaa Yesu d…Ē…Ē na baa nyiŇč gb…õ…õ ko e. 60BalaŇč burum k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa k…õr…õ…õ Yesu n…Ē…Ēse, m…Ēna be te nyiŇč nombiikum keŇč baa mo gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē ya. W…Ēle w…Ēle na te baala bala baale k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa yako nyi, 61‚ÄúDe nyiiw…Ē balee kei yakow…Ē nyi ‚ÄėWaa tale tekerii Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa kei l…Ē, na waa mo weeya atooro ma ke bela.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 62Bot…Ē…Ē te saese kegyia Ňčon korow…Ē seŇč te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúNombia Ňčan b…õ…õ mo gyakaa n d…Ē…Ē n…õ, n be tale tira Ňče n…Ē…Ē yee?‚ÄĚ 63M…Ēna Yesu te gyuusu …Ē n…Ē…Ē ya. KeŇčte saese kegyia Ňčon yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúMa te k…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa kekpaakekpaa n…õ nae fa neŇč, akpaa nyaŇč y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč, Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ bu dokoloŇč kooŇč Ňčon e na, yako daa‚ÄĚ 64KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKeŇč yaa n yakow…Ē n…õ. M…Ēna m…õ…õ yako …õ popou …õ ke nyii nyiaa, wee daale n…õ …õ kena maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon kyaa Wurubuar…õ doŇč pou gbeŇčgyoo Ňčon dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē, na …õ ka bese naa maŇč na me ta lee Ňč…Ēma man adido m…õ…õ k…ĒŇč.‚ÄĚ
65Saese kegyia Ňčon ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, …Ē kyaŇča …Ē kegba tirii (KeŇč nawolo nyi nombia Ňčan ta wee e gbaa) te …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆Ü te kolosi mmusuo nombia! WoŇč nombii yaa d…õ…õ gyae d…õ…õ tekaa nyii kpu bela? N…õn…õ…õ …õm…õ…õ gbagba …õ nyii mmusuo nombia Ňčan …Ē be kolosi n…õ. 66Sena y…õna ya agyueŇč lee nombia k…õŇča man?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte be tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆Ü kaboena yeŇč.‚ÄĚ 67KeŇčte be t…Ē…Ē n…Ē…ĒloŇčse woli …Ē siagb…õ…õ te be takee e ak…Ētase. Baale m…Ē Ňčmeree e …Ē lakaase man, 68te be yako e nyiaa ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Kristo, wola daa Ňčon …Ē be gy…Ē neŇč.‚ÄĚ
Pita keb…õ…õ Yesu
Maake 14:66-72; Luka 22:56-62; Gy…Ēn 18:15-18, 25-27
69DebaŇč kenaŇč na Pita kyaa dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man. KeŇčte saese kegyia Ňčon alebu Ňčolo k…ĒŇčaw…Ē …Ē gyaŇč kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč m…Ē n kpu…õ na Yesu Ňčon …Ē ba lee Galelia t…õ…õle man n…õ.‚ÄĚ 70KeŇčte Pita l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč balaŇč baŇč pou siaman bot…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…Ē…Ē gyeŇč nombia Ňčan n ne yako n…õ man korakora ya.‚ÄĚ 71Te …Ē leew…Ē gyu dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč denan…Ē…Ē man. Bot…Ē…Ē te alebubii Ňčolo m…Ē besew…Ē naa e te …Ē yako …Ē tebia baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee kei m…Ē kpu…õ na Yesu Ňčon …Ē ba lee Nasar…õte don…Ē…Ē man n…õ.‚ÄĚ 72KeŇčte Pita besew…Ē l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč te …Ē kaŇča ntam nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…Ē…Ē gyeŇč balee kei korakora ya.‚ÄĚ 73Te kyare ya n…õ, balaŇč baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē Pita gyaŇč kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKei d…Ē…Ē ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč ke kaeŇč nyi nyaŇč m…Ē n kpu…õ na balee kei. N kekolosi koraŇč nawolo nyi n y…õ…õ be man Ňčolo e.‚ÄĚ 74Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita fias…õ…õ …Ē ne falaa …Ē wose te …Ē kaŇča ntam nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…Ē…Ē gyeŇč balee kei korakora ya.‚ÄĚ DebokenaŇč man te kegyim l…Ē…Ē woya. 75KeŇčte Pita t…Ē…Ēse nombia Ňčan Yesu be yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúP…õte kegyim ke l…Ē woya na, nyaŇč ke b…õ…õ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŇč maŇč ya‚ÄĚ n…õ. Te Pita leew…Ē deb…Ē…Ēman ke kyaŇč …Ē nyee wii.

english_text:
Jesus Is Taken to Pilate
(Mark 15.1; Luke 23.1,2; John 18.28-32)
1Early the next morning all the chief priests and the nation's leaders met and decided that Jesus should be put to death. 2They tied him up and led him away to Pilate the governor.
The Death of Judas
(Acts 1.18,19)
3 Judas had betrayed Jesus, but when he learned that Jesus had been sentenced to death, he was sorry for what he had done. He returned the 30 silver coins to the chief priests and leaders 4and said, ‚ÄúI have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúSo what? That's your problem,‚ÄĚ they replied. 5Judas threw the money into the temple and then went out and hanged himself.
6The chief priests picked up the money and said, ‚ÄúThis money was paid to have a man killed. We can't put it in the temple treasury.‚ÄĚ 7Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners. 8This is why people still call that place ‚ÄúField of Blood.‚ÄĚ 9 So the words of the prophet Jeremiah came true,
‚ÄúThey took
the thirty silver coins,
the price of a person
among the people of Israel.
10They paid it
for a potter's field,
as the Lord
had commanded me.‚ÄĚ
Pilate Questions Jesus
(Mark 15.2-5; Luke 23.3-5; John 18.33-38)
11Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, ‚ÄúAre you the king of the Jews?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThose are your words!‚ÄĚ Jesus answered. 12And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
13Pilate asked him, ‚ÄúDon't you hear what crimes they say you have done?‚ÄĚ 14But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed.
The Death Sentence
(Mark 15.6-15; Luke 23.13-26; John 18.39‚ÄĒ19.16)
15During Passover the governor always freed a prisoner chosen by the people. 16At that time a well-known terrorist named Jesus Barabbas was in jail. 17So when the crowd came together, Pilate asked them, ‚ÄúWhich prisoner do you want me to set free? Do you want Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called the Messiah?‚ÄĚ 18Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
19While Pilate was judging the case, his wife sent him a message. It said, ‚ÄúDon't have anything to do with that innocent man. I have had nightmares because of him.‚ÄĚ
20But the chief priests and the leaders convinced the crowds to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed. 21Pilate asked the crowd again, ‚ÄúWhich of these two men do you want me to set free?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúBarabbas!‚ÄĚ they shouted.
22Pilate asked them, ‚ÄúWhat am I to do with Jesus, who is called the Messiah?‚ÄĚ
They all yelled, ‚ÄúNail him to a cross!‚ÄĚ
23Pilate answered, ‚ÄúBut what crime has he done?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúNail him to a cross!‚ÄĚ they yelled even louder.
24 Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, ‚ÄúI won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!‚ÄĚ
25Everyone answered, ‚ÄúWe and our own families will take the blame for his death!‚ÄĚ
26Pilate set Barabbas free. Then he ordered his soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip and nail him to a cross.
Soldiers Make Fun of Jesus
(Mark 15.16-21; John 19.2,3)
27The governor's soldiers led Jesus into the fortress and brought together the rest of the troops. 28They stripped off Jesus' clothes and put a scarlet robe on him. 29They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, ‚ÄúHey, you king of the Jews!‚ÄĚ 30Then they spit on him. They took the stick from him and beat him on the head with it.
Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross
(Mark 15.22-32; Luke 23.27-43; John 19.17-27)
31When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross. 32On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
33They came to a place named Golgotha, which means ‚ÄúPlace of a Skull.‚ÄĚ 34 There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
35 The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes. 36Then they sat down to guard him. 37Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, ‚ÄúThis is Jesus, the King of the Jews.‚ÄĚ 38The soldiers also nailed two criminals on crosses, one to the right of Jesus and the other to his left.
39 People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and 40 shouted, ‚ÄúSo you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!‚ÄĚ
41The chief priests, the leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses also made fun of Jesus. They said, 42‚ÄúHe saved others, but he can't save himself. If he is the king of Israel, he should come down from the cross! Then we will believe him. 43 He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.‚ÄĚ 44The two criminals also said cruel things to Jesus.
The Death of Jesus
(Mark 15.33-41; Luke 23.44-49; John 19.28-30)
45At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock. 46 Then about that time Jesus shouted, ‚ÄúEli, Eli, lema sabachthani?‚ÄĚ which means, ‚ÄúMy God, my God, why have you deserted me?‚ÄĚ
47Some of the people standing there heard Jesus and said, ‚ÄúHe's calling for Elijah.‚ÄĚ 48 One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
49Others said, ‚ÄúWait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.‚ÄĚ 50Once again Jesus shouted, and then he died.
51 At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart. 52Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life. 53They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
54The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, ‚ÄúThis man really was God's Son!‚ÄĚ
55 Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance. 56Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of James and John were some of these women.
Jesus Is Buried
(Mark 15.42-47; Luke 23.50-56; John 19.38-42)
57That evening a rich disciple named Joseph from the town of Arimathea 58went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph, 59who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth. 60Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
61All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
62On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate. 63 They said, ‚ÄúSir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death. 64So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.‚ÄĚ
65Pilate said to them, ‚ÄúAll right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.‚ÄĚ 66So they sealed it tight and placed soldiers there to guard it.

Nawolo nyi k…Ēba keŇč dana ayim…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ 7KeŇčte kegyiise baŇč moo ba nyeeya baŇč na d…ĒŇča te be s…õ…õw…Ē nyi, baa mo k…Ēba kenaŇč ke l…õ…õ t…õ…õle saabuse maare Ňčolo gyaŇč, na baa kyaa wuu Ňč…Ē…Ēla bot…Ē…Ē. 8Mena d…Ē…Ē kaa lii naŇč gy…õŇč n…õ, b…õ…õ baake t…õ…õle kenaŇč bo nyi fatabo t…õ…õle. 9K…õŇča pou yela Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan …Ē be t…õ…õ …Ē dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Gyeremia d…Ē…Ē yako see n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē mena. ∆Ü yakow…Ē nyiaa,
‚ÄúBa moo k…Ēba wolom bia sao na kufu 30 Ňčan Ňče y…õ…õ koya keŇč Iseraetena be dii …Ē nyeeman n…õ,

english_text:
Jesus Is Alive
(Mark 16.1-8; Luke 24.1-12; John 20.1-10)
1The Sabbath was over, and it was almost daybreak on Sunday when Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb. 2Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it. 3The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow. 4The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
5The angel said to the women, ‚ÄúDon't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross. 6He isn't here! God has raised him to life, just as Jesus said he would. Come, see the place where his body was lying. 7Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.‚ÄĚ
8The women were frightened and yet very happy, as they hurried from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples. 9Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him. 10Then Jesus said, ‚ÄúDon't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.‚ÄĚ
Report of the Guard
11While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened. 12So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money. 13They said to the soldiers, ‚ÄúTell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep. 14If the governor hears about this, we will talk to him. You won't have anything to worry about.‚ÄĚ 15The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story.
What Jesus' Followers Must Do
(Mark 16.14-18; Luke 24.36-49; John 20.19-23; Acts 1.6-8)
16 Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him. 17They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
18Jesus came to them and said:
I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth! 19 Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit, 20and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.

Te m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, me kpu…õ na Ňčon debaŇč kamas…õ kelii t…õ…õle kei …Ēto.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
The Preaching of John the Baptist
(Matthew 3.1-12; Luke 3.1-18; John 1.19-28)
1This is the good news about Jesus Christ, the Son of God. 2 It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet,
‚ÄúI am sending my messenger
to get the way ready
for you.
3 In the desert
someone is shouting,
‚ÄėGet the road ready
for the Lord!
Make a straight path
for him.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
4So John the Baptist showed up in the desert and told everyone, ‚ÄúTurn back to God and be baptized! Then your sins will be forgiven.‚ÄĚ
5From all Judea and Jerusalem crowds of people went to John. They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the Jordan River.
6 John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
7John also told the people, ‚ÄúSomeone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals. 8I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit!‚ÄĚ
The Baptism of Jesus
(Matthew 3.13-17; Luke 3.21,22)
9About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River. 10As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove. 11 A voice from heaven said, ‚ÄúYou are my own dear Son, and I am pleased with you.‚ÄĚ
Jesus and Satan
(Matthew 4.1-11; Luke 4.1-13)
12At once God's Spirit made Jesus go into the desert. 13He stayed there for 40 days while Satan tested him. Jesus was with the wild animals, but angels took care of him.
Jesus Begins His Work
(Matthew 4.12-17; Luke 4.14,15)
14After John was arrested, Jesus went to Galilee and told the good news that comes from God. 15 He said, ‚ÄúThe time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!‚ÄĚ
Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen
(Matthew 4.18-22; Luke 5.1-11)
16As Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew. They were fishermen and were casting their nets into the lake. 17Jesus said to them, ‚ÄúFollow me! I will teach you how to bring in people instead of fish.‚ÄĚ 18Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
19Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets. 20At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him.
A Man with an Evil Spirit
(Luke 4.31-37)
21Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching. 22 Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses. 23Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled, 24‚ÄúJesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.‚ÄĚ
25Jesus told the evil spirit, ‚ÄúBe quiet and come out of the man!‚ÄĚ 26The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
27Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, ‚ÄúWhat is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.‚ÄĚ 28News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee.
Jesus Heals Many People
(Matthew 8.14-17; Luke 4.38-41)
29As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew. 30When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever. 31Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
32That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus. 33In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house. 34Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
35Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray. 36Simon and the others started looking for him. 37And when they found him, they said, ‚ÄúEveryone is looking for you!‚ÄĚ
38Jesus replied, ‚ÄúWe must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.‚ÄĚ 39 Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons.
Jesus Heals a Man
(Matthew 8.1-4; Luke 5.12-16)
40A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, ‚ÄúYou have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.‚ÄĚ
41Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, ‚ÄúI want to! Now you are well.‚ÄĚ 42At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
43After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way. 44 He said, ‚ÄúDon't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.‚ÄĚ
45The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.

english_text:
Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Walk
(Matthew 9.1-8; Luke 5.17-26)
1Jesus went back to Capernaum, and a few days later people heard that he was at home. 2Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door.
Jesus was still teaching 3when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk. 4But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
5When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the man, ‚ÄúMy friend, your sins are forgiven.‚ÄĚ
6Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were sitting there. They started wondering, 7‚ÄúWhy would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.‚ÄĚ
8At once, Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said, ‚ÄúWhy are you thinking such things? 9Is it easier for me to tell this man his sins are forgiven or to tell him to get up and pick up his mat and go on home? 10I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.‚ÄĚ So Jesus said to the man, 11‚ÄúGet up! Pick up your mat and go on home.‚ÄĚ
12The man got right up. He picked up his mat and went out while everyone watched in amazement. They praised God and said, ‚ÄúWe have never seen anything like this!‚ÄĚ
Jesus Chooses Levi
(Matthew 9.9-13; Luke 5.27-32)
13Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them. 14As he walked along, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus. Levi was sitting at the place for paying taxes, and Jesus said to him, ‚ÄúFollow me!‚ÄĚ So he got up and went with Jesus.
15Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
16Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, ‚ÄúWhy does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?‚ÄĚ
17Jesus heard them and answered, ‚ÄúHealthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do. I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.‚ÄĚ
People Ask about Going without Eating
(Matthew 9.14-17; Luke 5.33-39)
18The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWhy do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?‚ÄĚ
19Jesus answered:
The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them. 20But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
21No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
22No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins.
A Question about the Sabbath
(Matthew 12.1-8; Luke 6.1-5)
23 One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along. 24Some Pharisees asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWhy are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!‚ÄĚ
25 Jesus answered, ‚ÄúHaven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need? 26 It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.‚ÄĚ
27Jesus finished by saying, ‚ÄúPeople were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people. 28So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.‚ÄĚ
translation_text:
Yesu keky…Ē …Ētakase Ňčolo kawee
Mateo 9:1-8; Luka 5:17-26
1Weeya akalans…õ…õ Ňčaale w…Ēle n…õ, Yesu besew…Ē gyu Kapanum don…Ē…Ē man te balaŇč nyiiw…Ē nyi …Ē te k…ĒŇč d…õ…õ. 2KeŇčte balaŇč burum kaa wuuw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē, keŇč nyi nae daseŇčae be doo ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē gb…õ…õ be doo keŇč Ňčolo waa mo gyoo dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man gba ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē ba gyeŇča deni keŇč man Yesu kyaa n…õ nyee d…Ē…Ē te be wolee yenaŇč daale, te be tisinaa kaweete Ňčon na …Ē dofoŇčoŇč keŇč pou d…õ…õd…õ…õ see Yesu siaman. 5Yesu kena mena keŇč balaŇč baŇč be l…õ…õ e di n…õ, …Ē yako kaweete Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe bu, me ta mo n nombiakum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ neŇč.‚ÄĚ 6Bot…Ē…Ē te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ yakow…Ē ba nyeeya man nyiaa, 7‚ÄúWeera d…Ē…Ē te balee kei ne kolosi mena? ∆Ü te kolosi mmusuo nombia. Lee Wurubuar…õ w…Ēle na, am…Ēte kyaa la keŇč waa tale mo dukum ky…õ…õ?‚ÄĚ 8DebokenaŇč man te Yesu gyeŇča kpene keŇč b…õ…õ gyueŇč n…õ te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč d…Ē…Ē te …õ na gyueŇč k…õŇča …õ nyeeya man? 9Nombia k…õŇča man woŇčti wo…õla na keyako fa …Ētakase Ňčon? ‚ÄėMe ta mo n dukum ky…õ…õ neŇč,‚Äô yaa ‚ÄėKoro seŇč na n ka mo n dofoŇčoŇč na n ke ta?‚Äô 10M…Ēna m…õ…õ gyae …õ ka gyeŇč nyi, MaŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu dana doŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē nyi maa mo dukum ky…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yako kaweete Ňčon nyiaa, 11‚ÄúKoro seŇč. Gate n dofoŇčoŇč, na n ke ta gyu n d…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ 12DebokenaŇč man te kaweete Ňčon korow…Ē seŇč …Ē naw…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē gate …Ē dofoŇčoŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē …Ē doo n…õ, te …Ē leew…Ē laŇč …Ē d…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč pou siaman. Nombia k…õŇča doo balaŇč baŇč pou n…Ē…Ē te ba lese Wurubuar…õ yele yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúDa te naa ta mena nombia k…õŇča din…Ē…Ē see ya.‚ÄĚ
Yesu ke baake Lewi
Mateo 9:9-13; Luka 5:27-32
13KenaŇč w…Ēle te Yesu besew…Ē gyu Galelia boo n…Ē…Ēman, te balaŇč burum k…ĒŇčaw…Ē …Ē gyaŇč te …Ē wola w…Ē Wurubuar…õ nombia. 14∆Ü ke ta …Ē ne lam n…õ, …Ē naa Alfeose bu Lewi Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ lempool…õ…õre e n…õ, na …Ē kyaa …Ē lempoo del…õe man. Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúSila maŇč.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Lewi korow…Ē sila e.
15KenaŇč w…Ēle n…õ, Lewi baake Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč gyu …Ē d…õ…õ be kaa di. Lempool…õ…õra na nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra burum sila Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč gyu bot…Ē…Ē, te baŇč na w…Ē pou ke diiw…Ē bom. 16Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee Farasiitena dikpii keŇč man kena Ňčgba Yesu ne dii bom na lempool…õ…õra na nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra n…õ be b…Ē…Ēse …Ē kaseela baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera d…Ē…Ē te Yesu ne dii bom na lempool…õ…õra na nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra?‚ÄĚ 17Yesu kenyii mena n…õ te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKaweesetena te kaboena nyi baa ky…Ē w…Ē kawee. BalaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē na wee ya n…õ, d…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ē ne hia kawee keky…Ē ya. Ma te k…ĒŇč nyi me kaa baake balaŇč baŇč be gb…õ…õneŇč wulaa ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ ya. M…Ēna me k…ĒŇčae na me kaa baake balaŇč baŇč be y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õraw…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ
Ba ke b…Ē…Ēse Yesu n…Ē…Ēkebake nombia
Mateo 9:14-17; Luka 5:33-39
18Wee daale balaŇč baale naaw…Ē na Gy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre silala na Farasiitena ne bake be n…Ē…Ē. KeŇčte be b…Ē…Ēse Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera d…Ē…Ē te Gy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre silala na Farasiitena ne bake be n…Ē…Ē, te nyaŇč wui k…õw…Ē d…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ē ne bake be n…Ē…Ē ya?‚ÄĚ 19Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúSena te balaŇč baŇč be kyaa alow…Ēlet…õ…õ n…õ baa tale bake be n…Ē…Ē keŇč alow…Ēle baale kyaa be gyaŇč? Alow…Ēle baale nyiŇča …Ē kyaa be gyaŇč n…õ, b…Ē…Ē gyae baa tale bake be n…Ē…Ē ya. 20M…Ēna debaŇč daale gyae kek…ĒŇč keŇč baa mo alow…Ēle baale Ňčon lee be gyaŇč. Mena wee kenaŇč man te baa bake be n…Ē…Ē. 21Ňäolo b…õ…õ kara kanyaŇč w…Ēle mo taa kakyeŇčgbelaa keŇč ba yem n…õ b…Ē…Ē ya. Akpaa …Ē y…õ…õ mena n…õ, kanyaŇč w…Ēle keŇč gyae ke tao kakyeŇčgbelaa keŇč wolose ke b…Ē…Ē keŇč y…õ…õ ke dinaa. 22Mena m…Ē te Ňčolo b…õ…õ mo soloŇč w…Ēle do wonembu t…Ēne ogele bene man ya. Akpaa …Ē y…õ…õ mena na soloŇč keŇč gyae ke tao t…Ēne keŇč lii, na soloŇč keŇč na wonembu t…Ēne ogele keŇč pou ke w…Ēl…õ…õ. B…õ…õ mo soloŇč w…Ēle bo do wonembu t…Ēne ogele w…Ēle man.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kawola lee kef…õ…õfowee wose man
Mateo 12:1-8; Luka 6:1-5
23Kelii Gyudatena kef…õ…õfowee n…õ, Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč gyuuw…Ē ka mo w…Ē…Ē daale man. Na ween…õ…õ Ňčan seŇč…õ…õ w…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man n…õ y…õ…õ Ňčgba kefaa n…õ. KeŇčte ba kpera Ňčaale posaa b…õ…õ tao. 24KeŇčte Farasiitena b…Ē…Ēse Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera te n kaseela baŇč ne y…õ…õ kpene keŇč d…õ…õ k…Ēle n…õ da kef…õ…õfowee?‚ÄĚ 25Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź te kala ta lee Wurubuar…õ t…Ēne keŇč man kpene keŇč de naana Defidi be y…õ…õ debaŇč keŇč tanam be kyaŇč Ňčon na …Ē tebia, te ba wose be kyaŇč w…Ē n…õ yee? 26DebaŇč keŇč Abiata y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia e n…õ, de naana Defidi gyoow…Ē Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man, te …Ē dii bodobodo keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo deesi fa Wurubuar…õ keŇč Ňčolo be dana gb…õ…õ waa di ke kpu na Wurubuar…õ saese ya n…õ. KeŇčte …Ē moo Ňčaale m…Ē fa …Ē tebia baŇč be kpu…õ na e n…õ, te baŇč m…Ē diiw…Ē.‚ÄĚ 27Te Yesu torow…Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ y…õ…õ kef…õ…õfowee bo fa walaŇč. ∆Ü te y…õ…õ walaŇč fa kef…õ…õfowee ya. 28Mena d…Ē…Ē maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon y…õna kef…õ…õfowee keŇč gba GbeŇčgyoo.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
A Man with a Paralyzed Hand
(Matthew 12.9-14; Luke 6.6-11)
1The next time Jesus went into the synagogue, a man with a paralyzed hand was there. 2The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
3Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him. 4Then he asked, ‚ÄúOn the Sabbath should we do good deeds or evil deeds? Should we save someone's life or destroy it?‚ÄĚ But no one said a word.
5Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, ‚ÄúStretch out your hand.‚ÄĚ He did, and his bad hand was healed.
6The Pharisees left. And at once they started making plans with Herod's followers to kill Jesus.
Large Crowds Come to Jesus
7Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea, 8and Jerusalem. People came from Idumea, as well as other places east of the Jordan River. They also came from the region around the towns of Tyre and Sidon. All of these crowds came because they had heard what Jesus was doing. 9 He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
10After Jesus had healed many people, the other sick people begged him to let them touch him. 11And whenever any evil spirits saw Jesus, they would fall to the ground and shout, ‚ÄúYou are the Son of God!‚ÄĚ 12But Jesus warned the spirits not to tell who he was.
Jesus Chooses His Twelve Apostles
(Matthew 10.1-4; Luke 6.12-16)
13Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went. 14Then he chose twelve of them to be his apostles, so they could be with him. He also wanted to send them out to preach 15and to force out demons. 16Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter. 17There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means ‚ÄúThunderbolts.‚ÄĚ 18Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, 19and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus.
Jesus and the Ruler of Demons
(Matthew 12.22-32; Luke 11.14-23; 12.10)
20Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat. 21When Jesus' family heard what he was doing, they thought he was crazy and went to get him under control.
22 Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, ‚ÄúThis man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.‚ÄĚ
23Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said:
How can Satan force himself out? 24A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long. 25And a family that fights won't last long either. 26So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
27How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
28I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are. 29 But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
30Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him.
Jesus' Mother and Brothers
(Matthew 12.46-50; Luke 8.19-21)
31Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them. 32The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, ‚ÄúYour mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.‚ÄĚ
33Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWho is my mother and who are my brothers?‚ÄĚ 34Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, ‚ÄúHere are my mother and my brothers. 35Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.‚ÄĚ
translation_text:

Yele kei nawolo nyi b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombia bo yakaa yakaa na doŇč Ňčgba …Ēga kefae n…õ, 18na Anderiase na Filipo na Batolomeo na Mateo na Tomase na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Tadeose na Kaanan baale Sim…Ēn Ňčon …Ē ne gyae …Ē balaŇč nideli n…õ, 19na Gyudase Isekar…Ēte Ňčon …Ē ba lese Yesu fa n…õ.

Mateo 12:25-29; Luka 11:17-22

KeŇčte balaŇč burum k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa wuu …Ē d…Ē…Ē, keŇč Ňčon na …Ē kaseela bee nyiŇč gb…õ…õ baa di ween…õ…õ paŇčpaŇč gba ya. 21∆Ü loŇčtona kenyii mena n…õ be gyuuw…Ē be kaa kyaŇč e gyu d…õ…õ, nawolo nyi na balaŇč baŇč kpa …Ē nyee ne w…Ēl…õ…õ. 22Bot…Ē…Ē te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man k…ĒŇč bot…Ē…Ē n…õ yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúFeliŇčkum…õ…õ pou gyoo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi B…õlis…õbu n…õ doola Yesu man. Ňäon faa na e doŇč te …Ē na mo gberaa feliŇčkum…õ…õ lee balaŇč man n…õ.‚ÄĚ 23KeŇčte Yesu baake balaŇč baŇč k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč yako w…Ē aduya man nyiaa, ‚ÄúSena te …Ēb…Ēnsam waa tale gegi …Ē wose? 24Akpaa t…õ…õle daale man balaŇč de kpase ba wose bee yoo na d…ĒŇča n…õ, t…õ…õle kenaŇč be gyae ke seŇč ya. 25Mena m…Ē te akpaa dekpaŇčalaŇč daale man balaŇč de kpase ba wose bee yoo na d…ĒŇča n…õ, dekpaŇčalaŇč kenaŇč m…Ē be gyae ke tale seŇč ya. 26Te akpaa …Ēb…Ēnsam na …Ē tewul…õ…õ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo te ba koro seŇč tia d…ĒŇča na, …Ē be gyae waa tale seŇč ya. english_text:
A Story about a Farmer
(Matthew 13.1-9; Luke 8.4-8)
1 The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore. 2He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
3Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field. 4While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds. 5Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep. 6But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots. 7Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain. 8But a few seeds did fall on good ground where the plants grew and produced 30 or 60 or even 100 times as much as was scattered.
9Then Jesus said, ‚ÄúIf you have ears, pay attention.‚ÄĚ
Why Jesus Used Stories
(Matthew 13.10-17; Luke 8.9,10)
10When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories. 11He answered:
I have explained the secret about God's kingdom to you, but for others I can use only stories. 12 The reason is,
‚ÄúThese people will look
and look, but never see.
They will listen and listen,
but never understand.
If they did,
they would turn to God
and be forgiven.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer
(Matthew 13.18-23; Luke 8.11-15)
13Jesus then told them:
If you don't understand this story, you won't understand any others. 14What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom. 15The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them. 16The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it at once. 17But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
18The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. 19But they start worrying about the needs of this life. They are fooled by the desire to get rich and to have all kinds of other things. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything. 20The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and welcome the message. They produce 30 or 60 or even 100 times as much as was planted.
Light
(Luke 8.16-18)
21 Jesus also said:
You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand? 22 There is nothing hidden that will not be made public. There is no secret that will not be well known. 23If you have ears, pay attention!
24 Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated‚ÄĒand even worse. 25 Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose what little they have.
Another Story about Seeds
26Again Jesus said:
God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field. 27The farmer sleeps at night and is up and around during the day. Yet the seeds keep sprouting and growing, and he doesn't understand how. 28It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain. 29 Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle.
A Mustard Seed
(Matthew 13.31,32; Luke 13.18,19)
30Finally, Jesus said:
What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it? 31It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world. 32But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade.
The Reason for Teaching with Stories
(Matthew 13.34,35)
33Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand. 34He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them.
A Storm
(Matthew 8.23-27; Luke 8.22-25)
35That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, ‚ÄúLet's cross to the east side.‚ÄĚ 36So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along. 37Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
38Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, ‚ÄúTeacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?‚ÄĚ
39Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
40Jesus asked his disciples, ‚ÄúWhy were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?‚ÄĚ
41Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, ‚ÄúWho is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!‚ÄĚ

english_text:
A Man with Evil Spirits
(Matthew 8.28-34; Luke 8.26-39)
1Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa. 2When he was getting out of the boat, a man with an evil spirit quickly ran to him 3from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain. 4He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him. 5Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
6When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down. 7He shouted, ‚ÄúJesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!‚ÄĚ 8The man said this because Jesus had already told the evil spirit to come out of him.
9Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWhat is your name?‚ÄĚ
The man answered, ‚ÄúMy name is Lots, because I have ‚Äėlots‚Äô of evil spirits.‚ÄĚ 10He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
11Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding. 12So the evil spirits begged Jesus, ‚ÄúSend us into those pigs! Let us go into them.‚ÄĚ 13Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about 2,000 pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
14The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened. 15When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
16Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs. 17Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
18When Jesus was getting into the boat, the man begged to go with him. 19But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, ‚ÄúGo home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.‚ÄĚ
20The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed.
A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman
(Matthew 9.18-26; Luke 8.40-56)
21Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him. 22The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet 23and started begging him for help. He said, ‚ÄúMy little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.‚ÄĚ 24Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
25In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years. 26 She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
27The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes. 28She had said to herself, ‚ÄúIf I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.‚ÄĚ 29As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
30At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, ‚ÄúWho touched my clothes?‚ÄĚ
31His disciples said to him, ‚ÄúLook at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?‚ÄĚ 32But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
33The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
34Jesus said to the woman, ‚ÄúYou are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.‚ÄĚ
35While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, ‚ÄúYour daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?‚ÄĚ
36Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, ‚ÄúDon't worry. Just have faith!‚ÄĚ
37Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John. 38They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise. 39Then Jesus went inside and said to them, ‚ÄúWhy are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.‚ÄĚ 40But the people laughed at him.
After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was. 41-42He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, ‚ÄúTalitha, koum!‚ÄĚ which means, ‚ÄúLittle girl, get up!‚ÄĚ The girl got right up and started walking around.
Everyone was greatly surprised. 43But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, ‚ÄúGive her something to eat.‚ÄĚ

Mateo 8:28-34; Luka 8:26-39

KeŇčte balee Ňčon fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúYesu, Wurubuar…õ Adidote Bu, woŇč te n ne gyae me gyaŇč? M…õ…õ mo Wurubuar…õ m…õ…õ doo neŇč teree nyi, na naase maŇč diyem ya.‚ÄĚ 9Te Yesu b…Ē…Ēse balee Ňčon nyi, ‚ÄúN yele y…õna sena?‚ÄĚ Te …Ē yako e nyi, ‚ÄúMa yele y…õna dikpii, nawolo nyi da boe.‚ÄĚ 10KeŇčte balee Ňčon waase Yesu agbaatee agbaatee nyi, …Ē na gegi feliŇčkum…õ…õ Ňčan lee depuŇč kenaŇč man ya. 11Kek…ĒŇč mena na kekuiriise dikpii daale seŇč…õ…õ bula keŇč teŇča man bee dii. 12KeŇčte feliŇčkum…õ…õ Ňčan doo Yesu teree nyi, ‚ÄúWaaseda, fa daa gb…õ…õ na de kaa gyoo kekuiriise k…õw…Ē man.‚ÄĚ 13Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu s…õ…õw…Ē te ba leew…Ē ka gyoo kekuiriise baŇč man. Kekuiriise baŇč baa y…õ…õ Ňčgba kakpoŇčse ala n…õ, te be yeuw…Ē tisi dekeŇčkeŇč kyoloŇčkyoloŇč keŇč gyu ka gyoo loŇč man, te be popou munuw…Ē.
14Mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ k…õ…õ kekuiriise baŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ yeuw…Ē, te be gyuuw…Ē ke yako kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ fa balaŇč baŇč be kyaa don…Ē…Ē keŇč man n…õ na akuraase Ňčan man pou. KeŇčte balaŇč k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa k…õ…õ kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ. 15Mena d…Ē…Ē te kyare ya te balaŇč k…ĒŇčaw…Ē Yesu gyaŇč te be naa walaŇč Ňčon naf…Ē feliŇčkum…õ…õ doo …Ē man n…õ baŇč…õ…õ, na …Ē doona wa ab…Ē…Ē na …Ē diteŇč keŇč te taŇč. Ba kena nyi balee Ňčon diteŇč te taŇč n…õ, gyakoloŇč gyoo w…Ē nideli. 16Bot…Ē…Ē te balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē naa kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč balee Ňčon feliŇčkum…õ…õ doo …Ē man na kekuiriise baŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, yako nombia Ňčan pou be k…ĒŇč n…õ fa balaŇč akaŇč baŇč. 17KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč waase Yesu nyi waa lee be t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē. 18Yesu ka mo waa gyoo degbele man n…õ, balee Ňčon …Ē be ky…Ē e n…õ waase e nyi waa sila e. 19M…Ēna Yesu te s…õ…õ ya te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúTa gyu n d…õ…õ, na n kaa yako n loŇčtona kpene keŇč n GbeŇčgyoo be y…õ…õ fa neŇč, na mena keŇč …Ē be wii n waraŇčase n…õ.‚ÄĚ 20Mena d…Ē…Ē balee Ňčon laŇčaw…Ē, te …Ē gyuuw…Ē don…Ē…Ēse kufu Ňčan Ňče kyaa dupuŇč kenaŇč man n…õ …Ē ne yako kpene keŇč Yesu be y…õ…õ fa e n…õ. Te gyakoloŇč gyoo balaŇč baŇč pou.
Yesu kegyuusu Gyariose bu alebubii Ňčolo, na …Ē keky…Ē alo Ňčon fatabo ne gy…Ē e n…õ kawee
Mateo 9:18-26; Luka 8:40-56
21KenaŇč w…Ēle te Yesu besew…Ē gyoo degbele man toŇč boo keŇč gyu diŇčgyiŇč. KeŇčte balaŇč burum k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa wuu …Ē d…Ē…Ē boo n…Ē…Ēman bot…Ē…Ē. 22KeŇčte Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man kegyia Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Gyariose n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē. ∆Ü kena Yesu n…õ, …Ē ke buŇčaw…Ē …Ē naw…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ, 23te …Ē waase e nideli nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe bu alebubii ne gyae waa yeŇč. K…ĒŇč na n kaa mo n nyiŇčmaa gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē na waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč.‚ÄĚ 24Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu sila e laŇč. Be kee gyu na balaŇč burum silana w…Ē, na b…õ…õ soko e.
25Na alo Ňčolo kyaa keŇč fatabo ne gy…Ē e kulutooneŇč kufu ala yaa. 26∆Ü te naa diyem nideli teeneŇčtena nyiŇčmaa man, te …Ē w…Ēl…õ…õ …Ē k…Ēba keŇč pou …Ē dana n…õ nyi waa nyiŇč kawee keky…Ē. M…Ēna …Ē kawee keŇč na bese bo y…õ…õ doŇč kpu. 27-28Alo Ňčon ke nyii Yesu wose man nombia n…õ, …Ē gyoow…Ē balaŇč baŇč man kaa sila Yesu te …Ē yakow…Ē …Ē nyeeman nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa me nyiŇč mo me nyiŇčmaa tina …Ē kegba koraŇč na, me kawee gyae ke taŇč.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte …Ē Ňčereketee …Ē wose do balaŇč baŇč man ka seŇč Yesu w…Ēle, te …Ē moo …Ē nyiŇčmaa tina …Ē kegba. 29Karatetee dokoloŇč te fatabo keŇč ne gy…Ē e n…õ karaw…Ē, te …Ē naaw…Ē …Ē wose man nyi …Ē kawee te taŇč. 30DebokenaŇč man te Yesu naaw…Ē nyi doŇč daale ta lee …Ē man. KeŇčte …Ē besenaw…Ē k…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč gyaŇčee te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAm…Ēte tinaa na ma kegba?‚ÄĚ 31Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē kaseela baŇč yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč gbagba n naa mena keŇč balaŇč be wuu n d…Ē…Ē, na n na bese b…Ē…Ēse nyi ‚ÄėAm…Ēte tinaa na maŇč?‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 32M…Ēna Yesu gyakaa …Ē ne k…õo nyi waa naa walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be tina …Ē kegba n…õ. 33Alo Ňčon kena mena keŇč …Ē wose be y…õ…õ e n…õ, …Ē moo gyakoloŇč na wose keseree kaa buŇč Yesu naw…Ē…Ēt…õ…õ te …Ē yako e kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ pou. 34Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe bu, n kel…õ…õ maŇč di d…Ē…Ē te n nyiŇča kawee keky…Ē. Ta wosef…õ…õreŇč man, nawolo nyi n kaale keŇč pou te taŇč.‚ÄĚ
35Yesu ka seŇč bot…Ē…Ē …Ē ne kolosi n…õ, baale leew…Ē Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man kegyia Gyariose Ňčon d…õ…õ kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN bu alebu Ňčon ta yem. Weera te n ne naase de GbeŇčgyoo Ňčon diyem bela‚ÄĚ 36M…Ē Yesu ta mo balaŇč baŇč nombia Ňčan b…Ē…Ē kaa yako Ňčon n…õ y…õ…õ kolo ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yako …Ēsom deni man kegyia Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúNa yee ya, nyaŇč l…õ…õ maŇč di.‚ÄĚ 37Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu te yela Ňčolo ke sila e kpu na Pita na Gyemisi, na Gyemisi …Ē naabu Gy…Ēn baageŇč ya. 38Be ka benaa Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man kegyia Gyariose Ňčon d…õ…õ n…õ, Yesu nyiiw…Ē na balaŇč ne wii na b…õ…õ fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč. 39Yesu ka gyoo dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man n…õ te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse balaŇč baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera te …õ ne wii te …õ ne fae yeesa? Bu Ňčon ta yeŇča ta ya, …Ē doo dosoroŇč bo.‚ÄĚ 40Bot…Ē…Ē te balaŇč baŇč boŇčo Yesu. KeŇčte …Ē gegi balaŇč baŇč pou lee te …Ē moo bu Ňčon …Ē kya na …Ē naa, na …Ē kaseela batooro baŇč be silana e n…õ, te be gyuuw…Ē deni keŇč man woo Ňčon doo n…õ. 41KeŇčte …Ē kyaŇča bu Ňčon nyiŇčmaa man te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúTalita kumi!‚ÄĚ Kei nawolo nyi, ‚ÄúAlebubii, me kpa koro.‚ÄĚ 42Karatetee dokoloŇč te alebu Ňčon korow…Ē seŇč …Ē ne t…õ…õ. KeŇčte gyakoloŇč gyoo w…Ē nideli. Na bu Ňčon te di kulutooneŇč kufu ala. 43Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu faree w…Ē nideli nyi be na yeli walaŇč na walaŇč ke nyii nombii kei ya. Te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi baa gyae kolo fa bu Ňčon ke di.

english_text:
The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus
(Matthew 13.53-58; Luke 4.16-30)
1Jesus left and returned to his hometown with his disciples. 2The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, ‚ÄúHow can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles? 3Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?‚ÄĚ The people were upset because of what he was doing.
4 But Jesus said, ‚ÄúProphets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.‚ÄĚ 5Jesus could not work any miracles there, except to heal a few sick people by placing his hands on them. 6He was surprised that the people did not have any faith.
Instructions for the Twelve Apostles
(Matthew 10.5-15; Luke 9.1-6)
Jesus taught in all the neighboring villages. 7Then he called together his twelve apostles and sent them out two by two with power over evil spirits. 8 He told them, ‚ÄúYou may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money. 9It's all right to wear sandals, but don't take along a change of clothes. 10When you are welcomed into a home, stay there until you leave that town. 11 If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.‚ÄĚ
12The apostles left and started telling everyone to turn to God. 13 They forced out many demons and healed a lot of sick people by putting olive oil on them.
The Death of John the Baptist
(Matthew 14.1-12; Luke 9.7-9)
14 Jesus became so well-known that Herod the ruler heard about him. Some people thought he was John the Baptist, who had come back to life with the power to work miracles. 15Others thought he was Elijah or some other prophet who had lived long ago. 16But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, ‚ÄúThis must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.‚ÄĚ
17-18 Herod had earlier married Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. But John had told him, ‚ÄúIt isn't right for you to take your brother's wife!‚ÄĚ So, in order to please Herodias, Herod arrested John and put him in prison.
19Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it 20because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
21Finally, Herodias got her chance when Herod gave a great birthday celebration for himself and invited his officials, his army officers, and the leaders of Galilee. 22The daughter of Herodias came in and danced for Herod and his guests. She pleased them so much that Herod said, ‚ÄúAsk for anything, and it's yours! 23I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.‚ÄĚ
24The girl left and asked her mother, ‚ÄúWhat do you think I should ask for?‚ÄĚ
Her mother answered, ‚ÄúThe head of John the Baptist!‚ÄĚ
25The girl hurried back and told Herod, ‚ÄúHere and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!‚ÄĚ
26Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests. 27At once he ordered a guard to cut off John's head there in prison. 28The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
29When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb.
Jesus Feeds Five Thousand
(Matthew 14.13-21; Luke 9.10-17; John 6.1-14)
30After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught. 31But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, ‚ÄúLet's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.‚ÄĚ 32They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone. 33But many people saw them leave and figured out where they were going. So people from every town ran on ahead and got there first.
34 When Jesus got out of the boat, he saw the large crowd that was like sheep without a shepherd. He felt sorry for the people and started teaching them many things.
35That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, ‚ÄúThis place is like a desert, and it's already late. 36Let the crowds leave, so they can go to the farms and villages near here and buy something to eat.‚ÄĚ
37Jesus replied, ‚ÄúYou give them something to eat.‚ÄĚ
But they asked him, ‚ÄúDon't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?‚ÄĚ
38Then Jesus said, ‚ÄúHow much bread do you have? Go and see!‚ÄĚ
They found out and answered, ‚ÄúWe have five small loaves of bread and two fish.‚ÄĚ 39Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass. 40They sat down in groups of 100 and groups of 50.
41Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
42After everyone had eaten all they wanted, 43Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
44There were 5,000 men who ate the food.
Jesus Walks on the Water
(Matthew 14.22-33; John 6.15-21)
45At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away. 46Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
47Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake. 48He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
49When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming. 50All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, ‚ÄúDon't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.‚ÄĚ 51He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused. 52Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread.
Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret
(Matthew 14.34-36)
53Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret. 54As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus. 55So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was. 56In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
translation_text:
Ba ke b…õ…õ Yesu Nasar…õte don…Ē…Ē man
Mateo 13:53-58; Luka 4:16-30
1KenaŇč w…Ēle te Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč lee bot…Ē…Ē gyu Ňčon gbagba …Ē d…õ…õ. 2Gyudatena kef…õ…õfowee kelii n…õ, …Ē korow…Ē gyu b…Ē …Ēsom deni man …Ē na wola w…Ē Wurubuar…õ nombia. ∆Ü nombia Ňčan …Ē na wolo n…õ doo balaŇč baŇč pou n…Ē…Ē te be b…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAleŇč yaa balee kei nyiŇča ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča pou lee? AleŇč yaa …Ē nyiŇča mena nyansa kei lee? K…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia Ňčan …Ē ne y…õ…õ? 3Na kapenta Ňčon yaa yee? Na …Ē naa y…õna M…õ…õre yee? Na …Ē naabia y…õna Gyemisi na Gyos…õfo na Sim…Ēn na Gyuda yee? Na daa na …Ē desina kyaa la k…õbo yee?‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē ba te l…õ…õ e do ya. 4Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalaŇč ne bu Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre yenaŇč kamas…õ, m…Ēna Ňčon gbagba …Ē don…Ē…Ē man balaŇč, na …Ē loŇčtona na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena bee buu na e ya.‚ÄĚ 5Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu te tale y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia burum …Ē d…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē ya. ∆Ü moo …Ē nyiŇčmaa bo gyakaa balaŇč kyomii baale bo d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē ky…Ē…Ē w…Ē be kaweese. 6Yesu wose yeŇča e Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē balaŇč gbagba te l…õ…õ e di ya n…õ d…Ē…Ē. KeŇčte …Ē leew…Ē don…Ē…Ē keŇč man, ta kila akuraase Ňčan man …Ē na wola w…Ē Wurubuar…õ nombia.
Yesu kekpila …Ē kaseela kufu bala baŇč
Mateo 10:5-15; Luka 9:1-6
7Te Yesu baake …Ē kaseela kufu bala baŇč te …Ē kpilii w…Ē bala bala. Te …Ē fa w…Ē doŇč keŇč baa mo di feliŇčkum…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē. 8KeŇčte …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź na mo kolo tela kpu na …õ gbutuse ya. ∆ź na mo ween…õ…õ, yaa akpo yaa k…Ēba tela ya. 9∆ź do nkyokotase, m…Ēna …õ na mo kegbase tela kpu ya. 10∆ź gyu don…Ē…Ē daale man te dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man be l…õ…õ Ňčon n…õ, …õ kyaa bot…Ē…Ē kaboena …õ ka mo lee don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man. 11Te akpaa …õ gyu yenaŇč daale te ba te l…õ…õ Ňčon yaa be te nyii Ňčon ya n…õ, …õ lee bot…Ē…Ē na …õ kpiisi ya nkyokotase aŇčasa l…Ē w…Ē na ke di adans…õ…õ tia w…Ē nyi Wurubuar…õ be gyae waa tina w…Ē yela ya.‚ÄĚ 12Mena d…Ē…Ē be gyuuw…Ē kekolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan fa balaŇč, nyi baa ky…õ…õkee lee be gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč. 13Be gegi feliŇčkum…õ…õ burum lee balaŇč man, te ba moo nuŇč fee kaweesetena burum te ba wose y…õ…õ w…Ē doŇč.
Gy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre yeŇč
Mateo 14:1-12; Luka 9:9-13, 19-20
14Ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou Yesu be y…õ…õ n…õ, yela …Ē yele yaaseew…Ē gyu yenaŇč kamas…õ te gyoo Herode m…Ē nyiiw…Ē. Be ke nyii Yesu yele n…õ, na baale kpa ‚ÄúGy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre Ňčon …Ē ba yem n…õ yaa Wurubuar…õ gyuusuw…Ē lee yeŇč man, mena d…Ē…Ē te …Ē nyiŇča doŇč …Ē ne y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia na nombia dinaana k…õŇča n…õ.‚ÄĚ 15Baale m…Ē kpa, ‚ÄúŇäon y…õna Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Elia,‚ÄĚ te baale m…Ē kpa ‚Äú∆Ü y…õ…õ gyaŇčgba Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyaa kaalaŇč n…õ man Ňčolo e.‚ÄĚ 16Gyoo Herode kenyii mena n…õ …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúGy…Ēn Ňčon m…Ē…Ē kara …Ē nyee n…õ bese la foŇč n…õ!‚ÄĚ 17-18Nawolo nyi na Gy…Ēn gyakaa …Ē ne yako gyoo Herode nyi, ‚ÄúGb…õ…õ be doo nyi waa mo …Ē kegyia Filipo w…õ…õle yala Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ n…õ ya.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē gyoo Herode gbagba yela be kyaŇča Gy…Ēn te ba doo e agbaraagbaraase mo e ket…Ē deni. 19Nombii kei d…Ē…Ē Herodia moo Gy…Ēn do …Ē t…Ē…Ē man, te …Ē gyae gb…õ…õ keŇč waa mo ko e m…Ēna …Ē b…õ…õ tale ya. 20Nawolo nyi gyoo Herode gyeŇč nyi Gy…Ēn y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman, te …Ē ta mo …Ē wose m…Ē fa Wurubuar…õ. Mena d…Ē…Ē gyoo Herode na weese Gy…Ēn keŇč nyi nombiikum daale be y…õ…õ e ya. DebaŇč kamas…õ keŇč gyoo Herode de nyii Gy…Ēn n…Ē…Ēwoya na b…õ…õ seŇč e nideli ya, m…Ēna …Ē ne gyae nyi waa tei e.
21K…õŇča pou w…Ēle n…õ, gb…õ…õ kaa torow…Ē fa Herodia. Nawolo nyi Gyoo Herode kee di …Ē kalolawee n…õ, …Ē baake …Ē kegyiise na …Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyiise na Galeliatena baŇč ba wose dana obuo n…õ, nyi be kaa di wee keŇč ky…Ē e. 22Ba ke gyaŇčee keŇč n…õ, Herodia bu alebu k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa s…Ēe te y…õ…õ gyoo Herode na balaŇč baŇč pou kyeo. Bot…Ē…Ē te gyoo Herode yako alebu Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúYako maŇč kpene kamas…õ keŇč n ne gyae, na maa fa neŇč.‚ÄĚ 23KeŇčte gyoo Herode kaŇča ntam kpu fa bu Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúKpene kamas…õ keŇč n b…Ē…Ēse maŇč na maa fa neŇč. Akpaa me gyoori koraŇč te n ne gyae na, maa kpase ke man fa neŇč.‚ÄĚ 24Bot…Ē…Ē te alebu Ňčon leew…Ē yenaŇč gyu ke b…Ē…Ēse …Ē naa nyiaa, ‚ÄúNna, woŇč te maa b…Ē…Ēse?‚ÄĚ Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē naa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúYako nyi n ne gyae Gy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre Ňčon nyee.‚ÄĚ 25DebokenaŇč man te alebu Ňčon barasew…Ē gyu gyoo Herode gyaŇč ke yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…õ…õ gyae n ke kara Gy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre nyee do tasa taŇčtaŇčalaŇč man fa maŇč.‚ÄĚ 26Nombii kei takaa gyoo Herode nideli, m…Ēna ntam keŇč …Ē be kam Ňč…Ē…Ēla baŇč siaman d…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ē te tale b…õ…õ ya. 27Mena d…Ē…Ē debokenaŇč man te …Ē kpila …Ē balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ k…õr…õ…õ balaŇč nyeeya n…õ man Ňčolo nyi, waa gyu ke kara Gy…Ēn nyee k…Ēna e. KeŇčte balee Ňčon gyuu deni keŇč man Gy…Ēn doo n…õ ka lese e kara …Ē nyee. 28KeŇčte …Ē moo ke do tasa taŇčtaŇčalaŇč man k…Ēna gyoo Herode te …Ē moow…Ē fa alebu Ňčon, te Ňčon m…Ē moo ke fa …Ē naa. 29Gy…Ēn kaseela baŇč kenyii …Ē yeŇč n…õ, be gyuuw…Ē ka mo e ke wuu.
Yesu kefa balaŇč kakpoŇčse banoŇč ween…õ…õ
Mateo 14:13-21; Luka 9:10-17; Gy…Ēn 6:1-14
30Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŇč …Ē be kpilii n…õ besew…Ē k…ĒŇč kaa yako e nombia Ňčan pou b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ, na Ňčan pou b…Ē…Ē wola balaŇč n…õ. 31Na mena keŇč balaŇč na gyem tisi be d…Ē…Ē beiŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, be te nyiŇč gb…õ…õ baa di ween…õ…õ gba ya. 45DebokenaŇč man te Yesu yela …Ē kaseela baŇč gyoow…Ē degbele man kyaŇč gb…õ…õ bee gyu B…õtesaida don…Ē…Ē keŇč ke kyaa boo keŇč diŇčgyiŇč n…õ, na Ňčon ke l…Ē balaŇč baŇč gb…õ…õ. 46∆Ü ke l…Ē balaŇč baŇč gb…õ…õ taŇč n…õ, …Ē gyuuw…Ē bula d…Ē…Ē …Ē kaa fane. 47Kelii balin…Ē…Ē n…õ, na degbele keŇč te lii boo keŇč nsana nsana, te kaa Yesu waageŇč boo keŇč diŇčgyiŇč. 48Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu k…õ…õw…Ē na feliŇč ne gy…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee na …Ē kaseela baŇč, mena d…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ kaa na degbele keŇč kesaa.
