english_text:
The Teaching of the Ancestors
(Matthew 15.1-9)
1Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus. 2They noticed that some of his disciples ate without first washing their hands.
3The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating. 4None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
5The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWhy don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?‚ÄĚ
6 Jesus replied:
You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said,
‚ÄúAll of you praise me
with your words,
but you never really
think about me.
7It is useless for you
to worship me,
when you teach rules
made up by humans.‚ÄĚ
8You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught. 9You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings! 10 Didn't Moses command you to respect your father and mother? Didn't he tell you to put to death all who curse their parents? 11But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God. 12You won't let those people help their parents. 13And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad.
What Really Makes People Unclean
(Matthew 15.10-20)
14Jesus called the crowd together again and said, ‚ÄúPay attention and try to understand what I mean. 15-16The food that you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.‚ÄĚ
17After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant. 18He answered, ‚ÄúDon't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean. 19It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.‚ÄĚ By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
20Then Jesus said:
What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean. 21Out of your heart come evil thoughts, vulgar deeds, stealing, murder, 22unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness. 23All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God.
A Woman's Faith
(Matthew 15.21-28)
24Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway. 25A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet. 26The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter. 27But Jesus said, ‚ÄúThe children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.‚ÄĚ
28The woman replied, ‚ÄúLord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.‚ÄĚ
29Jesus answered, ‚ÄúThat's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.‚ÄĚ 30When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone.
Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
31Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis. 32Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
33After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue. 34Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, ‚ÄúEffatha!‚ÄĚ which means ‚ÄúOpen up!‚ÄĚ 35At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
36Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it. 37They were completely amazed and said, ‚ÄúEverything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Jesus Feeds Four Thousand
(Matthew 15.32-39)
1One day another large crowd gathered around Jesus. They had not brought along anything to eat. So Jesus called his disciples together and said, 2‚ÄúI feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. 3Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.‚ÄĚ
4The disciples said, ‚ÄúThis place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?‚ÄĚ
5Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, ‚ÄúSeven small loaves of bread.‚ÄĚ
6After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd. 7They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
8-9The crowd of about 4,000 people ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
As soon as Jesus had sent the people away, 10he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha.
A Sign from Heaven
(Matthew 16.1-4)
11 The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven. 12 Jesus groaned and said, ‚ÄúWhy are you always looking for a sign? I can promise you that you will not be given one!‚ÄĚ 13Then he left them. He again got into a boat and crossed over to the other side of the lake.
The Yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod
(Matthew 16.5-12)
14The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat. 15 Jesus warned them, ‚ÄúWatch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.‚ÄĚ
16The disciples talked this over and said to each other, ‚ÄúHe must be saying this because we don't have any bread.‚ÄĚ
17Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, ‚ÄúWhy are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed? 18 Are your eyes blind and your ears deaf? Don't you remember 19how many baskets of leftovers you picked up when I fed those 5,000 people with only five small loaves of bread?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúYes,‚ÄĚ the disciples answered. ‚ÄúThere were twelve baskets.‚ÄĚ
20Jesus then asked, ‚ÄúAnd how many baskets of leftovers did you pick up when I broke seven small loaves of bread for those 4,000 people?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúSeven,‚ÄĚ they answered.
21‚ÄúDon't you know what I am talking about by now?‚ÄĚ Jesus asked.
Jesus Heals a Blind Man at Bethsaida
22As Jesus and his disciples were going into Bethsaida, some people brought a blind man to him and begged him to touch the man. 23Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything. 24The man looked up and said, ‚ÄúI see people, but they look like trees walking around.‚ÄĚ
25Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly. 26Jesus said to him, ‚ÄúYou may return home now, but don't go into the village.‚ÄĚ
Who Is Jesus?
(Matthew 16.13-20; Luke 9.18-21)
27Jesus and his disciples went to the villages near the town of Caesarea Philippi. As they were walking along, he asked them, ‚ÄúWhat do people say about me?‚ÄĚ
28 The disciples answered, ‚ÄúSome say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah. Others say you are one of the prophets.‚ÄĚ
29 Then Jesus asked, ‚ÄúBut who do you say I am?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúYou are the Messiah!‚ÄĚ Peter replied.
30Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him.
Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death
(Matthew 16.21-28; Luke 9.22-27)
31Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, ‚ÄúThe nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.‚ÄĚ 32Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant.
Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. 33But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, ‚ÄúSatan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.‚ÄĚ
34 Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said:
If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me. 35 If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it. 36What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? 37What could you give to get back your soul?
38Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.

Yesu kekolosi lee …Ē yeŇč na …Ē kefoŇč wose man
Mateo 16:21-28; Luka 9:22-27
31KenaŇč w…Ēle te Yesu fias…õ…õ …Ē na wola …Ē kaseela baŇč nombia nyiaa, ‚ÄúKaboena nyi maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon maa naa diyem nideli, na Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč ke b…õ…õ maŇč nyi na maŇč y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon ya. Baa ko maŇč, m…Ē weeya atoorote keŇč na maa foŇč lee yeŇč man.‚ÄĚ 32Yesu ka toro nombia k…õŇča yako fa …Ē kaseela baŇč n…õ, te Pita moo e lee yenaŇč te …Ē faree e nyi …Ē na kolosi mena ya. 33Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu g…õ…õsew…Ē k…õ…õ …Ē kaseela baŇč te …Ē fae Pita nyiaa, ‚ÄúTa lee me gyaŇč, …Ēb…Ēnsam! M…Ēna waa mo …Ē …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč seele, na waa sila maŇč. 35Nawolo nyi walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae waa l…õ…õ Ňčon gbagba nyeedoŇč na, ke gyae ka lee …Ē nyiŇčmaa man. Te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē nyeedoŇč da lee …Ē nyiŇčmaa man lee maŇč Yesu na me nombia d…Ē…Ē n…õ, waa bese nyiŇč ke. 36Akpaa Ňčolo de nyiŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē pou te …Ē nyeedoŇč da lee …Ē nyiŇčmaa man na, woŇč t…Ēn…Ē…Ē te waa nyiŇč? 37Yaa woŇč te walaŇč waa tale mo ky…õ…õkee na …Ē nyeedoŇč? 38Akpaa maŇč Yesu na me nombia de y…õ…õ Ňčolo des…õŇč gy…õŇč wee kei balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē besena be w…Ēle tee Wurubuar…õ te be y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õraw…Ē n…õ siaman na, kenaŇč na wee keŇč maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon maa bese k…ĒŇč daa na me kya Wurubuar…õ na Wurubuar…õ kpilala baŇč gyoorobiiri man n…õ, walaŇč ŇčonaŇč m…Ē nombia gyae ke y…õ…õ maŇč des…õŇč.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
1I can assure you that some of the people standing here will not die before they see God's kingdom come with power.
The True Glory of Jesus
(Matthew 17.1-13; Luke 9.28-36)
2 Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John with him. They went up on a high mountain, where they could be alone. There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. 3And his clothes became much whiter than any bleach on earth could make them. 4Then Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
5Peter said to Jesus, ‚ÄúTeacher, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.‚ÄĚ 6But Peter and the others were terribly frightened, and he did not know what he was talking about.
7 The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, ‚ÄúThis is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!‚ÄĚ 8At once the disciples looked around, but they saw only Jesus.
9As Jesus and his disciples were coming down the mountain, he told them not to say a word about what they had seen, until the Son of Man had been raised from death. 10So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words ‚Äúraised from death.‚ÄĚ
11 The disciples asked Jesus, ‚ÄúDon't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?‚ÄĚ
12 Jesus answered:
Elijah certainly will come to get everything ready. But don't the Scriptures also say that the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected? 13I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would.
Jesus Heals a Boy
(Matthew 17.14-20; Luke 9.37-43a)
14When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
15The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
16Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWhat are you arguing about?‚ÄĚ
17Someone from the crowd answered, ‚ÄúTeacher, I brought my son to you. A demon keeps him from talking. 18Whenever the demon attacks my son, it throws him to the ground and makes him foam at the mouth and grit his teeth in pain. Then he becomes stiff. I asked your disciples to force out the demon, but they couldn't do it.‚ÄĚ
19Jesus said, ‚ÄúYou people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.‚ÄĚ
20They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
21Jesus asked the boy's father, ‚ÄúHow long has he been like this?‚ÄĚ
The man answered, ‚ÄúEver since he was a child. 22The demon has often tried to kill him by throwing him into a fire or into water. Please have pity and help us if you can!‚ÄĚ
23Jesus replied, ‚ÄúWhy do you say ‚Äėif you can‚Äô? Anything is possible for someone who has faith!‚ÄĚ
24At once the boy's father shouted, ‚ÄúI do have faith! Please help me to have even more.‚ÄĚ
25When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, ‚ÄúI order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.‚ÄĚ
26The spirit screamed and made the boy shake all over. Then it went out of him. The boy looked dead, and almost everyone said he was. 27But Jesus took hold of his hand and helped him stand up.
28After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, ‚ÄúWhy couldn't we force out that demon?‚ÄĚ
29Jesus answered, ‚ÄúOnly prayer can force out this kind of demon.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Again Speaks about His Death
(Matthew 17.22,23; Luke 9.43b-45)
30Jesus left with his disciples and started through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know about it, 31because he was teaching the disciples that the Son of Man would be handed over to people who would kill him. But three days later he would rise to life. 32The disciples did not understand what Jesus meant, and they were afraid to ask.
Who Is the Greatest?
(Matthew 18.1-5; Luke 9.46-48)
33Jesus and his disciples went to his home in Capernaum. After they were inside the house, Jesus asked them, ‚ÄúWhat were you arguing about along the way?‚ÄĚ 34 They had been arguing about which one of them was the greatest, and so they did not answer.
35 After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, ‚ÄúIf you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!‚ÄĚ
36Then Jesus asked a child to stand near him. He put his arm around the child and said, 37 ‚ÄúWhen you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.‚ÄĚ
For or against Jesus
(Luke 9.49,50)
38John said, ‚ÄúTeacher, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But he wasn't one of us, and we told him to stop.‚ÄĚ
39Jesus said to his disciples:
Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me. 40 Anyone who isn't against us is for us. 41 And anyone who gives you a cup of water in my name, just because you belong to me, will surely be rewarded.
Temptations To Sin
(Matthew 18.6-9; Luke 17.1,2)
42It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks. 43-44 So if your hand causes you to sin, cut it off! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed than to have two hands and be thrown into the fires of hell that never go out. 45-46If your foot causes you to sin, chop it off. You would be better off to go into life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell. 47 If your eye causes you to sin, get rid of it. You would be better off to go into God's kingdom with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell. 48 The worms there never die, and the fire never stops burning.
49Everyone must be salted with fire.
50 Salt is good. But if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made salty again? Have salt among you and live at peace with each other.

Luka 9:49-50

english_text:
Teaching about Divorce
(Matthew 19.1-12; Luke 16.18)
1After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
2Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. So they came up to him and asked if it was right for a man to divorce his wife. 3Jesus asked them, ‚ÄúWhat does the Law of Moses say about this?‚ÄĚ
4 They answered, ‚ÄúMoses allows a man to write out divorce papers and send his wife away.‚ÄĚ
5Jesus replied, ‚ÄúMoses gave you this law because you are so heartless. 6 But in the beginning God made a man and a woman. 7 That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. 8He becomes like one person with his wife. Then they are no longer two people, but one. 9And no one should separate a couple that God has joined together.‚ÄĚ
10When Jesus and his disciples were back in the house, they asked him about what he had said. 11 He told them, ‚ÄúA man who divorces his wife and marries someone else is unfaithful to his wife. 12A woman who divorces her husband and marries again is also unfaithful.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Blesses Little Children
(Matthew 19.13-15; Luke 18.15-17)
13Some people brought their children to Jesus so he could bless them by placing his hands on them. But his disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
14When Jesus saw this, he became angry and said, ‚ÄúLet the children come to me! Don't try to stop them. People who are like these little children belong to the kingdom of God. 15 I promise you that you cannot get into God's kingdom, unless you accept it the way a child does.‚ÄĚ 16Then Jesus took the children in his arms and blessed them by placing his hands on them.
A Rich Man
(Matthew 19.16-30; Luke 18.18-30)
17As Jesus was walking down a road, a man ran up to him. He knelt down, and asked, ‚ÄúGood teacher, what can I do to have eternal life?‚ÄĚ
18Jesus replied, ‚ÄúWhy do you call me good? Only God is good. 19 You know the commandments. ‚ÄėDo not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others. Do not cheat. Respect your father and mother.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
20The man answered, ‚ÄúTeacher, I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.‚ÄĚ
21Jesus looked closely at the man. He liked him and said, ‚ÄúThere's one thing you still need to do. Go sell everything you own. Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come with me.‚ÄĚ
22When the man heard Jesus say this, he went away gloomy and sad because he was very rich.
23Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, ‚ÄúIt's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!‚ÄĚ 24The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, ‚ÄúIt's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom! 25In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.‚ÄĚ
26Jesus' disciples were even more amazed. They asked each other, ‚ÄúHow can anyone ever be saved?‚ÄĚ
27Jesus looked at them and said, ‚ÄúThere are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.‚ÄĚ
28Peter replied, ‚ÄúRemember, we left everything to be your followers!‚ÄĚ
29Jesus told him:
You can be sure that anyone who gives up home or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or land for me and for the good news 30will be rewarded. In this world they will be given 100 times as many houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and pieces of land, though they will also be mistreated. And in the world to come, they will have eternal life. 31 But many who are now first will be last, and many who are now last will be first.
Jesus Again Tells about His Death
(Matthew 20.17-19; Luke 18.31-34)
32The disciples were confused as Jesus led them toward Jerusalem, and his other followers were afraid. Once again, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and told them what was going to happen to him. He said:
33We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners, 34who will make fun of him and spit on him. They will beat him and kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.
The Request of James and John
(Matthew 20.20-28)
35James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came up to Jesus and asked, ‚ÄúTeacher, will you do us a favor?‚ÄĚ
36Jesus asked them what they wanted, 37and they answered, ‚ÄúWhen you come into your glory, please let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left.‚ÄĚ
38 Jesus told them, ‚ÄúYou don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?‚ÄĚ
39‚ÄúYes, we are!‚ÄĚ James and John answered.
Then Jesus replied, ‚ÄúYou certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must! 40But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.‚ÄĚ
41When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with James and John. 42 But Jesus called the disciples together and said:
You know that those foreigners who call themselves kings like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over the people they rule. 43 But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others. 44And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave. 45 The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people.
Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus
(Matthew 20.29-34; Luke 18.35-43)
46Jesus and his disciples went to Jericho. And as they were leaving, they were followed by a large crowd. A blind beggar by the name of Bartimaeus son of Timaeus was sitting beside the road. 47When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, ‚ÄúJesus, Son of David, have pity on me!‚ÄĚ 48Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, ‚ÄúSon of David, have pity on me!‚ÄĚ
49Jesus stopped and said, ‚ÄúCall him over!‚ÄĚ
They called out to the blind man and said, ‚ÄúDon't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.‚ÄĚ 50The man threw off his coat as he jumped up and ran to Jesus.
51Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWhat do you want me to do for you?‚ÄĚ
The blind man answered, ‚ÄúMaster, I want to see!‚ÄĚ
52Jesus told him, ‚ÄúYou may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.‚ÄĚ
At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.

13Bot…Ē…Ē te balaŇč moo bia k…ĒŇč Yesu gyaŇč nyi waa mo …Ē nyiŇčmaa gyakaa be d…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna …Ē kaseela baŇč ŇčmanyaŇč balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē mo bia baŇč k…ĒŇč n…õ bo. 14Yesu kena mena n…õ, te y…õ…õ e gyoŇč ya te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź yela bia baŇč kek…ĒŇč me gyaŇč, …õ na t…Ē w…Ē gb…õ…õ ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose Ňčgba bia k…õw…Ē din…Ē…Ē n…õ wui. 15AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyiaa, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē te y…Ēk…Ēse …Ē wose do Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õ Ňčgba biikyaa e ya n…õ, …Ē be gyae waa gyoo Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč man korakora ya.‚ÄĚ 16KeŇčte Yesu kyaŇča bia baŇč nyiŇčmaa man te …Ē moo …Ē nyiŇčmaa gyakaa be d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē hiraa w…Ē.
K…Ēbate Ňčolo wose man nombia
Mateo 19:16-30; Luka 18:18-30
17Yesu ka mo gb…õ…õ …Ē ne gyu n…õ, tobaale Ňčolo yeuw…Ē kaa buŇč …Ē naw…Ē…Ēt…õ…õ te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo kpaakpaa, woŇč te kaboena nyi maa y…õ…õ na maa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa?‚ÄĚ 18Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč d…Ē…Ē te n ne baake maŇč walaŇč kpaakpaa? Lee Wurubuar…õ w…Ēle n…õ, walaŇč kamas…õ be kyaa keŇč …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč kpaakpaate e ya.‚ÄĚ 19Ňägba n gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan ne yako nyi, ‚ÄúNa ko walaŇč ya, na lee n baale yaa n w…õ…õle w…Ēle ya, na Ňčm…õ…õle ya, na kara n d…Ē…Ē n…Ē…Ē ya, na Ňčere Ňčolo do ya, bu n kya na n naa n…õ?‚ÄĚ 20KeŇčte balee Ňčon m…Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, me wulaa mee di mmaraase k…õŇča pou d…Ē…Ē leenaŇč me biat…õ…õ‚ÄĚ 21Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu k…õ…õ …Ē siaman yididi, na …Ē ne gyae …Ē nombia, mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúTa bese ka neŇč kolo dokoloŇč n ke y…õ…õ. Gyu ke y…Ē…Ē nya ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou n dana n…õ, na n ka mo k…Ēba keŇč kp…õl…õ…õ ayematena, na n ke k…ĒŇč kaa sila maŇč. N y…õ…õ mena na, n ke nyiŇč ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa see n wose Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč adido.‚ÄĚ 22Tobaale Ňčon ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, …Ē moo waraŇčase lee bot…Ē…Ē nawolo nyi na …Ē dana …Ē wose nideli.
23KeŇčte Yesu k…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč gyaŇčee te …Ē yako …Ē kaseela baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúDana doŇč fa balaŇč baŇč be dana ba wose nideli n…õ, nyi baa gyoo Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč man.‚ÄĚ 24∆Ü kaseela baŇč ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, doo be n…Ē…Ē. Te Yesu besew…Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe bia, Ňčgba …õ naa mena keŇč gyae ke y…õ…õ doŇč fa balaŇč baŇč be dana ba wose nideli n…õ, nyi baa gyoo Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč man. 25Gyae ke y…õ…õ doŇč fa balaŇč baŇč be dana ba wose nideli n…õ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč man, kela nyi kurum…Ē…Ē ka gyoo Ňčarale b…Ē…Ē man lee.‚ÄĚ 26∆Ü kaseela baŇč ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, besew…Ē doo be n…Ē…Ē nideli te be b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyi nn…õ te doo na, am…Ēte ne gyae la waa nyiŇč nyeekel…õ…õ baa?‚ÄĚ 27Te Yesu k…õ…õ w…Ē yididi yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalaŇč gyaŇč na dana doŇč, m…Ēna Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč na be dana doŇč ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč na, kpene kamas…õ gyae ke tale k…ĒŇč mena.‚ÄĚ 28Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, daa m…Ē te tina kpene kamas…õ keŇč de dana n…õ yela, te de silana neŇč.‚ÄĚ 29KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyiaa, …õ man Ňčolo be kyaa keŇč …Ē be tina …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč yaa …Ē naabia yaa …Ē desina, yaa …Ē kya yaa …Ē naa yaa …Ē bia yaa …Ē t…õ…õlese yela lee maŇč na nyeekel…õ…õ nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē, 30te …Ē be gyae waa bese nyiŇč akpaŇčalaŇčse na naabia na desina na naa na kya na bia na t…õ…õlese akpeŇč s…ĒŇčonoŇč (100) kpu gy…õŇč weeya k…õŇča man ya. M…Ē kayey…õ…õ gyae ke k…ĒŇč …Ē d…Ē…Ē, na balaŇč kek…Ēla e kpu. Te kenaŇč w…Ēle na, waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa. 31M…Ē balaŇč burum baŇč be y…õ…õ siamantenaw…Ē n…õ baa kaa bese w…Ēle nyibaŇč, na w…Ēle nyibaŇč kaa bese siamantena.‚ÄĚ
Yesu ka bese kolosi lee …Ē yeŇč wose man
Mateo 20:17-19; Luka 18:31-34
32Lee bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč moo gb…õ…õ bee gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man, na Yesu d…ĒŇč…õ…õ la be siaman. Be kee gyu n…õ, …Ē kaseela baŇč wose yeŇča w…Ē, te gyakoloŇč m…Ē kyaŇča balaŇč baŇč be silana w…Ē n…õ. KeŇčte …Ē baake …Ē kaseela kufu bala baŇč lee yenaŇč te …Ē yako w…Ē kpene keŇč gyae ke k…ĒŇč …Ē d…Ē…Ē n…õ nyiaa, 33‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ! Dee gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man baa. Baa lese maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon fa Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč. Baa di me nombia na baa bu maŇč f…Ē…Ē nyi baa ko maŇč, na baa mo maŇč do balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ nyiŇčmaa man. 34Baa kulu maŇč na baa t…Ē n…Ē…ĒloŇč woli maŇč, na baa Ňčmeree maŇč dambiise na baa ko maŇč. M…Ēna weeya atooro w…Ēle na, maa foŇč lee yeŇč man.‚ÄĚ
Sebedeo bia
Mateo 20:20-28
35KenaŇč w…Ēle n…õ, Sebedeo bia Gyemisi na Gy…Ēn k…ĒŇčaw…Ē Yesu gyaŇč kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, d…õ…õ gyae nyi kpene kamas…õ keŇč de b…Ē…Ēse neŇč na, n ke y…õ…õ ke fa daa.‚ÄĚ 36Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúWoŇč te …õ ne gyae nyi maa y…õ…õ fa Ňčon?‚ÄĚ 37KeŇčte be yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúD…õ…õ gyae nyi de man Ňčolo ke kyaa n dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē, na Ňčolo m…Ē ke kyaa n demena d…Ē…Ē n gyoori keŇč man.‚ÄĚ 38Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź ba gyeŇč kpene keŇč …õ ne b…Ē…Ēse n…õ ya. ∆ź ke tale nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē naa diyem keŇč maa naa n…õ, yaa …õ ke tale gyoo kayey…õ…õ keŇč man maa gyoo n…õ?‚ÄĚ 39KeŇčte be tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyi, ‚ÄúAyi, d…õ…õ tale.‚ÄĚ Te Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź ke tale naa diyem keŇč maa naa, na …õ ka gyoo kayey…õ…õ keŇč maa gyoo n…õ man. 40M…Ēna m…Ē…Ē taŇč doŇč keŇč maa lese walaŇč Ňčon waa kyaa ma dunoluŇč yaa ma demena d…Ē…Ē ya. Mena akyaese k…õŇča doe fa balaŇč baŇč me kya Wurubuar…õ gbagba be desina see w…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ 41Yesu kaseela kufu akaŇč baŇč kenyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, ba gyeŇča baŇč Gyemisi na Gy…Ēn d…Ē…Ē. 42Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu baake …Ē kaseela baŇč pou yilaa te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź gyeŇč nyi t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyooneŇč ne di gyoori bo be balaŇč d…Ē…Ē, te be kegyiise baŇč m…Ē dana doŇč be d…Ē…Ē. 43M…Ē …õm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē, na mena te kaboena nyi d…õ…õ doo …õ man ya. ∆ź man walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae nyi waa bese …õ kegyia n…õ, kaboena nyi waa bese …õ kegbetibii. 44Te …õ man walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae nyi waa bese …õ siamante n…õ, kaboena nyi waa bese …õ popou …õ kegbetibii. 45Nawolo nyi maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon te k…ĒŇč nyi balaŇč baa som maŇč ya, m…Ēna me k…ĒŇčae me kaa som balaŇč, na maa yeŇč mo ma nyeedoŇč l…õ…õ balaŇč burum nyee.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kegyuusu Batimeo sia
Mateo 20:29-34; Luka 18:35-43
46KenaŇč w…Ēle te Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč gyuuw…Ē Gyeriko don…Ē…Ē man. Ňäon na …Ē kaseela na balaŇč burum baŇč ka lee bot…Ē…Ē bee gyu n…õ, be naa Timeo bu Batimeo Ňčon …Ē sia be y…õl…õ…õ n…õ na …Ē kyaa gb…õ…õnaa n…Ē…Ēman …Ē na solee ab…Ē…Ē. 47Balee Ňčon kenyii nyi Yesu Nasar…õte baale Ňčon ne k…ĒŇč n…õ, …Ē fae yeesa yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúYesu, gyoo Defidi …Ē naanabii, wii me waraŇčase.‚ÄĚ 48KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč man burum too be n…Ē…Ē gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē nyi waa wu …Ē n…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ gba te …Ē ne fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúGyoo Defidi …Ē naanabii, wii me waraŇčase.‚ÄĚ 49Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu seŇčaw…Ē yako w…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆ź baake e fa maŇč.‚ÄĚ Te be baake siay…õl…õ…õsate Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúYela ke y…õ…õ neŇč gyoŇč na n ka koro seŇč. ∆Ü ne baake neŇč.‚ÄĚ 50Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē fuŇča …Ē kegba wurikyim l…Ē yenaŇč, te …Ē toŇčaw…Ē seŇč ta k…ĒŇč Yesu gyaŇč. 51KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč te n ne gyae nyi maa y…õ…õ fa neŇč?‚ÄĚ Bot…Ē…Ē te siay…õl…õ…õsate Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, m…õ…õ gyae me sia ke gyuusu‚ÄĚ 52Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúTa, n ke l…õ…õ maŇč di d…Ē…Ē n kawee te taŇč.‚ÄĚ DebokenaŇč man te …Ē sia gyuusuw…Ē, te …Ē sila Yesu laŇč.

english_text:
Jesus Enters Jerusalem
(Matthew 21.1-11; Luke 19.28-40; John 12.12-19)
1Jesus and his disciples reached Bethphage and Bethany near the Mount of Olives. When they were getting close to Jerusalem, Jesus sent two of them on ahead. 2He told them, ‚ÄúGo into the next village. As soon as you enter it, you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here. 3If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‚ÄėThe Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
4The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it, 5some of the people standing there asked, ‚ÄúWhy are you untying the donkey?‚ÄĚ 6They told them what Jesus had said, and the people let them take it.
7The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on. 8Many people spread clothes on the road, while others spread branches they had cut from the fields.
9 In front of Jesus and behind him, people went along shouting,
‚ÄúHooray!
God bless the one who comes
in the name of the Lord!
10God bless the coming kingdom
of our ancestor David.
Hooray for God
in heaven above!‚ÄĚ
11After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples.
Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree
(Matthew 21.18,19)
12When Jesus and his disciples left Bethany the next morning, he was hungry. 13From a distance Jesus saw a fig tree covered with leaves, and he went to see if there were any figs on the tree. But there were none, because it wasn't the season for figs. 14So Jesus said to the tree, ‚ÄúNever again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!‚ÄĚ The disciples heard him say this.
Jesus in the Temple
(Matthew 21.12-17; Luke 19.45-48; John 2.13-22)
15After Jesus and his disciples reached Jerusalem, he went into the temple and began chasing out everyone who was selling and buying. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and the benches of those who were selling doves. 16Jesus would not let anyone carry things through the temple. 17 Then he taught the people and said, ‚ÄúThe Scriptures say, ‚ÄėMy house should be called a place of worship for all nations.‚Äô But you have made it a place where robbers hide!‚ÄĚ
18The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
19That evening, Jesus and the disciples went outside the city.
A Lesson from the Fig Tree
(Matthew 21.20-22)
20As the disciples walked past the fig tree the next morning, they noticed that it was completely dried up, roots and all. 21Peter remembered what Jesus had said to the tree. Then Peter said, ‚ÄúTeacher, look! The tree you put a curse on has dried up.‚ÄĚ
22Jesus told his disciples:
Have faith in God! 23 If you have faith in God and don't doubt, you can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will. 24Everything you ask for in prayer will be yours, if you only have faith.
25-26 Whenever you stand up to pray, you must forgive what others have done to you. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your sins.
A Question about Jesus' Authority
(Matthew 21.23-27; Luke 20.1-8)
27Jesus and his disciples returned to Jerusalem. And as he was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses came over to him. 28They asked, ‚ÄúWhat right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?‚ÄĚ
29Jesus answered, ‚ÄúI have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things. 30Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?‚ÄĚ
31They thought it over and said to each other, ‚ÄúWe can't say that God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John. 32On the other hand, these people think that John was a prophet. So we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.‚ÄĚ
They were afraid of the crowd 33and told Jesus, ‚ÄúWe don't know.‚ÄĚ
Jesus replied, ‚ÄúThen I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.‚ÄĚ

Wurubuar…õ dee yela ka kek…ĒŇč.

Yesu kefalaa fiigi daŇč daale

Nawolo nyi na te lii ta debaŇč keŇč fiigi daase na see ya. 14Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē falaa daŇč keŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúLee gy…õŇč ne gyu n be gyae n ka tekaa se bia na Ňčolo ke di ya.‚ÄĚ Te …Ē kaseela baŇč nyii kpene keŇč …Ē be yako n…õ.
Yesu kegegi ab…Ē…Ēy…Ē…Ēra Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man
Mateo 21:12-17; Luka 19:45-48; Gy…Ēn 2:13-22
15Be kelii Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, …Ē gyuuw…Ē Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč dekpaŇčalaŇč man. ∆Ü ke gyu bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, na balaŇč baŇč ta mo bot…Ē…Ē besena kebu dedii. KeŇčte …Ē gegi balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē na baŇč b…õ…õ l…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē bot…Ē…Ē n…õ. ∆Ü besena baŇč b…õ…õ y…õl…õ…õ k…Ēbase n…õ tebuluse buŇčii, te …Ē t…õ…õse baŇč b…õ…õ y…Ē…Ē awuluŇčna n…õ gbel…õ…õ m…Ē l…Ē. 16Te …Ē te fa Ňčolo gb…õ…õ waa seele kolo aney…Ē…ĒŇč kaalaŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč dekpaŇčalaŇč man ya. 17Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse balaŇč baŇč nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź te kala ta Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan ne yako nyi, Wurubuar…õ kpa,
‚ÄėMe deni gyae ke y…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē keŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou baa k…ĒŇč kaa fane,
m…Ēna …õm…õ…õ ta besena ke y…õ…õ Ňčm…õ…õlatena d…õ…õgyaŇčee.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Renters of a Vineyard
(Matthew 21.33-46; Luke 20.9-19)
1 Jesus then told them this story:
A farmer once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
2When it was harvest time, he sent a servant to get his share of the grapes. 3The renters grabbed the servant, beat him up, and sent him away without a thing.
4The owner sent another servant, but the renters beat him on the head and insulted him terribly. 5Then the man sent another servant, and they killed him. He kept sending servant after servant. They beat some of them and killed some.
6The owner had a son he loved very much. Finally, he sent his son to the renters because he thought they would respect him. 7But they said to themselves, ‚ÄúSomeday he will own this vineyard. Let's kill him! That way we can have it all for ourselves.‚ÄĚ 8So they grabbed the owner's son, killed him, and threw his body out of the vineyard.
9Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWhat do you think the owner of the vineyard will do? He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard. 10 You surely know that the Scriptures say,
‚ÄėThe stone the builders
tossed aside
is now the most important
stone of all.
11This is something
the Lord has done,
and it is amazing to us.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
12The leaders knew that Jesus was really talking about them, and they wanted to arrest him. But because they were afraid of the crowd, they let him alone and left.
Paying Taxes
(Matthew 22.15-22; Luke 20.20-26)
13The Pharisees got together with Herod's followers. Then they sent some men to trick Jesus into saying something wrong. 14They went to him and said, ‚ÄúTeacher, we know that you are honest. You treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are. And you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. Tell us, should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?‚ÄĚ
15Jesus knew what they were up to, and he said, ‚ÄúWhy are you trying to test me? Show me a coin!‚ÄĚ
16They brought him a silver coin, and he asked, ‚ÄúWhose picture and name are on it?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThe Emperor's,‚ÄĚ they answered.
17Then Jesus told them, ‚ÄúGive the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.‚ÄĚ The men were amazed at Jesus.
Life in the Future World
(Matthew 22.23-33; Luke 20.27-40)
18 The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus and said:
19 Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother. 20There were once seven brothers. The first one married, but died without having any children. 21The second brother married his brother's widow, and he also died without having children. The same thing happened to the third brother, 22and finally to all seven brothers. At last the woman died. 23When God raises people from death, whose wife will this woman be? After all, she had been married to all seven brothers.
24Jesus answered:
You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God. 25When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven. 26 You surely know about people being raised to life. You know that in the story about Moses and the burning bush, God said, ‚ÄúI am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.‚ÄĚ 27He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living. You Sadducees are all wrong.
The Most Important Commandment
(Matthew 22.34-40; Luke 10.25-28)
28 One of the teachers of the Law of Moses came up while Jesus and the Sadducees were arguing. When he heard Jesus give a good answer, he asked him, ‚ÄúWhat is the most important commandment?‚ÄĚ
29 Jesus answered, ‚ÄúThe most important one says: ‚ÄėPeople of Israel, you have only one Lord and God. 30You must love him with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength.‚Äô 31 The second most important commandment says: ‚ÄėLove others as much as you love yourself.‚Äô No other commandment is more important than these.‚ÄĚ
32 The man replied, ‚ÄúTeacher, you are certainly right to say there is only one God. 33 It is also true that we must love God with all our heart, mind, and strength, and that we must love others as much as we love ourselves. These commandments are more important than all the sacrifices and offerings that we could possibly make.‚ÄĚ
34When Jesus saw that the man had given a sensible answer, he told him, ‚ÄúYou are not far from God's kingdom.‚ÄĚ After this, no one dared ask Jesus any more questions.
About David's Son
(Matthew 22.41-46; Luke 20.41-44)
35As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, ‚ÄúHow can the teachers of the Law of Moses say the Messiah will come from the family of King David? 36 The Holy Spirit led David to say,
‚ÄėThe Lord said to my Lord:
Sit at my right side
until I make your enemies
into a footstool for you.‚Äô
37If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be his son?‚ÄĚ
The large crowd enjoyed listening to Jesus teach.
Jesus Condemns the Pharisees and the Teachers of the Law of Moses
(Matthew 23.1-36; Luke 20.45-47)
38As Jesus was teaching, he said:
Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes and be greeted in the market. 39They like the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets. 40But they cheat widows out of their homes and pray long prayers just to show off. They will be punished most of all.
A Widow's Offering
(Luke 21.1-4)
41Jesus was sitting in the temple near the offering box and watching people put in their gifts. He noticed that many rich people were giving a lot of money. 42Finally, a poor widow came up and put in two coins worth only a few pennies. 43Jesus told his disciples to gather around him. Then he said:
I tell you that this poor widow has put in more than all the others. 44Everyone else gave what they didn't need. But she is very poor and gave everything she had. Now she doesn't have a cent to live on.

18KenaŇč w…Ēle te Sadukiitena baŇč b…Ē…Ē ne l…õ…õ di nyi walaŇč da yem na waa bese foŇč ya n…õ, man baale k…ĒŇčaw…Ē Yesu gyaŇč kaa b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, 19‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, de naana Mosesi Ňčmarase mmaraa fa daa nyi, akpaa walaŇč da yem tina …Ē w…õ…õle yela te be ta lola bia ya na, …Ē naabu waa yala lialo Ňčon na baa lola bia …Ē kegyia Ňčon yele man. 20K…ĒŇčaw…Ē nyi balee Ňčolo bia baala nyetooro baale kyaaw…Ē, te be kegyia Ňčon yala alo keŇč be ta lola bia ya te …Ē yeŇčaw…Ē. 21Balate Ňčon m…Ē yala alo ŇčonaŇč te Ňčon m…Ē yeŇčaw…Ē keŇč be ta lola bia ya. 35Yesu ke gyakaa …Ē na wolo Gyudatena …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč dekpaŇčalaŇč man n…õ …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúSena d…Ē…Ē te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč ne tale yako nyi, Kristo Ňčon Wurubuar…õ ba lese n…õ y…õ…õ gyoo Defidi …Ē naanabii e? 36Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon yela gyoo Defidi gbagba yakow…Ē nyiaa,
‚ÄėMe GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ yako me GbeŇčgyoo nyi,
Tisi kyaa me dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē,
kelii debaŇč keŇč maa mo n k…Ēlala pou do n t…õ…õ.‚Äô
37Te akpaa nyi gyoo Defidi baake nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon nyi …Ē ‚ÄėGbeŇčgyoo‚Äô na, kenaŇč na sena te nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon waa bese gyoo Defidi …Ē naanabii?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee bee tei n…õ gyoŇč.
Yesu kefaree …Ē silala lee Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč wose man
Mateo 23:1-36; Luka 20:45-47
38Yesu k…õ…õ wolo n…õ …Ē faree w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ …õ wose nideli na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč. B…õ…õ gyae kegbase d…Ē…ĒloŇč nyiŇčan kado na be d…ĒŇč na kebu agantaŇč man, na balaŇč kenyiŇč do w…Ē n…Ē…Ē. 39Akpaa be gyaŇčee Gyudatena …Ēsom den…õ…õ man, na bot…Ē…Ēse Ňčan b…õ…õ gyaŇčee di be n…Ē…Ē n…õ, na b…õ…õ gyae akyaese kpaakpaa baa kyaa. 40B…õ…õ beo liala l…õo ba ab…Ē…Ē kamas…õ Ňčan be dana n…õ. Te b…õ…õ fane balaŇč man kyare mo wola ba wose. Wurubuar…õ waa w…ĒŇč mena balaŇč benaŇč din…Ē…Ē deŇčela nideli.‚ÄĚ
Lialo diyemte Ňčolo k…Ēba kado
Luka 21:1-4
41Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man n…õ, …Ē ke kyaaw…Ē yenaŇč mo …Ē sia tee adakaa keŇč b…õ…õ do k…Ēba ke man n…õ. Te …Ē gyinaa …Ē sia …Ē ne k…õ…õ mena keŇč balaŇč na mo k…Ēba kaa do adakaa keŇč man. BalaŇč baŇč be dana k…Ēba n…õ, moo k…Ēba burum kaa do adakaa keŇč man. 42KeŇčte lialo diyemte Ňčolo moo k…Ēbabia ala, Ňčan Ňče gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba kotoku dokoloŇč n…õ kaa do adakaa keŇč man. 43Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu baake …Ē kaseela baŇč gyaŇčee te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyi lialo diyemte kei ta do k…Ēba kela balaŇč akaŇč baŇč pou. 44Nawolo nyi balaŇč baŇč pou lese k…Ēba kyomii bo lee burum Ňčan be dana n…õ man kaa do. M…Ē lialo diyemte kei d…Ē…Ē, keŇč na …Ē diyem koraŇč n…õ kpene kamas…õ keŇč …Ē dana n…õ yaa …Ē moow…Ē kaa do n…õ.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
The Temple Will Be Destroyed
(Matthew 24.1,2; Luke 21.5,6)
1As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him, ‚ÄúTeacher, look at these beautiful stones and wonderful buildings!‚ÄĚ
2Jesus replied, ‚ÄúDo you see these huge buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.‚ÄĚ
Warning about Trouble
(Matthew 24.3-14; Luke 21.7-19)
3Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew came to him in private. 4 They asked, ‚ÄúWhen will these things happen? What will be the sign that they are about to take place?‚ÄĚ
5Jesus answered:
Watch out and don't let anyone fool you! 6Many will come and claim to be me. They will use my name and fool many people.
7When you hear about wars and threats of wars, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end. 8Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. There will be earthquakes in many places, and people will starve to death. But this is just the beginning of troubles.
9 Be on your guard! You will be taken to courts and beaten with whips in their synagogues. And because of me, you will have to stand before rulers and kings to tell about your faith. 10But before the end comes, the good news must be preached to all nations.
11When you are arrested, don't worry about what you will say. You will be given the right words when the time comes. But you will not really be the ones speaking. Your words will come from the Holy Spirit.
12Brothers and sisters will betray each other and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed. 13 Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you keep on being faithful right to the end, you will be saved.
The Horrible Thing
(Matthew 24.15-21; Luke 21.20-24)
14 Someday you will see that ‚ÄúHorrible Thing‚ÄĚ where it should not be. Everyone who reads this must try to understand! If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains. 15 If you are on the roof of your house, don't go inside to get anything. 16If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat. 17It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children. 18Pray that it won't happen in winter. 19 This will be the worst time of suffering since God created the world, and nothing this terrible will ever happen again. 20If the Lord doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of his chosen and special ones, he will make the time shorter.
21If someone should say, ‚ÄúHere is the Messiah!‚ÄĚ or ‚ÄúThere he is!‚ÄĚ don't believe it. 22False messiahs and false prophets will come and work miracles and signs. They will even try to fool God's chosen ones. 23But be on your guard! That's why I am telling you these things now.
When the Son of Man Appears
(Matthew 24.29-31; Luke 21.25-28)
24 In those days, right after this time of suffering,
‚ÄúThe sun will become dark,
and the moon
will no longer shine.
25 The stars will fall,
and the powers in the sky
will be shaken.‚ÄĚ
26 Then the Son of Man will be seen coming in the clouds with great power and glory. 27He will send his angels to gather his chosen ones from all over the earth.
A Lesson from a Fig Tree
(Matthew 24.32-35; Luke 21.29-33)
28Learn a lesson from a fig tree. When its branches sprout and start putting out leaves, you know summer is near. 29So when you see all these things happening, you will know that the time has almost come. 30You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all this happens. 31The sky and the earth will not last forever, but my words will.
No One Knows the Day or Time
(Matthew 24.36-44)
32 No one knows the day or the time. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows. 33So watch out and be ready! You don't know when the time will come. 34 It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert. 35So be alert! You don't know when the master of the house will come back. It could be in the evening or at midnight or before dawn or in the morning. 36But if he comes suddenly, don't let him find you asleep. 37I tell everyone just what I have told you. Be alert!
translation_text:

Ňäolo ba gyeŇč mena debaŇč keŇč nombia k…õŇča gyae kek…ĒŇč ya

Gyis…õ me kya Ňčon …Ē kyaa adido n…õ waageŇč gyeŇča la. 33Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ k…õ…õ nideli na …õ ke desina …õ wose see, nawolo nyi …õ ba gyeŇč wee keŇč debaŇč kenaŇč gyae ke k…ĒŇč ya. 34Ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ba koro …Ē ne gyu gb…õ…õ, te …Ē ba mo …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč do …Ē tewul…õ…õ nyiŇčmaa man n…õ. ∆Ü faa be man walaŇč kamas…õ tom keŇč waa y…õ…õ, te …Ē yako Ňčon …Ē na deke denan…Ē…Ē keŇč n…õ nyi waa deke ke nideli. 35Mena d…Ē…Ē …õm…õ…õ m…Ē …õ doo …õ wose d…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi …õ ba gyeŇč wee keŇč dekpaŇčalaŇčte Ňčon waa k…ĒŇč ya. english_text:
A Plot To Kill Jesus
(Matthew 26.1-5; Luke 22.1,2; John 11.45-53)
1 It was now two days before Passover and the Festival of Thin Bread. The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were planning how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death. 2They were saying, ‚ÄúWe must not do it during the festival, because the people will riot.‚ÄĚ
At Bethany
(Matthew 26.6-13; John 12.1-8)
3 Jesus was eating in Bethany at the home of Simon, who once had leprosy, when a woman came in with a very expensive bottle of sweet-smelling perfume. After breaking it open, she poured the perfume on Jesus' head. 4This made some of the guests angry, and they complained, ‚ÄúWhy such a waste? 5We could have sold this perfume for more than 300 silver coins and given the money to the poor!‚ÄĚ So they started saying cruel things to the woman.
6But Jesus said:
Leave her alone! Why are you bothering her? She has done a beautiful thing for me. 7 You will always have the poor with you. And whenever you want to, you can give to them. But you won't always have me here with you. 8She has done all she could by pouring perfume on my body to prepare it for burial. 9You may be sure that wherever the good news is told all over the world, people will remember what she has done. And they will tell others.
Judas and the Chief Priests
(Matthew 26.14-16; Luke 22.3-6)
10Judas Iscariot was one of the twelve disciples. He went to the chief priests and offered to help them arrest Jesus. 11They were glad to hear this, and they promised to pay him. So Judas started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus.
Jesus Eats with His Disciples
(Matthew 26.17-25; Luke 22.7-14,21-23; John 13.21-30)
12It was the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, and the Passover lambs were being killed. Jesus' disciples asked him, ‚ÄúWhere do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?‚ÄĚ
13Jesus said to two of the disciples, ‚ÄúGo into the city, where you will meet a man carrying a jar of water. Follow him, 14and when he goes into a house, say to the owner, ‚ÄėOur teacher wants to know if you have a room where he can eat the Passover meal with his disciples.‚Äô 15The owner will take you upstairs and show you a large room furnished and ready for you to use. Prepare the meal there.‚ÄĚ
16The two disciples went into the city and found everything just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover meal.
17-18 While Jesus and the twelve disciples were eating together that evening, he said, ‚ÄúThe one who will betray me is now eating with me.‚ÄĚ
19This made the disciples sad, and one after another they said to Jesus, ‚ÄúYou surely don't mean me!‚ÄĚ
20He answered, ‚ÄúIt is one of you twelve men who is eating from this dish with me. 21The Son of Man will die, just as the Scriptures say. But it is going to be terrible for the one who betrays me. That man would be better off if he had never been born.‚ÄĚ
The Lord's Supper
(Matthew 26.26-30; Luke 22.14-23; 1 Corinthians 11.23-25)
22During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, ‚ÄúTake this. It is my body.‚ÄĚ
23Jesus picked up a cup of wine and gave thanks to God. He gave it to his disciples, and they all drank some. 24 Then he said, ‚ÄúThis is my blood, which is poured out for many people, and with it God makes his agreement. 25From now on I will not drink any wine, until I drink new wine in God's kingdom.‚ÄĚ 26Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives.
Peter's Promise
(Matthew 26.31-35; Luke 22.31-34; John 13.36-38)
27 Jesus said to his disciples, ‚ÄúAll of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say,
‚ÄėI will strike down
the shepherd,
and the sheep
will be scattered.‚Äô
28 But after I am raised to life, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.‚ÄĚ
29Peter spoke up, ‚ÄúEven if all the others reject you, I never will!‚ÄĚ
30Jesus replied, ‚ÄúThis very night before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.‚ÄĚ
31But Peter was so sure of himself that he said, ‚ÄúEven if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you!‚ÄĚ
All the others said the same thing.
Jesus Prays
(Matthew 26.36-46; Luke 22.39-46)
32Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told them, ‚ÄúSit here while I pray.‚ÄĚ
33Jesus took along Peter, James, and John. He was sad and troubled and 34told them, ‚ÄúI am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.‚ÄĚ
35-36Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt down on the ground and prayed, ‚ÄúFather, if it is possible, don't let this happen to me! Father, you can do anything. Don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.‚ÄĚ
37When Jesus came back and found the disciples sleeping, he said to Simon Peter, ‚ÄúAre you asleep? Can't you stay awake for just one hour? 38Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.‚ÄĚ
39Jesus went back and prayed the same prayer. 40But when he returned to the disciples, he found them sleeping again. They simply could not keep their eyes open, and they did not know what to say.
41When Jesus returned to the disciples the third time, he said, ‚ÄúAre you still sleeping and resting? Enough of this! The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners. 42Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is Arrested
(Matthew 26.47-56; Luke 22.47-53; John 18.3-12)
43Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a mob of men armed with swords and clubs were with him. They had been sent by the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses. 44Judas had told them ahead of time, ‚ÄúArrest the man I greet with a kiss. Tie him up tight and lead him away.‚ÄĚ
45Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, ‚ÄúTeacher!‚ÄĚ Then Judas kissed him, 46and the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
47Someone standing there pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
48Jesus said to the mob, ‚ÄúWhy do you come with swords and clubs to arrest me like a criminal? 49 Day after day I was with you and taught in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. But what the Scriptures say must come true.‚ÄĚ
50All of Jesus' disciples ran off and left him. 51One of them was a young man who was wearing only a linen cloth. And when the men grabbed him, 52he left the cloth behind and ran away naked.
Jesus Is Questioned by the Council
(Matthew 26.57-68; Luke 22.54,55,63-71; John 18.13,14,19-24)
53Jesus was led off to the high priest. Then the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses all met together. 54Peter had followed at a distance, and when he reached the courtyard of the high priest's house, he sat down with the guards to warm himself beside a fire.
55The chief priests and the whole council tried to find someone to accuse Jesus of a crime, so they could put him to death. But they could not find anyone to accuse him. 56Many people did tell lies against Jesus, but they did not agree on what they said. 57Finally, some men stood up and lied about him. They said, 58 ‚ÄúWe heard him say he would tear down this temple that we built. He also claimed that in three days he would build another one without any help.‚ÄĚ 59But even then they did not agree on what they said.
60The high priest stood up in the council and asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWhy don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?‚ÄĚ 61But Jesus kept quiet and did not say a word. The high priest asked him another question, ‚ÄúAre you the Messiah, the Son of the glorious God?‚ÄĚ
62 ‚ÄúYes, I am!‚ÄĚ Jesus answered.
‚ÄúSoon you will see
the Son of Man
sitting at the right side
of God All-Powerful,
and coming with the clouds
of heaven.‚ÄĚ
63At once the high priest ripped his robe apart and shouted, ‚ÄúWhy do we need more witnesses? 64 You heard him claim to be God! What is your decision?‚ÄĚ They all agreed he should be put to death.
65Some of the people started spitting on Jesus. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said, ‚ÄúTell us who hit you!‚ÄĚ Then the guards took charge of Jesus and beat him.
Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus
(Matthew 26.69-75; Luke 22.56-62; John 18.15-18,25-27)
66While Peter was still in the courtyard, a servant girl of the high priest came up 67and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, ‚ÄúYou were with Jesus from Nazareth!‚ÄĚ
68Peter replied, ‚ÄúThat isn't true! I don't know what you're talking about. I don't have any idea what you mean.‚ÄĚ He went out to the gate, and a rooster crowed.
69The servant girl saw Peter again and said to the people standing there, ‚ÄúThis man is one of them!‚ÄĚ
70‚ÄúNo, I'm not!‚ÄĚ Peter replied.
A little while later some of the people said to Peter, ‚ÄúYou certainly are one of them. You're a Galilean!‚ÄĚ
71This time Peter began to curse and swear, ‚ÄúI don't even know the man you're talking about!‚ÄĚ
72At once the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had told him, ‚ÄúBefore a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.‚ÄĚ So Peter started crying.

Na …õ ka bese naa maŇč na me d…ĒŇč Ňč…Ēma man adido m…õ…õ k…ĒŇč.‚ÄĚ 63Bot…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ saese kegyia Ňčon tirii Ňčon gbagba kegba te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč adans…õ…õ yaa d…õ…õ gyae dee nyii bela?‚ÄĚ 64∆ź te nyii mmusuo nombia Ňčan …Ē be yako yee? Te be b…Ē…Ēse e nyi, ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre, wola daa Ňčon …Ē be gy…Ē neŇč.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte ba moo e fa kot…Ēt…Ē…Ētena baŇč te baŇč m…Ē ke tuku e.
Pita keb…õ…õ Yesu
Mateo 26:69-75; Luka 22:56-62; Gy…Ēn 18:15-18, 25-27
66Pita ke kyaa dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man n…õ, Wurubuar…õ saese kegyia Ňčon alebu Ňčolo k…ĒŇčaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē. 67∆Ü kena Pita kyaa …Ē ne woli boalaŇč n…õ, …Ē k…õ…õ e yididi te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč m…Ē n kpu…õ na Yesu Nasar…õte baale Ňčon.‚ÄĚ 68M…Ē Pita l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč te …Ē yako e nyi, ‚ÄúM…Ē…Ē gyeŇč kpene keŇč n ne yako n…õ man korakora ya.‚ÄĚ Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita leew…Ē gyu denan…Ē…Ē keŇč man. 69Alebu Ňčon ka bese naa Pita bot…Ē…Ē n…õ …Ē yako balaŇč baŇč ba seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee kei m…Ē kpu…õ na w…Ē.‚ÄĚ 70M…Ē Pita besew…Ē b…õ…õ ke bela. KenaŇč w…Ēle n…õ, balaŇč baŇč ba seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē n…õ besew…Ē yako Pita nyiaa, ‚ÄúKe y…õ…õ ampaŇč nyi n kpu…õ na w…Ē, nawolo nyi n y…õ…õ Galelia baale e.‚ÄĚ 71Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita moo n…Ē…ĒdoŇč kolosi te …Ē kaŇča ntam kpu nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…Ē…Ē gyeŇč balee kei Ňčon …õ ne kolosi …Ē wose man nombia n…õ korakora ya‚ÄĚ 72DebokenaŇč man te kegyim l…Ē…Ē woya betabolate. KeŇčte Pita t…Ē…Ēse nombia Ňčan Yesu be yako e n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúP…õ kegyim kel…Ē woya betabola na, nyaŇč ke b…õ…õ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŇč maŇč ya.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte …Ē y…õl…õ…õw…Ē do …Ē ne wii.

english_text:
Pilate Questions Jesus
(Matthew 27.1,2,11-14; Luke 23.1-5; John 18.28-38)
1Early the next morning the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met together with the whole Jewish council. They tied up Jesus and led him off to Pilate.
2He asked Jesus, ‚ÄúAre you the king of the Jews?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThose are your words,‚ÄĚ Jesus answered.
3The chief priests brought many charges against Jesus. 4Then Pilate questioned him again, ‚ÄúDon't you have anything to say? Don't you hear what crimes they say you have done?‚ÄĚ 5But Jesus did not answer, and Pilate was amazed.
The Death Sentence
(Matthew 27.15-26; Luke 23.13-25; John 18.39‚ÄĒ19.16)
6During Passover, Pilate always freed one prisoner chosen by the people. 7And at that time there was a prisoner named Barabbas. He and some others had been arrested for murder during a riot. 8The crowd now came and asked Pilate to set a prisoner free, just as he usually did.
9Pilate asked them, ‚ÄúDo you want me to free the king of the Jews?‚ÄĚ 10Pilate knew that the chief priests had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
11But the chief priests told the crowd to ask Pilate to free Barabbas.
12Then Pilate asked the crowd, ‚ÄúWhat do you want me to do with this man you say is the king of the Jews?‚ÄĚ
13They yelled, ‚ÄúNail him to a cross!‚ÄĚ
14Pilate asked, ‚ÄúBut what crime has he done?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúNail him to a cross!‚ÄĚ they yelled even louder.
15Pilate wanted to please the crowd, so he set Barabbas free. Then he ordered his soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip and nail him to a cross.
Soldiers Make Fun of Jesus
(Matthew 27.27-30; John 19.2,3)
16The soldiers led Jesus inside the courtyard of the fortress and called together the rest of the troops. 17They put a purple robe on him, and on his head they placed a crown they had made out of thorn branches. 18They made fun of Jesus and shouted, ‚ÄúHey, you king of the Jews!‚ÄĚ 19Then they beat him on the head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt down and pretended to worship him.
20When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the purple robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross. 21 Simon from Cyrene happened to be coming in from a farm, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross. Simon was the father of Alexander and Rufus.
Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross
(Matthew 27.31-44; Luke 23.27-43; John 19.17-27)
22The soldiers took Jesus to Golgotha, which means ‚ÄúPlace of a Skull.‚ÄĚ 23There they gave him some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain, but he refused to drink it.
24 They nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes. 25It was about nine o'clock in the morning when they nailed him to the cross. 26On it was a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, ‚ÄúThis is the King of the Jews.‚ÄĚ 27-28 The soldiers also nailed two criminals on crosses, one to the right of Jesus and the other to his left.
29 People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and shouted, ‚ÄúHa! So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days. 30Save yourself and come down from the cross!‚ÄĚ
31The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses also made fun of Jesus. They said to each other, ‚ÄúHe saved others, but he can't save himself. 32If he is the Messiah, the king of Israel, let him come down from the cross! Then we will see and believe.‚ÄĚ The two criminals also said cruel things to Jesus.
The Death of Jesus
(Matthew 27.45-56; Luke 23.44-49; John 19.28-30)
33About noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until around three o'clock. 34 Then about that time Jesus shouted, ‚ÄúEloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?‚ÄĚ which means, ‚ÄúMy God, my God, why have you deserted me?‚ÄĚ
35Some of the people standing there heard Jesus and said, ‚ÄúHe is calling for Elijah.‚ÄĚ 36 One of them ran and grabbed a sponge. After he had soaked it in wine, he put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus. He said, ‚ÄúLet's wait and see if Elijah will come and take him down!‚ÄĚ 37Jesus shouted and then died.
38 At once the curtain in the temple tore in two from top to bottom.
39A Roman army officer was standing in front of Jesus. When the officer saw how Jesus died, he said, ‚ÄúThis man really was the Son of God!‚ÄĚ
40-41 Some women were looking on from a distance. They and many others had come with Jesus to Jerusalem. But even before this they had been his followers and had helped him while he was in Galilee. Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of the younger James and of Joseph were two of these women. Salome was also one of them.
Jesus Is Buried
(Matthew 27.57-61; Luke 23.50-56; John 19.38-42)
42It was now the evening before the Sabbath, and the Jewish people were getting ready for that sacred day. 43A man named Joseph from Arimathea was brave enough to ask Pilate for the body of Jesus. Joseph was a highly respected member of the Jewish council, and he was also waiting for God's kingdom to come.
44Pilate was surprised to hear that Jesus was already dead, and he called in the army officer to find out if Jesus had been dead very long. 45After the officer told him, Pilate let Joseph have Jesus' body.
46Joseph bought a linen cloth and took the body down from the cross. He had it wrapped in the cloth, and he put it in a tomb that had been cut into solid rock. Then he rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb.
47Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joseph were watching and saw where the body was placed.
translation_text:
Be kamo Yesu gyu gyoo Pilato gyaŇč
Mateo 27:1-2, 11-14; Luka 23:1-5; Gy…Ēn 18:28-38
1T…õ…õ kena nebo nebo fuu n…õ, saese kegyiise na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala gyaŇčeew…Ē te be bake Yesu nyeeman. Mena d…Ē…Ē be bake e te ba moo e gyu ka do Pilato Ňčon …Ē seŇč…õ…õ Romantena gyoo dinaa naw…Ē…Ē man n…õ, nyiŇčmaa man. 2KeŇčte Pilato b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč y…õna Gyudatena gyoo Ňčon?‚ÄĚ Te Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúMena yaa n yakow…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ 3Te Gyudatena saese kegyiise baŇč k…õr…õ…õ …Ē n…Ē…Ēse mo nombia burum gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē. 4KeŇčte Pilato b…Ē…Ēse e nyi, ‚ÄúBe ta mo nombia k…õŇča b…õ…õ gyakaa n d…Ē…Ē na, n ba lese n n…Ē…Ēse yee?‚ÄĚ 5Bot…Ē…Ē te nombia Ňčan doo gyoo Pilato n…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi Yesu te yako daale ya.
Be kalese Yesu fa yeŇč
Mateo 27:15-27; Luka 23:13-25; Gy…Ēn 18:39‚Äď19:16
6Gyudatena nyeekel…õ…õwee kedi kamas…õ n…õ, kaboena nyi Pilato waa tina balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyam t…Ē deni n…õ man dokoloŇč, Ňčon balaŇč benaŇč gbagba ne gyae n…õ yela fa w…Ē. 7Mena debaŇč kenaŇč na ba te kyaŇč Barabase t…Ē deni. Barabase kei kpu…õ balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē koro seŇč tia gyoo dinaa, te b…Ē…Ē ko balaŇč n…õ man. 8Bot…Ē…Ē te balaŇč dikpii keŇč k…ĒŇča Pilato gyaŇč nyi waa y…õ…õ kpene keŇč …Ē ne taŇč y…õ…õ debaŇč kamas…õ n…õ fa w…Ē. 9Te Pilato b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź ne gyae nyi maa lese Gyudatena gyoo Ňčon fa Ňčon?‚ÄĚ 10KeŇč d…Ē…Ē Pilato be b…Ē…Ēse mena y…õna nyi, …Ē gyeŇč nyi saese kegyiise baŇč ne k…Ēla Yesu d…Ē…Ē te ba lese e fa yeŇč. 11M…Ē Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise baŇč feefuw…Ē do balaŇč dikpii keŇč nyi, baa yako Pilato nyi waa lese Barabase fa w…Ē. 12Bot…Ē…Ē te Pilato b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúSena te …õ ne gyae maa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …õ ne baake e nyi Gyudatena gyoo n…õ?‚ÄĚ 13Te balaŇč dikpii keŇč fae yeesa nyi, ‚ÄúYela baa gy…Ē e nyeelabia sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē na waa yeŇč‚ÄĚ 14KeŇčte Pilato b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč dukum te …Ē y…õ…õw…Ē?‚ÄĚ M…Ēna be gyakaa bo b…õ…õ fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč nyi, ‚ÄúGy…Ē e nyeelabia sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē‚ÄĚ 15KeŇč Pilato ne gyae mena keŇč be w…Ēe ke f…õ…õ w…Ē d…Ē…Ē …Ē lese Barabase fa w…Ē, te …Ē yela …Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena tuku Yesu te …Ē moo e fa nyi baa gyu ke gy…Ē e nyeelabia sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē.
Be kekulu Yesu
Mateo 27:27-31
16KeŇčte yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč moo Yesu gyu Pilato dekpaŇčalaŇč man bot…Ē…Ē keŇč bee di nombia n…õ, te be baake be tebia yoon…Ē…Ētena akaŇč baŇč pou gyaŇčee. 17Te ba moo kegba yayam do e, te ba moo s…Ē…Ēse loo gyooro kot…Ēt…Ē…Ē wuu e. 18Na bee kulu e nyiaa, ‚ÄúGyudatena gyoo, n nyee d…õ…õ wolose‚ÄĚ 19Bot…Ē…Ē na be gyakaa b…õ…õ mo daŇč Ňčmeree e …Ē nyeeman, na b…õ…õ t…Ē n…Ē…ĒloŇč woli e na b…õ…õ buŇč …Ē naw…Ē…Ēt…õ…õ kulu e. 20Be ke kulu e taŇč n…õ, ba kpee kegba yayam keŇč te ba moo Ňčon gbagba wui keŇč do e. Te ba moo e lee be kaa gy…Ē e sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē.
Ba kegy…Ē Yesu …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē
Mateo 27:32-44; Luka 23:26-43; Gy…Ēn 19:17-27
21Be ka lee don…Ē…Ē keŇč man bee gyu n…õ, te be gyaŇčee na Sim…Ēn Ňčon …Ē ba lee Kirene don…Ē…Ē man n…õ na …Ē ne k…ĒŇč. Sim…Ēn kei bia y…õna Al…õkesanda na Rufuse. KeŇčte ba Ňčere e nyi waa l…õ…õ Yesu …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč seele. 22Te ba moo Yesu gyu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake nyi ‚ÄúGolgata‚ÄĚ keŇč nawolo nyi ‚ÄúNyeepookoo‚ÄĚ d…õ…õ n…õ. 23KeŇčte ba moo tee keŇč b…õ…õ baake nyi m…õ…õr…õ n…õ kyakale soloŇč man fa Yesu nyi waa ny…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna …Ē te ny…Ē…Ē ke ya. 24Ba ke gy…Ē e sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, te be kp…õl…õ…õ …Ē kegbase buŇčii te be l…Ē…Ē boe Ňče d…Ē…Ē, na baa naa keŇč walaŇč kamas…õ waa nyiŇč. 25Gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba nebo weese debaŇč atoorote man n…õ, te be gy…Ē…Ē Yesu sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē. 26KeŇčte be Ňčmarase dukum keŇč be kpa …Ē be y…õ…õ n…õ mataa …Ē nyee gb…õ…õ nyiaa,
‚Äúgyudatena gyoo Ňčon yaa.‚ÄĚ
27Bot…Ē…Ē te be gy…Ē…Ē Ňčm…õ…õlatena bala baale m…Ē sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaase d…Ē…Ē kpu na Yesu. Ňäolo sakaa …Ē dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē, te Ňčolo m…Ē sakaa …Ē demena d…Ē…Ē. 28Kei yela Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan k…ĒŇčaw…Ē mena. Nombia ŇčenaŇč y…õna nyi, ‚ÄúBe ta mo e kpu na nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra.‚ÄĚ 29Na balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ lam bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, na tee e na bee kpiisi ba nyeeya yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč Ňčon n kpa n ke tekerii Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč na n ka mo weeya atooro maa ke n…õ, 30l…õ…õ n wose na n ke tisi lee …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē na d…õ…õ k…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ 31Mena m…Ē te Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč m…Ē kulu e. Te be yako d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚Äú∆Ü ne tale l…õ…õ baale nyee, m…Ēna …Ē be tale l…õ…õ Ňčon gbagba nyee ya.‚ÄĚ 32Nyi akpaa Ňčon y…õna nyeedoŇč GbeŇčgyoo na Iseraetena gyoo Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ, kenaŇč na waa l…õ…õ …Ē wose lee …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē na d…õ…õ naa na d…õ…õ l…õ…õ e di. Te balaŇč bala baŇč b…Ē…Ē gy…Ē w…Ē sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaase d…Ē…Ē kpu na e n…õ m…Ē tee e mena.
Yesu yeŇč
Mateo 27:45-56; Luka 23:44-49; Gy…Ēn 19:28-30
33Weese ka seŇč d…õ…õ n…õ, ditiŇčt…õ…õ kaa l…õ…õ t…õ…õle keŇč d…Ē…Ē pou kelii …Ē ke g…õ…õse. 34Te weese ke g…õ…õse kenaŇč n…õ Yesu fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúEli, Eli, lama sabatani?‚ÄĚ KeŇč nawolo nyi, ‚ÄúMe Wurubuar…õ, Me Wurubuar…õ, weera te n lese n sia yela maŇč?‚ÄĚ 35BalaŇč baŇč ba seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē ke nyii mena n…õ be yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź nyii, …Ē ne baake Elaegya.‚ÄĚ 36DebokenaŇč man te be man Ňčolo yeuw…Ē ka mo kolo Ňčgba kokyaa keŇč gyae ke tale l…õ…õ loŇč n…õ do soloŇč denyaŇčesa daale man, te …Ē moo ke tooso na daŇč te …Ē gyinaa ke adido fa Yesu nyi waa ny…Ē…Ē. KeŇčte …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź seŇč d…õ…õ k…õ…õ nyi Elaegya waa k…ĒŇč kaa lese e lee …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 37Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu besew…Ē fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč, te …Ē kara weesi. 38Kek…ĒŇč mena n…õ, kanyaŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo kpase Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man n…õ b…õ…õsew…Ē ky…õ…õky…õ…õ ala lee adido kaa lii ka mok…Ē…Ē man.
39Yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon …Ē seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē kena Yesu ke fae yeesa kara weesi n…õ, …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee kei y…õna Wurubuar…õ bu Ňčon ampaŇč.‚ÄĚ
40Na ala baale seŇč…õ…õ lemlem b…õ…õ k…õ…õ. Ala benaŇč man baale y…õna M…õ…õre Ňčon …Ē ba lee Magedala n…õ, na Gyemisi kemaasa na Gyose be naa M…õ…õre na Salome. 41Ala k…õw…Ē silana Yesu k…õ…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē debaŇč keŇč …Ē kya Galelia n…õ. Na ala burum baŇč b…Ē…Ē sila e k…ĒŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ m…Ē seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē.
Yesu kewuu
Mateo 27:57-61; Luka 23:50-56; Gy…Ēn 19:38-42
42Na wee kenaŇč y…õ…õ wee keŇč Gyudatena na mo desina ba wose fa ba kef…õ…õfowee keŇč kedi. Ke lii balin…Ē…Ē n…õ, 43balee Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Gyos…õfo Ňčon …Ē ba lee Arimatia don…Ē…Ē man n…õ gy…Ē…Ē …Ē kon…Ē…Ē gyu Pilato gyaŇč ka sola e nyi waa mo Yesu woo Ňčon fa e …Ē kaa wuu. Gyos…õfo kei y…õ…õ don…Ē…Ē kegyia Ňčolo e, te Ňčon m…Ē ne gyeki Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč. 44Pilato ke nyii nyi Yesu ta yem n…õ, y…õ…õ e gyakoloŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē baake …Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúYesu Ňčon wulaa yeŇčaw…Ē?‚ÄĚ 45∆Ü ke nyii lee yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon n…Ē…Ēman nyi ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč n…õ, …Ē faa Gyos…õfo Ňčon gb…õ…õ nyi waa gyu ka mo woo Ňčon. 46Mena d…Ē…Ē Gyos…õfo gyuuw…Ē ke l…õ…õ akaral…õ…õ te …Ē lese woo Ňčon lee …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē moo akaral…õ…õ keŇč milii e. KeŇčte …Ē moo e ke wuu yeb…Ē…Ē keŇč b…Ē…Ē kare boe daale man n…õ man. Te …Ē biliŇči boe dinaa daale t…Ē yeb…Ē…Ē keŇč n…Ē…Ē. 47Na M…õ…õre Ňčon …Ē ba lee Magedala n…õ, na Gyose …Ē naa M…õ…õre seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē be naa bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…Ē…Ē wuu e n…õ.

english_text:
Jesus Is Alive
(Matthew 28.1-8; Luke 24.1-12; John 20.1-10)
1After the Sabbath, Mary Magdalene, Salome, and Mary the mother of James bought some spices to put on Jesus' body. 2Very early on Sunday morning, just as the sun was coming up, they went to the tomb. 3On their way, they were asking one another, ‚ÄúWho will roll the stone away from the entrance for us?‚ÄĚ 4But when they looked, they saw that the stone had already been rolled away. And it was a huge stone!
5The women went into the tomb, and on the right side they saw a young man in a white robe sitting there. They were alarmed.
6The man said, ‚ÄúDon't be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus from Nazareth, who was nailed to a cross. God has raised him to life, and he isn't here. You can see the place where they put his body. 7 Now go and tell his disciples, and especially Peter, that he will go ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.‚ÄĚ
8When the women ran from the tomb, they were confused and shaking all over. They were too afraid to tell anyone what had happened.
ONE OLD ENDING TO MARK'S GOSPEL
Jesus Appears to Mary Magdalene
(Matthew 28.9,10; John 20.11-18)
9Very early on the first day of the week, after Jesus had risen to life, he appeared to Mary Magdalene. Earlier he had forced seven demons out of her. 10She left and told his friends, who were crying and mourning. 11Even though they heard that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him, they still would not believe it.
Jesus Appears to Two Disciples
(Luke 24.13-35)
12Later, Jesus appeared in another form to two disciples, as they were on their way out of the city. 13But when these disciples told what had happened, the others would not believe either.
What Jesus' Followers Must Do
(Matthew 28.16-20; Luke 24.36-49; John 20.19-23; Acts 1.6-8)
14Afterwards, Jesus appeared to his eleven disciples as they were eating. He scolded them because they were too stubborn to believe the ones who had seen him after he had been raised to life. 15 Then he told them:
Go and preach the good news to everyone in the world. 16Anyone who believes me and is baptized will be saved. But anyone who refuses to believe me will be condemned. 17Everyone who believes me will be able to do wonderful things. By using my name they will force out demons, and they will speak new languages. 18They will handle snakes and will drink poison and not be hurt. They will also heal sick people by placing their hands on them.
Jesus Returns to Heaven
(Luke 24.50-53; Acts 1.9-11)
19 After the Lord Jesus had said these things to the disciples, he was taken back up to heaven where he sat down at the right side of God. 20Then the disciples left and preached everywhere. The Lord was with them, and the miracles they worked proved that their message was true.
ANOTHER OLD ENDING TO MARK'S GOSPEL
21-22 The women quickly told Peter and his friends what had happened. Later, Jesus sent the disciples to the east and to the west with his sacred and everlasting message of how people can be saved forever.
translation_text:
Yesu kefoŇč lee yeŇč man
Mateo 28:1-8; Luka 24:1-12; Gy…Ēn 20:1-10
1Gyudatena kef…õ…õfowee keŇč ke taŇč n…õ, M…õ…õre Ňčon …Ē ba lee Magedala na Gyemisi …Ē naa M…õ…õre na Salome, l…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē ofoŇč nyiŇčan be kaa buraa Yesu woo Ňčon. 2Kosida nebo nebo weese ke b…õ…õ n…õ, ala baŇč korow…Ē bee gyu yeb…Ē…Ē keŇč t…õ…õ. 3Be kee gyu na b…õ…õ b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúAm…Ēte ne gyae la waa biliŇči boe keŇč lee yeb…Ē…Ē keŇč n…Ē…Ēman fa daa?‚ÄĚ 4M…Ē be ke gyina be sia k…õ…õ na, be naaw…Ē nyi be te biliŇči boe dinaa dinaa keŇč lee yeb…Ē…Ē keŇč n…Ē…Ēman. 5Be ka mo baa gyoo yeb…Ē…Ē keŇč man n…õ, te be naa tobaale Ňčolo na …Ē doona kegba wolom wurikyim na …Ē baŇč…õ…õ dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē te be ŇčeŇčaw…Ē. 6Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē yako ala baŇč nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź na ŇčeŇčii ya.
