KeŇčte feliŇč dinaa daale korow…Ē ne gy…Ē…Ē loŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē, mena d…Ē…Ē ke gy…Ē…Ē loŇč do degbele keŇč man te Yesu kaseela baŇč sia y…õ…õ w…Ē nideli. 24Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu kaseela baŇč gyuuw…Ē ke gyuusu e yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, de GbeŇčgyoo d…õ…õ gyae dee munu.‚ÄĚ Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu korow…Ē te …Ē fae feliŇč keŇč na loŇč keŇč pou te Ňče y…õ…õw…Ē yididi. 25KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse …Ē kaseela baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera d…Ē…Ē te …õ b…õ…õ l…õ…õ maŇč di ya?‚ÄĚ GyakoloŇč kyaŇča w…Ē te ba wose yeŇča w…Ē te be b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúWalaŇč woŇčti y…õna kei? ∆Ü ne kolosi fa feliŇč na loŇč koraŇč na Ňčee nyii e.‚ÄĚ
Yesu keky…Ē walaŇč Ňčon feliŇčkum…õ…õ doo …Ē man n…õ
Mateo 8:28-34; Maake 5:1-20
26KeŇčte Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč toŇčaw…Ē gyu Gerasenetena t…õ…õle man keŇč kyaa Galelia boo diŇčgyiŇč n…õ. 27Yesu ka betaa lee degbele keŇč man p…õ, te baale Ňčolo Ňčon feliŇčkum…õ…õ doo …Ē man n…õ leew…Ē don…Ē…Ē keŇč man k…ĒŇč kaa gyaŇčee na e. ∆Ü te t…õ…õ diteŇčteli kyare te …Ē b…õ…õ kyaa d…õ…õ ya. ∆Ü kyae amuseese man. 28DebaŇč keŇč …Ē be naa Yesu n…õ …Ē fae yeesa te …Ē leew…Ē yala …Ē naw…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ, na …Ē ne fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúYesu Wurubuar…õ Adidote Bu, woŇč te n ne gyae me gyaŇč? M…õ…õ waase neŇč bo, na naase maŇč diyem ya.‚ÄĚ 29Nawolo nyi na Yesu taŇč ke fae feliŇčkum keŇč nyi ke d…õ…õ lee …Ē man. DebaŇč kamas…õ na feliŇčkum keŇč ne naase e diyem, na ba te kyaŇč e bake e agbaraagbaraase …Ē nyiŇčmaase na …Ē naw…Ē…Ē man na balaŇč ne gyee e. KeŇč koraŇč na …Ē ne tirii Ňča, na feliŇčkum keŇč ta mo e gyu kefula man. 30KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN yele y…õna sena?‚ÄĚ Te balee Ňčon tiran…Ē…Ē nyi ma yele y…õna ‚ÄúDikpii‚ÄĚ ∆Ü tiran…Ē…Ē mena nawolo nyi feliŇčkum…õ…õ burum doo …Ē man. 31Bot…Ē…Ē te feliŇčkum…õ…õ Ňčan waase Yesu nyi, …Ē na gegi w…Ē ka do b…Ē…Ē keŇč betaŇč …Ēto ya n…õ man ya.
32DebaŇč kenaŇč na kekuiriise dikpii daale seŇč…õ…õ bula daale d…Ē…Ē bee dii. KeŇčte feliŇčkum…õ…õ Ňčan waase Yesu nyi waa yela be kaa gyoo kekuiriise baŇč man, te Yesu faa w…Ē gb…õ…õ. 33Mena d…Ē…Ē feliŇčkum…õ…õ Ňčan leew…Ē balee Ňčon man ka gyoo kekuiriise baŇč man, te ba dikpii keŇč pou yeuw…Ē tisi bula kyoloŇčkyoloŇč keŇč gyu ka gyoo loŇč keŇč man te be munuw…Ē pou.
34BalaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ k…õ…õ kekuiriise baŇč d…Ē…Ē kena kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ, ba korow…Ē yeu gyu ke yako kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ fa balaŇč baŇč be kyaa don…Ē…Ē keŇč man n…õ na akuraase Ňčan man pou. 35KeŇčte balaŇč yeuw…Ē gyu be kaa k…õ…õ kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ. Ba ke k…ĒŇč Yesu gyaŇč n…õ, be naa walaŇč Ňčon naf…Ē feliŇčkum…õ…õ doo …Ē man n…õ baŇč…õ…õ Yesu naw…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ, na …Ē wose te f…õ…õ e keŇč …Ē doona ab…Ē…Ē. Te gyakoloŇč gyoo balaŇč baŇč pou. 36BalaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē naa kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ yako baŇč b…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč n…õ mena keŇč Yesu be ky…Ē balee Ňčon kawee. 37KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč pou be kyaa Gerasene t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē n…õ waase Yesu nyi waa lee be gyaŇč nawolo nyi gyakoloŇč dinaa ta gyoo w…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu gyoow…Ē degbele keŇč man te …Ē laŇčaw…Ē. 38WalaŇč Ňčon Yesu be gegi feliŇčkum…õ…õ lee …Ē man n…õ waase Yesu nyi waa sila e. M…Ēna Yesu b…õ…õw…Ē te …Ē yako e nyiaa, 39‚ÄúBese gyu n d…õ…õ, na n kaa yako kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ be y…õ…õ fa neŇč n…õ fa balaŇč.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē balee Ňčon gyuuw…Ē don…Ē…Ē keŇč man ke yako balaŇč pou kpene keŇč Yesu be y…õ…õ fa e n…õ.
Yesu kegyuusu Gyariose bu alebu lee yeŇč man
Mateo 9:18-26; Maake 5:21-43
40Yesu ka bese lee boo keŇč diŇčgyiŇč k…ĒŇč n…õ, balaŇč burum k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa gyaŇčee na e. Nawolo nyi na be popou ne k…õ…õ …Ē gb…õ…õ. 41Bot…Ē…Ē te balee Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake nyi Gyariose, Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man kegyia e n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa buŇč Yesu naw…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ sola e nyi waa k…ĒŇč …Ē d…õ…õ. 42Nawolo nyi …Ē bu alebu dokoloŇč kooŇč Ňčon …Ē be di kulutooneŇč kufu ala n…õ ne gyae waa yeŇč. Yesu kee gyuu keŇč n…õ na balaŇč baŇč be silana e te y…õ…õ nsendee ya, mena d…Ē…Ē na …Ē bee nyiŇč gb…õ…õ gba waa besena …Ē wose ya.
43Na alo Ňčolo kpu…õ balaŇč burum baŇč be silana Yesu n…õ man keŇč fatabo ne gy…Ē e kulutooneŇč kufu ala yaa, m…Ēna Ňčolo te tale ky…Ē e ya. 44Alo Ňčon moow…Ē balaŇč baŇč man ka lee Yesu w…Ēle te …Ē tinaa …Ē kegba n…Ē…Ēbii. DebokenaŇč man te fatabo keŇč ne gy…Ē e n…õ karaw…Ē. 45Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu b…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAm…Ēte tinaa na maŇč?‚ÄĚ BalaŇč baŇč pou keb…õ…õ nyi be te tina e ya n…õ te Pita yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, balaŇč burum ke kilisi neŇč gyaŇčee te b…õ…õ t…õ…õse d…ĒŇča kei na, n ne b…Ē…Ēse nyi am…Ēte tinaa na neŇč?‚ÄĚ 46M…Ēna Yesu yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúŇäolo te tina maŇč. Nawolo nyi ma te naa nyi doŇč daale ta lee me man.‚ÄĚ 47Alo Ňčon kena nyi …Ē be gyae waa tale weese …Ē wose ya n…õ, …Ē moo gyakoloŇč na wose keseree kaa buŇč Yesu naw…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ. Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē yako kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē …Ē be tina Yesu kegba, na mena keŇč …Ē kawee keŇč be taŇč debokenaŇč man n…õ balaŇč baŇč pou siaman. 48KeŇčte Yesu yako alo Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe bu, n kel…õ…õ maŇč di d…Ē…Ē te n nyiŇča kawee keky…Ē. Ta wosef…õ…õreŇč man.‚ÄĚ
49Yesu ke gyakaa …Ē ne kolosi n…õ, walaŇč Ňčolo lee Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man kegyia Gyariose Ňčon d…õ…õ kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN bu alebu Ňčon ta yem. Na tekaa naase de GbeŇčgyoo Ňčon diyem bela ya.‚ÄĚ
50Yesu ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ …Ē yako Gyariose nyiaa, ‚ÄúNa yee ya, nyaŇč l…õ…õ maŇč di. N bu Ňčon waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč.‚ÄĚ
51Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Gyariose d…õ…õ n…õ, …Ē te yeli Ňčolo ke sila e gyoo deni keŇč man ya. Pita na Gy…Ēn na Gyemisi na bu Ňčon …Ē kya na …Ē naa baageŇč te …Ē moow…Ē sila …Ē wose. 52Na balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē yilaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ pou ne wii na bee di walaŇčsa. Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź na wii ya, alebu kei ta yeŇča ta ya, …Ē doo dosoroŇč bo.‚ÄĚ
53KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč pou boŇčo Yesu, nawolo nyi ba gyeŇč nyi bu Ňčon ta yem. 54KeŇčte Yesu kyaŇča alebu Ňčon nyiŇčmaa man te …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe bu! Koro seŇč.‚ÄĚ 55Bot…Ē…Ē te bu Ňčon woo besew…Ē k…ĒŇč kaa gyoo …Ē man te …Ē korow…Ē seŇč debokenaŇč man. KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyi baa gyae kolo fa bu Ňčon na waa di. 56GyakoloŇč gyoo bu Ňčon …Ē kya na …Ē naa, te Yesu t…Ē…Ē w…Ē nideli nyi be na yako walaŇč na walaŇč kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ ya.

english_text:
Instructions for the Twelve Apostles
(Matthew 10.5-15; Mark 6.7-13)
1Jesus called together his twelve apostles and gave them complete power over all demons and diseases. 2Then he sent them to tell about God's kingdom and to heal the sick. 3 He told them, ‚ÄúDon't take anything with you! Don't take a walking stick or a traveling bag or food or money or even a change of clothes. 4When you are welcomed into a home, stay there until you leave that town. 5 If people won't welcome you, leave the town and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.‚ÄĚ
6The apostles left and went from village to village, telling the good news and healing people everywhere.
Herod Is Worried
(Matthew 14.1-12; Mark 6.14-29)
7 Herod the ruler heard about all that was happening, and he was worried. Some people were saying John the Baptist had come back to life. 8Others were saying Elijah had come or one of the prophets from long ago had come back to life. 9But Herod said, ‚ÄúI had John's head cut off! Who is this I hear so much about?‚ÄĚ Herod was eager to meet Jesus.
Jesus Feeds Five Thousand
(Matthew 14.13-21; Mark 6.30-44; John 6.1-14)
10The apostles came back and told Jesus everything they had done. He then took them with him to the village of Bethsaida, where they could be alone. 11But a lot of people found out about this and followed him. Jesus welcomed them. He spoke about God's kingdom and healed everyone who was sick.
12Late in the afternoon the twelve apostles came to Jesus and said, ‚ÄúSend the crowd to the villages and farms around here. They need to find a place to stay and something to eat. There is nothing in this place. It's like a desert!‚ÄĚ
13Jesus answered, ‚ÄúYou give them something to eat.‚ÄĚ
But they replied, ‚ÄúWe have only five small loaves of bread and two fish. If we are going to feed all these people, we will have to go and buy food.‚ÄĚ 14There were about 5,000 men in the crowd.
Jesus said to his disciples, ‚ÄúTell the people to sit in groups of 50.‚ÄĚ 15They did this, and all the people sat down. 16Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and fish and handed them to his disciples to give to the people.
17Everyone ate all they wanted. What was left over filled twelve baskets.
Who Is Jesus?
(Matthew 16.13-19; Mark 8.27-29)
18When Jesus was alone praying, his disciples came to him, and he asked them, ‚ÄúWhat do people say about me?‚ÄĚ
19 They answered, ‚ÄúSome say you are John the Baptist or Elijah or a prophet from long ago who has come back to life.‚ÄĚ
20 Jesus then asked, ‚ÄúBut who do you say I am?‚ÄĚ
Peter answered, ‚ÄúYou are the Messiah sent from God.‚ÄĚ
21Jesus strictly warned his disciples not to tell anyone about this.
Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death
(Matthew 16.20-28; Mark 8.30‚ÄĒ9.1)
22Jesus told his disciples, ‚ÄúThe nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. They will reject him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.‚ÄĚ
23 Then Jesus said to all the people:
If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross every day and follow me. 24 If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will save it. 25What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself or waste your life? 26If you are ashamed of me and my message, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in his glory and in the glory of his Father and the holy angels. 27You can be sure some of the people standing here will not die before they see God's kingdom.
The True Glory of Jesus
(Matthew 17.1-8; Mark 9.2-8)
28 About eight days later Jesus took Peter, John, and James with him and went up on a mountain to pray. 29While he was praying, his face changed, and his clothes became shining white. 30Suddenly Moses and Elijah were there speaking with him. 31They appeared in heavenly glory and talked about all that Jesus' death in Jerusalem would mean.
32Peter and the other two disciples had been sound asleep. All at once they woke up and saw how glorious Jesus was. They also saw the two men who were with him.
33Moses and Elijah were about to leave, when Peter said to Jesus, ‚ÄúMaster, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.‚ÄĚ But Peter did not know what he was talking about.
34While Peter was still speaking, a shadow from a cloud passed over, and they were frightened as the cloud covered them. 35 From the cloud a voice spoke, ‚ÄúThis is my chosen Son. Listen to what he says!‚ÄĚ
36After the voice had spoken, Peter, John, and James saw only Jesus. For some time they kept quiet and did not say anything about what they had seen.
Jesus Heals a Boy
(Matthew 17.14-18; Mark 9.14-27)
37The next day Jesus and his three disciples came down from the mountain and were met by a large crowd. 38Just then someone in the crowd shouted, ‚ÄúTeacher, please do something for my son! He is my only child! 39A demon often attacks him and makes him scream. It shakes him until he foams at the mouth, and it won't leave him until it has completely worn the boy out. 40I begged your disciples to force out the demon, but they couldn't do it.‚ÄĚ
41Jesus said to them, ‚ÄúYou people are stubborn and don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you?‚ÄĚ
Then Jesus said to the man, ‚ÄúBring your son to me.‚ÄĚ 42While the boy was being brought, the demon attacked him and made him shake all over. Jesus ordered the demon to stop. Then he healed the boy and gave him back to his father. 43Everyone was amazed at God's great power.
Jesus Again Speaks about His Death
(Matthew 17.22,23; Mark 9.30-32)
While everyone was still amazed at what Jesus was doing, he said to his disciples, 44‚ÄúPay close attention to what I am telling you! The Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies.‚ÄĚ 45But the disciples did not know what he meant. The meaning was hidden from them. They could not understand it, and they were afraid to ask.
Who Is the Greatest?
(Matthew 18.1-5; Mark 9.33-37)
46 Jesus' disciples were arguing about which one of them was the greatest. 47Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he had a child stand there beside him. 48 Then he said to his disciples, ‚ÄúWhen you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me. Whichever one of you is the most humble is the greatest.‚ÄĚ
For or against Jesus
(Mark 9.38-40)
49John said, ‚ÄúMaster, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But we told him to stop, because he isn't one of us.‚ÄĚ
50‚ÄúDon't stop him!‚ÄĚ Jesus said. ‚ÄúAnyone who isn't against you is for you.‚ÄĚ
A Samaritan Village Refuses To Receive Jesus
51Not long before it was time for Jesus to be taken up to heaven, he made up his mind to go to Jerusalem. 52He sent some messengers on ahead to a Samaritan village to get things ready for him. 53But he was on his way to Jerusalem, so the people there refused to welcome him. 54 When the disciples James and John saw what was happening, they asked, ‚ÄúLord, do you want us to call down fire from heaven to destroy these people?‚ÄĚ
55But Jesus turned and corrected them for what they had said. 56Then they all went on to another village.
Three People Who Wanted To Be Followers
(Matthew 8.19-22)
57Along the way someone said to Jesus, ‚ÄúI'll follow you anywhere!‚ÄĚ
58Jesus said, ‚ÄúFoxes have dens, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man doesn't have a place to call his own.‚ÄĚ
59Jesus told someone else to come with him. But the man said, ‚ÄúLord, let me wait until I bury my father.‚ÄĚ
60Jesus answered, ‚ÄúLet the dead take care of the dead, while you go and tell about God's kingdom.‚ÄĚ
61 Then someone said to Jesus, ‚ÄúI want to follow you, Lord, but first let me go back and take care of things at home.‚ÄĚ
62Jesus answered, ‚ÄúAnyone who starts plowing and keeps looking back isn't worth a thing to God's kingdom!‚ÄĚ

Yesu kekpila …Ē kaseela kufu bala baŇč

Gyoo dinaa Herode ne gyae waa naa Yesu
Mateo 14:1-2; Maake 6:14-16
7Galeliatena Gyoo dinaa Herode ke nyii ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou Yesu be y…õ…õ n…õ Ňče haa e …Ē kon…Ē…Ē man, nawolo nyi balaŇč baale ne yako nyi, ‚ÄúGy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre Ňčon …Ē ba yem n…õ foŇčala lee yeŇč man. 8Baale m…Ē kpa Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Elia b…õ…õ la lee, te baale m…Ē kpa Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra ben…õ…õ baŇč maŇč Ňčolo foŇčala lee yeŇč man.‚ÄĚ 9Bot…Ē…Ē te gyoo Herode yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMa te yela ba te kara Gy…Ēn nyee, te walaŇč woŇčti y…õna Ňčon mee nyii …Ē wose man gyakoloŇč nombia k…õŇča n…õ?‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yase …Ē sia gyae gb…õ…õ keŇč waa mo naa Yesu.
Yesu kefa balaŇč kakpoŇčse banoŇč ween…õ…õ
Mateo 14:13-21; Maake 6:30-44; Gy…Ēn 6:1-14
10DebaŇč keŇč kpilala kufu bala baŇč ba bese k…ĒŇč n…õ, be yako Yesu kpene kamas…õ keŇč b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ n…õ. KeŇčte Yesu baake w…Ē te Ňčon na w…Ē gyuu don…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi B…õtesaida n…õ man. 11BalaŇč dikpii keŇč kenyii nyi …Ē te lam bot…Ē…Ē n…õ be sila …Ē gb…õ…õ. Be ke gyu kena n…õ …Ē l…õ…õ w…Ē atuu te …Ē kolosi Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč wose man nombia fa w…Ē, te …Ē ky…Ē…Ē baŇč be hia kawee keky…Ē n…õ. 12Ke lii balin…Ē…Ē weese n…õ, Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŇč k…ĒŇčaw…Ē …Ē gyaŇč kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúYela balaŇč baŇč keta na baa gyu akuraase na don…Ē…Ēse Ňčan benaa n…õ man, na be kaa gyae ween…õ…õ na adoise, nawolo nyi k…õbo kei y…õ…õ Ňčale nsana keŇč kolo be kyaa ya.‚ÄĚ 13M…Ēna Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ fa w…Ē kolo baa di.‚ÄĚ Bot…Ē…Ē te be tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúŇäan pou de dana y…õna bodobodose abiliŇč anoŇč na kpebia bala. Mateo 16:20-28; Maake 8:30‚Äď9:1
22KeŇčte …Ē besew…Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKaboena nyi maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu maa naa diyem nideli. Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč baa b…õ…õ maŇč. Baa ko maŇč, te weeya atoorote keŇč man na, Wurubuar…õ waa gyuusu maŇč lee yeŇč man.‚ÄĚ

Ne y…õ…õ doŇč p…õte k…õ…õ koro …Ē d…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna ke gyakaa k…õ…õ w…Ēl…õ…õ e. 40Me waase n kaseela baŇč nyi baa gegi feliŇčkum keŇč, m…Ēna ba te tale ya.‚ÄĚ
41Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAo! ∆ź m…õ…õ gy…õŇč balaŇč baŇč …õ b…õ…õ l…õ…õ maŇč di ya te …õ gb…õ…õneŇč ba ten…õ…õ ya. Weeya aŇčmoŇč te kaboena nyi maa kyaa …õ gyaŇč na maa nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē fa Ňčon?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte …Ē yako balee Ňčon nyi, ‚ÄúMo n bu Ňčon k…Ēna maŇč.‚ÄĚ

Mateo 18:1-5; Maake 9:33-40
46KeŇčte aŇčmaareŇč gyoo Yesu kaseela baŇč man nyi be man woŇčti kela na …Ē d…Ē…Ē. 47Na Yesu gyeŇč kpene keŇč b…õ…õ gyueŇč n…õ te …Ē kyaŇča biikyaabii Ňčolo kaa seŇč …Ē yenaŇč, 48keŇčte …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWalaŇč Ňčon …Ē l…õ…õ biikyaabii kei ma yele man na …Ē te l…õ…õ maŇč, te Ňčon …Ē l…õ…õ maŇč na …Ē te l…õ…õ Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ. Nawolo nyi walaŇč Ňčon …õ b…õ…õ mo e y…õ…õ kolo …õ man ya n…õ, Ňčon kela na …õ popou.‚ÄĚ
49Bot…Ē…Ē te Gy…Ēn yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, de naa balee Ňčolo na …Ē ne gegi feliŇčkum…õ…õ lee n yele man te de t…Ē…Ē e, nawolo nyi …Ē be kpu…õ de man ya.‚ÄĚ 50KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź na t…Ē e gb…õ…õ ya, nawolo nyi Ňčon …Ē bee yoo na Ňčon ya n…õ, na …Ē seŇč…õ…õ …õ w…Ēle.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
The Work of the Seventy-Two Followers
1Later the Lord chose 72 other followers and sent them out two by two to every town and village where he was about to go. 2 He said to them:
A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in. 3 Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. 4 Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road. 5As soon as you enter a home, say, ‚ÄúGod bless this home with peace.‚ÄĚ 6If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you. 7 Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
8If the people of a town welcome you, eat whatever they offer. 9Heal their sick and say, ‚ÄúGod's kingdom will soon be here!‚ÄĚ
10 But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say, 11‚ÄúWe are shaking the dust from our feet as a warning to you. And you can be sure that God's kingdom will soon be here!‚ÄĚ 12 I tell you that on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than the people of that town!
The Unbelieving Towns
(Matthew 11.20-24)
13 You people of Chorazin are in for trouble! You people of Bethsaida are also in for trouble! If the miracles that took place in your towns had happened in Tyre and Sidon, the people there would have turned to God long ago. They would have dressed in sackcloth and put ashes on their heads. 14On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will. 15 People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
16 My followers, whoever listens to you is listening to me. Anyone who says ‚ÄúNo‚ÄĚ to you is saying ‚ÄúNo‚ÄĚ to me. And anyone who says ‚ÄúNo‚ÄĚ to me is really saying ‚ÄúNo‚ÄĚ to the one who sent me.
The Return of the Seventy-Two
17When the 72 followers returned, they were excited and said, ‚ÄúLord, even the demons obeyed when we spoke in your name!‚ÄĚ
18Jesus told them:
I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning. 19 I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you. 20But don't be happy because evil spirits obey you. Be happy that your names are written in heaven!
Jesus Thanks His Father
(Matthew 11.25-27; 13.16,17)
21At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said:
My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
22 My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
23Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, ‚ÄúYou are really blessed to see what you see! 24Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.‚ÄĚ
The Good Samaritan
25 An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. ‚ÄúTeacher,‚ÄĚ he asked, ‚Äúwhat must I do to have eternal life?‚ÄĚ
26Jesus answered, ‚ÄúWhat is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?‚ÄĚ
27 The man replied, ‚ÄúThe Scriptures say, ‚ÄėLove the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.‚Äô They also say, ‚ÄėLove your neighbors as much as you love yourself.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
28 Jesus said, ‚ÄúYou have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.‚ÄĚ
29But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWho are my neighbors?‚ÄĚ
30Jesus replied:
As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
31A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side. 32Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
33 A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him 34and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him. 35The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, ‚ÄúPlease take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.‚ÄĚ
36Then Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWhich one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?‚ÄĚ
37The expert in the Law of Moses answered, ‚ÄúThe one who showed pity.‚ÄĚ
Jesus said, ‚ÄúGo and do the same!‚ÄĚ
Martha and Mary
38 The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home. 39She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said. 40Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, ‚ÄúLord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!‚ÄĚ
41The Lord answered, ‚ÄúMartha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things, 42but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Prayer
(Matthew 6.9-13; 7.7-11)
1When Jesus had finished praying, one of his disciples said to him, ‚ÄúLord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his followers to pray.‚ÄĚ
2So Jesus told them, ‚ÄúPray in this way:
‚ÄėFather, help us
to honor your name.
Come and set up
your kingdom.
3Give us each day
the food we need.
4Forgive our sins,
as we forgive everyone
who has done wrong to us.
And keep us
from being tempted.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
5Then Jesus went on to say:
Suppose one of you goes to a friend in the middle of the night and says, ‚ÄúLet me borrow three loaves of bread. 6A friend of mine has dropped in, and I don't have a thing for him to eat.‚ÄĚ 7And suppose your friend answers, ‚ÄúDon't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.‚ÄĚ
8He may not get up and give you the bread, just because you are his friend. But he will get up and give you as much as you need, simply because you are not ashamed to keep on asking.
9So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you. 10Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks. 11Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish? 12Which one of you would give your child a scorpion if the child asked for an egg? 13As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks.
Jesus and the Ruler of Demons
(Matthew 12.22-30; Mark 3.20-27)
14Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed. 15 But some people said, ‚ÄúHe forces out demons by the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons!‚ÄĚ
16 Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God. 17Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said:
A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up. 18If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul. 19If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you. 20But if I use God's power to force out demons, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
21When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe. 22But if a stronger man comes and defeats him, he will carry off the weapons in which the strong man trusted. Then he will divide with others what he has taken. 23 If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the crop with me, you scatter it.
Return of an Evil Spirit
(Matthew 12.43-45)
24When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, ‚ÄúI will go back to the home I left.‚ÄĚ 25When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up, 26it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before.
Being Really Blessed
27While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, ‚ÄúThe woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!‚ÄĚ
28Jesus replied, ‚ÄúThat's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!‚ÄĚ
A Sign from God
(Matthew 12.38-42; Mark 8.12)
29 As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said:
You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given. 30 Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today. 31 When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon. 32 The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
Light
(Matthew 5.15; 6.22,23)
33 No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light. 34Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. 35So be sure your light isn't darkness. 36If you have light, and nothing is dark, then light will be everywhere, as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
Jesus Condemns the Pharisees and Teachers of the Law of Moses
(Matthew 23.1-36; Mark 12.38-40; Luke 20.45-47)
37When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat. 38The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating. 39So the Lord said to him:
You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil. 40You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside? 41If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
42 You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
43You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market. 44But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
45A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, ‚ÄúTeacher, you said cruel things about us.‚ÄĚ
46Jesus replied:
You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads. 47Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago. 48You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
49Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, ‚ÄúI will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.‚ÄĚ 50You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world. 51 This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
52You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
53Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things, 54so they could catch him saying something wrong.

english_text:
Warnings
1 As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples:
Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people. 2 Everything that is hidden will be found out, and every secret will be known. 3Whatever you say in the dark will be heard when it is day. Whatever you whisper in a closed room will be shouted from the housetops.
The One To Fear
(Matthew 10.28-31)
4 My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do. 5God is the one you must fear. Not only can he take your life, but he can throw you into hell. God is certainly the one you should fear!
6Five sparrows are sold for only a few cents, but God doesn't forget a single one of them. 7Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows.
Telling Others about Christ
(Matthew 10.32,33; 12.32; 10.19,20)
8If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers. 9But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them. 10 If you speak against the Son of Man, you can be forgiven, but if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you cannot be forgiven.
11 When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say. 12At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say.
A Rich Fool
13A man in a crowd said to Jesus, ‚ÄúTeacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.‚ÄĚ
14Jesus answered, ‚ÄúWho gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?‚ÄĚ
15Then he said to the crowd, ‚ÄúDon't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.‚ÄĚ
16So Jesus told them this story:
A rich man's farm produced a big crop, 17and he said to himself, ‚ÄúWhat can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.‚ÄĚ
18Later, he said, ‚ÄúNow I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods. 19 Then I'll say to myself, ‚ÄėYou have stored up enough good things to last for years to come. Live it up! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
20But God said to him, ‚ÄúYou fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?‚ÄĚ
21‚ÄúThis is what happens to people who store up everything for themselves, but are poor in the sight of God.‚ÄĚ
Worry
(Matthew 6.25-34)
22Jesus said to his disciples:
I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear. 23Life is more than food or clothing. 24Look at the crows! They don't plant or harvest, and they don't have storehouses or barns. But God takes care of them. You are much more important than any birds. 25Can worry make you live longer? 26If you don't have power over small things, why worry about everything else?
27 Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers. 28God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
29Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink. 30Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need. 31But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well.
Treasures in Heaven
(Matthew 6.19-21)
32My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom. 33Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it. 34Your heart will always be where your treasure is.
Faithful and Unfaithful Servants
(Matthew 24.45-51)
35 Be ready and keep your lamps burning 36 just like those servants who wait up for their master to return from a wedding feast. As soon as he comes and knocks, they open the door for him. 37Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them. 38Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning. 39 You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming. 40So always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come.
41Peter asked Jesus, ‚ÄúDid you say this just for us or for everyone?‚ÄĚ
42The Lord answered:
Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time? 43Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job. 44A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
45But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk. 46If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
47If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard. 48But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better.
Not Peace, but Trouble
(Matthew 10.34-36)
49I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire! 50 I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over. 51Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides. 52A family of five will be divided, with two of them against the other three. 53 Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other.
Knowing What To Do
(Matthew 16.2,3; 5.25,26)
54Jesus said to all the people:
As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, ‚ÄúIt's going to rain,‚ÄĚ and it does. 55When the south wind blows, you say, ‚ÄúIt's going to get hot,‚ÄĚ and it does. 56Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now. 57Why don't you understand the right thing to do? 58When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up. 59You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.

english_text:
Turn Back to God
1About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices. 2Jesus replied:
Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them? 3Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed. 4What about those 18 people who died when the tower in Siloam fell on them? Do you think they were worse than everyone else in Jerusalem? 5Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die.
A Story about a Fig Tree
6Jesus then told them this story:
A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any. 7So he said to the gardener, ‚ÄúFor three years I have come looking for figs on this tree, and I haven't found any yet. Chop it down! Why should it take up space?‚ÄĚ
8The gardener answered, ‚ÄúMaster, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow. 9Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.‚ÄĚ
Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
10One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue, 11and a woman was there who had been crippled by an evil spirit for 18 years. She was completely bent over and could not straighten up. 12When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, ‚ÄúYou are now well.‚ÄĚ 13He placed his hands on her, and at once she stood up straight and praised God.
14 The man in charge of the synagogue was angry because Jesus had healed someone on the Sabbath. So he said to the people, ‚ÄúEach week has six days when we can work. Come and be healed on one of those days, but not on the Sabbath.‚ÄĚ
15The Lord replied, ‚ÄúAre you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath? 16This woman belongs to the family of Abraham, but Satan has kept her bound for 18 years. Isn't it right to set her free on the Sabbath?‚ÄĚ 17Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing.
A Mustard Seed and Yeast
(Matthew 13.31-33; Mark 4.30-32)
18Jesus said, ‚ÄúWhat is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with? 19It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.‚ÄĚ
20Then Jesus said, ‚ÄúWhat can I compare God's kingdom with? 21It is like what happens when a woman mixes yeast into three batches of flour. Finally, all the dough rises.‚ÄĚ
The Narrow Door
(Matthew 7.13,14,21-23)
22As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages. 23Someone asked him, ‚ÄúLord, are only a few people going to be saved?‚ÄĚ
Jesus answered:
24Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to. 25Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, ‚ÄúSir, open the door for us!‚ÄĚ
But the owner will answer, ‚ÄúI don't know a thing about you!‚ÄĚ
26Then you will start saying, ‚ÄúWe dined with you, and you taught in our streets.‚ÄĚ
27 But he will say, ‚ÄúI really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!‚ÄĚ
28 Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom. 29People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom. 30 There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important.
Jesus and Herod
31At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, ‚ÄúYou had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!‚ÄĚ
32Jesus said to them:
Go tell that fox, ‚ÄúI am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.‚ÄĚ 33But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed.
Jesus Loves Jerusalem
(Matthew 23.37-39)
34 Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me. 35 Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say,
‚ÄúBlessed is the one who comes
in the name of the Lord.‚ÄĚ

Mateo 7:13-14, 21-23

english_text:
Jesus Heals a Sick Man
1One Sabbath, Jesus was having dinner in the home of an important Pharisee, and everyone was carefully watching Jesus. 2All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him. 3Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, ‚ÄúIs it right to heal on the Sabbath?‚ÄĚ 4But they did not say a word.
Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away. 5 Afterwards, Jesus asked the people, ‚ÄúIf your son or ox falls into a well, wouldn't you pull him out at once, even on the Sabbath?‚ÄĚ 6There was nothing they could say.
How To Be a Guest
7Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
8 When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited. 9Then the one who invited you will come and say, ‚ÄúGive your place to this other guest!‚ÄĚ You will be embarrassed and will have to sit in the worst place.
10When you are invited to be a guest, go and sit in the worst place. Then the one who invited you may come and say, ‚ÄúMy friend, take a better seat!‚ÄĚ You will then be honored in front of all the other guests. 11 If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
12Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him:
When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back. 13When you give a feast, invite the poor, the paralyzed, the lame, and the blind. 14They cannot pay you back. But God will bless you and reward you when his people rise from death.
The Great Banquet
(Matthew 22.1-10)
15After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, ‚ÄúThe greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!‚ÄĚ
16Jesus told him:
A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests. 17When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, ‚ÄúEverything is ready! Please come.‚ÄĚ
18One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, ‚ÄúI bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.‚ÄĚ
19Another guest said, ‚ÄúI bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.‚ÄĚ
20Still another guest said, ‚ÄúI've just now married, and I can't be there.‚ÄĚ
21The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, ‚ÄúGo as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.‚ÄĚ
22When the servant returned, he said, ‚ÄúMaster, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.‚ÄĚ
23His master then told him, ‚ÄúGo out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full. 24Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!‚ÄĚ
Being a Disciple
(Matthew 10.37,38)
25Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
26 You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
27 You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
28Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it? 29Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you. 30They will say, ‚ÄúYou started building, but could not finish the job.‚ÄĚ
31What will a king do if he has only 10,000 soldiers to defend himself against a king who is about to attack him with 20,000 soldiers? Before he goes out to battle, won't he first sit down and decide if he can win? 32If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off. 33So then, you cannot be my disciple unless you give away everything you own.
Salt and Light
(Matthew 5.13; Mark 9.50)
34Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again? 35It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!

25Wee daale balaŇč burum baale kesila Yesu bee gyuu n…õ, …Ē besena …Ē sia tee w…Ē te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, 26‚ÄúAkpaa Ňčolo ne k…ĒŇč me gyaŇč …Ē kaa sila maŇč te …Ē te gyae maŇč kela …Ē kya na …Ē naa na …Ē w…õ…õle na …Ē bia na …Ē kegyiise na …Ē naabia na …Ē deesina na Ňčon gbagba …Ē wose koraŇč ya n…õ, …Ē be gyae waa tale bese me kaseele ya. 27Te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ta mo …Ē …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč ka seŇč…õ…õ fa kaale na yeŇč na waa sila maŇč ya n…õ, …Ē be gyae waa tale bese me kaseele ya.
28Akpaa …õ man Ňčolo ne gyae waa baŇč deni dinaa na, …Ē b…õ…õ kyaa na waa bu deni keŇč ab…Ē…Ē koya k…õ…õ nyi k…Ēba keŇč …Ē dana n…õ gyae ka yee deni kenaŇč kebaŇč yee? 29Nawolo nyi akpaa …Ē see deni keŇč mok…Ē…Ē te …Ē te tale taŇč ke ya na, baŇč pou baa naa ke n…õ baa boŇčo e, 30nyiaa ‚ÄėBalee kei korow…Ē nyi …Ē ne baŇč deni m…Ēna …Ē nyiŇčmaa te gyaŇčee ya.‚Äô
31KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē yako nyiaa, akpaa gyoo Ňčolo dana yoon…Ē…Ētena kakpoŇčse kufu (10,000) te …Ē ne gyu …Ē kaa yoo na …Ē d…Ē…Ē gyoo Ňčon …Ē dana yoon…Ē…Ētena kakpoŇčse sao (20,000) na, …Ē be gyae waa kyaa na waa gyueŇč k…õ…õ nyi waa tale mo …Ē wui yoon…Ē…Ētena kakpoŇčse kufu (10,000) baŇč ke yoo na …Ē d…Ē…Ē Ňčon wui yoon…Ē…Ētena kakpoŇčse sao (20,000 ) baŇč yee? 32Nyi akpaa …Ē k…õ…õ nyi …Ē be tale yoo na e ya n…õ, debaŇč keŇč …Ē ta benaa ta e ya na waa kpila teree gyu ke doo e nyi waa yela baa desina ba nsana na baa kyaa wosef…õ…õreŇč man. 33Mena m…Ē te …õ man walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē be gyae waa tale tina kpene kamas…õ keŇč …Ē dana yela ya n…õ, …Ē be gyae waa tale bese me kaseele ya.‚ÄĚ
34KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúYabia dei m…Ēna akpaa ka gyoŇč keŇč da lee ke man n…õ, n be gyae n ke tale yela ka ke y…õ…õ gyoŇč bela ya. 35De k…ĒŇč mena na, yabia kenaŇč be dei fa kolo na kolo key…õ…õ bela ya, bee fuŇč ke bo leki. WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē dana deŇčela anenyiiŇč dei na, waa tei nombia Ňčan m…õ…õ yako n…õ.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
One Sheep
(Matthew 18.12-14)
1 Tax collectors and sinners were all crowding around to listen to Jesus. 2So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, ‚ÄúThis man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.‚ÄĚ
3Then Jesus told them this story:
4If any of you has 100 sheep, and one of them gets lost, what will you do? Won't you leave the 99 in the field and go look for the lost sheep until you find it? 5And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder 6and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, ‚ÄúLet's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.‚ÄĚ
7Jesus said, ‚ÄúIn the same way there is more happiness in heaven because of one sinner who turns to God than over 99 good people who don't need to.‚ÄĚ
One Coin
8Jesus told the people another story:
What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it? 9Then she will call in her friends and neighbors and say, ‚ÄúLet's celebrate! I've found the coin I lost.‚ÄĚ
10Jesus said, ‚ÄúIn the same way God's angels are happy when even one person turns to him.‚ÄĚ
Two Sons
11Jesus told them yet another story:
Once a man had two sons. 12The younger son said to his father, ‚ÄúGive me my share of the property.‚ÄĚ So the father divided his property between his two sons.
13Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living. 14He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
15He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs. 16He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
17Finally, he came to his senses and said, ‚ÄúMy father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death! 18I will go to my father and say to him, ‚ÄėFather, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. 19I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
20The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
21The son said, ‚ÄúFather, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.‚ÄĚ
22But his father said to the servants, ‚ÄúHurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet. 23Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate. 24This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.‚ÄĚ And they began to celebrate.
25The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing. 26So he called one of the servants over and asked, ‚ÄúWhat's going on here?‚ÄĚ
27The servant answered, ‚ÄúYour brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.‚ÄĚ 28The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house.
His father came out and begged him to go in. 29But he said to his father, ‚ÄúFor years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends. 30This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.‚ÄĚ
31His father replied, ‚ÄúMy son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours. 32But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.‚ÄĚ
translation_text:
Nam Ňčon …Ē ba yoo n…õ dudu
Mateo 18:12-14
1DebaŇč daale n…õ, lempool…õ…õra burum na nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra k…ĒŇčaw…Ē Yesu gyaŇč be kaa nyii nombia Ňčan …Ē na wolo n…õ. 2KeŇčte Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala Ňčorokosow…Ē yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee kei d…Ē…Ē …Ē ne l…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra do …Ē wose man te …Ē ne dii bom na w…Ē gba koraŇč.‚ÄĚ
3Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu gy…Ē…Ē dudu kei fa w…Ē nyiaa, 4‚ÄúAkpaa …õ man Ňčolo dana namense s…ĒŇčonoŇč (100) te be man Ňčolo da yoo na woŇč te …Ē ne y…õ…õ?‚ÄĚ ∆Ü be gyae waa tina s…ĒŇčonaara na kufu kadaale (99) baŇč yela Ňčale nsana bot…Ē…Ē na waa ta k…õo Ňčon …Ē ba yoo n…õ kaboena waa naa e yee? 5Te akpaa …Ē naa e na, …Ē be gyae waa nyaŇčee na waa gate e gyakaa …Ē degbaŇčgbaŇč d…Ē…Ē mo e gyu d…õ…õ yee? 6∆Ü gyu d…õ…õ n…õ waa baake …Ē gyoona na …Ē tebia gyaŇčee na waa yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź yela d…õ…õ nyaŇčee nawolo nyi ma te naa me nam Ňčon …Ē ba yoo n…õ. 7M…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, mena ke te akpaa nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õre dokoloŇč de ky…õ…õkee lee …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man te …Ē k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč n…õ, baŇč be kyaa Wurubuar…õ man adido n…õ baa nyaŇčee …Ē d…Ē…Ē kela balaŇč s…ĒŇčonaara na kufu kadaale baŇč b…õ…õ gyueŇč nyi be gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman, mena d…Ē…Ē tekaboena nyi baa ky…õ…õkee lee be gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man ya n…õ.‚ÄĚ
K…Ēbabii keŇč k…Ē…Ē yoo n…õ dudu
8Yesu besew…Ē yako nyiaa, akpaa alo Ňčolo dana k…Ēba wolom bia kufu te Ňče man dokoloŇč da yoo na woŇč te …Ē ne y…õ…õ? ∆Ü be gyae waa kyaŇčee kanea na waa gbee …Ē deni man na waa k…õo yenaŇč kamas…õ nideli kaboena waa naa ke yee? 9Te akpaa …Ē naa ke na, …Ē ne baake …Ē gyoona na …Ē tebia gyaŇčee na waa yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź yela d…õ…õ nyaŇčee nawolo nyi ma te naa me k…Ēba wolom bii keŇč k…Ē…Ē yoo n…õ. 10M…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, mena ke te akpaa nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õre dokoloŇč de ky…õ…õkee lee …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man te …Ē k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč n…õ, Wurubuar…õ kpilala baŇč be kyaa adido n…õ baa nyaŇčee …Ē d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kegy…Ē dudu lee bu Ňčon …Ē ba yoo n…õ wose man
11Yesu besew…Ē gy…Ē dudu kei fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee Ňčolo kyaaw…Ē na …Ē dana bia baala bala. 12Wee daale balee kei bu kemaasa Ňčon yako …Ē kya nyiaa, ‚ÄėTaata, kpala nya ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan n dana n…õ na n ka mo me wui fa maŇč.‚Äô Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē kya Ňčon kpala wa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan fa …Ē bia baŇč.
13Te di weeya ala ya te …Ē bu kemaasa Ňčon y…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan …Ē be nyim n…õ pou te …Ē moo k…Ēba keŇč do …Ē wose man te …Ē moo …Ē nyee baŇč t…õ…õle w…Ēle daale man. ∆Ü ke gyu bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ē moo k…Ēba keŇč …Ē dana n…õ ke y…õ…õ kehuhoori w…Ēl…õ…õ ke pou. 14∆Ü ke w…Ēl…õ…õ k…Ēba keŇč pou taŇč n…õ, tanam dinaa daale yalaw…Ē t…õ…õle kenaŇč pou d…Ē…Ē te …Ē wose kyaŇča e nideli. 15Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē gyuuw…Ē …Ē kaa di paa fa don…Ē…Ēte Ňčolo, te don…Ē…Ēte Ňčon moo e gyu nyi waa gyu ke k…õ…õ …Ē kekuiriise d…Ē…Ē. 16Na naf…Ē …Ē loo gyakaa nyi waa nyiŇč kekuiriise baŇč ween…õ…õ Ňčan Ňčaale di, m…Ēna Ňčolo te fa e Ňča ya.
17∆Ü sia ke ŇčmaŇčee e n…õ …Ē yako …Ē wose nyiaa, ‚ÄėMe kya tewul…õ…õ pou ne dii baa kaase, te maŇč kyaa k…õbo keŇč tanam ne gyae ke kara ma nyee?‚Äô 18Maa koro na maa bese gyu me kya gyaŇč na me kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄėTaata, ma te y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ tia Wurubuar…õ te ma te y…õ…õ tia nyaŇč m…Ē. 19Mena d…Ē…Ē ma tekaboena nyi nya baake maŇč n bu bela ya, mo maŇč y…õ…õ Ňčgba n tewul…õ…õ baŇč man Ňčolo n…õ.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 20Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē korow…Ē mo gb…õ…õ …Ē ne gyu …Ē kya gyaŇč. DebaŇč keŇč …Ē d…ĒŇč…õ…õ lemlem …Ē ne k…ĒŇč d…õ…õ n…õ, …Ē kya naa e te …Ē bu Ňčon nombia y…õ…õ e waraŇčase te …Ē yeuw…Ē kegyaŇčee na e te …Ē pukaa e nyaŇčee na e.
21Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē bu Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúTaata, ma te y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ tia Wurubuar…õ te ma te y…õ…õ tia nyaŇč m…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē ma tekaboena nyi nya baake maŇč n bu bela ya. Mo maŇč y…õ…õ Ňčgba n tewul…õ…õ baŇč man Ňčolo n…õ.‚ÄĚ
22KeŇčte …Ē kya Ňčon baake …Ē tewul…õ…õ baŇč yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź kaŇč …õ wose na …õ ka lese kegba d…Ē…ĒloŇč kpaakpaate keŇč k…ĒŇč kaa do e. ∆ź do e k…Ēlabia …Ē nyiŇčmaa man na …õ ka do e nkyokotase. 23Na …õ ke gyu ka mo nunkyue baale dinaa Ňčon k…ĒŇč kaa ko na dee di de n…Ē…Ē. 24Nawolo nyi y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba me bu kei yeŇčae te …Ē besew…Ē foŇč n…õ. Naf…Ē na …Ē ta yoo te n…õn…õ…õ ma te naa e bela.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte be fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ nyaŇčee na bee di be n…Ē…Ē.
25DebaŇč keŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ k…õŇča pou n…õ, na …Ē bu kegyia Ňčon kyaa w…Ē…Ēman. ∆Ü ka lee w…Ē…Ē …Ē ne k…ĒŇč te …Ē ba benaa d…õ…õ n…õ, …Ē nyii n…Ē…ĒneŇč na suya ne tao kela. 26KeŇčte …Ē baake …Ē kya tewul…õ…õ baŇč man Ňčolo b…Ē…Ēse e kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč dekpaŇčalaŇč man. 27Te …Ē tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN naabu Ňčon ta bese k…ĒŇč don…Ē…Ē te n kya koo nunkyue baale dinaa Ňčon, nawolo nyi …Ē ta bese k…ĒŇč d…õ…õ na nyeedoŇč.‚ÄĚ
28Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē bu kegyia Ňčon gyeŇča baŇč b…õ…õ nyi …Ē ba gyoo dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man bela ya. ∆Ü kya ke nyii mena na te …Ē leew…Ē k…ĒŇč kaa waase e nyi waa gyoo d…õ…õ. 29KeŇčte …Ē yako …Ē kya nyiaa, ‚ÄúTaata nyaŇč k…õ…õ, kulutooneŇč k…õŇča pou me kyaa m…õ…õ som neŇč Ňčgba gbeŇči n…õ, te ma te y…õ…õ deŇčele doŇč n d…Ē…Ē ya. WoŇč te n faa maŇč? N te faa ta maŇč asoobii paŇčpaŇč gba na daa na me gyoona m…Ē kedi de n…Ē…Ē ya. 30M…Ēna n bu kei Ňčon …Ē ba mo nya ab…Ē…Ē pou ke w…Ēl…õ…õ futooli ala d…Ē…Ē te …Ē ba bese k…ĒŇč don…Ē…Ē nyiŇčmaase wolo n…õ te n koo nunkyue baale dinaa Ňčon fa.‚ÄĚ 31KeŇčte …Ē kya Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe bu, debaŇč kamas…õ n kyae me gyaŇč te kpene kamas…õ keŇč me dana n…õ y…õ…õ n wui. 32M…Ēna kaboena nyi dee di de n…Ē…Ē na d…õ…õ nyaŇčee. Nawolo nyi y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba n naabu kei yeŇčae te …Ē besew…Ē foŇč n…õ, naf…Ē na …Ē ta yoo te n…õn…õ…õ da te naa e.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
A Dishonest Manager
1Jesus said to his disciples:
A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money. 2So the rich man called him in and said, ‚ÄúWhat is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.‚ÄĚ
3The manager said to himself, ‚ÄúWhat shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg. 4I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.‚ÄĚ
5Then one by one he called in the people who were in debt to his master. He asked the first one, ‚ÄúHow much do you owe my master?‚ÄĚ
6‚ÄúA hundred barrels of olive oil,‚ÄĚ the man answered.
So the manager said, ‚ÄúTake your bill and sit down and quickly write ‚Äė50.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
7The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, ‚ÄúHow much do you owe?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúA thousand sacks of wheat,‚ÄĚ the man replied.
The manager said, ‚ÄúTake your bill and write ‚Äė800.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
8The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
9 My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home. 10Anyone who can be trusted in little matters can also be trusted in important matters. But anyone who is dishonest in little matters will be dishonest in important matters. 11If you cannot be trusted with this wicked wealth, who will trust you with true wealth? 12And if you cannot be trusted with what belongs to someone else, who will give you something that will be your own? 13 You cannot be the slave of two masters. You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than to the other. You cannot serve God and money.
Some Sayings of Jesus
(Matthew 11.12,13; 5.31,32; Mark 10.11,12)
14The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him. 15But Jesus told them:
You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
16 Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in. 17 Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
18 It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman.
Lazarus and the Rich Man
19There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food. 20But a poor beggar named Lazarus was brought to the gate of the rich man's house. 21He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them. 22 The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham.
The rich man also died and was buried. 23 He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side, 24he said to Abraham, ‚ÄúHave pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.‚ÄĚ
25Abraham answered, ‚ÄúMy friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain. 26And besides, there is a deep ditch between us, and no one from either side can cross over.‚ÄĚ
27But the rich man said, ‚ÄúAbraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home. 28Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.‚ÄĚ
29Abraham answered, ‚ÄúYour brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.‚ÄĚ
30Then the rich man said, ‚ÄúNo, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.‚ÄĚ
31So Abraham said, ‚ÄúIf they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.‚ÄĚ
translation_text:
Tomy…õ…õre kegyia Ňčolo wose man dudu
1KeŇčte Yesu gy…Ē…Ē dudu fa …Ē kaseela baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúK…Ēbate Ňčolo kyaaw…Ē keŇč …Ē dana tobaale Ňčon …Ē ne k…õ…õ wa ab…Ē…Ē pou d…Ē…Ē, te be yako balee Ňčon nyi …Ē tobaale Ňčon ne w…Ēl…õ…õ …Ē k…Ēba. 2Mena d…Ē…Ē balee Ňčon baake …Ē tobaale Ňčon te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, woŇč nombia yaa mee nyii lee n wose man n…õ? K…ĒŇč kaa bu ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou doo n nyiŇčmaa man n…õ ak…Ēntaa fa maŇč, nawolo nyi n ba tekaa k…õ…õ ma ab…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē bela ya.‚ÄĚ 3Bot…Ē…Ē te tobaale Ňčon yakow…Ē …Ē nyee man nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe gbeŇčgyoo ne gyae waa gegi maŇč lee ma tom man. Sena te maa y…õ…õ? M…Ē…Ē taŇč doŇč me kaa fara ya, te ab…Ē…Ē kasolee m…Ē dana maŇč des…õŇč.‚ÄĚ 4KeŇčte …Ē yako …Ē wose nyi me ta gyeŇč kpene keŇč maa y…õ…õ keŇč nyi akpaa ma tom de w…Ēl…õ…õ na, maa nyiŇč balaŇč baŇč baa l…õ…õ maŇč be d…õ…õ. 5Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē baake balaŇč baŇč pou be dana …Ē gbeŇčgyoo Ňčon koma n…õ dokoloŇč dokoloŇč. KeŇčte …Ē b…Ē…Ēse gyaŇčgbate Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúKom aŇčmoŇč te n dana me gbeŇčgyoo?‚ÄĚ 6KeŇčte …Ē tiran…Ē…Ē fa nyiaa, ‚ÄúNuŇč garawaase s…ĒŇčonaara na kufu (90).‚ÄĚ Te tobaale Ňčon lese …Ē kom t…Ēne keŇč fa e te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúTisi kyaa bileŇč na n ke ky…õ…õkee ke y…õ…õ garawaase s…ĒŇčola na anoŇč (45).‚ÄĚ 7KeŇčte …Ē besew…Ē b…Ē…Ēse balate Ňčon m…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúKom aŇčmoŇč te nyaŇč dana me gbeŇčgyoo?‚ÄĚ Te Ňčon m…Ē tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMaŇč dana e kom kameese tasase kakpoŇč (1000).‚ÄĚ KeŇčte tobaale Ňčon lese …Ē kom t…Ēne fa e te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKy…õ…õkee ke y…õ…õ s…ĒŇčonoŇčse akpeŇč gyanaara (800).‚ÄĚ 8∆Ü gbeŇčgyoo Ňčon ke nyii wa ay…õ…õŇč n…õ, …Ē k…õl…õ…õ …Ē tobaale nkontopoote Ňčon nyi …Ē gyeŇč adao nideli. Nawolo nyi t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč gyeŇč adaosebia Ňčan baa mo kyaa na be tebia kela balaŇč baŇč bee yee Wurubuar…õ n…õ.
9‚ÄúMena d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyi …õ mo t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ deeli lese gyoori, na akpaa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan de taŇč n…õ, na ke yela Wurubuar…õ ke l…õ…õ Ňčon na …õ ke kyaa …Ē gyaŇč kekpaakekpaa. 10WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne di anokoare kolo peperee d…Ē…Ē kek…õ…õ man n…õ, waa di anokoare kolo dinaa m…Ē d…Ē…Ē kek…õ…õ man. Te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē bee di anokoare kolo peperee d…Ē…Ē kek…õ…õ man ya n…õ, …Ē be gyae waa di anokoare kolo dinaa m…Ē d…Ē…Ē kek…õ…õ man ya. 11Akpaa nyi n bee di anokoare t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē kek…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē ya na, am…Ēte ne gyae la waa mo ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa Ňčan Ňče dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ man n…õ do neŇč n nyiŇčmaa man? 12Te akpaa n te di anokoare k…õ…õ Ňčolo ab…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē nideli ya na, am…Ēte ne gyae la waa s…õ…õ mo nyaŇč gbagba wui do neŇč n nyiŇčmaa man? 13Tobaale kamas…õ be gyae waa tale som gbeŇčgyoona bala ya. De k…ĒŇč mena na waa k…Ēla Ňčolo na waa gyae Ňčolo nombia, yaa waa som Ňčolo kela Ňčolo. ∆ź be gyae …õ ke tale som Wurubuar…õ na k…Ēba bom ya.‚ÄĚ
14Farasiitena baŇč be sia ne y…õ…õ k…Ēba d…Ē…Ē nideli ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, ba boŇčo Yesu kulu e. 15KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ y…õna balaŇč baŇč …õ ne desina …õ wose balaŇč siaman, m…Ēna Wurubuar…õ gyeŇč …õ kon…Ē…Ēse man. Nawolo nyi kpene kamas…õ keŇč balaŇč ne bu ke nideli n…õ, ke y…õ…õ kolo anek…ĒlaŇč ke Wurubuar…õ siaman. 16Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan, na Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē te balaŇč ne dii p…õte Gy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre Ňčon k…ĒŇčaw…Ē. Lee mena debaŇč keŇč Gy…Ēn be k…ĒŇč pou na, bee kolosi Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč wose man nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan, te walaŇč kamas…õ na peree nyi waa gyoo ke man. 17Dana doŇč nyi kolo bii peperee ka yo lee Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan man, kela nyi adido na ateta ke Ňčaalaa. 18WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē b…õ…õ …Ē w…õ…õle te …Ē yala alo w…Ēle n…õ, Wurubuar…õ siaman na …Ētenate gyae Ňčolo w…õ…õle baa Te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon m…Ē …Ē yala alo Ňčon …Ē baale be b…õ…õ e na, Wurubuar…õ siaman na …Ētenate m…Ē gyae Ňčolo w…õ…õle baa.‚ÄĚ
K…Ēbate Ňčolo na Lasarose wose man nombia
19KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúK…Ēbate Ňčolo kyaaw…Ē keŇč …Ē na do ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa nyiŇčan Ňčan Ňče dana koyadoŇč te …Ē ne di gyoŇč wee kamas…õ. 20Wee na wee m…Ē na, b…õ…õ mo balee diyemte Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Lasarose n…õ, kaa see k…Ēbate Ňčon denan…Ē…Ē man. Na Lasarose kei wose pou te kulii gyam…õ…õ. 21∆Ü ne k…ĒŇč kaa kyaa bot…Ē…Ē gyakaa loo nyi waa nyiŇč k…Ēbate Ňčon ween…õ…õ Ňčan ne yale t…õ…õle man n…õ Ňčaale di. Na babaase gba ne k…ĒŇč kaa deŇčee …Ē gyam…õ…õ Ňčan. 22Wee daale n…õ, balee diyemte Ňčon kaa yeŇčaw…Ē te be wuu e te Wurubuar…õ kpilala kaa seele …Ē woo gyu Aberaham gyaŇč Wurubuar…õ man. KenaŇč w…Ēle te k…Ēbate Ňčon m…Ē kaa yeŇčaw…Ē te be wuu e te …Ē gyuuw…Ē ka gyoo boalaŇč keŇč ke b…õ…õ yem ya n…õ man. 23∆Ü ke doo bot…Ē…Ē …Ē ne naa diyem n…õ, …Ē gyinaa …Ē sia k…õ…õ adido na te …Ē naa Aberaham lemlem na Lasarose Ňčon kyaa …Ē yenaŇč. 24Bot…Ē…Ē te k…Ēbate Ňčon gyinaa …Ē n…Ē…Ē baake Aberaham nyiaa, ‚ÄėMe kya Aberaham! Wii me waraŇčase na n ke yela Lasarose ka mo …Ē nyimbii ke s…Ē…Ē loŇč man, na waa mo kaa tina me laakem na ma wose ke f…õ…õ maŇč kyomii, nawolo nyi m…õ…õ naa diyem nideli boalaŇč kei man!‚Äô 25KeŇčte Aberaham tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄėMe bu, t…Ē…Ēse nyi debaŇč keŇč n kyaa t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē n…õ n nyiŇča ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa di gyoŇč, na Lasarose Ňčon doo diyem man …Ē na yey…õ…õ, te n…õn…õ…õ Ňčon m…Ē ne di gyoŇč k…õbo te nyaŇč m…Ē n doo diyem man n na yey…õ…õ. 26Te agyakaŇč y…õna nyi, b…Ē…Ē dinaa dinaa daale doo daa na Ňčon nsana keŇč nyi baŇč be kyaa k…õbo n…õ be gyae baa tale gbaa k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč ya, mena m…Ē te baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ be gyae baa tale gbaa k…ĒŇč de gyaŇč k…õbo ya.‚Äô 27KeŇčte k…Ēbate Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄėMe kya, kenaŇč na m…õ…õ sola neŇč bo, kpila Lasarose ka bese gyu me kya d…õ…õ, 28nawolo nyi me dana naabia baala banoŇč na …Ē kaa faree w…Ē, na mena ya na baŇč m…Ē baa k…ĒŇč kaa naa diyem boalaŇč kei man.‚Äô 29KeŇčte Aberaham yako k…Ēbate Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄėBa gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan …Ē be yeli Mosesi be Ňčmarase see, na Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra ba wolo n…õ. Mena d…Ē…Ē baa tei nombia ŇčenaŇč nideli.‚Äô 30Bot…Ē…Ē te k…Ēbate Ňčon tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄėKoa me kya Aberaham! Akpaa Ňčolo da lee woya man k…õbo gyu keyako w…Ē n…õ, baa ky…õ…õkee lee be nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ man na baa k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč.‚Äô 31KeŇčte Aberaham yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄėNyi akpaa be te nyii Mosesi na Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra baŇč nombia ya n…õ, mena m…Ē te Ňčolo de fom lee yeŇč man k…õbo gyu be gyaŇč koraŇč na b…Ē…Ē gyae baa taŇč bua e ya.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Faith and Service
(Matthew 18.6,7,21,22; Mark 9.42)
1Jesus said to his disciples:
There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin 2would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their neck. 3 So be careful what you do.
Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry. 4Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, ‚ÄúI am sorry,‚ÄĚ you should still forgive that person.
5The apostles said to the Lord, ‚ÄúMake our faith stronger!‚ÄĚ
6Jesus replied:
If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
7If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, ‚ÄúWelcome! Come on in and have something to eat‚ÄĚ? 8No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, ‚ÄúPrepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.‚ÄĚ 9Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do. 10And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, ‚ÄúWe are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.‚ÄĚ
Ten Men with Leprosy
11On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee. 12As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance 13and shouted, ‚ÄúJesus, Master, have pity on us!‚ÄĚ
14 Jesus looked at them and said, ‚ÄúGo show yourselves to the priests.‚ÄĚ
On their way they were healed. 15When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God. 16He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
17Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWeren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine? 18Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?‚ÄĚ 19Then Jesus told the man, ‚ÄúYou may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.‚ÄĚ
God's Kingdom
(Matthew 24.23-28,37-41)
20Some Pharisees asked Jesus when God's kingdom would come. He answered, ‚ÄúGod's kingdom isn't something you can see. 21There is no use saying, ‚ÄėLook! Here it is‚Äô or ‚ÄėLook! There it is.‚Äô God's kingdom is here with you.‚ÄĚ
22Jesus said to his disciples:
The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not. 23When people say to you, ‚ÄúLook there,‚ÄĚ or ‚ÄúLook here,‚ÄĚ don't go looking for him. 24The day of the Son of Man will be like lightning flashing across the sky. 25But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today. 26 When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived. 27 People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
28 When Lot lived, people were also eating and drinking. They were buying, selling, planting, and building. 29But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone. 30The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
31 At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything. 32 Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
33 People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them. 34On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left. 35-36Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
37Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, ‚ÄúBut where will this happen, Lord?‚ÄĚ
Jesus said, ‚ÄúWhere there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.‚ÄĚ

Te nde balaŇč kadaale akaŇč baŇč? 18Weera d…Ē…Ē te Ňč…Ē…Ēle kei waageŇč y…õna walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ba bese k…ĒŇč …Ē kaa fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč?‚ÄĚ 19Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKoro na n ke ta, n kel…õ…õdi d…Ē…Ē te n nyiŇča kawee keky…Ē.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kekolosi lee Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč wose man
