english_text:
A Widow and a Judge
1Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
2In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people. 3In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, ‚ÄúMake sure that I get fair treatment in court.‚ÄĚ
4For a while the judge refused to do anything. Finally, he said to himself, ‚ÄúEven though I don't fear God or care about people, 5I will help this widow because she keeps on bothering me. If I don't help her, she will wear me out.‚ÄĚ
6The Lord said:
Think about what that crooked judge said. 7 Won't God protect his chosen ones who pray to him day and night? Won't he be concerned for them? 8He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith?
A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
9Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
10Two men went into the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector. 11The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, ‚ÄúGod, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there. 12I go without eating for two days a week, and I give you one tenth of all I earn.‚ÄĚ
13 The tax collector stood off at a distance and did not think he was good enough even to look up toward heaven. He was so sorry for what he had done that he pounded his chest and prayed, ‚ÄúGod, have pity on me! I am such a sinner.‚ÄĚ
14 Then Jesus said, ‚ÄúWhen the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Blesses Little Children
(Matthew 19.13-15; Mark 10.13-16)
15Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him. 16So Jesus called the children over to him and said, ‚ÄúLet the children come to me! Don't try to stop them. People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom. 17You will never get into God's kingdom unless you enter it like a child!‚ÄĚ
A Rich and Important Man
(Matthew 19.16-30; Mark 10.17-31)
18An important man asked Jesus, ‚ÄúGood Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?‚ÄĚ
19Jesus said, ‚ÄúWhy do you call me good? Only God is good. 20 You know the commandments: ‚ÄėBe faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others. Respect your father and mother.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
21He told Jesus, ‚ÄúI have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.‚ÄĚ
22When Jesus heard this, he said, ‚ÄúThere is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.‚ÄĚ 23When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
24Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, ‚ÄúIt's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom! 25In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.‚ÄĚ
26When the crowd heard this, they asked, ‚ÄúHow can anyone ever be saved?‚ÄĚ
27Jesus replied, ‚ÄúThere are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.‚ÄĚ
28Peter said, ‚ÄúRemember, we left everything to be your followers!‚ÄĚ
29Jesus answered, ‚ÄúYou can be sure that anyone who gives up home or wife or brothers or family or children because of God's kingdom 30will be given much more in this life. And in the future world they will have eternal life.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Again Tells about His Death
(Matthew 20.17-19; Mark 10.32-34)
31Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said:
We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there. 32He will be handed over to foreigners, who will make fun of him, mistreat him, and spit on him. 33They will beat him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.
34The apostles did not understand what Jesus was talking about. They could not understand, because the meaning of what he said was hidden from them.
Jesus Heals a Blind Beggar
(Matthew 20.29-34; Mark 10.46-52)
35When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road. 36The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening. 37Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by. 38So the blind man shouted, ‚ÄúJesus, Son of David, have pity on me!‚ÄĚ 39The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, ‚ÄúSon of David, have pity on me!‚ÄĚ
40Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked, 41‚ÄúWhat do you want me to do for you?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúLord, I want to see!‚ÄĚ he answered.
42Jesus replied, ‚ÄúLook and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.‚ÄĚ 43At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
translation_text:
Lialo Ňčolo na nombiadiiri Ňčolo wose man dudu
1KeŇčte Yesu gy…Ē…Ē dudu fa …Ē kaseela baŇč wola w…Ē nyiaa, kaboena nyi baa fane wee kamas…õ keŇč ba wose na y…Ēk…Ēse w…Ē ya.
2∆Ü yako w…Ē nyiaa ‚ÄúDon…Ē…Ē daale man n…õ nombiadiiri Ňčolo kyaaw…Ē keŇč …Ē bee yee Wurubuar…õ ya, te …Ē be famen…õ na walaŇč m…Ē ya. 3Na lialo Ňčolo m…Ē kyaa don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man. ∆Ü ne kyaa k…ĒŇč nombiadiiri Ňčon gyaŇč kaa waase e wee kamas…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄėK…õ…õ na n ke di daa na me walaŇč Ňčon daa na e be nyim n…õ nombia Ňčan nideli fa maŇč.‚Äô 4Naf…Ē nombiadiiri Ňčon b…õ…õw…Ē kelii debaŇč daale, m…Ēna w…Ēle w…Ēle n…õ …Ē yako …Ē wose nyiaa, ‚ÄėY…õ…õ ampaŇč nyi m…Ē…Ē ne yee Wurubuar…õ ya te m…Ē…Ē famen…õ na walaŇč m…Ē ya. 5M…Ēna alo kei nombia te poro maŇč mena d…Ē…Ē maa k…õ…õ na maa di …Ē nombia Ňčan nideli fa e, na …Ē be nyiŇč gyem tisi me d…Ē…Ē naase maŇč diyem bela ya.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
6Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź nyii kpene keŇč nombiadiiri kum kei be yako. 7Akpaa mena na Wurubuar…õ be gyae waa di nombia anokoare man fa …Ē balaŇč baŇč bee wii baake e nelim na ka weese n…õ yee? ∆ź ne l…õ…õdi nyi waa w…ĒŇčee …Ē wose nyi …Ē be ky…Ē w…Ē yee? 8M…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuar…õ waa di nombia Ňčan anokoare man fa w…Ē bileŇč na waa yela baa di deŇčele. Te nyi akpaa maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon ma bese k…ĒŇč na, balaŇč baa kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē keŇč baa l…õ…õ maŇč di?‚ÄĚ
Farasii baale Ňčolo na lempool…õ…õre Ňčolo wose man dudu
9Yesu besew…Ē gy…Ē dudu kei fa balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ k…õ…õ ba wose gy…Ē kon…Ē…Ē nyi be gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman, te b…Ē…Ē na mo walaŇč kamas…õ y…õ…õ kolo ya n…õ nyiaa! 10‚ÄúBaala bala baale gyuu Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man be kaa fane. Ňäolo y…õ…õ Farasii baale e te Ňčolo m…Ē y…õ…õ lempool…õ…õre e. 11Farasii baale Ňčon korow…Ē seŇč te …Ē fanew…Ē k…õl…õ…õ …Ē wose nyiaa, ‚ÄėWurubuar…õ m…õ…õ fa neŇč karaŇč nyi ma te y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õre e Ňčgba balaŇč akaŇč baŇč n…õ ya. M…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ Ňčgba Ňčm…õ…õlatena na balaŇč kum…õ…õ na kafoŇčtena yaa Ňčgba lempool…õ…õre kei n…õ ya. 12M…õ…õ bake me n…Ē…Ē betabola weeya nyetooro kamas…õ man, te m…õ…õ kpase ma kpene kamas…õ keŇč mee nyim n…õ akpeŇč kufu na me ta mo dekpeŇč dokoloŇč fa nyaŇč Wurubuar…õ.‚Äô 13M…Ēna lempool…õ…õre Ňčon d…Ē…Ē, Ňčon gyuuw…Ē ka seŇč yenaŇč keŇč nyi …Ē te gyina …Ē sia k…õ…õ adido gba ya te …Ē moo …Ē nyiŇčmaase gyakaa …Ē kon…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē waraŇčase man yako nyiaa, ‚ÄėWurubuar…õ, wii maŇč nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õre kei waraŇčase.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 14KeŇčte Yesu yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyi ba ke fane taŇč n…õ, lempool…õ…õre Ňčon te Wurubuar…õ l…õ…õw…Ē do nyi …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Ňčon Wurubuar…õ siaman. Nawolo nyi walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē gyina …Ē wose adido na Wurubuar…õ waa y…Ēk…Ēse e, te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē y…Ēk…Ēse …Ē wose na Wurubuar…õ waa gyina e adido.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kehiraa bia
Mateo 19:13-15; Maake 10:17-20
15Wee daale balaŇč baale moo be bia k…ĒŇč nyi Yesu waa mo …Ē nyiŇčmaa gyakaa be d…Ē…Ē na waa hiraa w…Ē. ∆Ü kaseela baŇč kena mena n…õ, be ŇčmanyaŇč balaŇč baŇč. 16KeŇčte Yesu baake bia baŇč k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč te …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź yela bia baŇč kek…ĒŇč me gyaŇč, …õ na t…Ē w…Ē gb…õ…õ ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose Ňčgba bia k…õw…Ē din…Ē…Ē n…õ wui. 17AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyiaa, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē te y…Ēk…Ēse …Ē wose do Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õ Ňčgba biikyaa e ya n…õ, …Ē be gyae waa gyoo Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč man korakora ya.‚ÄĚ
Yesu na k…Ēbate Ňčolo wose man nombia
Mateo 19:16-29; Maake 10:17-30
18Wee daale n…õ Gyudatena siamante Ňčolo kaa b…Ē…Ēse Yesu nyiaa ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo kpaakpaa, woŇč te maa y…õ…õ na maa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč?‚ÄĚ 19Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč d…Ē…Ē te n ne baake maŇč walaŇč kpaakpaa? Lee Wurubuar…õ w…Ēle n…õ walaŇč kamas…õ be kyaa keŇč …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč kpaakpaate e ya.‚ÄĚ 20Ňägba n gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan ne yako nyi, ‚ÄúNa lee n baale yaa n w…õ…õle w…Ēle ya, na ko walaŇč ya na Ňčm…õ…õle ya, na kara n d…Ē…Ē n…Ē…Ē ya. Bu n kya na n naa n…õ?‚ÄĚ 21KeŇčte balee Ňčon m…Ē tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa ‚ÄúMe wulaa mee di mmaraase k…õŇča pou d…Ē…Ē leenaŇč me biat…õ…õ‚ÄĚ 22Yesu ke nyii …Ē n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan n…õ …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúTa bese ka neŇč kolo dokoloŇč kooŇč n ke y…õ…õ. Gyu ke y…Ē…Ē nya ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou n dana n…õ na n ka mo k…Ēba keŇč kp…õl…õ…õ ayematena na n ke k…ĒŇč kaa sila maŇč. N y…õ…õ mena na n ke nyiŇč ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa see n wose Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč adido.‚ÄĚ 23Balee Ňčon ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, takaa e nideli nawolo nyi …Ē dana ab…Ē…Ē burum. 24KeŇčte Yesu g…õ…õsew…Ē k…õ…õ e te …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúGyae ke y…õ…õ doŇč fa baŇč be dana k…Ēba n…õ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč man. 25M…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, gyae ke y…õ…õ doŇč fa baŇč be dana k…Ēba n…õ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč man, kela nyi kurum…Ē…Ē ka gyoo Ňčarale b…Ē…Ē man lee.‚ÄĚ
26BalaŇč baŇč ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyi nn…õ te doo na, am…Ēte ne gyae la waa nyiŇč nyeekel…õ…õ baa?‚ÄĚ
27KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKpene keŇč deniwalaŇč be gyae waa tale y…õ…õ ya n…õ, Wurubuar…õ d…Ē…Ē waa tale y…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ 28Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe gbeŇčgyoo, daa d…Ē…Ē de te tina kpene kamas…õ keŇč de dana n…õ yela, te de d…ĒŇč…õ…õ de silana neŇč.‚ÄĚ 29KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē be tina …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč yaa …Ē w…õ…õle yaa …Ē naabia yaa …Ē kya na …Ē naa yaa …Ē bia yela lee Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, 30waa bese nyiŇč Ňča burum kpu gy…õŇč weeya k…õŇča man, na waa bese nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa wee daale.‚ÄĚ
Yesu besew…Ē kolosi lee …Ē yeŇč na …Ē kefoŇč wose man
Mateo 20:17-19; Maake 10:32-34
31Wee daale n…õ Yesu baake …Ē kaseela kufu bala baŇč lee yenaŇč te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúDee gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man baa, te kpene kamas…õ keŇč Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra be Ňčmarase lee maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon wose man n…õ, gyae ke k…ĒŇč mena. 32Baa mo maŇč do balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ nyiŇčmaa man, na baa kulu maŇč na baa tee maŇč na baa t…Ē n…Ē…ĒloŇč woli maŇč na baa tuku maŇč na baa ko maŇč. 33M…Ēna weeya atoorote keŇč n…õ, Wurubuar…õ waa gyuusu maŇč lee yeŇč man.‚ÄĚ
34Yesu kaseela baŇč te nyii nombia k…õŇča man ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ ta lese Ňče man wola w…Ē ya mena d…Ē…Ē be te nyii Ňče man dokoloŇč man ya.
Yesu keky…Ē siay…õl…õ…õsate Ňčolo kawee
Mateo 20:29-34; Maake 10:46-52
35Yesu k…õ…õ benaa Gyeriko don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, na siay…õl…õ…õsate Ňčolo kyaa gb…õ…õnaa man …Ē na solee ab…Ē…Ē. 36∆Ü ke nyii balaŇč burum baŇč k…õ…õ lam n…õ, …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyi woŇč k…ĒŇčala? 37KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč yako e nyiaa ‚ÄúYesu Nasar…õte baale Ňčon ne laŇča laa.‚ÄĚ 38Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē gyinaa …Ē n…Ē…Ē baake nyiaa, ‚ÄúYesu, Defidi …Ē naanabii! Wii me waraŇčase!‚ÄĚ 39BalaŇč baŇč be d…ĒŇč…õ…õ siaman n…õ ŇčmanyaŇč e nyi waa wu …Ē n…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ gba te …Ē desinaa fae yeesa nedoŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúDefidi …Ē naanabii! Wii me waraŇčase.‚ÄĚ 40Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu seŇčaw…Ē te …Ē yakow…Ē nyi baa mo siay…õl…õ…õsate Ňčon k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč. ∆Ü ka benaa e n…õ, Yesu b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, 41‚ÄúWoŇč te n ne gyae nyi maa y…õ…õ fa neŇč?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte siay…õl…õ…õsate Ňčon tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, m…õ…õ gyae me sia ke gyuusu.‚ÄĚ 42KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN sia dee gyuusu na n kena. N ke l…õ…õ maŇč di d…Ē…Ē te n nyiŇča kawee keky…Ē.‚ÄĚ 43DebokenaŇč man te …Ē sia gyuusuw…Ē, te …Ē naaw…Ē te …Ē sila Yesu na …Ē na lese Wurubuar…õ yele. BalaŇč dikpii keŇč pou kena n…õ, baŇč m…Ē lese Wurubuar…õ yele.

english_text:
Zacchaeus
1Jesus was going through Jericho, 2where a man named Zacchaeus lived. He was in charge of collecting taxes and was very rich. 3-4Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
5When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, ‚ÄúZacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.‚ÄĚ 6Zacchaeus hurried down and gladly welcomed Jesus.
7Everyone who saw this started grumbling, ‚ÄúThis man Zacchaeus is a sinner! And Jesus is going home to eat with him.‚ÄĚ
8Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, ‚ÄúI will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.‚ÄĚ
9Jesus said to Zacchaeus, ‚ÄúToday you and your family have been saved, because you are a true son of Abraham. 10 The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.‚ÄĚ
A Story about Ten Servants
(Matthew 25.14-30)
11 The crowd was still listening to Jesus as he was getting close to Jerusalem. Many of them thought that God's kingdom would soon appear, 12and Jesus told them this story:
A prince once went to a foreign country to be crowned king and then to return. 13But before leaving, he called in ten servants and gave each of them some money. He told them, ‚ÄúUse this to earn more money until I get back.‚ÄĚ
14But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, ‚ÄúWe don't want this man to be our king.‚ÄĚ
15After the prince had been made king, he returned and called in his servants. He asked them how much they had earned with the money they had been given.
16The first servant came and said, ‚ÄúSir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.‚ÄĚ
17‚ÄúThat's fine, my good servant!‚ÄĚ the king said. ‚ÄúSince you have shown that you can be trusted with a small amount, you will be given ten cities to rule.‚ÄĚ
18The second one came and said, ‚ÄúSir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.‚ÄĚ
19The king said, ‚ÄúYou will be given five cities.‚ÄĚ
20Another servant came and said, ‚ÄúSir, here is your money. I kept it safe in a handkerchief. 21You are a hard man, and I was afraid of you. You take what isn't yours, and you harvest crops you didn't plant.‚ÄĚ
22‚ÄúYou worthless servant!‚ÄĚ the king told him. ‚ÄúYou have condemned yourself by what you have just said. You knew I am a hard man, taking what isn't mine and harvesting what I've not planted. 23Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.‚ÄĚ
24Then he said to some other servants standing there, ‚ÄúTake the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.‚ÄĚ
25But they said, ‚ÄúSir, he already has ten times as much!‚ÄĚ
26 The king replied, ‚ÄúThose who have something will be given more. But everything will be taken away from those who don't have anything. 27Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!‚ÄĚ
Jesus Enters Jerusalem
(Matthew 21.1-11; Mark 11.1-11; John 12.12-19)
28When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem. 29As he was getting near Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples on ahead. 30He told them, ‚ÄúGo into the next village, where you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here. 31If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‚ÄėThe Lord needs it.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
32They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said. 33While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, ‚ÄúWhy are you doing that?‚ÄĚ
34They answered, ‚ÄúThe Lord needs it.‚ÄĚ
35Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on. 36And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him. 37When Jesus started down the Mount of Olives, his large crowd of disciples were happy and praised God because of all the miracles they had seen. 38 They shouted,
‚ÄúBlessed is the king who comes
in the name of the Lord!
Peace in heaven
and glory to God.‚ÄĚ
39Some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, ‚ÄúTeacher, make your disciples stop shouting!‚ÄĚ
40But Jesus answered, ‚ÄúIf they keep quiet, these stones will start shouting.‚ÄĚ
41When Jesus came closer and could see Jerusalem, he cried 42and said:
It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them. 43Jerusalem, the time will come when your enemies will build walls around you to attack you. Armies will surround you and close in on you from every side. 44They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you.
Jesus in the Temple
(Matthew 21.12-17; Mark 11.15-19; John 2.13-22)
45When Jesus entered the temple, he started chasing out the people who were selling things. 46 He told them, ‚ÄúThe Scriptures say, ‚ÄėMy house should be a place of worship.‚Äô But you have made it a place where robbers hide!‚ÄĚ
47 Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed. 48But they could not find a way to do it, because everyone else was eager to listen to him.
translation_text:
Yesu na Sakio wose man nombia
1Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Gyeriko don…Ē…Ē man …Ē ne lam n…õ, 2na balee Ňčolo kyaa bot…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē yele y…õna Sakio. ∆Ü y…õ…õ lempool…õ…õra kegyia e te …Ē dana k…Ēba m…Ē nideli. 3Sakio kei gyaew…Ē nyi waa naa Yesu m…Ēna …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč duguŇčte e d…Ē…Ē …Ē te tale naa e ya, nawolo nyi balaŇč baŇč boe. 4Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē kyaŇča gb…õ…õ gyu siaman ka gyeŇč daŇč daale, na waa nyiŇč naa e nawolo nyi bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu waa kaa mo. 5Yesu kelii daŇč keŇč t…õ…õ n…õ …Ē gyinaa …Ē sia k…õ…õ adido te …Ē naa Sakio te …Ē baake e nyiaa, ‚ÄúSakio tisi k…ĒŇč bileŇč, nawolo nyi gy…õŇč n d…õ…õ te maa soo.‚ÄĚ 6Mena d…Ē…Ē Sakio m…Ē tisiw…Ē bileŇč k…ĒŇč te …Ē l…õ…õ Yesu gyoŇč man. 7BalaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē naa n…õ fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ Ňčorokotee nyi, ‚ÄúBalee kei ne gyu …Ē kaa soo nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õre kei.‚ÄĚ 8Be ke gyu ka gyoo Sakio d…õ…õ n…õ, …Ē korow…Ē kaa seŇč balaŇč baŇč siaman te …Ē yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo k…õ…õ, maa kpase ma ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou me dana n…õ yaale fa ayematena. Te akpaa me te sisii Ňčolo na maa t…Ē e betabonaara kpu‚ÄĚ 9KeŇčte Yesu yako Sakio nyiaa, ‚ÄúGy…õŇč Wurubuar…õ te l…õ…õ …õm…õ…õ na n dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou nyee, nawolo nyi nyaŇč m…Ē n y…õ…õ Aberaham …Ē naanabii e. 10Nawolo nyi maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu k…ĒŇčae me kaa k…õo na maa l…õ…õ baŇč b…Ē…Ē yoo n…õ nyee.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kegy…Ē dudu lee k…Ēba bia Ňčaale wose man
Mateo 25:14-30
11BalaŇč baŇč kee tei mena nombia k…õŇča n…õ, Yesu besew…Ē gy…Ē dudu fa w…Ē nawolo nyi b…õ…õ benaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man, na balaŇč gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč gyae ke b…õ…õ lee debokenaŇč man. 12Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee Ňčolo kyaaw…Ē keŇč …Ē y…õ…õ gyooro bu e, te …Ē korow…Ē gyu t…õ…õle daale d…Ē…Ē lemlem …Ē kaa di gyoori na waa bese k…ĒŇč. 13DebaŇč keŇč …Ē ne gyae waa gyu n…õ …Ē baake …Ē tewul…õ…õ kufu baale te …Ē faa w…Ē k…Ēba ky…õ…õky…õ…õ te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äė∆ź mo k…Ēba kei besena …õ wose kelii debaŇč keŇč maa bese k…ĒŇč.‚Äô 14M…Ēna …Ē d…õ…õtena baŇč b…õ…õ gyae …Ē nombia ya, mena d…Ē…Ē be kpila balaŇč gyu keyako don…Ē…Ē keŇč man …Ē ne gyu n…õ kegyiise nyiaa, ‚ÄėDe b…õ…õ gyae balee kei kaa bese de gyoo ya.‚Äô 15M…Ēna kegyiise baŇč see balee Ňčon gyoo te …Ē besew…Ē k…ĒŇč …Ē d…õ…õ. ∆Ü ka bese k…ĒŇč n…õ …Ē kpilaw…Ē baake …Ē tewul…õ…õ baŇč …Ē be fa w…Ē k…Ēba n…õ nyi baa k…ĒŇč, na waa k…õ…õ kpene keŇč b…Ē…Ē nyim gyakaa be k…Ēbase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē. 16∆Ü tobaale gyaŇčgbate Ňčon k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄėMe gbeŇčgyoo, k…Ēba keŇč n ba mo fa maŇč n…õ ta lola kufu kpu na bene keŇč.‚Äô 17KeŇčte …Ē gbeŇčgyoo Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄėNyaŇč na key…õ…õ, n y…õ…õ tobaale kpaakpaate e! Mena keŇč n be di anokoare ab…Ē…Ē peperee k…õŇča man d…Ē…Ē n…õ, maa yela n ke k…õ…õ don…Ē…Ēse kufu d…Ē…Ē fa maŇč.‚Äô 18∆Ü tobaale balate Ňčon m…Ē k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄėMe gbeŇčgyoo, n k…Ēba keŇč n ba mo fa maŇč n…õ ta lola anoŇč kpu na bene keŇč.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 19Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč m…Ē n ke k…õ…õ don…Ē…Ēse anoŇč d…Ē…Ē fa maŇč.‚ÄĚ 20KeŇčte …Ē tobaale Ňčolo m…Ē k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe gbeŇčgyoo, n k…Ēba keŇč n ba mo fa maŇč n…õ yaa, ma moo ke kpaŇč na kakyeŇčgbelaa ka weese. 21Nawolo nyi na mee yee neŇč nyi n nombia dana doŇč. N na mo kpene keŇč te y…õ…õ n wui ya, te n ne k…Ē ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan n te duu ya n…õ m…Ē.‚ÄĚ 22KeŇčte …Ē gbeŇčgyoo Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN y…õ…õ tobaale kum e, nyaŇč gbagba n n…Ē…Ēwoya te maa mo bu neŇč f…Ē…Ē. N gyeŇč nyi me nombia dana doŇč te m…õ…õ mo kpene keŇč te y…õ…õ me wui ya, te m…õ…õ k…Ē ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan me te duu ya na, 23woŇč d…Ē…Ē te n ta mo me k…Ēba keŇč ka do k…Ēba d…õ…õsee man na me k…ĒŇč na, me kaa l…õ…õ keŇč na ka loŇč keŇč ya?‚ÄĚ 24Bot…Ē…Ē te be gbeŇčgyoo Ňčon yako baŇč ba seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē n…õ nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź l…õo k…Ēba keŇč lee …Ē gyaŇč na …õ ka mo fa walaŇč Ňčon …Ē k…Ēba ba lola kufu kpu n…õ.‚ÄĚ
25KeŇčte …Ē tewul…õ…õ baŇč yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe gbeŇčgyoo, Ňčgba Ňčon wulaa …Ē dana k…Ēba burum?‚ÄĚ
26Bot…Ē…Ē te be gbeŇčgyoo Ňčon tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyi walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē wulaa …Ē dana ab…Ē…Ē burum n…õ baa mo Ňčaale kpu e bela. Te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē betaŇč ya n…õ, kyomii keŇč gba …Ē dana koraŇč n…õ baa l…õo ke lee …Ē nyiŇčmaa man. 27Te me k…Ēlala baŇč ba te gyae nyi maa di gyoori be d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, …õ mo w…Ē k…ĒŇč na …õ kaa koe w…Ē me siaman k…õbo.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kegyu Gyerusal…õm gyoorobiiri man
Mateo 21:1-11; Maake 11:1-11; Gy…Ēn 12:12-19
28Yesu keyako nombia k…õŇča taŇč n…õ, …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē balaŇč baŇč siaman te ba moo ba nyee baŇč bee gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man. 29∆Ü k…õ…õ benaa B…õtifagi na B…õtani don…Ē…Ēse Ňčan kyaa oliifi bula keŇč teŇča man n…õ, …Ē kpila …Ē kaseela baŇč man balaŇč bala 30nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź gyu akuraa keŇč kyaa …õ siaman n…õ man. ∆ź betaa …õ na gyoo ke man p…õ n…õ, …õ kena afurum bu Ňčolo kpaŇč…õ…õ keŇč walaŇč na walaŇč te kyaa ta …Ē d…Ē…Ē see ya. ∆ź booli e na …õ ka mo e k…Ēna maŇč k…õbo. 31Akpaa Ňčolo de b…Ē…Ēse Ňčon nyi, ‚ÄėWeera d…Ē…Ē te …õ ne booli e?‚Äô na ‚Äė∆ź yako e nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu ne gyae na e.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 32KeŇčte baŇč Yesu be kpili n…õ gyuuw…Ē kena afurum bu Ňčon kpaŇč…õ…õ Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be yako w…Ē n…õ. 33Be kee booli afurum bu Ňčon n…õ …Ē gbeŇčgyoo b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera d…Ē…Ē te …õ ne booli ma afurum bu Ňčon?‚ÄĚ 34KeŇčte be tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo Yesu ne gyae na e.‚ÄĚ
35Te ba moo afurum bu Ňčon k…Ēna Yesu. Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē kaseela baŇč moo be kanyaŇčse gyakaa afurum bu Ňčon d…Ē…Ē te be gate Yesu gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē. 36Yesu kekyaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē …Ē ne gyuu n…õ, baale f…õl…õ…õ be kanyaŇčse l…Ē gb…õ…õnaa man fa e.
37Yesu k…õ…õ gyoo Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man, te …Ē ka benaa na bot…Ē…Ē keŇč gb…õ…õ keŇč be tisi lee oliifi bula keŇč nyee man ne k…ĒŇč ka teŇča man n…õ, …Ē silala burum baŇč pou sia gyeew…Ē te be fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ fae yeesa lese Wurubuar…õ yele gyoŇč man lee nombia dinaana na gyakoloŇč nombia Ňčan b…Ē…Ē naa n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 38Be fae yeesa yako nyiaa,
‚Äú∆ź fa d…õ…õ lese gyoo Ňčon …Ē ne k…ĒŇč
de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ yele man n…õ yele.
Wosef…õ…õreŇč d…õ…õ kyaa adido,
te Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa adido m…Ē n…õ
gyoorobiiri d…õ…õ kaboena e.‚ÄĚ
39Na Farasiitena baale kpu…õ na balaŇč baŇč be silana e n…õ te be yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, t…Ē n silala baŇč nyi baa wu be n…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 40KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa balaŇč k…õw…Ē de wu be n…Ē…Ē koraŇč n…õ, boya gyae ke fae yeesa lese Wurubuar…õ yele.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kewii fa Gyerusal…õmtena
41Yesu ka benaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē te …Ē kena ke n…õ, …Ē wiiw…Ē fa ke, 42keŇčte …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúNaf…Ē akpaa …õm…õ…õ Gyerusal…õmtena deŇč gyeŇč kpene keŇč gyae ke fa Ňčon wosef…õ…õreŇč gy…õŇč wee kei n…õ, naf…Ē na dei. M…Ēna …õ ba gyeŇč ke ya! 43Te weeya Ňčaale gyae kek…ĒŇč keŇč …õ k…Ēlala baa kilisi Ňčon, na baa t…Ē Ňčon do lee yenaŇč kamas…õ. 44Na baa w…Ēl…õ…õ …õm…õ…õ na …õ don…Ē…Ē keŇč pou keŇč nyi boe na boe be gyae ke gyakaa ke d…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē ya, nawolo nyi debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ be k…ĒŇč nyi …Ē kaa l…õ…õ Ňčon n…õ …õ b…õ…õ e bo.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kegegi ab…Ē…Ēy…Ē…Ēra Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man
Mateo 21:12-17; Maake 11:15-19; Gy…Ēn 2:13-22
45KeŇčte Yesu gyuuw…Ē ka gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč dekpaŇčalaŇč man. Na balaŇč ne y…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē gegi w…Ē, 46te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see ne yako nyi Wurubuar…õ kpa,
‚ÄėMe deni dinaa keŇč gyae ke y…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē keŇč baa fane.
M…Ēna …õm…õ…õ ta besena ke y…õ…õ Ňčm…õ…õlatena d…õ…õgyaŇčee!‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
47Wee na wee na Yesu ne kyaa wola balaŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man. Te saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise baŇč gyae gb…õ…õ nyi baa ko e. 48M…Ēna be ta gyeŇč gb…õ…õ keŇč baa mo kyaŇč e ya, nawolo nyi na balaŇč baŇč pou ne tei …Ē nombia Ňčan …Ē na wolo n…õ nideli keŇč b…Ē…Ē ne gyae Ňča ke laŇč w…Ē ya.

english_text:
A Question about Jesus' Authority
(Matthew 21.23-27; Mark 11.27-33)
1One day, Jesus was teaching in the temple and telling the good news. So the chief priests, the teachers, and the nation's leaders 2asked him, ‚ÄúWhat right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?‚ÄĚ
3Jesus replied, ‚ÄúI want to ask you a question. 4Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?‚ÄĚ
5They talked this over and said to each other, ‚ÄúWe can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John. 6And we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize. The crowd will stone us to death, because they think John was a prophet.‚ÄĚ
7So they told Jesus, ‚ÄúWe don't know who gave John the right to baptize.‚ÄĚ
8Jesus replied, ‚ÄúThen I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.‚ÄĚ
Renters of a Vineyard
(Matthew 21.33-46; Mark 12.1-12)
9 Jesus told the people this story:
A man once planted a vineyard and rented it out. Then he left the country for a long time. 10When it was time to harvest the crop, he sent a servant to ask the renters for his share of the grapes. But they beat up the servant and sent him away without anything. 11So the owner sent another servant. The renters also beat him up. They insulted him terribly and sent him away without a thing. 12The owner sent a third servant. He was also beaten terribly and thrown out of the vineyard.
13The owner then said to himself, ‚ÄúWhat am I going to do? I know what. I'll send my son, the one I love so much. They will surely respect him!‚ÄĚ
14When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, ‚ÄúSomeday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.‚ÄĚ 15So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWhat do you think the owner of the vineyard will do? 16I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.‚ÄĚ
When the people heard this, they said, ‚ÄúThis must never happen!‚ÄĚ
17 But Jesus looked straight at them and said, ‚ÄúThen what do the Scriptures mean when they say, ‚ÄėThe stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all‚Äô? 18Anyone who stumbles over this stone will get hurt, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.‚ÄĚ
19The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people.
Paying Taxes
(Matthew 22.15-22; Mark 12.13-17)
20Jesus' enemies kept watching him closely, because they wanted to hand him over to the Roman governor. So they sent some men who pretended to be good. But they were really spies trying to catch Jesus saying something wrong. 21The spies said to him, ‚ÄúTeacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are. 22Tell us, should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?‚ÄĚ
23Jesus knew they were trying to trick him. So he told them, 24‚ÄúShow me a coin.‚ÄĚ Then he asked, ‚ÄúWhose picture and name are on it?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThe Emperor's,‚ÄĚ they answered.
25Then he told them, ‚ÄúGive the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.‚ÄĚ 26Jesus' enemies could not catch him saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer and kept quiet.
Life in the Future World
(Matthew 22.23-33; Mark 12.18-27)
27 The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus 28 and said:
Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
29There were once seven brothers. The first one married, but died without having any children. 30The second one married his brother's widow, and he also died without having any children. 31The same thing happened to the third one. Finally, all seven brothers married this woman and died without having any children. 32At last the woman died. 33When God raises people from death, whose wife will this woman be? All seven brothers had married her.
34Jesus answered:
The people in this world get married. 35But in the future world no one who is worthy to rise from death will either marry 36or die. They will be like the angels and will be God's children, because they have been raised to life.
37 In the story about the burning bush, Moses clearly shows that people will live again. He said, ‚ÄúThe Lord is the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.‚ÄĚ 38So the Lord isn't the God of the dead, but of the living. This means that everyone is alive as far as God is concerned.
39Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses said, ‚ÄúTeacher, you have given a good answer!‚ÄĚ 40From then on, no one dared to ask Jesus any questions.
About David's Son
(Matthew 22.41-46; Mark 12.35-37)
41Jesus asked, ‚ÄúWhy do people say that the Messiah will be the son of King David? 42 In the book of Psalms, David himself says,
‚ÄėThe Lord said to my Lord,
Sit at my right side
43until I make your enemies
into a footstool for you.‚Äô
44David spoke of the Messiah as his Lord, so how can the Messiah be his son?‚ÄĚ
Jesus and the Teachers of the Law of Moses
(Matthew 23.1-36; Mark 12.38-40; Luke 11.37-54)
45While everyone was listening to Jesus, he said to his disciples:
46Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets. 47But they cheat widows out of their homes and then pray long prayers just to show off. These teachers will be punished most of all.
translation_text:
Ba keb…Ē…Ēse Yesu …Ē doŇč wose man nombia
Mateo 21:23-27; Maake 11:27-33
1Wee daale Yesu ke kyaa Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man …Ē na wola balaŇč nombia te …Ē ne kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan fa w…Ē n…õ, saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise k…ĒŇčaw…Ē …Ē gyaŇč 2kaa b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúYako daa, doŇč woŇčti yaa n dana n ne y…õ…õ mena ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča pou? Am…Ēte faa na neŇč mena doŇč kenaŇč?‚ÄĚ 3Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMaŇč m…Ē ne gyae maa b…Ē…Ēse Ňčon nombii dokoloŇč. 4Gy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčkes…Ē keŇč …Ē be s…Ē balaŇč n…õ, lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč yaa balaŇč gyaŇč?‚ÄĚ 5Bot…Ē…Ē te be l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč na d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč te d…õ…õ yako? Akpaa de yako nyi ka lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč na, waa b…Ē…Ēse daa nyi woŇč d…Ē…Ē te da te l…õ…õ Gy…Ēn nombia Ňčan di ya? 6Te akpaa de yako nyi ka lee balaŇč gyaŇč na, balaŇč dikpii k…õw…Ē pou baa fuŇčii daa boya. Nawolo nyi b…õ…õ l…õ…õ di nideli nyi Gy…Ēn y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre e.‚ÄĚ 7Mena d…Ē…Ē be tiran…Ē…Ē fa Yesu nyiaa ‚ÄúD…Ē…Ē gyeŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč k…Ē…Ē lee ya.‚ÄĚ 8KeŇčte Yesu m…Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa mena na, maŇč m…Ē ba wola Ňčon me doŇč keŇč da lee ya.‚ÄĚ
Paasetena baale dudu
Mateo 21:33-46; Maake 12:1-12
9Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu gy…Ē…Ē dudu kei fa balaŇč baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúW…Ē…Ēfarale Ňčolo fara w…Ē…Ē te …Ē duu daasebia Ňčaale Ňčan Ňče y…õ…õ Ňčgba akutuse te b…õ…õ baake Ňča nyi gerepo n…õ, te …Ē moo Ňča do paasetena baale nyiŇčmaa man nyi baa k…õ…õ Ňče d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē korow…Ē gyu t…õ…õle w…Ēle daale man lemlem ke kyaa bot…Ē…Ē kyare. 10DebaŇč kelii nyi b…õ…õ k…Ē daasebia Ňčan n…õ, …Ē kpila …Ē tobaale Ňčolo gyu paasetena baŇč gyaŇč nyi baa fa e …Ē wui n…Ē…Ē. Bot…Ē…Ē te paasetena baŇč tuku tobaale Ňčon, te be tina e yela …Ē laŇčaw…Ē naw…Ē…Ē kufu nyiŇčmaase kufu. 11W…Ē…Ēte Ňčon besew…Ē kpila tobaale w…Ēle bela, m…Ēna be tuku Ňčon m…Ē do e des…õŇč te be yela e …Ē laŇčaw…Ē naw…Ē…Ē kufu nyiŇčmaase kufu. 12Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē besew…Ē kpila tobaale batoorote, m…Ēna be tuku Ňčon m…Ē do e gyam…õ…õ te be gegi e lee w…Ē…Ē keŇč man.‚ÄĚ
13KeŇčte w…Ē…Ēte Ňčon yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč te maa y…õ…õ? Maa kpila maŇč gbagba me t…Ē…Ēman lolo bu, mberem Ňčon na baa bu e.‚ÄĚ
14M…Ēna paasetena baŇč kena bu Ňčon n…õ be yako d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúKei y…õna bu Ňčon waa di …Ē kya ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan. ∆ź yela d…õ…õ ko e na wa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan ka bese de wui.‚ÄĚ 15Mena d…Ē…Ē be w…ĒŇčee e lee w…Ē…Ē keŇč man ka ko.

english_text:
A Widow's Offering
(Mark 12.41-44)
1Jesus looked up and saw some rich people tossing their gifts into the offering box. 2He also saw a poor widow putting in a few cents. 3And he said, ‚ÄúI tell you that this poor woman has put in more than all the others. 4Everyone else gave what they didn't need. But she is very poor and gave everything she had.‚ÄĚ
The Temple Will Be Destroyed
(Matthew 24.1,2; Mark 13.1,2)
5Some people were talking about the beautiful stones used to build the temple and about the gifts that had been placed in it. Jesus said, 6‚ÄúDo you see these stones? The time is coming when not one of them will be left in place. They will all be knocked down.‚ÄĚ
Warning about Trouble
(Matthew 24.3-14; Mark 13.3-13)
7 Some people asked, ‚ÄúTeacher, when will all this happen? How can we know when these things are about to take place?‚ÄĚ
8Jesus replied:
Don't be fooled by those who will come and claim to be me. They will say, ‚ÄúI am Christ!‚ÄĚ and ‚ÄúNow is the time!‚ÄĚ But don't follow them. 9When you hear about wars and riots, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but this isn't the end.
10Nations will go to war against one another, and kingdoms will attack each other. 11There will be great earthquakes, and in many places people will starve to death and suffer terrible diseases. All sorts of frightening things will be seen in the sky.
12Before all this happens, you will be arrested and punished. You will be tried in your synagogues and put in jail. Because of me you will be placed on trial before kings and governors. 13But this will be your chance to tell about your faith.
14 Don't worry about what you will say to defend yourselves. 15I will give you the wisdom to know what to say. None of your enemies will be able to oppose you or to say that you are wrong. 16You will be betrayed by your own parents, brothers, family, and friends. Some of you will even be killed. 17Because of me, you will be hated by everyone. 18But don't worry! 19You will be saved by being faithful to me.
Jerusalem Will Be Destroyed
(Matthew 24.15-21; Mark 13.14-19)
20When you see Jerusalem surrounded by soldiers, you will know that it will soon be destroyed. 21If you are living in Judea at this time, run to the mountains. If you are in the city, leave it. And if you are out in the country, don't go back into the city. 22 This time of punishment is what is written about in the Scriptures. 23It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children! Everywhere in the land people will suffer horribly and be punished. 24Some of them will be killed by swords. Others will be carried off to foreign countries. Jerusalem will be overrun by foreign nations until their time comes to an end.
When the Son of Man Appears
(Matthew 24.29-31; Mark 13.24-27)
25 Strange things will happen to the sun, moon, and stars. The nations on earth will be afraid of the roaring sea and tides, and they won't know what to do. 26People will be so frightened that they will faint because of what is happening to the world. Every power in the sky will be shaken. 27 Then the Son of Man will be seen, coming in a cloud with power and great glory. 28When all of this starts happening, stand up straight and be brave. You will soon be set free.
A Lesson from a Fig Tree
(Matthew 24.32-35; Mark 13.28-31)
29Then Jesus told them a story:
When you see a fig tree or any other tree 30putting out leaves, you know that summer will soon come. 31So, when you see these things happening, you know that God's kingdom will soon be here. 32You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all of this takes place. 33The sky and the earth won't last forever, but my words will.
A Warning
34Don't spend all of your time thinking about eating or drinking or worrying about life. If you do, the final day will suddenly catch you 35like a trap. This day will surprise everyone on earth. 36Watch out and keep praying that you can escape all that is going to happen and that the Son of Man will be pleased with you.
37 Jesus taught in the temple each day, and he spent each night on the Mount of Olives. 38Everyone got up early and came to the temple to hear him teach.

Mateo 24:32-35; Maake 13:28-31
29KeŇčte Yesu gy…Ē…Ē dudu fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ fiigi daŇč keŇč na daase akaŇč Ňčan pou. 30Akpaa …õ naa Ňčgba Ňče tombia ne p…Ēr…Ēkee na, …õ gyeŇč nyi dekpao ta benaa. 31Mena m…Ē te akpaa …õ naa mena ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča ne k…ĒŇč na, …õ gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč ta benaa. 32AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyiaa, mena duuluŇč kei be gyae ke munu p…õte ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča pou ke k…ĒŇč ya. 33Adido na t…õ…õle kei pou gyae ke gyu …Ēto, m…Ēna me n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan d…Ē…Ē Ňčan gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.‚ÄĚ
34‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ …õ wose d…Ē…Ē nideli! ∆ź na yeli t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē kegyae ka bere …õ sia ya. ∆ź na mo soloŇč keny…Ē…Ē do …õ wose ya. ∆ź na gyueŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē nombia beiŇč, na wee keŇč be nyiŇč k…ĒŇč kaa futaa Ňčon ya. 35Nawolo nyi mena wee kenaŇč gyae ke k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei balaŇč pou d…Ē…Ē. 36Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ k…õ…õ …õ wose d…Ē…Ē nideli debaŇč kamas…õ na …õ ke fane na …õ kenyiŇč tale lese …õ wose lee nombia Ňčan pou gyae ke k…ĒŇč n…õ man, na …õ ke nyiŇč tale seŇč maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon siaman.‚ÄĚ
37Wee kamas…õ na, Yesu na wolo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man te balin…Ē…Ē de lii na …Ē ne gyu ke doo oliifi bula keŇč d…Ē…Ē. 38T…õ…õ de naa nebo nebo na, balaŇč baŇč pou ne gyu ke yilaa Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man na baa nyii …Ē nombia Ňčan.

english_text:
A Plot To Kill Jesus
(Matthew 26.1-5,14,16; Mark 14.1,2,10,11; John 11.45-53)
1 The Festival of Thin Bread, also called Passover, was near. 2The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were looking for a way to get rid of Jesus, because they were afraid of what the people might do. 3Then Satan entered the heart of Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve apostles.
4Judas went to talk with the chief priests and the officers of the temple police about how he could help them arrest Jesus. 5They were very pleased and offered to pay Judas some money. 6He agreed and started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus when the crowds were not around.
Jesus Eats with His Disciples
(Matthew 26.17-25; Mark 14.12-21; John 13.21-30)
7The day had come for the Festival of Thin Bread, and it was time to kill the Passover lambs. 8So Jesus said to Peter and John, ‚ÄúGo and prepare the Passover meal for us to eat.‚ÄĚ
9But they asked, ‚ÄúWhere do you want us to prepare it?‚ÄĚ
10Jesus told them, ‚ÄúAs you go into the city, you will meet a man carrying a jar of water. Follow him into the house 11and say to the owner, ‚ÄėOur teacher wants to know where he can eat the Passover meal with his disciples.‚Äô 12The owner will take you upstairs and show you a large room ready for you to use. Prepare the meal there.‚ÄĚ
13Peter and John left. They found everything just as Jesus had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
The Lord's Supper
(Matthew 26.26-30; Mark 14.22-26; 1 Corinthians 11.23-25)
14When the time came for Jesus and the apostles to eat, 15he said to them, ‚ÄúI have very much wanted to eat this Passover meal with you before I suffer. 16I tell you I will not eat another Passover meal until it is finally eaten in God's kingdom.‚ÄĚ
17Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he told the apostles, ‚ÄúTake this wine and share it with each other. 18I tell you that I will not drink any more wine until God's kingdom comes.‚ÄĚ
19Jesus took some bread in his hands and gave thanks for it. He broke the bread and handed it to his apostles. Then he said, ‚ÄúThis is my body, which is given for you. Eat this as a way of remembering me!‚ÄĚ
20 After the meal he took another cup of wine in his hands. Then he said, ‚ÄúThis is my blood. It is poured out for you, and with it God makes his new agreement. 21 The one who will betray me is here at the table with me! 22The Son of Man will die in the way that has been decided for him, but it will be terrible for the one who betrays him!‚ÄĚ
23Then the apostles started arguing about who would ever do such a thing.
An Argument about Greatness
24 The apostles got into an argument about which one of them was the greatest. 25 So Jesus told them:
Foreign kings order their people around, and powerful rulers call themselves everyone's friends. 26 But don't be like them. The most important one of you should be like the least important, and your leader should be like a servant. 27 Who do people think is the greatest, a person who is served or one who serves? Isn't it the one who is served? But I have been with you as a servant.
28You have stayed with me in all my troubles. 29So I will give you the right to rule as kings, just as my Father has given me the right to rule as a king. 30 You will eat and drink with me in my kingdom, and you will each sit on a throne to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
Jesus' Disciples Will Be Tested
(Matthew 26.31-35; Mark 14.27-31; John 13.36-38)
31Jesus said, ‚ÄúSimon, listen to me! Satan has demanded the right to test each one of you, as a farmer does when he separates wheat from the husks. 32But Simon, I have prayed that your faith will be strong. And when you have come back to me, help the others.‚ÄĚ
33Peter said, ‚ÄúLord, I am ready to go with you to jail and even to die with you.‚ÄĚ
34Jesus replied, ‚ÄúPeter, I tell you that before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.‚ÄĚ
Moneybags, Traveling Bags, and Swords
35 Jesus asked his disciples, ‚ÄúWhen I sent you out without a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals, did you need anything?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúNo!‚ÄĚ they answered.
36Jesus told them, ‚ÄúBut now, if you have a moneybag, take it with you. Also take a traveling bag, and if you don't have a sword, sell some of your clothes and buy one. 37 Do this because the Scriptures say, ‚ÄėHe was considered a criminal.‚Äô This was written about me, and it will soon come true.‚ÄĚ
38The disciples said, ‚ÄúLord, here are two swords!‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúEnough of that!‚ÄĚ Jesus replied.
Jesus Prays
(Matthew 26.36-46; Mark 14.32-42)
39Jesus went out to the Mount of Olives, as he often did, and his disciples went with him. 40When they got there, he told them, ‚ÄúPray that you won't be tested.‚ÄĚ
41Jesus walked on a little way before he knelt down and prayed, 42‚ÄúFather, if you will, please don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.‚ÄĚ
43Then an angel from heaven came to help him. 44Jesus was in great pain and prayed so sincerely that his sweat fell to the ground like drops of blood.
45Jesus got up from praying and went over to his disciples. They were asleep and worn out from being so sad. 46He said to them, ‚ÄúWhy are you asleep? Wake up and pray that you won't be tested.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is Arrested
(Matthew 26.47-56; Mark 14.43-50; John 18.3-11)
47While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd came up. It was led by Judas, one of the twelve apostles. He went over to Jesus and greeted him with a kiss.
48Jesus asked Judas, ‚ÄúAre you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?‚ÄĚ
49When Jesus' disciples saw what was about to happen, they asked, ‚ÄúLord, should we attack them with a sword?‚ÄĚ 50One of the disciples even struck at the high priest's servant with his sword and cut off the servant's right ear.
51‚ÄúEnough of that!‚ÄĚ Jesus said. Then he touched the servant's ear and healed it.
52Jesus spoke to the chief priests, the temple police, and the leaders who had come to arrest him. He said, ‚ÄúWhy do you come out with swords and clubs and treat me like a criminal? 53 I was with you every day in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. But this is your time, and darkness is in control.‚ÄĚ
Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus
(Matthew 26.57,58,67-75; Mark 14.53,54,66-72; John 18.12-18,25-27)
54Jesus was arrested and led away to the house of the high priest, while Peter followed at a distance. 55Some people built a fire in the middle of the courtyard and were sitting around it. Peter sat there with them, 56and a servant girl saw him. Then after she had looked at him carefully, she said, ‚ÄúThis man was with Jesus!‚ÄĚ
57Peter said, ‚ÄúWoman, I don't even know that man!‚ÄĚ
58A little later someone else saw Peter and said, ‚ÄúYou are one of them!‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúNo, I'm not!‚ÄĚ Peter replied.
59About an hour later another man insisted, ‚ÄúThis man must have been with Jesus. They both come from Galilee.‚ÄĚ
60Peter replied, ‚ÄúI don't know what you are talking about!‚ÄĚ Right then, while Peter was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
61The Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered that the Lord had said, ‚ÄúBefore a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.‚ÄĚ 62Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
63The men who were guarding Jesus made fun of him and beat him. 64They put a blindfold on him and said, ‚ÄúTell us who struck you!‚ÄĚ 65They kept on insulting Jesus in many other ways.
Jesus Is Questioned by the Council
(Matthew 26.59-66; Mark 14.55-64; John 18.19-24)
66At daybreak the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses got together and brought Jesus before their council. 67They said, ‚ÄúTell us! Are you the Messiah?‚ÄĚ
Jesus replied, ‚ÄúIf I said so, you wouldn't believe me. 68And if I asked you a question, you wouldn't answer. 69But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right side of God All-Powerful.‚ÄĚ
70Then they asked, ‚ÄúAre you the Son of God?‚ÄĚ
Jesus answered, ‚ÄúYou say I am!‚ÄĚ
71They replied, ‚ÄúWhy do we need more witnesses? He said it himself!‚ÄĚ

7Gyudatena wee dinaa keŇč bee di bodobodo keŇč bodobodo tee be doo ke man ya, te b…õ…õ mo nyeekel…õ…õ nambu Ňčon m…Ē deesi kelii n…õ, 8Yesu kpila Pita na Gy…Ēn nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź gyu ke desina ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan d…õ…õ mo di nyeekel…õ…õ ween…õ…õ Ňčan n…õ see.‚ÄĚ 9Bot…Ē…Ē te be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAleŇč te n ne gyae de kaa desina see?‚ÄĚ 10KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź betaa …õ na gyoo don…Ē…Ē keŇč man n…õ, …õ kena balee Ňčolo seelena loŇč na ogele. ∆ź sila e gyu dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man waa gyoo n…õ, 11na …õ ke yako dekpaŇčalaŇčte Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄėDe GbeŇčgyoo Ňčon kpa d…õ…õ b…Ē…Ēse neŇč nyi aleŇč te Ňč…Ē…Ēla deni keŇč daa na me kaseela d…õ…õ kaa di nyeekel…õ…õwee ween…õ…õ Ňčan n…õ kyaa?‚Äô 12Balee ŇčonaŇč waa wola Ňčon abansoro deni dinaa daale keŇč ke kyaa adido te b…Ē…Ē desina ke man nideli n…õ. ∆ź desina bot…Ē…Ē na de kaa di ween…õ…õ Ňčan.‚ÄĚ

Mateo 26:31-35; Maake 14:27-31; Gy…Ēn 13:36-38
31KeŇčte Yesu yako Sim…Ēn Pita nyi ‚ÄúSim…Ēn, Sim…Ēn nyii! ∆Üb…Ēnsam ta sola gb…õ…õ lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč waa k…õ…õse …õ popou k…õ…õ na waa kpase …õ man Ňčgba mena keŇč bee feefu am…Ēla lese bia n…õ. 32M…Ēna ma te fane fa nyaŇč Sim…Ēn na n kel…õ…õdi be y…Ēk…Ēse ya. 35Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúDebaŇč keŇč m…Ē…Ē kpila Ňčon keŇč …õ ta mo k…Ēba yaa akpo, yaa …õ ta mo nkyokotase tela ya n…õ kolo hia Ňčon?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte be tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKoa, kolo te hia daa ya.‚ÄĚ 36KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē nyaŇč Ňčon n dana k…Ēba yaa akpo na mo tela, te nyaŇč Ňčon n be dana sila ya na y…Ē…Ē n kegba na n ka mo ke l…õ…õ daale. 37Nawolo nyi ba te Ňčmarase ma wose man nombia see nyiaa, ‚ÄėBa te kala e do nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra man,‚Äô te m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase see lee ma wose man n…õ, kaboena nyi Ňče d…õ…õ k…ĒŇč mena. Yesu kefane G…õky…õmane
Mateo 26:36-46; Maake 14:32-42
39KeŇčte Yesu leew…Ē don…Ē…Ē keŇč man gyu oliifi bula keŇč d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē ne kyaa gyu n…õ, te …Ē kaseela baŇč sila e gyu. 40Be kelii bot…Ē…Ē n…õ Yesu yako …Ē kaseela baŇč nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź fane na …õ be nyiŇč gyoo kek…õ…õsek…õ…õ man ya.‚ÄĚ 41KeŇčte …Ē w…ĒŇča …Ē wose lee yenaŇč gyu siaman kyomii te …Ē ke buŇčaw…Ē fane nyiaa, 42‚ÄúTaata, akpaa ke y…õ…õ n kegyaebii ke na, lese maŇč lee kaale kei man. M…Ēna na y…õ…õ maŇč kegyaebii ya, y…õ…õ nyaŇč gbagba n kegyaebii.‚ÄĚ 43Bot…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ kpilale lee adido k…ĒŇč kaa do e kakyeŇč. 44Yesu k…õ…õ yey…õ…õ keŇč n…õ, …Ē kyaŇča …Ē wose fane nideli te dufuŇč keŇč ba lee e n…õ ky…Ēseew…Ē yala t…õ…õle man Ňčgba fatabo n…õ. 45∆Ü ke fane taŇč keŇč n…õ te …Ē besew…Ē gyu …Ē kaseela baŇč gyaŇč. ∆Ü ke gyu keŇč na …Ē ke naaw…Ē Ňčgba …Ē kaseela baŇč te dokii, nawolo nyi dek…Ēk…Ēr…Ēk…Ē…Ē te kyaŇč w…Ē. 46KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera …õ ne doo? ∆ź koro na …õ ke fane na …õ be nyiŇč gyoo kek…õ…õsek…õ…õ man ya.‚ÄĚ
Ba kekyaŇč Yesu
Mateo 26:47-56; Maake 14:43-50; Gy…Ēn 18:2-11
47Yesu ka seŇč …Ē ne kolosi n…õ balaŇč dikpii baale kaa b…õ…õw…Ē lee …Ē d…Ē…Ē, na Gyudase Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ Yesu kaseela kufu bala baŇč man Ňčolo e n…õ d…ĒŇč…õ…õ la be siaman. ∆Ü kena Yesu n…õ te …Ē k…ĒŇčaw…Ē …Ē kaa pukaa e. 48KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúGyudase, kepukaa te nyaŇč ka mo lese maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon fa?‚ÄĚ
49Yesu kaseela baŇč kena kpene keŇč ne gyae ke k…ĒŇč n…õ te be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, d…õ…õ mo de silase na dee yoo?‚ÄĚ 50Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu kaseela baŇč man Ňčolo moo …Ē yoosila kara saese kegyia tobaale Ňčolo dunoluŇč deŇčele lese ke l…Ē. 51KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź tina yela, …õ na y…õ…õ mena ya.‚ÄĚ Te …Ē tinaa balee Ňčon deŇčele keŇč te ka towaaw…Ē bela. 52KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse saese kegyiise na Wurubuar…õ d…õ…õ kot…Ēt…Ē…Ētena kegyiise na kegyiise baŇč b…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč bot…Ē…Ē be kaa kyaŇč e n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera te …õ moo yoosilase na akpokpose …õ ne k…ĒŇč me d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba me y…õ…õ walaŇčkum e n…õ? 53Wee kamas…õ daa na Ňčon ne kyaa la Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man, m…Ēna …õ te kyaŇč maŇč ya. N…õn…õ…õ te gb…õ…õ torow…Ē fa Ňčon nyi yaa kyaŇč maŇč, na …õ ke y…õ…õ …Ēb…Ēnsam ditiŇčt…õ…õ gyoo Ňčon kegyaebii.‚ÄĚ
Pita keb…õ…õ Yesu
Mateo 26:57-58, 69-75; Maake 14:53-54, 66-72; Gy…Ēn 18:12-18, 25-27
54Bot…Ē…Ē te be kyaŇča Yesu mo e gyu Wurubuar…õ saese kegyia Ňčon dekpaŇčalaŇč man, na Pita m…Ē silana w…Ē w…Ēle w…Ēle. 55KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč doo boalaŇč dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč nsana nsana te be yilaaw…Ē ke gyaŇč bee woli, te Pita m…Ē gyuuw…Ē ke kyaa be gyaŇč. 56Alebia baŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ tom dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man n…õ Ňčolo naa Pita kyaa boalaŇč keŇč gyaŇč, te …Ē gyene e nideli te …Ē yakow…Ē nyi ‚ÄúNa balee kei m…Ē kpu…õ na Yesu!‚ÄĚ 57KeŇčte Pita l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúAlebu, m…Ē…Ē gyeŇč e ya.‚ÄĚ 58Te kyare ya te Ňčolo m…Ē naa e te …Ē yako e bela nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč m…Ē n y…õ…õ be man Ňčolo e!‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Pita tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBaale, m…Ē…Ē kpu…õ na w…Ē ya!‚ÄĚ 59DebaŇč dokoloŇč daale w…Ēle n…õ, Ňčolo m…Ē kaa yase …Ē sia do Pita ŇčonaŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúKe y…õ…õ ampaŇč kaeŇč nyi balee kei kpu…õ na Yesu, nawolo nyi …Ē y…õ…õ Galelia baale e!‚ÄĚ 60Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBaale, m…Ē…Ē gyeŇč nombia Ňčan n ne yako n…õ man ya.‚ÄĚ Pita ke gyakaa …Ē ne kolosi n…õ te kegyim l…Ē…Ē woya. 61Bot…Ē…Ē te de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu g…õ…õsew…Ē k…õ…õ Pita, te Pita t…Ē…Ēse de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nombia Ňčan …Ē be yako e n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúP…õ kegyim ke l…Ē woya gy…õŇč na, nyaŇč ke b…õ…õ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŇč maŇč ya n…õ.‚ÄĚ 62KeŇčte Pita leew…Ē deb…Ē…Ē man gyu ke kyaŇč …Ē nyee wii nideli.
Be ketuku Yesu
Mateo 26:67-68; Maake 14:65
63BalaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ k…õ…õ Yesu d…Ē…Ē n…õ fias…õ…õ bee kulu e na bee tuku e. 64Ba moo kanyaŇč bake …Ē sia na b…õ…õ takee e ak…Ētase na b…õ…õ b…Ē…Ēse e nyi ‚ÄúYako daa, am…Ēte gy…Ē…Ē na neŇč?‚ÄĚ 65Te ba tee e tuubia kpaŇč kpaŇč kpu.
Be kamo Yesu gyu don…Ē…Ē kegyiise siaman
Mateo 26:59-66; Maake 14:55-64; Gy…Ēn 18:19-24
66T…õ…õ kena keŇč n…õ, don…Ē…Ē kegyiise na Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč gyaŇčeew…Ē te ba moo Yesu k…ĒŇč kaa seŇč be siaman. 67KeŇčte be yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa nyaŇč y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ na yako daa dee nyii.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa me yako Ňčon na …õ be gyae …õ ke l…õ…õ maŇč di ya, 68te akpaa me b…Ē…Ēse Ňčon nombia m…Ē n…õ …õ be gyae …õ ke tiran…Ē…Ē fa maŇč ya. 69M…Ēna lee debaŇč kei ne gyu n…õ, maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon maa kyaa Wurubuar…õ Kpatakpere Ňčon dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 70KeŇčte be popou b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa mena na nyaŇč y…õna Wurubuar…õ bu Ňčon?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúMena yaa …õ ne yako n…õ.‚ÄĚ 71KeŇčte be yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč nombia yaa d…õ…õ gyae d…õ…õ bese nyii kpu bela. Daa gbagba te nyii lee Ňčon gbagba n…Ē…Ēman!‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Pilate Questions Jesus
(Matthew 27.1,2,11-14; Mark 15.1-5; John 18.28-38)
1Everyone in the council got up and led Jesus off to Pilate. 2They started accusing him and said, ‚ÄúWe caught this man trying to get our people to riot and to stop paying taxes to the Emperor. He also claims that he is the Messiah, our king.‚ÄĚ
3Pilate asked Jesus, ‚ÄúAre you the king of the Jews?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThose are your words,‚ÄĚ Jesus answered.
4Pilate told the chief priests and the crowd, ‚ÄúI don't find him guilty of anything.‚ÄĚ
5But they all kept on saying, ‚ÄúHe has been teaching and causing trouble all over Judea. He started in Galilee and has now come all the way here.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is Brought before Herod
6When Pilate heard this, he asked, ‚ÄúIs this man from Galilee?‚ÄĚ 7After Pilate learned that Jesus came from the region ruled by Herod, he sent him to Herod, who was in Jerusalem at that time.
8For a long time Herod had wanted to see Jesus and was very happy because he finally had this chance. He had heard many things about Jesus and hoped to see him work a miracle.
9Herod asked him a lot of questions, but Jesus did not answer. 10Then the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses stood up and accused him of all kinds of bad things.
11Herod and his soldiers made fun of Jesus and insulted him. They put a fine robe on him and sent him back to Pilate. 12That same day Herod and Pilate became friends, even though they had been enemies before this.
The Death Sentence
(Matthew 27.15-26; Mark 15.6-15; John 18.39‚ÄĒ19.16)
13Pilate called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people. 14He told them, ‚ÄúYou brought Jesus to me and said he was a troublemaker. But I have questioned him here in front of you, and I have not found him guilty of anything that you say he has done. 15Herod didn't find him guilty either and sent him back. This man doesn't deserve to be put to death! 16-17I will just have him beaten with a whip and set free.‚ÄĚ
18But the whole crowd shouted, ‚ÄúKill Jesus! Give us Barabbas!‚ÄĚ 19Now Barabbas was in jail because he had started a riot in the city and had murdered someone.
20Pilate wanted to set Jesus free, so he spoke again to the crowds. 21But they kept shouting, ‚ÄúNail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!‚ÄĚ
22Pilate spoke to them a third time, ‚ÄúBut what crime has he done? I have not found him guilty of anything for which he should be put to death. I will have him beaten with a whip and set free.‚ÄĚ
23The people kept on shouting as loud as they could for Jesus to be put to death. 24Finally, Pilate gave in. 25He freed the man who was in jail for rioting and murder, because he was the one the crowd wanted to be set free. Then Pilate handed Jesus over for them to do what they wanted with him.
Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross
(Matthew 27.31-44; Mark 15.21-32; John 19.17-27)
26As Jesus was being led away, some soldiers grabbed hold of a man named Simon who was from Cyrene. He was coming in from the fields, but they put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.
27A large crowd was following Jesus, and in the crowd a lot of women were crying and weeping for him. 28Jesus turned to the women and said:
Women of Jerusalem, don't cry for me! Cry for yourselves and for your children. 29Someday people will say, ‚ÄúWomen who never had children are really fortunate!‚ÄĚ 30 At that time everyone will say to the mountains, ‚ÄúFall on us!‚ÄĚ They will say to the hills, ‚ÄúHide us!‚ÄĚ 31If this can happen when the wood is green, what do you think will happen when it is dry?
32Two criminals were led out to be put to death with Jesus. 33When the soldiers came to the place called ‚ÄúThe Skull,‚ÄĚ they nailed Jesus to a cross. They also nailed the two criminals to crosses, one on each side of Jesus.
34-35 Jesus said, ‚ÄúFather, forgive these people! They don't know what they're doing.‚ÄĚ
While the crowd stood there watching Jesus, the soldiers gambled for his clothes. The leaders insulted him by saying, ‚ÄúHe saved others. Now he should save himself, if he really is God's chosen Messiah!‚ÄĚ
36 The soldiers made fun of Jesus and brought him some wine. 37They said, ‚ÄúIf you are the king of the Jews, save yourself!‚ÄĚ
38Above him was a sign that said, ‚ÄúThis is the King of the Jews.‚ÄĚ
39One of the criminals hanging there also insulted Jesus by saying, ‚ÄúAren't you the Messiah? Save yourself and save us!‚ÄĚ
40But the other criminal told the first one off, ‚ÄúDon't you fear God? Aren't you getting the same punishment as this man? 41We got what was coming to us, but he didn't do anything wrong.‚ÄĚ 42Then he said to Jesus, ‚ÄúRemember me when you come into power!‚ÄĚ
43Jesus replied, ‚ÄúI promise that today you will be with me in paradise.‚ÄĚ
The Death of Jesus
(Matthew 27.45-56; Mark 15.33-41; John 19.28-30)
44Around noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until the middle of the afternoon. 45 The sun stopped shining, and the curtain in the temple split down the middle. 46 Jesus shouted, ‚ÄúFather, I put myself in your hands!‚ÄĚ Then he died.
47When the Roman officer saw what had happened, he praised God and said, ‚ÄúJesus must really have been a good man!‚ÄĚ
48A crowd had gathered to see the terrible sight. Then after they had seen it, they felt brokenhearted and went home. 49 All of Jesus' close friends and the women who had come with him from Galilee stood at a distance and watched.
Jesus Is Buried
(Matthew 27.57-61; Mark 15.42-47; John 19.38-42)
50-51There was a man named Joseph, who was from Arimathea in Judea. Joseph was a good and honest man, and he was eager for God's kingdom to come. He was also a member of the council, but he did not agree with what they had decided.
52Joseph went to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body. 53He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in fine cloth. Then he put it in a tomb that had been cut out of solid rock and had never been used. 54It was Friday, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
55The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and watched how Jesus' body was placed in the tomb. 56 Then they went to prepare some sweet-smelling spices for his burial. But on the Sabbath they rested, as the Law of Moses commands.
translation_text:

Mateo 27:1-2, 11-31; Maake 15:1-5; Gy…Ēn 18:28-38
1KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee bot…Ē…Ē n…õ pou korow…Ē mo Yesu gyu Pilato Ňčon …Ē seŇč…õ…õ Romantena gyoo dinaa naw…Ē…Ē man n…õ gyaŇč, 2te be fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ mo nombia gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe naa balee kei d…ĒŇč de balaŇč siaman …Ē na wola w…Ē nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ. ∆Ü ne b…õ…õ nyi be na t…Ē lempoo fa gyoo dinaa Kaesa ya, te …Ē ne gy…Ē …Ē kon…Ē…Ē yako nyi Ňčon y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ, te …Ē y…õ…õ gyoo m…Ē e.‚ÄĚ 3Bot…Ē…Ē te Pilato b…Ē…Ēse Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč y…õna Gyudatena gyoo Ňčon?‚ÄĚ Te Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúMena yaa n yakow…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ 4KeŇčte Pilato yako saese kegyiise baŇč na balaŇč dikpii keŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúMa te naa nombiikum keŇč balee kei be y…õ…õ keŇč kaboena nyi maa bu e f…Ē…Ē ya.‚ÄĚ 5M…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ gba te ba seŇč…õ…õ be n…Ē…Ēwoya d…Ē…Ē keŇčkeŇč nyi, ‚Äú∆Ü ta mo …Ē kawola keŇč w…Ēl…õ…õ balaŇč agyueŇč Gyudia t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē pou. ∆Ü fias…õ…õ lee Galelia t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē te n…õn…õ…õ …Ē te kaa lii de gyaŇč k…õbo.‚ÄĚ
6Pilato ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ …Ē b…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee kei y…õ…õ Galelia baale e?‚ÄĚ 7Pilato ka gyeŇč nyi Yesu lee gyoo Herode t…õ…õle man n…õ, …Ē yela ba mo e gyu gyoo Herode gyaŇč. DebaŇč kenaŇč na gyoo Herode m…Ē kyaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man bot…Ē…Ē. 8Gyoo Herode kena Yesu n…õ y…õ…õ e gyoŇč nideli, nawolo nyi …Ē wulaa nyii Yesu wose man nombia te …Ē ne gyae e m…Ē waa naa. Lee mena keŇč …Ē be nyii Yesu wose man nombia d…Ē…Ē n…õ, wa agyueŇč y…õna nyi waa y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia wola e. 9Mena d…Ē…Ē gyoo Herode b…Ē…Ēse Yesu nombia burum, m…Ēna …Ē te tira Ňče man dokoloŇč n…Ē…Ē ya. 10Bot…Ē…Ē na saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŇč seŇč…õ…õ keŇčkeŇč b…õ…õ k…õr…õ…õ nombia dinaana gyakaa Yesu d…Ē…Ē. 11KeŇčte gyoo Herode na …Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč kulu Yesu te ba do e des…õŇč. Ba doo e gyooro kegba te …Ē yela ba besena e gyina Pilato. 12Lee wee kenaŇč te gyoo Herode na Pilato nsana besew…Ē de, naf…Ē na b…Ē…Ē na gyoo ya.
13Bot…Ē…Ē te Pilato baake saese kegyiise baŇč na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise na balaŇč baŇč pou gyaŇčee, 14te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ moona balee kei k…ĒŇč me gyaŇč te …õ kpa …Ē na beo balaŇč baŇč. MaŇč gyoo ke man …õ siaman k…õbo m…Ēna ma te naa nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan …Ē be y…õ…õ te …õ ne kara …Ē n…Ē…Ē ya. 15Gyoo Herode m…Ē te naa nombiikum lee …Ē wose man ya, te …Ē yela ba besena e k…Ēna daa. N k…õ…õ paa na balee kei te y…õ…õ kolo kum keŇč kaboena nyi baa ko e ya. 16Mena d…Ē…Ē maa yela baa tuku e na baa tina e yela.‚ÄĚ 17Na ke y…õ…õ ba mmaraa ke nyi ba nyeekel…õ…õwee kedi kamas…õ na, kaboena nyi Pilato waa tina balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyam t…Ē deni man n…õ, be man Ňčolo Ňčon baŇč Gyudatena ne gyae n…õ yela fa w…Ē.
18Bot…Ē…Ē te balaŇč dikpii keŇč pou fae yeesa nedoŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúKo balee kei na n ke tina Barabase yela fa daa.‚ÄĚ 19KeŇč d…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ē kyaŇč Barabase t…Ē deni y…õna nyi, Ňčon t…õ…õ la balaŇč siaman te ba korow…Ē seŇč tia kegyiise te …Ē koo walaŇč nombia ŇčenaŇč man. 20KeŇč Pilato ne gyae waa tina Yesu yela d…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ē besew…Ē kolosi fa balaŇč baŇč. 21M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč gyakaa b…õ…õ fae yeesa nyiaa, ‚ÄúYela baa gy…Ē e sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē‚ÄĚ 22KeŇčte Pilato besew…Ē kolosi fa w…Ē betabotoorote nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč nombiikum yaa balee kei y…õ…õw…Ē? MaŇč te naa kpene keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ keŇč kaboena nyi baa ko e ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē maa yela baa tuku e na maa tina e yela.‚ÄĚ 23M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč seŇč…õ…õ keŇčkeŇč b…õ…õ fae yeesa nedoŇč nyi baa gy…Ē e sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē, te be yeesa Ňčan kela Pilato. 24Mena d…Ē…Ē Pilato y…õ…õ wa agyueŇč nyi waa y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč kegyaebii fa w…Ē. 25KeŇčte Pilato tinaa Barabase Ňčon b…Ē…Ē kyam t…Ē deni n…õ yela fa w…Ē. Barabase kei t…õ…õ la balaŇč baale siaman te ba korow…Ē seŇč tia kegyiise te …Ē koo walaŇč m…Ē. Ňäon y…õna walaŇč Ňčon balaŇč baŇč be yako nyi waa tina e yela fa w…Ē n…õ. Te …Ē moo Yesu fa w…Ē nyi baa gyu ke y…õ…õ e ba kegyaebii.

Mateo 27:32-44; Maake 15:21-32; Gy…Ēn 19:17-27
26Be ka mo Yesu lee Pilato gyaŇč bee gyuu n…õ, be gyaŇčee na balee Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Sim…Ēn Ňčon …Ē ba lee Kirene don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, na …Ē ta lee akuraa …Ē ne k…ĒŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man. KeŇčte be kyaŇča e te ba Ňčere e mo …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč seele e na …Ē silana Yesu w…Ēle w…Ēle.

M…Ēna …õ wii fa …õ wose na …õ bia. 29Nawolo nyi debaŇč daale ne k…ĒŇč keŇč …õ ke yako nyiaa, ‚Äė∆Ülamanbose, …õm…õ…õ baŇč bia te doo …õ t…Ē…Ēman see ya te bia te Ňčmase ya ade ya n…õ, …õm…õ…õ na nyeebam.‚Äô

‚Äė∆ź kaa Ňčere daa,‚Äô
na baa yako bulasebia m…Ē nyiaa,

31Akpaa daŇč laŇčte ne kpaa la nn…õ na, sena te daŇč dowolo gyae ke y…õ…õ?‚ÄĚ 32KeŇčte ba moo nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra bala baale m…Ē kaa kpu na Yesu na be kaa ko w…Ē.
33Be kelii bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi ‚ÄúNyeepookoo d…õ…õ‚ÄĚ n…õ, be gy…Ē…Ē Yesu sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē. KeŇčte be gy…Ē…Ē nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra bala baŇč m…Ē sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaase kpu na e. Ňäolo sakaa …Ē dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē te Ňčolo m…Ē sakaa …Ē demena d…Ē…Ē. 34KeŇčte Yesu yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúTaata! Mo balaŇč k…õw…Ē nombiakum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ w…Ē, nawolo nyi b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč kpene keŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ ya.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte be l…Ē…Ē boe mo kpala Yesu kegbase. 35Na balaŇč burum seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ k…õ…õ te Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč m…Ē kulu e nyiaa, ‚Äú∆Ü ne l…õ…õ balaŇč nyee, …õ yela waa l…õ…õ …Ē wose na d…õ…õ k…õ…õ. Ňägba Ňčon kpa Ňčon y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ.‚ÄĚ 36KeŇčte yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč m…Ē kulu e te be faa e soloŇč keŇč k…Ē…Ē nyaŇče n…õ, 37te be yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa nyaŇč y…õna Gyudatena gyoo Ňčon na, kenaŇč na l…õ…õ n wose!‚ÄĚ 38Te be Ňčmarase nombia k…õŇča see …Ē nyee adido nyiaa,
‚Äúkei y…õna gyudatena gyoo Ňčon.‚ÄĚ

44Weese ka seŇč d…õ…õ n…õ, weese tinaa kegy…Ē yela te ditiŇčt…õ…õ l…õ…õ t…õ…õle keŇč d…Ē…Ē pou kelii debaŇč keŇč weese be g…õ…õse n…õ. 45Bot…Ē…Ē te kanyaŇč keŇč kpaŇč…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man n…õ b…õ…õsew…Ē ky…õ…õky…õ…õ ala. 46KeŇčte Yesu fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúTaata, me ta mo ma woo do n nyiŇčmaa man.‚ÄĚ ∆Ü ka betaa yako mena p…õ te …Ē kara weesi. 47Yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon kena kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč n…õ, …Ē lese Wurubuar…õ yele yako nyiaa ‚ÄúAmpaŇč, balee kei gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman!‚ÄĚ 48BalaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee bot…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ k…õ…õ n…õ naa kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč, te ba besew…Ē gyu don…Ē…Ē man na ba nyiŇčmaase gyakaa ba nyeeya d…Ē…Ē waraŇčase man. 49M…Ēna baŇč pou ba gyeŇč Yesu na ala baŇč b…Ē…Ē sila e lee Galelia don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, seŇčaw…Ē lemlem b…õ…õ k…õ…õ nombia k…õŇča.

english_text:
Jesus Is Alive
(Matthew 28.1-10; Mark 16.1-8; John 20.1-10)
1Very early on Sunday morning the women went to the tomb, carrying the spices they had prepared. 2When they found the stone rolled away from the entrance, 3they went in. But they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus, 4and they did not know what to think.
Suddenly two men in shining white clothes stood beside them. 5The women were afraid and bowed to the ground. But the men said, ‚ÄúWhy are you looking in the place of the dead for someone who is alive? 6 Jesus isn't here! He has been raised from death. Remember that while he was still in Galilee, he told you, 7‚ÄėThe Son of Man will be handed over to sinners who will nail him to a cross. But three days later he will rise to life.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 8Then they remembered what Jesus had said.
9-10Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and some other women were the ones who had gone to the tomb. When they returned, they told the eleven apostles and the others what had happened. 11The apostles thought it was all nonsense, and they would not believe.
12But Peter ran to the tomb. And when he stooped down and looked in, he saw only the burial clothes. Then he returned, wondering what had happened.
Jesus Appears to Two Disciples
(Mark 16.12,13)
13That same day two of Jesus' disciples were going to the village of Emmaus, which was about eleven kilometers from Jerusalem. 14As they were talking and thinking about what had happened, 15Jesus came near and started walking along beside them. 16But they did not know who he was.
17Jesus asked them, ‚ÄúWhat were you talking about as you walked along?‚ÄĚ
The two of them stood there looking sad and gloomy. 18Then the one named Cleopas asked Jesus, ‚ÄúAre you the only person from Jerusalem who didn't know what was happening there these last few days?‚ÄĚ
19‚ÄúWhat do you mean?‚ÄĚ Jesus asked.
They answered:
Those things that happened to Jesus from Nazareth. By what he did and said he showed that he was a powerful prophet, who pleased God and all the people. 20Then the chief priests and our leaders had him arrested and sentenced to die on a cross. 21We had hoped that he would be the one to set Israel free! But it has already been three days since all this happened.
22Some women in our group surprised us. They had gone to the tomb early in the morning, 23but did not find the body of Jesus. They came back, saying they had seen a vision of angels who told them that he is alive. 24Some men from our group went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said. But they didn't see Jesus either.
25Then Jesus asked the two disciples, ‚ÄúWhy can't you understand? How can you be so slow to believe all that the prophets said? 26Didn't you know that the Messiah would have to suffer before he was given his glory?‚ÄĚ 27Jesus then explained everything written about himself in the Scriptures, beginning with the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets.
28When the two of them came near the village where they were going, Jesus seemed to be going farther. 29They begged him, ‚ÄúStay with us! It's already late, and the sun is going down.‚ÄĚ So Jesus went into the house to stay with them.
30After Jesus sat down to eat, he took some bread. He blessed it and broke it. Then he gave it to them. 31At once they knew who he was, but he disappeared. 32They said to each other, ‚ÄúWhen he talked with us along the road and explained the Scriptures to us, didn't it warm our hearts?‚ÄĚ 33So they got up and returned to Jerusalem.
The two disciples found the eleven apostles and the others gathered together. 34And they learned from the group that the Lord was really alive and had appeared to Peter. 35Then the disciples from Emmaus told what happened on the road and how they knew he was the Lord when he broke the bread.
What Jesus' Followers Must Do
(Matthew 28.16-20; Mark 16.14-18; John 20.19-23; Acts 1.6-8)
36While Jesus' disciples were talking about what had happened, Jesus appeared and greeted them. 37They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
38But Jesus said, ‚ÄúWhy are you so frightened? Why do you doubt? 39Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.‚ÄĚ
40After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet. 41The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, ‚ÄúDo you have something to eat?‚ÄĚ 42They gave him a piece of broiled fish. 43He took it and ate it as they watched.
44Jesus said to them, ‚ÄúWhile I was still with you, I told you that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to happen.‚ÄĚ
45Then he helped them understand the Scriptures. 46He told them:
The Scriptures say that the Messiah must suffer, then three days later he will rise from death. 47They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem, 48you must tell everything that has happened. 49 I will send you the one my Father has promised, but you must stay in the city until you are given power from heaven.
Jesus Returns to Heaven
(Mark 16.19,20; Acts 1.9-11)
50 Jesus led his disciples out to Bethany, where he raised his hands and blessed them. 51As he was doing this, he left and was taken up to heaven. 52After his disciples had worshiped him, they returned to Jerusalem and were very happy. 53They spent their time in the temple, praising God.

Yesu kegyu adido
Maake 16:19-20; Kpilala 1:9-11
50KeŇčte Yesu moo …Ē kaseela baŇč lee don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man gyu B…õtani don…Ē…Ē n…Ē…Ēman, bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē gyinaa …Ē nyiŇčmaase adido te …Ē hiraa …Ē kaseela baŇč. 51∆Ü kee hiraa w…Ē n…õ, …Ē tinaa w…Ē yela te Wurubuar…õ moo e laŇč adido. 52KeŇčte ba som e te ba moo kenyaŇčee dinaa bese k…ĒŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man, 53te debaŇč kamas…õ na bee gyu ke kyaa Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man lese Wurubuar…õ yele.

english_text:
The Word of Life
1In the beginning was the one
who is called the Word.
The Word was with God
and was truly God.
2From the very beginning
the Word was with God.
3And with this Word,
God created all things.
Nothing was made
without the Word.
Everything that was created
4received its life from him,
and his life gave light
to everyone.
5The light keeps shining
in the dark,
and darkness has never
put it out.
6 God sent a man named John,
7who came to tell
about the light
and to lead all people
to have faith.
8John wasn't this light.
He came only to tell
about the light.
9The true light that shines
on everyone
was coming into the world.
10The Word was in the world,
but no one knew him,
though God had made the world
with his Word.
11He came into his own world,
but his own nation
did not welcome him.
12Yet some people accepted him
and put their faith in him.
So he gave them the right
to be the children of God.
13They were not God's children
by nature or because
of any human desires.
God himself was the one
who made them his children.
14The Word became
a human being
and lived here with us.
We saw his true glory,
the glory of the only Son
of the Father.
From him the complete gifts
of undeserved grace and truth
have come down to us.
15John spoke about him and shouted, ‚ÄúThis is the one I told you would come! He is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.‚ÄĚ
16Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another. 17The Law was given by Moses, but Jesus Christ brought us undeserved kindness and truth. 18No one has ever seen God. The only Son, who is truly God and is closest to the Father, has shown us what God is like.
John the Baptist Tells about Jesus
(Matthew 3.1-12; Mark 1.1-8; Luke 3.15-17)
19-20The religious authorities in Jerusalem sent priests and temple helpers to ask John who he was. He told them plainly, ‚ÄúI am not the Messiah.‚ÄĚ 21 Then when they asked him if he were Elijah, he said, ‚ÄúNo, I am not!‚ÄĚ And when they asked if he were the Prophet, he also said ‚ÄúNo!‚ÄĚ
22Finally, they said, ‚ÄúWho are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!‚ÄĚ
23 John answered in the words of the prophet Isaiah, ‚ÄúI am only someone shouting in the desert, ‚ÄėGet the road ready for the Lord!‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
24Some Pharisees had also been sent to John. 25They asked him, ‚ÄúWhy are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?‚ÄĚ
26John told them, ‚ÄúI use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know. 27Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.‚ÄĚ 28John said this as he was baptizing east of the Jordan River in Bethany.
The Lamb of God
29The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him and said:
Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world! 30He is the one I told you about when I said, ‚ÄúSomeone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.‚ÄĚ 31I didn't know who he was. But I came to baptize you with water, so that everyone in Israel would see him.
32I was there and saw the Spirit come down on him like a dove from heaven. And the Spirit stayed on him. 33Before this I didn't know who he was. But the one who sent me to baptize with water had told me, ‚ÄúYou will see the Spirit come down and stay on someone. Then you will know that he is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.‚ÄĚ 34I saw this happen, and I tell you that he is the Son of God.
The First Disciples of Jesus
35The next day, John was there again, and two of his followers were with him. 36When he saw Jesus walking by, he said, ‚ÄúHere is the Lamb of God!‚ÄĚ 37John's two followers heard him, and they went with Jesus.
38When Jesus turned and saw them, he asked, ‚ÄúWhat do you want?‚ÄĚ
They answered, ‚ÄúRabbi, where do you live?‚ÄĚ The Hebrew word ‚ÄúRabbi‚ÄĚ means ‚ÄúTeacher.‚ÄĚ
39Jesus replied, ‚ÄúCome and see!‚ÄĚ It was already about four o'clock in the afternoon when they went with him and saw where he lived. So they stayed on for the rest of the day.
40One of the two men who had heard John and had gone with Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter. 41The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother and tell him, ‚ÄúWe have found the Messiah!‚ÄĚ The Hebrew word ‚ÄúMessiah‚ÄĚ means the same as the Greek word ‚ÄúChrist.‚ÄĚ
42Andrew brought his brother to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, ‚ÄúSimon son of John, you will be called Cephas.‚ÄĚ This name can be translated as ‚ÄúPeter.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Chooses Philip and Nathanael
43-44The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. There he met Philip, who was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter. Jesus said to Philip, ‚ÄúFollow me.‚ÄĚ
45Philip then found Nathanael and said, ‚ÄúWe have found the one that Moses and the Prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.‚ÄĚ
46Nathanael asked, ‚ÄúCan anything good come from Nazareth?‚ÄĚ
Philip answered, ‚ÄúCome and see.‚ÄĚ
47When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, ‚ÄúHere is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.‚ÄĚ
48‚ÄúHow do you know me?‚ÄĚ Nathanael asked.
Jesus answered, ‚ÄúBefore Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.‚ÄĚ
49Nathanael said, ‚ÄúRabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!‚ÄĚ
50Jesus answered, ‚ÄúDid you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater. 51 I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.‚ÄĚ
translation_text:
Wurubuar…õ nombii keŇč k…õ…õ fa nyeedoŇč
