Na Ňčon na Wurubuar…õ kyaa la, te Ňčon y…õna Wurubuar…õ. 2Ňäon na Wurubuar…õ kyaa la leenaŇč kewalaŇč kenaŇč man. 3∆Ü d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē y…õ…õ kpene kamas…õ, te Wurubuar…õ ta lee …Ē w…Ēle y…õ…õ kolo na kolo ya. 4Wurubuar…õ nombii kei ne faa na nyeedoŇč, te nyeedoŇč kenaŇč y…õna keŇčmaŇčee keŇč ne gyuusu balaŇč sia na baa naa Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õ. 5KeŇčmaŇčee kenaŇč ne ŇčmaŇčee ditiŇčt…õ…õ man, m…Ēna ditiŇčt…õ…õ keŇč b…õ…õ tale di ke d…Ē…Ē ya.
6DebaŇč daale liiw…Ē na Wurubuar…õ kpila …Ē kpilale Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Gy…Ēn. 7Ňäon k…ĒŇčala …Ē kaa di adans…õ…õ lee keŇčmaŇčee keŇč wose man fa balaŇč, na balaŇč pou kenyiŇč ta …Ē d…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ keŇčmaŇčee keŇč di. 8Na Ňčon Gy…Ēn gbagba y…õna keŇčmaŇčee keŇč ya, m…Ēna …Ē k…ĒŇčae kaa di adans…õ…õ lee keŇčmaŇčee keŇč wose man. 9Ňäon b…õ…õ baake e keŇčmaŇčee gbagba keŇč n…õ waa k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē, na …Ē kaa ŇčmaŇčee fa walaŇč kamas…õ. 10KeŇčmaŇčee kenaŇč kaa kyaaw…Ē t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. ∆Ü d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē y…õ…õ kpene kamas…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč ta gyeŇč e ya. 11∆Ü k…ĒŇčaw…Ē Ňčon gbagba …Ē balaŇč gyaŇč, m…Ēna …Ē balaŇč te l…õ…õ e ya. 12M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ e te b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ di n…õ, …Ē faa w…Ē gb…õ…õ te ba bese Wurubuar…õ bia. 13Ba te y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ bia Ňčgba mena keŇč alo na baale na lola bia ba kegyae man n…õ ya. M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ gbagba kegyaebii man te …Ē lese w…Ē nyi baa y…õ…õ …Ē bia.
14Ňäon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Wurubuar…õ nombii n…õ, kaa bese deniwalaŇč te …Ē kaa kyaaw…Ē de man weeya akalans…õ…õ Ňčaale. De naa …Ē gyoorobiiri keŇč nawolo nyi Ňčon waageŇč y…õna …Ē kya Wurubuar…õ bu dokoloŇč Ňčon n…õ. ∆Ü man te Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ na anokoare keŇč doo.
15Gy…Ēn dii adans…õ…õ lee …Ē wose man. ∆Ü fae yeesa yako nyi, ‚ÄúWalaŇč kei wose man nombia yaa me wulaa yako Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄėWalaŇč Ňčon …Ē d…ĒŇč me w…Ēle …Ē ne k…ĒŇč n…õ …Ē kela maŇč, nawolo nyi …Ē wulaa …Ē kyaa p…õte ba lola maŇč.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 16Lee …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč be wulu …Ē man n…õ d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē ne hiraa daa agbaatee agbaatee. 17Mosesi d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē mo mmaraase Ňčan fa daa, m…Ēna Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē te …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē wola daa …Ē b…õr…õ…õ na wa anokoare keŇč. 18Ňäolo na Ňčolo te naa ta Wurubuar…õ na …Ē sia ya, m…Ēna Wurubuar…õ bu dokoloŇč kooŇč Ňčon loŇč b…õ…õ lam Ňčon na …Ē kya nsana ya n…õ, kaa lesena Wurubuar…õ din…Ē…Ē wola daa.
Gy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčs…Ē…Ēre Ňčon kekolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia
Mateo 3:1-12; Maake 1:1-8; Luka 3:1-18
19Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč be kyaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, kpila Wurubuar…õ saese na Lewitena baale nyi be kaa b…Ē…Ēse Gy…Ēn nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč y…õna am…Ēte?‚ÄĚ Gy…Ēn ta beo w…Ē ya. 20M…Ēna …Ē torow…Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúNa maŇč y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ ya.‚ÄĚ 21KeŇčte be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKenaŇč na nyaŇč y…õna am…Ēte? NyaŇč y…õna Elaegya?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte …Ē tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúKoa, na maŇč y…õna Elaegya ya.‚ÄĚ Ba besew…Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč y…õna Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčon d…õ…õ daa n…õ?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Gy…Ēn tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyi ‚ÄúKoa, na maŇč ya.‚ÄĚ
22KeŇčte be b…Ē…Ēse e nyi ‚ÄúTe kenaŇč na nyaŇč y…õna am…Ēte? Yako daa nombii dokoloŇč na dee nyiŇč gyu ke yako baŇč b…Ē…Ē kpila daa n…õ. Sena te n ne yako lee nyaŇč gbagba n wose man?‚ÄĚ 23Bot…Ē…Ē te Gy…Ēn moo n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Yesaya be yako see n…õ tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMaŇč y…õna woya Ňčan ne fae yeesa depampaa dinaa keŇč man n…õ nyiaa, ∆ź tenee de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õ see e na …Ē kaa ta ke d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 24BalaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kpili gyu Gy…Ēn gyaŇč n…õ, man baale y…õ…õ Farasiitenaw…Ē, 25keŇčte be b…Ē…Ēse Gy…Ēn nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa na nyaŇč y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ ya, yaa Elaegya yaa Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčon ya na, woŇč d…Ē…Ē te n ne s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč?‚ÄĚ 26Bot…Ē…Ē te Gy…Ēn tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMaŇč d…Ē…Ē loŇč yakaa te me dana m…õ…õ s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč, m…Ēna Ňčolo kyaa …õ man keŇč …õ ba gyeŇč e ya. 27Ňäon y…õna walaŇč Ňčon …Ē d…ĒŇč me w…Ēle …Ē ne k…ĒŇč, keŇč ma te kaboe gba nyi maa buŇč booli …Ē nkyokota ŇčmeeseŇč koraŇč gba ya.‚ÄĚ 28Nombia k…õŇča pou k…ĒŇčae Gy…Ē…Ēdan boo diŇčgyiŇč don…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke B…õtani n…õ man, bot…Ē…Ē keŇč Gy…Ēn ne s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč n…õ.
29T…õ…õ kena n…õ, Gy…Ēn naa Yesu ne k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč te …Ē yakow…Ē lee Yesu wose man nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nambu Ňčon. Ňäon na lesena balaŇč lee be dukum man. 30Kei y…õna walaŇč Ňčon m…Ē…Ē wulaa yako Ňčon …Ē wose man nombia nyiaa, ‚ÄėWalaŇč Ňčon …Ē d…ĒŇč me w…Ēle …Ē ne k…ĒŇč n…õ …Ē kela maŇč. Nawolo nyi …Ē wulaa …Ē kyaa p…õte ba lola maŇč.‚Äô 31Naf…Ē na maŇč gbagba ba gyeŇč e ya, m…Ēna Ňčon d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ kpila maŇč nyi me kaa s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč, na maa lese e deb…Ē…Ē wola Iseraetena na baa nyiŇč gyeŇč e.‚ÄĚ 32KeŇčte Gy…Ēn dii adans…õ…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe naa Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon moo duwuluŇč din…Ē…Ē tisi lee adido kaa kyaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē. 33Naf…Ē maŇč gbagba ba gyeŇč e ya, m…Ēna Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč nyi me kaa mo loŇč s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč n…õ yako na maŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄėWalaŇč Ňčon n naa Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon de tisi kaa kyaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē n…õ, Ňčon ne gyaela waa mo Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč.‚Äô 34Ma te naa te mee di adans…õ…õ nyiaa, Ňčon y…õna Wurubuar…õ bu Ňčon.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kpilala gyaŇčgba nyibaŇč baŇč
35T…õ…õ kena n…õ, Gy…Ēn na …Ē kaseela bala besew…Ē gyu boo keŇč n…Ē…Ēman. 36KeŇčte Gy…Ēn naa Yesu ne lam. ∆Ü kena e n…õ te …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nambu Ňčon!‚ÄĚ 37Gy…Ēn kaseela bala baŇč kenyii Gy…Ēn n…Ē…Ēwoya k…õŇča n…õ, be tinaa Gy…Ēn yela gyu ke sila Yesu. 38Yesu g…õ…õsew…Ē k…õ…õ na te …Ē naa w…Ē keŇčte …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč yaa …õ ne gyae?‚ÄĚ Bot…Ē…Ē te baŇč m…Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, aleŇč te n kyaa?‚ÄĚ 39KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…ĒŇč na …õ kaa k…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē be gyuuw…Ē kena bot…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē kyaa. DebaŇč kenaŇč na te gy…Ē balin…Ē…Ē debaŇč anaara te be kyaaw…Ē …Ē gyaŇč wee kenaŇč pou. 40BalaŇč bala baŇč b…Ē…Ē nyii Gy…Ēn n…Ē…Ēwoya te b…Ē…Ē sila Yesu n…õ, be man Ňčolo y…õna Sim…Ēn Pita …Ē naabu Anderiase. 41Kpene keŇč Anderiase be taŇčgb…õ…õ y…õ…õ y…õna nyi, …Ē gyuuw…Ē ke k…õo …Ē kegyia Sim…Ēn te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDa te naa Mesaya Ňčon, nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ.‚ÄĚ 42KeŇčte Anderiase moo Sim…Ēn k…ĒŇč Yesu gyaŇč. Yesu kena Sim…Ēn n…õ …Ē k…õ…õ e yididi te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe gyeŇč nyi nyaŇč y…õna Gy…Ēn bu Sim…Ēn Ňčon. Baa baake neŇč nyi Kefase yaa Pita.‚ÄĚ Ka as…õ…õ y…õna nyi boe.
Yesu kebaake Filipo na Natan…õle
43T…õ…õ kena n…õ, Yesu y…õ…õ wa agyueŇč nyi waa gyu Galelia t…õ…õle man. KeŇčte …Ē naa Filipo te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKaa sila maŇč.‚ÄĚ 44Filipo lee don…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke B…õtesaida n…õ man. Don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man m…Ē te Anderiase na Pita m…Ē leew…Ē. 45Filipo m…Ē naa Natan…õle te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDa te naa walaŇč Ňčon Mosesi be Ňčmarase …Ē wose man nombia do mmaraa t…Ēne keŇč man, te Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra baŇč m…Ē be Ňčmarase …Ē wose man nombia n…õ. B…õ…õ baake e nyi Yesu. Ňäon y…õna Yesu, Gyos…õfo bu Ňčon …Ē ba lee Nasar…õte n…õ.‚ÄĚ 46KeŇčte Natan…õle b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNasar…õte ya? Kolo kpaakpaa daale gyae ke tale lee bot…Ē…Ē?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Filipo yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč k…ĒŇč na n kaa k…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ 47Yesu kena Natan…õle ne k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč n…õ …Ē kolosiw…Ē lee …Ē wose man nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ, Iserae baale gbagba yaa kei keŇč dekara be doo …Ē man ya.‚ÄĚ 48Bot…Ē…Ē te Natan…õle b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúSena te n y…õ…õw…Ē gyeŇč maŇč?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyi, ‚ÄúMe wulaa naa neŇč fiigi daŇč keŇč t…õ…õ p…õte Filipo baake neŇč.‚ÄĚ 49KeŇčte Natan…õle torow…Ē yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, nyaŇč y…õna Wurubuar…õ bu Ňčon! NyaŇč y…õna Iseraetena gyoo Ňčon!‚ÄĚ 50KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKeŇč m…Ē yako neŇč nyi me naa neŇč fiigi daŇč keŇč t…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te n ne l…õ…õ maŇč di? N kena nombia dinaana Ňčan kela k…õŇča.‚ÄĚ 51KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, …õ kena Ňčgba adido ta toro na Wurubuar…õ kpilala baŇč na gyem tisi maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Jesus at a Wedding in Cana
1Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee. 2Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
3When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, ‚ÄúThey don't have any more wine.‚ÄĚ
4Jesus replied, ‚ÄúMother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.‚ÄĚ
5Mary then said to the servants, ‚ÄúDo whatever Jesus tells you to do.‚ÄĚ
6At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about 100 liters. 7Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled, 8he said, ‚ÄúNow take some water and give it to the man in charge of the feast.‚ÄĚ
The servants did as Jesus told them, 9and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over 10and said, ‚ÄúThe best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!‚ÄĚ
11This was Jesus' first miracle, and he did it in the village of Cana in Galilee. There Jesus showed his glory, and his disciples put their faith in him. 12 After this, he went with his mother, his brothers, and his disciples to the town of Capernaum, where they stayed for a few days.
Jesus in the Temple
(Matthew 21.12,13; Mark 11.15-17; Luke 19.45,46)
13 Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem. 14There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables. 15So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
16Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, ‚ÄúGet those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.‚ÄĚ
17 The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúMy love for your house burns in me like a fire.‚ÄĚ
18The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWhat miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?‚ÄĚ
19 ‚ÄúDestroy this temple,‚ÄĚ Jesus answered, ‚Äúand in three days I will build it again!‚ÄĚ
20The leaders replied, ‚ÄúIt took 46 years to build this temple. What makes you think you can rebuild it in three days?‚ÄĚ
21But Jesus was talking about his body as a temple. 22And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus.
Jesus Knows What People Are Like
23In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles. 24But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him. 25No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.

13DebaŇč keŇč Gyudatena nyeekel…õ…õwee keŇč na benaa n…õ, Yesu gyuu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man. 14Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč dekpaŇčalaŇč man n…õ, …Ē naaw…Ē nyi baale ne y…Ē…Ē nunkyuese na namense na awuluŇčna, na balaŇč baale m…Ē kyak…õ…õ b…õ…õ y…õl…õ…õ k…Ēba. 15Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē moo ŇčmeeseŇč Ňčm…õ y…õ…õ dambila te …Ē moow…Ē gegi balaŇč baŇč na be nunkyuese na be namense pou lee dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man. ∆Ü yaasee be k…Ēbasebia Ňčan te …Ē besena be tebuluse Ňčan pou buŇčii. 16KeŇčte …Ē yako balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…Ē…Ē awuluŇčna n…õ nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź mo w…Ē lee k…õbo! ∆ź na mo me kya d…õ…õ besena …õ kebu dedii ya!‚ÄĚ 17Nombia k…õŇča yela …Ē kaseela baŇč t…Ē…Ēse Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan ne yako nyi, ‚ÄúO Wurubuar…õ, n deni dinaa keŇč man nombia ne hia maŇč nideli, te Ňče te di ma nyee m…Ē.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Jesus and Nicodemus
1There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader. 2One night he went to Jesus and said, ‚ÄúRabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.‚ÄĚ
3Jesus replied, ‚ÄúI tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!‚ÄĚ
4Nicodemus asked, ‚ÄúHow can a grown man ever be born a second time?‚ÄĚ
5Jesus answered:
I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit. 6Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God. 7Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above. 8Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
9‚ÄúHow can this be?‚ÄĚ Nicodemus asked.
10Jesus replied:
How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things? 11I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say. 12 If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
13 No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there. 14 And the Son of Man must be lifted up, just as the metal snake was lifted up by Moses in the desert. 15Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
16God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die. 17God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them! 18No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
19The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light. 20People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done. 21But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do.
Jesus and John the Baptist
22Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
23-24 John had not yet been put in jail. He was at Aenon near Salim, where there was a lot of water, and people were coming there for John to baptize them.
25John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing. 26They went to John and said, ‚ÄúRabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.‚ÄĚ
27John replied:
No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it. 28 You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
29At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad. 30Jesus must become more important, while I become less important.
The One Who Comes from Heaven
31God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others. 32He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him. 33But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful. 34The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
35 The Father loves the Son and has given him everything. 36Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.

14∆ź gyeŇč nyi gyaŇčgba keŇč na Wurubuar…õ yela Mosesi y…õ…õ dom te …Ē moow…Ē mataa na daŇč adido depampaa dinaa keŇč man. 16Lee mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ne gyae balaŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē moo …Ē bu dokoloŇč kooŇč Ňčon fa, nyi walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē l…õ…õ e di na …Ē be gyae waa yeŇč leki ya, m…Ēna waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa.‚ÄĚ 17Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ te kpila …Ē bu t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē nyi …Ē kaa bu balaŇč f…Ē…Ē ya, m…Ēna …Ē kpila e bo na balaŇč ke ta …Ē d…Ē…Ē nyiŇč nyeedoŇč.
18WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē l…õ…õ e di na b…Ē…Ē gyae baa bu e f…Ē…Ē ya, te Ňčon …Ē te l…õ…õ e di ya na be wulaa bu e f…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi …Ē te l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ bu dokoloŇč kooŇč Ňčon di ya. 19Nombia Ňčan b…Ē…Ē mo bu t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč f…Ē…Ē y…õna nyi, keŇčmaŇčee te k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna balaŇč ne gyae ditiŇčt…õ…õ bo kela keŇčmaŇčee keŇč. Nawolo nyi be nombia Ňčan b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ y…õ…õ dukum ke. 20WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne y…õ…õ dukum na, …Ē ne k…Ēla keŇčmaŇčee keŇč te …Ē b…õ…õ benaa ke ya. Nawolo nyi …Ē b…õ…õ gyae nyi …Ē nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan ka lee deb…Ē…Ē ya. 21M…Ēna walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa n…õ, …Ē na benaa keŇčmaŇčee keŇč. K…õ…õ, …Ē ne s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč, te balaŇč pou ne gyu …Ē gyaŇč.‚ÄĚ 27KeŇčte Gy…Ēn tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyi Wurubuar…õ te fa walaŇč gb…õ…õ nyi waa y…õ…õ kolo ya n…õ, …Ētenate be gyae waa tale y…õ…õ ya. 28∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ ke tale di maŇč adans…õ…õ nyi me yakow…Ē nyiaa, Na maŇč y…õna Nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ ya, m…Ēna Wurubuar…õ kpila maŇč bo nyi maa kyaŇč gb…õ…õ ta …Ē siaman k…ĒŇč.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
1Jesus knew that the Pharisees had heard that he was winning and baptizing more followers than John was. 2But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
3Jesus left Judea and started for Galilee again. 4This time he had to go through Samaria, 5 and on his way he came to the town of Sychar. It was near the field that Jacob had long ago given to his son Joseph. 6-8The well that Jacob had dug was still there, and Jesus sat down beside it because he was tired from traveling. It was noon, and after Jesus' disciples had gone into town to buy some food, a Samaritan woman came to draw water from the well.
Jesus asked her, ‚ÄúWould you please give me a drink of water?‚ÄĚ
9 ‚ÄúYou are a Jew,‚ÄĚ she replied, ‚Äúand I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?‚ÄĚ
10Jesus answered, ‚ÄúYou don't know what God wants to give you, and you don't know who is asking you for a drink. If you did, you would ask me for the water that gives life.‚ÄĚ
11‚ÄúSir,‚ÄĚ the woman said, ‚Äúyou don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water? 12Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?‚ÄĚ
13Jesus answered, ‚ÄúEveryone who drinks this water will get thirsty again. 14But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.‚ÄĚ
15The woman replied, ‚ÄúSir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.‚ÄĚ
16Jesus told her, ‚ÄúGo and bring your husband.‚ÄĚ
17-18The woman answered, ‚ÄúI don't have a husband.‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThat's right,‚ÄĚ Jesus replied, ‚Äúyou're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.‚ÄĚ
19The woman said, ‚ÄúSir, I can see that you are a prophet. 20My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.‚ÄĚ
21Jesus said to her:
Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem. 22You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world. 23But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him. 24God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
25The woman said, ‚ÄúI know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.‚ÄĚ
26‚ÄúI am that one,‚ÄĚ Jesus told her, ‚Äúand I am speaking to you now.‚ÄĚ
27The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
28The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people, 29‚ÄúCome and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?‚ÄĚ 30Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
31While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, ‚ÄúTeacher, please eat something.‚ÄĚ
32But Jesus told them, ‚ÄúI have food you don't know anything about.‚ÄĚ
33His disciples started asking each other, ‚ÄúHas someone brought him something to eat?‚ÄĚ
34Jesus said:
My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do. 35You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
36Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together. 37So the saying proves true, ‚ÄúSome plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.‚ÄĚ 38I am sending you to harvest crops in fields where others have done all the hard work.
39A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, ‚ÄúThis man told me everything I have ever done.‚ÄĚ 40They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
41Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say. 42They told the woman, ‚ÄúWe no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!‚ÄĚ
Jesus Heals an Official's Son
(Matthew 8.5-13; Luke 7.1-10)
43-44 Jesus had said, ‚ÄúProphets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.‚ÄĚ Then two days later he left 45 and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
46 While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick. 47And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
48Jesus told the official, ‚ÄúYou won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!‚ÄĚ
49The man replied, ‚ÄúLord, please come before my son dies!‚ÄĚ
50Jesus then said, ‚ÄúYour son will live. Go on home to him.‚ÄĚ The man believed Jesus and started back home.
51Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, ‚ÄúYour son is better!‚ÄĚ 52He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, ‚ÄúThe fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.‚ÄĚ
53The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, ‚ÄúYour son will live!‚ÄĚ So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
54This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
translation_text:

Sikya don…Ē…Ē kei benaa na t…õ…õle keŇč Gyek…Ēpo ba mo fa …Ē bu Gyos…õfo n…õ. 6Na Gyek…Ēpo ditiribuŇč doo bot…Ē…Ē. Yesu kelii bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, na …Ē te kaa mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē tisiw…Ē kyaa ditiribuŇč keŇč gyaŇč. DebaŇč kenaŇč na weese ta seŇč d…õ…õ.
7∆Ü ke kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ Samaria alo Ňčolo k…ĒŇčaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē …Ē kaa kyee loŇč, keŇčte Yesu sola e nyi ‚ÄúFa maŇč loŇč maa ny…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 8DebaŇč kenaŇč na, …Ē kaseela baŇč te lam don…Ē…Ē keŇč man be kaa l…õ…õ ween…õ…õ. 9Bot…Ē…Ē te Samaria alo Ňčon yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúN y…õ…õ Gyuda baale e te maŇč m…Ē y…õ…õ Samaria alo e. WoŇč d…Ē…Ē te n na sola maŇč loŇč?‚ÄĚ KeŇč d…Ē…Ē …Ē be b…Ē…Ēse mena y…õna nyi, Gyudatena na Samariatena b…õ…õ gyoo ya. 10KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa nyeŇč gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ kolo anefaŇč na walaŇč Ňčon …Ē na sola neŇč loŇč kei n…õ, naf…Ē nyaŇč ka sola e na waa fa neŇč loŇč keŇč k…õ…õ fa nyeedoŇč n…õ.‚ÄĚ
11Bot…Ē…Ē te alo Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKegyia, ditiribuŇč keŇč dana siiliŇč te n be dana kpene keŇč n ka mo kyee loŇč keŇč ya. Ňäon gbagba na …Ē bia na …Ē wonembia pou ny…Ē…Ē loŇč lee ditiribuŇč kei man.‚ÄĚ 13KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa alo Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúWalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon waa ny…Ē…Ē loŇč kei n…õ, loŇčboaneŇč gyae ka ko e bela. 14M…Ēna walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon waa ny…Ē…Ē loŇč keŇč maa fa e n…õ, loŇčboaneŇč be gyae ka ko e bela ya. 16KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúGyu ke baake n baale na …õ ke k…ĒŇč.‚ÄĚ 17Te alo Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúM…Ē…Ē dana baale ya.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN te yako anokoare nyi n be dana baale ya. 18N taŇč ke yala baala banoŇč, te Ňčon gyaŇč n kyaa debokeiman n…õ …Ē te y…õ…õ n baale e ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē nombia Ňčan n be yako maŇč n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč.‚ÄĚ 19Bot…Ē…Ē te alo Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKegyia ma te naa nyi n y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre e ampaŇč. 20De naana…Ē som Wurubuar…õ bo bula kei d…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna …õm…õ…õ Gyudatena kpa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man te kaboena nyi d…õ…õ som Wurubuar…õ.‚ÄĚ 21KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAlo l…õ…õ maŇč di, debaŇč daale gyae kek…ĒŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē gyae baa tekaa som Wurubuar…õ bula kei d…Ē…Ē yaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man ya. 22∆źm…õ…õ Samariatena na som Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …õ ba gyeŇč e ya, te daa Gyudatena d…Ē…Ē d…õ…õ som Wurubuar…õ Ňčon da gyeŇč e, nawolo nyi nyeekel…õ…õ gyae ke ta bo Gyudatena d…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč.

31DebaŇč kenaŇč na te …Ē kaseela baŇč waase e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo mo kolo tina n n…Ē…Ē‚ÄĚ 32KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe dana ween…õ…õ Ňčaale maa di keŇč …õ ba gyeŇč ya.‚ÄĚ 33Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē kaseela baŇč b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúDoona Ňčolo saa ween…õ…õ baa k…Ēna e?‚ÄĚ 34KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe ween…õ…õ y…õna nyi maa y…õ…õ me kya Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ kegyaebii, na maa y…õ…õ tom keŇč …Ē ba mo fa maŇč n…õ kelii …Ēto. 35∆źm…õ…õ gbagba ne tale yako nyi ‚ÄėTe ka gyaalaŇčna banaara na ween…õ…õ kek…õr…õ…õ te lii.‚Äô M…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, …õ gyina …õ sia na …õ ke k…õ…õ w…Ē…Ēse Ňčan. ∆ź kena nyi ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan te y…õ…õ na kek…õr…õ…õ. 36WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne k…õr…õ…õ ween…õ…õ Ňčan n…õ baa t…Ē e kom, te …Ē ne yilaa ab…Ē…Ē ŇčenaŇč bo see fa nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa keŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be fare na walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be k…õr…õ…õ n…õ pou baa bom di gyoŇč. 37Dudu keŇč b…Ē…Ē gy…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄėŇäolo ne duula te Ňčolo m…Ē ne karala‚Äô n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč. 38Me kpila Ňčon baa nyi ya gyu ke k…õr…õ…õ ween…õ…õ w…Ē…Ē keŇč …õ te y…õ…õ tom ya n…õ man. Baale y…õ…õna tom keŇč, te …õm…õ…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē …õ ne di ba tom keŇč b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ n…õ t…Ēn…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ
39BalaŇč baŇč be kyaa Samaria don…Ē…Ē keŇč man n…õ, be man burum l…õ…õ Yesu di nawolo nyi alo Ňčon yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆Ü te yako maŇč ma wose man nombia Ňčan pou wees…õ…õ n…õ.‚ÄĚ 40Mena d…Ē…Ē Samariatena baŇč kek…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč n…õ, be waase e nyi waa kyaa be gyaŇč, te Yesu dii weeya ala be gyaŇč. 41Yesu nombia Ňčan …Ē ba wolo n…õ yela balaŇč burum l…õ…õ e di. 42KeŇčte be yako alo Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē n…õ na n n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan d…Ē…Ē te de l…õ…õ e di ya, m…Ēna daa gbagba te nyii e te da te naa nyi Ňčon y…õna balaŇč pou nyeel…õ…õre gbagba Ňčon.‚ÄĚ
Yesu keky…Ē tomy…õ…õre kegyia Ňčolo bu kawee
43Yesu kedi weeya ala bot…Ē…Ē w…Ēle n…õ, …Ē leew…Ē bot…Ē…Ē bese gyu Galelia t…õ…õle man. 44Nawolo nyi Yesu gbagba yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúB…Ē…Ē ne l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre kamas…õ do Ňčon gbagba …Ē don…Ē…Ē man ya.‚ÄĚ 45Yesu kek…ĒŇč kaa gyoo Galelia n…õ, bot…Ē…Ētena l…õ…õ e nideli, nawolo nyi be naa nombia Ňčan pou …Ē be y…õ…õ Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man debaŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē gyu be kaa di ba nyeekel…õ…õwee keŇč n…õ.
46KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē gyu Kaanan don…Ē…Ē keŇč ke kyaa Galelia t…õ…õle man n…õ man. Mena don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man te …Ē besena loŇč y…õ…õ soloŇč n…õ. DebaŇč kenaŇč na tomy…õ…õre kegyia Ňčolo kyaa bot…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē bu na wee Kapanum don…Ē…Ē man. 47Tomy…õ…õre kegyia Ňčon kenyii nyi Yesu ta lee Gyudia k…ĒŇč Galelia bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ē gyuuw…Ē …Ē gyaŇč ke waase e nyi …Ē bu na wee …Ē doo Kapanum don…Ē…Ē man …Ē ne gyae waa yeŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē waa k…ĒŇč na …Ē kaa ky…Ē e kawee fa e. 48Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNa …õ kena gyakoloŇč nombia na nombia dinaana ya n…õ, naf…Ē …õ man walaŇč kamas…õ be gyae waa l…õ…õ maŇč di ya.‚ÄĚ 49KeŇčte kegyia Ňčon yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, waase k…ĒŇč dee gyu bileŇč na mena ya na me bu Ňčon waa yeŇč.‚ÄĚ 50Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúTa, n bu Ňčon waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč.‚ÄĚ Balee Ňčon l…õ…õ Yesu n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan di te …Ē laŇčaw…Ē. 51Balee Ňčon keta …Ē ne gyu …Ē d…õ…õ n…õ, …Ē gyaŇčee na …Ē tewul…õ…õ gb…õ…õnaa man te be yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN bu Ňčon wose te y…õ…õ e doŇč.‚ÄĚ 52Bot…Ē…Ē te balee Ňčon b…Ē…Ēse …Ē tewul…õ…õ baŇč nyiaa, debaŇč woŇčti man te me bu Ňčon wose y…õ…õ e doŇč? KeŇčte be tiran…Ē…Ē fa nyiaa, ‚ÄúDana weeseteŇčteŇč kegy…Ē Ňčgba debaŇč dokoloŇč n…õ, te bu Ňčon wose y…õ…õ e doŇč.‚ÄĚ 53Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē kya Ňčon t…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyiaa, debaŇč kenaŇč man p…õp…õ…õp…õ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN bu Ňčon wose gyae ke y…õ…õ e doŇč.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē Ňčon na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou l…õ…õ Yesu di. 54Yesu ka lee Gyudia k…ĒŇč Galelia t…õ…õle man n…õ, kei y…õna gyakoloŇč nombia bolate keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ.

english_text:
Jesus Heals a Sick Man
1Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival. 2In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
3-4Many sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed people were lying close to the pool.
5Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for 38 years. 6When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, ‚ÄúDo you want to be healed?‚ÄĚ
7The man answered, ‚ÄúSir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.‚ÄĚ
8Jesus told him, ‚ÄúPick up your mat and walk!‚ÄĚ 9Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
10 When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, ‚ÄúThis is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.‚ÄĚ
11But he replied, ‚ÄúThe man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.‚ÄĚ
12They asked him, ‚ÄúWho is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?‚ÄĚ 13But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
14Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, ‚ÄúYou are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.‚ÄĚ 15The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him. 16They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
17But Jesus said, ‚ÄúMy Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.‚ÄĚ 18 Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God.
The Son's Authority
19Jesus told the people:
I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do. 20The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed. 21Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
22The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone. 23The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him. 24I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
25I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live! 26The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son. 27And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
28Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man, 29 and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
30I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself.
Witnesses to Jesus
31If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth. 32But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true. 33 You sent messengers to John, and he told them the truth. 34I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved. 35 John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
36But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
37 The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face. 38You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
39 You search the Scriptures, because you think you will find eternal life in them. The Scriptures tell about me, 40but you refuse to come to me for eternal life.
41I don't care about human praise, 42but I do know that none of you love God. 43I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own. 44How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
45Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you. 46Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me. 47But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
translation_text:
Yesu keky…Ē …Ētakase Ňčolo kawee
1K…õŇča w…Ēle n…õ, Gyudatena ne di b…Ē …Ēsom wee dinaa daale Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man, te Yesu m…Ē gyuu bot…Ē…Ē. 2Na depaa daale kyaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke be d…õ…õ dei man nyi B…õtesaida. Mena depaa kei benaa na denan…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi ‚ÄúNamense Denan…Ē…Ē‚ÄĚ n…õ, te be baŇčee kef…õ…õse anoŇč kilisi ke. 3Kaweesetena kpookpoo burum Ňčgba siay…õl…õ…õsatena na …Ētakasese na baŇč ba yenaase ba yekee n…õ na lee don…Ē…Ē keŇč man kaa doo bot…Ē…Ē daa loŇč keŇč kewosi. 4Nawolo nyi be kyaa be kyaa na, Wurubuar…õ kpilale ne kyaa kaa wosi loŇč kenaŇč te kaweete Ňčon …Ē taŇčgb…õ…õ ka gyoo loŇč keŇč man na, …Ē kawee ne taŇč.
5Na balee Ňčolo Ňčon …Ē be tekerii kulutooneŇč sao na kufu gyanaara n…õ doo bot…Ē…Ē. 6Yesu kena balee ŇčonaŇč te …Ē ke nyii nyi …Ē ta wee kyare n…õ …Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN ne gyae n kawee ke taŇč?‚ÄĚ 7Bot…Ē…Ē te kaweete Ňčon tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, akpaa be wosi loŇč keŇč na, m…Ē…Ē ne nyiŇč Ňčon waa gate maŇč ka do ke man ya. Akpaa ma mo maa y…õ…õ kakyeŇč koro ka gyoo na Ňčolo te taŇčgb…õ…õ maŇč.‚ÄĚ 8Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKoro! Gate n dofoŇčoŇč na n ke ta.‚ÄĚ 9DebokenaŇč man te balee Ňčon kawee taŇčaw…Ē te …Ē korow…Ē gate …Ē dofoŇčoŇč te …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē laŇč. Mena wee kenaŇč y…õ…õ Gyudatena kef…õ…õfowee ke. 10Mena d…Ē…Ē Gyudatena kegyiise yako balee Ňčon b…Ē…Ē ky…Ē e n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúGy…õŇč y…õ…õ da kef…õ…õfowee ke, te da mmaraa b…õ…õ fa gb…õ…õ nyi nya seele n dofoŇčoŇč ya.‚ÄĚ 11KeŇčte balee Ňčon tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWalaŇč Ňčon …Ē be ky…Ē maŇč me kawee n…õ, yako na maŇč nyi maa gate ma dofoŇčoŇč na maa ta.‚ÄĚ 12Bot…Ē…Ē te be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúWalaŇč woŇčti yako na neŇč nyi nya gate n dofoŇčoŇč na n keta?‚ÄĚ 13M…Ēna balee Ňčon Yesu be ky…Ē e …Ē kawee n…õ, ta gyem nyi Yesu ky…Ē…Ēna e …Ē kawee keŇč ya. Nawolo nyi na balaŇč boe bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu m…Ē ta Ňčere gyoo be man. 14KenaŇč w…Ēle n…õ, Yesu kena balee ŇčonaŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man te …Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúK…õ…õ, n…õn…õ…õ n wose te y…õ…õ neŇč doŇč, na tekaa y…õ…õ dukum bela ya. Na mena ya na kpene keŇč gyae kek…ĒŇč n d…Ē…Ē n…õ, gyae ka kela kei.‚ÄĚ 15Bot…Ē…Ē te balee Ňčon gyuuw…Ē keyako Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč nyiaa, Yesu ky…Ē…Ē na e …Ē kawee keŇč.
16Mena d…Ē…Ē Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč naase Yesu diyem, nawolo nyi …Ē te ky…Ē kawee ba kef…õ…õfowee. 17KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe kya Wurubuar…õ ne y…õ…õ tom debaŇč kamas…õ kaa lii na gy…õŇč, mena d…Ē…Ē kaboena nyi maŇč m…Ē maa y…õ…õ tom mena.‚ÄĚ 18Lee mena nombia k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē n…õ Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč yase sia gyae gb…õ…õ keŇč baa mo ko Yesu. Nawolo nyi na keŇč …Ē be w…Ēl…õ…õ ba kef…õ…õfowee keŇč kaageŇč d…Ē…Ē ya, m…Ēna …Ē kpa …Ē kya y…õna Wurubuar…õ keŇč nawolo nyi …Ē dokoso y…õna Wurubuar…õ. 19KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, kolo be kyaa keŇč maŇč Wurubuar…õ bu Ňčon ne y…õ…õ ma wose d…Ē…Ē ya. Kpene keŇč me naa me kya ne y…õ…õ na keŇč te m…õ…õ y…õ…õ. Kpene keŇč kya Ňčon ne y…õ…õ na, keŇč te maŇč …Ē bu Ňčon m…Ē ne y…õ…õ. 20Nawolo nyi me kya ne gyae maŇč te …Ē na wola maŇč kpene kamas…õ keŇč Ňčon gbagba ne y…õ…õ n…õ. Waa wola maŇč ab…Ē…Ē dinaana Ňčan kela k…õŇča na maa y…õ…õ, na …õ wose ka yeŇč Ňčon. 21Mena keŇč kya Ňčon ne gyuusu woya te …Ē ne fa w…Ē nyeedoŇč n…õ, mena kee te maŇč …Ē bu Ňčon m…Ē maa fa balaŇč baŇč maŇč gbagba ne gyae n…õ nyeedoŇč. 22Kya Ňčon gbagba bee di balaŇč nombia ya. ∆Ü ta mo balaŇč nombia kedi pou do maŇč …Ē bu Ňčon nyiŇčmaa man, 23na balaŇč pou ke nyiŇč lese maŇč …Ē bu Ňčon yele Ňčgba mena keŇč b…õ…õ lese me kya Ňčon yele n…õ. WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē b…õ…õ lese maŇč …Ē bu Ňčon yele ya na, …Ē b…õ…õ lese me kya Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč …Ē bu Ňčon n…õ yele ya.
24AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē nyii me n…Ē…Ēwoya te …Ē l…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ di na, waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa te b…Ē…Ē gyae baa bu e f…Ē…Ē ya. Kei nawolo baa nyi …Ē ta lee yeŇč man k…ĒŇč nyeedoŇč man. 25AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, debaŇč daale ne k…ĒŇč te ke taŇč ke k…ĒŇč keŇč balaŇč baŇč be y…õ…õ Ňčgba woya n…õ, nawolo nyi ba te l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan di d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, baa nyii maŇč Wurubuar…õ bu Ňčon dei. BalaŇč baŇč baa nyii me dei te baa y…õ…õ kpene keŇč m…Ē…Ē yako w…Ē n…õ na baa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč. 26Mena keŇč me kya Ňčon ne fa balaŇč nyeedoŇč n…õ, mena m…Ē te …Ē faa maŇč …Ē bu doŇč nyi maa fa balaŇč nyeedoŇč. 27∆Ü te fa maŇč …Ē bu Ňčon doŇč nyi maa di balaŇč nombia, nawolo nyi maŇč y…õna DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon. 28∆ź na yeli nombia k…õŇča ke y…õ…õ Ňčon gyakoloŇč ya, nawolo nyi debaŇč daale gyae kek…ĒŇč keŇč woya pou baa nyii ma woya, 29na baa lee ba yeb…Ē…Ēse man k…ĒŇč. BaŇč b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa n…õ baa koro na baa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč, te baŇč b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ n…õ baa koro na baa bu w…Ē f…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ
Yesu adans…õ…õtena
30‚ÄúMaŇč gbagba b…õ…õ y…õ…õ kolo me baam d…Ē…Ē ya. Mena keŇč me kya Wurubuar…õ de yako maŇč n…õ, mena te mee di balaŇč nombia, te me nombia kedi m…Ē doo na ke gb…õ…õ. Nawolo nyi na maŇč gbagba kegyaebii te m…õ…õ y…õ…õ ya, m…Ēna me kya Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ kegyaebii te m…õ…õ y…õ…õ. 31Nyi akpaa maŇč gbagba de di ma wose man adans…õ…õ na, …õ be gyae …õ ke l…õ…õ maŇč di ya. 32M…Ēna Ňčolo kyaa keŇč …Ē ne di ma wose man adans…õ…õ, te ma gyeŇč nyi wa adans…õ…õ keŇč …Ē ne dii lee ma wose man n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč. 33∆ź kpila balaŇč gyu Gy…Ēn gyaŇč, te …Ē dii anokoare keŇč wose man adans…õ…õ fa Ňčon. 34Na m…õ…õ gyae bo nyi denibalaŇč baa di ma wose man adans…õ…õ ya, m…Ēna …õ nyeedoŇč kenyiŇč d…Ē…Ē te m…õ…õ yako k…õŇča. 35Gy…Ēn y…õ…õ Ňčgba kanea keŇč k…õ…õ naa te k…õ…õ Ňčalakee n…õ, te …õ gyaew…Ē nyi …õ ke di kanea kenaŇč gyoŇč kyomii. 36Me dana Ňčolo Ňčon …Ē ne di ma wose man adans…õ…õ, te wa adans…õ…õ kenaŇč kela Gy…Ēn wui keŇč …Ē be dii lee ma wose man n…õ. Nawolo nyi tom keŇč me kya Wurubuar…õ ba mo do maŇč me nyiŇčmaa man nyi maa y…õ…õ ke kelii …Ēto te m…õ…õ y…õ…õ ke n…õ, ne diina adans…õ…õ lee ma wose man nyi me kya ŇčonaŇč kpila na maŇč. 37Me kya Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ, Ňčon gbagba m…Ē ne di ma wose man adans…õ…õ. ∆ź te nyii ta …Ē n…Ē…Ēwoya ya, te …õ te naa ta …Ē din…Ē…Ē m…Ē ya. 38∆Ü nombia be doo …õ kon…Ē…Ēse man ya, nawolo nyi …õ b…õ…õ l…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be kpili n…õ di ya. 39∆ź ne yase …õ sia kasee Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan nawolo nyi na ya agyueŇč y…õna nyi Ňče man te …õ ke nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč. Te mena Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see k…õŇča ne di ma wose man adans…õ…õ. 40M…Ēna …õ b…õ…õ gyae …õ ke k…ĒŇč me gyaŇč na …õ kaa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč ya.
41Na ma kegyaebii y…õna nyi balaŇč baa k…õl…õ…õ maŇč ya. 42M…Ēna ma gyeŇč …õ din…Ē…Ē te ma gyeŇč nyi …õ b…õ…õ gyae Wurubuar…õ lee …õ kon…Ē…Ēse man ya. 43Me k…ĒŇčae me kya Ňčon yele man m…Ēna …õ te l…õ…õ maŇč ya. M…Ēna akpaa Ňčolo de k…ĒŇč Ňčon gbagba yele man na …õ ke l…õ…õ e. 44∆ź ne gyae nyi …õ ke k…õl…õ…õ d…ĒŇča, m…Ēna …õ b…õ…õ gyae nyi Wurubuar…õ dokoloŇč Ňčon ke k…õl…õ…õ Ňčon ya. KenaŇč na sena te …õ ke tale l…õ…õ maŇč di? 45∆ź na gyueŇč nyi maŇč ne gyaela me kaa yako …õ nombiakum…õ…õ fa me kya Wurubuar…õ ya. Mosesi Ňčon …õ ba mo …õ loo gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē n…õ, Ňčon ne gyaela waa mo …õ nombiakum…õ…õ gyu me kya siaman. 46Nyi yeŇč l…õ…õ Mosesi di na naf…Ē …õ kel…õ…õ maŇč m…Ē di, nawolo nyi ma wose man nombia te Mosesi Ňčmarasew…Ē. 47M…Ēna nyi akpaa …õ b…õ…õ l…õ…õ kpene keŇč …Ē be Ňčmarase n…õ di ya na, sena te …õ kel…õ…õ kpene keŇč m…õ…õ yako n…õ di?‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Feeding Five Thousand
(Matthew 14.13-21; Mark 6.30-44; Luke 9.10-17)
1Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias. 2A large crowd had seen him work miracles to heal the sick, and those people went with him. 3-4It was almost time for the Jewish festival of Passover, and Jesus went up on a mountain with his disciples and sat down.
5When Jesus saw the large crowd coming toward him, he asked Philip, ‚ÄúWhere will we get enough food to feed all these people?‚ÄĚ 6He said this to test Philip, since he already knew what he was going to do.
7Philip answered, ‚ÄúDon't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?‚ÄĚ
8Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said, 9‚ÄúThere is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?‚ÄĚ
10The ground was covered with grass, and Jesus told his disciples to tell everyone to sit down. About 5,000 men were in the crowd. 11Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
12The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted. 13The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
14After the people had seen Jesus work this miracle, they began saying, ‚ÄúThis must be the Prophet who is to come into the world!‚ÄĚ 15Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone.
Jesus Walks on the Water
(Matthew 14.22-27; Mark 6.45-52)
16That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake. 17They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them, 18and a strong wind was making the water rough.
19When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified. 20But he said, ‚ÄúI am Jesus! Don't be afraid!‚ÄĚ 21The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed.
The Bread That Gives Life
22The people who had stayed on the east side of the lake knew that only one boat had been there. They also knew that Jesus had not left in it with his disciples. But the next day 23some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks. 24They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus. 25They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, ‚ÄúRabbi, when did you get here?‚ÄĚ
26Jesus answered, ‚ÄúI tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted. 27 Don't work for food that spoils. Work for food that gives eternal life. The Son of Man will give you this food, because God the Father has given him the right to do so.‚ÄĚ
28‚ÄúWhat exactly does God want us to do?‚ÄĚ the people asked.
29Jesus answered, ‚ÄúGod wants you to have faith in the one he sent.‚ÄĚ
30They replied, ‚ÄúWhat miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do? 31 For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄėGod gave them bread from heaven to eat.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
32Jesus then told them, ‚ÄúI tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven. 33And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.‚ÄĚ
34The people said, ‚ÄúSir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!‚ÄĚ
35Jesus replied:
I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty. 36I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me. 37Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
38I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me, 39and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day. 40My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
41The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven. 42They were asking each other, ‚ÄúIsn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?‚ÄĚ
43Jesus told them:
Stop grumbling! 44No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day. 45 One of the prophets wrote, ‚ÄúGod will teach all of them.‚ÄĚ And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
46The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father. 47I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
48I am the bread that gives life! 49Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died. 50But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die. 51I am that bread from heaven! Everyone who eats it will live forever. My flesh is the life-giving bread I give to the people of this world.
52They started arguing with each other and asked, ‚ÄúHow can he give us his flesh to eat?‚ÄĚ
53Jesus answered:
I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man. 54But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day. 55My flesh is the true food, and my blood is the true drink. 56If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
57The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me. 58The bread that comes down from heaven isn't like what your ancestors ate. They died, but whoever eats this bread will live forever.
59Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things.
The Words of Eternal Life
60Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, ‚ÄúThis is too hard for anyone to understand.‚ÄĚ
61Jesus knew that his disciples were grumbling. So he asked, ‚ÄúDoes this bother you? 62What if you should see the Son of Man go up to heaven where he came from? 63 The Spirit is the one who gives life! Human strength can do nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are from that life-giving Spirit. 64But some of you refuse to have faith in me.‚ÄĚ Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
65Then Jesus said, ‚ÄúYou cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.‚ÄĚ
66Because of what Jesus said, many of his disciples turned their backs on him and stopped following him. 67Jesus then asked his twelve disciples if they also were going to leave him. 68 Simon Peter answered, ‚ÄúLord, there is no one else that we can go to! Your words give eternal life. 69We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.‚ÄĚ
70Jesus told his disciples, ‚ÄúI chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!‚ÄĚ 71Jesus was talking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. He would later betray Jesus, even though he was one of the twelve disciples.
translation_text:
Yesu kefa balaŇč kakpoŇčse banoŇč (5000) ween…õ…õ
Mateo 14:13-21; Maake 6:30-44; Luka 9:10-17
1Weeya akalans…õ…õ Ňčaale w…Ēle n…õ, Yesu toŇča Galelia depaa keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke bela nyi Tiberiase n…õ gyu ke diŇčgyiŇč. 2BalaŇč burum sila …Ē gb…õ…õ gyu bot…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi be naa gyakoloŇč nombia na nombia dinaana Ňčan …Ē be y…õ…õ lee mena keŇč …Ē be ky…Ē balaŇč kaweese n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 3KeŇčte Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč gyeŇča bula daale kekyaa bot…Ē…Ē. 4DebaŇč kenaŇč na Gyudatena nyeekel…õ…õwee kedi keŇč ta benaa.
5Yesu g…õ…õsew…Ē k…õ…õ te …Ē naaw…Ē nyi balaŇč burum ne k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč keŇčte …Ē b…Ē…Ēse Filipo nyiaa, ‚ÄúAleŇč te dee nyiŇč ween…õ…õ l…õ…õ fa balaŇč burum k…õw…Ē din…Ē…Ē kedi?‚ÄĚ 6Yesu b…Ē…Ēse Filipo mena nombia k…õŇča na waa mo k…õ…õ wa agyueŇč nawolo nyi Ňčon gbagba wulaa …Ē gyeŇč kpene keŇč waa y…õ…õ. 7Bot…Ē…Ē te Filipo tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúP…õ walaŇč kamas…õ kenyiŇč kyomii di koraŇč na, kaboena nyi d…õ…õ mo tomy…õ…õre kegyia Ňčolo gyaalaŇčna gyanaara k…Ēba bo l…õ…õ ween…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ 8KeŇčte …Ē kaseela baŇč Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Anderiase, Ňčon …Ē kegyia y…õna Sim…Ēn Pita n…õ yakow…Ē nyiaa, 9‚ÄúBu Ňčolo kyaa k…õbo keŇč …Ē dana bodobodose anoŇč na kpebia bala. M…Ēna woŇč te k…õŇča gyae key…õ…õ balaŇč burum k…õw…Ē.‚ÄĚ 10Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu yako …Ē kaseela baŇč nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź yela balaŇč baŇč pou ke tisi kyakee.‚ÄĚ Na Ňčalese murumuru Ňčaale kyaa bot…Ē…Ē, mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baŇč pou tisiw…Ē kyakee bot…Ē…Ē. Baala baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ baa y…õ…õ Ňčgba balaŇč kakpoŇčse banoŇč n…õ. 11KeŇčte Yesu moo bodobodose Ňčan te …Ē fanew…Ē fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč, te …Ē moo Ňča kp…õl…õ…õ fa balaŇč baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ. Mena ke te …Ē moo kpebia Ňčan m…Ē te …Ē fanew…Ē fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč te …Ē kp…õl…õ…õ Ňča fa balaŇč baŇč pou. BalaŇč baŇč diiw…Ē baa ba kegyaebii. 12BalaŇč baŇč pou kedi baa n…õ Yesu yako …Ē kaseela baŇč nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź t…Ē…Ēsee ween…õ…õ akaŇč Ňčan pou, …õ na yeli Ňča kew…Ēl…õ…õ ya.‚ÄĚ 13Ween…õ…õ Ňčan balaŇč baŇč be dii kaase n…õ, be t…Ē…Ēsee Ňča buruburu akaŇč Ňčan na be nyiŇča doŇčase kufu ala. 14BalaŇč baŇč kena gyakoloŇč nombia Ňčan Yesu be y…õ…õ n…õ be yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKei y…õna Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčon …Ē be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ ampaŇč.‚ÄĚ 15Na Yesu taŇč kena nyi balaŇč baŇč ne gyae be kaa kyaŇč e, na baa Ňčere e mo e see be gyoo mena d…Ē…Ē Ňčon waageŇč w…ĒŇča …Ē wose lee be man gyu ke kyaa bula keŇč d…Ē…Ē bela.
Yesu keta loŇč d…Ē…Ē
Mateo 14:22-33; Maake 6:45-52
16Kelii balin…Ē…Ē n…õ Yesu kaseela baŇč besew…Ē gyu depaa keŇč n…Ē…Ēman, 17keŇčte ba gyoow…Ē degbele daale man te ba moo ba nyee baŇč bee gyu Kapanum don…Ē…Ē man. Te wee …Ētomante keŇč n…õ, maa gyuusu mena walaŇč ŇčonaŇč lee yeŇč man na waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč. 45Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra baŇč man Ňčolo Ňčmarasew…Ē see nyiaa, ‚ÄėWurubuar…õ ne gyaela waa wola walaŇč kamas…õ nombia.‚Äô Mena d…Ē…Ē walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē tei me kya Wurubuar…õ nombia te …Ē kasee Ňča n…õ, waa k…ĒŇč me gyaŇč. 46Kei b…õ…õ wolo nyi Ňčolo te naa me kya Ňčon ya. WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē ba lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč k…ĒŇč n…õ, Ňčon waageŇč naa na e. 47AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyiaa, walaŇč Ňčon …Ē l…õ…õ maŇč di na waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa.
48MaŇč y…õna nyeedoŇč ween…õ…õ Ňčan. 49∆ź naana…Ē dii ween…õ…õ Ňčan b…õ…õ baake Ňča nyi maana n…õ depampaa keŇč man, m…Ēna ba yekeew…Ē. 50M…Ēna nyeedoŇč ween…õ…õ Ňčan ba lee Wurubuar…õ man k…ĒŇč te m…õ…õ yako Ňčon Ňča wose man nombia n…õ d…Ē…Ē, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē di ke na …Ē be gyae waa yeŇč ya. 51MaŇč y…õna nyeedoŇč ween…õ…õ Ňčan ba lee adido k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ. WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē di mena ween…õ…õ k…õŇča na, waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa. Ween…õ…õ Ňčan maa mo fa balaŇč na baa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč n…õ y…õna ma wosenaane.‚ÄĚ
52Bot…Ē…Ē te Gyudatena baŇč gyoo aŇčmaareŇč na d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúSena te balee kei waa tale mo …Ē wosenaane fa daa na dee di?‚ÄĚ 53KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyiaa, nyi akpaa …õ te di maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu wosenaane na …õ keny…Ē…Ē me fatabo ya n…õ, …õ be gyae …õ ke nyiŇč nyeedoŇč ya. 54WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon waa di ma wosenaane na waa ny…Ē…Ē me fatabo n…õ, waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč. Wee …Ētomante keŇč man n…õ, maa gyuusu mena walaŇč ŇčonaŇč lee yeŇč man. 55Nawolo nyi ma wosenaane y…õna ween…õ…õ anediŇč gbagba, te me fatabo m…Ē y…õna kolo aneny…Ē…ĒŇč gbagba. 56WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē di ma wosenaane te …Ē ny…Ē…Ē me fatabo na, daa na e ta bese walaŇč dokoloŇč. 57Me kya Ňčon …Ē ne fa balaŇč nyeedoŇč n…õ kpila na maŇč, te wa adaworoma d…Ē…Ē te me dana nyeedoŇč. Mena ke m…Ē te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē di ma wosenaane na, maa fa e nyeedoŇč. 58MaŇč y…õna ween…õ…õ Ňčan ba lee adido k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ. Ma te y…õ…õ Ňčgba ween…õ…õ Ňčan …õ naana…Ē be dii te b…Ē…Ē yekee n…õ ya. WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon wa di mena ween…õ…õ k…õŇča n…õ, waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa.‚ÄĚ 59DebaŇč keŇč Yesu na wolo Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man Kapanum don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, te …Ē yako mena nombia k…õŇča.
60Yesu kaseela baŇč kenyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ be yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMena kawola k…õŇča man dana doŇč. Am…Ēte ne gyaela waa tale l…õ…õ Ňča do?‚ÄĚ 61Yesu ka gyeŇč nyi …Ē kaseela baŇč na Ňčorokoso lee nombia k…õŇča wose man n…õ …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúNombia k…õŇča ne takaa Ňčon? 62KenaŇč na akpaa …õ naa maŇč DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon na gyem mee gyu adido, bot…Ē…Ē keŇč m…Ē…Ē lee na sena te …õ ke y…õ…õ? 63Wurubuar…õ feliŇč ne faa na balaŇč nyeedoŇč. Kpene keŇč denibalaŇč na mo baŇč gbagba be doŇč y…õ…õ n…õ be taŇč t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya. Nombia Ňčan m…Ē…Ē yako Ňčon n…õ lee Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon man te Ňč…õ…õ fa nyeedoŇč. 64M…Ēna …õ man baale b…õ…õ l…õ…õ di ya.‚ÄĚ Yesu yako nombia k…õŇča nawolo nyi lee kewalaŇč keŇč na, …Ē wulaa …Ē gyeŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyae baa l…õ…õ e di ya, na Ňčon waa kaa lese e fa. 65KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKei d…Ē…Ē te me yako Ňčon nyiaa, akpaa me kya te fa Ňčolo gb…õ…õ nyi waa k…ĒŇč me gyaŇč ya na, …Ē be gyae waa tale k…ĒŇč ya n…õ.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Jesus' Brothers Don't Have Faith in Him
1Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him. 2 It was almost time for the Festival of Shelters, 3and Jesus' brothers said to him, ‚ÄúWhy don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing. 4No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!‚ÄĚ 5Even Jesus' own brothers had not yet become his followers.
6Jesus answered, ‚ÄúMy time hasn't yet come, but your time is always here. 7The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things. 8Go on to the festival. My time hasn't yet come, and I am not going.‚ÄĚ 9Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee.
Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
10After Jesus' brothers had gone to the festival, he went secretly, without telling anyone.
11During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, ‚ÄúWhere is he?‚ÄĚ 12The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, ‚ÄúJesus is a good man,‚ÄĚ while others were saying, ‚ÄúHe is lying to everyone.‚ÄĚ 13But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
14When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching. 15The leaders were surprised and said, ‚ÄúHow does this man know so much? He has never been taught!‚ÄĚ
16Jesus replied:
I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me. 17If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me. 18If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie. 19Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
20The crowd replied, ‚ÄúYou're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?‚ÄĚ
21Jesus answered:
I worked one miracle, and it amazed you. 22 Moses commanded you to circumcise your sons. But it wasn't really Moses who gave you this command. It was your ancestors, and even on the Sabbath you circumcise your sons 23 in order to obey the Law of Moses. Why are you angry with me for making someone completely well on the Sabbath? 24Don't judge by appearances. Judge by what is right.
25Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, ‚ÄúIsn't this the man they want to kill? 26Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah? 27But how could that be? No one knows where the Messiah will come from, but we know where this man comes from.‚ÄĚ
28As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he shouted, ‚ÄúDo you really think you know me and where I came from? I didn't come on my own! The one who sent me is truthful, and you don't know him. 29But I know the one who sent me, because I came from him.‚ÄĚ
30Some of the people wanted to arrest Jesus right then. But no one even laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come. 31A lot of people in the crowd put their faith in him and said, ‚ÄúWhen the Messiah comes, he surely won't perform more miracles than this man has done!‚ÄĚ
Officers Sent To Arrest Jesus
32When the Pharisees heard the crowd arguing about Jesus, they got together with the chief priests and sent some temple police to arrest him. 33But Jesus told them, ‚ÄúI will be with you a little while longer, and then I will return to the one who sent me. 34You will look for me, but you won't find me. You cannot go where I am going.‚ÄĚ
35The people asked each other, ‚ÄúWhere can he go to keep us from finding him? Is he going to some foreign country where our people live? Is he going there to teach the Greeks? 36What did he mean by saying that we will look for him, but won't find him? Why can't we go where he is going?‚ÄĚ
Streams of Life-Giving Water
37 On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, ‚ÄúIf you are thirsty, come to me and drink! 38 Have faith in me, and you will have life-giving water flowing from deep inside you, just as the Scriptures say.‚ÄĚ 39Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory.
The People Take Sides
40When the crowd heard Jesus say this, some of them said, ‚ÄúHe must be the Prophet!‚ÄĚ 41Others said, ‚ÄúHe is the Messiah!‚ÄĚ Others even said, ‚ÄúCan the Messiah come from Galilee? 42 The Scriptures say that the Messiah will come from the family of King David. Doesn't this mean that he will be born in David's hometown of Bethlehem?‚ÄĚ 43The people started taking sides against each other because of Jesus. 44Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him.
The Leaders Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus
45When the temple police returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, they were asked, ‚ÄúWhy didn't you bring Jesus here?‚ÄĚ
46They answered, ‚ÄúNo one has ever spoken like this man!‚ÄĚ
47The Pharisees said to them, ‚ÄúHave you also been fooled? 48Not one of the chief priests or the Pharisees has faith in him. 49And these people who don't know the Law are under God's curse anyway.‚ÄĚ
50 Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said, 51‚ÄúOur Law doesn't let us condemn people before we hear what they have to say. We cannot judge them before we know what they have done.‚ÄĚ
52Then they said, ‚ÄúNicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.‚ÄĚ
A Woman Caught in Sin
53Everyone else went home,
translation_text:
Yesu na …Ē naabia
1Nombia k…õŇča w…Ēle n…õ Yesu t…õ…õw…Ē kila Galelia don…Ē…Ēse man. ∆Ü te gyae nyi waa gyu Gyudia t…õ…õle man ya, nawolo nyi Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ ne gyae gb…õ…õ baa ko e. 2DebaŇč kenaŇč na Gyudatena kef…õ…õse man kekyaa wee dinaa kedi ta benaa. 3Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu …Ē naabia yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKaboena nyi nya lee k…õbo na n ke gyu Gyuda t…õ…õle man na n kaseela baŇč ke nyiŇč naa gyakoloŇč nombia Ňčan n ne y…õ…õ n…õ. 4Nawolo nyi walaŇč ne gyae …Ē yele ka lee na, …Ē b…õ…õ y…õ…õ kolo weese ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē n keta n ne y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia k…õŇča n…õ, kaboena nyi nya lese n wose deb…Ē…Ē fa balaŇč pou.‚ÄĚ 5∆Ü naabia baŇč kolosi nombia k…õŇča nawolo nyi baŇč gbagba koraŇč te l…õ…õ e di ya. 6KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúDebaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba see fa maŇč n…õ te lii ta ya, m…Ēna …õm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē debaŇč kamas…õ dei fa Ňčon. 7T…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč b…õ…õ k…Ēla Ňčon ya m…Ēna maŇč d…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ k…Ēla maŇč, nawolo nyi me gyakaa m…õ…õ yako w…Ē nyi be nombia Ňčan b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ be dei ya. 8∆źm…õ…õ …õ ta gyu kedi wee dinaa keŇč, maŇč be gyae maa gyu ya nawolo nyi ma debaŇč keŇč te lii ta ya.‚ÄĚ 9∆Ü ke yako nombia k…õŇča fa w…Ē taŇč n…õ, te Ňčon d…Ē…Ēw…Ē Galelia t…õ…õle man.
10Yesu …Ē naabia ke laŇč ba wee dinaa keŇč dedii man n…õ, te Ňčon m…Ē korow…Ē gyu bot…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna …Ē weese …Ē wose bo. 11Na Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč d…ĒŇč…õ…õ b…õ…õ k…õo e na b…õ…õ b…Ē…Ēse nyiaa ‚Äú∆Ü kyaa aleŇč?‚ÄĚ 12KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč man balaŇč burum gyoo kekolosi na d…ĒŇča lee Yesu wose man. ‚ÄúBaale kpa …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč kpaakpaa e, te baale m…Ē kpa koa, …Ē na beo balaŇč bo na baa yo lee Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õneŇč Ňčan man.‚ÄĚ 13M…Ēna Ňčolo te tale kolosi …Ē wose man nombia balaŇč man ya, nawolo nyi be yee Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč.
14Be kedi ba wee dinaa keŇč kelii nsana nsana n…õ, Yesu gyuu Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man …Ē na wola balaŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia. 15∆Ü kawola keŇč doo Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč n…Ē…Ē te be b…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúSena te balee kei y…õ…õw…Ē gyeŇč nombia k…õŇča keŇč nyi …Ē te kasee lee Ňčolo gyaŇč ya?‚ÄĚ 16KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKpene keŇč m…õ…õ wolo n…õ ta lee maŇč gbagba gyaŇč ya, m…Ēna ka lee me kya Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ gyaŇč. 17WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae nyi waa di Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ētenate waa gyeŇč nyi kpene keŇč m…õ…õ wolo n…õ, lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč yaa lee maŇč gbagba gyaŇč. 18WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne kolosi Ňčon gbagba …Ē baam d…Ē…Ē n…õ, na …Ē ne gyae yele bo wa fa …Ē wose. Te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae bo nyi waa lese Ňčon …Ē be kpila e n…õ yele na, …Ē nombia doo na ke gb…õ…õ keŇč nyi dekara be doo ke man ya. 19Na Mosesi faa na Ňčon mmaraase Ňčan yee? M…Ēna …õ man Ňčolo bee di Ňče d…Ē…Ē ya. WoŇč d…Ē…Ē te …õ ne gyae …õ ka ko maŇč?‚ÄĚ 20Bot…Ē…Ē te balaŇč dikpii keŇč tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúFeliŇčkum…õ…õ doo n man. Am…Ēte ne gyae la waa ko neŇč?‚ÄĚ 21KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúGyakoloŇč nombii dokoloŇč te me y…õ…õw…Ē …õ kef…õ…õfowee, keŇč na doo na Ňčon gyakoloŇč n…õ. 22Mosesi yako Ňčon nyi ya kara …õ bia baalawose. (Kei ta lee Mosesi gyaŇč ya, m…Ēna ka lee …õ naana…Ē gyaŇč), mena d…Ē…Ē …õ ne kara …õ bia baalawose …õ kef…õ…õfowee. 23Nyi akpaa …õ ne kara …õ bia baalawose …õ kef…õ…õfo te keŇč b…õ…õ w…Ēl…õ…õ ya mmaraa ya na, weera d…Ē…Ē te …õ na gyeŇč baŇč do maŇč nyi ma te ky…Ē walaŇč dibiliŇč kawee kef…õ…õfowee? 24∆ź na barase bu balaŇč f…Ē…Ē ya, …õ di nombia na Ňče gb…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ
25KeŇčte Gyerusal…õmtena baale b…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúNa balee kei te b…õ…õ k…õo nyi baa ko n…õ yee? 26∆ź k…õ…õ …Ē ka seŇč balaŇč man …Ē ne kolosi keŇč Ňčolo b…õ…õ b…Ē…Ēse e daale ya. Yaa n…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē kegyiise baŇč te l…õ…õ e do nyi Ňčon y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon? 27Balee kei d…Ē…Ē de popou gyeŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē ba lee. M…Ēna akpaa nyi nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon waa k…ĒŇč na, Ňčolo be gyae waa gyeŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč waa lee ya.‚ÄĚ 28Yesu k…õ…õ wola ab…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man n…õ, …Ē gyinaa …Ē woya b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMena d…Ē…Ē …õ gyeŇč maŇč nideli na bot…Ē…Ē keŇč m…Ē…Ē lee? Na maŇč gbagba me baam d…Ē…Ē te me k…ĒŇčaw…Ē ya. WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ …Ē y…õ…õ anokoarete e, m…Ēna Ňčon d…Ē…Ē …õ ba gyeŇč e ya. 29M…Ēna maŇč d…Ē…Ē ma gyeŇč e nawolo …Ē gyaŇč te ma leew…Ē te Ňčon kpila na maŇč.‚ÄĚ 30Mena d…Ē…Ē be yela baa kyaŇč Yesu, m…Ēna Ňčolo te tale mo …Ē nyiŇčmaa tina e ya, nawolo nyi debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba see nyi waa yeŇč n…õ te lii ta ya. 31M…Ēna balaŇč burum l…õ…õ e di te be b…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon de k…ĒŇč na, waa y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia kela balee kei aney…õ…õŇč k…õŇča?‚ÄĚ
Be kpila balaŇč nyi baa gyu ke kyaŇč Yesu
32Farasiitena ke nyii mena keŇč balaŇč ne kolosi nombia k…õŇča adadawoya man lee Yesu wose man n…õ, baŇč na Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise kpila kot…Ēt…Ē…Ētena baŇč b…õ…õ deke Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč n…õ nyi baa gyu kekyaŇč e k…ĒŇč. 33KeŇčte Yesu yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMaa kyaa …õ gyaŇč bo weeya akalans…õ…õ Ňčaale na maa bese gyu walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ gyaŇč. 34∆ź ke k…õo maŇč m…Ēna …õ be gyae …õ kena maŇč ya, te bot…Ē…Ē keŇč me kyaa m…Ē n…õ, …õ be gyae …õ ke tale k…ĒŇč ya.‚ÄĚ 35Bot…Ē…Ē te Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúAleŇč yaa balee kei ne gyae waa yeu gyu te d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ naa e ya? Yaa …Ē ne gyae waa yeu ba gyu Girikitena gyaŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč de balaŇč be yaasee gyu n…õ …Ē kaa wola w…Ē ab…Ē…Ē?‚ÄĚ 36∆Ü ke yako nyi, ‚Äú∆ź ke k…õo maŇč m…Ēna …õ be gyae …õ kena maŇč ya, Te bot…Ē…Ē keŇč me gyu n…õ …õ be gyae …õ ke tale gyu ya n…õ, na sena yaa …Ē ne yako baa? Na woŇč nombia y…õna k…õŇča baa?‚ÄĚ
NyeedoŇč nombia
37Gyudatena kef…õ…õse man kekyaa weekedi …Ētomante keŇč n…õ, Yesu korow…Ē seŇč te …Ē gyinaa …Ē n…Ē…Ē kolosi te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa nyaŇč Ňčon loŇčboaneŇč dana neŇč na k…ĒŇč me gyaŇč na n kaa ny…Ē…Ē loŇč. 38‚ÄėWalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē l…õ…õ maŇč di na, nyeedoŇč nombia gyae ka lee …Ē man k…ĒŇč Ňčgba mena keŇč loŇč na lee ditiribuŇč man k…ĒŇč n…õ. Nn…õ te Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see yakow…Ē.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 39Yesu kekolosi kei n…õ, na Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon waa kaa mo do baŇč baa l…õ…õ e di n…õ man nombia yaa …Ē ne kolosi n…õ. DebaŇč kenaŇč na …Ē ta moo ta Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon do Ňčolo man ya, nawolo nyi Yesu gyoorobiiri keŇč ta lee ta deb…Ē…Ē ya.
BalaŇč kekpase lee Yesu d…Ē…Ē
40BalaŇč baŇč ke nyii Yesu n…Ē…Ēwoya k…õŇča n…õ be man baale yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAmpaŇč, balee kei y…õna Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ.‚ÄĚ 41Baale m…Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee kei y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa kaa k…ĒŇč n…õ ampaŇč. M…Ēna baale m…Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, na Galelia t…õ…õle man te nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon waa lee ya.‚ÄĚ 42Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan ne yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyeel…õ…õre Ňčon waa lee bo B…õtel…õh…õm don…Ē…Ē man bot…Ē…Ē keŇč Gyoo Defidi be kyaa n…õ, te waa lee bo gyoo Defidi loŇčtoro man.‚ÄĚ 43Lee Yesu d…Ē…Ē, balaŇč baŇč man kpasew…Ē. 44BalaŇč baŇč baale gyaew…Ē nyi baa kyaŇč Yesu, m…Ēna Ňčolo te tale mo …Ē nyiŇčmaa tina e ya.
Gyudatena kegyiise te l…õ…õ Yesu di ya
45W…Ēle w…Ēle keŇč n…õ, kot…Ēt…Ē…Ētena baŇč b…õ…õ deke Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč te b…Ē…Ē kpila w…Ē n…õ besew…Ē k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŇč gyaŇč. KeŇčte kegyiise baŇč b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera d…Ē…Ē te …õ te kyaŇč e k…ĒŇč ya?‚ÄĚ 46KeŇčte be tiran…Ē…Ē fa kegyiise baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúŇäolo na Ňčolo te kolosi ta see Ňčgba balee kei n…õ ya.‚ÄĚ 47Te Farasiitena baŇč b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúDoona …Ē taŇč beo …õm…õ…õ m…Ē? 48∆ź te nyii nyi don…Ē…Ē kegyiise k…õw…Ē man Ňčolo yaa Farasiitena k…õw…Ē man Ňčolo te l…õ…õ Yesu di? 49M…Ēna mena balaŇč burum k…õw…Ē baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč Mosesi mmaraa keŇč ya n…õ, baŇč doola kefaala t…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ 50Nikodemose Ňčon …Ē be kyaa gyu Yesu gyaŇč n…õ y…õ…õ kegyiise baŇč man Ňčolo e. Te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, 51‚ÄúDa mmaraa ne fa daa gb…õ…õ nyi d…õ…õ bu Ňčolo f…Ē…Ē keŇč de te nyii ta …Ē n…Ē…Ēman nombia yee?‚ÄĚ 52KeŇčte be tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeera nyaŇč m…Ē ne kolosi Ňčgba n y…õ…õ Galelia baale e n…õ? NyaŇč k…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan man nideli, n kena nyi Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčolo ta lee ta Galelia ya.‚ÄĚ 53KeŇčte be popou yaaseew…Ē dokoloŇč dokoloŇč gyu be d…õ…õse.

english_text:
1but Jesus walked out to the Mount of Olives. 2Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
3The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses brought in a woman who had been caught in bed with a man who wasn't her husband. They made her stand in the middle of the crowd. 4Then they said, ‚ÄúTeacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband. 5 The Law of Moses teaches that a woman like this should be stoned to death! What do you say?‚ÄĚ
6They asked Jesus this question, because they wanted to test him and bring some charge against him. But Jesus simply bent over and started writing on the ground with his finger.
7 They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, ‚ÄúIf any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!‚ÄĚ 8Once again he bent over and began writing on the ground. 9The people left one by one, beginning with the oldest. Finally, Jesus and the woman were there alone.
10Jesus stood up and asked her, ‚ÄúWhere is everyone? Isn't there anyone left to accuse you?‚ÄĚ
11‚ÄúNo sir,‚ÄĚ the woman answered.
Then Jesus told her, ‚ÄúI am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is the Light for the World
12 Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, ‚ÄúI am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.‚ÄĚ
13 The Pharisees objected, ‚ÄúYou are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!‚ÄĚ
14Jesus replied:
Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going. 15You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone. 16If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me. 17 Your Law requires two witnesses to prove that something is true. 18I am one of my witnesses, and the Father who sent me is the other one.
19‚ÄúWhere is your Father?‚ÄĚ they asked.
‚ÄúYou don't know me or my Father!‚ÄĚ Jesus answered. ‚ÄúIf you knew me, you would know my Father.‚ÄĚ
20Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come.
You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
21Jesus also told them, ‚ÄúI am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.‚ÄĚ
22The people asked, ‚ÄúDoes he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?‚ÄĚ
23Jesus answered, ‚ÄúYou are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't. 24This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.‚ÄĚ
25‚ÄúWho are you?‚ÄĚ they asked Jesus.
Jesus answered, ‚ÄúI am exactly who I told you at the beginning. 26There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.‚ÄĚ
27No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
28Jesus went on to say, ‚ÄúWhen you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me. 29The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.‚ÄĚ
30After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him.
The Truth Will Set You Free
31Jesus told the people who had faith in him, ‚ÄúIf you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples. 32 You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.‚ÄĚ
33 They answered, ‚ÄúWe are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?‚ÄĚ
34Jesus replied:
I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin! 35And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family. 36If the Son gives you freedom, you are free! 37I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts. 38I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you.
Your Father Is the Devil
39The people said to Jesus, ‚ÄúAbraham is our father!‚ÄĚ
Jesus replied, ‚ÄúIf you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did. 40Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that. 41But you are doing exactly what your father does.‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúDon't accuse us of having someone else as our father!‚ÄĚ they said. ‚ÄúWe just have one father, and he is God.‚ÄĚ
42Jesus answered:
If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own. 43Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying? 44 Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
45Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me. 46Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth. 47Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God.
Jesus and Abraham
48The people told Jesus, ‚ÄúWe were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!‚ÄĚ
49Jesus answered, ‚ÄúI don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me. 50I don't want honor for myself. But there is one who wants me to be honored, and he is also the one who judges. 51I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.‚ÄĚ
52Then the people said, ‚ÄúNow we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die? 53Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?‚ÄĚ
54Jesus replied, ‚ÄúIf I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God, 55even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says. 56Your father Abraham was really glad to see me.‚ÄĚ
57‚ÄúYou are not even 50 years old!‚ÄĚ they said. ‚ÄúHow could you have seen Abraham?‚ÄĚ
58Jesus answered, ‚ÄúI tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.‚ÄĚ 59The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
translation_text:
Alo futoolite Ňčolo nombia
1BalaŇč baŇč ke yaasee n…õ, Yesu gyuuw…Ē oliifi bula keŇč d…Ē…Ē. 2T…õ…õ kena n…õ …Ē besew…Ē k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man. BalaŇč baŇč pou be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ kaa wuuw…Ē …Ē d…Ē…Ē te …Ē tisiw…Ē kyaa …Ē na wola w…Ē Wurubuar…õ nombia. 3Bot…Ē…Ē te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŇč moo alo Ňčolo Ňčon …Ē ba lee …Ē baale w…Ēle te b…Ē…Ē kyaŇč e nyiŇčmaa nyiŇčmaa n…õ k…ĒŇč kaa seŇč Yesu na balaŇč baŇč pou siaman. 4KeŇčte be yako Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, da te kyaŇč alo kei …Ē ne gyae baala. 5Mosesi mmaraa Ňčan na wola daa nyi dee fuŇčii mena alo kei din…Ē…Ē boya ko. Te nyaŇč m…Ē n kpoo?‚ÄĚ 6Be yako nombia k…õŇča nyi baa mo teese Yesu n…Ē…Ēman, na baa nyiŇč mo nombia gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna Yesu g…ĒŇčasew…Ē te …Ē moo …Ē nyimbii Ňčmarase kolo t…õ…õle man. 7Be kaseŇč b…õ…õ daa e nyi waa tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē n…õ, …Ē korow…Ē seŇč te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč Ňčon n gyeŇč nyi n te y…õ…õ ta dukum see ya n…õ taŇčgb…õ…õ mo boe fuŇč e‚ÄĚ 8Te …Ē besew…Ē g…ĒŇčase Ňčmarase kolo t…õ…õle man. 9Be ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, be yaaseew…Ē dokoloŇč dokoloŇč tina e yela. Be man kegyiise baŇč taŇčgb…õ…õ la lee bot…Ē…Ē, te kaa Yesu na alo Ňčon na balaŇč dikpii keŇč baageŇč. 10KeŇčte Yesu korow…Ē seŇč te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse alo Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúAlo nde w…Ē? Ňäon y…õna nombiadiiri Ňčon. 51AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē di me nombia d…Ē…Ē n…õ …Ē be gyae waa yeŇč ya.‚ÄĚ
