english_text:
Jesus Heals a Man Born Blind
1As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth. 2Jesus' disciples asked, ‚ÄúTeacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?‚ÄĚ
3‚ÄúNo, it wasn't!‚ÄĚ Jesus answered. ‚ÄúBut because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him. 4As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work. 5 While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.‚ÄĚ
6After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes. 7Then he said, ‚ÄúGo wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.‚ÄĚ The man went and washed in Siloam, which means ‚ÄúOne Who Is Sent.‚ÄĚ When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
8The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man. 9Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, ‚ÄúI am that man.‚ÄĚ
10‚ÄúThen how can you see?‚ÄĚ they asked.
11He answered, ‚ÄúSomeone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.‚ÄĚ
12‚ÄúWhere is he now?‚ÄĚ they asked.
‚ÄúI don't know,‚ÄĚ he answered.
The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
13-14The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees. 15They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, ‚ÄúJesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.‚ÄĚ
16Some of the Pharisees said, ‚ÄúThis man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.‚ÄĚ
Others asked, ‚ÄúHow could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?‚ÄĚ
Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves, 17they asked the man, ‚ÄúWhat do you say about this one who healed your eyes?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúHe is a prophet!‚ÄĚ the man told them.
18But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents 19and asked them, ‚ÄúIs this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?‚ÄĚ
20The man's parents answered, ‚ÄúWe are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind. 21But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.‚ÄĚ
22-23The man's parents said this because they were afraid of their leaders. The leaders had already agreed that no one was to have anything to do with anyone who said Jesus was the Messiah.
24The leaders called the man back and said, ‚ÄúSwear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.‚ÄĚ
25The man replied, ‚ÄúI don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!‚ÄĚ
26‚ÄúWhat did he do to you?‚ÄĚ they asked. ‚ÄúHow did he heal your eyes?‚ÄĚ
27The man answered, ‚ÄúI have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?‚ÄĚ
28The leaders insulted the man and said, ‚ÄúYou are his follower! We are followers of Moses. 29We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.‚ÄĚ
30‚ÄúHow strange!‚ÄĚ the man replied. ‚ÄúHe healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from. 31We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners. 32And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind. 33Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.‚ÄĚ
34The leaders told the man, ‚ÄúYou have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?‚ÄĚ Then they said, ‚ÄúYou can never come back into any of our synagogues!‚ÄĚ
35When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, ‚ÄúDo you have faith in the Son of Man?‚ÄĚ
36He replied, ‚ÄúSir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.‚ÄĚ
37‚ÄúYou have already seen him,‚ÄĚ Jesus answered, ‚Äúand right now he is talking with you.‚ÄĚ
38The man said, ‚ÄúLord, I put my faith in you!‚ÄĚ Then he worshiped Jesus.
39Jesus told him, ‚ÄúI came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.‚ÄĚ
40When the Pharisees heard Jesus say this, they asked, ‚ÄúAre we blind?‚ÄĚ
41Jesus answered, ‚ÄúIf you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
A Story about Sheep
1Jesus said:
I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen. 2-3But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
4When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice. 5The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
6Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about.
Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
7Jesus said:
I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep. 8Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them. 9I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
10A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully. 11 I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep. 12Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock. 13Hired workers run away because they don't care about the sheep.
14 I am the good shepherd. I know my sheep, and they know me. 15 Just as the Father knows me, I know the Father, and I give up my life for my sheep. 16I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
17The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again. 18No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
19The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them. 20Many of them said, ‚ÄúHe has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?‚ÄĚ
21But others said, ‚ÄúHow could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is Rejected
22 That winter, Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Temple Festival. 23One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch, 24and the people gathered all around him. They said, ‚ÄúHow long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!‚ÄĚ
25Jesus answered:
I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am. 26But since you are not my sheep, you don't believe me. 27My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me, 28and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand. 29 My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands, 30and I am one with the Father.
31Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus. 32But he said, ‚ÄúI have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?‚ÄĚ
33They answered, ‚ÄúWe are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!‚ÄĚ
34 Jesus replied:
In your Scriptures doesn't God say, ‚ÄúYou are gods‚ÄĚ? 35You can't argue with the Scriptures, and God spoke to those people and called them gods. 36So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world. 37If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me. 38But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
39Again they wanted to arrest Jesus. But he escaped 40 and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there, 41many people came to him. They were saying, ‚ÄúJohn didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.‚ÄĚ 42A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.

Ňäolo be gyae waa tale l…õo w…Ē lee me gyaŇč ya. 29Me kya Ňčon …Ē ba mo w…Ē do me nyiŇčmaa man n…õ, dana doŇč kela kolo kamas…õ te Ňčolo be gyae waa tale l…õo w…Ē lee me kya nyiŇčmaa man ya. 30Daa na me kya y…õ…õ walaŇč dokoloŇč.‚ÄĚ
31Bot…Ē…Ē te Gyudatena baŇč gate boya baa fuŇč e bela. 32KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe kya te yeli ma te y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa burum …õ siaman, Ňče man woŇčti d…Ē…Ē te …õ ne gyae …õ ke fuŇčii maŇč boya?‚ÄĚ 33Te Gyudatena baŇč tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNa ab…Ē…Ē nya ay…õ…õŇč k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē te d…õ…õ gyae dee fuŇčii neŇč boya ya, m…Ēna mena keŇč n ne yako mmusuo nombia d…Ē…Ē. Nawolo nyi n y…õ…õ deniwalaŇč e, m…Ēna n na mo n wose maa na Wurubuar…õ.‚ÄĚ 34Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBa te Ňčmarase …õm…õ…õ gbagba mmaraa t…Ēne keŇč man nyi, Wurubuar…õ kpa, …õ y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ kemaasasew…Ē yee? 35Da gyeŇč nyi kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan ne yako n…õ y…õ…õ anokoare kekpaa. Te Wurubuar…õ de baake balaŇč baŇč …Ē ba mo …Ē nombia Ňčan fa n…õ nyi be y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ kemaasasew…Ē na, 36weera d…Ē…Ē te …õ ne yako maŇč Ňčon Wurubuar…õ ba lese maŇč te …Ē be kpila maŇč k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ nyi m…õ…õ mo ma wose maa na Wurubuar…õ keŇč m…õ…õ yako nyi me y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ bu e d…Ē…Ē? 37Akpaa me b…õ…õ y…õ…õ kpene keŇč me kya Ňčon kpa ma y…õ…õ ya na, …õ na l…õ…õ maŇč di ya, 38te akpaa m…õ…õ y…õ…õ Ňča na, nyi …õ be l…õ…õ maŇč di koraŇč ya na …õ l…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan m…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ di, na …õ ka gyeŇč na …õ kenyii as…õ…õ nyiaa daa na me kya Ňčon y…õ…õ dokoloŇč‚ÄĚ 39Mena d…Ē…Ē be gyaew…Ē baa kyaŇč e bela, te …Ē sarakeew…Ē lee be man.
40KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē toŇč Gy…Ē…Ēdan boo keŇč gyu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč Gy…Ēn be kyaa s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč kaalaŇč n…õ, te …Ē ke kyaaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē. 41BalaŇč burum k…ĒŇčaw…Ē …Ē gyaŇč te be yako d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúGy…Ēn te y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia ya, m…Ēna kpene kamas…õ keŇč …Ē be yako lee balee kei wose man n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč.‚ÄĚ 42KeŇčte balaŇč burum baŇč ba seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē n…õ l…õ…õ Yesu di.

english_text:
The Death of Lazarus
1-2 A man by the name of Lazarus was sick in the village of Bethany. He had two sisters, Mary and Martha. This was the same Mary who later poured perfume on the Lord's head and wiped his feet with her hair. 3The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
4When Jesus heard this, he said, ‚ÄúHis sickness won't end in death. It will bring glory to God and his Son.‚ÄĚ
5Jesus loved Martha and her sister and brother. 6But he stayed where he was for two more days. 7Then he said to his disciples, ‚ÄúNow we will go back to Judea.‚ÄĚ
8‚ÄúTeacher,‚ÄĚ they said, ‚Äúthe people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?‚ÄĚ
9Jesus answered, ‚ÄúAren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble. 10But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.‚ÄĚ 11Then he told them, ‚ÄúOur friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.‚ÄĚ
12They replied, ‚ÄúLord, if he is asleep, he will get better.‚ÄĚ 13Jesus really meant that Lazarus was dead, but they thought he was talking only about sleep.
14Then Jesus told them plainly, ‚ÄúLazarus is dead! 15I am glad I wasn't there, because now you will have a chance to put your faith in me. Let's go to him.‚ÄĚ
16Thomas, whose nickname was ‚ÄúTwin,‚ÄĚ said to the other disciples, ‚ÄúCome on. Let's go, so we can die with him.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
17When Jesus got to Bethany, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days. 18Bethany was less than three kilometers from Jerusalem, 19and many people had come from the city to comfort Martha and Mary because their brother had died.
20When Martha heard that Jesus had arrived, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed in the house. 21Martha said to Jesus, ‚ÄúLord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died. 22Yet even now I know that God will do anything you ask.‚ÄĚ
23Jesus told her, ‚ÄúYour brother will live again!‚ÄĚ
24 Martha answered, ‚ÄúI know he will be raised to life on the last day, when all the dead are raised.‚ÄĚ
25Jesus then said, ‚ÄúI am the one who raises the dead to life! Everyone who has faith in me will live, even if they die. 26And everyone who lives because of faith in me will never really die. Do you believe this?‚ÄĚ
27‚ÄúYes, Lord!‚ÄĚ she replied. ‚ÄúI believe you are the Christ, the Son of God. You are the one we hoped would come into the world.‚ÄĚ
28After Martha said this, she went and privately said to her sister Mary, ‚ÄúThe Teacher is here, and he wants to see you.‚ÄĚ 29As soon as Mary heard this, she got up and went out to Jesus. 30He was still outside the village where Martha had gone to meet him. 31Many people had come to comfort Mary, and when they saw her quickly leave the house, they thought she was going out to the tomb to cry. So they followed her.
32Mary went to where Jesus was. Then as soon as she saw him, she knelt at his feet and said, ‚ÄúLord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.‚ÄĚ
33When Jesus saw that Mary and the people with her were crying, he was terribly upset 34and asked, ‚ÄúWhere have you put his body?‚ÄĚ
They replied, ‚ÄúLord, come and you will see.‚ÄĚ
35Jesus started crying, 36and the people said, ‚ÄúSee how much he loved Lazarus.‚ÄĚ
37Some of them said, ‚ÄúHe gives sight to the blind. Why couldn't he have kept Lazarus from dying?‚ÄĚ
38Jesus was still terribly upset. So he went to the tomb, which was a cave with a stone rolled against the entrance. 39Then he told the people to roll the stone away. But Martha said, ‚ÄúLord, you know that Lazarus has been dead four days, and there will be a bad smell.‚ÄĚ
40Jesus replied, ‚ÄúDidn't I tell you that if you had faith, you would see the glory of God?‚ÄĚ
41After the stone had been rolled aside, Jesus looked up toward heaven and prayed, ‚ÄúFather, I thank you for answering my prayer. 42I know that you always answer my prayers. But I said this, so the people here would believe you sent me.‚ÄĚ
43When Jesus had finished praying, he shouted, ‚ÄúLazarus, come out!‚ÄĚ 44The man who had been dead came out. His hands and feet were wrapped with strips of burial cloth, and a cloth covered his face.
Jesus then told the people, ‚ÄúUntie him and let him go.‚ÄĚ
The Plot To Kill Jesus
(Matthew 26.1-5; Mark 14.1,2; Luke 22.1,2)
45Many of the people who had come to visit Mary saw the things Jesus did, and they put their faith in him. 46Others went to the Pharisees and told what Jesus had done. 47Then the chief priests and the Pharisees called the council together and said, ‚ÄúWhat should we do? This man is working a lot of miracles. 48If we don't stop him now, everyone will put their faith in him. Then the Romans will come and destroy our temple and our nation.‚ÄĚ
49One of the council members was Caiaphas, who was also high priest that year. He spoke up and said, ‚ÄúYou people don't have any sense at all! 50Don't you know it is better for one person to die for the people than for the whole nation to be destroyed?‚ÄĚ 51Caiaphas did not say this on his own. As high priest that year, he was prophesying that Jesus would die for the nation. 52Yet Jesus would not die just for the Jewish nation. He would die to bring together all of God's scattered people. 53From that day on, the council started making plans to put Jesus to death.
54Because of this plot against him, Jesus stopped going around in public. He went to the town of Ephraim, which was near the desert, and he stayed there with his disciples.
55It was almost time for Passover. Many of the Jewish people who lived out in the country had come to Jerusalem to get themselves ready for the festival. 56They looked around for Jesus. Then when they were in the temple, they asked each other, ‚ÄúYou don't think he will come here for Passover, do you?‚ÄĚ
57The chief priests and the Pharisees told the people to let them know if any of them saw Jesus. This is how they hoped to arrest him.

Ba kebake Yesu nyeeman
Mateo 26:1-5; Maake 14:1-2; Luka 22:12
45Gyudatena burum baŇč b…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč M…õ…õre gyaŇč te b…Ē…Ē naa kpene keŇč Yesu be y…õ…õ n…õ l…õ…õ e di. 46M…Ēna be man baale besew…Ē gyu Farasiitena baŇč gyaŇč gyu ke yako w…Ē kpene keŇč Yesu be y…õ…õ n…õ. 47KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baake don…Ē…Ē kegyiise gyaŇčee. Nawolo nyi balee kei ne y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia burum. 48Akpaa de tina e yela te …Ē gyakaa …Ē ne y…õ…õ mena tom kei na, balaŇč baŇč pou baa l…õ…õ e di na ke yela Romantena kaa y…õl…õ…õ de Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč na baa l…õo de t…õ…õle pou.‚ÄĚ 49Te balaŇč baŇč man Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Kaifase Ňčon …Ē be y…õ…õ saese kegyia kulutoo kenaŇč man n…õ yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …õ ba gyeŇč daale ya. 50∆ź ba gyeŇč nyi dei nyi walaŇč dokoloŇč waa yeŇč fa balaŇč baŇč pou kela keŇč nyi t…õ…õle pou balaŇč kew…Ēl…õ…õ yee?‚ÄĚ 51Te y…õ…õ Kaifase gbagba agyueŇč yaa …Ē moow…Ē yako nombia k…õŇča ya, m…Ēna keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia kulutoo kenaŇč man d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ yela …Ē yako mena nombia ŇčenaŇč see nyi, kaboena nyi Yesu waa yeŇč fa Gyudatena pou. 52Te y…õ…õ nyi Gyudatena baageŇč te Yesu waa yeŇč fa ya, m…Ēna waa yeŇč bo fa Wurubuar…õ bia baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē yaasee n…õ na waa nyiŇč mo w…Ē kaa yilaa. 53LeenaŇč wee kenaŇč keŇč kegyiise baŇč be gyaŇčee n…õ, te be fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ gyae gb…õ…õ keŇč baa mo ko Yesu. 54Lee mena d…Ē…Ē na Yesu b…õ…õ tekaa lese …Ē wose deb…Ē…Ē Gyudia t…õ…õle man bela ya. M…Ēna …Ē leew…Ē bot…Ē…Ē gyu don…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi Eferam te ka benaa na depampaa dinaa daale n…õ. Bot…Ē…Ē te Ňčon na …Ē kaseela baŇč gyuuw…Ē ke kyaa.
55Na Gyudatena nyeekel…õ…õwee kedi keŇč ta benaa, mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč burum lee don…Ē…Ēse Ňčan man gyu Gyerusal…õm be kaa gyoraa ba wose p…õna nyeekel…õ…õwee kedi keŇč kelii. 56DebaŇč kenaŇč man pou na b…õ…õ k…õo Yesu. Be ka seŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man n…õ be b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúSena y…õna ya gyueŇč? Doona …Ē be gyae waa k…ĒŇč nyeekel…õ…õwee kedi kei man ya?‚ÄĚ 57Na Wurubuar…õ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŇč te yako w…Ē nyiaa, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē gyeŇč Yesu dekyae na …Ē kaa yako w…Ē na be kaa kyaŇč e.

english_text:
At Bethany
(Matthew 26.6-13; Mark 14.3-9)
1Six days before Passover Jesus went back to Bethany, where he had raised Lazarus from death. 2A meal had been prepared for Jesus. Martha was doing the serving, and Lazarus himself was there.
3 Mary took a very expensive bottle of perfume and poured it on Jesus' feet. She wiped them with her hair, and the sweet smell of the perfume filled the house.
4A disciple named Judas Iscariot was there. He was the one who was going to betray Jesus, and he asked, 5‚ÄúWhy wasn't this perfume sold for 300 silver coins and the money given to the poor?‚ÄĚ 6Judas did not really care about the poor. He asked this because he carried the moneybag and sometimes would steal from it.
7Jesus replied, ‚ÄúLeave her alone! She has kept this perfume for the day of my burial. 8 You will always have the poor with you, but you won't always have me.‚ÄĚ
A Plot To Kill Lazarus
9A lot of people came when they heard that Jesus was there. They also wanted to see Lazarus, because Jesus had raised him from death. 10So the chief priests made plans to kill Lazarus. 11He was the reason that many of the people were turning from them and putting their faith in Jesus.
Jesus Enters Jerusalem
(Matthew 21.1-11; Mark 11.1-11; Luke 19.28-40)
12The next day a large crowd was in Jerusalem for Passover. When they heard that Jesus was coming for the festival, 13 they took palm branches and went out to greet him. They shouted,
‚ÄúHooray!
God bless the one who comes
in the name of the Lord!
God bless the King
of Israel!‚ÄĚ
14Jesus found a donkey and rode on it, just as the Scriptures say,
15 ‚ÄúPeople of Jerusalem,
don't be afraid!
Your King is now coming,
and he is riding
on a donkey.‚ÄĚ
16At first, Jesus' disciples did not understand. But after he had been given his glory, they remembered all this. Everything had happened exactly as the Scriptures said it would.
17-18A crowd had come to meet Jesus because they had seen him call Lazarus out of the tomb. They kept talking about him and this miracle. 19But the Pharisees said to each other, ‚ÄúThere is nothing we can do! Everyone in the world is following Jesus.‚ÄĚ
Some Greeks Want To Meet Jesus
20Some Greeks had gone to Jerusalem to worship during Passover. 21Philip from Bethsaida in Galilee was there too. So they went to him and said, ‚ÄúSir, we would like to meet Jesus.‚ÄĚ 22Philip told Andrew. Then the two of them went to Jesus and told him.
The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up
23Jesus said:
The time has come for the Son of Man to be given his glory. 24I tell you for certain that a grain of wheat that falls on the ground will never be more than one grain unless it dies. But if it dies, it will produce lots of wheat. 25 If you love your life, you will lose it. If you give it up in this world, you will be given eternal life. 26If you serve me, you must go with me. My servants will be with me wherever I am. If you serve me, my Father will honor you.
27Now I am deeply troubled, and I don't know what to say. But I must not ask my Father to keep me from this time of suffering. In fact, I came into the world to suffer. 28So Father, bring glory to yourself.
A voice from heaven then said, ‚ÄúI have already brought glory to myself, and I will do it again!‚ÄĚ 29When the crowd heard the voice, some of them thought it was thunder. Others thought an angel had spoken to Jesus.
30Then Jesus told the crowd, ‚ÄúThat voice spoke to help you, not me. 31This world's people are now being judged, and the ruler of this world is already being thrown out! 32If I am lifted up above the earth, I will make everyone want to come to me.‚ÄĚ 33Jesus was talking about the way he would be put to death.
34 The crowd said to Jesus, ‚ÄúThe Scriptures teach that the Messiah will live forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?‚ÄĚ
35Jesus answered, ‚ÄúThe light will be with you for only a little longer. Walk in the light while you can. Then you won't be caught walking blindly in the dark. 36Have faith in the light while it is with you, and you will be children of the light.‚ÄĚ
The People Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus
After Jesus had said these things, he left and went into hiding. 37He had worked a lot of miracles among the people, but they were still not willing to have faith in him. 38 This happened so that what the prophet Isaiah had said would come true,
‚ÄúLord, who has believed
our message?
And who has seen
your mighty strength?‚ÄĚ
39The people could not have faith in Jesus, because Isaiah had also said,
40 ‚ÄúThe Lord has blinded
the eyes of the people,
and he has made
the people stubborn.
He did this so that they
could not see
or understand,
and so that they
would not turn to the Lord
and be healed.‚ÄĚ
41Isaiah said this, because he saw the glory of Jesus and spoke about him. 42Even then, many of the leaders put their faith in Jesus, but they did not tell anyone about it. The Pharisees had already given orders for the people not to have anything to do with anyone who had faith in Jesus. 43And besides, the leaders liked praise from others more than they liked praise from God.
Jesus Came To Save the World
44In a loud voice Jesus said:
Everyone who has faith in me also has faith in the one who sent me. 45And everyone who has seen me has seen the one who sent me. 46I am the light that has come into the world. No one who has faith in me will stay in the dark.
47I am not the one who will judge those who refuse to obey my teachings. I came to save the people of this world, not to be their judge. 48But everyone who rejects me and my teachings will be judged on the last day by what I have said. 49I don't speak on my own. I say only what the Father who sent me has told me to say. 50I know that his commands will bring eternal life. This is why I tell you exactly what the Father has told me.

Na bot…Ē…Ē keŇč me kyaa na, Ňčon m…Ē ke nyiŇč kpu na maŇč. WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē na som maŇč na, me kya m…Ē waa lese …Ē yele.‚ÄĚ

27Te Yesu besew…Ē yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ dek…Ēk…Ēr…Ēk…Ē…Ē te kyaŇč maŇč nideli, te sena yaa maa yako? ‚ÄėMaa yako nyi me kya, lese maŇč lee mena kaale dinaa kei man?‚Äô M…Ēna mena kaale kei d…Ē…Ē te me k…ĒŇčaw…Ē t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 28KeŇčte Yesu fanew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe kya, lese n gyoorobiiri wola.‚ÄĚ Te be nyii woya Ňčaale kolosiw…Ē lee adido nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe te wulaa lese me gyoorobiiri wola, te maa bese lese ke wola.‚ÄĚ 29BalaŇč burum baŇč ba seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē te b…Ē…Ē nyii woya Ňčan n…õ, be man baale yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆Üga faela‚ÄĚ te baale m…Ē yakow…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo kolosi la na e lee adido.‚ÄĚ 30KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te mena n…Ē…Ēwoya k…õŇča k…ĒŇčaw…Ē, na maŇč d…Ē…Ē ya. 31N…õn…õ…õ y…õna debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ waa di balaŇč nombia t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. Nawolo nyi walaŇč Ňčon …Ē d…ĒŇč…õ…õ ditiŇčt…õ…õ man n…õ, …Ē ba gyeŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē ne gyu ya. 36WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne fa keŇčmaŇčee nyiŇča …Ē kyaa …õ gyaŇč n…õ, …õ l…õ…õ e di na …õ kenyiŇč bese balaŇč baŇč be doo keŇčmaŇčee man.‚ÄĚ Yesu kekolosi nombia k…õŇča taŇč n…õ, …Ē w…ĒŇča …Ē wose kekyaa yenaŇč daale keŇč b…Ē…Ē ne naa e ya.
Gyudatena burum te l…õ…õ Yesu di ya
37Yesu y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia burum be siaman, m…Ēna k…õŇča pou w…Ēle na be b…õe ba te l…õ…õ e di ya. 38Kei yela nombia Ňčan Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Yesaya be yako n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē ampaŇč. Yesaya yakow…Ē nyiaa,
‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, am…Ēte l…õ…õ na nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē yako n…õ di?
Am…Ēte te de GbeŇčgyoo lese …Ē w…Ē…Ēle wola?‚ÄĚ
39Lee mena d…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ē ne tale baa l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nombia di ya Ňčgba mena keŇč Yesaya be yako n…õ. 40∆Ü besew…Ē yako nyiaa,
‚ÄúWurubuar…õ te y…õl…õ…õ be sia na b…Ē…Ē nyiŇč naa ya,
te …Ē t…Ē…Ē ba agyueŇčse na b…Ē…Ē tale nyii kolo man ya.

41Yesaya te kolosi nombia k…õŇča yakaa ya, m…Ēna …Ē wulaa naa Yesu gyoorobiiri keŇč din…Ē…Ē keŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē kolosiw…Ē lee …Ē wose man. 42Nombia k…õŇča w…Ēle n…õ Gyudatena kegyiise burum l…õ…õ Yesu di, m…Ēna Farasiitena baŇč d…Ē…Ē ba te tale lese ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč deb…Ē…Ē ya, nawolo nyi be yeew…Ē nyi Farasiitena baŇč baa t…Ē…Ē w…Ē gb…õ…õ nyi be na k…ĒŇč b…Ē …Ēsom deni man bela ya. 43Be ta lese ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč deb…Ē…Ē ya nawolo nyi b…õ…õ gyae bo nyi balaŇč ka lese ba yele kela Wurubuar…õ ka lese ba yele.
44KeŇčte Yesu gyinaa …Ē n…Ē…Ē kolosi nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč Ňčon n l…õ…õ maŇč di n…õ, na maageŇč te n l…õ…õw…Ē di ya m…Ēna Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ m…Ē yaa n l…õ…õw…Ē di. 45NyaŇč walaŇč Ňčon n naa maŇč na, n te naa walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ. 46Me k…ĒŇčae t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba keŇčmaŇčee n…õ na maa ŇčmaŇčee fa balaŇč. NyaŇč Ňčon n l…õ…õ maŇč di na n be gyae n ke d…Ē…Ē ditiŇčt…õ…õ man ya. 47WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē nyii me nombia te …Ē te l…õ…õ Ňča do ya na, m…Ē…Ē gyae maa di …Ē nombia ya. Nawolo nyi ma te k…ĒŇč me kaa di balaŇč nombia t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē na maa bu w…Ē f…Ē…Ē ya, m…Ēna me k…ĒŇčae me kaa l…õ…õ ba nyee.
48NyaŇč Ňčon n b…õ…õ maŇč te n te l…õ…õ me nombia ya na, nombiadiiri Ňčolo kyaa keŇč waa di n nombia. N…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan m…Ē…Ē kolosi n…õ, Ňčan te baa mo di n nombia wee …Ētomante keŇč man na baa bu neŇč f…Ē…Ē. 49Nawolo nyi ma te kolosi maŇč gbagba me doŇč man ya, m…Ēna me kya Ňčon …Ē be kpila maŇč n…õ, Ňčon yako na maŇč kpene keŇč maa yako na mena keŇč maa yako ke. 50Te maŇč gyeŇč m…Ē nyi kpene keŇč …Ē be yako daa nyi d…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ, keŇč ne faa na nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē kpene keŇč m…õ…õ yako n…õ, y…õ…õ keŇč me kya be yako maŇč nyi maa yako.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Jesus Washes the Feet of His Disciples
1It was before Passover, and Jesus knew that the time had come for him to leave this world and to return to the Father. He had always loved his followers in this world, and he loved them to the very end.
2Even before the evening meal started, the devil had made Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, decide to betray Jesus.
3Jesus knew he had come from God and would go back to God. He also knew that the Father had given him complete power. 4So during the meal Jesus got up, removed his outer garment, and wrapped a towel around his waist. 5He put some water into a large bowl. Then he began washing his disciples' feet and drying them with the towel he was wearing.
6But when he came to Simon Peter, this disciple asked, ‚ÄúLord, are you going to wash my feet?‚ÄĚ
7Jesus answered, ‚ÄúYou don't really know what I am doing, but later you will understand.‚ÄĚ
8‚ÄúYou will never wash my feet!‚ÄĚ Peter replied.
‚ÄúIf I don't wash you,‚ÄĚ Jesus told him, ‚Äúyou don't really belong to me.‚ÄĚ
9Peter said, ‚ÄúLord, don't wash just my feet. Wash my hands and my head.‚ÄĚ
10Jesus answered, ‚ÄúPeople who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.‚ÄĚ 11Jesus knew who would betray him. That is why he said, ‚Äúexcept for one of you.‚ÄĚ
12 After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said:
Do you understand what I have done? 13You call me your teacher and Lord, and you should, because that is who I am. 14And if your Lord and teacher has washed your feet, you should do the same for each other. 15I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you. 16 I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them. 17You know these things, and God will bless you, if you do them.
18 I am not talking about all of you. I know the ones I have chosen. But what the Scriptures say must come true. And they say, ‚ÄúThe man who ate with me has turned against me!‚ÄĚ 19I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am. 20 I tell you for certain that anyone who welcomes my messengers also welcomes me, and anyone who welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me.
Jesus Tells What Will Happen to Him
(Matthew 26.20-25; Mark 14.17-21; Luke 22.21-23)
21After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, ‚ÄúI tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.‚ÄĚ 22They were confused about what he meant. And they just stared at each other.
23Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal, 24and Simon motioned for this disciple to find out which one Jesus meant. 25So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, ‚ÄúLord, which one of us are you talking about?‚ÄĚ
26Jesus answered, ‚ÄúI will dip this piece of bread in the sauce and give it to the one I was talking about.‚ÄĚ
Then Jesus dipped the bread and gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. 27Right then Satan took control of Judas.
Jesus said, ‚ÄúJudas, go quickly and do what you have to do.‚ÄĚ 28No one at the meal understood what Jesus meant. 29But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor. 30Judas took the piece of bread and went out.
It was already night.
The New Command
31After Judas had gone, Jesus said:
Now the Son of Man will be given glory, and he will bring glory to God. 32Then, after God is given glory because of him, God will bring glory to him, and God will do it very soon.
33 My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, ‚ÄúYou cannot go where I am going.‚ÄĚ 34 But I am giving you a new command. You must love each other, just as I have loved you. 35If you love each other, everyone will know that you are my disciples.
Peter's Promise
(Matthew 26.31-35; Mark 14.27-31; Luke 22.31-34)
36Simon Peter asked, ‚ÄúLord, where are you going?‚ÄĚ
Jesus answered, ‚ÄúYou can't go with me now, but later on you will.‚ÄĚ
37Peter asked, ‚ÄúLord, why can't I go with you now? I would die for you!‚ÄĚ
38‚ÄúWould you really die for me?‚ÄĚ Jesus asked. ‚ÄúI tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Jesus Is the Way to the Father
1Jesus said to his disciples, ‚ÄúDon't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me. 2There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you. 3After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together. 4You know the way to where I am going.‚ÄĚ
5Thomas said, ‚ÄúLord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?‚ÄĚ
6 ‚ÄúI am the way, the truth, and the life!‚ÄĚ Jesus answered. ‚ÄúWithout me, no one can go to the Father. 7If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.‚ÄĚ
8Philip said, ‚ÄúLord, show us the Father. That is all we need.‚ÄĚ
9Jesus replied:
Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father? 10Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
11Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do. 12I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father. 13Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father. 14I will do whatever you ask me to do.
The Holy Spirit Is Promised
15 Jesus said to his disciples:
If you love me, you will do as I command. 16Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you. 17The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
18I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you. 19In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live. 20Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you. 21 If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
22The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, ‚ÄúLord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?‚ÄĚ
23Jesus replied:
If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them. 24But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
25I have told you these things while I am still with you. 26But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
27I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
28You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
29I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me. 30I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me. 31I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him.
It is time for us to go now.

english_text:
Jesus Is the True Vine
1Jesus said to his disciples:
I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener. 2He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit. 3You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
4Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me. 5I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me. 6If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
7Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered. 8When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored. 9I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you. 10If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
11I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am. 12 Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you. 13The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them. 14And you are my friends, if you obey me. 15Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
16You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name. 17So I command you to love each other.
The World's Hatred
18If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first. 19If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you. 20 Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
21People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me. 22If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
23Everyone who hates me also hates my Father. 24I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too. 25 This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, ‚ÄúPeople hated me for no reason.‚ÄĚ
26I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me. 27Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.

english_text:
1I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away. 2You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor. 3They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me. 4I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said.
The Work of the Holy Spirit
I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things. 5But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going. 6You are very sad from hearing all of this. 7But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
8The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment. 9The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me. 10They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again. 11And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
12I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand. 13 The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen. 14The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you. 15Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you.
Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
16Jesus told his disciples, ‚ÄúFor a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.‚ÄĚ
17They said to each other, ‚ÄúWhat does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father? 18What is this ‚Äėlittle while‚Äô that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.‚ÄĚ
19Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said:
You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me. 20I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
21When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world. 22You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel. 23When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name. 24You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
25I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these. 26You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you. 27God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him. 28I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
29The disciples said, ‚ÄúNow you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples. 30At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.‚ÄĚ
31Jesus replied:
Do you really believe me? 32The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone. 33I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.

M…Ēna akpaa …Ē k…ĒŇč na waa yela baa gyeŇč nyi be nombia be dei ya. 9B…Ē…Ē gyeŇč kpene keŇč y…õ…õ dukum ke ya, nawolo nyi ba te l…õ…õ maŇč di ya. 10Te b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč kpene keŇč m…Ē doo na ke gb…õ…õ ya, nawolo nyi m…õ…õ bese mee gyu me kya Ňčon gyaŇč te …õ be gyae …õ ka tekaa naa maŇč bela ya. 11B…Ē…Ē gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia kedi keŇč wose man daale ya, nawolo nyi …Ēb…Ēnsam Ňčon …Ē ne di t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyoori n…õ, Wurubuar…õ te wulaa di …Ē nombia bu e f…Ē…Ē.
12Me dana nombia burum maa yako Ňčon, m…Ēna debokeiman man n…õ Ňče dana yuŇč kela Ňčon. 13M…Ēna akpaa Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon …Ē bee di dekara ya n…õ de k…ĒŇč na, waa lese nombia Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ pou n…õ wola Ňčon. Nawolo nyi na Ňčon gbagba …Ē baam d…Ē…Ē te …Ē ne kolosi ya, m…Ēna nombia Ňčan pou …Ē be nyii n…õ yaa …Ē ne kolosi na waa lese nombia Ňčan pou gyae ke k…ĒŇč wee daale n…õ wola Ňčon. 14Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon waa yela balaŇč kena me gyoorobiiri, nawolo nyi waa lese kpene kamas…õ keŇč me dana maa yako Ňčon n…õ wola Ňčon. 15Kpene kamas…õ keŇč me kya dana na ke y…õ…õ me wui, mena d…Ē…Ē te m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyi Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon waa lese kpene kamas…õ keŇč me dana maa yako Ňčon n…õ wola Ňčon.‚ÄĚ
∆ź waraŇčase gyae kabese gyoŇč
16KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúTeka kyomii …õ be gyae …õ kena maŇč bela ya, m…Ēna kenaŇč w…Ēle n…õ be gyae ke kyare ya, …õ ka bese naa maŇč.‚ÄĚ 17Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē kaseela baŇč baale b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúSena yaa balee kei ne yako baa, …Ē kpa ‚ÄėTeka kyomii …õ be gyae …õ kena maŇč bela ya, te kenaŇč w…Ēle na be gyae ke kyare ya na, …õ ka bese naa maŇč.‚Äô Nawolo nyi mee gyu me kya gyaŇč d…Ē…Ē?‚ÄĚ 18KeŇčte ba besew…Ē b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚Äú ‚ÄėTeka kyomii‚Äô kei m…Ē na wolo baa sena? D…Ē…Ē ne nyii nombia k…õŇča man ya.‚ÄĚ
19Na Yesu gyeŇč nyi b…õ…õ gyae baa b…Ē…Ēse e nombia k…õŇča man, mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKeŇč m…Ē…Ē yako Ňčon nyi, ‚ÄėTeka kyomii …õ be gyae …õ kena maŇč bela ya, m…Ēna kenaŇč w…Ēle na be gyae ke kyare ya …õ ka bese naa maŇč‚Äô n…õ yaa …õ ne b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča n…õ? 20AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, …õ ke wii na …õ ka yey…õ…õ m…Ēna balaŇč baŇč be loo gyakaa t…õ…õle kei ab…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē n…õ baa di gyoŇč. ∆źm…õ…õ …õ ka yey…õ…õ, m…Ēna …õ kayey…õ…õ keŇč gyae kaa bese gyoŇč kedi. 21Akpaa alo na doŇčee na …Ē na yey…õ…õ, nawolo nyi …Ē debaŇč te lii. M…Ēna akpaa …Ē lola bu Ňčon na, ne y…õ…õ e gyoŇč nyi …Ē ta lola bu k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. Mena gyoŇč kei ne yele …Ē ne wolee …Ē kaale keŇč …Ē be naa n…õ. 22Mena ke m…Ē yaa doo fa Ňčon. DebaŇč kei na …õ na yey…õ…õ, m…Ēna maa bese k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč na …õ wose ke f…õ…õ Ňčon, na …õ ke di gyoŇč. Te Ňčolo be gyae waa tale lese gyoŇč kenaŇč lee …õ man ya. 23Mena wee kenaŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, …õ be gyae …õ ke b…Ē…Ēse maŇč daale ya. M…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, kpene kamas…õ keŇč …õ ka sola me kya Ňčon ma yele man na, waa mo fa Ňčon. 24Kaalii na gyeŇč kei n…õ, …õ ta sola ta kolo ma yele man ya. ∆ź sola na …õ ke nyiŇč, na ke y…õ…õ Ňčon gyoŇč nideli.
25M…õ…õ yako Ňčon …õ ke nyii nyiaa, debaŇč daale gyae kelii na m…Ē…Ē gyae maa tekaa kolosi na Ňčon aduya man bela ya. M…Ēna maa toro me kya Ňčon nombia yako Ňčon. 26DebaŇč kenaŇč de lii n…õ, …õ ka sola e ma yele man. M…Ēna te y…õ…õ nyi maa seŇč …õ naw…Ē…Ē man na maa sola me kya Ňčon fa Ňčon ya. 27Nawolo nyi me kya Ňčon gbagba ne gyae Ňčon, keŇč …õ ne gyae maŇč te …õ be l…õ…õ di nyi ma lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 28Daa na me kya kyaa la p…õte ma lee …Ē gyaŇč k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē, te n…õn…õ…õ m…õ…õ bese m…õ…õ lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē mee gyu me kya ŇčonaŇč gyaŇč.‚ÄĚ
29Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē kaseela baŇč yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē n b…õ…õ kolosi aduya man fa daa bela ya. N ta lese nombia Ňčan man wola daa. 30N…õn…õ…õ da te naa nyi n gyeŇč kpene kamas…õ, te tekaboena nyi Ňčolo d…õ…õ b…Ē…Ēse neŇč nombia daale bela ya. Kei te yeli da te naa nyi n lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč.‚ÄĚ 31KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ …õ ne l…õ…õ maŇč di? 32DebaŇč keŇč ne k…ĒŇč te ke taŇč kek…ĒŇč, keŇč …õ popou …õ ke yaasee. WalaŇč kamas…õ waa gyu …Ē d…õ…õ tina maŇč yela na ke ka maageŇč. M…Ēna na maageŇč kyaa la ya, nawolo nyi me kya Ňčon kpu…õ na maŇč. 33M…õ…õ yako nombia k…õŇča baa, na …õ ke ta me d…Ē…Ē nyiŇč wosef…õ…õreŇč. BalaŇč baa yela …õ kena diyem nideli t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna …õ nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē na …õ ka seŇč keŇčkeŇč nawolo nyi me te di t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē!‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Jesus Prays
1After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed:
Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you. 2And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him. 3 Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent. 4I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do. 5Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
6You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you. 7They know that you gave me everything I have. 8I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me. 9I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them. 10All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
11Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one. 12 While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
13I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do. 14I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
15Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one. 16They don't belong to this world, and neither do I. 17Your word is the truth. So let this truth make them completely yours. 18I am sending them into the world, just as you sent me. 19I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
20I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me. 21I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
22I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one. 23I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
24Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created. 25Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me. 26I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
translation_text:
Yesu kefane
1Yesu ke yako nombia k…õŇča taŇč n…õ, …Ē gyinaa …Ē sia k…õ…õ adido te …Ē fanew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúTaata, debaŇč keŇč te lii. Lese maŇč n bu Ňčon yele na maŇč m…Ē ka lese n yele. 2Nawolo nyi n te fa maŇč doŇč balaŇč pou d…Ē…Ē, nyi maa fa baŇč pou n ba mo do maŇč me nyiŇčmaa man n…õ nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa. 3NyeedoŇč kekpaa kenaŇč y…õna nyiaa, balaŇč pou baa gyeŇč neŇč na baa gyeŇč nyi nyaŇč nyaageŇč y…õna Wurubuar…õ Ňčon n ne di anokoare. Na baa bese gyeŇč maŇč Yesu Kristo Ňčon n be kpili n…õ. 4Me ta lese n yele t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi ma te y…õ…õ tom keŇč n ba fa maŇč n…õ kelii k…Ē …Ēto. 5Taata, n…õn…õ…õ lese me gyoorobiiri keŇč me dana n siaman p…õte n be y…õ…õ kpene kamas…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ wola.
6Me ta lese n din…Ē…Ē wola balaŇč baŇč n ba lese w…Ē lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē fa maŇč n…õ. Be y…õ…õ n balaŇčw…Ē te n moo w…Ē fa maŇč. Be te di n nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē, 7te n…õn…õ…õ ba te naa nyi kpene kamas…õ keŇč me dana n…õ lee n gyaŇč. 8Nawolo nyi ma te yako w…Ē nombii kamas…õ keŇč n be yako maŇč n…õ, te be l…õ…õ Ňča do. Ba te naa nyi ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč nyi ma lee n gyaŇč, te ba te l…õ…õ di nyi nyaŇč kpila na maŇč. 9M…õ…õ fane baa fa w…Ē. Na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou yaa m…õ…õ fane maa fa ya. M…õ…õ fane maa fa balaŇč baŇč n ba mo w…Ē fa maŇč n…õ, nawolo nyi be y…õ…õ n wui. 10Kpene kamas…õ keŇč n dana n…õ y…õ…õ me wui, te keŇč maŇč m…Ē dana n…õ y…õ…õ n wui. Te n te t…õ…õ be d…Ē…Ē lese me gyoorobiiri wola. 11N…õn…õ…õ m…õ…õ k…ĒŇč baa n gyaŇč. M…Ē…Ē gyae maa tekaa kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē bela ya, m…Ēna baŇč s…õ…õ be kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. Me kya Ňčon ayim…õ…õ be doo n man ya, doŇč keŇč n ba mo gyee maŇč n…õ, mo mena doŇč kenaŇč gyee w…Ē nideli, na baa y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč Ňčgba mena keŇč daa na neŇč y…õ…õ dokoloŇč n…õ. 12DebaŇč keŇč me kyaa be gyaŇč n…õ, me gyee w…Ē nideli lee n doŇč keŇč n ba do me man n…õ d…Ē…Ē. Me gyee w…Ē nideli keŇč be man Ňčolo ta yoo ya. Ňäon kaboena nyi waa yo n…õ waageŇč yoo la, na ke yela Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan ke nyiŇč k…ĒŇč mena.
13N…õn…õ…õ m…õ…õ k…ĒŇč n gyaŇč. Te debaŇč kei keŇč me s…õ…õ me kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ, m…õ…õ yako nombia k…õŇča baa na ke yela gyoŇč keŇč doo me maŇč n…õ ke nyiŇč doo be man nideli. 14Ma te kolosi n nombia fa w…Ē m…Ēna t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč k…Ēla w…Ē, nawolo nyi baŇč na w…Ē ta tekaa y…õ…õ dokoloŇč ya, Ňčgba mena keŇč daa na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč ta tekaa y…õ…õ dokoloŇč ya n…õ. 15M…Ē…Ē ne fane nyi nya lese w…Ē lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ya, m…Ēna m…õ…õ fane bo nyi nya gyee w…Ē nideli na b…Ē…Ē yale walaŇčkum Ňčon k…Ēta man ya. 16Ňägba mena keŇč daa na t…õ…õle kei balaŇč te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč ya n…õ, mena kee yaa baŇč m…Ē na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč ya. 17Mo nya anokoare nombia Ňčan desina w…Ē see. Nawolo nyi n nombia Ňčan doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ. 18Mena keŇč n be kpila maŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ, mena ke yaa maŇč m…Ē ne kpila w…Ē t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. 19BaŇč d…Ē…Ē te ma moo ma wose fa neŇč, na baŇč m…Ē ke nyiŇč mo ba wose fa neŇč kekpaakekpaa.
20Na me kaseela k…õw…Ē baageŇč yaa m…õ…õ fane fa ya. M…Ēna m…õ…õ fane m…õ…õ fa balaŇč baŇč pou baa nyii me kaseela k…õw…Ē nombia te baa l…õ…õ maŇč di n…õ. 21M…õ…õ fane bela nyiaa, be popou baa y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč Ňčgba mena keŇč me doo nyaŇč me kya man te nyaŇč m…Ē n doo me man n…õ. Mena ke yaa yela baa doo de man na t…õ…õle kei balaŇč pou ke nyiŇč l…õ…õ di nyi nyaŇč kpila na maŇč. 22Ma te fa w…Ē gyoorobiiri keŇč n ba mo fa maŇč n…õ, na baa y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč Ňčgba mena keŇč daa na neŇč y…õ…õ dokoloŇč te loŇč b…õ…õ lam da nsana ya n…õ. 23Me doo be man Ňčgba mena keŇč nyaŇč m…Ē n doo me man n…õ, na baa nyiŇč y…õ…õ dokoloŇč keŇč nyi loŇč gba be lam ba nsana ya. Na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč ke nyiŇč gyeŇč nyi nyaŇč kpila na maŇč, te n ne gyae be nombia Ňčgba mena keŇč n ne gyae me nombia n…õ.
24Taata! M…õ…õ gyae nyi me kaseela baŇč n ba mo w…Ē fa maŇč n…õ, baa kyaa me gyaŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč me kyaa n…õ, na baa nyiŇč naa me gyoorobiiri keŇč n ba mo fa maŇč n…õ. N moo gyoorobiiri kei fa maŇč nawolo nyi, n wulaa n ne gyae maŇč p…õte n y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei. 25Me kya, nyaŇč y…õna walaŇč Ňčon n gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ. BalaŇč baŇč be loo gyakaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ, be ta gyeŇča ta neŇč ya m…Ēna maŇč d…Ē…Ē ma gyeŇč neŇč. Te me kaseela baŇč m…Ē gyeŇč nyi nyaŇč kpila na maŇč. 26Me ta lese n din…Ē…Ē wola w…Ē te maa bese lese ke wola w…Ē bela, na kegyae keŇč n ne gyae maŇč n…õ ke nyiŇč doo be man na maŇč m…Ē ke doo be man.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Jesus Is Betrayed and Arrested
(Matthew 26.47-56; Mark 14.43-50; Luke 22.47-53)
1When Jesus had finished praying, he and his disciples crossed the Kidron Valley and went into a garden. 2Jesus had often met there with his disciples, and Judas knew where the place was.
3-5Judas had promised to betray Jesus. So he went to the garden with some Roman soldiers and temple police, who had been sent by the chief priests and the Pharisees. They carried torches, lanterns, and weapons. Jesus already knew everything that was going to happen, but he asked, ‚ÄúWho are you looking for?‚ÄĚ
They answered, ‚ÄúWe are looking for Jesus from Nazareth!‚ÄĚ
Jesus told them, ‚ÄúI am Jesus!‚ÄĚ 6At once they all backed away and fell to the ground.
7Jesus again asked, ‚ÄúWho are you looking for?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúWe are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,‚ÄĚ they answered.
8This time Jesus replied, ‚ÄúI have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go. 9Then everything will happen, just as I said, ‚ÄėI did not lose anyone you gave me.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
10Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear. 11 Jesus told Peter, ‚ÄúPut your sword away. I must drink from the cup that the Father has given me.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is Brought to Annas
(Matthew 26.57,58; Mark 14.53,54; Luke 22.54)
12The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up. 13They took him first to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. 14 This was the same Caiaphas who had told the Jewish leaders, ‚ÄúIt is better if one person dies for the people.‚ÄĚ
Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus
(Matthew 26.69,70; Mark 14.66-68; Luke 22.55-57)
15Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. That disciple knew the high priest, and he followed Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest's house. 16Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in, 17but asked him, ‚ÄúAren't you one of that man's followers?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúNo, I am not!‚ÄĚ Peter answered.
18It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself.
Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest
(Matthew 26.59-66; Mark 14.55-64; Luke 22.66-71)
19The high priest questioned Jesus about his followers and his teaching. 20But Jesus told him, ‚ÄúI have spoken freely in front of everyone. And I have always taught in our synagogues and in the temple, where all of our people come together. I have not said anything in secret. 21Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.‚ÄĚ
22As soon as Jesus said this, one of the temple police hit him and said, ‚ÄúThat's no way to talk to the high priest!‚ÄĚ
23Jesus answered, ‚ÄúIf I have done something wrong, say so. But if not, why did you hit me?‚ÄĚ 24Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest.
Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus
(Matthew 26.71-75; Mark 14.69-72; Luke 22.58-62)
25While Simon Peter was standing there warming himself, someone asked him, ‚ÄúAren't you one of Jesus' followers?‚ÄĚ
Again Peter denied it and said, ‚ÄúNo, I am not!‚ÄĚ
26One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, ‚ÄúDidn't I see you in the garden with that man?‚ÄĚ
27Once more Peter denied it, and right then a rooster crowed.
Jesus Is Tried by Pilate
(Matthew 27.1,2,11-14; Mark 15.1-5; Luke 23.1-5)
28It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
29Pilate came out and asked, ‚ÄúWhat charges are you bringing against this man?‚ÄĚ
30 They answered, ‚ÄúHe is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.‚ÄĚ
31Pilate told them, ‚ÄúTake him and judge him by your own laws.‚ÄĚ
The crowd replied, ‚ÄúWe are not allowed to put anyone to death.‚ÄĚ 32 And so what Jesus said about his death would soon come true.
33Pilate then went back inside. He called Jesus over and asked, ‚ÄúAre you the king of the Jews?‚ÄĚ
34Jesus answered, ‚ÄúAre you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?‚ÄĚ
35‚ÄúYou know I'm not a Jew!‚ÄĚ Pilate said. ‚ÄúYour own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?‚ÄĚ
36Jesus answered, ‚ÄúMy kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.‚ÄĚ
37‚ÄúSo you are a king,‚ÄĚ Pilate replied.
‚ÄúYou are saying that I am a king,‚ÄĚ Jesus told him. ‚ÄúI was born into this world to tell about the truth. And everyone who belongs to the truth knows my voice.‚ÄĚ
38Pilate asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWhat is truth?‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is Sentenced to Death
(Matthew 27.15-31; Mark 15.6-20; Luke 23.13-25)
Pilate went back out and said, ‚ÄúI don't find this man guilty of anything! 39And since I usually set a prisoner free for you at Passover, would you like for me to set free the king of the Jews?‚ÄĚ
40They shouted, ‚ÄúNo, not him! We want Barabbas.‚ÄĚ Now Barabbas was a terrorist.
translation_text:
Ba kekyaŇč Yesu
Mateo 26:47-56; Maake 14:43-50; Luka 22:47-53
1Yesu ke fane taŇč n…õ, Ňčon na …Ē kaseela baŇč korow…Ē gyu ka toŇč boŇčobii keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke Kidr…Ēn n…õ, te be ka gyoow…Ē oliifi w…Ē…Ēbii daale man. 2Na Gyudase Ňčon …Ē ba lese Yesu fa n…õ m…Ē gyeŇč bot…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi Yesu na …Ē kaseela baŇč ne kyaa gyu ke gyaŇčee bot…Ē…Ē. 3Mena d…Ē…Ē Gyudase moo balaŇč burum baŇč be dana kanease na kefuluse te ba doona yoo ab…Ē…Ē n…õ gyu bot…Ē…Ē. Akpaa maŇč te …õ ne gyae ber…õ na, …õ yela me balaŇč k…õw…Ē ke ta.‚ÄĚ 9Kei yela nombia Ňčan …Ē be wulaa yako n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē mena. ∆Ü yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalaŇč baŇč nyaŇč me kya ba mo fa maŇč n…õ, ma te yeli be man dokoloŇč ka yo ya.‚ÄĚ 10Bot…Ē…Ē na Sim…Ēn Pita dana yoosila, te …Ē w…ĒŇča ke lese kara saese kegyia Ňčon tobaale Ňčolo dunoluŇč deŇčele lese ke l…Ē. Be kamo Yesu gyu saese kegyia Anase gyaŇč
12KeŇčte yoon…Ē…Ētena siamante Ňčon na …Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena dikpii keŇč, na Gyudatena kot…Ēt…Ē…Ētena baŇč kyaŇča Yesu te be bake e, 13te be taŇčgb…õ…õw…Ē mo e gyu Anase gyaŇč. Kaifase Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ saese kegyia e kulutoo kenaŇč man n…õ, …Ē neŇčgya y…õna Anase ŇčonaŇč. 14Kaifase kei faa na Gyudatena agyueŇč nyi, dei nyi walaŇč dokoloŇč waa yeŇč fa balaŇč baŇč pou.
Pita keb…õ…õ Yesu
Mateo 26:69-70; Maake 14:66-68; Luka 22:55-57
15DebaŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo Yesu bee gyu saese kegyia Ňčon d…õ…õ n…õ, na Sim…Ēn Pita na …Ē d…Ē…Ē kaseela baŇč man Ňčolo silana e w…Ēle w…Ēle. Te Pita m…Ē gyuuw…Ē ka seŇč be gyaŇč …Ē ne woli boalaŇč keŇč.
Saese kegyia Anase keb…Ē…Ēse Yesu nombia

19Be ka mo Yesu gyu saese kegyia Ňčon dekpaŇčalaŇč man n…õ, …Ē b…Ē…Ēse Yesu nombia lee …Ē kaseela na …Ē kawola keŇč wose man. 20KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMa seŇčaw…Ē balaŇč man kolosi. DebaŇč kamas…õ m…Ē na m…õ…õ wola Wurubuar…õ nombia Gyudatena …Ēsom den…õ…õ man, na Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč Gyudatena pou ne kyaa gyaŇčee ke man n…õ man. M…Ēna ma te yako kolo weese ya. 21WoŇč d…Ē…Ē te n ne b…Ē…Ēse maŇč mena nombia k…õŇča? B…Ē…Ēse balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē nyii me kawola keŇč n…õ. BaŇč gyeŇča na kpene keŇč m…Ē…Ē yako n…õ. 22Yesu kekolosi nombia k…õŇča n…õ te kegyiise baŇč man Ňčolo gy…Ē…Ē e …Ē lakaa man te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄėMena te kaboena nyi nya tiran…Ē…Ē fa saese kegyia Ňčon?‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 23KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa me yako nombiikum daale na wola maŇč ke. Te akpaa me nombia doo na ke gb…õ…õ na weera te ne gy…Ē maŇč?‚ÄĚ 24KeŇčte Anase yela ba moo Yesu gyu saese kegyia Kaifase gyaŇč, na …Ē s…õ…õ …Ē doo ŇčmeeseŇč Ňčan b…Ē…Ē mo bake e n…õ man.
Pita kabese b…õ…õ nyi …Ē ba gyeŇč Yesu ya
Mateo 26:71-75; Maake 14:69-72; Luka 22:57-62
25Sim…Ēn Pita ka seŇč bot…Ē…Ē …Ē ne woli boalaŇč keŇč n…õ te be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúŇägba nyaŇč m…Ē n y…õ…õ Yesu kaseela baŇč man Ňčolo e?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Pita tiran…Ē…Ē nyi ‚ÄúKoa, ma te y…õ…õ be man Ňčolo e ya.‚ÄĚ 26Bot…Ē…Ē te saese kegyia Ňčon tobaale Ňčolo Ňčon …Ē loŇčto y…õna tobaale Ňčon Pita be kara …Ē deŇčele n…õ b…Ē…Ēse Pita nyiaa, ‚ÄúŇägba me naa nyaŇč m…Ē n kpu…õ na w…Ē oliifi w…Ē…Ēbii keŇč man?‚ÄĚ 27KeŇčte Pita besew…Ē b…õ…õ nyi koa. DebokenaŇč man te kegyim l…Ē…Ē woya.
Be kamo Yesu gyu Romantena kegyia Pilato gyaŇč
Mateo 27:1-2, 11-14; Maake 15:1-5; Luka 23:1-5
28T…õ…õ kena nebo nebo fuu n…õ, ba moo Yesu lee Kaifase d…õ…õ gyu Pilato Ňčon …Ē seŇč…õ…õ Romantena gyoo dinaa naw…Ē…Ē man …Ē ne k…õ…õ Gyuda t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē n…õ d…õ…õ. DebaŇč kenaŇč na Gyudatena nyeekel…õ…õwee kedi keŇč ta benaa. Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč ta gyoo Pilato dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man ya. Nawolo nyi ba te gyae baa walaa ba wose p…õ baa di ba nyeekel…õ…õwee dinaa keŇč ya. 29Mena d…Ē…Ē kegyia Pilato leew…Ē k…ĒŇč be gyaŇč deb…Ē…Ē man kaa b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč dukum te balee kei y…õ…õw…Ē te …õ ne gyae …õ ke bu e f…Ē…Ē?‚ÄĚ 30KeŇčte be tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyi Ňčon te y…õ…õ bu kum e ya n…õ, naf…Ē d…Ē…Ē mo e k…ĒŇč k…õbo ya.‚ÄĚ 31Bot…Ē…Ē te Pilato yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź mo e ta na …õm…õ…õ gbagba kaa di …Ē nombia Ňčgba mena keŇč ya mmaraa na wola Ňčon n…õ.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ Romantena mmaraa b…õ…õ fa daa gb…õ…õ nyi d…õ…õ ko walaŇč ya.‚ÄĚ 32Kei yela Yesu nombia Ňčan …Ē be wulaa yako see lee …Ē yeŇč keŇč waa yeŇč wose man n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē ampaŇč.
33KeŇčte kegyia Pilato besew…Ē gyoo …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč man te …Ē baake Yesu b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč y…õna Gyudatena gyoo Ňčon?‚ÄĚ 34Te Yesu m…Ē besenaw…Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč gbagba nyeeman nombii yaa n ne b…Ē…Ēse maŇč n…õ yaa Ňčolo yako na ma wose man nombia fa neŇč?‚ÄĚ 35Bot…Ē…Ē te Pilato b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe y…õ…õ Gyuda baale e? NyaŇč gbagba n balaŇč na …õ saese kegyiise moona neŇč k…Ēna maŇč. WoŇč dukum te n y…õ…õw…Ē?‚ÄĚ 36KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe gyoori ta lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ya, nyi ke deŇč lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ, naf…Ē me tewul…õ…õ baa yoo l…õ…õ maŇč na b…Ē…Ē nyiŇč mo maŇč ka do Gyudatena nyiŇčmaa man ya. M…Ēna me gyoori ta lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ya.‚ÄĚ 37KeŇčte Pilato yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKenaŇč na Ňčgba n y…õ…õ gyoo e baa?‚ÄĚ Te Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKeŇč yaa n yakow…Ē n…õ. Mena nombia k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē te ba lola maŇč, te Ňčan m…Ē d…Ē…Ē te me k…ĒŇčaw…Ē t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē na me kaa yako anokoare keŇč. WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae anokoare keŇč n…õ …Ē ne tei maŇč.‚ÄĚ
38Bot…Ē…Ē te Pilato b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč y…õna anokoare kei?‚ÄĚ ∆Ü ke b…Ē…Ēse e nombia k…õŇča taŇč n…õ te …Ē besew…Ē lee k…ĒŇč Gyudatena baŇč gyaŇč kaa yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMa ta naa nombiikum daale lee …Ē wose man ya. 39M…Ēna ke y…õ…õ ya mmaraa ke nyi …õ nyeekel…õ…õwee kedi kamas…õ man na, maa tina balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyam t…Ē deni n…õ man dokoloŇč yela fa Ňčon. Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ ne gyae nyi maa tina Gyudatena gyoo Ňčon yela yaa sena?‚ÄĚ 40KeŇčte be fae yeesa yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúKoa, na Yesu te d…õ…õ gyae nyi nya tina e yela ya. Barabase te d…õ…õ gyae nyi nya tina e yela.‚ÄĚ Barabase kei y…õna walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be kyaa koro seŇč tia gyooneŇč te …Ē ba ko walaŇč n…õ.

english_text:
1Pilate gave orders for Jesus to be beaten with a whip. 2The soldiers made a crown out of thorn branches and put it on Jesus. Then they put a purple robe on him. 3They came up to him and said, ‚ÄúHey, you king of the Jews!‚ÄĚ They also hit him with their fists.
4Once again Pilate went out. This time he said, ‚ÄúI will have Jesus brought out to you again. Then you can see for yourselves that I have not found him guilty.‚ÄĚ
5Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said, ‚ÄúHere is the man!‚ÄĚ
6When the chief priests and the temple police saw him, they yelled, ‚ÄúNail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!‚ÄĚ
Pilate told them, ‚ÄúYou take him and nail him to a cross! I don't find him guilty of anything.‚ÄĚ
7The crowd replied, ‚ÄúHe claimed to be the Son of God! Our law says that he must be put to death.‚ÄĚ
8When Pilate heard this, he was terrified. 9He went back inside and asked Jesus, ‚ÄúWhere are you from?‚ÄĚ But Jesus did not answer.
10‚ÄúWhy won't you answer my question?‚ÄĚ Pilate asked. ‚ÄúDon't you know I have the power to let you go free or to nail you to a cross?‚ÄĚ
11 Jesus replied, ‚ÄúIf God had not given you the power, you couldn't do anything at all to me. But the one who handed me over to you did something even worse.‚ÄĚ
12Then Pilate wanted to set Jesus free. But the crowd again yelled, ‚ÄúIf you set this man free, you are no friend of the Emperor! Anyone who claims to be a king is an enemy of the Emperor.‚ÄĚ
13When Pilate heard this, he brought Jesus out. Then he sat down on the judge's bench at the place known as ‚ÄúThe Stone Pavement.‚ÄĚ In Aramaic this pavement is called ‚ÄúGabbatha.‚ÄĚ 14It was about noon on the day before Passover, and Pilate said to the crowd, ‚ÄúLook at your king!‚ÄĚ
15‚ÄúKill him! Kill him!‚ÄĚ they yelled. ‚ÄúNail him to a cross!‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúSo you want me to nail your king to a cross?‚ÄĚ Pilate asked.
The chief priests replied, ‚ÄúThe Emperor is our king!‚ÄĚ 16Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be nailed to a cross.
Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross
(Matthew 27.32-44; Mark 15.21-32; Luke 23.26-43)
Jesus was taken away, 17and he carried his cross to a place known as ‚ÄúThe Skull.‚ÄĚ In Aramaic this place is called ‚ÄúGolgotha.‚ÄĚ 18There Jesus was nailed to the cross, and on each side of him a man was also nailed to a cross.
19Pilate ordered the charge against Jesus to be written on a board and put above the cross. It read, ‚ÄúJesus of Nazareth, King of the Jews.‚ÄĚ 20The words were written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek.
The place where Jesus was taken wasn't far from the city, and many of the people read the charge against him. 21So the chief priests went to Pilate and said, ‚ÄúWhy did you write that he is King of the Jews? You should have written, ‚ÄėHe claimed to be King of the Jews.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
22But Pilate told them, ‚ÄúWhat is written will not be changed!‚ÄĚ
23After the soldiers had nailed Jesus to the cross, they divided up his clothes into four parts, one for each of them. But his outer garment was made from a single piece of cloth, and it did not have any seams. 24 The soldiers said to each other, ‚ÄúLet's not rip it apart. We will gamble to see who gets it.‚ÄĚ This happened so the Scriptures would come true, which say,
‚ÄúThey divided up my clothes
and gambled
for my garments.‚ÄĚ
The soldiers then did what they had decided.
25Jesus' mother stood beside his cross with her sister and Mary the wife of Clopas. Mary Magdalene was standing there too. 26When Jesus saw his mother and his favorite disciple with her, he said to his mother, ‚ÄúThis man is now your son.‚ÄĚ 27Then he said to the disciple, ‚ÄúShe is now your mother.‚ÄĚ From then on, that disciple took her into his own home.
The Death of Jesus
(Matthew 27.45-56; Mark 15.33-41; Luke 23.44-49)
28 Jesus knew that he had now finished his work. And in order to make the Scriptures come true, he said, ‚ÄúI am thirsty!‚ÄĚ 29A jar of cheap wine was there. Someone then soaked a sponge with the wine and held it up to Jesus' mouth on the stem of a hyssop plant. 30After Jesus drank the wine, he said, ‚ÄúEverything is done!‚ÄĚ He bowed his head and died.
A Spear Is Stuck in Jesus' Side
31The next day would be both a Sabbath and the Passover. It was a special day for the Jewish people, and they did not want the bodies to stay on the crosses during this day. So they asked Pilate to break the men's legs and take their bodies down. 32The soldiers first broke the legs of the other two men who were nailed there. 33But when they came to Jesus, they saw he was already dead, and they did not break his legs.
34One of the soldiers stuck his spear into Jesus' side, and blood and water came out. 35We know this is true, because it was told by someone who saw it happen. Now you can have faith too. 36 All this happened so that the Scriptures would come true, which say, ‚ÄúNo bone of his body will be broken‚ÄĚ 37 and ‚ÄúThey will see the one in whose side they stuck a spear.‚ÄĚ
Jesus Is Buried
(Matthew 27.57-61; Mark 15.42-47; Luke 23.50-56)
38Joseph from Arimathea was one of Jesus' disciples. He had kept it secret though, because he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. But now he asked Pilate to let him have Jesus' body. Pilate gave him permission, and Joseph took it down from the cross.
39 Nicodemus also came with about 30 kilograms of spices made from myrrh and aloes. This was the same Nicodemus who had visited Jesus one night. 40The two men wrapped the body in a linen cloth, together with the spices, which was how the Jewish people buried their dead. 41In the place where Jesus had been nailed to a cross, there was a garden with a tomb that had never been used. 42The tomb was nearby, and since it was the time to prepare for the Sabbath, they were in a hurry to put Jesus' body there.

english_text:
Jesus Is Alive
(Matthew 28.1-10; Mark 16.1-8; Luke 24.1-12)
1On Sunday morning while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been rolled away from the entrance. 2She ran to Simon Peter and to Jesus' favorite disciple and said, ‚ÄúThey have taken the Lord from the tomb! We don't know where they have put him.‚ÄĚ
3Peter and the other disciple started for the tomb. 4They ran side by side, until the other disciple ran faster than Peter and got there first. 5He bent over and saw the strips of linen cloth lying inside the tomb, but he did not go in.
6When Simon Peter got there, he went into the tomb and saw the strips of cloth. 7He also saw the piece of cloth that had been used to cover Jesus' face. It was rolled up and in a place by itself. 8The disciple who got there first then went into the tomb, and when he saw it, he believed. 9At that time Peter and the other disciple did not know that the Scriptures said Jesus would rise to life. 10So the two of them went back to the other disciples.
Jesus Appears to Mary Magdalene
(Mark 16.9-11)
11Mary Magdalene stood crying outside the tomb. She was still weeping, when she stooped down 12and saw two angels inside. They were dressed in white and were sitting where Jesus' body had been. One was at the head and the other was at the foot. 13The angels asked Mary, ‚ÄúWhy are you crying?‚ÄĚ
She answered, ‚ÄúThey have taken away my Lord's body! I don't know where they have put him.‚ÄĚ
14As soon as Mary said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there. But she did not know who he was. 15Jesus asked her, ‚ÄúWhy are you crying? Who are you looking for?‚ÄĚ
She thought he was the gardener and said, ‚ÄúSir, if you have taken his body away, please tell me, so I can go and get him.‚ÄĚ
16Then Jesus said to her, ‚ÄúMary!‚ÄĚ
She turned and said to him, ‚ÄúRabboni.‚ÄĚ The Aramaic word ‚ÄúRabboni‚ÄĚ means ‚ÄúTeacher.‚ÄĚ
17Jesus told her, ‚ÄúDon't hold on to me! I have not yet gone to the Father. But tell my disciples I am going to the one who is my Father and my God, as well as your Father and your God.‚ÄĚ 18Mary Magdalene then went and told the disciples she had seen the Lord. She also told them what he had said to her.
Jesus Appears to His Disciples
(Matthew 28.16-20; Mark 16.14-18; Luke 24.36-49)
19The disciples were afraid of the Jewish leaders, and on the evening of that same Sunday they locked themselves in a room. Suddenly, Jesus appeared in the middle of the group. He greeted them 20and showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they became very happy.
21After Jesus had greeted them again, he said, ‚ÄúI am sending you, just as the Father has sent me.‚ÄĚ 22Then he breathed on them and said, ‚ÄúReceive the Holy Spirit. 23 If you forgive anyone's sins, they will be forgiven. But if you don't forgive their sins, they will not be forgiven.‚ÄĚ
Jesus and Thomas
24Although Thomas the Twin was one of the twelve disciples, he wasn't with the others when Jesus appeared to them. 25So they told him, ‚ÄúWe have seen the Lord!‚ÄĚ
But Thomas said, ‚ÄúFirst, I must see the nail scars in his hands and touch them with my finger. I must put my hand where the spear went into his side. I won't believe unless I do this!‚ÄĚ
26A week later the disciples were together again. This time, Thomas was with them. Jesus came in while the doors were still locked and stood in the middle of the group. He greeted his disciples 27and said to Thomas, ‚ÄúPut your finger here and look at my hands! Put your hand into my side. Stop doubting and have faith!‚ÄĚ
28Thomas replied, ‚ÄúYou are my Lord and my God!‚ÄĚ
29Jesus said, ‚ÄúThomas, do you have faith because you have seen me? The people who have faith in me without seeing me are the ones who are really blessed!‚ÄĚ
Why John Wrote His Book
30Jesus worked many other miracles for his disciples, and not all of them are written in this book. 31But these are written so that you will put your faith in Jesus as the Messiah and the Son of God. If you have faith in him, you will have true life.
