Te akpaa …õ b…õ…õ …õ ta mo Ňčolo nombiakum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ e ya na, Wurubuar…õ m…Ē be gyae waa mo ky…õ…õ e ya.‚ÄĚ
Yesu kalese …Ē wose wola Tomase
24Wee kenaŇč keŇč Yesu be kaa lese …Ē wose wola …Ē kaseela kufu bala baŇč n…õ, na be man Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Tomase yaa Ata n…õ be kpu…õ na …Ē tebia ya. 25Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu kaseela akaŇč baŇč yako Tomase nyiaa, ‚ÄúDa te naa de GbeŇčgyoo Ňčon.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Tomase yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa me ta mo me sia naa nyeelabia gyaŇčtar…õ…õ Ňčan, na maa mo me nyimbii do …Ē yenaabia gyam keŇč man ya n…õ, maŇč be gyae maa l…õ…õ di korakora ya.‚ÄĚ 26Weeya gyanaara w…Ēle n…õ, na …Ē kaseela baŇč te gyaŇčee deni man bela. Wee kenaŇč na Tomase m…Ē kpu…õ na w…Ē. Ba te t…Ē asima Ňčan pou, karatetee dokoloŇč te Yesu kaa b…õ…õw…Ē lee be d…Ē…Ē te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWosef…õ…õreŇč d…õ…õ kyaa na Ňčon.‚ÄĚ 27KeŇčte …Ē yako Tomase nyiaa, ‚ÄúMo n nyiŇčmaa do me nyiŇčmaa b…Ē…Ē man na n ke k…õ…õ me nyiŇčmaase, na n ka mo n nyimbii do ma yenaŇč k…õ…õ. Tina aŇčmaareŇč kel…õ…õ yela na n ke l…õ…õ di.‚ÄĚ 28Bot…Ē…Ē te Tomase tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč y…õna me GbeŇčgyoo na me Wurubuar…õ.‚ÄĚ 29KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúKeŇč n be naa maŇč d…Ē…Ē te n l…õ…õw…Ē di? BalaŇč baŇč ba te naa maŇč ya te b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ di n…õ, baŇč na nyeebam.‚ÄĚ
30Yesu besew…Ē y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia burum Ňčan …Ē kaseela baŇč be naa na be sia, m…Ēna na Ňče popou te be Ňčmarasew…Ē do t…Ēne kei man ya. 31M…Ēna be Ňčmarase k…õŇča bo na …õ ke l…õ…õ di nyi Yesu y…õna Wurubuar…õ bu na nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon. Na nyaŇč Ňčon n l…õ…õ e di na, n ke nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč …Ē yele man.

english_text:
Jesus Appears to Seven Disciples
1Jesus later appeared to his disciples along the shore of Lake Tiberias. 2Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, and the brothers James and John, were there, together with two other disciples. 3 Simon Peter said, ‚ÄúI'm going fishing!‚ÄĚ
The others said, ‚ÄúWe will go with you.‚ÄĚ They went out in their boat. But they didn't catch a thing that night.
4Early the next morning Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize who he was. 5Jesus shouted, ‚ÄúFriends, have you caught anything?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúNo!‚ÄĚ they answered.
6 So he told them, ‚ÄúLet your net down on the right side of your boat, and you will catch some fish.‚ÄĚ
They did, and the net was so full of fish that they could not drag it up into the boat.
7Jesus' favorite disciple told Peter, ‚ÄúIt's the Lord!‚ÄĚ When Simon heard it was the Lord, he put on the clothes he had taken off while he was working. Then he jumped into the water. 8The boat was only about 100 meters from shore. So the other disciples stayed in the boat and dragged in the net full of fish.
9When the disciples got out of the boat, they saw some bread and a charcoal fire with fish on it. 10Jesus told his disciples, ‚ÄúBring some of the fish you just caught.‚ÄĚ 11Simon Peter got back into the boat and dragged the net to shore. In it were 153 large fish, but still the net did not rip.
12Jesus said, ‚ÄúCome and eat!‚ÄĚ But none of the disciples dared ask who he was. They knew he was the Lord. 13Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave some of it to his disciples. He did the same with the fish. 14This was the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death.
Jesus and Peter
15When Jesus and his disciples had finished eating, he asked, ‚ÄúSimon son of John, do you love me more than the others do?‚ÄĚ
Simon Peter answered, ‚ÄúYes, Lord, you know I do!‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThen feed my lambs,‚ÄĚ Jesus said.
16Jesus asked a second time, ‚ÄúSimon son of John, do you love me?‚ÄĚ
Peter answered, ‚ÄúYes, Lord, you know I love you!‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThen take care of my sheep,‚ÄĚ Jesus told him.
17Jesus asked a third time, ‚ÄúSimon son of John, do you love me?‚ÄĚ
Peter was hurt because Jesus had asked him three times if he loved him. So he told Jesus, ‚ÄúLord, you know everything. You know I love you.‚ÄĚ
Jesus replied, ‚ÄúFeed my sheep. 18I tell you for certain that when you were a young man, you dressed yourself and went wherever you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands. Then others will wrap your belt around you and lead you where you don't want to go.‚ÄĚ
19Jesus said this to tell how Peter would die and bring honor to God. Then he said to Peter, ‚ÄúFollow me!‚ÄĚ
Jesus and His Favorite Disciple
20 Peter turned and saw Jesus' favorite disciple following them. He was the same one who had sat next to Jesus at the meal and had asked, ‚ÄúLord, who is going to betray you?‚ÄĚ 21When Peter saw this disciple, he asked Jesus, ‚ÄúLord, what about him?‚ÄĚ
22Jesus answered, ‚ÄúWhat is it to you, if I want him to live until I return? You must follow me.‚ÄĚ 23So the rumor spread among the other disciples that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say he would not die. He simply said, ‚ÄúWhat is it to you, if I want him to live until I return?‚ÄĚ
24This disciple is the one who told all of this. He wrote it, and we know he is telling the truth.
25Jesus did many other things. If they were all written in books, I don't suppose there would be room enough in the whole world for all the books.
translation_text:
Yesu kalese …Ē wose wola …Ē kaseela baale
1K…õŇča pou w…Ēle n…õ, Yesu besew…Ē lese …Ē wose wola …Ē kaseela baale Tiberiase boo n…Ē…Ēman. Mena keŇč …Ē ba lese …Ē wose wola w…Ē yaa. 2∆Ü kaseela baŇč b…õ…õ baake w…Ē Sim…Ēn Pita na Tomase Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e Ata n…õ na Natan…õle Ňčon …Ē ba lee Kaanan don…Ē…Ē keŇč ke kyaa Galelia t…õ…õle man n…õ, na Sebedeo bia na …Ē kaseela bala baale gyuuw…Ē ke kyaa bom. 3KeŇčte Sim…Ēn Pita yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMee gyu boo man me kaa kyaŇč kpebia.‚ÄĚ Te baŇč m…Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDaa m…Ē dee sila neŇč gyu.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē be gyuuw…Ē ka gyoo degbele man te be laŇčaw…Ē. Be l…Ē…Ē asawu nelim kenaŇč pou m…Ēna ba te kyaŇč kolo ya.
4T…õ…õ kena nebo nebo fuu n…õ, be k…õ…õ na Ňčolo seŇč…õ…õ la boo n…Ē…Ēman, na n k…õ…õ n…õ Yesu ŇčonaŇč. M…Ēna …Ē kaseela baŇč ta gyeŇč e ya. 5KeŇčte Yesu b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúTewul…õ…õ, doona …õ te kyaŇč kolo ya?‚ÄĚ Te be tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúKoa de te nyiŇč kpebii dokoloŇč paŇčpaŇč gba ya.‚ÄĚ 6KeŇčte …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź fuŇč ya asawu keŇč l…Ē degbele keŇč dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē na …õ ke kyaŇč kpebia.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē be fuŇča asawu keŇč l…Ē bot…Ē…Ē yenaŇč te be kyaŇča kpebia burum keŇč nyi b…Ē…Ē ne tale baa w…ĒŇčee asawu keŇč gba ya.
7Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu kaseele Ňčon …Ē ne gyae …Ē nombia n…õ yako Pita nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo Yesu ŇčonaŇč.‚ÄĚ Sim…Ēn Pita kenyii nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu ŇčonaŇč n…õ te …Ē moo …Ē kegba wurikyim milii …Ē wose, nawolo nyi …Ē wulaa kpee ke. KeŇčte …Ē toŇčaw…Ē gyoo loŇč keŇč man y…õl…õ…õ …Ē ne gyu Yesu gyaŇč. 8M…Ēna kaseela akaŇč baŇč saa degbele keŇč b…õ…õ k…ĒŇč dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē na b…õ…õ w…ĒŇčee asawu keŇč kpebia be wulu ke man n…õ, nawolo nyi na ba dekyae keŇč taŇč lem na dekeŇčkeŇč ya. Ke lem gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba baam t…Ē…Ēse s…ĒŇčola na kufu n…õ (50).
9Be kelii dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ be naa boalaŇč na nyewalaŇč, na kpebia gyakaa ke d…Ē…Ē na bodobodo m…Ē doo yenaŇč. 10KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź mo kpebia baŇč …õ be kyam n…õ baale k…ĒŇč.‚ÄĚ 11Mena d…Ē…Ē Sim…Ēn Pita besew…Ē gyu degbele keŇč man, te …Ē ke w…ĒŇčee asawu keŇč k…ĒŇč dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē. Na kpebia dinaana baŇč b…Ē…Ē wulu asawu keŇč n…õ, be yaan…Ē…Ēle y…õ…õ s…ĒŇčonoŇč na s…ĒŇčola na kufu batooro (153). KeŇč na kpebia baŇč booroŇč kei koraŇč n…õ, asawu keŇč te tirii ya. 12KeŇčte Yesu yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…ĒŇč kaa di …õ nebo ween…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ M…Ēna …Ē kaseela baŇč Ňčolo te tale gy…Ē …Ē kon…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyi ‚ÄúNyaŇč y…õna am…Ēte ya?‚ÄĚ Nawolo nyi ba gyeŇč nyi be GbeŇčgyoo Yesu ŇčonaŇč. 13Te Yesu k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa mo bodobodo keŇč fa w…Ē, kenaŇč w…Ēle te …Ē moo kpebia baŇč m…Ē fa w…Ē. 14Yesu kefoŇč lee yeŇč man n…õ, kei y…õna betabotoorote keŇč …Ē ba lese …Ē wose wola …Ē kaseela baŇč.
Yesu kekolosi na Pita
15Be kedi ween…õ…õ Ňčan taŇč n…õ te Yesu b…Ē…Ēse Sim…Ēn Pita nyiaa, ‚ÄúGy…Ēn bu Sim…Ēn, n ne gyae maŇč kela n tebia k…õw…Ē k…õ…õ gyae maŇč?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Pita tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAyi, me GbeŇčgyoo, n gyeŇč nyi m…õ…õ gyae n nombia.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúK…õ…õ me silala baŇč d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč namensek…õ…õlere ne k…õ…õ namense d…Ē…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ 16Te Yesu besew…Ē b…Ē…Ēse e bolate nyiaa, ‚ÄúGy…Ēn bu Sim…Ēn, n ne gyae me nombia?‚ÄĚ Te Pita tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAyi me GbeŇčgyoo, nyaŇč gbagba gyeŇč nyi m…õ…õ gyae n nombia.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa ‚ÄúK…õ…õ me silala baŇč d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč namensek…õ…õlere ne k…õ…õ …Ē namense d…Ē…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ 17KeŇčte Yesu besew…Ē b…Ē…Ēse e betabotoorote nyiaa, ‚ÄúGy…Ēn bu Sim…Ēn, n ne gyae me nombia nideli?‚ÄĚ Nombia ŇčenaŇč takaa Pita nideli nawolo nyi …Ē te b…Ē…Ēse e betabotooro nyi, ‚ÄúN ne gyae maŇč nideli?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Pita yako e nyi, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, nyaŇč gbagba n gyeŇč kpene kamas…õ. N gyeŇč nyi m…õ…õ gyae neŇč nideli.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Yesu yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMena na k…õ…õ me silala baŇč d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč namensek…õ…õlere ne k…õ…õ …Ē namense d…Ē…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ 18M…õ…õ yako neŇč anokoare nyiaa, ‚ÄúDebaŇč keŇč n y…õ…õ tobaale e n…õ nyaŇč gbagba ne tale mo n kegba do n wose, na n ka koro gyu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč n ne gyae. M…Ēna n kaa Ňčm…õ…õ na Ňčolo ne gyae la waa do neŇč kegba na waa kyaŇč neŇč n nyiŇčmaa man mo neŇč gyu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč n b…õ…õ gyae nyi n ke gyu ya.‚ÄĚ 19Yesu yako nombia k…õŇča baa na kawola yeŇč keŇč Pita waa yeŇč na ka lese Wurubuar…õ yele. Yesu kekolosi nombia k…õŇča pou taŇč n…õ te …Ē yako Pita nyiaa, ‚ÄúSila maŇč!‚ÄĚ
20Ba ke ta bee gyu n…õ, Pita g…õ…õsew…Ē k…õ…õ te …Ē naaw…Ē Ňčgba kaseele Ňčon Yesu ne gyae …Ē nombia nideli n…õ silana w…Ē w…Ēle w…Ēle. Mena kaseele kei y…õna Ňčon …Ē be kyaa teelina Yesu debaŇč keŇč bee di be balin…Ē…Ē ween…õ…õ te …Ē be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, am…Ēte ne gyae la waa lese neŇč fa yeŇč n…õ?‚ÄĚ 21Pita kena kaseele Ňčon n…õ …Ē b…Ē…Ēse Yesu nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, sena te baa y…õ…õ balee kei m…Ē?‚ÄĚ 22KeŇčte Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa m…õ…õ gyae nyi waa kyaa kelii debaŇč keŇč maa bese k…ĒŇč na, n woŇč faa la? NyaŇč sila maŇč!‚ÄĚ 23Mena d…Ē…Ē nombia k…õŇča yaaseew…Ē Yesu kaseela baŇč man nyiaa, kaseele Ňčon Yesu ne gyae …Ē nombia n…õ be gyae waa yeŇč ya. M…Ēna Yesu te yako nyi …Ē be gyae waa yeŇč ya. KeŇč …Ē be yako y…õna nyi, ‚ÄúAkpaa m…õ…õ gyae nyi waa kyaa kelii debaŇč keŇč maa bese k…ĒŇč na, n woŇč faa la?‚ÄĚ
24Mena kaseele kei diina nombia k…õŇča wose man adans…õ…õ te …Ē Ňčmarase Ňča see. Te da gyeŇč m…Ē nyi wa adans…õ…õ keŇč y…õ…õ ampaŇč.
25Yesu y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē burum Ňčaale m…Ē. Akpaa beŇč Ňčmarase Ňča dokoloŇč dokoloŇč n…õ, t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē pou b…Ē…Ē gyae baa nyiŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč baa mo Ňča see ya.

english_text:
1 Theophilus, I first wrote to you about all that Jesus did and taught from the very first 2until he was taken up to heaven. But before he was taken up, he gave orders to the apostles he had chosen with the help of the Holy Spirit.
3For 40 days after Jesus had suffered and died, he proved in many ways that he had been raised from death. He appeared to his apostles and spoke to them about God's kingdom. 4 While he was still with them, he said:
Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do. 5 John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
Jesus Is Taken to Heaven
6While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, ‚ÄúLord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?‚ÄĚ
7Jesus said to them, ‚ÄúYou don't need to know the time of those events that only the Father controls. 8 But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.‚ÄĚ 9 After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him, 10but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky.
Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them. 11They said, ‚ÄúWhy are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.‚ÄĚ
Someone To Take the Place of Judas
12-13 The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James.
After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
14The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers. 15One day there were about 120 of the Lord's followers meeting together, and Peter stood up to speak to them. 16-17He said:
My friends, long ago by the power of the Holy Spirit, David said something about Judas, and what he said has now happened. Judas was one of us and had worked with us, but he brought the mob to arrest Jesus. 18 Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out. 19When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means ‚ÄúField of Blood.‚ÄĚ
20 In the book of Psalms it says,
‚ÄúLeave his house empty,
and don't let anyone
live there.‚ÄĚ
It also says,
‚ÄúLet someone else
have his job.‚ÄĚ
21-22 So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
23Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias. 24Then they all prayed, ‚ÄúLord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen 25to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.‚ÄĚ 26They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
translation_text:
1Kegyia Teyofilose, maŇč Luka ne Ňčmarase na t…Ēne kei mee yelee neŇč. Me t…Ēne gyaŇčgbate keŇč man n…õ, ma wola ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou Yesu be y…õ…õ na Ňčan …Ē ba wolo debaŇč keŇč …Ē be fi …Ē tom as…õ…õ, 2mo kelii wee keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo e laŇč adido n…õ. P…õ Wurubuar…õ ka mo Yesu gyu adido n…õ, Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon t…õ…õw…Ē Ňčon Yesu d…Ē…Ē wola …Ē kpilala baŇč …Ē ba lese n…õ tom keŇč baa y…õ…õ. 3Yesu Kristo ka yeŇč w…Ēle weeya s…ĒŇčola man n…õ, …Ē lese …Ē wose wola …Ē kaseela baŇč agbaatee agbaatee lee gb…õ…õneŇč burum man wola nyi …Ē delena, keŇč nyi Ňčolo be tale l…õ…õ ka aŇčmaareŇč ya. Be naa e na be sia, te …Ē kolosiw…Ē fa w…Ē lee Wurubuar…õ gyoorobiiri keŇč wose man. 4Ba kekyaa man n…õ, wee daale …Ē faree …Ē kaseela baŇč nyi ‚Äú∆ź na lee Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man ya, m…Ēna …õ kyaa bot…Ē…Ē daa kpene keŇč me kya be yako see nyi waa fa Ňčon n…õ.‚ÄĚ Me wulaa yako Ňčon ka wose man nombia nyi, 5loŇč te Gy…Ēn moow…Ē s…Ē Ňčon Wurubuar…õ loŇč, m…Ēna weeya akalans…õ…õ Ňčaale w…Ēle na Wurubuar…õ waa mo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon bo s…Ē Ňčon Wurubuar…õ loŇč.
Yesu adido kegyu
Maake 16:19-20; Luka 24:50-53
6DebaŇč keŇč Yesu na …Ē kpilala baŇč be yilaa n…õ te be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, mena d…Ē…Ē n…õn…õ…õ n ka besena gyoori keŇč fa Iseraetena?‚ÄĚ 7Bot…Ē…Ē te Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúKa te y…õ…õ kpene keŇč nyi …õ ka gyeŇč debaŇč keŇč ya, nawolo nyi debaŇč keŇč na wee keŇč mena nombii kei gyae ke k…ĒŇč d…Ē…Ē, kyae me kya gyaŇč. ∆Ü doŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē see Ňča. 8M…Ēna debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ waa mo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do …õ man n…õ, …õ ke nyiŇč doŇč. Na …õ ka bese ma adans…õ…õtena Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man, na Gyudia na Samaria t…õ…õlese pou d…Ē…Ē mo kelii t…õ…õle pou …Ēto.‚ÄĚ 9∆Ü kekolosi k…õŇča pou taŇč na, te Wurubuar…õ mo e laŇč adido be popou siaman ka gyoo Ňč…Ēma man. Mena d…Ē…Ē ba te naa e bela ya. 10Be ka seŇč bot…Ē…Ē be s…õ…õ b…õ…õ k…õ…õ adido fee n…õ, karatetee na tewul…õ…õ bala baale seŇč…õ…õ laa ba yenaŇč na ba doona ab…Ē…Ē wolom…õ…õ. 11Te be b…Ē…Ēse Yesu kpilala baŇč nyi, ‚ÄúGaleliatena, weera te …õ seŇč…õ…õ …õ ne k…õ…õ adido fee mena? Yesu kei, Ňčon Wurubuar…õ ba mo e lee …õ gyaŇč laŇč adido n…õ, mena ke te waa bese k…ĒŇč Ňčgba mena keŇč …õ be naa e …Ē ne gyu adido n…õ.‚ÄĚ
Ba lese Matiase nyi waa kyaa Gyudase naw…Ē…Ē man
12Te …Ē kpilala baŇč besew…Ē lee oliifi bula keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč Gyerusal…õm. Lee bot…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē keŇč man benaa na d…ĒŇča. Gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba w…Ē…Ē ŇčmeeseŇč sao na kufu n…õ. 13Ba ke k…ĒŇč kaa gyoo Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē keŇč man n…õ, te ba gyeŇčaw…Ē gyu abansoro deni be d…õ…õkyae keŇč man. BalaŇč baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē y…õna, Pita na Gy…Ēn na Anderiase na Filipo na Tomase na Batolomeo na Mateo na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Sim…Ēn Ňčon …Ē ne gyae …Ē balaŇč nideli n…õ, na Gyemisi bu Gyudase. 14Be popou na ala baale na Yesu …Ē naa M…õ…õre, na Yesu …Ē naabia ne y…õ…õ na n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč yilaa fane debaŇč kamas…õ.
15Wee daale balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ kaa yilaaw…Ē, na baa y…õ…õ Ňčgba balaŇč s…ĒŇčonoŇč na balaŇč sao n…õ (120). Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita korow…Ē seŇč yako w…Ē nyiaa, 16‚ÄúMe tebia, nombia Ňčan Wurubuar…õ nombia be yako see lee Gyudase wose man n…õ, kaboena nyi Ňče d…õ…õ k…ĒŇč mena. Gyudase kei wose man te gyoo Defidi yakow…Ē see nyiaa, Ňčon ne gyae la waa ta balaŇč baŇč baa gyu ke kyaŇč Yesu n…õ siaman.‚ÄĚ 17Naf…Ē na Gyudase kei kpu…õ de man, nawolo nyi Yesu lese na Ňčon m…Ē nyi waa kpu na daa na d…õ…õ y…õ…õ tom kei. 18K…Ēba keŇč Ňčon Gyudase be nyim lee …Ē nombiakum…õ…õ k…õŇča key…õ…õ man n…õ, te …Ē moow…Ē l…õ…õ t…õ…õle. T…õ…õle kenaŇč man te …Ē yalaw…Ē te …Ē t…Ē…Ē b…õ…õw…Ē te …Ē loobia pou leew…Ē. 19BalaŇč baŇč pou be kyaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ nyii mena nombii kei, mena d…Ē…Ē ba doo bot…Ē…Ē yele be d…õ…õ dei man nyi Ak…õledama, keŇč nawolo nyi Fatabo t…õ…õle. 20Gyoo Defidi yako Gyudase wose man nombia Wurubuar…õ N…Ē…ĒneŇč t…Ēne keŇč man nyi,
‚Äú∆Ü dekpaŇčalaŇč d…õ…õ doo yakaa keŇč Ňčolo na kyaa ke man ya.‚ÄĚ
∆Ü besew…Ē yako nyi,
‚ÄúWalaŇč w…Ēle waa kyaa …Ē naw…Ē…Ē man na waa y…õ…õ …Ē tom.‚ÄĚ
21-22‚ÄúMena d…Ē…Ē kaboena nyi Ňčolo waa kyaa …Ē naw…Ē…Ē man kpu na daa, na dee di de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu kefoŇč lee yeŇč man keŇč adans…õ…õ. Kaboena nyi walaŇč ŇčonaŇč waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon na …Ē kpu…õ na daa debaŇč keŇč pou daa na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu ne gyu d…õ…õ k…ĒŇč, lee debaŇč keŇč Gy…Ēn be s…Ē e Wurubuar…õ loŇč kelii debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo e lee de gyaŇč gyu adido n…õ.‚ÄĚ
23Mena d…Ē…Ē ba lese Gyos…õfo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Basabase yaa Gyustuse n…õ na Matiase. 24Te be fanew…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, nyaŇč gyeŇča na walaŇč kamas…õ kon…Ē…Ē man. Mena d…Ē…Ē wola daa tewul…õ…õ bala k…õw…Ē man Ňčon n ba lese nyi waa y…õ…õ Yesu kpilale, 25na waa kyaa Gyudase Ňčon …Ē be tina …Ē tom yela te …Ē be lam gyu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč kaboena e n…õ naw…Ē…Ē man.‚ÄĚ 26Mena d…Ē…Ē be l…Ē…Ē boe, te boe keŇč yalaw…Ē Matiase yele d…Ē…Ē. Kei nawolo nyi Ňčon te Wurubuar…õ lesew…Ē, te ba moo e kpu Yesu kpilala kufu daale baŇč man.

english_text:
The Coming of the Holy Spirit
1 On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place. 2Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting. 3Then they saw what looked like fiery tongues moving in all directions, and a tongue came and settled on each person there. 4The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
5Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem. 6And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages. 7They were excited and amazed, and said:
Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee? 8Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages? 9Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia, 10Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, parts of Libya near Cyrene, Rome, 11Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
12Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, ‚ÄúWhat does all this mean?‚ÄĚ
13Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, ‚ÄúThey are drunk.‚ÄĚ
Peter Speaks to the Crowd
14Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd:
Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say! 15You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning. 16But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
17 ‚ÄúWhen the last days come,
I will give my Spirit
to everyone.
Your sons and daughters
will prophesy.
Your young men
will see visions,
and your old men
will have dreams.
18In those days I will give
my Spirit to my servants,
both men and women,
and they will prophesy.
19‚ÄúI will work miracles
in the sky above
and wonders
on the earth below.
There will be blood and fire
and clouds of smoke.
20The sun will turn dark,
and the moon
will be as red as blood
before the great
and wonderful day
of the Lord appears.
21Then the Lord
will save everyone
who asks for his help.‚ÄĚ
22Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this. 23 God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross. 24 But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power. 25 What David said are really the words of Jesus,
‚ÄúI always see the Lord
near me,
and I will not be afraid
with him at my right side.
26Because of this,
my heart will be glad,
my words will be joyful,
and I will live in hope.
27The Lord won't leave me
in the grave.
I am his holy one,
and he won't let
my body decay.
28He has shown me
the path to life,
and he makes me glad
by being near me.‚ÄĚ
29My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here. 30 But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
31David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay. 32All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
33Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
34 David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, ‚ÄúThe Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side, 35until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.‚ÄĚ 36Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
37When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, ‚ÄúFriends, what should we do?‚ÄĚ
38Peter said to them, ‚ÄúTurn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit. 39This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.‚ÄĚ
40Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, ‚ÄúI beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.‚ÄĚ 41On that day about 3,000 believed his message and were baptized. 42They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together.
Life among the Lord's Followers
43Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked. 44 All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had. 45They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need. 46Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely, 47while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.

De baale m…Ē lee Kreti na Arebia, m…Ēna dee nyii bee kolosi nombia dinaana Ňčan Wurubuar…õ be y…õ…õ n…õ daa gbagba de deise man. 12GyakoloŇč gyoo w…Ē te ba nyeeya man m…Ē y…õ…õ w…Ē basaa te be b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyiaa, na nombia k…õŇča as…õ…õ y…õna sena baa? 13M…Ēna be man baale m…Ē kulu w…Ē nyi kei d…Ē…Ē be d…õ…õsee.

14Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita na …Ē tebia Yesu kpilala kufu daale baŇč korow…Ē seŇč, te …Ē gyinaa …Ē woya kolosi fa balaŇč dikpii keŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe tebia Gyudatena na …õm…õ…õ baŇč pou …õ kyaa Gyerusal…õm n…õ, …õ tei me gyaŇč nideli na maa wola Ňčon kpene keŇč nombii kei nawolo. 15Ne y…õ…õ Ňčon nyi balaŇč k…õw…Ē d…õ…õsee, m…Ēna na be d…õ…õsee ya. Nawolo nyi nebo weese gba yaa, n…õn…õ…õ te gy…Ē…Ē debaŇč kadaale. 16M…Ēna nombii kei y…õna kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Gyow…õle be kolosi see nyi ke gyae ke k…ĒŇč n…õ. 17Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Gyow…õle yakow…Ē see nyi,

22Pita gyina …Ē kekolosi keŇč d…Ē…Ē nyiaa, Iseraetena, …õ tei nombia k…õŇča nideli. Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē Yesu Nasar…õte baale Ňčon d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia lese …Ē w…Ē…Ēle wola …õ man Ňčgba mena keŇč …õm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyeŇč n…õ. K…õŇča diina adans…õ…õ nyi Wurubuar…õ kpila na e. 23Lee Wurubuar…õ gbagba kegyaebii na …Ē ka gyeŇč man te …Ē wulaa bakew…Ē see nyi baa lese Yesu fa Ňčon, te …õ yela balaŇč kum…õ…õ gy…Ē…Ē e …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē ko. 24M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ gyuusu e lee yeŇč man, te …Ē lese e lee yeŇč weeleŇč man, nawolo nyi yeŇč be tale kyaŇč e see …Ē gyaŇč kekpaakekpaa ya. 25Gyoo Defidi yakow…Ē lee Yesu wose man nyi,

29Me tebia, m…õ…õ gyae maa yako Ňčon kolo lee de naana Defidi wose man, …Ē yeŇčaw…Ē te be wuu e, …Ē yeb…Ē…Ē s…õ…õ doo de gyaŇč k…õbo kaa lii na gy…õŇč. 30Na …Ē y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre e, te …Ē gyeŇč nombia Ňčan Wurubuar…õ be kaŇč ntam yako see lee …Ē wose man nyi, waa mo Defidi duuluŇč man Ňčolo see gyoo na waa kyaa …Ē gyoorogbeli keŇč d…Ē…Ē di gyoori Ňčgba Ňčon Defidi n…õ. 31Gyoo Defidi naa kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ waa kaa y…õ…õ, mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē kolosiw…Ē lee Kristo kefoŇč lee yeŇč man wose man nombia nyi, ‚ÄėWurubuar…õ te tina e yela yeb…Ē…Ē man na waa dala ya.‚Äô 32Wurubuar…õ te gyuusu Yesu kei lee yeŇč man, te de popou ne di adans…õ…õ nyi nombii kei y…õ…õ ampaŇč. 33∆Ü kya Wurubuar…õ gyinaa e adido te …Ē moo e see …Ē dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē moo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon fa e Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be kaŇč ntam see n…õ. 37BalaŇč baŇč kenyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, takaa w…Ē nideli te be b…Ē…Ēse Pita na Yesu kpilala akaŇč baŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe tebia, sena yaa d…õ…õ y…õ…õ?‚ÄĚ 38Te Pita tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúWalaŇč kamas…õ waa ky…õ…õkee lee …Ē nombiakum…õ…õ man, na baa s…Ē e Wurubuar…õ loŇč lee Yesu Kristo yele man, na Wurubuar…õ ka mo …Ē nombiakum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ e, na waa mo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do …Ē man. 39Wurubuar…õ nombii keŇč …Ē be yako see nyi waa mo …Ē feliŇč do …õ man n…õ, y…õ…õ …õm…õ…õ na …õ bia na baŇč pou be kyaa lemlem, na baŇč pou de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ waa baake w…Ē k…ĒŇč Ňčon gbagba gyaŇč n…õ wui.‚ÄĚ 40Pita kolosiw…Ē fa w…Ē nideli te …Ē faree w…Ē te …Ē sola w…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆ź lese …õ wose lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč kum…õ…õ k…õw…Ē man, na Wurubuar…õ ke l…õ…õ …õ nyee.‚ÄĚ 41Be man balaŇč burum l…õ…õ …Ē nombia Ňčan di, te be s…Ē…Ē w…Ē Wurubuar…õ loŇč. BalaŇč kakpoŇčse batooro (3000) kaa kpu la na Kristotena dikpii keŇč mena wee kenaŇč.
BaŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ kekyaabii man
42Be kyaŇča Yesu kpilala baŇč kawola man nideli, te be popou ne y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč gyaŇčee debaŇč kamas…õ. B…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč di ween…õ…õ Ňčan Yesu kpa baa di na baa mo t…Ē…Ēse e n…õ, te b…õ…õ bese y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč gyaŇčee fane.
43Yesu Kpilala baŇč y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia burum, te gyakoloŇč gyoo balaŇč baŇč pou. 44BaŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ, y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč ba kekyaa man, na b…õ…õ k…õ…õ ba ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan be dana n…õ nyi y…õ…õ be popou wui. 45Te be y…Ē…Ē ba ab…Ē…Ē Ňčaale m…Ē, na b…õ…õ kpala k…Ēba keŇč fa walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčgba mena keŇč ne hia e n…õ. 46Wee kamas…õ na, be dikpii keŇč ne gyaŇčee be Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man. B…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč te b…õ…õ mo kon…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa, na sia kagye di ween…õ…õ bom ba akpaŇčalaŇčse man. 47B…õ…õ lese Wurubuar…õ yele, te balaŇč pou m…Ē ne k…õl…õ…õ w…Ē. Te wee kamas…õ m…Ē n…õ, de GbeŇčgyoo na mo balaŇč w…Ēl…õ…õ baŇč …Ē ne l…õ…õ ba nyeeya n…õ kaa kpu na w…Ē.

english_text:
Peter and John Heal a Lame Man
1The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple. 2A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
3The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money. 4But they looked straight at him and said, ‚ÄúLook up at us!‚ÄĚ
5The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something. 6But Peter said, ‚ÄúI don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.‚ÄĚ 7Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up.
At once the man's feet and ankles became strong, 8and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God. 9Everyone saw him walking around and praising God. 10They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man.
Peter Speaks in the Temple
11While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch. 12Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said:
Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious? 13 The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
14 You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free, 15and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done. 16You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
17My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing. 18But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise. 19So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven. 20Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah. 21But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
22 Moses said, ‚ÄúThe Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you. 23 No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.‚ÄĚ
24Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening. 25 You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, ‚ÄúAll nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.‚ÄĚ 26God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.

english_text:
Peter and John Are Brought in Front of the Council
1The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived. 2These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death. 3It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night. 4But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about 5,000 followers of the Lord.
5The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem. 6The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family. 7They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, ‚ÄúBy what power and in whose name have you done this?‚ÄĚ
8Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
9You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed. 10But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life. 11 He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all. 12Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
13The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus. 14But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
15The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other, 16‚ÄúWhat can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen. 17But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.‚ÄĚ 18So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
19Peter and John answered, ‚ÄúDo you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him? 20We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.‚ÄĚ
21-22The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than 40 years old, and everyone was praising God for what had happened.
Peter and Others Pray for Courage
23As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them. 24 When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said:
Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them. 25 And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say:
‚ÄúWhy are all the Gentiles
so furious?
Why do people
make foolish plans?
26The kings of earth
prepare for war,
and the rulers
join together
against the Lord
and his Messiah.‚ÄĚ
27 Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah. 28They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
29Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message. 30Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
31After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message.
Sharing Possessions
32 The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other. 33In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers, 34and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money 35to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
36-37Joseph was one of the followers who had sold a piece of property and brought the money to the apostles. He was a Levite from Cyprus, and the apostles called him Barnabas, which means ‚Äúone who encourages others.‚ÄĚ
translation_text:
Be kamo Pita na Gy…Ēn gyu don…Ē…Ē kegyiise siaman
1DebaŇč keŇč Pita na Gy…Ēn s…õ…õ b…õ…õ kolosi fa balaŇč baŇč n…õ, te Wurubuar…õ saese na kegyia Ňčon …Ē ne k…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ gyee Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč n…õ, na balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ baake w…Ē nyi Sadukiitena n…õ baale k…ĒŇčaw…Ē be gyaŇč. 2Ba gyeŇča baŇč nideli nawolo nyi, Pita na Gy…Ēn na wola balaŇč baŇč nyi, Yesu te fom lee yeŇč man, keŇč nawolo nyi balaŇč pou baa foŇč lee yeŇč man bela. 3Mena d…Ē…Ē be kyaŇča Pita na Gy…Ēn ka do deni man, na t…õ…õ te lam d…Ē…Ē, be yela be doow…Ē kelii t…õ…õ kena. 4M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē nyii nombia Ňčan Pita na Gy…Ēn be yako n…õ, be man burum l…õ…õ Ňča di, mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ, be man baala wukuw…Ē kelii balaŇč kakpoŇčse banoŇč (5000).
5T…õ…õ kena keŇč n…õ, Gyudatena kegyiise na be siamantena na ba mmaraa wolala baŇč gyaŇčeew…Ē Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man. 6Be gyaŇčeew…Ē na saese pou kegyia Anase na Kaifase na Gy…Ēn na Al…õkesanda na baala baale baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee saese pou kegyia Ňčon dekpaŇčalaŇč man n…õ. 7Be yela ba moo Pita na Gy…Ēn kaa seŇč be siaman te be b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúWoŇč w…Ē…Ēle yaa am…Ēte yele man yaa …õ ky…Ē…Ē balee Ňčon?‚ÄĚ 8Te Pita Ňčon Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon doo …Ē man nideli n…õ, tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúKegyiise na siamantena, 9akpaa gy…õŇč …õ te baake daa kaa seŇč …õ siaman …õ ne b…Ē…Ēse daa lee kolo kpaakpaa keŇč d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ, te …Ētakase Ňčon be nyiŇč doŇč koro seŇč n…õ d…Ē…Ē na, 10kenaŇč na m…õ…õ gyae …õm…õ…õ na Iseraetena pou ka gyeŇč nyi, Yesu Kristo Ňčon …Ē ba lee Nasar…õte n…õ yele man yaa balee aseŇčaŇč …õ siaman kei nyiŇča doŇč n…õ. Ňäon te …õ gy…Ē…Ēw…Ē sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē ko, te Wurubuar…õ gyuusu e lee yeŇč man. 11Yesu y…õna walaŇč Ňčon Wurubuar…õ nombia t…Ēne be yako nyi,
‚ÄėBoe keŇč …õm…õ…õ deni maara baŇč be b…õ…õ ke n…õ,
keŇč kaa bese na deni mok…Ē…Ē kasee boe keŇč ne yeli deni keŇč ne y…õ…õ doŇč nideli.‚Äô
12Yesu waageŇč kooŇč y…õna nyeel…õ…õre, Ňčon waageŇč kooŇč te Wurubuar…õ lesew…Ē t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē nyi de baake …Ē yele na waa l…õ…õ da nyee.‚ÄĚ
13Mena keŇč Pita na Gy…Ēn be kolosi nideli te ba te yee gyakoloŇč ya, te kegyiise baŇč kenyii nyi be y…õ…õ balaŇč yakaa nyibaŇčw…Ē keŇč be te gyu sukuu ya n…õ, ba wose yeŇča w…Ē. Bot…Ē…Ē te be naaw…Ē nyi baŇč na Yesu t…õ…õ la. 14M…Ēna ba kena balee Ňčon b…Ē…Ē ky…Ē e seŇč…õ…õ Pita na Gy…Ēn gyaŇč bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, ba te tale nyiŇč nombiikum daale yako lee ba wose man ya. 15Mena d…Ē…Ē be yako w…Ē nyi baa lee nombia dedii deni keŇč man, bot…Ē…Ē te kegyiise baŇč moo ba nyeeya baŇč na d…ĒŇča. 16B…õ…õ b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyi, ‚ÄúWoŇč yaa d…õ…õ y…õ…õ balaŇč k…õw…Ē? WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, gyeŇč nyi baŇč y…õ…õna gyakoloŇč nombii kei, te d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ tale l…õ…õ ka aŇčmaareŇč ya. 17Mena keŇč d…Ē…Ē ne gyae nyi baa mo nombia k…õŇča kpeŇčkpeleŇč d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, …õ yela d…õ…õ t…Ē w…Ē nideli nyi be na tekaa kolosi Yesu kei nombia fa walaŇč na walaŇč ya.‚ÄĚ 18Mena d…Ē…Ē be baake w…Ē gyoo be gyaŇč bela, te be t…Ē…Ē w…Ē nyi be na tekaa kolosi Yesu nombia, yaa baa wola balaŇč kolo lee …Ē yele man korakora bela ya. 19KeŇčte Pita na Gy…Ēn tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ k…õ…õ nombia k…õŇča man nideli nyi woŇčti dei la Wurubuar…õ siaman? Dee nyii …õ wui yaa dee nyii Wurubuar…õ wui? 20Nawolo nyi daa d…Ē…Ē, d…Ē…Ē tale tina kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē naa, na keŇč d…Ē…Ē nyii n…õ kekolosi yela ya.‚ÄĚ 21Mena d…Ē…Ē kegyiise baŇč t…Ē…Ē w…Ē nideli bela, te be tina w…Ē yela. Be te nyiŇč gb…õ…õ keŇč baa mo w…ĒŇč ba deŇčela ya, nawolo nyi balaŇč baŇč pou na lese Wurubuar…õ yele lee kpene keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 22Balee Ňčon b…Ē…Ē ky…Ē e kawee kei n…õ, kela kulutooneŇč s…ĒŇčola (40).
Kristotena baŇč kefane
23Be ke tina Pita na Gy…Ēn yela n…õ, te ba besew…Ē gyu be tebia gyaŇč keyako w…Ē kpene kamas…õ keŇč saese kegyiise na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise baŇč be yako w…Ē n…õ. 24Kristotena baŇč kenyii nombia ŇčenaŇč n…õ, be popou moo n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč fane fa Wurubuar…õ nyi, ‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo, nyaŇč Ňčon n be y…õ…õ adido na ateta, na …õpo na kpene kamas…õ keŇč doo Ňče man. 25NyaŇč t…õ…õ la n feliŇč Ňčon d…Ē…Ē te n yela de naana Defidi Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ n tobaale e n…õ yakow…Ē nyi,
‚ÄėWeera d…Ē…Ē te t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč na gyeŇčee baŇčse,
te b…õ…õ bake nombiakum…õ…õ yakaa yakaa,
m…Ēna Ňče bee dee ya n…õ?
26T…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyooneŇč ne desina ba wose fa yoo,
te siamantena baŇč m…Ē na koro seŇč tia de GbeŇčgyoo
Wurubuar…õ na nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon …Ē be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ.‚Äô
27AmpaŇč te gyoo Herode na P…Ēnti…Ēse Pilato na Iseraetena na baŇč m…Ē ba te y…õ…õ Iseraetenaw…Ē ya n…õ, gyaŇčeew…Ē don…Ē…Ē kei man te be bakew…Ē tia n tobaale Yesu Ňčon ayim…õ…õ be doo …Ē wose man ya, te nyaŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo e y…õ…õ nyeel…õ…õre na gyoo n…õ. 28Be gyaŇčeew…Ē te be y…õ…õ kpene keŇč nyaŇč Wurubuar…õ n be wula yako see nyi gyae kek…ĒŇč n…õ, lee n doŇč na n kegyaebii man. 29N…õn…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ, k…õ…õ mena keŇč b…õ…õ Ňčmaase daa na n ka do daa n tewul…õ…õ kakyeŇč na d…õ…õ tale gy…Ē de kon…Ē…Ē kolosi n nombia Ňčan. 30Wurubuar…õ koro seŇč na n keky…Ē kaweese, na n ke ta de d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia lese n w…Ē…Ēle wola lee n tobaale Yesu Ňčon ayim…õ…õ be doo …Ē wose man ya n…õ yele man.‚ÄĚ 31Ba ke fane taŇč keŇč n…õ, te bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee n…õ wosiw…Ē te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon kaa gyoow…Ē be popou man nideli te be gy…Ē…Ē be kon…Ē…Ē kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan.
Kristotena baŇč n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč key…õ…õ
32BalaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Kristo Yesu di n…õ, kyaaw…Ē n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč man te ba agyueŇč m…Ē y…õ…õ dokoloŇč. Ňäolo b…õ…õ yako nyi …Ē kolo y…õ…õ waageŇč wui ya, m…Ēna be kp…õl…õ…õ ba ab…Ē…Ē pou fa d…ĒŇča. 33Yesu kpilala baŇč moo Wurubuar…õ doŇč dinaa keŇč di adans…õ…õ nyi, de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu te fom lee yeŇč man, te Wurubuar…õ moo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ. 34Kolo te hia be man Ňčolo ya, nawolo nyi baŇč be dana den…õ…õ na t…õ…õlese n…õ y…Ē…Ē Ňča na b…õ…õ mo k…Ēba keŇč b…Ē…Ē nyim n…õ, 35k…Ēna Yesu kpilala baŇč na be ta mo kpala fa w…Ē. B…õ…õ fa walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčgba mena keŇč ne hia e n…õ. 36Mena ke te Lewi baale Ňčolo Ňčon …Ē ba lee Saipor…Ēse t…õ…õle man, te …Ē yele y…õna Gyos…õfo n…õ y…õ…õw…Ē. Yesu kpilala baŇč doo e yele nyi Banabase, keŇč nawolo nyi walaŇč Ňčon …Ē na do balaŇč kakyeŇč. 37Ňäon m…Ē ka moo …Ē t…õ…õle keŇč …Ē dana n…õ y…Ē…Ē, te …Ē moo k…Ēba keŇč …Ē be nyim n…õ kaa fa Yesu kpilala baŇč.

english_text:
Peter Condemns Ananias and Sapphira
1Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property. 2But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves.
So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles, 3Peter said, ‚ÄúWhy has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit? 4The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!‚ÄĚ
5 As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened. 6Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
7Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband. 8Peter asked her, ‚ÄúTell me, did you sell the property for this amount?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúYes,‚ÄĚ she answered, ‚Äúthat's the amount.‚ÄĚ
9Then Peter said, ‚ÄúWhy did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!‚ÄĚ 10At once she fell at Peter's feet and died.
When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband. 11The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened.
Peter's Unusual Power
12The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch. 13No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
14Many men and women started having faith in the Lord. 15Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them. 16A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed.
Trouble for the Apostles
17The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous. 18They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail. 19But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said, 20‚ÄúGo to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.‚ÄĚ 21So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching.
The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail. 22The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said, 23‚ÄúWe found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.‚ÄĚ 24The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
25Just then someone came in and said, ‚ÄúNow those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!‚ÄĚ 26The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
27When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them, 28 ‚ÄúWe told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.‚ÄĚ
29Peter and the apostles replied:
We don't obey people. We obey God. 30You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life 31and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven. 32We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
33When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles. 34But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while. 35Then he said to the council:
Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men. 36Not long ago Theudas claimed to be someone important, and about 400 men joined him. But he was killed, and all his followers were scattered. That was the end of that.
37Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
38So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail. 39 But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God.
The council members agreed with what he said, 40and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
41The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus. 42Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_text:
Ananiase na Safira
1Balee Ňčolo kyaaw…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake e nyi Ananiase, na …Ē w…õ…õle m…Ē yele y…õna Safira. BaŇč m…Ē gyuuw…Ē ka mo ba agyapad…õ…õ daale y…Ē…Ē. 2Ňäon na …Ē w…õ…õle y…õ…õ n…Ē…Ē te ba moo k…Ēba keŇč b…Ē…Ē nyim n…õ man Ňčaale weese, te baale Ňčon moo akaŇč keŇč gyu kefa Yesu kpilala baŇč. 3Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita b…Ē…Ēse e nyi, ‚ÄúAnaniase, weera d…Ē…Ē te n yela …Ēb…Ēnsam dii n d…Ē…Ē mena, te …Ē yela n dii dekara fa Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon, te n moo n k…Ēba keŇč n be nyim lee agyapad…õ…õ keŇč n be y…Ē…Ē n…õ man Ňčaale weese? 4Agyapad…õ…õ keŇč te y…õ…õ n wui p…õte n y…Ē…Ē ke yee? N y…Ē…Ē ke taŇč m…Ēna k…Ēba keŇč n be nyim n…õ te y…õ…õ n wui yee? N…õn…õ…õ weera n bakew…Ē n kon…Ē…Ē man te n y…õ…õ nombiikum kei mena? Na denibalaŇč te n dii dekara fa ya, Wurubuar…õ te n dii dekara fa.‚ÄĚ 5Ananiase ka betaa nyii nombia k…õŇča p…õ, te kara e l…Ē te …Ē yeŇčaw…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē gyakoloŇč gyoo baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē nyii nombia ŇčenaŇč n…õ. 6KeŇčte tewul…õ…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa mo kolo milii e, te be gate e lee gyu kewuu.
7Te kyare ya te …Ē w…õ…õle Ňčon m…Ē kaa gyoow…Ē bot…Ē…Ē m…Ēna Ňčon ba gyeŇč kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč ya. 8Te Pita b…Ē…Ēse alo Ňčon nyiaa, ‚ÄúYako maŇč, k…Ēba keŇč …õm…õ…õ na n baale Ananiase be nyim lee agyapad…õ…õ keŇč key…Ē…Ē man pou yaa …Ē moow…Ē k…ĒŇč n…õ?‚ÄĚ Te …Ē tiran…Ē…Ē nyi ayi, mena te de y…Ē…Ē ke. 9Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita b…Ē…Ēse e nyi, ‚ÄúWeera te …õm…õ…õ na n baale y…õ…õ n…Ē…Ē nyi …õ kek…õ…õse de GbeŇčgyoo feliŇč Ňčon k…õ…õ! K…õ…õ, balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē moo n baale kewuu n…õ, baŇč seŇč…õ…õ la denan…Ē…Ē man n…õ, baa seele nyaŇč m…Ē lee.‚ÄĚ 10Karatetee dokoloŇč te kara Ňčon m…Ē l…Ē Pita naw…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ te …Ē yeŇčaw…Ē. Tewul…õ…õ baŇč kek…ĒŇč kaa gyoo te ba kena nyi Ňčon m…Ē ta yem n…õ, te ba mo e lee gyu kewuu …Ē baale yenaŇč. 11Mena d…Ē…Ē gyakoloŇč dinaa daale gyoo Kristotena dikpii keŇč pou, na baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ.
Kristo kpilala baŇč keky…Ē kaweesetena
12Yesu kpilala baŇč y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia na nombia dinaana burum balaŇč baŇč man. Te baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu Kristo di n…õ, ne gyu ke yilaa Solom…Ēn poto keŇč man. 13Na balaŇč ne bu w…Ē nideli, m…Ēna balaŇč yenaasetena baŇč yee mena keŇč baa kaa kpu na w…Ē. 14M…Ēna ala na baala burum l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di, te Wurubuar…õ moo w…Ē kaa kpu na Kristotena baŇč. 15Lee gyakoloŇč nombia Ňčan Yesu kpilala baŇč ne y…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē, balaŇč na mo be kaweesetena kaa doosi afoŇčoŇčse na mpase d…Ē…Ē gb…õ…õnaa n…Ē…Ēman, na akpaa Pita de k…ĒŇč …Ē ne lam na …Ē w…ĒŇčtaa ke yala be man baale d…Ē…Ē, na baa nyiŇč kawee keky…Ē. 16BalaŇč lee don…Ē…Ēse Ňčan benaa na Gyerusal…õm n…õ k…ĒŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē keŇč man, te ba moo be kaweesetena na baŇč feliŇčkum…õ…õ ne naase w…Ē diyem n…õ k…ĒŇč te be ky…Ē…Ē w…Ē pou.
Kristotena baŇč diyem kena
17Mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia Ňčon, na …Ē kegyiise na baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee Sadukiitena dikpii man n…õ sia y…õ…õw…Ē Yesu kpilala baŇč d…Ē…Ē, te be bakew…Ē see nyi baa gyae kolo y…õ…õ w…Ē. 18Mena d…Ē…Ē be kyaŇča Yesu kpilala baŇč te ba moo w…Ē ket…Ē deni. 19M…Ēna ke nelim n…õ, de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ kpilale k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa toro deni keŇč fa w…Ē, te …Ē moo w…Ē lee deni keŇč man, te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi, 20‚Äú∆ź gyu ka seŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man na …õ kekolosi nyeedoŇč w…Ēle kei wose man nombia fa balaŇč baŇč pou.‚ÄĚ 21Yesu Kristo kpilala baŇč y…õ…õw…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon be yako n…õ, mena d…Ē…Ē nebo nebo fuu te be gyuuw…Ē Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man, te be fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ wola balaŇč baŇč. Wurubuar…õ Sae kegyia Ňčon na …Ē kegyiise baake Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč pou, te be gyaŇčeew…Ē te be kpilaw…Ē nyi be ka mo Yesu kpilala baŇč lee deni keŇč man k…ĒŇč. 22M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kpili n…õ gyuuw…Ē kelii bot…Ē…Ē na, ba te naa Yesu kpilala baŇč deni keŇč man ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē ba besew…Ē k…ĒŇč kaa yako kegyiise baŇč nyi, 23‚ÄúDe gyuu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…Ē…Ē kyaŇč w…Ē do n…õ na disim keŇč t…Ē…õ kiiŇč, na baŇč m…Ē bee gyee ke n…õ seŇč…õ…õ be daseŇčae man. M…Ēna da toro disim keŇč na, da te naa Yesu kpilala baŇč deni keŇč man ya.‚ÄĚ 24DebaŇč keŇč kegyia Ňčon …Ē ne k…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ gyee Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, na saese kegyiise baŇč be nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, ta seŇč w…Ē nideli ya. Te be b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyi, ‚ÄúNa woŇč y…õna tewul…õ…õ k…õw…Ē baa?‚ÄĚ 25Bot…Ē…Ē te balee Ňčolo k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa yako w…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆ź nyii, balaŇč baŇč …õ be kyam do deni man n…õ, seŇč…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man b…õ…õ wola balaŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia.‚ÄĚ 26Mena d…Ē…Ē kegyia Ňčon …Ē ne k…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč bee gyee Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, na …Ē tewul…õ…õ gyuuw…Ē ka moo w…Ē k…ĒŇč kegyiise baŇč gyaŇč, m…Ēna be ta mo w…Ē doŇč man ya, nawolo nyi bee yee nyi balaŇč baŇč baa fuŇčii w…Ē boya. 27Be ka mo w…Ē k…ĒŇč nombiadiira baŇč siaman n…õ, te sae kegyia Ňčon yako w…Ē 28nyi, ‚ÄúDe faree Ňčon nyi …õ na wola kolo lee Yesu yele man ya, m…Ēna …õ k…õ…õ ya aney…õ…õŇč, …õ ta mo …Ē nombia Ňčan …õ na wolo n…õ wulu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē pou, te …õ ne gyae …õ ka mo …Ē yeŇč seele daa.‚ÄĚ 29Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita na Yesu kpilala akaŇč baŇč tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúKaboena nyi dee nyii Wurubuar…õ dei kela balaŇč wui.‚ÄĚ 30De naana…Ē Wurubuar…õ Ňčon gyuusu na Yesu Ňčon …õ be gy…Ē…Ē e sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē ko e n…õ lee yeŇč man. 31Wurubuar…õ gyina e adido te …Ē mo e see …Ē dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē moo e y…õ…õ siamante na nyeel…õ…õre, na waa yela Iseraetena ke nyiŇč gb…õ…õ ky…õ…õkee lee be nombiakum…õ…õ man na baa k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč, na waa mo be nombiakum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ w…Ē. 32Daa ne diina nombia k…õŇča adans…õ…õ. Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon …Ē ba mo do baŇč bee nyii …Ē dei man n…õ, m…Ē ne di nombia k…õŇča adans…õ…õ.
33Kegyiise baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee n…õ nyii nombia k…õŇča na ba gyeŇča baŇč nideli, mena d…Ē…Ē be gyaew…Ē nyi baa ko Yesu kpilala baŇč. 34M…Ēna be man Ňčolo, Ňčon …Ē doo Farasiitena dikpii keŇč man te b…õ…õ baake e nyi Gamaleli, te …Ē y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ mmaraa keŇč wolale e, te …Ē wose man dana obuo don…Ē…Ē keŇč man n…õ, korow…Ē seŇč be man. Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē yela ba lese Yesu kpilala baŇč yenaŇč. 35Te …Ē yako kegyiise baŇč nyi, ‚ÄúMe tebia Iseraetena, …õ k…õ…õ kpene keŇč …õ ne gyae …õ ke y…õ…õ balaŇč k…õw…Ē n…õ nideli. 36DebaŇč duguŇč daale kaa laŇčaw…Ē na, balee Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Teudase n…õ korow…Ē seŇč te …Ē y…õ…õ …Ē wose Ňčgba walaŇč dinaa Ňčolo n…õ, te balaŇč s…ĒŇčonoŇč akpeŇč anaara (400) korow…Ē sila e. M…Ēna be ka ko e na …Ē balaŇč baŇč pou m…Ē yaaseew…Ē, keŇčte …Ē kawola keŇč pou m…Ē kaa y…õ…õw…Ē yakaa. 37Kei m…Ē w…Ēle debaŇč keŇč b…õ…õ kala balaŇč n…õ, te Galelia baale Gyuda korow…Ē seŇč, te Ňčon m…Ē kaa beo balaŇč burum sila …Ē wose. Be ka ko Ňčon m…Ē n…õ, …Ē balaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē sila e n…õ yaaseew…Ē. 38Mena d…Ē…Ē nombia k…õŇča man n…õ, m…õ…õ yako Ňčon nyi …õ na y…õ…õ balaŇč k…õw…Ē kolo ya. ∆ź yela baa ta, nawolo nyi akpaa kpene keŇč b…õ…õ wolo te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ, lee deniwalaŇč gyaŇč na, keŇč m…Ē gyae ke y…õ…õ yakaa bo mena. 39M…Ēna akpaa ka lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč na, …õ be gyae …õ ke tale t…Ē w…Ē gb…õ…õ ya. ∆ź kena bo nyi …õ ne yoo bo na Wurubuar…õ.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē kegyiise baŇč m…Ē y…õ…õw…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč Gamaleli be yako w…Ē n…õ.
40Mena d…Ē…Ē be baake Yesu kpilala baŇč k…ĒŇč te be yela be tuku w…Ē, te be t…Ē…Ē w…Ē nyi be na kolosi kolo lee Yesu yele man bela ya. Te be tina w…Ē yela. 41Yesu kpilala baŇč ka lee kegyiise baŇč gyaŇč n…õ na be sia ta gye, nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ te k…õ…õ w…Ē nyi baŇč m…Ē kaboena nyi baa do w…Ē des…õŇč lee Yesu d…Ē…Ē. 42Te wee kamas…õ na, bee gyu Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa man na balaŇč akpaŇčalaŇčse man. B…õ…õ wola nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan nyi Yesu y…õna Kristo, nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ. Te be te tina nombia k…õŇča kawola yela ya.

english_text:
Seven Leaders for the Church
1A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
2The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, ‚ÄúWe should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables. 3My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things. 4We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.‚ÄĚ
5This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch. 6These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work. 7God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord.
Stephen Is Arrested
8God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people. 9But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves ‚ÄúFree Men.‚ÄĚ They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him. 10But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him. 11So they talked some men into saying, ‚ÄúWe heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!‚ÄĚ
12They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
13Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, ‚ÄúThis man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses. 14We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.‚ÄĚ 15 Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_text:
Ba lese balaŇč nyetooro nyi baa ky…Ē Yesu kpilala baŇč
1Weeya ala ŇčenaŇč man na baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ korow…Ē bee wuku te kekolosi kaa gyoo Gyudatena baŇč b…õ…õ kolosi Giriki dei, na Gyudatena akaŇč baŇč man. Gyudatena baŇč b…õ…õ kolosi Giriki dei n…õ, kpa Gyudatena akaŇč baŇč ne wuritii w…Ē ween…õ…õ kekpala man, nawolo nyi be kpa wee kamas…õ ween…õ…õ Ňčan b…õ…õ kp…õl…õ…õ n…õ, bee tuŇč baŇč wui liala baŇč ya. 2KeŇčte Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŇč baake baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ gyaŇčee, te be yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúTekaboena nyi daa d…õ…õ bese tina Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan keyako yela, na d…õ…õ k…ĒŇč kaa k…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē kekp…õl…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē bela ya. 3Mena d…Ē…Ē de tebia, …õ k…õ…õ …õ man na …õ ka lese baala nyetooro baŇč ba wose man dana obuo, te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon doo be man nideli te be sia be kare m…Ē n…õ, na d…õ…õ mo mena tom kei do be nyiŇčmaa man. 4Na daa ke nyiŇč mo de doŇč pou do kefane, na Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan keyako man.‚ÄĚ 5Nombia naŇč Yesu kaseela baŇč be yako n…õ, seŇča balaŇč baŇč pou nideli. Te ba lese Sitifin Ňčon …Ē ne l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di nideli te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč doo …Ē man nideli n…õ. Te ba besew…Ē lese Filipo na Prokoro na Nikan…Ē na Tim…Ēn na Pamena na Nikolase Ňčon …Ē ba lee Anti…Ēke te …Ē ba mo …Ē wose kpu Gyuda …Ēsom keŇč man n…õ. 6KeŇčte ba moo balaŇč nyetooro k…õw…Ē kaa seŇč Yesu kpilala baŇč siaman. Te ba moo be nyiŇčmaase gyakaa be d…Ē…Ē te be fanew…Ē fa w…Ē.
7Mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan yaaseew…Ē bileŇčbileŇč, te baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ wukuw…Ē burum Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē keŇč man. Te Wurubuar…õ saese burum baale m…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di.
Ba kekyaŇč Sitifin
8Te Sitifin Ňčon Wurubuar…õ ba mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele e nideli, te …Ē doŇč doo …Ē man n…õ y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia na nombia dinaana balaŇč baŇč man. 9M…Ēna Gyudatena baale baŇč b…õ…õ gyaŇčee Gyudatena …Ēsom deni keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi gbeŇča baŇč b…Ē…Ē tina w…Ē yela n…õ …Ēsom deni man n…õ, fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč na Sitifin. BalaŇč benaŇč baale lee Kirene na Al…õkesanderia na Silisia na Esia t…õ…õlese man. 10M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon faa Sitifin nyansa te …Ē kolosiw…Ē nideli mena d…Ē…Ē be te nyiŇč keŇč baa yako bela ya.
11KeŇč d…Ē…Ē be gyuuw…Ē ke feefu do balaŇč baale nyi baa di dekara gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúDe nyiiw…Ē …Ē ne kolosi mmusuo nombia gyakaa de naana Mosesi na Wurubuar…õ d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 12Nn…õ te be w…Ēl…õ…õ e fa balaŇč baŇč na …Ēsom kegyiise na mmaraa wolala baŇč pou, te ba korow…Ē seŇč …Ē d…Ē…Ē te ba kyaŇča e gyu don…Ē…Ē kegyiise siaman. 13Mena d…Ē…Ē be gyae balaŇč baale k…ĒŇč, te be kaa dii ak…õr…õ…õ gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúDebaŇč kamas…õ na balee kei ne kolosi bo tia de Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč, na Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan Mosesi ba wola daa n…õ.‚ÄĚ 14Nawolo nyi de nyiiw…Ē Ňčgba …Ē ne yako nyi, ‚ÄúYesu Ňčon …Ē ba lee Nasar…õte n…õ, waa y…õl…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa kei l…Ē, na waa ky…õ…õkee ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou Mosesi ba mo see daa n…õ.‚ÄĚ 15Te nombiadiira baŇč be kyaa deni keŇč man n…õ, k…õ…õ Sitifin n…õ yididi te be naaw…Ē Ňčgba …Ē sia gb…õ…õ te ky…õ…õkee y…õ…õ Ňčgba Wurubuar…õ kpilale sia gb…õ…õ n…õ.

english_text:
Stephen's Speech
1The high priest asked Stephen, ‚ÄúAre they telling the truth about you?‚ÄĚ
2 Stephen answered:
Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran. 3God told him, ‚ÄúLeave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.‚ÄĚ 4 Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran.
After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live. 5 God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children. 6 God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated 400 years. 7 But he also said, ‚ÄúI will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.‚ÄĚ
8 God said to Abraham, ‚ÄúEvery son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.‚ÄĚ So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons. 9 These men were our ancestors.
Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him 10 and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
11 Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat. 12But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time. 13 It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
14 Joseph sent for his father and his relatives. In all, there were 75 of them. 15 His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did. 16 Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
17 Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased. 18Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph. 19 He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
20 During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home. 21 Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son. 22Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
23 When Moses was 40 years old, he wanted to help the Israelites because they were his own people. 24One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian. 25Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
26The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, ‚ÄúMen, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?‚ÄĚ
27But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, ‚ÄúWho made you our ruler and judge? 28Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?‚ÄĚ 29 When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
30 Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai. 31Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said, 32‚ÄúI am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.‚ÄĚ Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
33The Lord said to him, ‚ÄúTake off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy. 34With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.‚ÄĚ
35 This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, ‚ÄúWho made you our leader and judge?‚ÄĚ God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
36 In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for 40 years. 37 Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, ‚ÄúGod will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.‚ÄĚ 38 Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us. 39But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
40 The people said to Aaron, ‚ÄúMake some gods to lead us! Moses led us out of Egypt, but we don't know what's happened to him now.‚ÄĚ 41 Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
42 God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, ‚ÄúPeople of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those 40 years in the desert. 43Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.‚ÄĚ
44 The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen. 45 Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David. 46 He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel. 47 And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
48But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
49 ‚ÄúHeaven is my throne,
and the earth
is my footstool.
What kind of house
will you build for me?
In what place will I rest?
50I have made everything.‚ÄĚ
51 You stubborn and hardheaded people! You are always fighting against the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors did. 52Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him. 53Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it.
Stephen Is Stoned to Death
54When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious. 55 But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side. 56Then Stephen said, ‚ÄúI see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!‚ÄĚ
57The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen 58and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
59As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, ‚ÄúLord Jesus, please welcome me!‚ÄĚ 60He knelt down and shouted, ‚ÄúLord, don't blame them for what they have done.‚ÄĚ Then he died.

Waa lee bo …õm…õ…õ gbagba …õ balaŇč man.‚Äô 38Mosesi kei na Iseraetena baŇč ke kyaa depampaa keŇč man n…õ, Wurubuar…õ kpilale kaa kolosiw…Ē na e Sinai bula keŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nyeedoŇč nombia Ňčan fa de naana…Ē, te Ňče kaa tuŇča daa m…Ē.

Te baŇč be kon…Ē…Ēse man na, naf…Ē be gyaew…Ē nyi baa bese gyu Igyipite. 40Mena d…Ē…Ē be yako Aar…Ēn nyi, ‚ÄėY…õ…õ b…õ…õneŇč fa daa na baa ta de siaman, nawolo nyi d…Ē…Ē gyeŇč kpene keŇč be y…õ…õ Mosesi Ňčon …Ē ba mo daa lee Igyipite n…õ ya.‚Äô 41Weeya ŇčenaŇč man te be y…õ…õ nunkyue bu mo y…õ…õ be bane, te be faa ke kedi te be dii be n…Ē…Ē mo lese kpene keŇč baŇč gbagba ba mo be nyiŇčmaase y…õ…õ n…õ yele. 42Mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ lese …Ē sia yela w…Ē, te …Ē moo w…Ē fa adido kyolobiise kasom, Ňčgba mena keŇč b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase do Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra t…Ēne keŇč man n…õ. Ba Ňčolo Ňčmarasew…Ē nyi Wurubuar…õ kpa,
‚ÄėO Iseraetena, na maŇč te …õ koo wonembia deesi fa
kulutooneŇč s…ĒŇčola (40) depampaa dinaa keŇč man ya.
43∆ź bane Mol…õki te …õ y…õ…õ kanyaŇč deni do ke,

BaŇč y…õna ban…õ…õ baŇč …õ be y…õ…õ nyi …õ ka som!
Mena d…Ē…Ē maa yela baa mo Ňčon gyu lemlem,
naŇč Babil…Ēn don…Ē…Ē keŇč w…Ēle.‚Äô
44Depampaa dinaa keŇč man n…õ, na de naana…Ē dana kanyaŇč deni dinaa daale keŇč ne di w…Ē adans…õ…õ nyi Wurubuar…õ kyaa be gyaŇč. Be y…õ…õ kanyaŇč deni dinaa kei Ňčgba mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba lese wola Mosesi nyi waa y…õ…õ ke n…õ. 45De naana…Ē ke l…õ…õ kanyaŇč deni dinaa kei lee be kya…Ē gyaŇč n…õ, ba moo ke sila be siamante Gyosua kelii t…õ…õle keŇč Wurubuar…õ be yeli b…Ē…Ē yoo gegi ke balaŇč te …Ē be l…õo ke fa w…Ē n…õ man. KanyaŇč deni dinaa kei doow…Ē bot…Ē…Ē kelii debaŇč keŇč Defidi be kaa di gyoori n…õ. 46Defidi kei nombia gyoo Wurubuar…õ sia, te …Ē yako Wurubuar…õ nyi waa fa e gb…õ…õ na waa baŇč …Ēsom deni dinaa gbagba fa …Ē naana Gyek…Ēpo Wurubuar…õ Ňčon. 47M…Ēna …Ē bu Solom…Ēn kaa baŇča na …Ēsom deni dinaa kenaŇč. 48M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kela kpene kamas…õ n…õ, b…õ…õ kyaa deni keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo nyiŇčmaase ma ke n…õ man ya. Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčolo yako mena nombii kei nyiaa,
49De GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ kpa,
‚ÄėAdido te me gyooro gbeli kyaa,
te t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē y…õna me nae d…õ…õsee.
WoŇč deni din…Ē…Ē yaa …õ ke ma fa maŇč,
yaa aleŇč te maa kyaa f…õ…õfo?
50Na maŇč gbagba y…õ…õna ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča pou yee?‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
51Te Sitifin yako w…Ē bela nyiaa, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ kon…Ē…ĒyuŇčtena, …õ dana kon…Ē…ĒyuŇč Ňčgba balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē ne nyii Wurubuar…õ dei ya n…õ, te …õ ne y…õ…õ deŇčele doŇč Ňčgba …õ kya…Ē n…õ. Nawolo nyi debaŇč kamas…õ na …õ na koro seŇč tia Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon. 52Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčolo kyaaw…Ē keŇč …õ naana…Ē te naase e diyem yee? Ba koo Wurubuar…õ kpilala baŇč b…Ē…Ē wula yako see nyi Yesu Kristo Ňčon …Ē nombia ten…õ…õ n…õ waa k…ĒŇč n…õ, te n…õn…õ…õ …õ lese Yesu ŇčonaŇč fa te ba koo e. 53∆źm…õ…õ y…õna baŇč …õ be nyiŇč Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan lee …Ē kpilala gyaŇč, m…Ēna …õ te di Ňče d…Ē…Ē ya.‚ÄĚ
Be ke fuŇčii Sitifin boya
54DebaŇč keŇč don…Ē…Ē kegyiise baŇč be nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, be sia y…õ…õw…Ē …Ē d…Ē…Ē te ba gyeŇča baŇč nideli. 55M…Ēna Sitifin Ňčon Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon doo …Ē man nideli n…õ k…õ…õ adido na, te …Ē naa Wurubuar…õ gyoorobiiri keŇč, na Yesu m…Ē seŇč…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē. 56Te Sitifin yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ, m…õ…õ naa Ňčgba adido ta toro, na DeniwalaŇč Bu Ňčon seŇč…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ dunoluŇč d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 57∆Ü keyako mena n…õ te kegyiise baŇč moo be nyiŇčmaase t…Ē ba deŇčela, te be fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč. Karatetee te be popou kaa wuuw…Ē …Ē d…Ē…Ē, 58te be w…ĒŇčee e lee don…Ē…Ē keŇč man ke fuŇčii e boya. BaŇč b…Ē…Ē gyu be kaa fuŇčii e boya n…õ, kpee ba kegba wurikyimse Ňčan fa tobaale Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi S…Ē…Ēl n…õ. 59Be kee fuŇčii Sitifin boya Ňčan n…õ, na …Ē ne fane nyi, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo Yesu, l…õ…õ ma woo.‚ÄĚ 60Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē ŇčmiŇčaw…Ē …Ē doona d…Ē…Ē te …Ē fae yeesa nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo Yesu, na mo nombiakum…õ…õ k…õŇča t…Ē w…Ē kom ya.‚ÄĚ ∆Ü kekolosi nombia k…õŇča taŇč p…õ, te …Ē kara weesi. Be k…õ…õ ko Sitifin na S…Ē…Ēl m…Ē seŇč…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē …Ē na do w…Ē kakyeŇč.

english_text:
1-2Saul approved the stoning of Stephen. Some faithful followers of the Lord buried Stephen and mourned very much for him.
Saul Makes Trouble for the Church
At that time the church in Jerusalem suffered terribly. All of the Lord's followers, except the apostles, were scattered everywhere in Judea and Samaria. 3 Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail.
The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
4The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news. 5Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ. 6They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles. 7Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed. 8Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
9For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great. 10Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, ‚ÄúThis man is the power of God called ‚ÄėThe Great Power.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
11For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him. 12But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized. 13Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
14The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John. 15When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit. 16Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus. 17Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
18Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money 19and said to Peter and John, ‚ÄúLet me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.‚ÄĚ
20Peter said to him, ‚ÄúYou and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift! 21You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right. 22Get rid of these evil thoughts and ask God to forgive you. 23I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.‚ÄĚ
24Simon said, ‚ÄúPlease pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.‚ÄĚ
25After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria.
Philip and an Ethiopian Official
26The Lord's angel said to Philip, ‚ÄúGo south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.‚ÄĚ 27So Philip left.
An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship 28and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
29The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot. 30Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, ‚ÄúDo you understand what you are reading?‚ÄĚ
31The official answered, ‚ÄúHow can I understand unless someone helps me?‚ÄĚ He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
32 The man was reading the passage that said,
‚ÄúHe was led like a sheep
on its way to be killed.
He was silent as a lamb
whose wool
is being cut off,
and he did not say
a word.
33He was treated like a nobody
and did not receive
a fair trial.
How can he have children,
if his life
is snatched away?‚ÄĚ
34The official said to Philip, ‚ÄúTell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?‚ÄĚ 35So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
36-37As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, ‚ÄúLook! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?‚ÄĚ 38He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
39After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
40Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_text:

9Don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man na balee Ňčolo kyaa bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake e nyi Sim…Ēn. ∆Ü ne y…õ…õ baalabiiri nombia wola Samariatena, keŇč nyi ba wose na gbirisi w…Ē. ∆Ü ne gyina …Ē wose adido wola …Ē wose nyi …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč dinaa Ňčolo e. 10Mena d…Ē…Ē walaŇč kamas…õ, bia na kegyiise pou ne tei e te b…õ…õ yako nyi, ‚ÄúŇäon y…õna Wurubuar…õ doŇč keŇč b…õ…õ baake nyi doŇč dinaa n…õ.‚ÄĚ 11∆Ü ta mo …Ē baalabiiri nombia k…õŇča wola w…Ē yela ba wose te gbirisi w…Ē kyare, mena d…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ l…õ…õ e di nideli. 12M…Ēna Filipo ke yako w…Ē Wurubuar…õ gyoori na Yesu Kristo wose man nombia Ňčan n…õ be l…õ…õw…Ē di, te be s…Ē…Ē ala na baala pou Wurubuar…õ loŇč. 13Sim…Ēn gbagba m…Ē l…õ…õ nombia Ňčan di te be s…Ē…Ē e Wurubuar…õ loŇč, te …Ē moo …Ē wose do na Filipo nideli. ‚ÄúBa te kparaa e laŇč Ňčgba nam Ňčon bee gyu be kaa ko e n…õ.
