english_text:
Saul Becomes a Follower of the Lord
(Acts 22.6-16; 26.12-18)
1Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest 2and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way. 3When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him. 4He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, ‚ÄúSaul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?‚ÄĚ
5‚ÄúWho are you?‚ÄĚ Saul asked.
‚ÄúI am Jesus,‚ÄĚ the Lord answered. ‚ÄúI am the one you are so cruel to. 6Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.‚ÄĚ
7 The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone. 8Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus, 9and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
10A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, ‚ÄúLord, here I am.‚ÄĚ
11The Lord said to him, ‚ÄúGet up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying, 12and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.‚ÄĚ
13Ananias replied, ‚ÄúLord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem. 14Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.‚ÄĚ
15The Lord said to Ananias, ‚ÄúGo! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me. 16I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.‚ÄĚ
17Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, ‚ÄúSaul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.‚ÄĚ
18 Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized. 19Then he ate and felt much better.
Saul Preaches in Damascus
For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus. 20Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God. 21Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, ‚ÄúIsn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?‚ÄĚ
22Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
23 Later some of them made plans to kill Saul, 24but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him. 25Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket.
Saul in Jerusalem
26When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower. 27Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
28Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord. 29He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him. 30But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
31The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow.
Peter Heals Aeneas
32While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda. 33There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move. 34Peter said to Aeneas, ‚ÄúJesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.‚ÄĚ At once he stood up.
35Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord.
Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
36In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means ‚Äúdeer.‚ÄĚ She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor. 37But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room. 38Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, ‚ÄúPlease come with us as quickly as you can!‚ÄĚ 39At once, Peter went with them.
The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
40After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, ‚ÄúTabitha, get up!‚ÄĚ The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up. 41He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet.
Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death. 42Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord. 43Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_text:
S…Ē…Ēl kel…õ…õ Yesu di
1Weeya ŇčenaŇč pou man na S…Ē…Ēl s…õ…õ …Ē ne Ňčmaase de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu silala baŇč nyi waa ko w…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē gyuu Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia Ňčon gyaŇč, 2ka sola e l…õ…õ …Ē nyiŇčmaa man t…Ēne keŇč gyae ke fa e doŇč na waa mo gyu Damasekuse don…Ē…Ē man kefa Gyudatena …Ēsom den…õ…õ man kegyiise baŇč. Na baa fa e gb…õ…õ waa kyaŇč walaŇč kamas…õ, ala na baala baŇč pou …Ē naa nyi be silana Yesu kawola w…Ēle kei n…õ. Na waa nyiŇč kyaŇč w…Ē k…ĒŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man kaa t…Ē deni. 3∆Ü ke gyu ka benaa Damasekuse don…Ē…Ē n…õ, debokenaŇč man te boalaŇč daale lee adido kaa Ňčmaale pol…õ kilisi e. 4Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē leew…Ē yala te …Ē nyii woya Ňčaale baake e b…Ē…Ēse nyi, ‚ÄúS…Ē…Ēl, S…Ē…Ēl, weera d…Ē…Ē te n ne naase maŇč diyem mena?‚ÄĚ 5Bot…Ē…Ē te S…Ē…Ēl tiran…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyaŇč am…Ēte ŇčonaŇč me GbeŇčgyoo?‚ÄĚ Te Ňčon m…Ē yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMaŇč y…õna Yesu Ňčon n ne naase diyem n…õ. 6M…Ēna koro na n ke gyu don…Ē…Ē keŇč man, na baa yako neŇč kpene keŇč kaboena nyi nya y…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ 7BalaŇč baŇč baŇč na S…Ē…Ēl d…ĒŇč…õ…õ n…õ, ba woya yeuw…Ē lee be gyaŇč keŇč ba te tale gyuusu be n…Ē…Ē ya. 10Na Yesu silale Ňčolo kyaa Damasekuse bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake e nyi Ananiase. ∆Ü nyiiw…Ē de GbeŇčgyoo baake e Ňčgba dos…õ…õ man n…õ nyi, ‚ÄúAnaniase.‚ÄĚ Te …Ē kpaaw…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo maŇč yaa!‚ÄĚ 11Te de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu yako e nyi, ‚ÄúKoro gyu Gyuda d…õ…õ, ke kyaa gb…õ…õnaa keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi gb…õ…õnaa keŇč ka Ten…õ…õ n…õ n…Ē…Ēman, na n ke b…Ē…Ēse S…Ē…Ēl Ňčon …Ē ba lee Tasuse n…õ d…Ē…Ē. ∆Ü kyaa bot…Ē…Ē …Ē ne fane. 12Wurubuar…õ ta lese wola Ňčon S…Ē…Ēl m…Ē Ňčgba dos…õ…õ man n…õ nyi, balee Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Ananiase n…õ, te k…ĒŇč …Ē kaa mo …Ē nyiŇčmaase gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē, na waa bese naa bela.‚ÄĚ 13Ananiase tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo, balaŇč burum te yako maŇč balee kei wose man nombia, na mena keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ n balaŇč baŇč be kyaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ akpanyaŇčnyaŇč. 14∆Ü l…õ…õ t…Ēne keŇč ne fa e doŇč baa lee Gyudatena saese kegyiise gyaŇč k…ĒŇč Damasekuse k…õbo, na …Ē kaa kyaŇč baŇč pou b…õ…õ som neŇč n…õ.‚ÄĚ 15KeŇčte de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu tiran…Ē…Ē fa Ananiase nyiaa, ‚ÄúGyu …Ē gyaŇč, nawolo nyi maŇč lese na e nyi waa som maŇč, na waa yela baŇč pou ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya, na be gyooneŇč na Iseraetena pou ke nyii ma yele. 16MaŇč ne gyae la maa wola e mena keŇč kaboena nyi waa naa diyem nideli lee ma yele d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ
17KeŇčte Ananiase gyuuw…Ē ka gyoo dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man. Te …Ē moo …Ē nyiŇčmaase gyakaa S…Ē…Ēl nyee d…Ē fane nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe d…Ē…Ē S…Ē…Ēl, de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Ňčon …Ē ba lese …Ē wose wola neŇč gb…õ…õnaa man debaŇč keŇč ne k…ĒŇč k…õbo n…õ, Ňčon kpila na maŇč n gyaŇč nyi me kaa fane fa neŇč na n kabese naa. Na Wurubuar…õ kenyiŇč mo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do n man.‚ÄĚ 18DebokenaŇč man te kolo leew…Ē …Ē sia Ňčan man yala Ňčgba kpebii b…Ē…Ēl…õ…õ bia n…õ, te S…Ē…Ēl sia Ňčan gyuusuw…Ē, te …Ē besew…Ē …Ē ne naa bela. ∆Ü korow…Ē seŇč te ba ke s…Ē…Ē e Wurubuar…õ loŇč. 19KenaŇč w…Ēle te be faa e ween…õ…õ. ∆Ü ke di taŇč n…õ, …Ē wose besew…Ē y…õ…õ e doŇč. Te …Ē dii weeya akalans…õ…õ Ňčaale Yesu silala baŇč gyaŇč Damasekuse don…Ē…Ē man bot…Ē…Ē.
S…Ē…Ēl kekolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Damasekuse
20DebokenaŇč man te S…Ē…Ēl fias…õ…õ …Ē ne kolosi Yesu nombia Gyudatena …Ēsom den…õ…õ man nyi, Yesu y…õna Wurubuar…õ bu Ňčon. 21BaŇč pou b…Ē…Ē nyii e n…õ wose gbirisi w…Ē, te be b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyi, ‚ÄúNa balee kei kyaa la Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man …Ē ne naase baŇč b…õ…õ som Yesu n…õ diyem yee? Na keŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē k…ĒŇčaw…Ē k…õbo …Ē kaa kyaŇč w…Ē na waa mo w…Ē gyina saese kegyiise baŇč yee?‚ÄĚ 22Te S…Ē…Ēl besew…Ē nyiŇč doŇč kpu te …Ē kolosiw…Ē lese Ňče man nideli fa Gyudatena baŇč nyi, Yesu y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ. ∆Ü kolosiw…Ē nideli keŇč nyi Gyudatena baŇč be kyaa Damasekuse don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, te tale gyuusu be n…Ē…Ē bela ya. 23Weeya ala Ňčaale w…Ēle n…õ, Gyudatena kegyiise gyaŇčeew…Ē y…õ…õ n…Ē…Ē nyi baa ko S…Ē…Ēl. 24M…Ēna S…Ē…Ēl nyii be nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan b…Ē…Ē bake …Ē nyeeman n…õ. Nelim na ka weese pou na, b…õ…õ deke don…Ē…Ē keŇč anaan…Ē…Ēse Ňčan na baa nyiŇč ko e. 25M…Ēna nelim daale n…õ, …Ē kaseela baŇč moo e do dokyoŇčoloŇč man, te be tisinaa e mo nfansere man gbaa dekyaŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē maa kilisi don…Ē…Ē keŇč n…õ, te …Ē yeuw…Ē laŇč Gyerusal…õm.
S…Ē…Ēl kegyu Gyerusal…õm
26S…Ē…Ēl ka bese gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, …Ē gyaew…Ē nyi waa mo …Ē wose kpu na Yesu silala baŇč. M…Ēna be popou ne yee nawolo nyi na ba te l…õ…õ ta di nyi, Ňčon m…Ē ta bese Yesu silale gbagba ya. 27KeŇčte Banabase moo e gyu Kristo kpilala baŇč gyaŇč. Te …Ē yako w…Ē mena keŇč S…Ē…Ēl be naa de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Damasekuse gb…õ…õnaa man, na mena keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu be kolosi na e, na mena keŇč …Ē be gy…Ē …Ē kon…Ē…Ē mo Yesu yele keyako Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Damasekuse don…Ē…Ē man n…õ. 28Mena d…Ē…Ē S…Ē…Ēl kpuuw…Ē na Yesu silala baŇč. ∆Ü kyaaw…Ē be gyaŇč te be gy…Ē…Ē be kon…Ē…Ē ta na Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu yele man. 29S…Ē…Ēl kolosiw…Ē nideli te Ňčon na Gyudatena baŇč bee kolosi Giriki dei n…õ, l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč nideli. Te be gyae gb…õ…õ keŇč baa mo ko e. 30Mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu silala baŇč ka gyeŇč mena n…õ, ba moo e gyu Kaesaria don…Ē…Ē man te be yela …Ē laŇča Tasuse.
31Mena d…Ē…Ē Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Gyudia na Galelia na Samaria t…õ…õlese man n…õ, wose f…õ…õ w…Ē mena debaŇč kenaŇč man. Be l…õ…õ Yesu nombia Ňčan di nideli, te be t…õ…õw…Ē Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii man, te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon doo w…Ē kakyeŇč te be wukuw…Ē kpu.
Pita keky…Ē Anease na D…Ēkase
32K…ĒŇčaw…Ē debaŇč daale nyi Pita keta …Ē ne kila t…õ…õle keŇč n…õ, …Ē gyuu Lida don…Ē…Ē man …Ē kaa k…õ…õ Kristotena baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ. 33KeŇčte …Ē ke naa balee Ňčolo na …Ē yele y…õna Anease Ňčon …Ē wose pou ba yem n…õ. ∆Ü doow…Ē dofoŇčoŇč d…Ē…Ē kulutooneŇč gyanaara keŇč …Ē b…õ…õ tale koro ya. 34Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita baake e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAnease k…õ…õ, Yesu Kristo te ky…Ē neŇč n kawee. Koro seŇč na n ke b…Ē n dofoŇčoŇč!‚ÄĚ DebokenaŇč man te Anease korow…Ē seŇč. 35BalaŇč baŇč pou be kyaa Lida na Sar…Ēn kena Anease d…ĒŇč…õ…õ n…õ, be l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nombia Ňčan di te ba bese …Ē silala.
36Na alo Ňčolo m…Ē kyaa Gyopa keŇč Ňčon m…Ē y…õ…õ Yesu silale e te …Ē yele y…õna Tabita. Giriki dei man na baa baake e nyi D…Ēkase keŇč nawolo nyi Ňčaa. DebaŇč kamas…õ na alo kei ne y…õ…õ deeli fa balaŇč, te …Ē ne ky…Ē ayematena m…Ē nideli. 37DebaŇč kenaŇč man n…õ, …Ē gyoo kawee te …Ē yeŇčaw…Ē. Te be s…Ē…Ē e teeli abansoro deni daale man. 38Lida na Gyopa don…Ē…Ēse taŇč lem na d…ĒŇča ya. Te kaseela baŇč be kyaa Gyopa kenyii nyi Pita kyaa Lida n…õ, be kpila tewul…õ…õ bala gyu …Ē gyaŇč nyi, ‚ÄúPita, waaseda, y…õ…õ bileŇč na n kek…ĒŇč de gyaŇč.‚ÄĚ
39Mena d…Ē…Ē Pita korow…Ē sila w…Ē. Be ka gyoo bot…Ē…Ē n…õ te ba moo e gyu deni adidote keŇč man. Te liala baŇč pou kaa yilaaw…Ē kilisi Pita bee wii, na b…õ…õ mo kegbase na ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan Tabita be Ňčare fa w…Ē p…õte …Ē ba yem n…õ wola e. 40KeŇčte Pita lese be popou lee deni keŇč man, te …Ē ŇčmiŇčaw…Ē …Ē doona d…Ē…Ē te …Ē fanew…Ē. Te …Ē besenaw…Ē k…õ…õ woo Ňčon te …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúTabita, koro!‚ÄĚ Te woo Ňčon gyuusu …Ē sia, te …Ē kena Pita n…õ, …Ē korow…Ē kyaa. 41KeŇčte Pita kyaŇča e …Ē nyiŇčmaa man ky…Ē e te …Ē korow…Ē seŇč. Te …Ē baake Yesu silala baŇč na liala baŇč, te …Ē moo D…Ēkase do w…Ē be nyiŇčmaa man na …Ē delena. 42Nombia k…õŇča yaaseew…Ē kila Gyopa keŇč pou, te balaŇč burum kaa l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di. 43KenaŇč w…Ēle na Pita dii weeya burum Gyopa don…Ē…Ē man bot…Ē…Ē. Na …Ē kyae Sim…Ēn Ňčon …Ē ne y…õ…õ wonembia t…Ēna wose man tom n…õ gyaŇč.

english_text:
Peter and Cornelius
1In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called ‚ÄúThe Italian Unit.‚ÄĚ 2Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
3One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name. 4Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, ‚ÄúWhat is this all about?‚ÄĚ
The angel answered, ‚ÄúGod has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor. 5Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter. 6He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.‚ÄĚ 7After saying this, the angel left.
Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God. 8He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
9 The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray 10and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision. 11He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners. 12In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds. 13A voice said to him, ‚ÄúPeter, get up! Kill these and eat them.‚ÄĚ
14 But Peter said, ‚ÄúLord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.‚ÄĚ
15The voice spoke to him again, ‚ÄúWhen God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.‚ÄĚ
16This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
17Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house 18and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
19While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, ‚ÄúThree men are here looking for you. 20Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.‚ÄĚ
21Peter went down and said to the men, ‚ÄúI am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?‚ÄĚ
22They answered, ‚ÄúCaptain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.‚ÄĚ 23Peter invited them to spend the night.
The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius. 24The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
25When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him. 26But Peter took hold of him and said, ‚ÄúStand up! I am nothing more than a human.‚ÄĚ
27As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there, 28and Peter said to them, ‚ÄúYou know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit. 29I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.‚ÄĚ
30Cornelius answered:
Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me. 31He said, ‚ÄúCornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor. 32Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.‚ÄĚ
33I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
34 Peter then said:
Now I am certain that God treats all people alike. 35God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from. 36This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
37You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized. 38God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil. 39We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem.
Jesus was put to death on a cross. 40But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen. 41Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
42God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead. 43Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
44While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening. 45Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles. 46Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God.
Peter said, 47‚ÄúThese Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.‚ÄĚ 48Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_text:
Pita kekolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia fa K…Ēnelio
1Balee Ňčolo kyaaw…Ē Kaesaria don…Ē…Ē man na …Ē yele y…õna K…Ēnelio. ∆Ü y…õ…õ Itali t…õ…õle yoon…Ē…Ētena dikpii siamante e. Na yoon…Ē…Ētena s…ĒŇčonoŇč doo la …Ē t…õ…õ. 2∆Ü y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ba mo …Ē wose fa Wurubuar…õ, te Ňčon na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou ne yee Wurubuar…õ nideli. ∆Ü dana w…Ēe fa Gyudatena ayematena te …Ē ne fane fa Wurubuar…õ debaŇč kamas…õ. 3Wee daale balin…Ē…Ē weese debaŇč atoorote n…õ, …Ē naa kolo na …Ē sia Ňčgba dos…õ…õ man n…õ. ∆Ü naa Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo ta lee adido k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč te …Ē baake e nyi, ‚ÄúK…Ēnelio.‚ÄĚ 4Te K…Ēnelio k…õ…õ e yididi gyakoloŇč man te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse e nyi, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo weera?‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ te l…õ…õ n kefane. ∆Ü te naa mena keŇč n ne y…õ…õ w…Ēe fa ayematena baŇč, te Ňče ta gyoo …Ē sia. 5Mena d…Ē…Ē kpila balaŇč gyu Gyopa don…Ē…Ē keŇč man, be kaa baake walaŇč Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Sim…Ēn yaa Pita n…õ. 6∆Ü kyaa Sim…Ēn Ňčon …Ē ne y…õ…õ wonembia t…Ēna wose man tom n…õ d…õ…õ. ∆Ü d…õ…õ kyaa …õpo n…Ē…Ēman.‚ÄĚ 7Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon kek…ĒŇč kaa kolosi fa e taŇč te …Ē be lam n…õ, K…Ēnelio baake …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč man tewul…õ…õ bala, na yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč bee gyee e n…õ man Ňčolo, Ňčon …Ē benaa na e te …Ē ne yee Wurubuar…õ n…õ. 8Te …Ē yako w…Ē kpene keŇč pou be k…ĒŇč n…õ, te …Ē kpila w…Ē Gyopa don…Ē…Ē man.
9T…õ…õ kena weeseteŇčteŇč keŇč be d…ĒŇč bee gyuu te be ka benaa Gyopa don…Ē…Ē keŇč n…õ, debaŇč kenaŇč te Pita m…Ē gyeŇčaw…Ē laŇč abansoro d…Ē…Ē …Ē kaa fane. 10Tanam kyaŇča e te …Ē gyae kolo waa di, m…Ēna debaŇč keŇč b…õ…õ saa ween…õ…õ Ňčan n…õ, …Ē naa kolo na …Ē sia Ňčgba dos…õ…õ man n…õ. 11∆Ü naaw…Ē Ňčgba adido ba toro na, te …Ē naa kolo Ňčgba kanyaŇč dinaa daale n…õ, na be dana ke n…Ē…Ēsebia anaara Ňčan man lee adido, te be tisinaa ke kaa see t…õ…õle man. 12Na wonembia kpookpoo doo ke man. BaŇč be dana naw…Ē…Ē anaara, na baŇč bee tuu be t…Ē…Ēse d…Ē…Ē, na baŇč bee furufu n…õ doo ke man. 13KeŇčte …Ē nyii n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčaale nyi, ‚ÄúPita, koro na n ka ko di.‚ÄĚ 14Te Pita yakow…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúKoa, dee gyu lem me GbeŇčgyoo, nawolo nyi me te dii ta kolo kum yaa kpene keŇč d…õ…õ k…Ēle n…õ see ya.‚ÄĚ 15Te n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan besew…Ē yako e bela nyi, ‚ÄúKpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ be l…õ…õ do nyi ke dei n…õ, na baake ke nyi kolo anek…ĒlaŇč bela ya.‚ÄĚ 16Kei y…õ…õw…Ē mena betabotooro. Te debokenaŇč man ba moo kanyaŇč keŇč laŇč adido bela.
17Pita kekyaa …Ē na gyueŇč nombia Ňčan …Ē be naa n…õ wose man n…õ, na K…Ēnelio kpilala baŇč m…Ē te b…Ē…Ēse gyu kena dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man Sim…Ēn kyaa, te be gyuuw…Ē ka seŇč ka denan…Ē…Ē man. 18KeŇčte be baake Ňčolo dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúK…õbo te balee Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Sim…Ēn Pita n…õ kyaa?‚ÄĚ 19Na Pita s…õ…õ …Ē na gyueŇč nombia Ňčan …Ē be naa n…õ wose man, te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúSim…Ēn, nyii k…õbo, tewul…õ…õ batooro baale ne gyae neŇč. 20Mena d…Ē…Ē desina n wose na n ke tisi gyu be gyaŇč. Sila w…Ē ta, na l…õ…õ n wose aŇčmaareŇč ya nawolo nyi maŇč kpila na w…Ē.‚ÄĚ 21Mena d…Ē…Ē Pita tisiw…Ē gyu balaŇč baŇč gyaŇč te …Ē ke yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMaŇč y…õna walaŇč Ňčon …õ ne k…õo n…õ. ∆ź keta gyakoloŇč?‚ÄĚ 22KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúDe gbeŇčgyoo yoon…Ē…Ētena dikpii siamante Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Konelio n…õ kpila na daa n gyaŇč. ∆Ü y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne yee Wurubuar…õ, te Ňčon na e m…Ē nsana dei, te Gyudatena pou ne bu e. Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo k…ĒŇčala kaa yako e nyi, waa kpila kaa mo neŇč k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč, na waa nyii kpene keŇč n dana fa e.‚ÄĚ 23Te Pita moo balaŇč baŇč gyu …Ē d…õ…õ, te …Ē yela be doow…Ē …Ē gyaŇč.
T…õ…õ kena keŇč n…õ, Pita desina …Ē wose te Ňčon na w…Ē pou laŇčaw…Ē. ∆Ü tebia Kristotena baale m…Ē leew…Ē Gyopa sila w…Ē gyu. 24T…õ…õ kena te ba gyoo Kaesaria, na K…Ēnelio ne k…õ…õ be gb…õ…õ mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yilaa …Ē loŇčtona na …Ē gyoona kpaakpaa nyibaŇč. 25Pita ka gyoo dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man n…õ, K…Ēnelio kaa gyaŇčeew…Ē na e te …Ē tisiw…Ē buŇč …Ē naw…Ē…Ēt…õ…õ obuo man. 26KeŇčte Pita yako e nyi ‚ÄúKoro, na y…õ…õ mena ya. Nawolo nyi maŇč m…Ē y…õ…õ deniwalaŇč e Ňčgba nyaŇč n…õ.‚ÄĚ 27Te Ňčon na Pita moo afa key…Ē…Ē mo gyoo dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man, te Pita ke naa balaŇč burum baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē yilaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ. 28Te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyeŇč nideli nyi naf…Ē daa Gyudatena …Ēsom k…õ…õ wolo man na, gb…õ…õ be doo keŇč nyi Gyuda baale ka mo …Ē wose do na walaŇč Ňčon …Ē te y…õ…õ Gyuda baale e ya, yaa waa gyu …Ē gyaŇč ya. M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ ta lese wola maŇč nyi, me na k…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčolo nyi …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč yakaate e ya. 29Lee mena d…Ē…Ē n ke kpila baake maŇč na, ma te b…õ…õ nyi m…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč ya. M…õ…õ gyae maa nyii kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē n be kpili baake maŇč.‚ÄĚ 30Bot…Ē…Ē te K…Ēnelio tiran…Ē…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúWeeya anaara yaa Ňčgba me kyaa me d…õ…õ m…õ…õ fane balin…Ē…Ē weese Ňčgba debaŇč atoorote man n…õ, debokenaŇč man te baale Ňčolo kaa b…õ…õw…Ē lee me d…Ē…Ē. Na …Ē doona ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan ne Ňčalakee n…õ, te …Ē kaa seŇčaw…Ē me siaman. 31Te …Ē yako maŇč nyi, ‚ÄėK…Ēnelio, Wurubuar…õ te nyii n kefane te …Ē ne t…Ē…Ēse n w…Ēe keŇč n dana fa ayematena n…õ, mena d…Ē…Ē 32kpila gyu Gyopa don…Ē…Ē man ke baake Sim…Ēn Pita, …Ē kyaa Sim…Ēn Ňčon …Ē ne y…õ…õ wonembia t…Ēna wose man tom te …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč kyaa …õpo n…Ē…Ēman n…õ gyaŇč.‚Äô 33Mena d…Ē…Ē te me kpila balaŇč n gyaŇč debokenaŇč man. N y…õ…õ ke nideli Ňčgba n be k…ĒŇč n…õ. N…õn…õ…õ de popou te kaa gyaŇčee k…õbo Wurubuar…õ siaman d…õ…õ daa neŇč, na dee nyii n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ kpa nya yako daa n…õ.‚ÄĚ
Pita kekolosi
34Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita gyinaa …Ē n…Ē…Ēwoya nyi, ‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē ma te naa ampaŇč nyi balaŇč pou y…õ…õ ky…õ…õky…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman, te …Ē b…õ…õ k…õ…õ Ňčolo siaman ya. 35M…Ēna walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne yee e te …Ē ne y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ na, …Ē ne l…õ…õ e do. ∆Ü be famen…õ na …Ētenate dalee ya. 36∆źm…õ…õ gbagba gyeŇč n…Ē…Ēwoya kpaakpaa Ňčan Wurubuar…õ ba mo fa Iseraetena n…õ. ∆Ü yako w…Ē nyi baa sila Kristo Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč kamas…õ GbeŇčgyoo e n…õ, na baŇč na Ňčon Wurubuar…õ nsana ke de na baa nyiŇč wosef…õ…õreŇč. 37∆ź gyeŇč nombii dinaa keŇč be k…ĒŇč Iserae t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē, keŇč be fias…õ…õ lee Galelia t…õ…õle man debaŇč keŇč Gy…Ēn be kolosi lee Wurubuar…õ loŇčkes…Ē keŇč wose man nombia Ňčan w…Ēle n…õ. 38∆ź gyeŇč mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo …Ē feliŇč na …Ē doŇč keŇč do Yesu Nasar…õte baale Ňčon man, mena d…Ē…Ē Yesu t…õ…õw…Ē y…õ…õ deeli te …Ē ky…Ē…Ē baŇč pou …Ēb…Ēnsam be di be d…Ē…Ē n…õ kaweese. ∆Ü y…õ…õ k…õŇča pou nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ kpu…õ na e. 39Te dee di adans…õ…õ lee kpene kamas…õ keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ Gyudatena t…õ…õle man na Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ. Be gy…Ē…Ē e sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē ko. 40M…Ēna weeya atoorote keŇč man n…õ, Wurubuar…õ gyuusu e lee yeŇč man, te …Ē yela …Ē lese …Ē wose wola balaŇč. 41Na balaŇč pou naa na e ya, m…Ēna daa baŇč Wurubuar…õ be wulaa lese nyi d…õ…õ y…õ…õ wa adans…õ…õtena n…õ, daa daageŇč naa na e. Wurubuar…õ ke gyuusu e lee yeŇč man m…Ē n…õ, daa na e diiw…Ē te de ny…Ē…Ēw…Ē. 42Te …Ē yako daa nyi dee kolosi …Ē nombia fa balaŇč na dee di adans…õ…õ lee …Ē wose man nyi, Ňčon y…õna walaŇč Ňčon Wurubuar…õ ba lese nyi, waa di balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē yekee na baŇč be kyaa na be sia n…õ pou nombia. 43Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra baŇč pou kolosiw…Ē lee …Ē wose man nyi, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon waa l…õ…õ e di na, Wurubuar…õ waa mo …Ē nombiakum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ e lee Yesu yele man.‚ÄĚ
Wurubuar…õ ta mo …Ē feliŇč do balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ man
44Pita kegyakaa …Ē ne kolosi n…õ, te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon kaa gyoo baŇč pou bee tei e n…õ man. 45Te Gyudatena baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di te b…Ē…Ē sila Pita k…ĒŇč n…õ, wose gbirisi w…Ē Ňčgba Wurubuar…õ ba mo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya m…Ē man n…õ. 46Nawolo nyi be nyiiw…Ē bee kolosi deise kpookpoo, na b…õ…õ lese Wurubuar…õ yele. KeŇčte Pita yako w…Ē nyiaa, 47‚ÄúWurubuar…õ ta mo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do balaŇč k…õw…Ē m…Ē man, Ňčgba mena keŇč keŇč …Ē ba mo do de man n…õ. Ňäolo waa tale t…Ē w…Ē gb…õ…õ nyi be na s…Ē w…Ē Wurubuar…õ loŇč yee?‚ÄĚ 48Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yela be s…Ē…Ē w…Ē Wurubuar…õ loŇč lee Yesu Kristo yele man. Te ba sola e nyi, waa kyaa be gyaŇč weeya ala.

english_text:
Peter Reports to the Church in Jerusalem
1The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message. 2So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and 3they said, ‚ÄúYou stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!‚ÄĚ
4Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
5I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me. 6When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds. 7I heard a voice saying to me, ‚ÄúPeter, get up! Kill these and eat them.‚ÄĚ
8But I said, ‚ÄúLord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.‚ÄĚ
9The voice from heaven spoke to me again, ‚ÄúWhen God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.‚ÄĚ 10This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
11Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying. 12The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man 13who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter. 14Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
15After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning. 16 I remembered that the Lord had said, ‚ÄúJohn baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.‚ÄĚ 17God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
18When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, ‚ÄúGod has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!‚ÄĚ
The Church in Antioch
19 Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
20Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus. 21The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him. 22News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
23When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts. 24Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
25Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul. 26He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
27During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch. 28 One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor. 29The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea. 30So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.

11DebaŇč kenaŇč na baala batooro baŇč Konelio be kpili lee Kaesaria k…ĒŇč me gyaŇč n…õ, te kaa sem dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man me kyaa n…õ. 12Te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč yako maŇč nyi, me na l…õ…õ ma wose aŇčmaareŇč ya, maa sila w…Ē gyu. Me tebia looro k…õw…Ē sila maŇč lee Gyopa gyu Kaesaria te de popou gyuuw…Ē ka gyoo Konelio d…õ…õ. 13Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē yako daa mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ kpilale be kaa b…õ…õ lee …Ē d…Ē…Ē …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč man, te …Ē yako e nyi kpila gyu Gyopa ke baake balee Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Sim…Ēn Pita n…õ. 14Waa k…ĒŇč kaa yako Ňčon nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan gyae ke yela …õm…õ…õ na n dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou ke nyiŇč nyeekel…õ…õ. 15Me ke gyina me n…Ē…Ē mee kolosi p…õ, te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa gyoo be man, Ňčgba mena keŇč ba gyoo de man kewalaŇč keŇč n…õ. 16Bot…Ē…Ē te me t…Ē…Ēse de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu n…Ē…Ēwoya Ňčan …Ē be yako nyi, ‚ÄėLoŇč te Gy…Ēn moow…Ē s…Ē balaŇč Wurubuar…õ loŇč, m…Ēna Wurubuar…õ waa mo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon bo s…Ē Ňčon Wurubuar…õ loŇč.‚Äô 17Mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo …Ē feliŇč do de man debaŇč keŇč man d…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo di n…õ, mena ke yaa …Ē moow…Ē do baŇč m…Ē man. Kristotena dikpii keŇč kyaa Anti…Ēke n…õ
19Be ka ko Sitifin w…Ēle n…õ, baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Kristo di n…õ yaaseew…Ē lee mena keŇč b…õ…õ naase w…Ē diyem d…Ē…Ē. Baale gyuuw…Ē naŇč Tunisia na Saipor…Ēse t…õ…õle man, na Anti…Ēke don…Ē…Ē man b…õ…õ yako Yesu nombia Ňčan fa Gyudatena baageŇč. 20M…Ēna baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Kristo di n…õ man baale baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee Saipor…Ēse na Kirene n…õ, gyuu Anti…Ēke ke yako de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu wose man nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan fa balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ. 21De GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ seŇčaw…Ē be w…Ēle te balaŇč burum l…õ…õ nombia Ňčan di te be ky…õ…õkeew…Ē lee be gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man, te be k…ĒŇčaw…Ē de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu gyaŇč.
22DebaŇč keŇč Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Gyerusal…õm be nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, be kpila Banabase gyu Anti…Ēke. 23∆Ü ke gyu ka gyoo bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē kena mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele w…Ē n…õ, y…õ…õ e gyoŇč te …Ē doo be popou kakyeŇč nyi baa mo be kon…Ē…Ē pou sila de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu. 24Banabase y…õ…õ walaŇč kpaakpaate Ňčon …Ē be l…õ…õ Kristo nombia Ňčan di, te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč doo …Ē man nideli. De GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ moo balaŇč burum kaa kpu na Kristotena dikpii keŇč.
25Te Banabase gyuu Tasuse …Ē kaa k…õo S…Ē…Ēl. 26∆Ü kena e n…õ …Ē moo e k…ĒŇč Anti…Ēke. Kulutoo dibiliŇč pou na Banabase na S…Ē…Ēl ne kyaa gyaŇčee Yesu kaseela dikpii dinaa keŇč na b…õ…õ wola w…Ē. Anti…Ēke bot…Ē…Ē te be taŇčgb…õ…õw…Ē baake Yesu silala baŇč nyi Kristotena.
27Weeya ŇčenaŇč man te Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra baale lee Gyerusal…õm gyu Anti…Ēke. 28KeŇčte be man Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e Agabuse n…õ korow…Ē seŇč te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč t…õ…õw…Ē …Ē d…Ē…Ē yako nombia see nyiaa, tanam dinaa daale gyae kek…ĒŇč t…õ…õle keŇč pou d…Ē…Ē. (Tanam dinaa kei k…ĒŇčaw…Ē mena ampaŇč debaŇč keŇč gyoo dinaa Klaudiuse ne di gyoori n…õ.) 29Mena d…Ē…Ē kaseela baŇč y…õ…õ ba agyueŇč nyi be man walaŇč kamas…õ waa fa kolo …Ē ke talebii man, na baa mo yelee be tebia Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Gyudia t…õ…õle man n…õ. 30Te ampaŇč be y…õ…õw…Ē mena. Ba moo ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan b…Ē…Ē faa n…õ do Banabase na S…Ē…Ēl nyiŇčmaa man, te ba moow…Ē gyina kegyiise baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ.

english_text:
Herod Causes Trouble for the Church
1At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church. 2He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John. 3 When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread. 4He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
5While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him.
Peter Is Rescued
6The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail. 7Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, ‚ÄúQuick! Get up!‚ÄĚ
The chains fell off his hands, 8and the angel said, ‚ÄúGet dressed and put on your sandals.‚ÄĚ Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, ‚ÄúNow put on your coat and follow me.‚ÄĚ 9Peter left with the angel, but he thought everything was only a dream. 10They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
11Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, ‚ÄúI am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.‚ÄĚ 12Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
13Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer. 14When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
15‚ÄúYou are crazy!‚ÄĚ everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, ‚ÄúIt must be his angel.‚ÄĚ 16But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
17Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, ‚ÄúTell James and the others what has happened.‚ÄĚ After that, he left and went somewhere else.
18The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter. 19Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while.
Herod Dies
20 Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod, 21and a day was set for them to meet with him.
Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech. 22The people shouted, ‚ÄúYou speak more like a god than a man!‚ÄĚ 23At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
24God's message kept spreading. 25And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_text:

1DebaŇč kenaŇč man te gyoo Herode fias…õ…õ …Ē ne naase Kristotena baŇč baale diyem. 2∆Ü yela ba moo sila ko Gy…Ēn …Ē kegyia Gyemisi. 3∆Ü kena nyi kpene keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ n…õ dana Gyudatena baŇč gyoŇč n…õ, te …Ē yela be kyaŇča Pita m…Ē. Na ke y…õ…õ Gyudatena wee dinaa keŇč bee di bodobodo keŇč tee keŇč ne yeli bodobodo na koro n…õ be doo ke man ya n…õ. 4∆Ü ke kyaŇč Pita do deni man w…Ēle n…õ, …Ē moo e do yoon…Ē…Ētena akpeŇč anaara nyiŇčmaa man nyi baa gyee e. Yoon…Ē…Ētena banaara doo la dekpeŇč kamas…õ man. Herode y…õ…õ ke nyi akpaa be di ba nyeekel…õ…õwee dinaa keŇč taŇč n…õ, na waa di …Ē nombia balaŇč nsana. 5Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yela Pita doow…Ē deni man bot…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna Kristotena dikpii keŇč ne fane nideli fa Wurubuar…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē.
Wurubuar…õ kpilale kalese Pita lee deni man
6Nelim keŇč p…õ t…õ…õ kena, na gyoo Herode ka lese Pita lee deni man na baa di …Ē nombia n…õ, na …Ē doo yoon…Ē…Ētena bala nsana keŇč b…Ē…Ē do e agbaraagbaraase ala. DebaŇč kenaŇč na yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč bee gyee denan…Ē…Ē keŇč m…Ē seŇč…õ…õ be daseŇčae man. 7Karatetee dokoloŇč na te de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo b…õ…õw…Ē lee bot…Ē…Ē te deni keŇč man ŇčmaŇčeew…Ē. Te …Ē kyaŇča Pita …Ē degbaŇčgbaŇč d…Ē…Ē wosi e te …Ē gyuusu e yako e nyi, ‚ÄúKoro bileŇč.‚ÄĚ DebokenaŇč man te agbaraagbaraase Ňčan leew…Ē …Ē nyiŇčmaase man yala. 8Bot…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon yako e nyiaa, ‚ÄúMo nya ab…Ē…Ē do na n ka do nyaŇč nkyokotase.‚ÄĚ Te Pita m…Ē y…õ…õw…Ē mena te kpilale Ňčon yako e bela nyi, mo n kegba wurikyim keŇč do na n ke sila maŇč. 9Te Pita sila e lee deb…Ē…Ē man. M…Ēna …Ē ta gyem nyi kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon ne y…õ…õ n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč ya. Nawolo nyi Ňčon gyaŇč na, y…õ…õ e bo Ňčgba dos…õ…õ n…õ.
10Be t…õ…õw…Ē gbaa balaŇč gyaŇčgba nyibaŇč baŇč bee gyee bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, na baŇč be gyakaa be d…Ē…Ē n…õ, kaboena be ke lii nyeele denan…Ē…Ē keŇč na mo lee don…Ē…Ē keŇč man n…õ gyaŇč. Be kelii bot…Ē…Ē na, te nyeele denan…Ē…Ē keŇč torow…Ē fa w…Ē ka wose d…Ē…Ē, te ba leew…Ē laŇč. Ba ke ta bee gyuu na, te kpilale Ňčon Ňčaalaaw…Ē yela Pita karatetee dokoloŇč. 11Bot…Ē…Ē te Pita sia ŇčmaŇčee e te …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ, me ta gyem nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ kpila na …Ē kpilale nyi …Ē kaa lese maŇč lee gyoo Herode nyiŇčmaa man, na Ňčan pou naf…Ē Gyudatena be bake see nyi baa y…õ…õ maŇč n…õ.‚ÄĚ
12Pita kena mena nombia k…õŇča n…õ, te …Ē gyuu Gy…Ēn Maake …Ē naa M…õ…õre d…õ…õ. DebaŇč kenaŇč na balaŇč burum te yilaa bot…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ fane. 13Pita ke gy…Ē be disim keŇč te alebu Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Roda n…õ, gyuuw…Ē ke k…õ…õ nyi am…Ēte ne gy…Ē…Ē laa. 14∆Ü ke gyu kenyii woya Ňčan te …Ē ba gyem nyi Pita woya ŇčenaŇč n…õ, y…õ…õ e gyoŇč nideli te …Ē woleew…Ē nyi waa toro e denan…Ē…Ē keŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yeuw…Ē keyako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúPita te k…ĒŇč …Ē seŇč…õ…õ denan…Ē…Ē man!‚ÄĚ 15Te be b…Ē…Ēse e nyiaa, ‚ÄúYaa n na gyoo diteŇč bo?‚ÄĚ M…Ēna …Ē yase …Ē sia yako w…Ē nyi y…õ…õ ampaŇč. KeŇčte be yakow…Ē nyi gyae ke y…õ…õ …Ē woo bo. 16Na Pita gyakaa bo …Ē ne gy…Ē denan…Ē…Ē keŇč. N…õn…õ…õ be ka toro denan…Ē…Ē keŇč na Pita seŇč…õ…õ laa, mena d…Ē…Ē ba wose gbirisi w…Ē. 17Te Pita gyina …Ē nyiŇčmaa adido mo yako w…Ē nyi baa wu be n…Ē…Ē. Te …Ē yako w…Ē mena keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ ba lese e lee deni keŇč man. Te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆ź yako Gyemisi na Kristotena akaŇč baŇč mena nombia k…õŇča.‚ÄĚ Te …Ē korow…Ē laŇč yenaŇč w…Ēle.
18T…õ…õ kena keŇč n…õ, te aŇčmaareŇč dinaa daale gyoow…Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč nsana. Be b…Ē…Ēse d…ĒŇča nyi, ‚ÄúAleŇč yaa Pita moow…Ē baa?‚ÄĚ 19Gyoo Herode yela be k…õo Pita nideli, m…Ēna ba te naa e ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē baake yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyee e n…õ, nyii be n…Ē…Ēman nombia, te …Ē yela ba koo w…Ē pou. KenaŇč w…Ēle n…õ, gyoo Herode leew…Ē Gyudia t…õ…õle man gyu kekyaa Kaesaria don…Ē…Ē man kyomii.
Gyoo Herode yeŇč
20Gyoo Herode gyeŇča baŇč Taetena na Sid…Ēntena d…Ē…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč benaŇč y…õ…õ n…Ē…Ē te be gyuuw…Ē kena e. Be taŇčgb…õ…õw…Ē mo nombia ŇčenaŇč do Bilasituse n…Ē…Ēman nyi waa doo e teree fa w…Ē, nawolo nyi don…Ē…Ēse ala ŇčenaŇč pou ne nyiŇč be ween…õ…õ bo lee gyoo ŇčonaŇč t…õ…õle man. Bilasituse y…õna gyoo Ňčon dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč pou kegyia. 21Wee keŇč b…Ē…Ē see kelii n…õ, te gyoo Herode moo …Ē gyooro ab…Ē…Ē do, te …Ē gyuuw…Ē ka gyeŇč gyakaa …Ē gyooro gbeli keŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē kolosiw…Ē fa balaŇč baŇč pou. 22Te balaŇč baŇč pou fae yeesa yako nyi, ‚ÄúKei d…Ē…Ē te y…õ…õ deniwalaŇč ne kolosi la ya, y…õ…õ bane Ňčolo e.‚ÄĚ 23DebokenaŇč man te de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ kpilale k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa gy…Ē e do kawee, nawolo nyi …Ē ta lese Wurubuar…õ yele ya. Te gyoo Ňčon wose pou gyoo feel…õ…õ te …Ē yeŇčaw…Ē.
24K…õŇča pou man na, Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan s…õ…õ Ňč…õ…õ yaasee na balaŇč burum m…Ē ne l…õ…õ Ňča di. 25DebaŇč keŇč Banabase na S…Ē…Ēl be taŇč ba tom keŇč n…õ, ba besew…Ē lee Gyerusal…õm gyu Anti…Ēke. Be kee gyu keŇč n…õ, te ba moo Gy…Ēn Maake sila ba wose.

english_text:
Barnabas and Saul Are Chosen and Sent
1The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul. 2While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, ‚ÄúAppoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.‚ÄĚ 3Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way.
Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
4After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus. 5They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
6Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet. 7He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul. 8But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
9Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas 10and said, ‚ÄúYou son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord? 11The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.‚ÄĚ
Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand. 12When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord.
Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
13Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem. 14The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
15After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, ‚ÄúFriends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.‚ÄĚ
16Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said:
People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen! 17 The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out, 18 and for about 40 years he took care of them in the desert. 19 He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people. 20 All this happened in about 450 years.
Then God gave our people judges until the time of the prophet Samuel, 21 but the people demanded a king. So for 40 years God gave them King Saul, the son of Kish from the tribe of Benjamin. 22 Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, ‚ÄúDavid the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.‚ÄĚ
23God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus. 24 But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized. 25 Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, ‚ÄúWho do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.‚ÄĚ
26Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone. 27The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
28-29 They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed.
After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb. 30But God raised him from death! 31 Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
32God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news 33 that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus,
‚ÄúYou are my son because today
I have become your Father.‚ÄĚ
34 God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said,
‚ÄúI will make to you
the same holy promises
that I made to David.‚ÄĚ
35 And in another psalm it says, ‚ÄúGod will never let the body of his Holy One decay.‚ÄĚ
36When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed. 37But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
38My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins. 39But everyone who has faith in Jesus is set free. 40Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
41 ‚ÄúLook, you people
who make fun of God!
Be amazed
and disappear.
I will do something today
that you won't believe,
even if someone
tells you about it!‚ÄĚ
42As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath. 43After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
44The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord. 45When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
46But Paul and Barnabas bravely said:
We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles. 47 The Lord has given us this command,
‚ÄúI have placed you here
as a light
for the Gentiles.
You are to take
the saving power of God
to people everywhere on earth.‚ÄĚ
48This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
49The message about the Lord spread all over this region. 50But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
51 Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
52But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_text:
Be kekpila Banabase na S…Ē…Ēl
1Kristotena dikpii keŇč ke kyaa Anti…Ēke don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, na be dana Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra na wolala be man. BaŇč y…õna Banabase na Sime…Ēn Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi walaŇč bibiini n…õ na Lusiuse Ňčon …Ē ba lee Kirene n…õ, na S…Ē…Ēl na Manayen Ňčon b…Ē…Ē k…õ…õle Ňčon na gyoo Herode Antipase bom n…õ. Gyoo Herode Antipase y…õna walaŇč Ňčon …Ē seŇč…õ…õ gyoo dinaa naw…Ē…Ē man Galelia t…õ…õle man. 2Wee daale be k…õ…õ som Wurubuar…õ n…õ, be bake be n…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ fane te Wurubuar…õ feliŇč yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź lese Banabase na S…Ē…Ēl fa maŇč, na baa y…õ…õ tom keŇč d…Ē…Ē m…Ē…Ē baake w…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ 3Mena d…Ē…Ē ba kebake be n…Ē…Ē fane taŇč n…õ, ba moo be nyiŇčmaase gyakaa be d…Ē…Ē fane fa w…Ē te be l…Ē…Ē w…Ē gb…õ…õ.
4Wurubuar…õ feliŇč ke kpila Banabase na S…Ē…Ēl n…õ, be gyuu Selusia. Bot…Ē…Ē te ba moo degbele dinaa mo loŇč d…Ē…Ē gyu Saipor…Ēse t…õ…õle keŇč loŇč be kilisi ke n…õ man. 5Be kelii Salamise n…õ be yako Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Gyudatena …Ēsom den…õ…õ man. Na Gy…Ēn Maake m…Ē kpu…õ na w…Ē …Ē ne ky…Ē w…Ē.
6Be kila t…õ…õle kenaŇč d…Ē…Ē pou gbaa ke gyu ke lii Pafose don…Ē…Ē man. Bot…Ē…Ē te ba ke naa ŇčmeeseŇčte Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Baa Yesu n…õ. ∆Ü y…õ…õ Gyuda baale e te …Ē moo …Ē wose y…õ…õ Ňčgba Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre n…õ. 7Na …Ē y…õ…õ gyoo Ňčon …Ē seŇč…õ…õ gyoo dinaa naw…Ē…Ē man t…õ…õle kenaŇč man n…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē e. B…õ…õ baake e nyi Segiuse P…Ēluse. english_text:
Paul and Barnabas in Iconium
1Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord. 2But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
3Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
4The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles. 5Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
6-7But when the two apostles found out what was happening, they escaped to the region of Lycaonia. They preached the good news there in the towns of Lystra and Derbe and in the nearby countryside.
Paul and Barnabas in Lystra
8In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk. 9The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man 10and shouted, ‚ÄúStand up!‚ÄĚ The man jumped up and started walking around.
11When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, ‚ÄúThe gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!‚ÄĚ 12The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
13The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates. 14When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
15 Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. 16In times past, God let each nation go its own way. 17But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
18Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
19Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead. 20But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe.
Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
21Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia. 22They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, ‚ÄúWe have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.‚ÄĚ 23Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
24Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia, 25where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia 26and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
27After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe. 28Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.

Ba doo Banabase nyi Sewuse, te ba doo P…Ē…Ēl m…Ē nyi Hemise nawolo nyi Ňčon y…õna be kolosire. 13Na be bane Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Sewuse n…õ deni seŇč…õ…õ don…Ē…Ē keŇč n…Ē…Ēman. Te bane Sewuse sae Ňčon moo nunkyuese na kyeŇčkyeŇčk…Ē…Ēse kaa seŇč don…Ē…Ē keŇč n…Ē…Ēman, nawolo nyi Ňčon na balaŇč dikpii keŇč ne gyae baa fa P…Ē…Ēl na Banabase kedi Ňčgba b…õ…õneŇč n…õ.
14M…Ēna Banabase na P…Ē…Ēl ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, be kyaŇča ba kegbase tirii na kawola nyi nombia Ňčan te takaa w…Ē, te ba moo yeesa yeu karatetee dokoloŇč kagyoo balaŇč dikpii burum baŇč nsana nyi, 15‚ÄúDe tebia, woŇč nombia yaa k…õŇča, weera …õ ne y…õ…õ mena? Daa m…Ē y…õ…õ denibalaŇčw…Ē Ňčgba …õm…õ…õ n…õ. De k…ĒŇčae de kaa yako Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan fa Ňčon, na …õ ke tina …õ nombia k…õŇča Ňčan be taŇč t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya n…õ key…õ…õ yela, na …õ ke k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē b…õ…õ yem ya n…õ gyaŇč. Ňäon y…õ…õna adido na ateta na …õpo, na kpene kamas…õ keŇč doo Ňče man n…õ. 16GyaŇčgba keŇč na, …Ē yela balaŇč y…õ…õ kpene keŇč baŇč gbagba ne gyae. 17M…Ēna …Ē na lese …Ē wose wola nyi …Ē kyaa ampaŇč, lee deeli keŇč …Ē ne y…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē ne fa Ňčon …Ēga na …õ w…Ē…Ēse man ween…õ…õ debaŇč kpaakpaate man. ∆Ü ne fa Ňčon ween…õ…õ te …Ē ne yeli …õ sia na gye.‚ÄĚ 18Kristo kpilala baŇč ke yako k…õŇča koraŇč na, y…õ…õ w…Ē doŇč p…õte be talew…Ē t…Ē balaŇč baŇč gb…õ…õ nyi be na fa w…Ē kedi ya.
19Gyudatena baale lee Anti…Ēke keŇč kyaa Pisidia t…õ…õle man n…õ, na Ikonium k…ĒŇč bot…Ē…Ē kaa w…Ēl…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč agyueŇč te ba seŇčaw…Ē be w…Ēle. KeŇčte be fuŇčii P…Ē…Ēl boya, te be tuu e na t…õ…õle lee don…Ē…Ē keŇč man kel…Ē don…Ē…Ē n…Ē…Ēman. Na baŇč gyeŇč nyi …Ē ta yem. 20M…Ēna Yesu silala baŇč kek…ĒŇč kaa yilaa …Ē gyaŇč n…õ, …Ē korow…Ē bese gyu don…Ē…Ē keŇč man. Ke t…õ…õ kena na te Ňčon na Banabase laŇča D…õbi don…Ē…Ē man.
P…Ē…Ēl na Banabase besew…Ē gyu Anti…Ēke keŇč kyaa Siria t…õ…õle man n…õ
21P…Ē…Ēl na Banabase kekolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan D…õbi don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, balaŇč burum k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa l…õ…õ Yesu di te ba bese …Ē silala, KeŇčte ba besew…Ē gyu Listra na Ikonium na Anti…Ēke keŇč kyaa Pisidia n…õ. 22Be kolosiw…Ē fa Kristotena baŇč te ba doo w…Ē kakyeŇč nyi Kristo nombia Ňčan b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ di n…õ, baa kyaŇč Ňča keŇčkeŇč. Ba besew…Ē do w…Ē kakyeŇč nyi, ‚ÄúKaboena nyi d…õ…õ naa diyem nideli p…õna d…õ…õ tale gyoo Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč man.‚ÄĚ 23Kristotena dikpii kamas…õ man na, P…Ē…Ēl na Banabase na lese siamantena fa w…Ē. Ba lese w…Ē taŇč na, b…õ…õ bake be n…Ē…Ē fane fa w…Ē, na be ta mo be popou do de GbeŇčgyoo Kristo Ňčon b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ e di n…õ nyiŇčmaa man.
24Ba ke ta kila Pisidia t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē taŇč n…õ, be gyuu Pamfilia t…õ…õle man. 25Be kolosi Kristo nombia Ňčan P…õga don…Ē…Ē man. Be ka lee bot…Ē…Ē na, te be gyuuw…Ē Atalia don…Ē…Ē man. 26Be ka lee bot…Ē…Ē m…Ē n…õ, ba moow…Ē loŇč d…Ē…Ē bese k…ĒŇč Anti…Ēke. Anti…Ēke bot…Ē…Ē te Kristotena baŇč fanew…Ē mo w…Ē do Wurubuar…õ nyiŇčmaa man nyi, waa mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele w…Ē na baa gyu ke y…õ…õ tom keŇč b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ taŇč n…õ. 27Ba ke k…ĒŇč kaa gyoo bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, be yilaa Kristotena dikpii keŇč pou te be yako w…Ē tom keŇč pou Wurubuar…õ be t…õ…õ be d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ, na mena keŇč …Ē ba toro gb…õ…õ fa balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya, te baŇč m…Ē be l…õ…õ Kristo nombia Ňčan di n…õ. 28Lee kenaŇč w…Ēle na, be kyaaw…Ē be tebia Kristotena baŇč gyaŇč weeya burum.

english_text:
1 Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught. 2This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders.
The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
3The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy. 4When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do. 5But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, ‚ÄúGentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.‚ÄĚ
6The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles. 7 They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said:
My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him. 8 He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us. 9God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
10Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors. 11But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
12Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
13After they had finished speaking, James said:
My friends, listen to me! 14Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people. 15This agrees with what the prophets wrote,
16 ‚ÄúI, the Lord, will return
and rebuild
David's fallen house.
I will build it from its ruins
and set it up again.
17Then other nations
will turn to me
and be my chosen ones.
I, the Lord, say this.
18I promised it long ago.‚ÄĚ
19And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God. 20 We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
21We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues.
A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
22The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers. 23They wrote a letter that said:
We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
24We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them! 25So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul. 26These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ. 27We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
28The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you. 29 But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well.
We send our best wishes.
30The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter. 31When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged. 32Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
33The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well. 34-35But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord.
Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
36Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, ‚ÄúLet's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.‚ÄĚ 37Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark. 38 But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
39Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus, 40but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care. 41They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.

D…õ…õ l…õ…õ di nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu b…õr…õ…õ keŇč d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ l…õ…õ da nyee, Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be l…õ…õ baŇč m…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ

36DebaŇč daale w…Ēle n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl yako Banabase nyi, ‚ÄúYela d…õ…õ bese gyu don…Ē…Ē kamas…õ keŇč man d…Ē…Ē gyu kekolosi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nombia Ňčan n…õ, na de kaa k…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Kristo nombia Ňčan di n…õ kekyaabii man.‚ÄĚ 37Naf…Ē Banabase gyaew…Ē nyi waa mo Gy…Ēn Maake kpu na ba wose. 38M…Ēna P…Ē…Ēl k…õ…õ ke nyi tekaboena nyi waa sila w…Ē ya.
