english_text:
Timothy Works with Paul and Silas
1Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek. 2The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy, 3and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
4As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions. 5The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord.
Paul's Vision in Troas
6Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia. 7After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them. 8So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
9During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, ‚ÄúCome over to Macedonia and help us!‚ÄĚ 10After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there.
Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
11We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis. 12From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia.
We spent several days in Philippi. 13Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came. 14One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying. 15Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, ‚ÄúIf you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.‚ÄĚ Finally, we accepted her invitation.
Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
16One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners. 17The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, ‚ÄúThese men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.‚ÄĚ
18This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, ‚ÄúIn the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!‚ÄĚ At once the evil spirit left her.
19When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court. 20They told the officials, ‚ÄúThese Jews are upsetting our city! 21They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.‚ÄĚ
22The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip. 23After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully. 24The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
25About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened. 26Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
27When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself. 28But Paul shouted, ‚ÄúDon't harm yourself! No one has escaped.‚ÄĚ
29The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas. 30After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, ‚ÄúWhat must I do to be saved?‚ÄĚ
31They replied, ‚ÄúHave faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.‚ÄĚ
32Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord. 33While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized. 34They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
35The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go. 36The jailer told Paul, ‚ÄúThe officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.‚ÄĚ
37But Paul told the police, ‚ÄúWe are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.‚ÄĚ
38When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid. 39So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town. 40But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_text:
Timoti sila P…Ē…Ēl na Silase
1P…Ē…Ēl gyuu D…õbi don…Ē…Ē man, te …Ē gyuu Listra don…Ē…Ē man m…Ē. Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē ke naa Kristo baale Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Timoti n…õ. ∆Ü naa y…õ…õ Gyuda alo Ňčon …Ē be l…õ…õ Yesu di, m…Ēna …Ē kya y…õ…õ Giriki baale e. 2Kristotena baŇč pou be kyaa Listra na Ikonium don…Ē…Ēse man n…õ dii adans…õ…õ kpaakpaate lee …Ē wose man. 3Na P…Ē…Ēl ne gyae waa mo Timoti sila …Ē wose d…Ē…Ē, …Ē yela be kara …Ē baala wose, nawolo nyi Gyudatena baŇč pou be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ gyeŇč nyi Timoti …Ē kya y…õ…õ Giriki baale e. 4Ba ke ta b…õ…õ lam don…Ē…Ēse Ňčan man n…õ, be yako w…Ē nombia Ňčan Kristo kpilala na kegyiise baŇč be kyaa Gyerusal…õm be yako see nyi baa di Ňče d…Ē…Ē n…õ. 5Mena d…Ē…Ē Kristotena baŇč seŇčaw…Ē keŇčkeŇč ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč man nideli, na wee kamas…õ na balaŇč ne kaa kpuu na w…Ē.
Ba ke baake P…Ē…Ēl gyu Masedonia t…õ…õle man
6P…Ē…Ēl na …Ē tebia t…õ…õw…Ē ka mo na Firigia na Galatia t…õ…õlese Ňčan man, nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon te fa w…Ē gb…õ…õ nyi baa gyu kekolosi nombia Ňčan Esia t…õ…õle man ya. 7Be ke gyu kelii Misia t…õ…õle wulu man n…õ, be y…õ…õ kakyeŇč nyi baa gbaa gyoo Bitinia t…õ…õle man, m…Ēna Yesu feliŇč Ňčon te fa w…Ē gb…õ…õ ya. 8Mena d…Ē…Ē be gbaa Misia t…õ…õle man tisi laŇč Torase don…Ē…Ē man. 9Ke nelim na, te P…Ē…Ēl naa kolo na …Ē sia Ňčgba dos…õ…õ man n…õ nyi, …Ē te naa balee Ňčolo ta lee Masedonia t…õ…õle man k…ĒŇč …Ē na sola e nyi, ‚ÄúGbaa k…ĒŇč de gyaŇč Masedonia na n kaa ky…Ē daa.‚ÄĚ 10P…Ē…Ēl kena ke p…õ, te da korow…Ē desina da wose bileŇč na dee gyu Masedonia nawolo nyi, de l…õ…õw…Ē di nyi Wurubuar…õ baake na daa nyi dee gyu ke yako Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan fa balaŇč bot…Ē…Ē m…Ē.
Lidia kel…õ…õ Yesu di
11Da moo degbele dinaa lee Torase teeŇč gyu Samoterase. T…õ…õ naaw…Ē na te de gyuu Neyapolise don…Ē…Ē man. 12De ka lee bot…Ē…Ē na, te de gyuuw…Ē Filipi don…Ē…Ē man. Filipi don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč y…õ…õ Masedonia t…õ…õle yenaŇč daale don…Ē…Ē dinaa ke. Na Romantena kyaa don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man burum, te de kyaaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē weeya akalans…õ…õ Ňčaale. 13Gyudatena kef…õ…õfowee kelii n…õ, te da leew…Ē don…Ē…Ē keŇč man gyu boo daale n…Ē…Ēman. Na da gyeŇč nyi d…õ…õ naa Gyudatena d…õ…õgyaŇčee fane bot…Ē…Ē. De lii bot…Ē…Ē na te de tisiw…Ē kyaa. Na ala baale te yilaa bot…Ē…Ē, te de fias…õ…õ dee kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia fa w…Ē. 14Ala baŇč b…Ē…Ē tei de nombia n…õ, man Ňčolo yele y…õna Lidia. ∆Ü lee Tiatira don…Ē…Ē keŇč man te …Ē ne y…Ē…Ē agoo kanyaŇčse Ňčaale, na …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon m…Ē …Ē na som Wurubuar…õ nideli. Mena d…Ē…Ē de GbeŇčgyoo toro wa agyueŇč, te …Ē l…õ…õ nombia Ňčan P…Ē…Ēl ne yako n…õ do nideli. 15Mena d…Ē…Ē be s…Ē…Ē Ňčon na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou Wurubuar…õ loŇč. Te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúAkpaa …õ te l…õ…õ maŇč do nyi ampaŇč ma te l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di na, kenaŇč na m…õ…õ sola Ňčon nyi …õ k…ĒŇč kaa kyaa me d…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ Te …Ē waase daa nideli mo daa gyu …Ē d…õ…õ.
Ba kekyaŇč P…Ē…Ēl na Silase t…Ē deni
16Wee daale n…õ da korow…Ē dee gyu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ fane n…õ, te de gyaŇčee na alebubii Ňčolo na …Ē y…õ…õ balaŇč baale alebu e. FeliŇčkum daale doo …Ē man, keŇč ne yeli …Ē ne tale gbala. Lee …Ē kegbala d…Ē…Ē …Ē gbeŇčgyoona baŇč nyiŇča k…Ēba nideli. 17Bot…Ē…Ē te alebu kei sila daa na P…Ē…Ēl na …Ē ne fae yeesa nyi, ‚ÄúBalaŇč k…õw…Ē y…õna Wurubuar…õ adidote Ňčon tewul…õ…õ. Mena keŇč …õ ke y…õ…õ na Wurubuar…õ ke l…õ…õ …õ nyee n…õ yaa b…õ…õ wola Ňčon n…õ.‚ÄĚ 18∆Ü y…õ…õw…Ē mena weeya burum te y…õ…õ P…Ē…Ēl ahiŇč te …Ē g…õ…õsew…Ē k…õ…õ e, te …Ē faree e yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúLee Yesu Kristo yele man, m…õ…õ yako nyaŇč feliŇčkum keŇč doo …Ē man nyi, lee …Ē man!‚ÄĚ DebokenaŇč man te feliŇčkum keŇč leew…Ē …Ē man laŇč.
19DebaŇč keŇč alebu Ňčon gbeŇčgyoona be naa nyi be k…Ēba kenyiŇč gb…õ…õ te t…Ē…Ē n…õ, te ba ke kyaŇča P…Ē…Ēl na Silase te be w…ĒŇčee w…Ē gyu kegyiise d…õ…õgyaŇčee man balaŇč nsana. 20Ba moo w…Ē gyu Roman kegyiise gyaŇč te ba ke yako w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúBalaŇč k…õw…Ē y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē, te ba te k…ĒŇč b…õ…õ w…Ēl…õ…õ don…Ē…Ē keŇč. 21B…õ…õ wola ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan na seŇč tia da mmaraase. Daa y…õ…õ Romantenaw…Ē, d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ tale di Ňče d…Ē…Ē ya.‚ÄĚ 22Mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč dikpii keŇč pou kaa ky…Ē…Ē w…Ē te ba seŇčaw…Ē tia w…Ē. Kegyiise baŇč yela ba kpee P…Ē…Ēl na Silase kegbase te be yela dambiise yee w…Ē nideli. 23Be ke tuku w…Ē nideli taŇč n…õ ba moo w…Ē do deni man, te be faree deni keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õre Ňčon nyi waa t…Ē deni keŇč nideli na b…Ē…Ē nyiŇč lee ya. 24Deni keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õre Ňčon kenyii nombia k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē, …Ē moo w…Ē do deni t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč man te …Ē gy…Ē…Ē w…Ē …ĒpaŇč be naw…Ē…Ē man.
25Kelii t…õ…õ nsana n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl na Silase korow…Ē b…õ…õ fane na bee kpee n…Ē…ĒneŇč lese Wurubuar…õ yele. Na balaŇč akaŇč baŇč m…Ē be doo deni man n…õ ne tei w…Ē. 26DebokenaŇč man te t…õ…õle wosiw…Ē keŇčkeŇč, te deni keŇč mok…Ē…Ē wosiw…Ē. Den…õ…õ Ňčan anaan…Ē…Ēse pou torow…Ē, te be naw…Ē…Ē leew…Ē …ĒpaŇč keŇč man. 27Deni keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õre Ňčon kefoŇč n…õ, …Ē naaw…Ē nyi den…õ…õ Ňčan ta toro te …Ē w…ĒŇča …Ē sila lese nyi waa ko …Ē wose. Nawolo nyi Ňčon gyeŇč nyi balaŇč baŇč pou be doo deni keŇč man n…õ ta lee yeu. 28KeŇčte P…Ē…Ēl fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč yako e nyi, ‚ÄúNa gooli n wose ya, de popou kyaa k…õbo!‚ÄĚ 29Mena d…Ē…Ē deni keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õre Ňčon yela ba moo kanea k…Ēna e, te …Ē yeuw…Ē gyoo deni keŇč man te …Ē ke ŇčmiŇčaw…Ē P…Ē…Ēl na Silase naw…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ. Na …Ē ne seree kpakpakpakpa. 30Te …Ē moo w…Ē lee te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆ź waase da, woŇč te maa y…õ…õ na Wurubuar…õ ke l…õ…õ ma nyee?‚ÄĚ 31Bot…Ē…Ē te be tiran…Ē…Ē yako e nyi, ‚ÄúL…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di, na Wurubuar…õ ke l…õ…õ …õm…õ…õ na n dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou nyee.‚ÄĚ 32Te be kolosi de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan fa deni keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õre Ňčon na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou. 33Bot…Ē…Ē te deni keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õre Ňčon moo P…Ē…Ēl na Silase gyu ka nyekee be gyam…õ…õ, te baŇč m…Ē s…Ē…Ē Ňčon na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou Wurubuar…õ loŇč. 34∆Ü moo w…Ē gyu …Ē d…õ…õ ke fa w…Ē ween…õ…õ. Y…õ…õ Ňčon na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou gyoŇč, nawolo nyi ba te kaa l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ di.
35T…õ…õ kena te Romantena kegyiise baŇč kpila kot…Ēt…Ē…Ētena kegyiise nyi, baa gyu keyako deni keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õre Ňčon nyi, ‚ÄúWaa lese P…Ē…Ēl na Silase na baa ta.‚ÄĚ 36Mena d…Ē…Ē deni keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õre Ňčon yako P…Ē…Ēl nyi, ‚ÄúKegyiise baŇč te kpila balaŇč k…ĒŇč nyi maa tina Ňčon yela na …õ ke ta, mena d…Ē…Ē …õ ke tale ta wosef…õ…õreŇč man.‚ÄĚ 37M…Ēna P…Ē…Ēl yako kot…Ēt…Ē…Ētena kegyiise baŇč nyi, ‚ÄúBe te di de nombia ya te be tuku daa balaŇč nsana te be kyaŇča daa ke t…Ē deni, keŇč de y…õ…õ Romantena gbagbaw…Ē. Te n…õn…õ…õ b…õ…õ gyae baa yo tina daa yela nyi d…õ…õ ta. Koa, d…Ē…Ē gyu ya, kegyiise baŇč gbagba baa k…ĒŇč na be kaa lese daa.‚ÄĚ 38Mena d…Ē…Ē kot…Ēt…Ē…Ētena kegyiise baŇč besew…Ē gyu ke yako nombia k…õŇča fa Romantena kegyiise baŇč. Te be kenyii nyi P…Ē…Ēl na Silase y…õ…õ Romantenaw…Ē n…õ, gyakoloŇč gyoo w…Ē. 39Mena d…Ē…Ē be k…ĒŇčaw…Ē be gyaŇč kaa doo w…Ē teree, te ba lese w…Ē lee deni keŇč man, te be waase w…Ē nyi baa lee be don…Ē…Ē keŇč man. 40P…Ē…Ēl na Silase ka lee deni keŇč man n…õ, be gyuu alo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Lidia n…õ d…õ…õ. Te ba ke naa Kristotena baŇč bot…Ē…Ē te be kolosiw…Ē fa w…Ē do w…Ē kakyeŇč te be laŇčaw…Ē.

english_text:
Trouble in Thessalonica
1After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city. 2So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures 3to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about. 4Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
5The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home. 6But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, ‚ÄúPaul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here, 7and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.‚ÄĚ
8The officials and the people were upset when they heard this. 9So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go.
People in Berea Welcome the Message
10That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue. 11The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true. 12Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
13When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
14At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea. 15Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible.
Paul in Athens
16While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city. 17He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market. 18Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him.
People were asking, ‚ÄúWhat is this know-it-all trying to say?‚ÄĚ
Some even said, ‚ÄúPaul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.‚ÄĚ
19They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, ‚ÄúTell us what your new teaching is all about. 20We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.‚ÄĚ
21More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new. 22So Paul stood up in front of the council and said:
People of Athens, I see that you are very religious. 23 As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, ‚ÄúTo an Unknown God.‚ÄĚ You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him. 24 This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands. 25 He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people. 26 From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
27 God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us, 28and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. ‚ÄúWe are his children,‚ÄĚ just as some of your poets have said.
29Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made. 30In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him. 31He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
32As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, ‚ÄúWe will hear you talk about this some other time.‚ÄĚ 33When Paul left the council meeting, 34some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_text:
P…Ē…Ēl na Silase kekolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Tesalonika don…Ē…Ē man
1P…Ē…Ēl na Silase moow…Ē Anfipolise na Apolonia don…Ē…Ēse man te be gyuu Tesalonika don…Ē…Ē man, na Gyudatena …Ēsom deni kyaa bot…Ē…Ē. 2P…Ē…Ēl gyuu Gyudatena …Ēsom deni keŇč man, Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē ne taŇč y…õ…õ n…õ. P…Ē…Ēl na balaŇč benaŇč moo Gyudatena kef…õ…õfoweeya atooro s…Ē…ĒŇč gyoo Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan man. 3Na …Ē na lese nombia Ňčan man wola w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúKaboena nyi Kristo Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ waa naa diyem na waa foŇč lee yeŇč man. Te …Ē besew…Ē yako nyi, Yesu Ňčon nombia m…õ…õ yako Ňčon n…õ, Ňčon y…õna Kristo nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon.‚ÄĚ 4Te Gyudatena baale agyueŇč ky…õ…õkeew…Ē te be l…õ…õ Yesu di, te be sila P…Ē…Ēl na Silase. Girikitena baŇč bee yee Wurubuar…õ na ala burum baale, baŇč ba wose man dana obuo n…õ m…Ē l…õ…õ nombia Ňčan do.
5M…Ēna Gyudatena baŇč ba te l…õ…õ Yesu di ya n…õ, man baale sia y…õ…õw…Ē P…Ē…Ēl na Silase d…Ē…Ē, te ba korow…Ē yilaa don…Ē…Ē keŇč man aboŇčtena, te be wosi don…Ē…Ē keŇč ampaŇč ampaŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē be gyuu balee Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Gyas…Ēn n…õ d…õ…õ b…õ…õ k…õo P…Ē…Ēl na Silase baa lese w…Ē fa balaŇč dikpii keŇč. 6KeŇč ba te naa w…Ē d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, be w…ĒŇčee Gyas…Ēn na Kristotena baale gyu don…Ē…Ē keŇč kegyiise gyaŇč na yeesa nyi, ‚ÄúBalaŇč k…õw…Ē y…õ…õ t…õ…õley…õl…õ…õlaw…Ē. Ba de laŇčae kamas…õ na bee loo nombia do, te n…õn…õ…õ ba te kaa lii de gyaŇč k…õbo, 7te Gyas…Ēn d…Ē…Ē Ňčon l…õ…õ w…Ē see …Ē d…õ…õ. B…õ…õ w…Ēl…õ…õ gyoo dinaa Kaesa mmaraase Ňčan, te b…õ…õ yako nyi gyoo Ňčolo kyaa bela Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Yesu.‚ÄĚ 8Don…Ē…Ē keŇč mantena na kegyiise baŇč kenyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, ba nyonow…Ē nideli. 9Mena d…Ē…Ē be yela Gyas…Ēn na balaŇč akaŇč baŇč t…Ē…Ē k…Ēba daale mo seŇč be w…Ēle nyi, b…Ē…Ē tekaa y…õ…õ mena bela ya te be tina w…Ē yela.
P…Ē…Ēl na Silase kegyu Beria don…Ē…Ē man
10T…õ…õ ke yu p…õ te Kristotena baŇč moo P…Ē…Ēl na Silase lee don…Ē…Ē keŇč man, te be gyuu Beria don…Ē…Ē man. Be ke gyu ka gyoo bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, be gyuuw…Ē Gyudatena …Ēsom deni keŇč man. 11BalaŇč baŇč be kyaa Beria n…õ, gyeŇč nombia kela Tesalonikatena, nawolo nyi ba moo be kon…Ē…Ēse pou l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan. Te b…õ…õ gyoo Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan man wee kamas…õ, na b…õ…õ k…õ…õ nyi nombia Ňčan P…Ē…Ēl na wola w…Ē n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč? 12Mena d…Ē…Ē Gyudatena balaŇč burum l…õ…õ Kristo nombia Ňčan di. Te Girikitena ala baŇč ba wose man dana obuo na Girikitena baala burum m…Ē l…õ…õ Kristo nombia Ňčan di. 13Gyudatena baŇč be kyaa Tesalonika kenyii nyi P…Ē…Ēl ne yako Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Beria m…Ē n…õ, be k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa wosi bot…Ē…Ē w…Ēl…õ…õ P…Ē…Ēl na …Ē tebia fa w…Ē. 14DebokenaŇč man te Kristotena baŇč l…Ē…Ē P…Ē…Ēl gb…õ…õ te …Ē laŇča …õpo n…Ē…Ēman. M…Ēna Silase na Timoti d…Ē…Ē baŇč d…Ē…Ēw…Ē Beria bot…Ē…Ē. 15BalaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē moo P…Ē…Ēl ka tee n…õ, moo e kelii na At…õnse don…Ē…Ē man. Be ka bese b…õ…õ k…ĒŇč Beria n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl doo w…Ē tiila nyi baa yako Silase na Timoti nyi be kaa tuŇč e bileŇč.
P…Ē…Ēl kyaa At…õnse don…Ē…Ē man
16P…Ē…Ēl k…õ…õ gyeki Silase na Timoti At…õnse don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, …Ē naaw…Ē nyi b…õ…õneŇč te wulu don…Ē…Ē keŇč man mena d…Ē…Ē takaa e nideli. 17Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē gyuu Gyudatena …Ēsom deni keŇč man, na Ňčon na Gyudatena na balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya te bee yee Wurubuar…õ n…õ, na gyoo Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan man nideli. Mena te wee kamas…õ m…Ē, …Ē ne kolosi fa balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ k…ĒŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč balaŇč ne gyaŇčee n…õ. 18Bot…Ē…Ē te wolala baale baŇč b…õ…õ baake w…Ē nyi Epikuritena na Set…Ēyikitena n…õ, na Ňčon P…Ē…Ēl w…ĒŇčee nombia Ňčaale. P…Ē…Ēl kekolosi lee Yesu kabese foŇč lee yeŇč man nombia n…õ, balaŇč baale b…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyi ‚ÄúNa woŇč yaa nkpee kei ne yako baa? Te baale m…Ē kpa, mberem …Ē ne kolosi b…õ…õneŇč w…Ēl…õ…õ baale nombia bo.‚ÄĚ 19Mena d…Ē…Ē ba moo P…Ē…Ēl gyu be d…õ…õgyaŇčee keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi Areopaguse n…õ, kegyiise siaman. Te be b…Ē…Ēse P…Ē…Ēl nyi, ‚ÄúNyaŇč ke tale lese n kawola w…Ēle kei man wola daa? 20Nawolo nyi n nombia Ňčan n na wolo n…õ, ta bese nombia w…Ēl…õ…õ da deŇčela man. Mena d…Ē…Ē d…õ…õ gyae nyi n ka lese Ňče man wola daa.‚ÄĚ 21BalaŇč baŇč be kyaa At…õnse don…Ē…Ē keŇč man na Ňč…Ē…Ēla baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, b…õ…õ y…õ…õ kolo kpu na nombia w…Ēl…õ…õ kenyii na Ňča keyako ya.
22KeŇčte P…Ē…Ēl korow…Ē seŇč kegyiise baŇč be kyaa Areopaguse d…õ…õgyaŇčee keŇč siaman te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAt…õnsetena, ma te naa nyi gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man na, …õ dana y…Ē …Ēsom keŇč man nideli. 23Nawolo nyi ma keta mee kila don…Ē…Ē keŇč na, me naa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan …õ na som n…õ, te me naaw…Ē nyi …õ te Ňčmarase gyakaa …õ kedeesa boe daale d…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄėWurubuar…õ Ňčon d…Ē…Ē gyeŇč e ya n…õ kedeesa boe.‚Äô Mena Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …õ ba gyeŇč e ya te …õ na som e n…õ, …Ē nombia yaa m…õ…õ gyae maa yako Ňčon n…õ. 24Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei na kpene kamas…õ keŇč doo ke man n…õ, Ňčon y…õna adido na ateta GbeŇčgyoo. ∆Ü b…õ…õ kyaa den…õ…õ Ňčan denibalaŇč be bam te b…õ…õ som Ňče man n…õ man ya. 25Te y…õ…õ m…Ē nyi kolo ne hia na e, te …Ē ne gyae nyi denibalaŇč baa y…õ…õ fa e ya. Nawolo nyi Ňčon ne faa na balaŇč pou nyeedoŇč na wees…õ…õ na kpene kamas…õ. 26WalaŇč dokoloŇč d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē yela debiise balaŇč pou k…ĒŇčaw…Ē t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. Ňäon see na debaŇč, na bot…Ē…Ē keŇč walaŇč kamas…õ waa kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. 27Wurubuar…õ y…õ…õ k…õŇča pou see na balaŇč kenyiŇč k…õo e na baa naa e. M…Ēna …Ē be dana lem lee de man Ňčolo gyaŇč ya. 28Ňägba mena keŇč Ňčolo be yako see nyi, Ňčon d…Ē…Ē te de dana nyeedoŇč te d…õ…õ tale wosi da wose te de kyaa n…õ. ∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ n…Ē…ĒneŇč kpeere Ňčolo yako nombii kei nyi, daa m…Ē de y…õ…õ …Ē biaw…Ē. 29Mena d…Ē…Ē keŇč de y…õ…õ …Ē biaw…Ē d…Ē…Ē, de na k…õ…õ nyi Wurubuar…õ y…õ…õ Ňčgba k…Ēba yayam, yaa nyeele wolom, yaa boe keŇč balaŇč ba mo ba agyueŇč na ba adao mo ke y…õ…õ bane see n…õ ya. 30GyaŇčgba keŇč na Wurubuar…õ lese …Ē sia lee nombia Ňčan balaŇč ba gyeŇč ya te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …Ē ne faree balaŇč pou yenaŇč kamas…õ nyi, baa ky…õ…õkee lee be gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man na baa k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč. 31Nawolo nyi …Ē ta lese wee daale see, keŇč waa ta walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ba lese see n…õ d…Ē…Ē, na waa di walaŇč kamas…õ nombia gb…õ…õ kpaakpaate man. Mena keŇč balaŇč kenyiŇč gyeŇč nyi mena nombia k…õŇča y…õ…õ ampaŇč d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē gyuusu walaŇč ŇčonaŇč lee yeŇč man.‚ÄĚ
32BalaŇč baŇč kenyii mena keŇč P…Ē…Ēl ne kolosi lee yeŇč w…Ēle kefoŇč wose man nombia n…õ, be man baale kulu e te baale m…Ē yako e nyi, ‚ÄúD…õ…õ gyae n ka bese yako daa mena nombia k…õŇča bela.‚ÄĚ 33Mena d…Ē…Ē P…Ē…Ēl korow…Ē lee balaŇč baŇč degyaŇčee keŇč man. 34BalaŇč baŇč baale seŇčaw…Ē P…Ē…Ēl w…Ēle te be l…õ…õ Yesu di. Be man baale y…õna Dinisuse Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ gyaŇčee n…õ kegyia Ňčolo e, na alo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e Damarise n…õ, na be man balaŇč burum baale.

english_text:
Paul in Corinth
1Paul left Athens and went to Corinth, 2where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla 3and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
4Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over. 5But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah. 6Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, ‚ÄúWhatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.‚ÄĚ
7Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue. 8Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
9One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, ‚ÄúDon't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop! 10I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.‚ÄĚ 11Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
12While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court 13and said, ‚ÄúThis man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!‚ÄĚ
14Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, ‚ÄúIf you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you. 15But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.‚ÄĚ 16Then he sent them out of the court. 17The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio.
Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
18 After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
19The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there. 20They asked him to stay longer, but he refused. 21He told them goodbye and said, ‚ÄúIf God lets me, I will come back.‚ÄĚ
22Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch. 23After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith.
Apollos in Ephesus
24A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures. 25He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
26Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
27Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace. 28He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_text:

Ňäolo be gyae waa tale y…õ…õ neŇč kolo ya, nawolo nyi balaŇč burum kyaa don…Ē…Ē kei man keŇč be y…õ…õ me wui.‚ÄĚ 11Mena d…Ē…Ē P…Ē…Ēl kyaaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē kulutoo dokoloŇč na yaale na …Ē na wola w…Ē Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan.
12DebaŇč keŇč Galio ba seŇč Romantena gyoo dinaa naw…Ē…Ē man dii gyoori Girikitena d…Ē…Ē n…õ, te Gyudatena y…õ…õ n…Ē…Ē te be kyaŇča P…Ē…Ēl mo e gyu nombia dedii man. 13Te be yakow…Ē nyi ‚ÄúBalee kei na wola balaŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom w…Ēle daale keŇč ne tia da mmaraase.‚ÄĚ 14P…Ē…Ēl ka mo waa kolosi p…õ, te Galio yako Gyudatena baŇč nyi, ‚ÄúNyi akpaa deŇč y…õ…õ nyi nombiikum daale yaa balee kei y…õ…õw…Ē na, naf…Ē maa nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē fa Ňčon. 15M…Ēna kei d…Ē…Ē, ke y…õ…õ n…Ē…Ēwoya na yela, na …õm…õ…õ gbagba ya mmaraase wose man te …õ ne w…ĒŇčee d…Ē…Ē, …õm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyu kedi …õ nombia. Nombia k…õŇča din…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē, maŇč be gyae maa tale di Ňča ya.‚ÄĚ 16Te …Ē yela be gegi w…Ē lee nombia dedii man bot…Ē…Ē. 17KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč pou korow…Ē kyaŇč Sositene Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ …Ēsom deni keŇč kegyia e n…õ, te be tuku e nombia dedii man bot…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna Galio te taŇč bua w…Ē ya.
P…Ē…Ēl kabese gyu Anti…Ēke don…Ē…Ē man
18P…Ē…Ēl kyaaw…Ē Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Korinto n…õ gyaŇč kelii debaŇč daale, te …Ē korow…Ē lee bot…Ē…Ē mo loŇč d…Ē…Ē gyu Siria t…õ…õle man. ∆Ü ne gyu na te …Ē moo Pirisila na Akuila kpu na …Ē wose. ∆Ü ke gyu ka gyoo Kenkiria don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, …Ē gbee …Ē nyee lee ntam keŇč …Ē be kam fa Wurubuar…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 19Be ke gyu ka gyoo Efeso don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, …Ē tinaa Pirisila na Akuila yela, te waageŇč gyuu Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man, te Ňčon na w…Ē gyoo Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan man. 20KeŇčte balaŇč baŇč yako e nyi naf…Ē waa kyaa be gyaŇč kyomii te …Ē b…õ…õw…Ē. 21∆Ü kee gyu na te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa Wurubuar…õ de s…õ…õ na, maa bese k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč.‚ÄĚ Te …Ē moow…Ē loŇč d…Ē…Ē lee Efeso don…Ē…Ē man laŇč. 22∆Ü kelii Kaesaria don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, …Ē leew…Ē bot…Ē…Ē gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man ka do Kristotena baŇč n…Ē…Ē, te …Ē laŇča Anti…Ēke. 23∆Ü dii weeya akalans…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē, kenaŇč w…Ēle n…õ …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē Galatia na Firigia t…õ…õlese man do Kristotena baŇč kakyeŇč.

Pirisila na Akuila kenyii …Ē nombia Ňčan …Ē na wolo n…õ, be kaa moo e gyu be d…õ…õ te be desinaw…Ē lese Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan man wola e nideli. 27DebaŇč keŇč Apolo be y…õ…õ wa agyueŇč nyi …Ē ne gyu Akaya Girikitena t…õ…õle man n…õ, Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Efeso n…õ doo e kakyeŇč, te be faa e be nyiŇčmaa man t…Ēne nyi waa mo gyina Kristotena baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, nyi baa l…õ…õ e nideli. ∆Ü ka gyoo bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ē ky…Ē…Ē baŇč lee Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ keŇč d…Ē…Ē, b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di n…õ nideli. 28Be gyaŇčee balaŇč man b…õ…õ l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč lee Wurubuar…õ nombia man na …Ē ne di Gyudatena baŇč d…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi …Ē na mo Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan wola w…Ē nyi Yesu y…õna nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ.

english_text:
Paul in Ephesus
1While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers. 2He asked them, ‚ÄúWhen you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúNo!‚ÄĚ they answered. ‚ÄúWe have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.‚ÄĚ
3‚ÄúThen why were you baptized?‚ÄĚ Paul asked.
They answered, ‚ÄúBecause of what John taught.‚ÄĚ
4 Paul replied, ‚ÄúJohn baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.‚ÄĚ 5After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus. 6Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied. 7There were about twelve men in this group.
8For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over, 9but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day 10for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message.
The Sons of Sceva
11God gave Paul the power to work great miracles. 12People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
13Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, ‚ÄúCome out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!‚ÄĚ
14Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this, 15when an evil spirit said to them, ‚ÄúI know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?‚ÄĚ 16Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
17When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus. 18Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing. 19Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about 50,000 silver coins. 20So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful.
The Riot in Ephesus
21After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, ‚ÄúFrom there I will go on to Rome.‚ÄĚ 22So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
23At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way. 24A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money. 25Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said:
Friends, you know we make a good living at this. 26But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all. 27Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
28When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, ‚ÄúGreat is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!‚ÄĚ 29Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
30Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him. 31A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
32Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
33Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on. 34But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, ‚ÄúGreat is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!‚ÄĚ
35Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said:
People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here? 36No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish. 37You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
38If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there. 39But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council. 40We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
41After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_text:
P…Ē…Ēl kabese gyu Efeso don…Ē…Ē man
1DebaŇč keŇč Apolo kyaa Korinto n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl besew…Ē kila men…õ…õ kelii Efeso don…Ē…Ē man. Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē ke naa Yesu silala baale, 2te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúDebaŇč keŇč …õ be l…õ…õ Yesu di na, Wurubuar…õ moo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do …õ man?‚ÄĚ Te be tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyi, ‚ÄúDe te nyii ta gba nyi Wurubuar…õ feliŇč daale kyaa ya.‚ÄĚ 3Te P…Ē…Ēl b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē bela nyi, ‚ÄúKenaŇč na Wurubuar…õ loŇč woŇčti yaa be s…Ē…Ē Ňčon?‚ÄĚ Te be tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyiaa, ‚ÄúGy…Ēn Wurubuar…õ loŇčkes…Ē te be s…Ē…Ē daa.‚ÄĚ 4Bot…Ē…Ē te P…Ē…Ēl yako w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúBaŇč b…Ē…Ē ky…õ…õkee lee be gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man n…õ, baŇč te Gy…Ēn s…Ē…Ē w…Ē Wurubuar…õ loŇč. KeŇčte …Ē yako Iseraetena nyi, baa l…õ…õ Ňčon waa k…ĒŇč Ňčon Gy…Ēn w…Ēle n…õ di. Ňäon y…õna Yesu.‚ÄĚ 5Be ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, be yela be s…Ē…Ē w…Ē Wurubuar…õ loŇč lee de gbeŇčgyoo Yesu yele man. 6P…Ē…Ēl moo …Ē nyiŇčmaa gyakaa be d…Ē…Ē, te Wurubuar…õ moo …Ē feliŇč do be man. Bot…Ē…Ē te be kolosi deise w…Ēl…õ…õ, te Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē be d…Ē…Ē kolosi nombia. 7Na be y…õ…õ Ňčgba baala kufu bala (12) n…õ.
8GyaalaŇčna batooro s…Ē…ĒŇč na, P…Ē…Ēl ne gyu Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man, na …Ē ne gy…Ē …Ē kon…Ē…Ē kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan fa w…Ē. Ňäon na w…Ē na gyoo Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan man, te …Ē ne w…ĒŇčee ba agyueŇč k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyoori deedii keŇč wose man nombia man, na baa nyiŇč l…õ…õ Yesu di. 9M…Ēna be man baale y…õ…õ kon…Ē…ĒyuŇč te be b…õ…õw…Ē b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu nombia Ňčan di ya, te be yako nombiakum…õ…õ lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nyeedoŇč gb…õ…õ keŇč …Ē ba wolo n…õ wose man balaŇč nsana. Mena d…Ē…Ē P…Ē…Ēl moo silala baŇč kpu na …Ē wose, te ba korow…Ē tina balaŇč baŇč yela. Wee kamas…õ na b…õ…õ gyaŇčee Tiranuse deni dinaa keŇč …Ē na wola ab…Ē…Ē n…õ man gyoo Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan man. 10∆Ü y…õ…õ kei men…õ…õ kulutooneŇč ala mena d…Ē…Ē walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa Esia t…õ…õle keŇč man n…õ, na baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ pou nyii de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nombia Ňčan.
Siifa bia
11Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē P…Ē…Ēl d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia na nombia dinaana. 12KeŇč nyi balaŇč moo adukuse na kanyaŇčsebia ke tina P…Ē…Ēl, na be ta mo ke tina be kaweesetena na ba wose te y…õ…õ w…Ē doŇč, te baŇč m…Ē feliŇčkum…õ…õ doo be man na, Ňč…õ…õ lee be man.
13Na Gyudatena baale m…Ē d…ĒŇč…õ…õ bee kili gegi feliŇčkum…õ…õ lee balaŇč wose man. Be gy…Ē…Ē be kon…Ē…Ē mo de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu yele nyi baa gegi feliŇčkum…õ…õ. B…õ…õ gyae baa gegi feliŇčkum…õ…õ lee balaŇč wose man na b…õ…õ yako nyi, ‚ÄúLee Yesu Ňčon P…Ē…Ēl ne yako …Ē nombia n…õ yele man, m…õ…õ faree Ňčon nyi ‚Äė∆ź lee …Ē man.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ 14Na Gyudatena saese kegyia Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Siifa n…õ bia baala nyetooro baale ne y…õ…õ na mena nombia k…õŇča. 15Be k…õ…õ y…õ…õ mena n…õ, wee daale feliŇčkum daale b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúMa gyeŇč Yesu te ma gyeŇč P…Ē…Ēl m…Ē, te …õm…õ…õ y…õna bom…Ē?‚ÄĚ 16Mena d…Ē…Ē balee Ňčon feliŇčkum keŇč doo …Ē man n…õ yeuw…Ē gyoo w…Ē ketuku. ∆Ü tuku w…Ē tirii ba kegbase, te ba moo fatabose yekii lee dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man diteŇčteli.
17Te Gyudatena na baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya te be kyaa Efeso don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, nyii nombia k…õŇča na, gyakoloŇč gyoo w…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē ba moo obuo fa de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu, te be ta mo …Ē yele do nsendee ya. 18Te baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ, be man balaŇč burum leew…Ē balaŇč nsana t…Ēl…õ…õ be n…Ē…Ēwoya lese be nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ n…õ deb…Ē…Ē. 19Be man ŇčmeeseŇčtena burum m…Ē moo ba ŇčmeeseŇč t…Ēna Ňčan b…õ…õ Ňčmarase ba ŇčmeeseŇč nombia do Ňče man n…õ k…ĒŇč, te P…Ē…Ēl na …Ē tebia too Ňča balaŇč baŇč pou siaman. Ba mo t…Ēna Ňčan b…Ē…Ē too n…õ pou koya bom na, te y…õ…õ k…Ēba nsendee ya. 20Lee mena d…Ē…Ē de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nombia Ňčan yaaseew…Ē gyu lem te balaŇč l…õ…õ Ňča do nideli.
Efeso don…Ē…Ē kesiŇčii
21Nombia k…õŇča pou w…Ēle n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl y…õ…õ wa agyueŇč nyi waa mo na Masedonia na Giriki t…õ…õlese man, na waa gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man. Bot…Ē…Ē te …Ē yakow…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúAkpaa me gyu kelii bot…Ē…Ē na, kaboena nyi maa gyu Roman don…Ē…Ē man m…Ē.‚ÄĚ 22Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē kpila balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ ky…Ē e n…õ man balaŇč bala, baŇč y…õna Timoti na Erasituse nyi baa gyu Masedonia, na Ňčon P…Ē…Ēl kekyaa Esia t…õ…õle man kyomii.
23DebaŇč kenaŇč man te aŇčmaareŇč dinaa daale gyoo Efesotena man lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nyeedoŇč gb…õ…õ keŇč …Ē silala baŇč na wolo n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 24Na nyeele wolom luure Ňčolo kyaa Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Demetiriyu. ∆Ü na mo nyeele wolom luu be bane Atemise den…õ…õbia y…Ē…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ mena tom kei n…õ nyiŇča k…Ēba nideli. 25KeŇčte Demetiriyu Ňčon baake …Ē tomy…õ…õra, na baŇč pou b…õ…õ y…õ…õ mena tom kenaŇč din…Ē…Ē n…õ te …Ē yako w…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe tebia Ňčgba …õ gyeŇč nyi dee nyiŇč k…Ēba lee da tom kei man. 26∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ ne naa te …õ ne nyii mena keŇč P…Ē…Ēl kei ne w…Ēl…õ…õ balaŇč nyeeya, te …Ē na wola balaŇč nyi kpene keŇč b…õ…õ mo nyiŇčmaase y…õ…õ n…õ, te y…õ…õ kpene keŇč kaboena nyi baa som ya. Na Efeso k…õbo kaageŇč te …Ē ne w…Ēl…õ…õ balaŇč nyeeya ya, m…Ēna …Ē ta mo …Ē nombia ŇčenaŇč kila Esia t…õ…õle kei pou. 27Te y…õ…õ nyi da tom kei kaageŇč ne gyae la ke w…Ēl…õ…õ ya, m…Ēna de bane alo Atemise deni dinaa keŇč Esia na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou ne kaa som n…õ, yele gyae ke yala te de bane Atemise Ňčon m…Ē be gyae waa tekaa nyiŇč obuo bela ya.‚ÄĚ
28BalaŇč dikpii keŇč ke nyii nombia k…õŇča n…õ, ba gyeŇča baŇč te ba gyoo yeesa kefae nyi, ‚ÄúDaa Efesotena bane Atemise Ňčon y…õ…õ bane dinaa e!‚ÄĚ 29Mena d…Ē…Ē don…Ē…Ē keŇč siŇčiiw…Ē, te ba ke kyaŇča Gayose na Arisitakuse. BaŇč y…õna Masedonia tewul…õ…õ bala baŇč be kpu…õ na P…Ē…Ēl n…õ, keŇčte be y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč w…ĒŇčee w…Ē gyu be d…õ…õgyaŇčee keŇč man. 30Naf…Ē P…Ē…Ēl gyaew…Ē nyi waa gyu bot…Ē…Ē, m…Ēna kaseela baŇč te fa e gb…õ…õ ya. 31Te Esiatena kegyiise baŇč be y…õ…õ P…Ē…Ēl …Ē gyoonaw…Ē n…õ, kpilaw…Ē ka sola P…Ē…Ēl nyi …Ē na fa waa gyu ba degyaŇčee keŇč man ya. 32DebaŇč kenaŇč na don…Ē…Ē keŇč te siŇčii na b…õ…õ fae yeesa. Baale kpa kei, baale m…Ē ne yako Ňčaale kpoo. Be man dikpii dinaa daale m…Ē d…Ē…Ē, baŇč ba gyeŇč kpene keŇč be k…ĒŇč d…Ē…Ē te b…Ē…Ē kaa gyaŇčee bot…Ē…Ē ya. 33Te Gyudatena baŇč moo nombia gyakaa Al…õkesanda d…Ē…Ē. Be t…õ…õse e lee siaman k…ĒŇč balaŇč nsana, mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baŇč baale gyueŇčw…Ē nyi Al…õkesanda gyae na nombia Ňčan k…ĒŇč, keŇčte Al…õkesanda gyinaa …Ē nyiŇčmaa adido nyi waa kolosi lese …Ē n…Ē…Ēse. 34M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč ka gyeŇč nyi …Ē y…õ…õ Gyuda baale e n…õ, ba seŇčaw…Ē nombii dokoloŇč d…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ fae yeesa Ňčgba debaŇčse ala n…õ nyi, ‚ÄúDaa Efesotena bane Atemise y…õ…õ bane dinaa e!‚ÄĚ
35KeŇčte don…Ē…Ē keŇč t…Ēne Ňčmarasere Ňčon yela balaŇč baŇč pou tinaa kekolosi yela te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúEfesotena, am…Ēte kyaa la t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē keŇč nyi …Ē ba gyeŇč nyi Efeso don…Ē…Ē y…õna de bane dinaa Atemise deni na …Ē boe keŇč k…õ…õ wola …Ē din…Ē…Ē keŇč ba lee adido kaa yala n…õ nyee ya? 36Ňäolo be kyaa keŇč nyi waa tale l…õ…õ mena nombia k…õŇča aŇčmaareŇč ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē, …õ na barase y…õ…õ kolo ya. 37BalaŇč k…õw…Ē baŇč …õ ba mo w…Ē k…ĒŇč n…õ, te Ňčm…õ…õle kolo lee de bane deni man ya. Te ba te yako nombiikum m…Ē tia de bane kei ya. 38Akpaa Demetiriyu na …Ē tebia na Ňčolo dana nombia na, nombia kedi weeya kyaa te kegyiise baŇč m…Ē kyaa. Baa tale mo d…ĒŇča gyu bot…Ē…Ē. 39M…Ēna nyi akpaa …õ dana nombia w…Ēl…õ…õ Ňčaale na, kaboena nyi don…Ē…Ēsetena gbagba baa di Ňča Ňčgba mena keŇč da mmaraa keŇč na wolo n…õ. 40Akpaa da te k…õ…õ nideli ya na, dee nyiŇč nombia lee gy…õŇč basabasa kei d…Ē…Ē. Te akpaa de nyiŇč nombia m…Ē na, d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ tale tiran…Ē…Ē lese da wose lee Ňče man ya, nawolo nyi basabasa kei be taŇč nyee na naw…Ē…Ē ya.‚ÄĚ 41∆Ü kekolosi taŇč n…õ, te …Ē yela balaŇč baŇč pou yaaseew…Ē.

english_text:
Paul Goes through Macedonia and Greece
1When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia. 2As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece 3and stayed there for three months.
Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia. 4With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus. 5They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there. 6After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week.
Paul's Last Visit to Troas
7On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning. 8In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps. 9A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
10Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, ‚ÄúDon't worry! He's alive.‚ÄĚ 11After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left. 12Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy.
The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
13Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there. 14When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene. 15The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus. 16Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost.
Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
17From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him. 18When they got there, he said:
You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia. 19Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble. 20When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you. 21I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
22I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there. 23In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem. 24 But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
25I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again. 26I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you! 27I have told you everything God wants you to know. 28Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
29I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you. 30Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers. 31Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
32I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people. 33I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes. 34You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends. 35By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, ‚ÄúMore blessings come from giving than from receiving.‚ÄĚ
36After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed. 37Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him. 38They were especially sad because Paul had told them, ‚ÄúYou will never see me again.‚ÄĚ
Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_text:
P…Ē…Ēl kegyu Masedonia na Giriki t…õ…õlese man
1Basabasa keŇč w…Ēle n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl baake Kristotena baŇč pou yilaa te …Ē kolosiw…Ē fa w…Ē do w…Ē kakyeŇč te …Ē kalaa w…Ē, te …Ē lee bot…Ē…Ē laŇč Masedonia. 2∆Ü t…õ…õw…Ē kila bot…Ē…Ē yenaŇč pou, na …Ē ne kolosi do balaŇč baŇč pou kakyeŇč te …Ē leew…Ē bot…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč Giriki t…õ…õle man. 3∆Ü kyaaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē gyaalaŇčna batooro. ∆Ü k…õ…õ desina …Ē wose waa gyu Siria t…õ…õle man na te …Ē naaw…Ē nyi Gyudatena ne bake …Ē nyeeman. Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē y…õ…õ wa agyueŇč nyi waa bese ka mo na Masedonia t…õ…õle man. 4BalaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē sila e gyu n…õ y…õna Piruse Ňčon …Ē ba lee Beria n…õ bu Sopata, na Arisitakuse na Sekunduse baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee Tesalonika n…õ, na Gayose Ňčon …Ē ba lee D…õbi n…õ, na Timoti na Tikikuse na Tirofimuse baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee Esia t…õ…õle man n…õ. 5Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē kyaŇča gb…õ…õ te ba ke kyaaw…Ē Torase don…Ē…Ē man daa daa. 6Gyudatena bodobodo keŇč tee be doo ke man ya kedi wee dinaa keŇč w…Ēle n…õ, da moo degbele dinaa lee Filipi. Weeya anoŇč w…Ēle n…õ te de ka tuŇča baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyaŇč gb…õ…õ laŇč n…õ Torase don…Ē…Ē man te de dii weeya nyetooro bot…Ē…Ē.
P…Ē…Ēl kegyuusu Yutikuse lee yeŇč man
7Gyudatena kef…õ…õfowee keŇč w…Ēle n…õ de popou kaa gyaŇčeew…Ē dee di ween…õ…õ Ňčan de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu ba wola daa nyi dee di na d…õ…õ mo t…Ē…Ēse e n…õ. Lee keŇč t…õ…õ de naa na P…Ē…Ēl waa bese d…Ē…Ē, …Ē kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan men…õ…õ kelii t…õ…õ nsana. 8Na kanease burum doo abansoro deni keŇč man d…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee n…õ. 9P…Ē…Ēl k…õ…õ kolosi keŇč n…õ, na tobaale Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Yutikuse baŇč…õ…õ abansoro deni kebaŇč gyakaa atoorote keŇč nfansere d…Ē…Ē …Ē ne deŇčeli. Lee P…Ē…Ēl kekolosi kyare d…Ē…Ē dosoroŇč moo tobaale Ňčon, te tira e lee bot…Ē…Ē kaa l…Ē t…õ…õle man. Be gyuuw…Ē ke gate e na …Ē ta yem. 10KeŇčte P…Ē…Ēl tisiw…Ē gyu ke buŇč tobaale Ňčon d…Ē…Ē te …Ē pukaa e te …Ē gyuusu e lee yeŇč man. P…Ē…Ēl kekalaa Efeso kegyiise baŇč

Nawolo nyi …Ē yako daa nyi waa kyaŇč gb…õ…õ na …Ē naaw…Ē, na de kaa mo e bot…Ē…Ē. 14∆Ü ke k…ĒŇč kaa tuŇč daa Asose don…Ē…Ē man keŇč n…õ, da moo e kpu na da wose gyu Mitilene don…Ē…Ē keŇč man. 15T…õ…õ kena keŇč n…õ te da moo degbele dinaa keŇč bela kelii Kiyose t…õ…õlebii keŇč loŇč be kilisi ke n…õ yenaŇč. Nawolo nyi …Ē ne gyae bileŇč nyi akpaa …Ē tale na, waa gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man ke tuŇč Gyudatena wee dinaa keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi Pentekosite n…õ.
17∆Ü ke kyaa Miletuse na, te …Ē kpilaw…Ē ka mo Efeso Kristotena …Ēsom kegyiise baŇč. 18Ba ke k…ĒŇč kaa gyoo n…õ, …Ē yako w…Ē nyi ‚Äú∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyeŇč nyi, leenaŇč wee gyaŇčgbate keŇč m…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč Esia t…õ…õle man n…õ, daa na Ňčon kyaaw…Ē nideli weeya ŇčenaŇč pou man. 19∆ź gyeŇč mena keŇč m…Ē…Ē y…Ēk…Ēse ma wose te m…Ē…Ē mo siasaraŇč y…õ…õ tom keŇč nideli Ňčgba de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ tobaale n…õ. Te …õ naa mena keŇč Gyudatena ba koro seŇč me d…Ē…Ē te b…Ē…Ē do maŇč sibiiyam n…õ. 20∆ź gyeŇč nyi debaŇč keŇč mee kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan fa Ňčon balaŇč nsana na ya akpaŇčalaŇčse man n…õ, me ta mo kolo weese Ňčon ya. Ma lese ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan gyae ke ky…Ē Ňčon n…õ pou wola Ňčon. 21Gyudatena na Girikitena pou te me fareew…Ē nyi, baa ky…õ…õkee lee be nombiakum…õ…õ man na baa k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč na baa l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di. 22Te n…õn…õ…õ, m…õ…õ gyae maa y…õ…õ kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon ne gyae d…Ē…Ē mee gyu Gyerusal…õm, m…Ēna m…Ē…Ē gyeŇč kpene keŇč gyae ke k…ĒŇč me d…Ē…Ē bot…Ē…Ē ya. 23Nombii dokoloŇč keŇč ma gyeŇč y…õna nyi, Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon ne faree maŇč don…Ē…Ē kamas…õ man nyi, baa naase maŇč diyem na baa kyaŇč maŇč t…Ē deni. 24M…Ēna m…Ē…Ē ne k…õ…õ ma nyeedoŇč nyi ke y…õ…õ kolo ke fa maŇč ya. M…õ…õ gyae maa y…õ…õ tom keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu be fa maŇč nyi maa y…õ…õ n…õ ke si k…Ē …Ēto. Tom kenaŇč y…õna nyi, maa yako …Ē nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan fa balaŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ waa mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele w…Ē.‚ÄĚ
25N…õn…õ…õ ma gyeŇč nyi …õm…õ…õ baŇč pou m…Ē…Ē kili …õ d…Ē…Ē yako Ňčon Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč wose man nombia Ňčan n…õ, …õ man Ňčolo be gyae waa tekaa naa maŇč bela ya. 26Mena d…Ē…Ē gy…õŇč wee kei m…õ…õ yako Ňčon baa nyi, akpaa …õ man Ňčolo da yoo lee Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õ man na, me n…Ē…Ē be doo ya. 27Nawolo nyi ma te yako Ňčon Wurubuar…õ nyeeman nombia Ňčan pou. 28∆ź k…õ…õ …õ wose d…Ē…Ē nideli, na …õ ke k…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon ba mo w…Ē do …õ nyiŇčmaa man n…õ m…Ē d…Ē…Ē nideli. ∆ź k…õ…õ Kristotena dikpii keŇč d…Ē…Ē nideli Ňčgba mena keŇč wonembu k…õ…õlere ne k…õ…õ …Ē wonembia d…Ē…Ē n…õ. BaŇč y…õna baŇč Wurubuar…õ be yela …Ē bu ba yem l…õ…õ ba nyeeya n…õ. 29Ma gyeŇč nyi ma ke laŇč w…Ēle na, balaŇč kum…õ…õ baa kaa gyoo …õ man Ňčgba mena keŇč gbougbouse na gyoo wonembia man n…õ, te b…Ē…Ē gyae baa tina Ňčon yela ya. 30Te debaŇč daale gyae kelii n…õ, baale baa lee …õm…õ…õ gbagba …õ dikpii keŇč man na baa beo …õ tebia Kristotena baŇč baale sila ba wose. 31Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ doo …õ wose d…Ē…Ē, na …õ ke t…Ē…Ēse nyi kulutooneŇč atooro s…Ē…ĒŇč te me faree Ňčon na siasaraŇč nelim na ka weese pou.
32Te n…õn…õ…õ m…õ…õ mo Ňčon m…õ…õ do Wurubuar…õ nyiŇčmaa man. Nombia Ňčan na wola Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ keŇč …Ē dana fa deniwalaŇč n…õ, ne gyae la ke ky…Ē Ňčon na …õ ke tale seŇč nombia Ňčan …õ be l…õ…õ di n…õ man nideli. Wurubuar…õ waa mo …Ē nyiŇčmaa gyakaa …õm…õ…õ na …Ē balaŇč akaŇč baŇč …Ē ba lese n…õ pou d…Ē…Ē. 33Me sia te y…õ…õ Ňčolo k…Ēba yayam yaa …Ē k…Ēba, yaa wa ab…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē ya. 34∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyeŇč nyi ma nyiŇčmaase k…õŇča te ma moow…Ē y…õ…õ tom te me nyiŇča ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou gyae ke hia daa na me tebia n…õ. 35Ma wola Ňčon kpene kamas…õ man nyi, kaboena nyi walaŇč kamas…õ waa y…õ…õ tom nideli mena, na waa nyiŇč kolo ky…Ē baŇč b…Ē…Ē dana doŇč ya n…õ. Na …õ ke t…Ē…Ēse de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu kawola keŇč nyi, ‚ÄúKolo kefa dana gyoŇč na wosef…õ…õreŇč kela kolo kenyiŇč lee Ňčolo gyaŇč.‚ÄĚ
36∆Ü kekolosi k…õŇča taŇč n…õ, be popou tisiw…Ē buŇč ba doona d…Ē…Ē te be fanew…Ē. 37KeŇčte be man walaŇč kamas…õ moo kewii pukaa e te be kalaa e. 38KeŇč …Ē be yako w…Ē nyi b…Ē…Ē gyae baa tekaa naa e bela ya n…õ, keŇč y…õna kpene keŇč be takaa w…Ē nideli. Ba moo e ka tee, te …Ē gyoo degbele dinaa keŇč man laŇč.

english_text:
Paul Goes to Jerusalem
1After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara. 2We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
3We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo. 4We found the Lord's followers and stayed with them for a week. The Holy Spirit had told them to warn Paul not to go on to Jerusalem. 5But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed. 6Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
7We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day. 8 The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles, 9and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
10 We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea. 11He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, ‚ÄúThe Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.‚ÄĚ 12After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
13But Paul answered, ‚ÄúWhy are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!‚ÄĚ
14Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, ‚ÄúLord, please make us willing to do what you want.‚ÄĚ
15Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem. 16Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning.
Paul Visits James
17When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us. 18Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present. 19Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles. 20Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul:
My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses. 21But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
22What should we do now that our people have heard you are here? 23 Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God. 24Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
25 Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
26The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them.
Paul Is Arrested
27When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him. 28They were shouting, ‚ÄúFriends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.‚ÄĚ 29 Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
30The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut. 31The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot. 32So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered.
As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul. 33The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done. 34Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress. 35As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him. 36The crowd followed and kept shouting, ‚ÄúKill him! Kill him!‚ÄĚ
Paul Speaks to the Crowd
37When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, ‚ÄúCan I say something to you?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúHow do you know Greek?‚ÄĚ the commander asked. 38‚ÄúAren't you that Egyptian who started a riot not long ago and led 4,000 terrorists into the desert?‚ÄĚ
39‚ÄúNo!‚ÄĚ Paul replied. ‚ÄúI am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.‚ÄĚ
40The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_text:

Ňäon Filipo m…Ē kpu…õ balaŇč nyetooro baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyaa lese Gyerusal…õm nyi baa k…õ…õ ween…õ…õ kekp…õl…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē n…õ man. 9Na …Ē dana bia alebia banaara baŇč ba te yala ta ya, na Wurubuar…õ ne t…õ…õ be d…Ē…Ē kolosi.
10De ke di weeya akalans…õ…õ Ňčaale bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, te Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčolo Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Agabuse n…õ, lee Gyudia t…õ…õle man k…ĒŇč. 11∆Ü k…ĒŇča de gyaŇč na, te …Ē moo P…Ē…Ēl ab…Ēs…Ē…Ē mo baake Ňčon gbagba …Ē naw…Ē…Ē na …Ē nyiŇčmaase te …Ē yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúNn…õ yaa Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon ne yako. Nn…õ te Gyudatena baŇč be kyaa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, baa bake Ňčon …Ē tee ab…Ēs…Ē…Ē kei n…õ, na baa lese e fa balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ.‚ÄĚ 12DebaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē nyii mena nombia k…õŇča n…õ, te daa na balaŇč akaŇč baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ waase P…Ē…Ēl nyi …Ē na gyu Gyerusal…õm ya. 13KeŇčte P…Ē…Ēl tiran…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúWeera …õ ne wii te …õ ne yeli dek…Ēk…Ēr…Ēk…Ē…Ē ne kyaŇč maŇč mena? 15Da ke kyaa bot…Ē…Ē kyomii n…õ, da moo da wose te de laŇča Gyerusal…õm. 16Kristotena baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee Kaesaria n…õ man baale sila daa, te ba moo daa gyu Mas…Ēne Ňčon …Ē ba lee Saipor…Ēse don…Ē…Ē man n…õ d…õ…õ. Mas…Ēne kei y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be wulaa l…õ…õ Kristo di leenaŇč kewalaŇč keŇč.

BalaŇč banaara baale kyaa de gyaŇč k…õbo keŇč b…Ē…Ē kaŇč ntam. 24Gyu ke kpu na balaŇč banaara k…õw…Ē na …õ ka gberaa …õ wose, na n ke t…Ē kom keŇč baa gyoo n…õ, na baa nyiŇč tale tisi ba nyeeya. Be na di fatabo ya te be na di wonembu Ňčon ba te kara …Ē nyee p…õte …Ē ba yem n…õ ya. N…õn…õ…õ baa lese ba wose lee kafoŇč nombia man.‚ÄĚ 26Mena d…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ kena keŇč na, te P…Ē…Ēl moo balaŇč banaara baŇč, te be gyuuw…Ē kegberaa ba wose. Te …Ē gyuu Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man ka see wee keŇč ba wose kegberaa keŇč gyae ke taŇč, na wee keŇč be man walaŇč kamas…õ waa fa Wurubuar…õ kedi.
Ba kekyaŇč P…Ē…Ēl
27Weeya nyetooro Ňčan k…õ…õ benaa keŇč n…õ, Gyudatena baale baŇč b…Ē…Ē lee Esia t…õ…õle man n…õ naa P…Ē…Ēl Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man. Te be feefuw…Ē do balaŇč dikpii keŇč b…Ē…Ē gyaŇčee bot…Ē…Ē n…õ te be kyaŇča e, 28te be fae yeesa nyi, ‚ÄúIseraetena …õ kaa ky…Ē daa, balee kei y…õna walaŇč Ňčon …Ē d…ĒŇč yenaŇč kamas…õ …Ē na wola balaŇč pou nombia tia de balaŇč na Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan …Ē be yeli Mosesi be Ňčmarase see fa daa n…õ, na Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč. Lee kenaŇč w…Ēle koraŇč na, …Ē ta mo balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man mo ayim…õ…õ kaa buraa ke.‚ÄĚ 29KeŇč d…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ē yako mena y…õna nyi, na be wulaa naa Efeso baale Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Tirofimuse n…õ, na P…Ē…Ēl d…ĒŇč…õ…õ don…Ē…Ē keŇč man, mena d…Ē…Ē na baŇč gyeŇč nyi P…Ē…Ēl ta mo e gyu Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man.
30Bot…Ē…Ē te don…Ē…Ē keŇč pou siŇčiiw…Ē te balaŇč yeuw…Ē lee yenaŇč kamas…õ k…ĒŇč kaa kyaŇč P…Ē…Ēl te be w…ĒŇčee e lee Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man. Karatetee dokoloŇč te be kyaŇča ka asima Ňčan pou t…Ē. 31Be k…õ…õ gyae baa ko e n…õ, te tiila ke tuŇča Romantena yoon…Ē…Ētena siamante Ňčon nyi, Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē keŇč pou te siŇčii. 32DebokenaŇč man te …Ē moo yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyiise na yoon…Ē…Ētena baale, te be yeuw…Ē gyu balaŇč dikpii keŇč gyaŇč. BalaŇč baŇč kena siamante Ňčon na yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč n…õ, te be tinaa P…Ē…Ēl ketuku yela. 33Be siamante Ňčon gyuu P…Ē…Ēl gyaŇč te …Ē ke kyaŇča e te …Ē yakow…Ē nyi baa mo agbaraagbaraase ala bake e. KeŇčte …Ē b…Ē…Ēse w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúAm…Ēte y…õna balee kei, te woŇč yaa …Ē y…õ…õw…Ē?‚ÄĚ 34BalaŇč baŇč baale ne yako nombii dokoloŇč na baale m…Ē ne yako Ňčaale kpoo. Bot…Ē…Ē kee wosi d…Ē…Ē, yoon…Ē…Ētena siamante Ňčon ta gyeŇč nombia Ňčan nyee na Ňče naw…Ē…Ē ya, mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yako …Ē tewul…õ…õ baŇč nyi baa mo e gyu ba dekyae man. 35Be ka mo e kelii bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ mo gyeŇč gyoo deni keŇč man n…õ, balaŇč baŇč sia key…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč gate P…Ē…Ēl seele adido. 36Na balaŇč baŇč be silana w…Ē n…õ, ne fae yeesa nyii, ‚Äú∆ź ko e.‚ÄĚ
37Be k…õ…õ gyae baa mo P…Ē…Ēl gyoo yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč dekyae keŇč man n…õ, …Ē yako yoon…Ē…Ētena siamante Ňčon nyi, ‚ÄúM…õ…õ gyae maa yako neŇč nombii daale.‚ÄĚ Te …Ē b…Ē…Ēse P…Ē…Ēl nyi, ‚ÄúN ne nyii Giriki dei?‚ÄĚ 38‚ÄúNa nyaŇč y…õna Igyipite baale Ňčon n be kyaa koro seŇč tia kegyiise te n ba mo awudiina kakpoŇčse banaara (4,000) sila n wose gyu kefula man n…õ yee?‚ÄĚ 39Te P…Ē…Ēl tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyi, ‚ÄúKoa, me y…õ…õ Gyuda baale e te ba lola maŇč bo Silisia t…õ…õle man. BalaŇč baŇč ke wu be n…Ē…Ēse n…õ, te P…Ē…Ēl kolosiw…Ē Hiburu dei man fa w…Ē.

english_text:
1‚ÄúMy friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!‚ÄĚ 2When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
3 I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
4 I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women. 5The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
6 One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me. 7I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, ‚ÄúSaul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?‚ÄĚ
8‚ÄúWho are you?‚ÄĚ I answered.
The Lord replied, ‚ÄúI am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.‚ÄĚ 9Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
10I asked, ‚ÄúLord, what do you want me to do?‚ÄĚ
Then he told me, ‚ÄúGet up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.‚ÄĚ 11The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
12In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there. 13He came to me and said, ‚ÄúSaul, my friend, you can now see again!‚ÄĚ
At once I could see. 14Then Ananias told me, ‚ÄúThe God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice. 15You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard. 16What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.‚ÄĚ
17After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision 18of the Lord who said to me, ‚ÄúHurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.‚ÄĚ
19I replied, ‚ÄúLord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you. 20 Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.‚ÄĚ
21But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
22The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, ‚ÄúGet rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.‚ÄĚ 23They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air.
Paul and the Roman Army Commander
24The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
25While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, ‚ÄúIs it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?‚ÄĚ
26When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, ‚ÄúWhat are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!‚ÄĚ
27The commander went to Paul and asked, ‚ÄúTell me, are you a Roman citizen?‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúYes,‚ÄĚ Paul answered.
28The commander then said, ‚ÄúI paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.‚ÄĚ
But Paul replied, ‚ÄúI was born a Roman citizen.‚ÄĚ
29The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains.
Paul Is Tried by the Council
30The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.

english_text:
1Paul looked straight at the council members and said, ‚ÄúMy friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!‚ÄĚ
2Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth. 3 Paul turned to the high priest and said, ‚ÄúYou whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.‚ÄĚ
4The men standing beside Paul asked, ‚ÄúDon't you know you are insulting God's high priest?‚ÄĚ
5 Paul replied, ‚ÄúOh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.‚ÄĚ
6 When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, ‚ÄúMy friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.‚ÄĚ
7As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides. 8 The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these, 9and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, ‚ÄúWe don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.‚ÄĚ
10The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
11That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, ‚ÄúDon't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.‚ÄĚ
A Plot To Kill Paul
12-13The next morning more than 40 Jewish men got together and vowed that they would not eat or drink anything until they had killed Paul. 14Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, ‚ÄúWe have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul. 15You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.‚ÄĚ
16When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it. 17So Paul said to one of the army officers, ‚ÄúTake this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.‚ÄĚ
18The officer took him to the commander and said, ‚ÄúThe prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.‚ÄĚ
19The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, ‚ÄúWhat do you want to tell me?‚ÄĚ
20He answered, ‚ÄúSome men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him. 21But please don't do what they say. More than 40 men are going to attack Paul. They have made a vow not to eat or drink anything until they have killed him. Even now they are waiting to hear what you decide.‚ÄĚ
22The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, ‚ÄúDon't let anyone know you told me this.‚ÄĚ
Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
23The commander called in two of his officers and told them, ‚ÄúBy nine o'clock tonight have 200 soldiers ready to go to Caesarea. Take along 70 men on horseback and 200 foot soldiers with spears. 24Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.‚ÄĚ
25The commander wrote a letter that said:
26Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
27Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
28I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council 29and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
30As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
31The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris. 32The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way. 33When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
34The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia. 35The governor said, ‚ÄúI will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.‚ÄĚ After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_text:

Be kalese P…Ē…Ēl gyu Kaesaria don…Ē…Ē man
23Mena d…Ē…Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena siamante Ňčon baake …Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyiise baŇč man balaŇč bala te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi, ‚Äú∆ź gyae yoon…Ē…Ētena s…ĒŇčonoŇč akpeŇč ala (200) na yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč b…õ…õ kyaa d…õ…õky…ĒŇčse n…õ s…ĒŇčotooro na kufu (70), na yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč b…õ…õ mo apiase yokii n…õ s…ĒŇčonoŇč akpeŇč ala (200). Baa desina ba wose na baa koro nelim debaŇč kadaalete man gyu Kaesaria. 24∆ź gyae d…õ…õky…ĒŇč fa P…Ē…Ēl na …õ ka mo e ka do gyoo Felisi Ňčon …Ē ne k…õ…õ bot…Ē…Ē t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē n…õ nyiŇčmaa man wosef…õ…õreŇč man.‚ÄĚ 25Te yoon…Ē…Ētena siamante Ňčon Ňčmarase t…Ēne fa w…Ē nyi.

Gyoo Ňčon ke nyii nyi …Ē lee Silisia t…õ…õle man n…õ, 35…Ē yakow…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúDebaŇč keŇč balaŇč baŇč …õm…õ…õ na w…Ē be nyiŇč nombia de k…ĒŇč n…õ, na maa nyii n n…Ē…Ēwoya.‚ÄĚ Te …Ē yakow…Ē nyi baa mo P…Ē…Ēl gyu gyoo Herode dekpaŇčalaŇč dinaa keŇč man na baa gyee e.

english_text:
Paul Is Accused in the Court of Felix
1Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul. 2So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him:
Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off. 3All of us are always grateful for what you have done. 4I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
5This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes. 6-8When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
9The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said.
Paul Defends Himself
10The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began:
I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
11It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough. 12Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself. 13There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
14I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets. 15I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil. 16And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
17 After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices. 18This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
19Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now. 20Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council. 21 The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, ‚ÄúI am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!‚ÄĚ
22Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, ‚ÄúI will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.‚ÄĚ 23He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him.
Paul Is Kept under Guard
24Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus. 25But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, ‚ÄúThat's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.‚ÄĚ 26After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
27Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.

1Weeya anoŇč w…Ēle n…õ, Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia Ananiase na don…Ē…Ē kegyiise baale na L…Ēya Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e T…õtul…Ēse n…õ gyuu Kaesaria ke t…Ēl…õ…õ be n…Ē…Ēwoya fa gyoo Felisi lee P…Ē…Ēl wose man. 2Bot…Ē…Ē te be baake P…Ē…Ēl k…ĒŇč. 10Gyoo Felisi ke fa P…Ē…Ēl gb…õ…õ nyi waa kolosi n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl t…Ēl…õ…õ …Ē n…Ē…Ēwoya nyi, ‚ÄúMa gyeŇč nyi n ne di nombia t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē te y…õ…õ gy…õŇč ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē dana maŇč gyoŇč Ňčgba m…õ…õ kolosi maa lese me n…Ē…Ēse fa neŇč n…õ. 11NyaŇč gbagba n gyoo ke man na, n kena nyi weeya kufu ala yaa Ňčgba m…Ē…Ē gyu Gyerusal…õm me kaa som n…õ. 12Te balaŇč baŇč daa na w…Ē be nyim n…õ, te naa daa na Ňčolo ke l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man ya. Ba te naa maŇč nyi maa loo nombia do balaŇč Gyudatena …Ēsom deni man, yaa yenaŇč daale ya. 13Te b…Ē…Ē gyae baa tale gy…Ē be kon…Ē…Ē na baa seŇč kolo d…Ē…Ē wola nyi nombia Ňčan b…õ…õ mo gyakaa me d…Ē…Ē n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč ya. 14M…Ēna ma te s…õ…õ nyi, m…õ…õ som de naana…Ē Wurubuar…õ lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu gb…õ…õ keŇč baŇč ne yako nyi ke y…õ…õ …Ēsom kum ke n…õ man. Te m…õ…õ l…õ…õ kpene kamas…õ keŇč b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase do Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan …Ē be yeli Mosesi be Ňčmarase see n…õ, na Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra t…Ēna man n…õ di. 15Me loo gyakaa Wurubuar…õ nombii dokoloŇč kei d…Ē…Ē nyi, yeŇč w…Ēle na, balaŇč kpaakpaa na balaŇč kum…õ…õ pou baa foŇč lee yeŇč man Ňčgba mena keŇč baŇč m…Ē loo gyakaa n…õ. 16Mena d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ y…õ…õ kakyeŇč wee kamas…õ na maa y…õ…õ kpene keŇč me kon…Ē…Ē be gyae ke bu maŇč f…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ na balaŇč siaman ya.

22Bot…Ē…Ē te Felisi Ňčon …Ē wulaa …Ē gyeŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nyeedoŇč gb…õ…õ keŇč wose man nombia nideli n…õ, tuu nombia kedi wee keŇč see wee w…Ēle.
