M…Ēna waa fa …Ē tebia gb…õ…õ baa fa e kpene kamas…õ keŇč ne hia e.
24Ke di weeya ala Ňčaale n…õ, Felisi na …Ē w…õ…õle Durusila Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ Gyuda alo e n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē. KeŇčte Felisi yela P…Ē…Ēl k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa kolosi fa w…Ē, te P…Ē…Ēl kolosiw…Ē lee Kristo Yesu kel…õ…õdi wose man. 25P…Ē…Ēl ke gyakaa …Ē ne kolosi lee ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan ten…õ…õ na deeli key…õ…õ, na wose d…Ē…Ē kedi na mena keŇč Yesu waa di balaŇč pou nombia wee daale n…õ, gyakoloŇč gyoo Felisi te …Ē yakow…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúTekaboe mena ta p…õ, me nyiŇč ma wose na maa baake neŇč bela.‚ÄĚ 26Na …Ē sia gyakaa nyi P…Ē…Ēl waa fa e k…Ēba na waa tina e yela mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē kyaa …Ē kyaa na, …Ē te baake P…Ē…Ēl kolosi na e. 27KulutooneŇčse ala w…Ēle n…õ, Pokiuse Fesituse kaa dii gyoori gyoo Felisi naw…Ē…Ē man. M…Ēna keŇč Felisi ne gyae waa y…õ…õ Gyudatena deeli d…Ē…Ē, …Ē ta lese P…Ē…Ēl lee deni man ya.

english_text:
Paul Asks To Be Tried by the Roman Emperor
1Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem. 2There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus 3if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way. 4But Festus told them, ‚ÄúPaul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself. 5If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.‚ÄĚ
6Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court. 7As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything. 8Then Paul spoke in his own defense, ‚ÄúI have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.‚ÄĚ
9Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, ‚ÄúAre you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?‚ÄĚ
10Paul replied, ‚ÄúI am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation. 11If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.‚ÄĚ
12After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, ‚ÄúYou have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!‚ÄĚ
Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
13A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus. 14They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said:
Felix left a man here in jail, 15and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty. 16I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
17So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in. 18But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would. 19Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
20Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there. 21But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
22Then Agrippa said to Festus, ‚ÄúI would also like to hear what this man has to say.‚ÄĚ
Festus answered, ‚ÄúYou can hear him tomorrow.‚ÄĚ
23The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in 24and said:
King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death. 25I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
26I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write. 27It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.

Fesituse kekolosi na gyoo Agiripa

Nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan maŇč ba gyueŇč nyi naf…Ē baa yako lee …Ē wose man n…õ, na Ňčan gba te be yakow…Ē ya. 19M…Ēna baŇč na P…Ē…Ēl l…õ…õ d…ĒŇča aŇčmaareŇč bo lee be Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom wose man, na balee Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi Yesu Ňčon …Ē ba yem te P…Ē…Ēl ne gy…Ē …Ē kon…Ē…Ē yako nyi …Ē delena n…õ wose man. 20Na m…Ē…Ē gyeŇč mena keŇč maa gyoo nombia k…õŇča din…Ē…Ē man ya, mena d…Ē…Ē me b…Ē…Ēse P…Ē…Ēl nyi waa s…õ…õ gyu Gyerusal…õm na be kaa di …Ē nombia bot…Ē…Ē? 21M…Ēna P…Ē…Ēl solaw…Ē nyi baa yela waa doo be nyiŇčmaa man na gyoo dinaa Kaesa kedi …Ē nombia. english_text:
Paul's Defense before Agrippa
1Agrippa told Paul, ‚ÄúYou may now speak for yourself.‚ÄĚ
Paul stretched out his hand and said:
2King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me. 3You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
4-5 All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other. 6Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
7Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me. 8 Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
9 I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth. 10I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed. 11I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
12King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests. 13About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me. 14We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, ‚ÄúSaul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!‚ÄĚ
15‚ÄúWho are you?‚ÄĚ I asked.
Then the Lord answered, ‚ÄúI am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to. 16Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.‚ÄĚ
17The Lord also said, ‚ÄúI will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to. 18I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.‚ÄĚ
19King Agrippa, I obeyed this vision from heaven. 20 First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, ‚ÄúStop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.‚ÄĚ
21That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me. 22But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen. 23 I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
24Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, ‚ÄúPaul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.‚ÄĚ
25But Paul replied, ‚ÄúHonorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense. 26None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.‚ÄĚ
27Then Paul said to Agrippa, ‚ÄúDo you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.‚ÄĚ
28Agrippa asked Paul, ‚ÄúIn such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?‚ÄĚ
29Paul answered, ‚ÄúWhether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.‚ÄĚ
30Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up. 31But before they left, they said, ‚ÄúThis man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.‚ÄĚ
32Agrippa told Festus, ‚ÄúPaul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.‚ÄĚ

N ne naase n wose diyem bo Ňčgba mena keŇč b…õ…õ w…ĒŇčee wonembu te Ňčon m…Ē ne w…ĒŇčee …Ē wose lee w…Ēle naase …Ē wose diyem n…õ.‚Äô 15Te me b…Ē…Ēsew…Ē nyi, ‚ÄėNyaŇč am…Ēte ŇčonaŇč me GbeŇčgyoo?‚Äô Te de GbeŇčgyoo tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄėMaŇč y…õna Yesu Ňčon n ne naase diyem n…õ. 16M…Ēna koro seŇč ma lese ma wose baa wola neŇč, na maa mo neŇč y…õ…õ me tobaale na n ke di adans…õ…õ lee kpene keŇč n be naa lee ma wose man na Ňčan maa kaa wola neŇč n…õ. 17Maa l…õ…õ neŇč lee nyaŇč gbagba n balaŇč, na baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya te mee kpila neŇč be gyaŇč n…õ nyiŇčmaa man. 18Mee kpila neŇč baa na n kaa gyuusu be sia, na baa tale lee ditiŇčt…õ…õ man k…ĒŇč keŇčmaŇčee man, na baa tale lee …Ēb…Ēnsam nyiŇčmaa man k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč. 19Mena d…Ē…Ē gyoo Agiripa, ma te y…õ…õ deŇčele doŇč lee kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba lese wola maŇč lee adido, te m…Ē…Ē naa ke na me sia Ňčgba dos…õ…õ man n…õ ya. 20Me taŇčgb…õ…õw…Ē yako Wurubuar…õ nombia Damasekuse te me gyuu Gyerusal…õm na Gyudia t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē pou. KenaŇč w…Ēle te me kaa kolosi Ňča fa baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ. Me yako w…Ē nyi baa ky…õ…õkee lee be gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man na baa k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč, na baa y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan gyae kawola nyi ba te ky…õ…õkee lee be gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč n…õ. 21Kei d…Ē…Ē te Gyudatena kyaŇča maŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man te b…õ…õ gyae gb…õ…õ baa ko maŇč n…õ. 22M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ ta seŇč me w…Ēle kaa lii gy…õŇč, mena d…Ē…Ē te me talew…Ē seŇč k…õbo keŇčkeŇč m…õ…õ di nombia k…õŇča adans…õ…õ fa bia na kegyiise pou nyi, kpene keŇč m…õ…õ yako n…õ na keŇč Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra na Mosesi be yako see nyi gyae ke k…ĒŇč n…õ, y…õ…õ dokoloŇč. 23Nombii keŇč b…Ē…Ē yako see y…õna nyi, nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ, waa naa diyem te Ňčon ne gyaela waa taŇčgb…õ…õ foŇč lee yeŇč man, na waa wola …Ē balaŇč Gyudatena na baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ pou nyi, keŇčmaŇčee keŇč man baa ta na baa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč n…õ te k…ĒŇč.‚ÄĚ
24Bot…Ē…Ē te gyoo Fesituse gy…Ē…Ē P…Ē…Ēl n…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē fae e nyi, ‚ÄúP…Ē…Ēl, n nyee ne w…Ēl…õ…õ do? N t…Ēne kagyeŇč beiŇč na doo na neŇč diteŇč n…õ.‚ÄĚ 25KeŇčte P…Ē…Ēl tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúGyoo kpaakpaa! Ma nyee b…õ…õ w…Ēl…õ…õ ya, me sia ala kaeŇč. Nombia Ňčan mee kolosi n…õ y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa Ňča, te Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ m…Ē. 26Gyoo Agiripa, n gyeŇč nombia k…õŇča pou man mena d…Ē…Ē maa tale gy…Ē me kon…Ē…Ē kolosi na neŇč lee me kon…Ē…Ē man. M…õ…õ l…õ…õ di nyi n gyeŇč nombia k…õŇča man keŇč dokoloŇč koraŇč te lam n d…Ē…Ē ya, nawolo nyi ba te y…õ…õ Ňča weese ya. 27Gyoo Agiripa, n ne l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra baŇč n…Ē…Ēwoya di? Ma gyeŇč nyi n ne l…õ…õ Ňča di.‚ÄĚ 28KeŇčte gyoo Agiripa m…Ē b…Ē…Ēse P…Ē…Ēl nyi, ‚ÄúN gyeŇč nyi nyaŇč ke tale yela maa bese Kristobaale debokeiman?‚ÄĚ 29Te P…Ē…Ēl tiran…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúGy…õŇč bo o kyoo bo o, maŇč ne fane bo nyi Wurubuar…õ waa yela …õm…õ…õ na balaŇč akaŇč baŇč pou …õ be tei maŇč gy…õŇč wee kei n…õ, …õ ka bese Kristotena Ňčgba maŇč P…Ē…Ēl n…õ. M…Ēna be na do Ňčon akyakaase bo ya!‚ÄĚ
30KeŇčte gyoo Agiripa na Fesituse Ňčon …Ē seŇč…õ…õ Romantena gyoo dinaa naw…Ē…Ē man n…õ, na B…õ…õnise na balaŇč akaŇč baŇč korow…Ē lee deni keŇč man. 31Be ka koro n…õ be kolosiw…Ē na d…ĒŇča nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee kei te y…õ…õ dukum keŇč kaboena nyi baa ko e yaa baa do e deni ya.‚ÄĚ 32KeŇčte gyoo Agiripa yako Fesituse nyi, ‚ÄúNyi balee kei deŇč te yako nyi d…õ…õ mo …Ē nombia gyina gyoo dinaa Kaesa ya na, naf…Ē d…õ…õ tale tina e yela.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
Paul Is Taken to Rome
1When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners. 2We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
3The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed. 4When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind. 5Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia. 6There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
7We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind. 8We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
9By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past. 10Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, ‚ÄúMen, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.‚ÄĚ 11But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
12The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there.
The Storm at Sea
13When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete. 14But soon a strong wind called ‚ÄúThe Northeaster‚ÄĚ blew against us from the island. 15The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
16We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place, 17but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
18The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard. 19Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear. 20For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
21Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men:
You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss. 22But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
23I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel 24to tell me, ‚ÄúPaul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.‚ÄĚ 25Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised. 26But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
27For 14 days and nights we had been blown around over the Mediterranean Sea. But about midnight the sailors realized we were getting near land. 28They measured and found that the water was about 40 meters deep. A little later they measured again and found it was only about 30 meters. 29The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
30The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship. 31But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, ‚ÄúIf the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.‚ÄĚ 32The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
33Just before daylight Paul begged the people to eat something. He told them, ‚ÄúFor 14 days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing. 34I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.‚ÄĚ
35After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some. 36They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something. 37There were 276 people on the ship, 38and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter.
The Shipwreck
39Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach. 40They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach. 41But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
42The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping. 43But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore. 44Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_text:
Be kamo P…Ē…Ēl laŇč Roman
1KeŇčte be bakew…Ē see nyi d…õ…õ mo degbele dinaa na dee gyu Romantena gyoo dinaa Ňčon gyaŇč Itali t…õ…õle man. Te ba moo P…Ē…Ēl na balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyam t…Ē deni n…õ baale do yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e Gyuli…Ēse n…õ nyiŇčmaa man. Gyuli…Ēse y…õ…õ Roman gyoo dinaa Ňčon yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčolo e. 2Te da gyoo degbele dinaa keŇč k…Ē…Ē lee Adaramitium don…Ē…Ē man kee gyu Esia yenaŇč don…Ē…Ēse Ňčan Ňče kya boo n…Ē…Ēman n…õ, te de kuluw…Ē laŇč. Na Arisitakuse Ňčon …Ē ba lee Tesalonika don…Ē…Ē keŇč ke kyaa Masedonia t…õ…õle man n…õ doo de man. 3T…õ…õ kena keŇč n…õ, de gyuuw…Ē ka gyoo Sid…Ēn don…Ē…Ē man, bot…Ē…Ē te Gyuli…Ēse naa P…Ē…Ēl waraŇčase te …Ē faa e gb…õ…õ te …Ē gyuu …Ē tebia gyaŇč na baa fa e kpene keŇč ne hia e. 4De ka koro lee bot…Ē…Ē dee gyu keŇč n…õ, na feliŇč ne gy…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč de gyaŇč mena d…Ē…Ē da moow…Ē na Saipor…Ēse t…õ…õlebii keŇč loŇč be kilisi ke n…õ yenaŇč, nawolo nyi na feliŇč b…õ…õ gy…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč bot…Ē…Ē ya. 5Da moow…Ē …õpo d…Ē…Ē ke laŇč Silisia na Pamfilia t…õ…õlese yenaŇč te de ka gyoo Mira don…Ē…Ē keŇč ke kyaa Lisiase t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē n…õ. 6Bot…Ē…Ē te yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon naa degbele dinaa daale na ke ta lee Al…õkesanda kee gyu Itali te …Ē lese daa do ke maŇč. 7Degbele keŇč moo daa d…õ…õd…õ…õ te de naa diyem m…Ē nideli weeya burum p…õte de ka benaa don…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke Kiniduse n…õ. FeliŇč keŇč te fa daa gb…õ…õ d…õ…õ mo da nyee baŇč bot…Ē…Ē keŇč naf…Ē dee gyu n…õ ya, mena d…Ē…Ē de gyuuw…Ē ka mo na Kreti t…õ…õle keŇč loŇč be kilisi ke n…õ, ata ata kelii Salimone don…Ē…Ē yenaŇč d…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi na feliŇč keŇč b…õ…õ gy…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč bot…Ē…Ē yenaŇč ya. 8Da moow…Ē na …õpo n…Ē…Ē n…Ē…Ē, te de kaaw…Ē m…Ē nideli p…õte de kaa lii bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake nyi Agbelese daseŇčae kpaakpaa, keŇč benaa na don…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake nyi Lasea n…õ.
9De w…Ēl…õ…õ debaŇč boo keŇč d…Ē…Ē ampaŇč ampaŇč, te wee keŇč Gyudatena ne fa Wurubuar…õ kedi na waa mo ba dukum ky…õ…õ w…Ē n…õ kaa laŇčaw…Ē. Mena debaŇč kenaŇč na feliŇč ne yeli loŇč d…Ē…Ē keta ne y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nideli. Mena d…Ē…Ē P…Ē…Ēl kolosiw…Ē fa w…Ē nyi, 10‚ÄúMe tebia, ma te naa nyi d…õ…õ naa diyem nideli na dee di gyerebi de gb…õ…õ kei man. Na degbele dinaa kei na kasol…Ēse Ňčan ŇčaageŇč ne gyae la ke w…Ēl…õ…õ ya, m…Ēna balaŇč m…Ē baa yekee. Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ yela d…õ…õ kyaa k…õbo.‚ÄĚ 11M…Ēna yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon b…õ…õ P…Ē…Ēl n…Ē…Ēwoya bo, te …Ē moo degbele dinaa keŇč gbeŇčgyoo na saare Ňčon wui. 12KeŇčte be kpa agbelese d…õ…õseŇčae kenaŇč be dei na kekyaa kyeŇčgbelelee debaŇč kenaŇč man ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē be man dekpeŇč dinaa gyaew…Ē nyi baa kyaŇč ba wose na be kaa lii don…Ē…Ē keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi Fonisi n…õ, na baa kyaa bot…Ē…Ē kyeŇčgbelelee debaŇč kenaŇč man. Fonisi y…õ…õ agbelese d…õ…õseŇčae keŇč ke kyaa Kreti t…õ…õle man te ke sia tee bo weese d…õ…õlee na weese d…õ…õyalae nsana.
FeliŇčkum kegy…Ē …õpo keŇč d…Ē…Ē
13FeliŇčbii daale k…õ…õ gy…Ē…Ē lee ata yenaŇč n…õ, balaŇč baŇč gyueŇčw…Ē nyi baa nyiŇč gb…õ…õ ta ba kegyaebii, mena d…Ē…Ē be w…ĒŇčee nyeele keŇč ne kyaŇč degbele dinaa seŇč debodokoloŇč dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, kaa do degbele dinaa keŇč man te be kuluw…Ē ta na Kreti …õpo n…Ē…Ē n…Ē…Ē. 14Te kyare ya te feliŇč dinaa daale gy…Ē…Ēw…Ē lee weese d…õ…õlee man demena d…Ē…Ē, lee Kreti t…õ…õlebii keŇč loŇč be kilisi ke n…õ yenaŇč k…ĒŇč. 15FeliŇč keŇč gy…Ē…Ēw…Ē gyaŇčee na degbele dinaa keŇč mena d…Ē…Ē ka te tale gyu ke siaman bela ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē de yela feliŇč keŇč w…ĒŇčee daa gyu bot…Ē…Ē keŇč k…õ…õ gyae. 16Te de gyuuw…Ē ka mo na Kauda t…õ…õlebii keŇč loŇč be kilisi ke n…õ yenaŇč, na feliŇč b…õ…õ gy…Ē…Ē bot…Ē…Ē nideli ya, m…Ēna de kaaw…Ē p…õte de talew…Ē lese degbele bii keŇč b…õ…õ mo l…õ…õ balaŇč nyeeya n…õ lee degbele dinaa keŇč man. 17BalaŇč baŇč w…ĒŇčee degbelebii keŇč do degbele dinaa keŇč man te ba moo ŇčmeeseŇč bake degbele dinaa keŇč na feliŇč be nyiŇč gy…Ē ke tekerii ya. Be yeew…Ē nyi degbele dinaa keŇč kaa ta d…Ē…Ē Libia boo n…Ē…Ē aŇčasa man ya, mena d…Ē…Ē be tisinaa degbele dinaa keŇč kanyaŇčse te be tinaa degbele keŇč yela, te feliŇč moo ke ke d…ĒŇč d…õ…õ d…õ…õ. 18FeliŇčkum keŇč moo daa kyeŇčgbeŇč kyeŇčgbeŇč boo keŇč d…Ē…Ē, mena d…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ kena n…õ te be fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ lese kasol…Ēse lee degbele dinaa keŇč man do loŇč man. 19Ke t…õ…õ kena n…õ, ba lese degbele keŇč gbagba ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan Ňčaale fuŇčii leki do …õpo man. 20Dii weeya burum na de b…õ…õ tekaa naa weese yaa kyolobiise ya, na feliŇč keŇč m…Ē gyakaa ne gy…Ē…Ē akpanyaŇčnyaŇč man, mena d…Ē…Ē de ta teka gyakaa loo nyi dee nyiŇč nyeedoŇč bela ya.
21BalaŇč baŇč kyaaw…Ē weeya burum keŇč be bee di ween…õ…õ ya, keŇčte P…Ē…Ēl korow…Ē seŇč be siaman yako w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúMe tebia, nyi yeŇč nyii kpene keŇč m…Ē…Ē yako Ňčon nyi …õ na kulu degbele dinaa keŇč lee Kreti ya n…õ, naf…Ē deŇč te naa diyem kei na dee di gyerebi kei m…Ē ya. 22N…õn…õ…õ m…õ…õ sola Ňčon bo nyi …õ nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē, …õ man Ňčolo be gyae waa yeŇč ya m…Ēna degbele dinaa keŇč kaageŇč ne gyae la ke w…Ēl…õ…õ. 23Nawolo nyi dana nelim me Wurubuar…õ Ňčon me y…õ…õ …Ē wui te m…õ…õ som e n…õ, kpila …Ē kpilale k…ĒŇč me gyaŇč, 24kaa yako maŇč nyi, ‚ÄėP…Ē…Ēl na yee ya, kaboena nyi nya gyu ka seŇč gyoo dinaa Kaesa siaman, na baa di n nombia Ňčan. Lee nyaŇč d…Ē…Ē, Wurubuar…õ waa gyee baŇč pou be doo degbele dinaa keŇč man n…õ.‚Äô 25Mena d…Ē…Ē me tebia, …õ nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi m…õ…õ l…õ…õ di nyi Ňčan pou Wurubuar…õ be yako n…õ gyae ke k…ĒŇč Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be yako maŇč n…õ. 26M…Ēna d…õ…õ kaa lee t…õ…õlebii daale keŇč loŇč be kilisi n…õ d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ
Degbele dinaa keŇč kew…Ēl…õ…õ
27Weeya kufu anaarate keŇč man nelim n…õ, na feliŇčkum keŇč s…õ…õ k…õ…õ w…ĒŇčee daa Aderia …õpo d…Ē…Ē. T…õ…õ nsana n…õ, baŇč b…õ…õ saa degbele dinaa keŇč n…õ naaw…Ē nyi be ta benaa t…õ…õlebii daale. 28Te ba moo kpene keŇč b…õ…õ mo maa loŇč siiliŇč n…õ mo do loŇč keŇč man te be naaw…Ē nyi ke siiliŇč man y…õ…õ naw…Ē…Ē t…Ē…Ēse s…ĒŇčonoŇč na sao(120). Be ke gyu be siaman kyomii n…õ, ba besew…Ē maa loŇč keŇč te be naaw…Ē nyi y…õ…õ naw…Ē…Ē t…Ē…Ēse s…ĒŇčonaara na kufu (90). 29Be yeew…Ē nyi degbele dinaa keŇč gyae ka too na boe, mena d…Ē…Ē ba lese nyeela anaara Ňčan ne kyaŇč agbelese seŇč debodokoloŇč n…õ do loŇč man degbele keŇč w…Ēle. Te be fanew…Ē nyi t…õ…õ d…õ…õ naa bileŇč. 30BaŇč b…õ…õ saa degbele dinaa keŇč n…õ yela baa yeu tina daa yela, mena d…Ē…Ē ba lese degbelebii keŇč ke gyakaa degbele dinaa keŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ gyakaa loŇč d…Ē…Ē. Te ba beow…Ē y…õ…õ Ňčgba bee gyu be kaa fuŇč nyeela Ňčan ne kyaŇč degbele dinaa seŇč debodokoloŇč n…õ do loŇč man degbele keŇč siaman. 31Te P…Ē…Ēl yako yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon na yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč nyi, ‚ÄúAkpaa balaŇč k…õw…Ē te kyaa degbele dinaa kei man ya na, …õ be gyae …õ ke nyiŇč nyeedoŇč ya.‚ÄĚ 32Mena d…Ē…Ē yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč tirii ŇčmeeseŇč Ňčan b…Ē…Ē mo kpaŇč na degbelebii keŇč n…õ te loŇč w…ĒŇčee ke laŇč.
33T…õ…õ k…õ…õ ŇčmaŇčee maa keŇč n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl doo w…Ē kakyeŇč nyi baa di ween…õ…õ. Te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúGy…õŇč weeya kpaakpaa kufu anaara yaa Ňčgba …õ w…Ēe ne l…Ē Ňčon, te …õ n…Ē…Ē m…Ē te daa ta kolo ya. 34Mena d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ waase Ňčon bo nyi yaa di na …õ ke nyiŇč nyiŇč nyeedoŇč, nawolo nyi kolo na kolo be kyaa keŇč gyae ke y…õ…õ …õ man Ňčolo ya.‚ÄĚ 35∆Ü kekolosi n…Ē…Ēwoya k…õŇča taŇč n…õ, …Ē moo bodobodo te …Ē fanew…Ē fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč be popou siaman, te …Ē kara ke te …Ē diiw…Ē. 36Bot…Ē…Ē te be popou w…Ēe f…õ…õ w…Ē te ba moo ween…õ…õ do be n…Ē…Ēman. 37Daa baŇč pou de doo degbele dinaa keŇč man n…õ, y…õ…õ balaŇč s…ĒŇčonoŇč akpeŇč ala na s…ĒŇčotooro na kufu looro (276). 38Be popou kedi baa n…õ, ba lese ween…õ…õ Ňčan doo degbele keŇč man n…õ fuŇč do …õpo man, na debgele dinaa keŇč ke nyiŇč koro yuŇč.
39T…õ…õ k…õ…õ naa keŇč n…õ, be naaw…Ē Ňčgba …õpo keŇč yenaŇč daale te s…Ē…Ē gyoo t…õ…õle daale man, na aŇčasa doo bot…Ē…Ē m…Ēna be ta gyeŇč t…õ…õle dokoloŇč kenaŇč ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē be y…õ…õ ba gyueŇč nyi akpaa baa tale na, baa besena degbele dinaa keŇč gyu ka seŇč bot…Ē…Ē. 40Te be k…õr…õ…õ ŇčmeeseŇč Ňčan b…Ē…Ē mo kpaŇč na nyeela Ňčan ne kyaŇč degbele dinaa seŇč debodokoloŇč n…õ do loŇč man, te be booli ŇčmeeseŇč Ňčan b…Ē…Ē mo bake na woni keŇč ne kii degbele dinaa n…õ. Te be gyinaa degbele dinaa keŇč kanyaŇč adido degbele keŇč siaman te feliŇč gy…Ē…Ēw…Ē, te tuu degbele dinaa keŇč sia tee dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē gb…õ…õ. 41M…Ēna degbele dinaa keŇč siagb…õ…õ gyuuw…Ē ka gyoo aŇčasa man te ke d…Ē…Ēw…Ē debodokoloŇč, te ka ateta keŇč Ňčerew…Ē gyoo aŇčasa Ňčan man te ke d…Ē…Ēw…Ē keŇč ke bee wosi gba ya. Te feliŇč dinaa keŇč ne gy…Ē…Ē n…õ yela …õpo loŇč keŇč gy…Ē…Ē degbele dinaa keŇč w…Ēle gb…õ…õ aguŇč kpetekpetekpete.
42Yoon…Ē…Ētena baŇč y…õ…õ ba agyueŇč nyi baa ko balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kyam do deni n…õ pou, na be man Ňčolo be nyiŇč y…õl…õ…õ toŇč yeu ya. 43M…Ēna yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon gyaew…Ē waa l…õ…õ P…Ē…Ēl nyee mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē t…Ē…Ē w…Ē nyi be na y…õ…õ Ňčgba mena keŇč b…Ē…Ē bake see n…õ ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē yako balaŇč baŇč pou nyi baŇč baa tale y…õl…õ…õ na, baa taŇčgb…õ…õ toŇč gyoo loŇč man na baa y…õl…õ…õ toŇč gyu dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē. 44Te baŇč b…Ē…Ē kaa n…õ baa g…Ētaa degbele dinaa keŇč be y…õl…õ…õ n…õ daase pampasaŇčse Ňčan be kyakaree n…õ, na baa toŇč gyu dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē. Nn…õ te de popou y…õ…õw…Ē toŇč gyu dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē keŇč Ňčolo te d…Ē…Ē ya.

english_text:
On the Island of Malta
1When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta. 2The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
3After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand. 4When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, ‚ÄúThis man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.‚ÄĚ
5Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed. 6The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, ‚ÄúThis man is a god.‚ÄĚ
7The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days. 8His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
9After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed. 10The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed.
From Malta to Rome
11Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as ‚ÄúThe Twin Gods.‚ÄĚ 12We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days. 13From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli. 14There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
15Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged.
Paul in Rome
16We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
17Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said:
My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans. 18They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death. 19 The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor.
But I don't have anything to say against my own nation. 20I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
21The leaders replied, ‚ÄúNo one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you. 22But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.‚ÄĚ
23They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
24Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not. 25Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, ‚ÄúThe Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet 26 to tell our ancestors,
‚ÄėGo to these people
and tell them:
You will listen and listen,
but never understand.
You will look and look,
but never see.
27All of you
have stubborn hearts.
Your ears are stopped up,
and your eyes are covered.
You cannot see or hear
or understand.
If you could,
you would turn to me,
and I would heal you.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
28-29Paul said, ‚ÄúYou may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.‚ÄĚ
30For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him. 31He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_text:
Maleta dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē
1De ka lee …õpo man kaa seŇč dekeŇčkeŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ te balaŇč baŇč be kyaa bot…Ē…Ē t…õ…õlebii kei keŇč loŇč be kilisi ke n…õ d…Ē…Ē yako daa nyi, b…õ…õ baake bot…Ē…Ē nyi Maleta. 2Don…Ē…Ēsetena benaŇč k…õ…õ de d…Ē…Ē nideli. Na …Ēga te fias…õ…õ …Ē ne Ňč…õ…õ, mena d…Ē…Ē ba doo boalaŇč fa daa nyi dee woli nawolo nyi, na waare kyaa. 3P…Ē…Ēl ke kuu daase Ňčaale waa do boalaŇč keŇč man n…õ, te boalaŇč ogyefeliŇč keŇč yela dom keŇčkeŇčteŇčte Ňčolo leew…Ē daase Ňčan man kaa milii …Ē nyiŇčmaa. 4Don…Ē…Ēsetena baŇč kena mena n…õ be yako d…ĒŇča nyi, ‚ÄúBalee kei waa y…õ…õ awudiini e. Nawolo nyi, nyi …Ē y…õ…õ nyeebam Ňčgba …Ē te d…Ē…Ē …õpo man koraŇč ya n…õ, …Ē nombiakum…õ…õ be gyae ke yela waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč ya.‚ÄĚ 5M…Ēna P…Ē…Ēl ŇčmanyaŇč dom Ňčon do boalaŇč man, keŇč kolo te y…õ…õ e ya. 6BalaŇč baŇč gyueŇčw…Ē nyi waa bokee, yaa gyae ke kara e l…Ē karatetee dokoloŇč na waa yeŇč. M…Ēna ba ke daa gyu siaman te ba kena nyi kolo te y…õ…õ e ya n…õ, be ky…õ…õkee ba agyueŇč nyi balee kei waa y…õ…õ bane Ňčolo e.
7Bot…Ē…Ē gyoo yele y…õna Pubiliyuse. KeŇčte P…Ē…Ēl gyuuw…Ē ka mo …Ē nyiŇčmaa gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē fane fa Wurubuar…õ fa e, te …Ē ky…Ē…Ē e kawee. 9Ke k…ĒŇč mena n…õ, kaweesetena akaŇč baŇč pou be kyaa Maleta t…õ…õle keŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē, te P…Ē…Ēl ky…Ē…Ē w…Ē be kaweese pou. 10BalaŇč benaŇč moo obuo dinaa fa daa, te be fa daa ab…Ē…Ē burum, te de ka koro lee bot…Ē…Ē dee gyuu n…õ, be fa daa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan gyae ke hia daa gb…õ…õnaa man n…õ.
P…Ē…Ēl ka lee Maleta gyu Roman don…Ē…Ē man
11De dii gyaalaŇčna batooro t…õ…õlebii keŇč d…Ē…Ē p…õte da leew…Ē bot…Ē…Ē. KeŇčte da moo degbele dinaa keŇč k…Ē…Ē lee Al…õkesanda don…Ē…Ē man kaa seŇč bot…Ē…Ē dugbutu t…õ…õ n…õ. T…õ…õ kena keŇč n…õ, feliŇč daale lee ata yenaŇč kaa gy…Ē, mena d…Ē…Ē ka weeya alate man te de gyuuw…Ē ka gyoo Puteyoli don…Ē…Ē man. 14Bot…Ē…Ē te de naa Kristotena baale te ba sola daa nyi dee di weeya gyanaara be gyaŇč. Nn…õ te de t…õ…õw…Ē men…õ…õ ka gyoo Roman don…Ē…Ē man. 15Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Roman kenyii da nkaŇč n…õ, be man baale k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa gyaŇčee na daa naŇč Apiuse don…Ē…Ē man, te baale m…Ē kaa gyaŇčee na daa Ňč…Ē…Ēla d…õ…õsoe den…õ…õ atooro Ňčan man. P…Ē…Ēl kena w…Ē n…õ, doo e kakyeŇč te …Ē faa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč. 16De ka gyoo Roman don…Ē…Ē man n…õ, yoon…Ē…Ētena kegyia Ňčon faa P…Ē…Ēl gb…õ…õ nyi waa gyu ke kyaa bot…Ē…Ē keŇč dei fa e n…õ, te ba lese yoon…Ē…Ēte Ňčolo nyi waa gyee e.
P…Ē…Ēl kekolosi Roman don…Ē…Ē man
17Weeya atooro w…Ēle n…õ, P…Ē…Ēl baake Gyudatena kegyiise baŇč be kyaa don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man n…õ gyaŇčee. Ba ke gyaŇčee keŇč n…õ te …Ē yako w…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúMe tebia Iseraetena, ma te y…õ…õ kolo kum tia de tebia yaa tia amanber…õse Ňčan de naana…Ē ba wola daa koraŇč ya, m…Ēna be kyaŇča maŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man te ba moo maŇč do Romantena nyiŇčmaa man. 18Ba korow…Ē gyoo me nombia man na, be yela baa tina maŇč yela, nawolo nyi ba te naa nombiikum keŇč m…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ keŇč kaboena nyi baa ko man ya. 19M…Ēna keŇč Gyudatena be b…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē, yela ma solaw…Ē nyi maa mo me nombia k…ĒŇč gyoo dinaa gyaŇč, m…Ēna maŇč d…Ē…Ē ma gyeŇč nyi daa na me tebia betaŇč daale ya. 20Kei d…Ē…Ē te me baake Ňčon na maa nyiŇč lese nombia Ňčan man wola Ňčon. WalaŇč Ňčon Iseraetena sia gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ d…Ē…Ē te me doo akyakaase k…õŇča man n…õ.‚ÄĚ 21KeŇčte kegyiise baŇč tiran…Ē…Ē fa e nyi, ‚ÄúDa te l…õ…õ n wose man t…Ēne lee Gyudia t…õ…õle man ya. Te de walaŇč Ňčolo m…Ē ta lee bot…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč kaa yako daa kolo kum lee n wose man ya. 22M…Ēna d…õ…õ gyae d…õ…õ gyeŇč nya agyueŇč nawolo nyi da gyeŇč nyi yenaŇč kamas…õ na balaŇč ne kolosi tia dikpii keŇč man n doo n…õ.‚ÄĚ
23Te balaŇč baŇč na P…Ē…Ēl see wee keŇč baa gyaŇčee. Wee kenaŇč kelii n…õ, be man balaŇč burum k…ĒŇčaw…Ē bot…Ē…Ē keŇč P…Ē…Ēl kyaa n…õ. Lee nebo kelii balin…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ē kolosi Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč wose man nombia. ∆Ü moo Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan …Ē be yeli Mosesi be Ňčmarase see n…õ, na Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra n…Ē…Ēwoya aŇčmaraseŇč see kolosi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu wose man nombia fa w…Ē na baa nyiŇč l…õ…õ e di. 24Be man baale l…õ…õ …Ē nombia Ňčan di m…Ēna baale d…Ē…Ē ba te l…õ…õ Ňča di ya. 25M…Ēna, p…õna P…Ē…Ēl n…Ē…Ē ke si na, nombii dokoloŇč te …Ē yakow…Ē te aŇčmaareŇč gyoo w…Ē, te be popou korow…Ē yaasee. Nombii keŇč …Ē be yako w…Ē y…õna nyi, ‚ÄúNombii keŇč Wurubuar…õ feliŇč be t…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Yesaya d…Ē…Ē yako fa …õ naana…Ē n…õ, doo na ke gb…õ…õ. ∆Ü yako w…Ē nyi,
26‚ÄėGyu ke yako balaŇč k…õw…Ē nyi,
∆ź ke tei, na …õ ke tei,
m…Ēna …õ be gyae …õ ke nyii nombia ŇčenaŇč man ya.

27Nawolo nyi balaŇč k…õw…Ē agyueŇč ta yem.

28Te P…Ē…Ēl kolosiw…Ē t…Ē n…Ē…Ē nyi, ‚ÄúLee mena d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ gyae …õ ka gyeŇč nyi, Wurubuar…õ te yeli be ta mo nyeedoŇč nombia Ňčan gyina baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ. BaŇč d…Ē…Ē baa tei Ňča.‚ÄĚ 29P…Ē…Ēl ke yako nombia k…õŇča n…õ, te baageŇč baageŇč moo aŇčmaareŇč koro yaasee.
30KulutooneŇč ala s…Ē…ĒŇč na P…Ē…Ēl kyaa bot…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē be hare deni …Ē kyaa n…õ, na …Ē ne l…õ…õ baŇč pou b…õ…õ k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč n…õ nideli. 31∆Ü gy…Ē…Ē …Ē kon…Ē…Ē kolosi Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu wose man nombia Ňčan fa balaŇč keŇč Ňčolo te t…Ē e gb…õ…õ ya.

english_text:
1From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus.
God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news 2that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures. 3-4This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
5Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith. 6You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
7This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people.
I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
A Prayer of Thanks
8First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith. 9God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
10In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you. 11I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith. 12What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
13 My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places. 14-15It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome.
The Power of the Good News
16 I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles. 17 The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúThe people God accepts because of their faith will live.‚ÄĚ
Everyone Is Guilty
18From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth. 19They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them. 20 God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse. 21 They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark. 22They claim to be wise, but they are fools. 23 They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
24So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies. 25They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
26God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural. 27Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
28 Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things. 29They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip, 30say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil.
These people don't respect their parents. 31They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others. 32They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_text:
1T…Ēne kei lee maŇč P…Ē…Ēl Ňčon me y…õ…õ Yesu Kristo tobaale e n…õ gyaŇč. Wurubuar…õ baake na maŇč nyi me kaa y…õ…õ …Ē kpilale, te …Ē lese maŇč see nyi maa yako …Ē nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan. 2GyaŇčgba gyaŇčgba keŇč n…õ, Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē wa akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra d…Ē…Ē yako …Ē nombia k…õŇča see …Ē nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan man. 3Nombia kpaakpaa k…õŇča y…õ…õ …Ē bu Yesu Kristo Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo e n…õ nombia Ňča. Lee lolo gb…õ…õ man na, Yesu kei lee gyoo Defidi duuluŇč man. 4Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon doŇč keŇč …Ē ba mo gyuusu Yesu lee yeŇč man n…õ, keŇč lese la wola nideli nyi …Ē y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ bu e keŇč ayim…õ…õ be doo …Ē man ya.
5Wurubuar…õ t…õe Ňčon Kristo ŇčonaŇč d…Ē…Ē mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele daa nyi de kaa bese …Ē kpilala. Na ke yela t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou ke l…õ…õ Ňčon Wurubuar…õ di, na baa nyii …Ē dei na baa lese …Ē yele. 6∆źm…õ…õ Romantena baŇč Wurubuar…õ be baake Ňčon te …õ be kaa bese Yesu Kristo balaŇč n…õ, …õm…õ…õ m…Ē kpu…õ mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē man.
7Lee mena d…Ē…Ē te m…õ…õ Ňčmarase t…Ēne kei mee yelee …õm…õ…õ Romantena baŇč pou Wurubuar…õ ne gyae Ňčon te …Ē be baake Ňčon nyi …õ kaa bese …Ē balaŇč n…õ. De kya Wurubuar…õ na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be b…õr…õ…õ boele Ňčon, na baa fa Ňčon wosef…õ…õreŇč.
8P…õ maa kolosi nombii daale n…õ, me d…ĒŇč Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč lee …õ popou d…Ē…Ē. 11∆ź nombia ne gy…Ē maŇč loo, mena d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ gyae nyi maa k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč na maa yela …õ ke nyiŇč Wurubuar…õ feliŇč ab…Ē…Ē anefaŇč Ňčan, na Ňče ka do Ňčon doŇč …õ kel…õ…õdi man. 12Kei nawolo baa nyi akpaa me k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč na, daa na Ňčon pou kel…õ…õdi gyae ka do d…ĒŇča kakyeŇč.
13Me tebia m…õ…õ gyae …õ ka gyeŇč nyi naf…Ē me gyaew…Ē agbaatee agbaatee nyi maa k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč, na Wurubuar…õ ke ta me d…Ē…Ē yela balaŇč ke l…õ…õ Yesu di nideli …õ man, Ňčgba mena keŇč …õ tebia baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ be l…õ…õ e di debiise akaŇč Ňčan man n…õ, m…Ēna gb…õ…õ ta toro fa maŇč debaŇč kenaŇč man ya.

Siakaralatena na ayiŇčtena pou, balaŇč baŇč ba gyeŇč nombia na baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč nombia ya n…õ pou. 15Lee kei d…Ē…Ē te me sia y…õ…õw…Ē nyi maa kolosi Kristo nombia Ňčan fa …õm…õ…õ baŇč …õ kyaa Roman don…Ē…Ē man m…Ē n…õ.
16Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan kekolosi be dana maŇč des…õŇč ya. Nawolo nyi Ňčan y…õna Wurubuar…õ doŇč keŇč …Ē na mo l…õ…õ walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne l…õ…õ Ňča di n…õ nyee. Gyudatena taŇčgb…õ…õ la nyii nombia k…õŇča, p…õte balaŇč akaŇč baŇč m…Ē kaa nyii Ňča. 17Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan na wola na mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ne y…õ…õ p…õte balaŇč gb…õ…õneŇč na tenee …Ē siaman. Nombia ŇčenaŇč nawolo nyi akpaa walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne l…õ…õ Yesu di n…õ de tale seŇč …Ē kel…õ…õdi man lee kewalaŇč kelii …Ēto na, …Ētenate gb…õ…õneŇč gyae ka tenee Wurubuar…õ siaman. Be Ňčmarase mena nombii kei Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan man nyiaa, ‚ÄúWalaŇč Ňčon lee …Ē kel…õ…õdi d…Ē…Ē te …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč da ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman na, waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa.‚ÄĚ
Wurubuar…õ baŇč
18Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa adido n…õ ta lese wola nyi …Ē ta gyeŇč baŇč balaŇč kum…õ…õ na balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē ne nyii …Ē dei ya n…õ d…Ē…Ē. Nawolo nyi be nombiakum…õ…õ bee yeli Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ n…õ ka lee deb…Ē…Ē ya. 19Wurubuar…õ ta gyeŇč baŇč nawolo nyi gb…õ…õ keŇč man balaŇč baa mo gyeŇč e n…õ te y…õ…õ nombia aweesesa ya. Ňäon Wurubuar…õ gbagba lese na Ňča wola w…Ē. 20Wurubuar…õ wose man nombia Ňčaale kyaa keŇč nyi walaŇč be tale naa Ňča na …Ē sia fon…õ…õ ya. Ňäan y…õna …Ē doŇč kekpaakekpaa na …Ē din…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē dana keŇč nawolo nyi …Ē y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ e n…õ. M…Ēna n k…õ…õ na leenaŇč t…õ…õle key…õ…õ kewalaŇč keŇč na, Wurubuar…õ din…Ē…Ē kenaŇč ba wees…õ…õ ya. Nawolo nyi ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou …Ē be y…õ…õ n…õ na lese …Ē din…Ē…Ē wola nideli keŇč balaŇč baa tale naa na baa l…õ…õ di. Mena d…Ē…Ē walaŇč Ňčon …Ē te l…õ…õ di ya na, …Ē taŇč nombii daale waa yako mo l…õ…õ …Ē wose ya.
21Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ kyaa m…Ēna b…Ē…Ē na mo obuo keŇč kaboena e n…õ fa e ya, te b…Ē…Ē na lese …Ē yele m…Ē ya. Nyansa nombii kamas…õ be doo ba agyueŇč man ya, te ditiŇčt…õ…õ wolowolo doo la be kon…Ē…Ēse man. 22B…õ…õ y…õ…õ Ňčgba be sia te kare n…õ, m…Ēna be y…õ…õ ayiŇčtenaw…Ē. 23Be te tina Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa kekpaakekpaa n…õ yela, gyu b…õ…õ som ban…õ…õ Ňčan b…Ē…Ē maa yaa b…Ē…Ē yae Ňča Ňčgba balaŇč yaa gyebuse yaa wonembia n…õ.
24Mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ m…Ē ta besena …Ē w…Ēle tee w…Ē, te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombia nkaraŇčkaraŇč Ňčan ba lee baŇč gbagba be kon…Ē…Ēse man n…õ. Be dana kew…Ēl…õ…õ nombia bee fulu ba wose. 25Be te tina Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ n…õ kel…õ…õdi yela gyu b…õ…õ l…õ…õ ak…õr…õ…õ nombia di. Mena d…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ som ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan Wurubuar…õ be y…õ…õ n…õ bo tina Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be y…õ…õ Ňča n…õ yela. Ňäon Wurubuar…õ te kaboena nyi d…õ…õ lese …Ē yele kekpaakekpaa Am…õn.
26KeŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ ta besena …Ē w…Ēle tee w…Ē te ba gyoo des…õŇč nombia key…õ…õ. Be man ala baŇč gba koraŇč te tina baala gyaŇč kedoo Ňčgba mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba wolo n…õ yela gyu bee doo na d…ĒŇča. 27Mena m…Ē te baala m…Ē b…õ…õ ala gyaŇč kedoo Ňčgba mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba wolo n…õ yela gyu b…õ…õ yeŇč d…ĒŇča yeŇč, te ba gyoo des…õŇč nombia nkaraŇčkaraŇč key…õ…õ na d…ĒŇča. Mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ waa w…ĒŇč ba deŇčela Ňčgba mena keŇč kaboena n…õ.
28KeŇč b…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan kagyeŇč d…Ē…Ē, Ňčon m…Ē ta besena …Ē w…Ēle tee w…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē nombia nkaraŇčkaraŇč te b…õ…õ gyueŇč te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombia Ňčan tekaboena nyi naf…Ē balaŇč baa y…õ…õ ya n…õ. 29Nombiakum…õ…õ te wulu be man. AkpanyaŇčnyaŇč na nombiakum…õ…õ na sibii na balaŇč kek…Ēla na sia key…õ…õ balaŇč ab…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē, na balaŇč kako na kale nombia na ak…õr…õ…õ kedi na nombia ka mo gyakaa balaŇč d…Ē…Ē te wulu be man. Mena m…Ē te b…õ…õ yako balaŇč wose man nombia. 30B…õ…õ mo nombiakum…õ…õ gyakaa d…ĒŇča d…Ē…Ē, te b…õ…õ k…Ēla Wurubuar…õ. Be dana deboŇč te b…Ē…Ē ne bu balaŇč ya, te b…õ…õ wola ba wose m…Ē. B…õ…õ gyae gb…õ…õneŇč w…Ēl…õ…õ Ňčan baa mo y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ debaŇč kamas…õ, te b…Ē…Ē ne bu ba lolala m…Ē ya. 31B…Ē…Ē dana nyansa ya te b…Ē…Ē ne di nombia Ňčan b…õ…õ yako n…õ d…Ē…Ē ya. B…Ē…Ē ne gyae balaŇč ya, te b…Ē…Ē ne wii balaŇč waraŇčase m…Ē ya. 32KeŇč na keŇč ba gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ mmaraa nawolo nyi walaŇč Ňčon …Ē doo mena din…Ē…Ē kei man na …Ētenate be gyae waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa ya koraŇč n…õ, be gyakaa b…õ…õ y…õ…õ mena nombiakum…õ…õ k…õŇča kpu. KenaŇč w…Ēle koraŇč n…õ, b…õ…õ bese do balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ mena nombiakum…õ…õ k…õŇča m…Ē n…õ kakyeŇč.

english_text:
God's Judgment Is Fair
1 Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things. 2We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way. 3Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse? 4 You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
5But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness. 6 God will reward each of us for what we have done. 7He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever. 8But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil. 9All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles. 10But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles. 11 God doesn't have any favorites!
12Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says. 13God accepts those who obey his Law, but not those who simply hear it.
14Some people naturally obey the Law's commands, even though they don't have the Law. 15This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned, 16when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says.
The Jews and the Law
17Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God. 18By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right. 19You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark. 20And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
21But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal? 22You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples. 23You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God. 24 It is just as the Scriptures tell us, ‚ÄúYou have made foreigners say insulting things about God.‚ÄĚ
25Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised. 26In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised. 27So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
28Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew. 29 To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_text:

7Baale ta mo be loo gyakaa nyi baa nyiŇč gyoorobiiri na yele na nyeedoŇč keŇč ka dokoso be kyaa ya n…õ. Mena d…Ē…Ē bee nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa weeya pou man. BaŇč te Wurubuar…õ waa fa nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa keŇč.
8M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč lee sibii d…Ē…Ē te b…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ anokoare keŇč gyu ke sila nombiakum…õ…õ n…õ, Wurubuar…õ baŇč kum na …Ē deŇčele kew…ĒŇč keŇč gyae ke k…ĒŇč be d…Ē…Ē na baa naa diyem nideli. 9Wurubuar…õ waa yela kaale na kayey…õ…õ ke k…ĒŇč balaŇč baŇč pou b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē. Ke gyae ke fias…õ…õ bo lee Gyudatena d…Ē…Ē, p…õna ke k…ĒŇč balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ m…Ē d…Ē…Ē. 10M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ waa yela balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa n…õ ke nyiŇč gyoorobiiri na yele na wosef…õ…õreŇč. Lee mena d…Ē…Ē n na do Wurubuar…õ des…õŇč. 24Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan ne yako nyiaa, lee …õm…õ…õ Gyudatena nombia ay…õ…õsa d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ ne kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuar…õ.
25Akpaa n ne di Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ, kenaŇč na n baala wose kekara keŇč dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna akpaa n bee di Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, kenaŇč na n baala wose kekara keŇč y…õ…õ yakaa. KenaŇč na y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba n te kara n baala wose gba ya n…õ. 26Nyi akpaa Ňčolo te kara …Ē baala wose ya te …Ē ne di Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ, Wurubuar…õ siaman na y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba …Ētenate kara …Ē baala wose baa n…õ. 27NyaŇč Gyuda baale, n gyeŇč mmaraase Ňčan nawolo nyi be Ňčmarase Ňča bo see neŇč te be kara n baala wose m…Ē, m…Ēna n bee di Ňče d…Ē…Ē ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē walaŇč Ňčon …Ē te kara …Ē baala wose ya te …Ē ne di mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ, waa bu neŇč f…Ē…Ē.
28Nawolo nyi te y…õ…õ nyi keŇč Ňčolo ne y…õ…õ Gyudatena ab…Ē…Ē ay…õ…õsa d…Ē…Ē te …Ētenate y…õ…õ Gyuda baale e ya. Te na baala wose m…Ē kekara kaageŇč na wola la nyi Ňčolo y…õ…õ Gyuda baale gbagba e ya. 29Akpaa nyi Ňčolo y…õ…õ Gyuda baale gbagba e na, …Ē kon…Ē…Ē man na wola la. Te baala wose kekara gbagba m…Ē y…õ…õ kon…Ē…Ē man nombia Ňča. Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon ne ky…õ…õkee na …Ētenate kon…Ē…Ē. Ka te y…õ…õ nyi mmaraase Ňčan b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase see n…õ d…Ē…Ē kedi ya. Mena walaŇč ŇčonaŇč n…õ, Wurubuar…õ na lese na …Ē yele. Te y…õ…õ denibalaŇč ya.

english_text:
1What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised? 2It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews. 3It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith? 4 No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God,
‚ÄúYour words
will be proven true,
and in court
you will win your case.‚ÄĚ
5If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask. 6But the answer is, ‚ÄúNo.‚ÄĚ Otherwise, how could God judge the world? 7Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner. 8You might as well say, ‚ÄúLet's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!‚ÄĚ Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well.
No One Is Good
9What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said. 10 The Scriptures tell us,
‚ÄúNo one is acceptable to God!
11Not one of them understands
or even searches for God.
12They have all turned away
and are worthless.
There isn't one person
who does right.
13 Their words are like
an open pit,
and their tongues are good
only for telling lies.
Each word is as deadly
as the fangs of a snake,
14 and they say nothing
but bitter curses.
15 These people quickly
become violent.
16Wherever they go,
they leave ruin
and destruction.
17They don't know how
to live in peace.
18 They don't even fear God.‚ÄĚ
19We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty. 20 God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin.
God's Way of Accepting People
21Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses. 22 God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ. 23All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory. 24But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins. 25-26 God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
27What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith. 28We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law. 29Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is! 30 There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith. 31Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.

english_text:
The Example of Abraham
1Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham? 2If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God. 3 The Scriptures say, ‚ÄúGod accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.‚ÄĚ
4Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working. 5But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him. 6In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
7-8 ‚ÄúWhat a blessing
when God forgives our sins
and our evil deeds.
What a blessing
when the Lord erases our sins
from his book.‚ÄĚ
9Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him. 10But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
11 Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised. 12This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised.
The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
13 God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable. 14 If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
15God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken. 16 Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all. 17 The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
18 God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations. 19 Abraham's faith never became weak, not even when he was nearly 100 years old. He knew he was almost dead and that his wife Sarah could not have children. 20But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
21Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised. 22So God accepted him, 23just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham. 24They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life. 25 God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.

Mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baŇč pou ba te kara ta be baala wose ya te b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di n…õ, …Ē te yeli be gb…õ…õneŇč ta ten…õ…õ Ňčon Wurubuar…õ siaman. Kei te yeli Aberaham te kaa bese baŇč m…Ē be naana. 12Ňäon Aberaham ŇčonaŇč bese yaa …Ē y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kara be baala wose n…õ m…Ē be naana. Na keŇč b…Ē…Ē kara be baala wose n…õ kaageŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē bese be naana ya, m…Ēna keŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di kpu Ňčgba mena keŇč Ňčon Aberaham be l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di p…õte b…Ē…Ē kara …Ē baala wose n…õ d…Ē…Ē te …Ē bese be naana.
13Wurubuar…õ yakow…Ē see nyiaa, Aberaham na …Ē duuluŇč man balaŇč ne gyae la baa di t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. Te y…õ…õ nyi keŇč Aberaham be di Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē, d…Ēkaa te Wurubuar…õ yako nombia k…õŇča ya, m…Ēna keŇč Aberaham be l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di te Wurubuar…õ be l…õ…õ e do nyi …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Ňčon Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ d…Ē…Ē te …Ē yako nombia ŇčenaŇč. 14Nyi akpaa balaŇč baŇč bee di mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ yako keyako see kei n…õ, kenaŇč na Wurubuar…õ kel…õ…õdi keŇč y…õ…õ yakaa te …Ē keyako see keŇč m…Ē be dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya. 15Nawolo nyi mmaraase na moona Wurubuar…õ baŇč k…ĒŇč. Te bot…Ē…Ē keŇč m…Ē mmaraase be kyaa ya na, dukum m…Ē key…õ…õ be kyaa ya.
16Mena d…Ē…Ē lee kel…õ…õdi d…Ē…Ē te balaŇč baa tale nyiŇč Wurubuar…õ ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan …Ē be yako see n…õ. Na ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča ka bese kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ na mo boele balaŇč yakaa. Kei na wolo baa nyi Aberaham duuluŇč man balaŇč pou baa nyiŇč mena ab…Ē…Ē ŇčenaŇč. Te y…õ…õ nyi baŇč bee di mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ baageŇč ne gyae la baa nyiŇč Ňča ya, m…Ēna baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ di Ňčgba mena keŇč Aberaham be l…õ…õ di n…õ m…Ē baa nyiŇč mena ab…Ē…Ē ŇčenaŇč. Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ siaman na, Aberaham y…õna de popou de naana. 17Ňägba mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see ne yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúMa te yeli n ta bese t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou be naana.‚ÄĚ Aberaham Ňčon …Ē be l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di n…õ, …Ē y…õ…õ de naana e Wurubuar…õ siaman. Ňäon Wurubuar…õ kei ne gyuusu na woya lee yeŇč man, te Ňčon ne tale la yela ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan naf…Ē Ňč…Ē…Ē kyaa ya n…õ ne k…ĒŇč Ňčgba Ňče wulaa Ňče kyaa n…õ.
18Na kolo na kolo be kyaa keŇč gyae ke yela Aberaham ka mo …Ē loo gyakaa nyi, Wurubuar…õ waa y…õ…õ kpene keŇč …Ē be yako see nyi waa y…õ…õ n…õ ya. M…Ēna Aberaham l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di te …Ē moo …Ē loo pou gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē. Lee mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē kaa bese t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou be naana, Ňčgba mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi, ‚Äú∆Ü duuluŇč man balaŇč baa bo Ňčgba kyolobiise n…õ.‚ÄĚ 19DebaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ be yako Aberaham mena nombii kei n…õ, na …Ē te di kulutooneŇč s…ĒŇčonoŇč (100) mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē te Ňčm…õ…õ keŇč nyi Ňčolo loo be gyakaa nyi waa tale lola bela ya. Mena m…Ē te …Ē w…õ…õle Saara m…Ē te Ňčm…õ…õ gbaa lolo. K…õŇča pou w…Ēle n…õ, Aberaham ta lee w…Ēle Wurubuar…õ kel…õ…õdi man ya. 20Mena m…Ē te …Ē te l…õ…õ aŇčmaareŇč nyi nombia Ňčan Wurubuar…õ be yako see n…õ be gyae ke k…ĒŇč mena ya, m…Ēna …Ē y…õ…õ kakyeŇč seŇč …Ē kel…õ…õdi keŇč man keŇčkeŇč te …Ē lese Wurubuar…õ yele. 21Nawolo nyi na …Ē gyeŇč lee …Ē kon…Ē…Ē man nideli nyi, Wurubuar…õ dana doŇč keŇč waa tale y…õ…õ kpene keŇč …Ē be yako see nyi waa y…õ…õ n…õ. 22Lee Aberaham kel…õ…õdi kei d…Ē…Ē te ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ l…õ…õ e do nyi …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ …Ē siaman.‚ÄĚ
23Ba ke Ňčmarase nyi ‚Äú∆Ü l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di d…Ē…Ē te …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč teneew…Ē Wurubuar…õ siaman‚ÄĚ n…õ, na Aberaham waageŇč d…Ē…Ē te be Ňčmarase mena nombii kenaŇč ya. 24M…Ēna daa baŇč m…Ē d…õ…õ l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be gyuusu de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo lee yeŇč man n…õ di, na waa yela de gb…õ…õneŇč ka tenee …Ē siaman n…õ m…Ē d…Ē…Ē te be Ňčmarase mena nombii kenaŇč. 25Lee de nombiakum…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ lese Yesu Kristo nyi …Ē kaa yeŇč fa daa. Mena m…Ē te …Ē gyuusu e lee yeŇč man, na ke yela de gb…õ…õneŇč ka tenee Ňčon Wurubuar…õ siaman.

english_text:
What It Means To Be Acceptable to God
1By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God. 2Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God. 3But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure. 4And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope 5that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
6Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful. 7No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person. 8But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
9But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger. 10Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son. 11And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us.
Adam and Christ
12 Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die. 13Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law. 14Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did.
In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later. 15But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
16There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times. 17Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
18 Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life. 19Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
20The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful. 21Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_text:
Daa na Wurubuar…õ nsana ta bese de
1N…õn…õ…õ lee keŇč d…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di d…Ē…Ē, Wurubuar…õ te l…õ…õ daa do nyi de gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ …Ē siaman. Te lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo ŇčonaŇč d…Ē…Ē, gyoŇč te kaa doo daa na Wurubuar…õ nsana. 2Lee de Kristo kel…õ…õdi d…Ē…Ē te de nyiŇča Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ keŇč …Ē ba mo boele daa te de d…ĒŇč ke man n…õ. Mena d…Ē…Ē dana daa gyoŇč, nawolo nyi de loo gyakaa nyi d…õ…õ kaa di gyoori kpu na e. 3Na keŇč kaageŇč ya. KenaŇč w…Ēle na dana daa gyoŇč bela lee da diyem kena man, nawolo nyi diyem kena ne yele la walaŇč ne tale kyaŇč …Ē wose seŇč keŇčkeŇč. 4WalaŇč de kyaŇč …Ē wose seŇč keŇčkeŇč na, ne yeli Wurubuar…õ ne l…õ…õ …Ētenate do. Mena m…Ē te nyi walaŇč da gyem nyi Wurubuar…õ te l…õ…õ e do na, ne yeli …Ē ne nyiŇč kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē waa mo …Ē loo gyakaa. 5Mena loo kegyakaa kei m…Ē be gyae ka do daa des…õŇč ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ te t…õ…õ …Ē feliŇč Ňčon …Ē ba mo do de man n…õ d…Ē…Ē mo …Ē balaŇč kegyae do de man.
6Nawolo nyi debaŇč keŇč de s…õ…õ de y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õraw…Ē te d…Ē…Ē dana doŇč keŇč d…õ…õ tale mo ky…õ…õkee da kekyaabii ya n…õ, Kristo k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa yeŇč fa daa debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba see n…õ man. 7Gyae ke y…õ…õ doŇč nideli p…õte Ňčolo ke tale l…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ yeŇč yeŇč. M…Ēna nyi akpaa Ňčolo ne y…õ…õ deeli fa balaŇč na, mberem na Ňčolo waa s…õ…õ l…õ…õ …Ē yeŇč yeŇč. 8M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ ta lese wola nyi …Ē ne gyae daa nideli, nawolo nyi debaŇč keŇč de s…õ…õ de y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õraw…Ē n…õ, …Ē yela Kristo kaa yeŇčaw…Ē fa daa. 9Lee Kristo fatabo keŇč …Ē ba mo feefo de nombiakum…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē, te Wurubuar…õ l…õ…õ daa do nyi de gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ …Ē siaman. Mena m…Ē te lee Kristo fatabo kenaŇč d…Ē…Ē, Wurubuar…õ waa l…õ…õ da nyee wee keŇč waa mo baŇč di t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou nombia n…õ. 10Naf…Ē na de y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč d…õ…õ k…Ēla Wurubuar…õ, m…Ēna lee …Ē bu yeŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē …Ē ta mo daa y…õ…õ …Ē gyoona baŇč …Ē ne gyae. Nyi …Ē bu Yesu yeŇč keŇč te yeli de ta bese …Ē gyoona na, kenaŇč na Yesu nyeedoŇč keŇč …Ē be nyim n…õ be gyae ke yela waa l…õ…õ da nyee kekpaa yee? 11Te y…õ…õ nyi …Ē l…õ…õ da nyee bo kaageŇč ya, m…Ēna lee Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē dee di gyoŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom keŇč man. Nawolo nyi lee Ňčon Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē gyoŇč te kaa doo daa na Wurubuar…õ nsana.
Be ka mo Adam maa na Kristo
12Lee walaŇč dokoloŇč d…Ē…Ē te nombiakum…õ…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. Ňäon y…õna de naana Adam. Nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ m…Ē moona yeŇč k…ĒŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē te yeŇč nyiŇča doŇč balaŇč d…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi walaŇč kamas…õ te y…õ…õ dukum. 13P…õte Wurubuar…õ ka mo mmaraase Ňčan fa Mosesi na, nombiakum…õ…õ wulaa kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna bot…Ē…Ē keŇč mmaraa be kyaa ya n…õ, b…Ē…Ē ne kala dukum key…õ…õ do kolo man ya. 14LeenaŇč de naana Adam debaŇč kaa lii de naana Mosesi debaŇč man n…õ, yeŇč ne dii na doŇč balaŇč pou d…Ē…Ē. BalaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ deŇčele doŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia ayakoŇč see Ňčan d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba Adam koraŇč ya n…õ, yeŇč dii doŇč be d…Ē…Ē. Adam seŇč…õ…õ fa walaŇč Ňčon waa k…ĒŇč n…õ. WalaŇč ŇčonaŇč y…õna Yesu Kristo.
15Ke y…õ…õ anokoare ke nyi mena balaŇč bala k…õw…Ē y…õ…õ kpookpoo. Nawolo nyi kpene keŇč be t…õ…õ Adam d…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč, na kpene keŇč be t…õ…õ Kristo d…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč n…õ te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč ya. Lee Adam dukum keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē te balaŇč burum nyiŇča yeŇč. Te lee Yesu Kristo b…õr…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ lese …Ē b…õr…õ…õ wola balaŇč burum, kela balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē yem lee Adam dukum d…Ē…Ē n…õ. 16Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ keŇč …Ē ba mo boele daa n…õ, na kpene keŇč Adam dukum keŇč ba mo k…ĒŇč n…õ te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč ya. Adam dukum keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ n…õ yele la te balaŇč pou dii f…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ siaman. M…Ēna balaŇč nombiakum…õ…õ burum k…õŇča w…Ēle na, Wurubuar…õ ta mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele daa te …Ē moo da akum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ daa te …Ē yela de kaa bese balaŇč baŇč be gb…õ…õneŇč tenee …Ē siaman. 17Lee walaŇč dokoloŇč dukum key…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te yeŇč dii gyoori balaŇč pou d…Ē…Ē. M…Ēna keŇč Yesu Kristo be y…õ…õ fa daa n…õ, ke t…Ēn…Ē…Ē kela kpene keŇč Adam dukum keŇč ba mo k…ĒŇč n…õ. Wurubuar…õ te t…õ…õ Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē wii de waraŇčase nideli te …Ē moo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele daa te …Ē yela da bese balaŇč baŇč de gb…õ…õneŇč tenee …Ē siaman. WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be wii …Ē waraŇčase, te …Ē ba mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele e te …Ē be yeli …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč ba tenee …Ē siaman n…õ, waa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa na waa yela Ňčon na Yesu Kristo ke di gyoori.
18Mena d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč lee walaŇč dokoloŇč nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te balaŇč pou be di f…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ, mena ke m…Ē te lee walaŇč dokoloŇč nombia kpaakpaa key…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ waa l…õ…õ balaŇč burum do nyi be gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ …Ē siaman na waa fa w…Ē nyeedoŇč. 19Mena keŇč lee walaŇč dokoloŇč deŇčele doŇč d…Ē…Ē te balaŇč pou ba bese nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra n…õ, mena ke m…Ē te lee walaŇč dokoloŇč obuo d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ waa l…õ…õ balaŇč burum do nyi be gb…õ…õneŇč tenee …Ē siaman.
20Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan k…ĒŇčae na ke yela balaŇč kena be nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ nideli. M…Ēna debaŇč keŇč nombiakum…õ…õ na boo na, Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ keŇč m…Ē na boo kpu. 21Naf…Ē na nombiakum…õ…õ ne diina doŇč balaŇč d…Ē…Ē, te ne kpase baŇč na Wurubuar…õ nsana. M…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ ta mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele daa yela de ta bese balaŇč baŇč be gb…õ…õneŇč tenee Ňčon Wurubuar…õ siaman, na ke yela d…õ…õ ta de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu d…Ē…Ē nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa.

english_text:
Dead to Sin but Alive because of Christ
1What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better? 2No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning? 3Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death? 4 When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
5If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him. 6We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin. 7We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
8As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him. 9We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die. 10When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God. 11In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
12Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires 13or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God. 14Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law.
Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
15What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not! 16Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him. 17You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received. 18Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
19I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
20When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God. 21But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death. 22Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life. 23Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_text:
Kristo d…Ē…Ē te de nyeedoŇč dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē
1Akpaa nn…õ te nombia k…õŇča doo na, na sena yaa d…õ…õ yako baa? Kaboena nyi d…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē da dukum key…õ…õ man na Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ keŇč ka bo yaa? 2Korakora, be k…ĒŇč mena ya. Nawolo nyi da te w…ĒŇč da wose lee dukum key…õ…õ man. Mena d…Ē…Ē tekaboena nyi d…õ…õ bese gyu ke kyaa da dukum key…õ…õ man bela ya. 3DebaŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē s…Ē daa Wurubuar…õ loŇč te daa na Yesu Kristo be y…õ…õ dokoloŇč n…õ, na …õ ba gyeŇč nyi da te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč baa lee …Ē yeŇč keŇč man yee? 4Ba ke s…Ē daa Wurubuar…õ loŇč keŇč n…õ, na y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba daa na Yesu Kristo yeŇča la te be wuu daa na e n…õ, nawolo nyi daa na e te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč de kya Wurubuar…õ ba mo …Ē gyoorobiiri doŇč keŇč gyuusu Yesu Kristo lee woya man fa e nyeedoŇč n…õ, mena te kaboena nyi daa m…Ē d…õ…õ kyaa din…Ē…Ē w…Ēle man.
5Nawolo nyi akpaa daa na Yesu te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč lee …Ē yeŇč man na, kenaŇč na Wurubuar…õ waa gyuusu daa m…Ē lee yeŇč man Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be gyuusu Yesu lee yeŇč man n…õ. 6Da gyeŇč nyi debaŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē gy…Ē e mataa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē ba yem n…õ, na Ňčon Yesu na de din…Ē…Ē bene keŇč yaa be gy…Ē…Ēw…Ē ko n…õ. Kei te yeli doŇč keŇč nombiakum…õ…õ dana de d…Ē…Ē n…õ n…Ē…Ē ta yem, keŇč de ta tekaa y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ gbeŇčaw…Ē bela ya. 7Nawolo nyi akpaa Ňčolo da yem na, …Ē te tira …Ē wose lee nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ man. 8Nyi akpaa daa na Kristo ta yem na, kenaŇč na d…õ…õ l…õ…õ di nyi daa na e m…Ē d…õ…õ kyaa nyeedoŇč man. 9Da gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ gyuusuna Yesu Kristo lee yeŇč man, te …Ē be gyae waa yeŇč bela ya. Nawolo nyi yeŇč ba tekaa dana doŇč …Ē d…Ē…Ē bela ya. 10YeŇč keŇč …Ē ba yem n…õ, …Ē yeŇčae karatetee dokoloŇč na waa ko nombiakum…õ…õ n…Ē…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē n…õn…õ…õ keŇč …Ē kyaa nyeedoŇč man n…õ, …Ē kyae fa Wurubuar…õ. 11Mena ke m…Ē te kaboena nyi yaa k…õ…õ …õ wose nyi …õm…õ…õ m…Ē yeŇčae na …õ ka ko nombiakum…õ…õ n…Ē…Ē, te n…õn…õ…õ …õ kyae fa Wurubuar…õ. Nawolo nyi …õm…õ…õ na Kristo Yesu te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč.
12Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ na tekaa yela nombiakum…õ…õ kedi doŇč …õ wosenaane keŇč gyae ka yeŇč n…õ d…Ē…Ē, na …õ ke sila ab…Ē…Ē kum…õ…õ Ňčan …õm…õ…õ gbagba ne gyae t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ ya. 13∆ź na mo …õ wosenaane yenaŇč daale fa nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ ya. M…Ēna …õ mo …õ wose fa Wurubuar…õ Ňčgba balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē lese w…Ē lee yeŇč man k…ĒŇč nyeedoŇč man n…õ. ∆ź mo …õ wose pou fa Wurubuar…õ na waa mo Ňčon y…õ…õ kpene keŇč dei te ke doo na ke gb…õ…õ n…õ. 14Tekaboena nyi nombiakum…õ…õ dee di doŇč …õ d…Ē…Ē bela ya, nawolo nyi …õ ba tekaa …õ doo Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan t…õ…õ ya, m…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …õ d…ĒŇč bo Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ keŇč man.
