Ňäon lesena daa te …Ē kpila daa nyi dee gyu ke y…õ…õ …Ē kegyaebii. 22Ňäon moona …Ē nyiŇčmaa gyakaa de d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē moo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do de kon…Ē…Ēse man mo kyaŇč nae, na ke di adans…õ…õ nyi de y…õ…õ …Ē wui, te dee nyiŇč ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou …Ē be desina see fa daa n…õ.

english_text:
1I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful. 2If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad? 3The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy. 4At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you.
Forgiveness
5I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones. 6Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
7When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair. 8You should make them sure of your love for them.
9I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions. 10I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven. 11I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
12 When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way. 13But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
14I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume. 15-16In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death.
No one really has what it takes to do this work. 17A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.

english_text:
God's New Agreement
1Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them. 2But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand. 3 You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
4We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God. 5We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do. 6 He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
7 The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory. 8So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful? 9If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious? 10In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all. 11The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
12This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely. 13 We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away. 14The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
15When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over 16 with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord. 17The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free. 18 So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
translation_text:
1Nombia da ayakoŇč k…õŇča n…õ, nawolo nyi da bese yaa d…õ…õ k…õl…õ…õ da wose baa yaa? Yaa kaboena nyi d…õ…õ tela t…Ēne keŇč gyae ka lese daa wola n…õ k…Ēna Ňčon? Yaa d…õ…õ l…õ…õ t…Ēne keŇč gyae ka lese daa wola n…õ lee …õ gyaŇč, Ňčgba mena keŇč baale ne y…õ…õ n…õ? 2Tekaboena nyi d…õ…õ y…õ…õ mena ya, nawolo nyi …õm…õ…õ gbagba y…õna t…Ēne keŇč na lese de din…Ē…Ē wola, keŇč Kristo be Ňčmarase do de kon…Ē…Ēse man n…õ. Mena d…Ē…Ē …õm…õ…õ y…õna t…Ēne keŇč walaŇč kamas…õ waa kala na waa naa de din…Ē…Ē. 3Mena d…Ē…Ē …õm…õ…õ na wola la nyi, …õm…õ…õ y…õna Kristo t…Ēne keŇč be t…õ…õ de d…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč n…õ. Kei te y…õ…õ t…Ēne keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo nyiŇčmaa yakaa Ňčmarase ya. M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ feliŇč te ba moow…Ē Ňčmarase ke. Ka te y…õ…õ Ňčgba mena keŇč gyaŇčgbatena ne Ňčmarase gyakaa boya paŇčsapaŇčsa d…Ē…Ē n…õ ya, m…Ēna be Ňčmarase Ňča bo do deniwalaŇč kon…Ē…Ē man.
4D…õ…õ yako mena nombia k…õŇča pou nawolo nyi, d…õ…õ l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di lee Kristo yele man. 5Daa gbagba de doŇč man na, naf…Ē da tekaboena tom kei key…õ…õ ya. Nawolo nyi daa gbagba b…õ…õ tale y…õ…õ kolo de baam d…Ē…Ē ya. De doŇč pou lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč. 6Ňäon faa na daa doŇč te de kaa bese Wurubuar…õ n…Ē…Ēkebakesee w…Ēle keŇč somra. N…Ē…Ēkebakesee w…Ēle kei doŇč ta lee mmaraase Ňčan b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase see n…õ man ya, m…Ēna doŇč kenaŇč lee Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon gyaŇč. Nawolo nyi mmaraase Ňčan b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase see n…õ ne k…Ēna yeŇč. M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon d…Ē…Ē, Ňčon ne fa nyeedoŇč bo.
7Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē, be Ňčmarase Ňča bo gyakaa boya patalapatala d…Ē…Ē. Te debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo mmaraase Ňčan fa Ňčon Mosesi n…õ, Ňče k…ĒŇčae Wurubuar…õ gyoorobiiri man te ke yela Mosesi siagb…õ…õ Ňčalakeew…Ē keŇč Iseraetena te tale k…õ…õ …Ē siagb…õ…õ ya. M…Ēna mena keŇčalakee kenaŇč d…Ē…Ē, na ke n…Ē…Ē na yem. Mena d…Ē…Ē akpaa nyi Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan na mo yeŇč k…ĒŇč n…õ, k…ĒŇčae na Wurubuar…õ gyoorobiiri keŇč na, 8kenaŇč na Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon …Ē ne fa nyeedoŇč n…õ, wui gyoorobiiri be gyae ka kela kei yee? 9Nyi akpaa …Ēsom keŇč ne bu balaŇč f…Ē…Ē n…õ dana gyoorobiiri mena na, kenaŇč na …Ē som keŇč ne yele balaŇč gb…õ…õneŇč na tenee Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ, wui gyoorobiiri be gyae ka kela kenaŇč yee? 10Gyoorobiiri keŇč ke kyaa debaŇč kei n…õ d…Ē…Ē, te yeli gyoorobiiri keŇč naf…Ē ke kyaa gyaŇčgba keŇč n…õ te Ňčaalaa. 11Akpaa nyi keŇč ka te kyaa kekpaa ya n…õ k…ĒŇčaw…Ē na gyoorobiiri na, kenaŇč na keŇč gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa n…õ wui gyoorobiiri gyae ka bo kela ke.
12KeŇč de dana mena loo kegyakaa kei d…Ē…Ē, te d…õ…õ tale gy…Ē de kon…Ē…Ē kolosi. 13Daa be doo Ňčgba Mosesi Ňčon …Ē ba mo kolo wu …Ē siagb…õ…õ, na Iseraetena be nyiŇč naa …Ē siagb…õ…õ debaŇč keŇč keŇčalakee keŇč n…Ē…Ē na yem n…õ ya. 14Ke y…õ…õ anokoare ke nyi, na ba agyueŇčse ta toro ta na baa nyii Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan man ya. Te kaa lii na gy…õŇč kei n…õ, akpaa b…õ…õ kala Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan doo n…Ē…Ēkebakesee bene keŇč man n…õ, b…Ē…Ē ne nyii Ňče man ya. Na y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba kolo s…õ…õ wu…õ ba agyueŇčse d…Ē…Ē n…õ. Nawolo nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Kristo kel…õ…õdi kaageŇč ne gyae la ke tale gyuusu walaŇč agyueŇč, na …Ē kala Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan na, waa tale nyii Ňče man. 15Kaa lii na gy…õŇč kei koraŇč n…õ, akpaa Iseraetena de kala Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan na, b…Ē…Ē ne nyii Ňče man ya. Y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba kolo s…õ…õ wu…õ ba agyueŇčse d…Ē…Ē n…õ. 16M…Ēna akpaa nyi Ňčolo da mo …Ē wose fa de GbeŇčgyoo Kristo na, waa gyuusu kpene keŇč wu…õ walaŇč ŇčonaŇč agyueŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, na waa nyii …Ē nombia Ňčan man. 17Nawolo nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Kristo na Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon y…õ…õ dokoloŇč. Te bot…Ē…Ē keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo feliŇč Ňčon kyaa na, walaŇč ne nyiŇč …Ē wose lee mmaraase Ňčan t…õ…õ. 18Te daa baŇč kolo be wu…õ de siagb…õ…õ ya n…õ, d…õ…õ lese de GbeŇčgyoo gyoorobiiri keŇč wola. Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon ne ky…õ…õkee de din…Ē…Ē na ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba Kristo din…Ē…Ē n…õ, te debaŇč kamas…õ m…Ē na dee nyiŇč …Ē gyoorobiiri keŇč kpu. Mena nombia k…õŇča lee de GbeŇčgyoo Kristo gyaŇč. Ňäon m…Ē y…õna Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon.

english_text:
Treasure in Clay Jars
1God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up. 2We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted. 3If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
4The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like. 5We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants. 6 The Scriptures say, ‚ÄúGod commanded light to shine in the dark.‚ÄĚ Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
7We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us. 8We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up. 9In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again. 10-11We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us. 12This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
13In the Scriptures it says, ‚ÄúI spoke because I had faith.‚ÄĚ We have this same kind of faith. So we speak 14because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you. 15All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him.
Faith in the Lord
16We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day. 17These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing. 18Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
translation_text:
Wurubuar…õ doŇč doo Kristotena man
1Lee mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ be wii de waraŇčase, te …Ē ba mo tom kei do daa de nyiŇčmaa man d…Ē…Ē n…õ, d…Ē…Ē ne yee ya. 2Mena d…Ē…Ē te da lese da wose lee des…õŇč nombia man, te d…Ē…Ē ne y…õ…õ kolo m…Ē weese ya. D…Ē…Ē na mo adao gb…õ…õneŇč man mo kolo kyakale na Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan ya. M…Ēna d…õ…õ lese Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Ňče y…õ…õ anokoare n…õ man nideli, na walaŇč kamas…õ kagyeŇč nyi kpene keŇč d…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ, te y…õ…õ dekara Wurubuar…õ siaman ya. 3Te akpaa Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan y…õ…õ nombia aweeseŇč Ňča na, kenaŇč na Ňče y…õ…õ nombia aweeseŇč Ňča bo fa balaŇč baŇč baa yo n…õ baageŇč. 4Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē b…õ…õ l…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan di ya, nawolo nyi …Ēb…Ēnsam Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyoo e n…õ, ta mo w…Ē do ditiŇčt…õ…õ man. Mena d…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ē ne naa keŇčmaŇčee keŇč doo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan na lese …Ē gyoorobiiri wola n…õ ya. Te b…Ē…Ē ne naa, na baa gyeŇč nyi Kristo y…õna Wurubuar…õ din…Ē…Ē gbagba Ňčon ya. 5Nombia Ňčan dee kolosi fa Ňčon n…õ, te y…õ…õ daa gbagba da wose man nombia yaa dee kolosi fa Ňčon ya. M…Ēna Yesu Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan yaa dee kolosi fa Ňčon, nyi Ňčon y…õna de GbeŇčgyoo te Ňčon m…Ē d…Ē…Ē te d…õ…õ som Ňčon. 6Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be yako nyi, ‚ÄúKeŇčmaŇčee d…õ…õ lee ditiŇčt…õ…õ man ŇčmaŇčee n…õ,‚ÄĚ Ňčon ke yela la te keŇčmaŇčee ŇčmaŇčeew…Ē de kon…Ē…Ēse man, na ke yela d…õ…õ gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ gyoorobiiri keŇč doo Kristo man n…õ.
7M…Ēna daa baŇč Wurubuar…õ gyooro ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča doo de man n…õ, de y…õ…õ Ňčgba kpene keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo t…õ…õle ma ke n…õ. Na ka wola nyi doŇč dinaa keŇč doo de man n…õ, lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč. Ke ta lee daa gbagba gyaŇč ya. 8D…õ…õ naa diyem lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man, m…Ēna da seŇč…õ…õ keŇčkeŇč. DebaŇč Ňčaale man na da agyueŇč bee doo debodokoloŇč ya, m…Ēna da wose bee gbirisi daa ya. 9B…õ…õ naase daa diyem, m…Ēna Wurubuar…õ b…õ…õ lese …Ē sia yela daa ya. B…õ…õ gy…Ē daa l…Ē yeŇč yeŇč, m…Ēna d…Ē…Ē na yem ya. 10Wee na wee na d…õ…õ naa diyem yeŇč yeŇč da wose man, Ňčgba mena keŇč Yesu m…Ē be naa n…õ. Na kawola nyi nyeedoŇč keŇč Yesu dana n…õ, keŇč ke te daa m…Ē dana. 11Daa baŇč de kyaa na de sia n…õ, wee na wee na lee Yesu d…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ lese daa fa yeŇč. Na ke yela walaŇč kamas…õ kena nyi, Yesu nyeedoŇč keŇč doo daa baŇč da wosenaane kei gyae ka yeŇč n…õ m…Ē man. 12Lee mena d…Ē…Ē daa ne gyaŇčee na yeŇč nombia wee kamas…õ, na …õm…õ…õ kenyiŇč nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa.
13Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see yenaŇč daale ne yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nombia di d…Ē…Ē, te me kolosi …Ē nombia.‚ÄĚ Kel…õ…õdi dokoloŇč kenaŇč te daa m…Ē l…õ…õw…Ē di, mena d…Ē…Ē te dee kolosi …Ē nombia n…õ. 14Da gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be gyuusu de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu lee yeŇč man n…õ, waa ta Yesu d…Ē…Ē gyuusu daa na waa mo Ňčon kpu na daa kaa seŇč Ňčon Wurubuar…õ siaman. 15∆ź deeli d…Ē…Ē te nombia k…õŇča pou k…ĒŇčaw…Ē mena, na Ňčgba mena keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele balaŇč burum te …Ē ne l…õ…õ ba nyee n…õ, ke yela baa fane fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč nideli na baa lese …Ē yele.
16Lee mena d…Ē…Ē da wose b…õ…õ y…Ēk…Ēse daa ya. Nawolo nyi keŇč na da wosenaane k…õ…õ y…Ēk…Ēse kei koraŇč n…õ, de din…Ē…Ē w…Ēle keŇč d…Ē…Ē kee nyiŇč doŇč wee kamas…õ. 17Diyem keŇč d…õ…õ naa debaŇč kei n…õ, ka be gyae ke kyare ya na ka te lam. Ke gyae ke yela dee nyiŇč gyoorobiiri keŇč gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa, keŇč ka dokoso be kyaa ya te ka kela diyem keŇč d…õ…õ naa n…õ. 18Mena d…Ē…Ē, d…Ē…Ē na mo de loo gyakaa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan d…õ…õ naa na de sia fon…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē ya, m…Ēna d…õ…õ mo de loo bo gyakaa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan d…Ē…Ē ne naa na de sia ya n…õ d…Ē…Ē. Nawolo nyi ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan d…õ…õ mo de sia naa Ňča n…õ, Ňče b…õ…õ kyare ya na Ňča te lam. M…Ēna ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan d…Ē…Ē na mo de sia naa Ňča ya n…õ, Ňče gyae ke doo kekpaakekpaa.

english_text:
1 Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever. 2While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home. 3We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
4These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die. 5God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it. 6So always be cheerful!
As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord. 7But we live by faith, not by what we see. 8We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord. 9But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him. 10 After all, Christ will judge each of us for the good or the bad that we do while living in these bodies.
Bringing People to God
11We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are. 12We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
13If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good. 14We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died. 15And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
16We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way. 17Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new. 18God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
19What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace. 20We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God. 21Christ never sinned! But God treated him as a sinner, so Christ could make us acceptable to God.
translation_text:
Da dekyae w…Ēle Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč
1Da gyeŇč nyi t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē de kyaa n…õ, d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ kyare ya na de ta yem. M…Ēna akpaa da yem na, Wurubuar…õ waa fa daa wosenaane w…Ēle adido, keŇč ka te y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē wosenaane ke ya. Wurubuar…õ gbagba y…õ…õ na ke, te ke gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa. 2DebaŇč kei na d…õ…õ keketee da wose man, nawolo nyi d…õ…õ gyae nideli nyi Wurubuar…õ waa faa daa mena wosenaane w…Ēle kei. 3Nyi akpaa Wurubuar…õ da mo ke do daa na, d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ ta diteŇčteli ya. 4DebaŇč keŇč de s…õ…õ de doo wosenaane kei man n…õ, d…õ…õ yey…õ…õ Ňčgba kasol…Ē daale na Ňčere na daa n…õ. Na d…õ…õ yako baa nyi yeŇč yaa d…õ…õ gyae debokeiman ya. M…Ēna naf…Ē da kegyaebii y…õna nyi Wurubuar…õ waa mo wosenaane w…Ēle keŇč kyaa adido n…õ do daa. Na wosenaane w…Ēle keŇč kaa kyaa wosenaane bene keŇč na yem n…õ naw…Ē…Ē man. 5Wurubuar…õ gbagba desina na daa fa mena keky…õ…õkee kenaŇč, te …Ē moo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do de man. Ňäon te …Ē moow…Ē see ateta, mo kyaŇč ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou waa kaa y…õ…õ fa daa n…õ nae.
6Mena d…Ē…Ē debaŇč kamas…õ na da wose b…õ…õ y…Ēk…Ēse daa ya. Da gyeŇč nyi debaŇč keŇč de s…õ…õ de kyaa wosenaane kei man k…õbo n…õ, de ta gyoo ta bot…Ē…Ē keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu kyaa n…õ ya. 7Da ke kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ, nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ di n…õ Ňčan te de silana. Na ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan d…õ…õ naa na de sia n…õ, te de silana ya. 8Lee mena d…Ē…Ē de dana lookegyakaa nideli. Naf…Ē da kegyaebii y…õna nyi, d…õ…õ lee wosenaane kei man na de kaa kyaa de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu gyaŇč. 9Mena d…Ē…Ē nyi de kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē bo o, yaa da yeŇčae o, kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē d…õ…õ yase de sia y…õna nyi, d…õ…õ y…õ…õ kpene keŇč gyae ka gyoo de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu sia. 10Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi walaŇč kamas…õ waa k…ĒŇč kaa seŇč Kristo siaman na waa di …Ē nombia, na waa t…Ē walaŇč kamas…õ kom Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ētenate be y…õ…õ …Ē tom t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ. Dukum dana ka kom ket…Ē, te debam m…Ē dana ka kom ket…Ē.
Yesu d…Ē…Ē te daa na Wurubuar…õ dana d…Ē…Ēre
11Da gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ keyee kedoobii man d…Ē…Ē, te d…õ…õ do balaŇč kakyeŇč Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom man. Wurubuar…õ gyeŇč de din…Ē…Ē, te d…õ…õ l…õ…õdi nyi …õm…õ…õ m…Ē …õ gyeŇč de din…Ē…Ē lee …õ kon…Ē…Ēse man. 12Te y…õ…õ nyi da bese yaa d…õ…õ k…õl…õ…õ da wose bo fa Ňčon ya. M…Ēna d…õ…õ t…Ē…Ēse Ňčon nombia Ňčan gyae ke yela …õ ke tale gyina …õ wose adido lee daa d…Ē…Ē n…õ bo. Dek…ĒŇč mena na, …õ ke tale nyiŇč nombii yako balaŇč baŇč bee gyina ba wose adido lee ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan b…õ…õ mo be sia naa Ňča n…õ d…Ē…Ē, kela Ňčan Ňče doo deniwalaŇč kon…Ē…Ē man n…õ. 13Akpaa nombia k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē te …õ yako nyi da nyeeya ne w…Ēl…õ…õ na, Ňče w…Ēl…õe fa Wurubuar…õ. Te akpaa da nyeeya doo m…Ē na, kenaŇč na …õm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te da nyeeya doo. 14Kristo kegyae keŇč te di da nyee, nawolo nyi da gyeŇč nyi Ňčon l…õ…õ na balaŇč pou yeŇč yeŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba balaŇč pou yeŇča la n…õ. 15Kristo yeŇča la fa balaŇč pou, na ke yela baŇč be kyaa na be sia n…õ be nyiŇč kyaa fa ba wose ya. M…Ēna baa kyaa fa Yesu Kristo Ňčon …Ē ba yem fa w…Ē, te Wurubuar…õ be gyuusu e n…õ.
16Mena d…Ē…Ē lee n…õn…õ…õ ne gyu na, d…Ē…Ē ne k…õ…õ balaŇč Ňčgba mena keŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč ne k…õ…õ w…Ē n…õ ya. GyaŇčgba keŇč na, naf…Ē de k…õ…õ Kristo m…Ē mena. M…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē, d…Ē…Ē na tekaa k…õ…õ e mena bela ya. 17Lee mena d…Ē…Ē akpaa nyi Ňčolo de l…õ…õ Kristo di na, …Ē ta bese walaŇč w…Ēle. ∆Ü din…Ē…Ē bene keŇč te lam, te n…õn…õ…õ …Ē te nyiŇč din…Ē…Ē w…Ēle. 18Nombia k…õŇča pou lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč. Ňäon t…õ…õ la Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē desina daa na Ňčon Wurubuar…õ nsana, te …Ē faa daa tom nyi d…õ…õ yako …Ē nombia k…õŇča, na d…õ…õ mo desina Ňčon na balaŇč nsana. 19Nombii keŇč d…õ…õ yako Ňčon y…õna nyiaa, Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õ la Kristo d…Ē…Ē desina Ňčon na balaŇč pou nsana, te …Ē moo be nombiakum…õ…õ pou ky…õ…õ w…Ē. Te …Ē moo Ňčon na balaŇč nsana kedesina tom kei do daa de nyiŇčmaa man. 20Mena d…Ē…Ē daa y…õna Kristo kpilala baŇč da seŇč…õ…õ …Ē naw…Ē…Ē man, keŇč Wurubuar…õ ne t…õ…õ de d…Ē…Ē kolosi sola Ňčon. Da seŇč…õ…õ Kristo naw…Ē…Ē man d…õ…õ sola Ňčon nyi, …õ yela …õm…õ…õ na Wurubuar…õ nsana key…õ…õ gyoŇč. 21Kristo te y…õ…õ nombiikum kamas…õ ya. M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ yela …Ē kaa moo de nombiakum…õ…õ pou seele. Te …Ē yeŇčaw…Ē Ňčgba nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õre n…õ, na ke yela de nombia ka tenee Wurubuar…õ siaman lee Ňčon Kristo d…Ē…Ē.

english_text:
1We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace. 2 In the Scriptures God says,
‚ÄúWhen the time came,
I listened to you,
and when you needed help,
I came to save you.‚ÄĚ
That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
3We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems. 4But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times. 5 We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food. 6But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real. 7We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
8Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not. 9We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed, 10and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
11Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you. 12We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us. 13I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us.
The Temple of the Living God
14Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same? 15Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't? 16 Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says,
‚ÄúI will live with these people
and walk among them.
I will be their God,
and they will be
my people.‚ÄĚ
17 The Lord also says,
‚ÄúLeave them and stay away!
Don't touch anything
that isn't clean.
Then I will welcome you
18 and be your Father.
You will be my sons
and my daughters,
as surely as I am God,
the All-Powerful.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
1My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him.
The Church Makes Paul Happy
2Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone. 3I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die. 4I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
5 After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears. 6But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us. 7Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
8I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while. 9Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us. 10When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
11Just look what God has done by making you feel sorry! You sincerely want to prove you are innocent. You are angry. You are shocked. You are eager to see that justice is done. You have proved that you were completely right in this matter. 12When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us. 13And we were greatly encouraged.
Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about. 14We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true. 15Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him. 16It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.

KeŇč na da diyem keŇč d…õ…õ naa kei koraŇč n…õ, mena din…Ē…Ē kei na do maŇč kakyeŇč nideli te gyoŇč keŇč doo me man m…Ē n…õ be taŇč …Ēto ya.
5DebaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč Masedonia t…õ…õle man n…õ, de te nyiŇč da wose gba koraŇč d…õ…õ kyaa f…õ…õfo ya. BalaŇč naase daa diyem lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man. Baale korow…Ē seŇč tia daa, yela gyakoloŇč gyoo daa. 6M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē ne yako do balaŇč baŇč dek…Ēk…Ēr…Ēk…Ē…Ē be kyaŇč w…Ē n…õ, Ňčon t…õ…õ la Tituse Ňčon …Ē ba lee …õ gyaŇč n…õ d…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč kaa yako do daa m…Ē. 7Na Tituse kek…ĒŇč kaageŇč keŇč doo na daa kakyeŇč ya, m…Ēna kakyeŇč keŇč …õ ba do e n…õ m…Ē kpu…õ. Nawolo nyi …Ē yako daa mena keŇč me nombia ne gy…Ē Ňčon loo, na mena keŇč …õ ne wii maŇč, na mena keŇč …õ be desina …õ wose nyi …õ ke yoo seŇč me w…Ēle n…õ. Mena d…Ē…Ē te yeli me sia ta gye nideli.
8Me nyiiw…Ē nyi t…Ēne keŇč m…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase yelee Ňčon n…õ te f…õ…õ …õ w…Ēe ya. M…Ēna te takaa maŇč Ňčgba m…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase ke n…õ ya. Me naaw…Ē nyi t…Ēne kenaŇč man nombia takaa Ňčon ber…õ, mena d…Ē…Ē takaa maŇč m…Ē. M…Ēna ma gyeŇč nyi ka be gyae ke takaa Ňčon kekpaakekpaa ya. 9Lee mena d…Ē…Ē n…õn…õ…õ dana maŇč gyoŇč. Te y…õ…õ nyi keŇč t…Ēne keŇč man nombia be takaa Ňčon d…Ē…Ē ya, m…Ēna keŇč ka be yele …õ be ky…õ…õkee lee …õ gb…õ…õneŇč kum…õ…õ man d…Ē…Ē. Te yeli …õ ne gyae me nombia, te …õ loo gyakaa nyi …õ kena maŇč. Te yeli …õ te yase …õ sia …õ ke desina nombia ŇčenaŇč man, na …õ ke w…ĒŇč dukumy…õ…õre Ňčon deŇčele. Lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man na, …õ ta lese wola nyi …õ ba gyeŇč kolo lee nombiikum kei man ya.
12Mena t…Ēne keŇč m…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase yelee Ňčon n…õ, na walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be y…õ…õ dukum, yaa Ňčon b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ dukum tia e n…õ baageŇč d…Ē…Ē te me Ňčmarase ke ya. M…Ēna me Ňčmarase ke bo na Wurubuar…õ ke yela …õ ka gyeŇč lee …õm…õ…õ gbagba …õ kon…Ē…Ē man nyi, de nombia y…õ…õ …õ nombia Ňča Wurubuar…õ siaman. 13Mena ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča pou na do daa kakyeŇč.
Kei w…Ēle na, debaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē naa nyi Tituse sia ta gye lee mena keŇč …õ popou be ky…Ē e, te wa agyueŇč be y…õ…õ e dokoloŇč n…õ, y…õ…õ daa m…Ē gyoŇč nideli. 14Me k…õl…õ…õ Ňčon fa Tituse, te …õ te yeli des…õŇč ke kyaŇč maŇč ya. Mena keŇč kpene kamas…õ keŇč d…Ē…Ē yako Ňčon y…õ…õ ampaŇč n…õ, mena m…Ē te da ke k…õl…õ…õ Ňčon fa Tituse keŇč te kaa y…õ…õ ampaŇč. 15Akpaa …Ē k…õ…õ mena keŇč …õ be bu e, na mena keŇč …õ ba mo obuo l…õ…õ e n…õ, ne yeli …Ē ne gyae Ňčon nideli kpu. 16Dana maŇč gyoŇč nideli, nawolo nyi ma gyeŇč nyi maa tale mo ma wose l…Ē …õ d…Ē…Ē nideli.

english_text:
Generous Giving
1 My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace. 2Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously. 3They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to. 4They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people. 5And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
6Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun. 7You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
8I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown. 9You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
10A year ago you were the first ones to give, and you gave because you wanted to. So listen to my advice. 11I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving. 12It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
13I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair 14for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share, 15 just as the Scriptures say,
‚ÄúThose who gathered
too much
had nothing left.
Those who gathered
only a little
had all they needed.‚ÄĚ
Titus and His Friends
16I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do. 17When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you. 18With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news. 19The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help. 20We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift. 21 But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
22We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much. 23Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ. 24Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.

Kegyae keŇč …õ ne gyae nyi …õ ke fa n…õ, …õ mo mena kegyae kenaŇč fa kelii …Ēto lee …õ ketalebii man. 12Nyi akpaa Ňčolo ne gyae nyi waa fa kolo lee …Ē kon…Ē…Ē man na, kpene keŇč …Ē dana te …Ē na lese fa lee …Ē kon…Ē…Ē man n…õ, keŇč na gyoo na Wurubuar…õ sia. Na kpene keŇč …Ē be dana waa fa n…õ ya. 13Na da kegyaebii y…õna nyi baale wose d…õ…õ f…õ…õ w…Ē, na …õm…õ…õ wose kekyaŇč Ňčon ya. M…Ēna kpene keŇč d…õ…õ gyae y…õna nyi, …õ popou wose d…õ…õ f…õ…õ Ňčon ky…õ…õky…õ…õ. 14DebaŇč kei d…Ē…Ē …õm…õ…õ dana, mena d…Ē…Ē kaboena nyi yaa ky…Ē baŇč ba wose be kyaŇč w…Ē n…õ. Na wee daale …õm…õ…õ m…Ē wose de kyaŇč Ňčon na, baŇč m…Ē ke nyiŇč ky…Ē Ňčon. Dek…ĒŇč mena na, …õ popou wose gyae ke f…õ…õ Ňčon ky…õ…õky…õ…õ. 15Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see yakow…Ē lee ween…õ…õ Ňčan Wurubuar…õ be fa Iseraetena lee adido n…õ wose man nyiaa, ‚ÄúWalaŇč Ňčon …Ē be yilaa Ňča burum na, …Ē n…Ē…Ēn…Ē…Ēle te …Ē nyiŇčaw…Ē. P…Ē…Ēl kekpila Tituse Korinto don…Ē…Ē man
16M…õ…õ fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be yeli Tituse ne gyae …õ nombia, Ňčgba mena keŇč maŇč m…Ē ne gyae …õ nombia n…õ. 17Na keŇč d…Ē…Ē waase Tituse d…Ē…Ē te …Ē s…õ…õw…Ē nyi waa k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč ya. M…Ēna …õ nombia wulaa gy…Ē…Ē e loo, te …Ē wulaa y…õ…õ wa agyueŇč nyi waa k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč. 18Dee kpila de gyoo Kristobaale Ňčolo nyi waa kpu na Tituse na baa k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč. De gyoo ŇčonaŇč k…õ…õ t…õ…õ kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan d…Ē…Ē, te yeli …Ē te nyiŇč yele Kristotena pou man. 19AgyakaŇč y…õna nyi, Kristotena baŇč pou lese na e nyi waa kpu na daa na d…õ…õ ta l…õ…õ k…Ēbase Ňčan d…õ…õ l…õ…õ n…õ. Mena tom kei de d…ĒŇč…õ…õ d…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ, na lese Wurubuar…õ yele te k…õ…õ lese de kakyeŇč keŇč daa m…Ē na doo na d…õ…õ mo ky…Ē Ňčon n…õ wola. 20D…õ…õ k…õ…õ da wose d…Ē…Ē nideli keŇč nyi, Ňčolo be nyiŇč nombiikum daale kolosi lee da wose man, lee mena keŇč d…õ…õ mo tela mena k…Ēbase Ňčan …õ ne faa n…õ ya. 21Nawolo nyi da kegyaebii y…õna nyi, d…õ…õ y…õ…õ kpene kamas…õ ke gb…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē. De ta gyoo …Ē man naa nyi, gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man na …Ē dana kakyeŇč. ∆Ü sia te y…õ…õ nyi waa k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč na …Ē kaa ky…Ē Ňčon, nawolo nyi …Ē te l…õ…õ Ňčon di nideli. 23Tewul…õ…õ k…õw…Ē man na, Tituse y…õna walaŇč Ňčon daa na e ne y…õ…õ tom dokoloŇč som Ňčon. Tewul…õ…õ akaŇč baŇč d…Ē…Ē, Kristotena baŇč lese na w…Ē nyi be kaa seŇč be naw…Ē…Ē man, na baa lese Kristo yele. 24Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ lese …õ balaŇč kegyae keŇč wola w…Ē, na ke yela baa gyeŇč kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē dee gyina da wose adido lee …õ d…Ē…Ē n…õ, na Kristotena baŇč man balaŇč pou ke nyiŇč naa.

english_text:
The Money for God's People
1I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people. 2I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give. 3This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
4Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more. 5So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
6Remember this saying,
‚ÄúA few seeds make
a small harvest,
but a lot of seeds make
a big harvest.‚ÄĚ
7 Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give. 8God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others. 9 The Scriptures say,
‚ÄúGod freely gives his gifts
to the poor,
and always does right.‚ÄĚ
10 God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need. 11You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
12What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God. 13The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else. 14Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much. 15Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
translation_text:
1Lee toto keŇč …õ na doo nyi baa mo ky…Ē …õ tebia Kristotena n…õ d…Ē…Ē, naf…Ē tekaboena nyi maa Ňčmarase Ňčon kolo lee ka wose man bela ya. 2Nawolo nyi ma gyeŇč mena keŇč …õ ne y…õ…õ kakyeŇč nyi …õ ke ky…Ē …õ tebia lee …õ kon…Ē…Ē man. Mena d…Ē…Ē ne yeli m…õ…õ k…õl…õ…õ Ňčon fa Masedoniatena. M…õ…õ yako w…Ē nyi leenaŇč dewii te …õm…õ…õ baŇč …õ kyaa Akaya t…õ…õle man n…õ, desina …õ wose fa mena toto kei kado. KakyeŇč keŇč …õ dana …õ na do mena toto kei n…õ ta do balaŇč burum m…Ē kakyeŇč. 3Mee kpila tewul…õ…õ k…õw…Ē …õ gyaŇč na de k…õ…õ k…õl…õ…õ Ňčon keŇč n…õ be y…õ…õ yakaa ya. Nawolo nyi debaŇč kamas…õ na, m…õ…õ k…õl…õ…õ Ňčon fa Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Masedonia n…õ nyi …õm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …õ taŇč desina …õ wose see. 4Akpaa nyi …õ te desina …õ wose ya te Masedoniatena baale de sila maŇč k…ĒŇč na, baa kaa naa nyi me k…õ…õ k…õl…õ…õ Ňčon keŇč n…õ y…õ…õ yakaa. Dek…ĒŇč mena n…õ na daa daageŇč ne gyae la dee di des…õŇč ya, m…Ēna …õm…õ…õ m…Ē …õ sia gyae ka yeŇč. 5Mena d…Ē…Ē me k…õ…õ ke nyi, kaboena nyi maa kpila tewul…õ…õ k…õw…Ē na baa kyaŇč gb…õ…õ k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč na …õ ke nyiŇč yilaa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan …õ be yako see nyi …õ ke fa n…õ see. KenaŇč na akpaa me k…ĒŇč na be gyae ke y…õ…õ nyi m…õ…õ Ňčere Ňčon bo ya, m…Ēna …õm…õ…õ gbagba faa la lee …õ kon…Ē…Ē man.
6∆ź t…Ē…Ēse nyiaa, nyi akpaa Ňčolo te s…õ…õ Ňčmatee ab…Ē…Ē bia ka bo ya na, mena m…Ē te …Ē be gyae waa kara ka bo ya. M…Ēna akpaa Ňčolo de Ňčmatee ab…Ē…Ē bia burum na, mena m…Ē te waa kara burum. 7Mena keŇč walaŇč kamas…õ be yako see nyi waa fa lee …Ē kon…Ē…Ē man n…õ, waa fa mena. Te y…õ…õ nyi b…õ…õ Ňčere e bo ya. Nawolo nyi walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne booli …Ē sia fa kolo lee …Ē kon…Ē…Ē man na, Ňčon te Wurubuar…õ ne gyae. 8Wurubuar…õ waa tale mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč boele Ňčon, na waa yela …õ ke nyiŇč kpene kamas…õ keŇč …õ ne gyae debaŇč kamas…õ. Waa yela …õ ke nyiŇč gbaa, na …õ ka mo Ňčaale y…õ…õ deeli. 9Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see yakow…Ē yenaŇč daale nyiaa,
‚ÄúWurubuar…õ te gyuusu …Ē nyiŇčmaa …Ē ne fa ayematena ab…Ē…Ē.
∆Ü b…õr…õ…õ keŇč …Ē dana n…õ be gyae ke taŇč ya.‚ÄĚ
10Wurubuar…õ ne faa na w…Ē…Ēfarale ab…Ē…Ē duuluŇč, te Ňčon ke ne faa na daa ween…õ…õ. Mena d…Ē…Ē waa fa Ňčon ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan …õ ne gyae n…õ pou na waa yela Ňče ke wuku. Na …õ kenyiŇč y…õ…õ w…Ēe nideli fa ayematena. 11Wurubuar…õ waa yela …õ ke nyiŇč …õ wose nideli kpene kamas…õ man, na …õ ke tale y…õ…õ w…Ēe debaŇč kamas…õ. Te mena keŇč …õ ne t…õ…õ de d…Ē…Ē fa balaŇč ab…Ē…Ē n…õ, gyae ke yela balaŇč ke fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč.
12Nawolo nyi mena w…Ēe ya aney…õ…õŇč kei n…õ, te yeli Kristotena burum te nyiŇč kpene keŇč b…õ…õ gyae, te b…õ…õ lese Wurubuar…õ yele nideli. 13∆ź ta lese …õ din…Ē…Ē wola lee …õ deeli key…õ…õ keŇč man. ∆ź deeli key…õ…õ keŇč gyae kawola nyi na Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan ŇčaageŇč te …õ l…õ…õw…Ē di ya, m…Ēna balaŇč baa naa nyi …õ ne di Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē. Lee mena d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baa lese Wurubuar…õ yele. 14Baa fane fa Ňčon nideli lee be kon…Ē…Ē man. Nawolo nyi b…õ…õ naa Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ dinaa keŇč doo …õ man n…õ. 15∆ź yela d…õ…õ do Wurubuar…õ n…Ē…Ē, nawolo nyi …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč …Ē ba mo boele denibalaŇč n…õ ka dokoso be kyaa ya.

english_text:
Paul Defends His Work for Christ
1Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle. 2Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way. 3We live in this world, but we don't act like its people 4or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments 5and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ. 6And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
7You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do. 8Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag. 9And I am not trying to scare you with my letters. 10Some of you are saying, ‚ÄúPaul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.‚ÄĚ 11Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
12We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves. 13We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work. 14We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
15We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal. 16Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done. 17 The Scriptures say, ‚ÄúIf you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.‚ÄĚ 18You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
translation_text:

1MaŇč P…Ē…Ēl gbagba ne waase na Ňčon nombii kei d…Ē…Ē. ∆ź man baale ne yako nyi akpaa me kyaa …õ gyaŇč na m…Ē…Ē dana n…Ē…ĒdoŇč ya. M…Ēna akpaa ma lee …õ gyaŇč na, me n…Ē…ĒdoŇč kela kpene kamas…õ. M…õ…õ mo de GbeŇčgyoo Kristo kon…Ē…Ē kenyiŇč na …Ē wose key…Ēk…Ēse m…õ…õ sola Ňčon nyiaa, 2akpaa me k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč na …õ na yeli maa mo sibiiyam kolosi na Ňčon, Ňčgba mena keŇč maa kolosi na baŇč b…õ…õ yako nyi d…õ…õ y…õ…õ da ab…Ē…Ē bo Ňčgba t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč n…õ ya.

7∆źm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …õ ne k…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē bo adido adido. Nawolo nyi …Ē moo …Ē doŇč kenaŇč bo fa daa, na ke yela …õ kel…õ…õdi keŇč ka bee. M…Ēna te y…õ…õ nyi ka ke w…Ēl…õ…õ Ňčon bo ya. 9M…Ē…Ē ne gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčon Ňčgba, m…õ…õ mo me t…Ēna keŇčmarase Ňčan bo Ňčmaase Ňčon n…õ ya. 10Nawolo nyi balaŇč baale ne yako nyiaa, me t…Ēna Ňčan m…õ…õ Ňčmarase n…õ dana doŇč, te Ňče man nombia m…Ē dana yuŇč. M…Ēna akpaa maŇč gbagba kyaa …õ gyaŇč na, me n…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ y…õ…õ doŇč ya te ma kekolosi m…Ē y…õ…õ yakaa. 11Kaboena nyi mena balaŇč benaŇč baa gyeŇč nyiaa, mena keŇč de kyaa lem te d…õ…õ Ňčmarase t…Ēna yelee Ňčon n…õ, akpaa de kyaa …õ gyaŇč koraŇč na mena ke te d…õ…õ y…õ…õ.
12Kegbagba na, d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ taŇč mo da wose maa na balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ k…õl…õ…õ ba wose n…õ ya. ∆ź k…õ…õ be diyiŇč nombia. Be Ňčmarasew…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa Ňčolo ne gyina …Ē wose adido na, kaboena nyi waa gyina …Ē wose adido lee kpene keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ be y…õ…õ fa e n…õ d…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ 18Nawolo nyi na walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne k…õl…õ…õ …Ē wose n…õ te Wurubuar…õ ne l…õ…õ e do ya, m…Ēna walaŇč Ňčon Wurubuar…õ ne k…õl…õ…õ e n…õ, te …Ē ne l…õ…õ e do.

english_text:
Paul and the False Apostles
1Please put up with a little of my foolishness. 2I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ. 3 But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way. 4We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message. 5I think I am as good as any of those super apostles. 6I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
7Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge? 8I robbed other churches by taking money from them to serve you. 9 Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden. 10As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this. 11And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
12I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing. 13Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ. 14And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light. 15So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve.
Paul's Sufferings for Christ
16I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little. 17When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord. 18Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I. 19And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool. 20In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face. 21I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way.
If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do. 22Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I. 23 Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
24 Five times my own people gave me 39 lashes with a whip. 25 Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea. 26 During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
27I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm. 28Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches. 29When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
30If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am. 31God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever! 32 The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me. 33But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.

english_text:
Visions from the Lord
1I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me. 2I know about one of Christ's followers who was taken up into the third heaven 14 years ago. I don't know if the man was still in his body when it happened, but God certainly knows.
3As I said, only God really knows if this man was in his body at the time. 4But he was taken up into paradise, where he heard things too wonderful to tell. 5I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
6Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say. 7Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
8Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away. 9But he replied, ‚ÄúMy gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.‚ÄĚ So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am. 10Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong.
Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
11I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles. 12When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle. 13You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
14I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children. 15So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much? 16You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you. 17Were you cheated by any of those I sent to you? 18I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
19Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
20I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob. 21I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
translation_text:

Nawolo nyi n wose key…Ēk…Ēse man te nyaŇč kena me doŇč.‚ÄĚ Mena d…Ē…Ē dana maŇč gyoŇč nyi m…õ…õ wola ma wose bo lee ma wose key…Ēk…Ēse man, na Kristo doŇč keŇč kenyiŇč doo me man. 10Mena d…Ē…Ē lee Kristo d…Ē…Ē n…õ, wose key…Ēk…Ēse man bo o tuubia man bo o, diyem kena man yaa diyem kenaase man yaa kaale man bo koraŇč na, ma wose ne f…õ…õ maŇč. Nawolo nyi ma wose key…Ēk…Ēse man te m…õ…õ naa Kristo doŇč.

11Me ta bese diyiŇčte, m…Ēna …õm…õ…õ yele la te ma bese diyiŇčte. ∆źm…õ…õ te kaboena nyi naf…Ē yaa k…õl…õ…õ maŇč. Nawolo nyi ma te y…õ…õ kolo e ma wose d…Ē…Ē koraŇč ya na, m…Ē…Ē doo balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ baake ba wose nyi be y…õ…õ Kristo kpilalaw…Ē n…õ t…õ…õ lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man ya. 12Ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou kaboena nyi walaŇč waa y…õ…õ na kawola nyi …Ē y…õ…õ Kristo kpilale e n…õ, Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē me d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ Ňča …õ man. Tituse kaa l…õ…õ …õ nyeeman kolo bo di yaa? Na agyueŇč dokoloŇč te daa na e dana yee? Yaa na gb…õ…õ dokoloŇč d…Ē…Ē te daa na e d…ĒŇč yee?
19∆źm…õ…õ gyeŇč nyi da seŇč…õ…õ baa dee tira de nombia k…õŇča n…Ē…Ē na dee di deŇčele? Koa te y…õ…õ mena ya. Wurubuar…õ ne naa daa nyi Kristo kegyaebii man yaa dee kolosi n…õ. Me tebia kpaakpaa, lee …õ deeli d…Ē…Ē te de kolosi nombia k…õŇča pou mena n…õ. 20Me w…Ēe ne l…Ē maŇč nyi akpaa me k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč na, m…Ē…Ē gyae maa naa Ňčon nyi …õ kyaa Ňčgba mena keŇč m…õ…õ gyae n…õ ya, te …õm…õ…õ m…Ē be gyae …õ kena me din…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč …õ ne gyae nyi …õ kena ke n…õ ya. Me w…Ēe ne l…Ē maŇč nyi mberem na kale nombia, na sibii na baŇč na yoo nombia na balaŇč kew…Ēl…õ…õ, na wose kawola na nombia nkaraŇčkaraŇč key…õ…õ gyae ke doo …õ man. 21Me w…Ēe ne l…Ē maŇč nyi akpaa me k…ĒŇč bela na, Wurubuar…õ waa y…Ēk…Ēse maŇč …õ siaman na me t…Ē…Ē ke kyoŇčii maŇč lee …õ man baale nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan b…õ…õ y…õ…õ, te b…Ē…Ē ne gyae baa ky…õ…õkee lee Ňče man ya n…õ d…Ē…Ē. Me w…Ēe ne l…Ē maŇč nyi be s…õ…õ b…õ…õ y…õ…õ kafoŇč na nombia nkaraŇčkaraŇč, te b…Ē…Ē ne gyae baa ky…õ…õkee lee be nombiakum…õ…õ ŇčenaŇč man k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč ya.

english_text:
Final Warnings and Greetings
1 I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúAny charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.‚ÄĚ 2During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning. 3This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful! 4Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
5Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed. 6I hope you will discover we have not failed. 7We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
8All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it. 9Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better. 10I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
11Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other.
Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you. 12Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
13I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will bless you and be kind to you! May God bless you with his love, and may the Holy Spirit join all your hearts together.
translation_text:
1Kei y…õna betabotoorote Ňčgba m…õ…õ k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč n…õ. M…õ…õ gyae …õ ke t…Ē…Ēse nombii keŇč doo Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see yenaŇč daale n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúP…õna …õ ke bu Ňčolo f…Ē…Ē na, kaboena nyi yaa nyii lee balaŇč bala, yaa balaŇč batooro n…Ē…Ēman nyi nombii keŇč y…õ…õ ampaŇč.‚ÄĚ 2Ma kek…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč bolate keŇč n…õ, me wulaa faree balaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ kaalaŇč n…õ, na balaŇč akaŇč baŇč pou. DebaŇč kei keŇč m…Ē…Ē kyaa …õ gyaŇč ya n…õ, mena yaa me gyakaa m…õ…õ faree Ňčon nyiaa, akpaa ma bese k…ĒŇč na, m…Ē…Ē gyae maa mo baŇč be s…õ…õ b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ n…õ wose ky…õ…õ w…Ē ya. 3∆źm…õ…õ kpa …õ ne gyae …õ kena nyi Kristo ne t…õ…õ la me d…Ē…Ē kolosi n…õ, …õ kena ke. ∆ź kena nyi Kristo ba wo…õ keŇč …Ē be gyae waa w…ĒŇč …õ deŇčele ya. ∆Ü dana doŇč …õ d…Ē…Ē. 4Ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč nyi be gy…Ē…Ē e mataa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē, Ňčgba walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be taŇč doŇč ya n…õ. M…Ēna debokeiman d…Ē…Ē, …Ē te fom lee yeŇč man te …Ē kyaa Wurubuar…õ doŇč man. Mena m…Ē te daa m…Ē be taŇč doŇč ya. M…Ēna keŇč de doo Kristo man d…Ē…Ē, Wurubuar…õ doŇč doo de man te de dana d…õ…õ som Ňčon.
5∆ź gyoo …õ wose man nideli na …õ ke k…õ…õ nyi …õ ne l…õ…õ Yesu di nideli? Yaa …õ ba gyeŇč nyi …õm…õ…õ na Yesu Kristo y…õ…õ dokoloŇč yee? Akpaa …õ ba gyeŇč mena ya na, kenaŇč na …õ te yale …õ kel…õ…õdi man. 6M…õ…õ l…õ…õ di nyi …õ ka gyeŇč nyi, daa d…Ē…Ē da te yala ta da kel…õ…õdi man ya. 7D…õ…õ fane fa Wurubuar…õ nyiaa, …Ē na yeli …õ ke y…õ…õ kolo kum ya. Te y…õ…õ keŇč nyi balaŇč ke k…õl…õ…õ daa nyi da te y…õ…õ daale d…Ē…Ē ya, m…Ēna akpaa dek…ĒŇč nyi da te yale da kel…õ…õdi man koraŇč na, d…õ…õ gyae bo nyi …õ ke y…õ…õ nombia Ňčan ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ. 8Nawolo nyi d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ tale lee nombii na anokoare keŇč w…Ēle ya. Ke man te da seŇč…õ…õ debaŇč kamas…õ. 9Nyi akpaa daa de y…Ēk…Ēse te …õm…õ…õ seŇč…õ…õ keŇčkeŇč …õ kel…õ…õdi man na, dana daa gyoŇč. Nawolo nyi d…õ…õ fane bo nyi yaa tale seŇč keŇčkeŇč …õ kel…õ…õdi man Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii man. 10Mena d…Ē…Ē te de kyaa lem na lem te m…õ…õ Ňčmarase t…Ēne kei mee yelee Ňčon n…õ. KenaŇč na akpaa me k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč na, m…Ē…Ē gyae maa mo doŇč keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu be fa maŇč n…õ kolosi na Ňčon akpanyaŇčnyaŇč man ya. Nawolo nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ faa maŇč doŇč kei bo, nyi maa mo ky…Ē Ňčon na …õ ka bee …õ kel…õ…õdi man. ∆Ü te fa maŇč ke bo nyi maa mo w…Ēl…õ…õ Ňčon ya.
11Me tebia, me n…Ē…Ēwoya …Ēto y…õna nyiaa, …õ kyaa nideli. ∆ź yase …õ sia na …õ ke kyaa nideli kelii …Ēto, na …õ ke tei nombia Ňčan m…Ē…Ē yako Ňčon n…õ. ∆ź y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč na …õ ke kyaa wosef…õ…õreŇč man. Na Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae balaŇč te …Ē y…õ…õ wosef…õ…õreŇč GbeŇčgyoo e n…õ ke kyaa na Ňčon. 12∆ź mo Kristo balaŇč kegyae keŇč do d…ĒŇča n…Ē…Ē lee …õ kon…Ē…Ē man. 13Wurubuar…õ balaŇč baŇč pou be kyaa k…õbo n…õ na do Ňčon n…Ē…Ē. 14De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo waa mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele Ňčon, na Wurubuar…õ kegyae keŇč ke doo …õ man, na Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon ke yela …õ ke y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč.

english_text:
1-2From the apostle Paul and from all the Lord's followers with me.
I was chosen to be an apostle by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised him from death. No mere human chose or appointed me to this work.
To the churches in Galatia:
3I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! 4Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world. 5God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen.
The Only True Message
6I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message, 7when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ. 8I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven. 9I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
10I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ.
How Paul Became an Apostle
11My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach. 12It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
13 You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it. 14 I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us. 15 But even before I was born, God had chosen me by his gift of undeserved grace and had decided 16to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone. 17I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
18 Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for 15 days. 19The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother. 20And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
21Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia. 22But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person. 23They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy. 24And because of me, they praised God.
translation_text:
1T…Ēne kei lee maŇč P…Ē…Ēl gyaŇč. Me y…õ…õ Yesu Kristo kpilale e keŇč na deniwalaŇč Ňčolo lese na maŇč ya, te na deniwalaŇč Ňčolo m…Ē kpila na maŇč ya. M…Ēna Yesu Kristo gbagba, na de kya Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be gyuusu Yesu lee yeŇč man n…õ lese na maŇč te …Ē kpila maŇč. 2M…õ…õ Ňčmarase ke mee yelee …õm…õ…õ Kristotena akpii akpii Ňčan pou …õ kyaa Galatia t…õ…õle man n…õ. Daa na me tebia Kristotena baŇč pou be kyaa me gyaŇč k…õbo n…õ na do Ňčon n…Ē…Ē.
3De kya Wurubuar…õ na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be b…õr…õ…õ boele Ňčon, na baa fa Ňčon wosef…õ…õreŇč. 4De nombiakum…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te Yesu Kristo moo …Ē wose fa yeŇč, na waa lese daa lee gy…õŇč duuluŇč kei nombiakum…õ…õ man. Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ de kya e n…õ, kegyaebii man te nombia k…õŇča pou k…ĒŇčaw…Ē mena. 5Ňäon Wurubuar…õ te kaboena nyi d…õ…õ lese …Ē yele kekpaakekpaa. Am…õn.
Nombii w…Ēle daale be kyaa Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan w…Ēle ya
6∆źm…õ…õ Galatiatena, …õ nombia dana maŇč gyakoloŇč gbaa. ∆źm…õ…õ baŇč Wurubuar…õ be t…õ…õ Kristo b…õr…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē baake Ňčon n…õ, …õ k…õ…õ mena keŇč …õ be barase lee …Ē nombia Ňčan w…Ēle. N…õn…õ…õ …õ te gyu …õ silana kawola w…Ēle keŇč balaŇč ne dii ak…õr…õ…õ de man nyi Ňče y…õ…õ Kristo nombia Ňča n…õ. 7N k…õ…õ gbagba na, mena kawola kenaŇč ta lee Kristo gyaŇč korakora ya. M…Ēna balaŇč baale ne w…Ēl…õ…õ na ya agyueŇč. B…õ…õ gyae bo nyi baa tulii Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan. 8M…Ēna akpaa nyi daa gbagba, yaa Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo koraŇč da lee adido k…ĒŇč kaa kolosi nombia w…Ēl…õ…õ Ňčaale kpoo fa Ňčon lee Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan daa be wulaa kolosi fa Ňčon n…õ w…Ēle na, …Ētenate d…õ…õ laako. Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ waa w…Ēl…õ…õ e kekpaakekpaa. 9De te wulaa yako Ňčon mena nombia k…õŇča. M…Ēna ma bese yaa m…õ…õ yako Ňčon bela nyiaa, akpaa Ňčolo de k…ĒŇč kaa yako Ňčon nombia w…Ēl…õ…õ Ňčaale bela lee Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan daa be yako Ňčon n…õ w…Ēle na, Wurubuar…õ waa w…Ēl…õ…õ …Ētenate kekpaakekpaa.
10Me k…õ…õ yako Ňčon nombia k…õŇča na, nawolo baa nyi m…õ…õ gyae nyi denibalaŇč ke k…õl…õ…õ maŇč yaa? Koa, na mena ya. Yaa m…õ…õ gyae baa nyi maa y…õ…õ denibalaŇč kegyaebii? Nyi akpaa me s…õ…õ m…õ…õ gyae bo nyi denibalaŇč ke k…õl…õ…õ maŇč na, kenaŇč na naf…Ē ma te y…õ…õ Kristo tobaale e ya.
Mena keŇč P…Ē…Ēl ba bese Kristo kpilale
11Me tebia m…õ…õ gyae …õ ka gyeŇč nyiaa, Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan m…Ē…Ē kolosi fa Ňčon n…õ te y…õ…õ nombia Ňčan denibalaŇč be kyaa bake see ya. 12Na deniwalaŇč Ňčolo moona mena nombia k…õŇča do maŇč me nyiŇčmaa man ya, te na deniwalaŇč Ňčolo m…Ē wola na maŇč mena nombia k…õŇča ya. M…Ēna Yesu Kristo lese na mena nombia k…õŇča wola maŇč.
13∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyeŇč me din…Ē…Ē debaŇč keŇč me doo Gyudatena …Ēsom keŇč man n…õ. Me dii ba mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē nideli, te me naase Kristotena baŇč Wurubuar…õ ba lese w…Ē n…õ diyem akpanyaŇčnyaŇč man, te me gyae gb…õ…õ nyi naf…Ē maa w…Ēl…õ…õ nombia Ňčan be silana n…õ. 14Me yase me sia kasee Gyudatena …Ēsom mmaraase Ňčan nideli gyu siaman, kela me tebia Gyudatena baŇč daa na w…Ē y…õ…õ akoso n…õ pou. Ma moo ma nyee pou do de naana…Ē beneŇč nombia Ňčan key…õ…õ man.
15M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč d…Ē…Ē wii me waraŇčase, te …Ē baake maŇč nyi me kaa y…õ…õ …Ē tom. ∆Ü wulaa lese maŇč see nyi me kaa bese …Ē tobaale p…õte ba lola maŇč. 16Lee Ňčon Wurubuar…õ gbagba kegyae man d…Ē…Ē te …Ē baake maŇč te …Ē lese …Ē bu Yesu wola maŇč. Na maa nyiŇč kolosi …Ē bu Yesu nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan fa balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ. Wurubuar…õ ke baake maŇč keŇč n…õ, me te gyu Ňčolo gyaŇč nyi waa fa maŇč agyueŇč lee nombia k…õŇča man ya. 17Mena ke m…Ē te me te gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man nyi me kaa naa Kristo kpilala gyaŇčgba nyibaŇč baŇč nyi baa fa maŇč agyueŇč lee nombia k…õŇča man ya. M…Ēna ma korow…Ē karatetee dokoloŇč gyu Arebia t…õ…õle man, p…õte ma besew…Ē k…ĒŇč Damasekuse don…Ē…Ē man.
18KulutooneŇčse atooro w…Ēle n…õ, me gyuu Pita gyaŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man, te me kyaaw…Ē …Ē gyaŇč weeya kufu anoŇč. 19Me ke gyu bot…Ē…Ē n…õ, ma te naa Kristo kpilala baŇč man Ňčolo ya gyis…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu …Ē naabu Gyemisi waageŇč. 20Wurubuar…õ gbagba gyeŇč nyi nombia Ňčan m…õ…õ Ňčmarase n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč.
21KenaŇč w…Ēle na te me gyuu Siria na Silisia t…õ…õlese d…Ē…Ē. 22Mena debaŇč kenaŇč na, Kristotena akpii akpii Ňčan pou be kyaa Gyudia t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē n…õ ba gyeŇč maŇč gbagba ya. 23Kpene keŇč bee nyii y…õna nyiaa, ‚ÄúBalee Ňčon gyaŇčgba …Ē ne naase daa diyem n…õ, n…õn…õ…õ Ňčon d…ĒŇč…õ…õ la …Ē ne kolosi Kristo kel…õ…õdi keŇč naf…Ē …Ē be gyae gb…õ…õ nyi waa w…Ēl…õ…õ ke n…õ wose man nombia n…õ.‚ÄĚ 24Mena d…Ē…Ē ba lese Wurubuar…õ yele lee mena keŇč …Ē be ky…õ…õkee maŇč n…õ d…Ē…Ē.

english_text:
1 Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus. 2But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
3Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised. 4We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves. 5But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
6 Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message. 7They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews. 8God, who had sent Peter on a mission to the Jews, was now using me to preach to the Gentiles.
9James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews. 10They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do.
Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
11When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong. 12He used to eat with Gentile followers of the Lord, until James sent some Jewish followers. Peter was afraid of the Jews and soon stopped eating with Gentiles. 13He and the others hid their true feelings so well that even Barnabas was fooled. 14But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said:
Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
15We are Jews by birth and are not sinners like Gentiles. 16 But we know that God accepts only those who have faith in Jesus Christ. No one can please God by simply obeying the Law. So we put our faith in Christ Jesus, and God accepted us because of our faith.
17When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't! 18But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first. 19It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God.
I have been nailed to the cross with Christ. 20I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me. 21I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
translation_text:
Kristo kpilala baŇč kel…õ…õ P…Ē…Ēl do ba wose man
1KulutooneŇč kufu anaara w…Ēle n…õ, ma besew…Ē gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man. Me ke gyu kei na, daa na Banabase na Tituse gyuu la. 2Wurubuar…õ lese la wola maŇč nyi maa gyu Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē man bot…Ē…Ē mena d…Ē…Ē te me gyuuw…Ē. De ke gyu bot…Ē…Ē na, d…õ…õ kyaa gyaŇčee na Kristotena kegyiise baŇč baageŇč bo. De gyaŇčee na, m…õ…õ lese Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan me d…ĒŇč mee kolosi fa balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ man wola w…Ē, na daa na w…Ē ke y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč. Nawolo nyi ma te gyae nyi tom keŇč m…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ kaalaŇč, na keŇč me gyakaa m…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ ke y…õ…õ yakaa ya. 3Me d…Ē…Ē Tituse Ňčon …Ē silana maŇč n…õ, na …Ē y…õ…õ Giriki baale e. M…Ēna Ňčolo na Ňčolo ta Ňčere e nyi waa kara …Ē baala wose p…õna baa l…õ…õ e do nyi …Ē y…õ…õ Kristo tobaale e ya. 4BalaŇč baale beo la mo ba wose nyi be y…õ…õ Kristotenaw…Ē te be kaa kpuuw…Ē de man. BaŇč gyae la nyi naf…Ē baa kara e …Ē baala wose. Mena balaŇč benaŇč w…õ…õlee la kaa gyoo de man, te b…õ…õ gyae nyi baa w…Ēl…õ…õ da wosekenyiŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē nyim lee de GbeŇčgyoo Kristo man n…õ. WoŇč d…Ē…Ē te n…õn…õ…õ n na Ňčere balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ nyi baa di Gyudatena mmaraase d…Ē…Ē?‚ÄĚ
15Lolo man na daa na neŇč pou y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē. Da te y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē, te balaŇč ne baake w…Ē nyi be y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õraw…Ē n…õ ya. 16Daa Gyudatena baŇč de y…õ…õ Kristotenaw…Ē n…õ d…Ē…Ē, da gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ b…õ…õ l…õ…õ walaŇč do nyi …Ē gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Ňčon Wurubuar…õ siaman nyi keŇč …Ē ne di mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ d…Ē…Ē ya, m…Ēna walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di na te Wurubuar…õ ne l…õ…õ e do. Mena d…Ē…Ē daa m…Ē te l…õ…õ Yesu di na Wurubuar…õ ke nyiŇč l…õ…õ daa do nyi de gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ …Ē siaman. Na te y…õ…õ nyi da mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē kedi d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ l…õ…õ daa do ya. Nawolo nyi naf…Ē akpaa Wurubuar…õ ne l…õ…õ balaŇč bo do nyi be gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ …Ē siaman mo lee mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē kedi d…Ē…Ē na, kenaŇč na Kristo yeŇč keŇč …Ē ba yem n…õ be dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya.

english_text:
Faith Is the Only Way
1You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you? 2I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him? 3How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you? 4Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing? 5God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
6 The Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith. 7 And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham. 8 Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him. 9This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
10 Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, ‚ÄúEveryone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.‚ÄĚ 11 No one can please God by obeying the Law. The Scriptures also say, ‚ÄúThe people God accepts because of their faith will live.‚ÄĚ
12 The Law isn't based on faith. It promises life only to people who obey its commands. 13 But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse. 14And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit.
The Law and the Promise
15My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement. 16That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ. 17 What I am saying is that the Law cannot change or cancel God's promise made 430 years before the Law was given. 18 If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
19What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people. 20There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him.
Slaves and Children
21Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law. 22But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
23The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith. 24In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God. 25But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
26All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus. 27And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes. 28Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman. 29 So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.

Akpaa nyi mmaraase Ňčaale kyaa keŇč gyae ke tale fa walaŇč nyeedoŇč na, kenaŇč na naf…Ē walaŇč de di Ňče d…Ē…Ē na, …Ētenate gb…õ…õneŇč gyae ka tenee Wurubuar…õ siaman. 22M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see ne yako nyiaa, dukum te di t…õ…õle kei balaŇč pou d…Ē…Ē mena d…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ē ne tale baa di mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē ya. Lee mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ yakow…Ē see nyiaa, balaŇč baŇč pou baa l…õ…õ Yesu Kristo di na, baa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč …Ē be yako see nyi waa fa balaŇč n…õ.
D…Ē…Ē tekaa y…õ…õ mmaraa keŇč gbeŇčaw…Ē bela ya
23P…õte Yesu Kristo ke k…ĒŇč na, Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan te t…Ē daa do Ňčgba mena keŇč b…õ…õ t…Ē walaŇč do deni man n…õ. KenaŇč w…Ēle na te Wurubuar…õ kaa lesew…Ē wola daa nyiaa, nyi walaŇč de l…õ…õ Yesu Kristo di na waa tale nyiŇč …Ē wose lee mmaraase Ňčan t…õ…õ. 24Kei nawolo baa nyi, na Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan ne diina de d…Ē…Ē p…õte Kristo k…ĒŇčaw…Ē. Kristo ke k…ĒŇč w…Ēle na, lee Ňčon Kristo kel…õ…õdi man te walaŇč gb…õ…õneŇč na tenee Wurubuar…õ siaman. 25N…õn…õ…õ keŇč d…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Kristo di d…Ē…Ē, mmaraase Ňčan ba tekaa dana doŇč de d…Ē…Ē bela ya.
26Lee mena keŇč …õ be l…õ…õ Yesu Kristo di d…Ē…Ē, te …õ popou bese Wurubuar…õ bia gbagba n…õ. 27Nawolo nyi …õm…õ…õ baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē s…Ē Ňčon Wurubuar…õ loŇč Kristo man n…õ, te yeli …õm…õ…õ na Kristo ta bese walaŇč dokoloŇč. Lee mena d…Ē…Ē Kristo din…Ē…Ē keŇč te kaa doo …õ man. 28Lee mena d…Ē…Ē n y…õ…õ Gyuda baale o, n te y…õ…õ Gyuda baale ya o, n y…õ…õ gbeŇči o yaa n y…õ…õ gyooro bu e, n y…õ…õ alo e yaa n y…õ…õ baale o n…õ, keŇč be famen…õ bela ya. Nawolo nyi …õ popou te y…õ…õ dokoloŇč Kristo man. 29Nyi akpaa …õ y…õ…õ Kristo balaŇčw…Ē na, kenaŇč na …õ y…õ…õ Aberaham duuluŇč man balaŇčw…Ē. Mena ke m…Ē te Wurubuar…õ waa hiraa Ňčon, Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be yako see nyi waa hiraa Aberaham n…õ.

english_text:
1Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs. 2This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set. 3This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
4But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law, 5 so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children. 6 Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father. 7You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised.
Paul's Concern for the Galatians
8Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real. 9But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers? 10You even celebrate certain days, months, seasons, and years. 11I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
12My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me 13when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick. 14My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself. 15Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me. 16Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
17Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention. 18It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you. 19My children, I am in terrible pain until Christ may be seen living in you. 20I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled.
Hagar and Sarah
21Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says? 22 In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free. 23The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
24All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave. 25Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
26 But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave. 27 The Scriptures say about her,
‚ÄúYou have never had children,
but now you can be glad.
You have never given birth,
but now you can shout.
Once you had no children,
but now you will have
more children than a woman
who has been married
for a long time.‚ÄĚ
28My friends, you were born because of this promise, just as Isaac was. 29 But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today. 30 The Scriptures say, ‚ÄúGet rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.‚ÄĚ 31My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
