1N…õn…õ…õ da kena nyi nombia ta kela daa keŇč d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ tale kaa naa Ňčon d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, de y…õ…õ da agyueŇč nyi d…õ…õ bese kyaa At…õnse don…Ē…Ē man. 2Mena d…Ē…Ē dee kpila de d…Ē…Ē Timoti Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ tobaale e, te …Ē kpu…õ na daa Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan kekolosi man n…õ …õ gyaŇč. Dee kpila e nyi …Ē kaa do Ňčon kakyeŇč na …õ ka seŇč keŇčkeŇč …õ kel…õ…õdi man, 3na nyi akpaa kayey…õ…õ kei din…Ē…Ē de k…ĒŇč …õ d…Ē…Ē na, …õ man Ňčolo be nyiŇč bese …Ē w…Ēle ya. Nawolo nyi …õm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyeŇč nideli nyiaa, mena kayey…õ…õ kei y…õ…õ kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi gyae ke k…ĒŇč de d…Ē…Ē. 4DebaŇč keŇč de kyaa …õ gyaŇč n…õ, de wulaa yako Ňčon agbaatee agbaatee nyiaa, baa naase daa diyem Ňčgba mena keŇč be k…ĒŇč te …õ ne naa n…õ. 5∆ź kayey…õ…õ keŇč …õ na yey…õ…õ n…õ ne takaa maŇč nideli, te ma te gyae gb…õ…õ keŇč maa mo k…ĒŇč bot…Ē…Ē kina. Mena d…Ē…Ē te mee kpila Timoti …õ gyaŇč. Me kpila e bo nyi waa k…ĒŇč kaa k…õ…õ nyi …õ seŇč…õ…õ …õ kel…õ…õdi keŇč man keŇčkeŇč. Nawolo nyi me w…Ēe ne l…Ē maŇč nyi, nn…õ …Ēb…Ēnsam te kaa k…õ…õse Ňčon k…õ…õ yela …õ te tina …õ kel…õ…õdi keŇč yela. Nyi de k…ĒŇč mena na, na da tom keŇč pou te y…õ…õ yakaa.
6M…Ēna Timoti ka lee …õ gyaŇč k…ĒŇč yaa, te …Ē te k…ĒŇč kaa yako daa nombia kpaakpaa nyi …õ seŇč…õ…õ keŇčkeŇč …õ kel…õ…õdi man te …õ ne gyae balaŇč m…Ē. ∆Ü te kaa yako daa nyi debaŇč kamas…õ na …õ ne t…Ē…Ēse daa, te …õ sia te y…õ…õ nyi …õ kena daa Ňčgba mena keŇč daa m…Ē ne gyae nyi d…õ…õ naa Ňčon n…õ. 7De tebia, mena d…Ē…Ē keŇč na de kayey…õ…õ na be k…õ…õ naase daa diyem pou man na, …õ kel…õ…õdi keŇč na do daa kakyeŇč. 8Nawolo nyi lee mena keŇč …õ seŇč…õ…õ keŇčkeŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nombia Ňčan …õ be l…õ…õ di n…õ man d…Ē…Ē, de w…Ēe te f…õ…õ daa nideli. 9N…Ē…Ēkado woŇčti kaboela keŇč d…õ…õ tale mo do Wurubuar…õ, lee kpene keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ fa Ňčon n…õ d…Ē…Ē? D…õ…õ fa e karaŇč lee gyoŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē nyim …Ē siaman lee …õm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 10Nelim na ka weese na d…õ…õ fane fa Wurubuar…õ nyiaa, waa yela d…õ…õ bese naa Ňčon na de sia, na d…õ…õ ky…Ē Ňčon na …õ ka seŇč …õ kel…õ…õdi keŇč man keŇčkeŇč kelii …Ēto.
11D…õ…õ fane nyi de kya Wurubuar…õ gbagba na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu, baa toro gb…õ…õ fa daa na d…õ…õ k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč. 12D…õ…õ fane nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu waa yela …õ balaŇč kegyae keŇč ke doo …õ man nideli, na …õ ke nyiŇč gyae d…ĒŇča na balaŇč pou, Ňčgba mena keŇč daa m…Ē ne gyae Ňčon n…õ. 13De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu waa desina …õ kon…Ē…Ēse man, na wee keŇč Ňčon na balaŇč baŇč pou Wurubuar…õ be desina w…Ē see fa …Ē wose n…õ baa k…ĒŇč na, …õ ke y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč ayim…õ…õ na nombiikum kamas…õ be doo …õ man ya de kya Wurubuar…õ ŇčonaŇč siaman.

english_text:
A Life That Pleases God
1Finally, my dear friends, since you belong to the Lord Jesus, we beg and urge you to live as we taught you. Then you will please God. You are already living that way, but try even harder. 2Remember the instructions we gave you as followers of the Lord Jesus. 3God wants you to be holy, so don't be immoral in matters of sex. 4Respect and honor your wife. 5Don't be a slave of your desires or live like people who don't know God. 6You must not cheat any of the Lord's followers in matters of sex. Remember, we warned you that he punishes everyone who does such things. 7God didn't choose you to be filthy, but to be pure. 8So if you don't obey these rules, you are not really disobeying us. Instead, you are disobeying God, who gives you his Holy Spirit.
9We don't have to write you about the need to love each other. God has taught you to do this, 10and you already have shown your love for all his people in Macedonia. But, my dear friends, we ask you to do even more. 11Try your best to live quietly, to mind your own business, and to work hard, just as we taught you to do. 12 Then you will be respected by people who are not followers of the Lord, and you won't have to depend on anyone.
The Lord's Coming
13My friends, we want you to understand how it will be for those followers who have already died. Then you won't grieve over them and be like people who don't have any hope. 14We believe Jesus died and was raised to life. We also believe that when God brings Jesus back again, he will bring with him all who had faith in Jesus before they died. 15 Our Lord Jesus told us that when he comes, we won't go up to meet him ahead of his followers who have already died.
16 With a loud command and with the shout of the chief angel and a blast of God's trumpet, the Lord will return from heaven. Then those who had faith in Christ before they died will be raised to life. 17Next, all of us who are still alive will be taken up into the clouds together with them to meet the Lord in the sky. From that time on we will all be with the Lord forever. 18Encourage each other with these words.
translation_text:
Nombia Ňčan na gyoo Wurubuar…õ sia
1De tebia, nombia Ňčan be kaa m…õ…õ gyae maa yako Ňčon y…õna nyi, da wola Ňčon mena keŇč yaa kyaa na …õ kekyaabii na …õ nombia ay…õ…õsa pou kenyiŇč gyoo Wurubuar…õ sia, Ňčgba mena keŇč …õ be taŇč wulaa …õ ne y…õ…õ n…õ. D…õ…õ waase Ňčon lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu yele man nyi, …õ kyaŇč ke keŇčkeŇč na …õ ke y…õ…õ kela mena gba. 2Nawolo nyi …õ gyeŇč nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo doŇč d…Ē…Ē wola Ňčon n…õ.
3Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii y…õna nyi …õ na mo ayim…õ…õ wuu …õ wose ya, na …õ ka lese …õ wose lee kafoŇč nombia man. 4∆ź man walaŇč kamas…õ waa gyeŇč mena keŇč waa tela …Ē wose nideli lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man, Ňčgba mena keŇč na gyoo Wurubuar…õ sia te ke dana obuo m…Ē n…õ. 5∆ź na y…õ…õ kew…Ēl…õ…õ nombia Ňčgba mena keŇč balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ ya ne y…õ…õ n…õ ya. 6Mena ke m…Ē te Ňčolo na y…õ…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē nombiikum yaa …Ē na Ňčere e do ya. WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne y…õ…õ mena nombiakum…õ…õ k…õŇča pou na, de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ waa w…ĒŇč …Ētenate deŇčele Ňčgba mena keŇč d…Ē…Ē wulaa yako Ňčon te d…Ē…Ē faree Ňčon n…õ. 7Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ te baake daa k…ĒŇč …Ē gyaŇč nyi de kaa y…õ…õ ayim…õ…õ nombia ya, m…Ēna …Ē baake daa bo nyi de kaa y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč ayim…õ…õ be doo ba wose man ya. 8Mena d…Ē…Ē walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē b…õ…õ mena kawola kei na, na deniwalaŇč nombia te …Ē b…õ…õw…Ē ya m…Ēna Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē ba mo …Ē feliŇč do …õ man n…õ nombia yaa …Ē b…õ…õw…Ē.
9N…õn…õ…õ lee …õm…õ…õ na …õ tebia d…ĒŇča kegyae wose man nombia d…Ē…Ē, tekaboena nyi d…õ…õ bese Ňčmarase ke man kolo yelee Ňčon bela ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ te wulaa wola Ňčon nyi yaa gyae d…ĒŇča. 10Ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč nyi …õ wulaa …õ ne gyae …õ tebia Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Masedonia t…õ…õle keŇč pou d…Ē…Ē n…õ. M…Ēna de tebia, d…õ…õ waase Ňčon bo nyi yaa y…õ…õ kela mena.
11∆ź y…õ…õ kakyeŇč na …õ ke kyaa wosef…õ…õreŇč man, na …õ man walaŇč kamas…õ ke nyii na …Ē wui. ∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ kyaŇč …õ wose y…õ…õ tom, Ňčgba mena keŇč d…Ē…Ē wulaa wola Ňčon n…õ. 12∆ź y…õ…õ mena na, …õ be gyae …õ ka mo …õ wose l…Ē Ňčolo d…Ē…Ē ya. Kei gyae ke yela balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kpu…õ na Ňčon ya n…õ ke bu Ňčon.
De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu kabese k…ĒŇč
13De tebia, lee balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē yekee n…õ wose man nombia d…Ē…Ē, d…Ē…Ē ne gyae …õ ke kyaa ditiŇčt…õ…õ man na …õ ka yey…õ…õ Ňčgba balaŇč baŇč be loo be gyakaa kolo d…Ē…Ē n…õ ya. 14D…õ…õ l…õ…õ di nyi Yesu yeŇčaw…Ē te Wurubuar…õ gyuusu e lee yeŇč man. Mena ke m…Ē te d…õ…õ l…õ…õ di nyi wee keŇč Yesu waa bese k…ĒŇč na, Wurubuar…õ waa gyuusu baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ e di p…õte b…Ē…Ē yem n…õ lee yeŇč man na baa kpu na e. 15De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nombia Ňčan …Ē ba wolo te d…õ…õ yako Ňčon n…õ y…õna nyi wee keŇč waa bese k…ĒŇč n…õ, daa baŇč d…Ē…Ē kaa te de s…õ…õ de kyaa nyeedoŇč man n…õ, d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ taŇčgb…õ…õ baŇč b…Ē…Ē yekee n…õ foŇč gyu ke gyaŇčee na e ya. 16Nawolo nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu gbagba waa mo yeesa dinaa kolosi lee adido k…ĒŇč, te Wurubuar…õ kpilale kegyia Ňčon m…Ē waa mo yeesa kolosi, na baa wola Wurubuar…õ kaber…õ keŇč. Dek…ĒŇč mena na, balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ e di p…õte b…Ē…Ē yem n…õ, baŇč ne gyae la baa taŇčgb…õ…õ foŇč lee yeŇč man. 17KenaŇč w…Ēle na, daa baŇč de s…õ…õ de kyaa nyeedoŇč man debaŇč kenaŇč n…õ, baa mo daa na w…Ē pou gyu adido Ňč…Ēma man, na de kaa gyaŇčee na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu na daa na e ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa. 18Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ mo mena nombia k…õŇča do d…ĒŇča kakyeŇč.

english_text:
1I don't need to write you about the time or date when all this will happen. 2 You surely know that the Lord's return will be as a thief coming at night. 3People will think they are safe and secure. But destruction will suddenly strike them like the pains of a woman about to give birth. And they won't escape.
4My dear friends, you don't live in darkness, and so that day won't surprise you like a thief. 5You belong to the light and live in the day. We don't live in the night or belong to the dark. 6Others may sleep, but we should stay awake and be alert. 7People sleep during the night, and some even get drunk. 8 But we belong to the day. So we must stay sober and let our faith and love be like a suit of armor. Our firm hope that we will be saved is our helmet.
9God doesn't intend to punish us, but wants us to be saved by our Lord Jesus Christ. 10Christ died for us, so we could live with him, whether we are alive or dead when he comes. 11This is why you must encourage and help each other, just as you are already doing.
Final Instructions and Greetings
12My friends, we ask you to be thoughtful of your leaders who work hard and tell you how to live for the Lord. 13Show them great respect and love because of their work. Try to get along with each other. 14My friends, we beg you to warn anyone who isn't living right. Encourage anyone who feels left out, help all who are weak, and be patient with everyone. 15Don't be hateful to people, just because they are hateful to you. Rather, be good to each other and to everyone else.
16Always be joyful 17and never stop praying. 18Whatever happens, keep thanking God because of Jesus Christ. This is what God wants you to do.
19Don't turn away God's Spirit 20or ignore prophecies. 21Put everything to the test. Accept what is good 22and don't have anything to do with evil.
23I pray that God, who gives peace, will make you completely holy. And may your spirit, soul, and body be kept healthy and faultless until our Lord Jesus Christ returns. 24The one who chose you can be trusted, and he will do this.
25Friends, please pray for us.
26Give the Lord's followers a warm greeting.
27In the name of the Lord I beg you to read this letter to all his followers.
28I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
translation_text:
∆ź desina …õ wose daa Yesu kabese k…ĒŇč
1De tebia, b…õ…õ tekaa hia nyi Ňčolo waa bese Ňčmarase kolo lee debaŇč yaa wee keŇč mena nombia k…õŇča gyae kek…ĒŇč n…õ wose man nombia fa Ňčon bela ya. 2Nawolo nyi …õm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyeŇč nyi wee keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu waa bese k…ĒŇč n…õ, gyae ke y…õ…õ bo Ňčgba mena keŇč Ňčm…õ…õlate ne k…ĒŇč nelim n…õ. 3DebaŇč keŇč balaŇč baa yako nyi, ‚ÄúDe kyaa wosef…õ…õreŇč man, te de w…Ēe m…Ē b…õ…õ l…Ē daa ya n…õ,‚ÄĚ mena debaŇč kenaŇč te kayey…õ…õ dinaa gyae kaa futaa w…Ē, Ňčgba mena keŇč kadoŇčee ne futaa fulute n…õ. Dek…ĒŇč mena na, be man Ňčolo na Ňčolo be gyae waa tale lese …Ē wose lee mena kayey…õ…õ kenaŇč man ya.
4M…Ēna de tebia, …õm…õ…õ ba tekaa doo ditiŇčt…õ…õ man, na wee kenaŇč kaa futaa Ňčon Ňčgba Ňčm…õ…õlate n…õ ya. 5Nawolo nyi …õ popou y…õ…õ keŇčmaŇčee man balaŇčw…Ē, te …õ doo keŇčmaŇčee kenaŇč man. D…Ē…Ē tekaa y…õ…õ ditiŇčt…õ…õ man balaŇč baŇč be d…ĒŇč ditiŇčt…õ…õ man n…õ ya. 6Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ na yeli dee doo wolee da wose, Ňčgba mena keŇč baale ne y…õ…õ ne ya. M…Ēna …õ yela dee doo da wose d…Ē…Ē na d…õ…õ deke nideli. 7Nawolo nyi baŇč bee doo n…õ ne doo bo nelim, te baŇč m…Ē b…õ…õ d…õ…õse n…õ ne d…õ…õse bo balin…Ē…Ē. 8M…Ēna keŇč daa doo keŇčmaŇčee man d…Ē…Ē, …õ yela d…õ…õ k…õ…õ da wose d…Ē…Ē nideli na d…õ…õ mo da kel…õ…õdi na de balaŇč kegyae mo y…õ…õ nyeele pampasaŇč mo gyee da wose Ňčgba yoon…Ē…Ētena n…õ, na d…õ…õ mo da nyeekel…õ…õ keŇč d…Ē…Ē de loo gyakaa n…õ wuu da nyeeya d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba nyeele kot…Ēt…Ē…Ē n…õ. 9Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ te baake daa nyi waa naase daa diyem ya, m…Ēna …Ē baake daa bo nyi d…õ…õ ta de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē nyiŇč nyeekel…õ…õ. 10Ňäon de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu yeŇča la fa daa, mena d…Ē…Ē nyi de kyae na de sia yaa da yeŇčae koraŇč na, daa na e pou d…õ…õ kyaa. 11Mena d…Ē…Ē …õ kolosi fa d…ĒŇča na …õ ka do d…ĒŇča kakyeŇč, Ňčgba mena keŇč …õ ne taŇč y…õ…õ n…õ.
12De tebia, n…õn…õ…õ d…õ…õ waase Ňčon bo nyiaa, balaŇč baŇč de GbeŇčgyoo ba lese w…Ē nyi baa y…õ…õ …õ siamantena, te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ ba tom na kakyeŇč kolosi fa Ňčon n…õ, …õ bu w…Ē nideli. 13∆ź gyae w…Ē na …õ ke bu w…Ē nideli lee ba tom keŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē, na …õ ke kyaa wosef…õ…õreŇč man na d…ĒŇča. 14De tebia, dee kolosi fa Ňčon nyiaa, …õ kolosi fa keb…ĒŇčaletena baŇč. ∆ź kolosi do baŇč gyakoloŇč dana w…Ē n…õ. ∆ź ky…Ē baŇč b…Ē…Ē dana doŇč ya n…õ, na …õ ke nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē fa walaŇč kamas…õ. 15∆ź k…õ…õ nyi Ňčolo na Ňčolo na mo dukum t…Ē dukum kom ya, m…Ēna …õ y…õ…õ deeli fa d…ĒŇča na balaŇč pou debaŇč kamas…õ.
16∆ź kyaa gyoŇč man debaŇč kamas…õ. 17∆ź fane debaŇč kamas…õ. 18∆ź do Wurubuar…õ n…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē pou man debaŇč kamas…õ. Mena nombia k…õŇča te Wurubuar…õ ne gyae nyi yaa y…õ…õ, nawolo nyi …õm…õ…õ na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo y…õ…õ dokoloŇč.
19∆ź na ko Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon …Ē ba mo do …õ man n…õ n…Ē…Ē ya. 20∆ź na l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan …Ē be t…õ…õ wa akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra d…Ē…Ē kolosi n…õ aŇčmaareŇč ya. 21M…Ēna …õ gyoo nombia pou man k…õ…õ, na …õ ke kyaŇč Ňčan dei n…õ man nideli. 22∆ź w…ĒŇč …õ wose lee nombiikum kamas…õ man.
23D…õ…õ sola Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ wosef…õ…õreŇč Wurubuar…õ e n…õ, nyi waa gberaa …õ wose ayim…õ…õ pou leki na waa yela …õ gb…õ…õneŇč ka tenee keŇč nombiikum daale na doo …õ man ya, mo kelii wee keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo waa bese k…ĒŇč n…õ. 24Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē be baake Ňčon n…õ, waa yela kek…ĒŇč mena nawolo nyi …Ē ne di …Ē nombia d…Ē…Ē.
25De tebia, …õ fane fa daa m…Ē. 26∆ź do de tebia Kristotena baŇč pou n…Ē…Ē nideli. 27M…õ…õ waase Ňčon de GbeŇčgyoo yele man nyiaa, …õ kala t…Ēne kei fa …õ tebia Kristotena baŇč pou. 28De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo waa mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele …õ popou na waa seŇč …õ w…Ēle.

english_text:
1 From Paul, Silas, and Timothy.
To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God our Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ.
2I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
When Christ Returns
3My dear friends, we always have good reason to thank God for you, because your faith in God and your love for each other keep growing all the time. 4This is why we brag about you to all of God's churches. We tell them how patient you are and how you keep on having faith, even though you are going through a lot of trouble and suffering.
5All this shows that God judges fairly and is making you fit to share in his kingdom for which you are suffering. 6It is only right for God to punish everyone who is causing you trouble, 7but he will give you relief from your troubles. God will do the same for us, when the Lord Jesus comes from heaven with his powerful angels 8and with a flaming fire.
Our Lord Jesus will punish anyone who doesn't know God and won't obey his message. 9 Their punishment will be eternal destruction, and they will be kept far from the presence of our Lord and his glorious strength. 10This will happen on the day when the Lord returns to be praised and honored by all who have faith in him and belong to him. This includes you, because you believed what we said.
11God chose you, and we keep praying that God will make you worthy of being his people. We pray for God's power to help you do all the good things you hope to do and your faith makes you want to do. 12Then, because of the undeserved grace of God and our Lord Jesus Christ, you will bring honor to the name of our Lord Jesus, and he will bring honor to you.
translation_text:
1T…Ēne kei lee maŇč P…Ē…Ēl na Silase na Timoti gyaŇč. D…õ…õ Ňčmarase ke dee yelee …õm…õ…õ Kristotena baŇč …õ kyaa Tesalonika don…Ē…Ē man, te …õ y…õ…õ de kya Wurubuar…õ na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo balaŇčw…Ē n…õ.
2De kya Wurubuar…õ na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be b…õr…õ…õ boele Ňčon na baa fa Ňčon wosef…õ…õreŇč.
3De tebia, kaboena nyi wee na wee na d…õ…õ do Wurubuar…õ n…Ē…Ē lee …õm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč kaboena n…õ. Nawolo nyi …õ kel…õ…õdi keŇč na bee kpu, te walaŇč kamas…õ m…Ē balaŇč kegyae keŇč …Ē dana fa …Ē d…Ē…Ē n…õ na bee kpu. 4Lee mena d…Ē…Ē d…õ…õ tale gy…Ē de kon…Ē…Ē kolosi …õ wose man nombia Wurubuar…õ balaŇč man yenaŇč kamas…õ. Nawolo nyi …õ kayey…õ…õ na …õ diyem kena pou man koraŇč na, …õ ne y…õ…õ kakyeŇč seŇč …õ kel…õ…õdi man.
5K…õŇča pou na lese la wola nyiaa, Wurubuar…õ ne di …Ē nombia na ke gb…õ…õ bo. Kei gyae ke yela …õ ke kaboena Wurubuar…õ gyoori keŇč d…Ē…Ē …õ ne naa diyem n…õ man kagyoo. 6Wurubuar…õ ne di …Ē nombia na ke gb…õ…õ, mena d…Ē…Ē waa t…Ē balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ naase Ňčon diyem n…õ kom, 7na waa yela daa baŇč de y…õ…õ …Ē balaŇčw…Ē te d…õ…õ naa diyem n…õ ke f…õ…õfo wee keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu na …Ē kpilala baŇč be dana doŇč te boalaŇč gyae ke kilisiw…Ē lee adido k…ĒŇč n…õ. 8Wurubuar…õ waa w…ĒŇč balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč e ya, te be ta mo de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nyeedoŇč nombia Ňčan y…õ…õ kolo ya n…õ deŇčele. 9Waa w…ĒŇč mena balaŇč benaŇč deŇčele, na baa naa diyem kekpaakekpaa. KeŇč kpu…õ bela y…õna nyi, waa lese w…Ē lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu na …Ē gyoori dinaa keŇč man. 10DebaŇč kenaŇč man keŇč waa bese k…ĒŇč n…õ, …Ē balaŇč baŇč Wurubuar…õ be baake w…Ē te …Ē be desina w…Ē see fa …Ē wose n…õ baa lese …Ē yele adido, na baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ e di n…õ ke k…õl…õ…õ e. ∆źm…õ…õ m…Ē …õ ke kpu na w…Ē, nawolo nyi …õ l…õ…õ nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē yako Ňčon n…õ di.
11Lee mena nombia k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē te d…õ…õ fane fa Ňčon debaŇč kamas…õ n…õ. D…õ…õ sola Wurubuar…õ nyi waa yela …õ ke kaboena …Ē kebaake keŇč …Ē be baake Ňčon n…õ, na waa fa Ňčon doŇč na …õ ka mo t…Ē…Ēle nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan pou …õ ne gyae …õ ke y…õ…õ n…õ. Na …õ ke tale y…õ…õ tom keŇč …õ ne y…õ…õ n…õ kelii …Ēto lee mena keŇč …õ ne l…õ…õ e di n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 12De k…ĒŇč mena na, de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu yele gyae ka koro adido. BalaŇč baa naa …Ē gyoori keŇč, te Yesu d…Ē…Ē …õm…õ…õ m…Ē …õ yele gyae ka lee. Lee de kya Wurubuar…õ, na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo b…õr…õ…õ keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo boele Ňčon d…Ē…Ē, baa yela kek…ĒŇč mena.

english_text:
The Lord's Return
1 When our Lord Jesus returns, we will be gathered up to meet him. So I ask you, my friends, 2not to be easily upset or disturbed by people who claim the Lord has already come. They may say they heard this directly from the Holy Spirit, or from someone else, or even that they read it in one of our letters. 3But don't be fooled! People will rebel against God. Then before the Lord returns, the wicked one who is doomed to be destroyed will appear. 4 He will brag and oppose everything holy or sacred. He will even sit in God's temple and claim to be God. 5Don't you remember I told you this while I was still with you?
6You already know what is holding this wicked one back until it is time for him to come. 7His mysterious power is already at work, but someone is holding him back. And the wicked one won't appear until this someone is out of the way. 8 Then he will appear, but the Lord Jesus will kill him simply by breathing on him. He will be completely destroyed by the Lord's glorious return.
9 When the wicked one appears, Satan will pretend to work all kinds of miracles, wonders, and signs. 10Lost people will be fooled by his evil deeds. They could be saved, but they will refuse to love the truth and accept it. 11So God will make sure they are fooled into believing a lie. 12All of them will be punished, because they would rather do evil than believe the truth.
Be Faithful
13My friends, the Lord loves you, and it is only natural for us to thank God for you. God chose you to be the first ones to be saved. His Spirit made you holy, and you put your faith in the truth. 14God used our preaching as his way of inviting you to share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ. 15My friends, this is why you must remain faithful and follow closely what we taught you in person and by our letters.
16God our Father loves us. He treats us with undeserved grace and has given us eternal comfort and a wonderful hope. We pray that our Lord Jesus Christ and God our Father 17will encourage you and help you always to do and say the right thing.
translation_text:
1Me tebia, lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo kabese k…ĒŇč na mena keŇč waa yilaa de popou mena wee kenaŇč n…õ man nombia yaa m…õ…õ gyae maa lese wola Ňčon nideli n…õ. 2BalaŇč baale d…ĒŇč b…õ…õ yako nyiaa, de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu taŇč ke k…ĒŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē d…õ…õ sola Ňčon bo nyi, …õ na yele mena nombia k…õŇča ke yela …õ w…Ēe kel…Ē Ňčon ya. Nn…õ balaŇč baale te di dekara nyi daa kpa Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon yako na daa nombia k…õŇča te daa m…Ē kaa yako Ňčon yaa daa gbagba yako na Ňčon nombia k…õŇča yaa daa gbagba Ňčmarase na t…Ēne yelee Ňčon nyi Yesu taŇč ke k…ĒŇč. 3∆ź na yeli Ňčolo ka beo Ňčon lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man ya. Nawolo nyi p…õna wee dinaa kenaŇč kek…ĒŇč na, balaŇč baa koro seŇč tia Wurubuar…õ. Mena ke te baa lese walaŇč Ňčon …Ē bee di mmaraase d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ deb…Ē…Ē p…õna wee kenaŇč kelii. Ňäon y…õna walaŇčkum Ňčon boalaŇč man te waa doo kekpaakekpaa n…õ. 4Waa gyina …Ē wose adido, na waa koro seŇč tia Wurubuar…õ na kpene kamas…õ keŇč balaŇč na som n…õ. Waa kaa kyaa Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man, na waa yako nyi Ňčon y…õna Wurubuar…õ.
5∆ź b…õ…õ t…Ē…Ēse nyi debaŇč keŇč me kyaa …õ gyaŇč na, m…õ…õ kyaa yako Ňčon mena nombia k…õŇča yee? 6M…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ …õ gyeŇč kpene keŇč be kyaŇč e d…Ē…Ēse d…Ē…Ē te …Ē te k…ĒŇča ta ya. DebaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba see e nyi waa k…ĒŇč n…õ de lii na, waa k…ĒŇč. 7BalaŇč taŇč ke fias…õ…õ b…õ…õ lee Wurubuar…õ w…Ēle y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ weese. M…Ēna p…õna nombii keŇč m…Ē…Ē yako Ňčon nyi gyae kek…ĒŇč n…õ ke k…ĒŇč na, kaboena nyi Wurubuar…õ waa lese walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne t…Ē ke gb…õ…õ n…õ. 8KenaŇč w…Ēle na te baa lese walaŇč Ňčon …Ē bee di mmaraase d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ deb…Ē…Ē. De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu n…Ē…Ēman wees…õ…õ te waa mo feefu e ko, na waa mo …Ē gyoorobiiri keŇčalakee keŇč w…Ēl…õ…õ e kekpaakekpaa. 9WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē bee di mmaraase d…Ē…Ē ya na, waa mo …Ēb…Ēnsam doŇč bo k…ĒŇč. Waa kaa beo y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia na nombia dinaana burum. 10Waa mo nombiakum…õ…õ kamas…õ beo baŇč pou baa yeŇč leki n…õ. Baa yeŇč leki kekpaa nawolo nyi ba te b…õ…õ nyi b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ nombia Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ, Ňčan gyae ke yela Wurubuar…õ ke l…õ…õ ba nyee n…õ do ya. 11Lee mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ te yeli ak…õr…õ…õ nombia te kaa gyoo be man, te ak…õr…õ…õ nombia ŇčaageŇč te b…õ…õ l…õ…õ di. 12Mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ waa bu balaŇč baŇč pou ba te l…õ…õ anokoare nombia Ňčan di ya, te b…õ…õ gyae ak…õr…õ…õ nombia bo n…õ f…Ē…Ē.
13M…Ēna …õm…õ…õ de tebia baŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu ne gyae Ňčon n…õ d…Ē…Ē, kaboena nyi debaŇč kamas…õ na d…õ…õ fane fa Wurubuar…õ karaŇč lee …õ d…Ē…Ē. Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ wulaa lese Ňčon see leenaŇč kewalaŇč keŇč nyi waa l…õ…õ …õ nyee. Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē …Ē feliŇč Ňčon doŇč man te …Ē desina Ňčon see, nawolo nyi …õ l…õ…õ nombia Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ n…õ di. 14Ňäon Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õ la nyeedoŇč nombia Ňčan d…õ…õ yako n…õ d…Ē…Ē baake Ňčon k…ĒŇč nyeedoŇč kei man, na …õ ke nyiŇč gb…õ…õ gyoo de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo gyoori keŇč man. 15De tebia, lee mena d…Ē…Ē …õ seŇč keŇčkeŇč na …õ ke kyaŇč de nombia Ňčan d…õ…õ wola Ňčon n…õ, na Ňčan dee kolosi fa Ňčon n…õ, na Ňčan d…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase yelee Ňčon n…õ man nideli.
16De kya Wurubuar…õ ne gyae daa, te …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč d…Ē…Ē do daa kakyeŇč kekpaakekpaa, te …Ē faa daa loo kegyakaa kpaakpaate. D…õ…õ solo nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo gbagba na de kya Wurubuar…õ, 17baa do Ňčon kakyeŇč na baa fa Ňčon doŇč na …õ ke tale y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa lee …õ kekolosi na ya ab…Ē…Ē ay…õ…õsa pou man.

english_text:
Pray for Us
1Finally, our friends, please pray for us. This will help the message about the Lord to spread quickly, and others will respect it, just as you do. 2Pray that we may be kept safe from worthless and evil people. After all, not everyone has faith. 3But the Lord can be trusted to make you strong and protect you from harm. 4He has made us sure that you are obeying what we taught you and that you will keep on obeying. 5I pray that the Lord will guide you to be as loving as God and as patient as Christ.
Warnings against Laziness
6My dear friends, in the name of the Lord Jesus, I beg you not to have anything to do with any of your people who loaf around and refuse to obey the instructions we gave you. 7You surely know that you should follow our example. We didn't waste our time loafing, 8and we didn't accept food from anyone without paying for it. We didn't want to be a burden to any of you, so night and day we worked as hard as we could.
9We had the right not to work, but we wanted to set an example for you. 10We also gave you this rule: If you don't work, you don't eat. 11Now we learn that some of you just loaf around and won't do any work, except the work of a busybody. 12So, for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ, we ask and beg these people to settle down and start working for a living. 13Dear friends, you must never become tired of doing right.
14Be on your guard against any followers who refuse to obey what we have written in this letter. Put them to shame by not having anything to do with them. 15Don't consider them your enemies. Instead, speak kindly to them as you would to any other follower.
Final Prayer
16I pray that the Lord, who gives peace, will always bless you with peace. May the Lord be with all of you.
17I always sign my letters as I am now doing: PAUL.
18I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to all of you.
translation_text:
1De tebia, nombia Ňčan be kaa m…õ…õ gyae maa yako Ňčon y…õna nyi …õ fane fa daa, na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu nombia Ňčan ke nyiŇč gb…õ…õ yaasee bileŇčbileŇč na balaŇč ke l…õ…õ Ňča di obuo man Ňčgba mena keŇč …õ be l…õ…õ Ňča di obuo man n…õ. 2∆ź bese fane nyi Wurubuar…õ waa lese daa lee nombiakum…õ…õ na balaŇč kum…õ…õ nyiŇčmaa man, nawolo nyi na balaŇč pou l…õ…õ na Wurubuar…õ di ya. 3Wurubuar…õ y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne di …Ē nombia d…Ē…Ē, mena d…Ē…Ē waa do Ňčon doŇč na waa k…õ…õ …õ d…Ē…Ē keŇč nyi …õ ba gyoo walaŇčkum Ňčon nyiŇčmaa man ya. 4D…õ…õ l…õ…õ di lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu yele man nyi …õ ne y…õ…õ nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē wola Ňčon n…õ. Te d…õ…õ l…õ…õ di m…Ē nyi …õ ke y…õ…õ mena kelii …Ēto. 5D…õ…õ sola de GbeŇčgyoo nyi waa desina …õ kon…Ē…Ēse na …õ ka gyeŇč kegyae keŇč Wurubuar…õ ne gyae Ňčon n…õ, na …õ ke nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē seŇč kayey…õ…õ man Ňčgba mena keŇč Kristo m…Ē be nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē seŇč n…õ.
6De tebia, d…õ…õ faree Ňčon lee Yesu Kristo yele man nyiaa, Kristobaale kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ keb…ĒŇčalete e te …Ē nombia ay…õ…õsa m…Ē b…õ…õ kyaa na nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē wola Ňčon ya n…õ, …õ w…ĒŇč …õ wose lee mena walaŇč ŇčonaŇč man. 7∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ gyeŇč mena keŇč kaboena nyi yaa kasee daa, nawolo nyi debaŇč keŇč de kyaa …õ gyaŇč n…õ da te y…õ…õ keb…ĒŇčale ya. 8Mena m…Ē te de te di Ňčolo ween…õ…õ yakaa keŇč nyi da te t…Ē e kom ya. M…Ēna de yase de sia kyaŇč da wose y…õ…õ tom nideli nelim na ka weese, na d…Ē…Ē nyiŇč bese kasol…Ē …õ man Ňčolo d…Ē…Ē ya. 9Ka te y…õ…õ nyi gb…õ…õ be doo keŇč nyi d…õ…õ l…õ…õ kolo lee …õ gyaŇč bo d…Ē…Ē te de y…õ…õ tom mena ya, m…Ēna de y…õe mena na kabese kolo kekasee fa Ňčon na …õ ke nyiŇč kasee daa. 10DebaŇč keŇč de kyaa …õ gyaŇč koraŇč na de faa Ňčon mmaraa nyiaa, ‚ÄúNyi akpaa Ňčolo b…õ…õ gyae nyi waa y…õ…õ tom ya n…õ, tekaboena nyi waa di ya.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
1From Paul.
God our Savior and Christ Jesus commanded me to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, who gives us hope.
2 Timothy, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind and merciful to you. May they bless you with peace!
Warning against False Teaching
3When I was leaving for Macedonia, I asked you to stay on in Ephesus and warn certain people there to stop spreading their false teachings. 4You needed to warn them to stop wasting their time on senseless stories and endless lists of ancestors. Such things only cause arguments. They don't help anyone to do God's work that can only be done by faith.
5You must teach people to have genuine love, as well as a good conscience and true faith. 6There are some who have given up these for nothing but empty talk. 7They want to be teachers of the Law of Moses. But they don't know what they are talking about, even though they think they do.
8We know the Law is good, if it is used in the right way. 9We also understand it wasn't given to control people who please God, but to control lawbreakers, criminals, godless people, and sinners. It is for wicked and evil people, and for murderers, who would even kill their own parents. 10The Law was written for people who are sexual perverts or who live as homosexuals or are kidnappers or liars or won't tell the truth in court. It is for anything else that opposes the correct teaching 11of the good news the glorious and wonderful God has given me.
Being Thankful for God's Kindness
12I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me the strength for my work because he knew he could trust me. 13 I used to say terrible and insulting things about him, and I was cruel. But Christ had mercy on me because I didn't know what I was doing, and I had not yet put my faith in him. 14Christ Jesus our Lord treated me with undeserved grace and has greatly blessed my life with faith and love just like his own.
15‚ÄúChrist Jesus came into the world to save sinners.‚ÄĚ This saying is true, and it can be trusted. I was the worst sinner of all! 16But since I was worse than anyone else, God had mercy on me and let me be an example of the endless patience of Christ Jesus. He did this so that others would put their faith in Christ and have eternal life. 17I pray that honor and glory will always be given to the only God, who lives forever and is the invisible and eternal King! Amen.
18Timothy, my son, the instructions I am giving you are based on what some prophets once said about you. If you follow these instructions, you will fight like a good soldier. 19You will be faithful and have a clear conscience. Some people have made a mess of their faith because they didn't listen to their consciences. 20Two of them are Hymenaeus and Alexander. I have given these men over to the power of Satan, so they will learn not to oppose God.
translation_text:

P…Ē…Ēl ke faree Timoti lee ak…õr…õ…õ wolala wose man

Na n ke t…Ē balaŇč baŇč be kyaa Efeso bot…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ wola ak…õr…õ…õ kawola Kristo …Ēsom keŇč man n…õ nyi baa tina Ňča yela bileŇč. 4Yako w…Ē nyi be na w…Ēl…õ…õ debaŇč na beneŇč beneŇč Ňč…Ēla kegy…Ē, na be naana…Ē kalola kegyakaare nombia kawola ya. Nawolo nyi k…õŇča pou na mo aŇčmaareŇč bo k…ĒŇč, te Ňče bee yeli balaŇč ke y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii ya. N l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di na te nyaŇč ke tale y…õ…õ …Ē kegyaebii. 5KeŇč d…Ē…Ē te m…õ…õ yako neŇč mena nombia k…õŇča y…õna nyiaa, m…õ…õ gyae nyi kegyae keŇč ba lee kon…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa man n…õ, na kon…Ē…Ē man nombia kpaakpaa na Kristo kel…õ…õdi kpaakpaate keŇč ke doo Kristo walaŇč kamas…õ man. 6BalaŇč baale ta yoo gb…õ…õ lee ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa k…õŇča man te ba gyoo afa kum…õ…õ key…Ē…Ē. 7B…õ…õ gyae nyi baa bese Wurubuar…õ mmaraa wolala, m…Ēna kpene keŇč baŇč gbagba ne kolosi na nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē ba seŇč…õ…õ keŇčkeŇč bee kolosi n…õ b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč Ňče man ya.
8Da gyeŇč nyi mmaraa dei. Akpaa Ňčolo de di ke d…Ē…Ē nideli na ke dei fa e. 9Da gyeŇč m…Ē nyi ba te y…õ…õ mmaraa bo fa balaŇč kpaakpaa ya. M…Ēna be y…õ…õ ke bo fa baŇč b…Ē…Ē ne di mmaraase Ňčan d…Ē…Ē ya, na baŇč b…Ē…Ē ne y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose ya, na baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ ya na nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra na baŇč ayim…õ…õ doo ba wose man na baŇč b…Ē…Ē ne sila Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õneŇč ya, na baŇč b…õ…õ koe be kya…Ē yaa be na…Ē n…õ na baŇč m…Ē b…õ…õ koe balaŇč n…õ, 10na kafoŇčtena na baala baŇč bee doo na d…ĒŇča, na baŇč b…õ…õ mo balaŇč gyae k…Ēba, na ak…õr…õ…õtena na baŇč b…õ…õ beo kaŇč ntamse, na kpene kamas…õ keŇč b…õ…õ kyaa na kawola kpaakpaate keŇč ya n…õ. 11Mena kawola kpaakpaa kei kyaaw…Ē na nyeedoŇč nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan Wurubuar…õ ba mo do me nyiŇčmaa man nyi maa kolosi fa balaŇč n…õ. Kawola kpaakpaa kei lee Wurubuar…õ doŇčte Ňčon balaŇč na lese …Ē yele n…õ gyaŇč.
Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ keŇč …Ē ba mo boele P…Ē…Ēl n…õ

M…õ…õ fa e karaŇč Ňčgba …Ē be k…õ…õ maŇč nyi me y…õ…õ walaŇč kpaakpaa e, te …Ē ba lese maŇč nyi me kaboena nyi maa y…õ…õ …Ē tom keŇč …Ē ba mo do maŇč me nyiŇčmaa man n…õ. 13GyaŇčgba keŇč na me kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Yesu, te me naase …Ē kaseela baŇč diyem te me k…Ēla w…Ē m…Ē. M…Ēna k…õŇča pou w…Ēle na Wurubuar…õ wii me waraŇčase, nawolo nyi na m…Ē…Ē gyeŇč keŇč m…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ ya te ma te l…õ…õ Kristo m…Ē di ya. 14De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo moo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč boele maŇč nideli, te …Ē yela me l…õ…õ e di te …Ē moo …Ē kegyae keŇč do me man.
15Kei y…õna anokoare nombii keŇč kaboena nyi walaŇč kamas…õ waa l…õ…õ di. Nombii kenaŇč y…õna nyi, Kristo Yesu k…ĒŇčae t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē …Ē kaa l…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra nyee. MaŇč P…Ē…Ēl gba y…õna nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra pou nyee. 16M…Ēna lee mena nombii kei d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ wii me waraŇčase, na Yesu Kristo ke nyiŇč ta maŇč nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õra pou nyee kei d…Ē…Ē lese …Ē kon…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē dana n…õ wola. Na ka ke y…õ…õ kekasee nombii fa balaŇč baŇč pou baa kaa l…õ…õ e di na baa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč n…õ. 17∆ź yela d…õ…õ k…õl…õ…õ e nyi, ‚ÄúNyaŇč nyaageŇč y…õna Wurubuar…õ na gyoo Ňčon n kyaa kekpaakekpaa. NyaŇč y…õna Wurubuar…õ Ňčon n b…õ…õ yem ya, te Ňčolo m…Ē b…õ…õ naa neŇč na …Ē sia fon…õ…õ ya. NyaŇč nyaageŇč y…õna Wurubuar…õ Ňčon n kaboena nyi baa mo obuo na gyoorobiiri fa neŇč kekpaakekpaa. Am…õn.‚ÄĚ
18Me bu Timoti, nombia Ňčan m…Ē…Ē mo do n nyiŇčmaa man, na Ňčan Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra be wulaa yako see lee n wose man n…õ, mena nombia ŇčenaŇč pou y…õ…õ dokoloŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē mo nombia ŇčenaŇč tela Ňčgba yoo ab…Ē…Ē n…õ, na n ka mo yoo na ak…õr…õ…õ nombia wolala baŇč. 19Na tina n kel…õ…õdi na n kon…Ē…Ē man nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan yela ya. Baale te b…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa k…õŇča te be yela ba Kristo kel…õ…õdi keŇč m…Ē w…Ēl…õ…õw…Ē. 20BalaŇč benaŇč baale y…õna Hameneyose na Al…õkesanda. Me ta mo w…Ē fa …Ēb…Ēnsam nyi waa w…ĒŇč ba deŇčela na b…Ē…Ē tekaa kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuar…õ bela ya.

english_text:
How To Pray
1First of all, I ask you to pray for everyone. Ask God to help and bless them all, and tell God how thankful you are for each of them. 2Pray for kings and others in power, so we may live quiet and peaceful lives as we worship and honor God. 3This kind of prayer is good, and it pleases God our Savior. 4God wants everyone to be saved and to know the whole truth, which is,
5There is only one God,
and Christ Jesus
is the only one
who can bring us
to God.
Jesus was truly human,
and he gave himself
to rescue all of us.
6God showed us this
at the right time.
7 This is why God chose me to be a preacher and an apostle of the good news. I am telling the truth. I am not lying. God sent me to teach the Gentiles about faith and truth.
8I want everyone everywhere to lift innocent hands toward heaven and pray, without being angry or arguing with each other.
9 I would like for women to wear modest and sensible clothes. They should not have fancy hairdos, or wear expensive clothes, or put on jewelry made of gold or pearls. 10Women who claim to love God should do helpful things for others, 11and they should learn by being quiet and paying attention. 12They should be silent and not be allowed to teach or to tell men what to do. 13 After all, Adam was created before Eve, 14 and the man Adam wasn't the one who was fooled. It was the woman Eve who was completely fooled and sinned. 15But women will be saved by having children, if they stay faithful, loving, holy, and modest.
translation_text:
Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom
1Mena d…Ē…Ē kpene keŇč m…õ…õ gyae …õ ke taŇčgb…õ…õ y…õ…õ y…õna nyi, m…õ…õ waase Ňčon nyi yaa fane fa walaŇč kamas…õ na …õ ka sola Wurubuar…õ wosef…õ…õreŇč fa w…Ē. ∆ź sola Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ fa w…Ē, na …õ ke fa e karaŇč lee kpene kamas…õ keŇč …Ē ne y…õ…õ fa w…Ē n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 2Mena m…Ē te kaboena nyi yaa fane fa gyooneŇč na balaŇč baŇč pou bee di doŇč de d…Ē…Ē n…õ, na de w…Ēe ke f…õ…õ daa na dee nyiŇč kyaa wosef…õ…õreŇč man na dee nyiŇč som Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õ kpaakpaate man lee da kekyaabii man. 3Mena nombia k…õŇča deila, te Ňč…õ…õ gyoo da nyeel…õ…õre Wurubuar…õ Ňčon sia. 4Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ ne gyae nyi waa l…õ…õ walaŇč kamas…õ nyee na baa gyeŇč wa anokoare keŇč. 5Wurubuar…õ dokoloŇč kooŇč kyaa la, te walaŇč dokoloŇč kooŇč m…Ē doo la Wurubuar…õ na balaŇč nsana. Ňäon y…õna Yesu Kristo. 6Yesu kei moona …Ē wose fa yeŇč t…Ē balaŇč pou nyeeman kom lee ba dukum keŇč b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē. Mena nombii kei te Wurubuar…õ y…õ…õw…Ē debaŇč keŇč …Ē ba see n…õ man, na kedi adans…õ…õ fa balaŇč pou nyi …Ē ne gyae nyi baa nyiŇč nyeekel…õ…õ. 7Lee kei d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ lese maŇč nyi maa bese …Ē kpilale, na maa yako …Ē nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan fa balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ. 8Mena d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ gyae nyi baala baa gyina ba nyiŇčmaase Ňčan ayim…õ…õ be doo Ňče man ya n…õ adido, na baa fane debaŇč kamas…õ. Be na mo baŇč na aŇčmaareŇč do ba kefane man ya. 9Mena m…Ē te m…õ…õ gyae nyi ala baa do ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan kaboena n…õ, na baa desina Ňča nideli. Be na do ba nyeeny…Ē…Ēse adaose beiŇč Ňčgba balaŇč yakaa nyibaŇč n…õ ya. Te be na mo k…Ēba ab…Ē…Ē na ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan Ňča koya dana doŇč n…õ mia ba wose Ňčgba mena keŇč tekaboena n…õ ya. 10M…Ēna baa y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan kaboena na balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ som Wurubuar…õ n…õ.
11Kaboena nyi ala baa y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose na baa kyaa yididi na baa kasee Wurubuar…õ nombia. 12M…Ē…Ē ne fa ala gb…õ…õ nyi baa wola yaa baa di doŇč baala d…Ē…Ē ya. Kaboena nyi baa wuu be n…Ē…Ē. 13Nawolo nyi Adam te Wurubuar…õ taŇčgb…õ…õw…Ē y…õ…õ, p…õte …Ē y…õ…õ Efa. 14Na Adam te …Ēb…Ēnsam beow…Ē ya. M…Ēna alo Ňčon te …Ēb…Ēnsam beow…Ē te …Ē leew…Ē Wurubuar…õ w…Ēle y…õ…õ dukum. 15M…Ēna akpaa alo de kyaa …Ē kel…õ…õdi man nideli na balaŇč kegyae te ayim…õ…õ be doo …Ē kon…Ē…Ē man ya na, Wurubuar…õ waa l…õ…õ e …Ē kalola man.

english_text:
Church Officials
1It is true that anyone who desires to be a church official wants to be something worthwhile. 2 That's why officials must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. They must be self-controlled, sensible, well-behaved, friendly to strangers, and able to teach. 3They must not be heavy drinkers or troublemakers. Instead, they must be kind and gentle and not love money.
4Church officials must be in control of their own families, and they must see that their children are obedient and always respectful. 5If they don't know how to control their own families, how can they look after God's people?
6They must not be new followers of the Lord. If they are, they might become proud and be doomed along with the devil. 7Finally, they must be well-respected by people who are not followers. Then they won't be trapped and disgraced by the devil.
Church Officers
8Church officers should be serious. They must not be liars, heavy drinkers, or greedy for money. 9And they must have a clear conscience and hold firmly to what God has shown us about our faith. 10They must first prove themselves. Then if no one has anything against them, they can serve as officers.
11Women must also be serious. They must not gossip or be heavy drinkers, and they must be faithful in everything they do.
12Church officers must be faithful in marriage. They must be in full control of their children and everyone else in their home. 13Those who serve well as officers will earn a good reputation and will be highly respected for their faith in Christ Jesus.
The Mystery of Our Religion
14I hope to visit you soon. But I am writing these instructions, 15so if I am delayed, you will know how everyone who belongs to God's family ought to behave. After all, the church of the living God is the strong foundation of truth.
16Here is the great mystery of our religion:
Christ came as a human.
The Spirit proved
that he pleased God,
and he was seen by angels.
Christ was preached
to the nations.
People in this world
put their faith in him,
and he was taken up to glory.
translation_text:
Kristotena kegyiise kalese
1Ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč nyi ‚ÄúAkpaa Ňčolo ne gyae waa y…õ…õ Kristotena kegyia na, tom kpaakpaate yaa …Ē ne gyae n…õ.‚ÄĚ 2Kaboena nyi Kristotena kegyia waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē wose man be dana nombiikum keŇč balaŇč baa naa kolosi ya. Kaboena nyi …Ē w…õ…õle d…õ…õ y…õ…õ dokoloŇč. Waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē sia doo t…õ…õle man te …Ē ne tale di …Ē wose d…Ē…Ē. Waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē wose man dana obuo te …Ē ne tale l…õ…õ Ňč…Ē…Ēla. Waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne tale wola nombia m…Ē. 3∆Ü na y…õ…õ soloŇčny…Ē…Ēre e ya te …Ē na y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē wose ne gyae nombia m…Ē ya, m…Ēna waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē wo…õ. ∆Ü na y…õ…õ kale gyaere e ya, te …Ē na y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē sia ne y…õ…õ k…Ēba beiŇč ya. 4Waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne tale k…õ…õ …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč d…Ē…Ē nideli, te …Ē bia m…Ē ne nyii …Ē dei nideli te bee bu e lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man. 5Nawolo nyi akpaa Ňčolo b…õ…õ tale k…õ…õ …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč d…Ē…Ē nideli ya na, sena te waa tale k…õ…õ Kristotena dikpii keŇč d…Ē…Ē nideli? 6WalaŇč ŇčonaŇč na y…õ…õ walaŇč w…Ēle …Ēsom keŇč man ya. Na mena ya na waa gyina …Ē wose adido na waa yala, na Wurubuar…õ ke bu e f…Ē…Ē Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ēb…Ēnsam be y…õ…õ te Wurubuar…õ be bu e f…Ē…Ē n…õ. 7Kaboena nyi balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē doo Kristo man koraŇč ya n…õ, baa baake Kristotena kegyia ŇčonaŇč yele kpaakpaa, na …Ē be nyiŇč yala …Ēb…Ēnsam k…Ēta man na des…õŇč kedi e ya.
8Mena m…Ē te kaboena nyi kegyiise baŇč b…õ…õ ky…Ē siamantena baŇč …Ēsom keŇč man n…õ, baa y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč ba wose man dana obuo te balaŇč m…Ē ne l…õ…õ w…Ē di nideli. Tekaboena nyi baa y…õ…õ soloŇčny…Ē…Ēraw…Ē ya, te be sia m…Ē na y…õ…õ k…Ēba d…Ē…Ē beiŇč ya. 9Baa mo kon…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa kyaŇč anokoare keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba lese wola w…Ē lee ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč man n…õ nideli. 10Kaboena nyi Kristotena baa gyoo kegyiise baŇč man nideli, na baa k…õ…õ nyi nombiikum kamas…õ be doo be man ya p…õna baa mo w…Ē see kegyiise.
11Mena m…Ē te kaboena nyi be w…õ…õla baa y…õ…õ ala baŇč ba wose man dana obuo, te b…Ē…Ē ne gyae balaŇč wose man nombia kekolosi ya. Kaboena nyi baa y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man, na balaŇč ke nyiŇč tale mo ba wose l…Ē be d…Ē…Ē.
12Kaboena nyi kegyia w…õ…õle d…õ…õ y…õ…õ dokoloŇč, te waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne tale k…õ…õ …Ē bia na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč d…Ē…Ē nideli. 13Kegyiise baŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ tom nideli n…õ, baa nyiŇč daseŇčae kpaakpaa na baa nyiŇč doŇč mo di adans…õ…õ lee ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč doo Yesu Kristo man n…õ man.

Te m…õ…õ Ňčmarase neŇč t…Ēne kei baa na, 15nyi me d…Ē…Ē w…Ēle koraŇč n…õ, t…Ēne kei gyae ke yela n ka gyeŇč mena keŇč kaboena nyi balaŇč baa kyaa Wurubuar…õ dekpaŇčalaŇč man. Anokoare keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba lese wola n…õ, Kristotena dikpii kei y…õna ka mok…Ē…Ē na ka tuula. 16Nyeekel…õ…õ nombia Ňčan ta tekaa y…õ…õ nombia aweeseŇč d…Ē …Ēsom keŇč man ya n…õ, y…õ…õ nombia Ňčan Ňče dana yuŇč. Nombia k…õŇča te d…õ…õ mo n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč yako nyiaa,
‚ÄúWurubuar…õ ta lese …Ē wose wola Ňčgba deniwalaŇč n…õ.
Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon ta lese e wola nyi
…Ē gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman.

te Wurubuar…õ moo e gyu adido gyoorobiiri man.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
People Will Turn from Their Faith
1God's Spirit clearly says that in the last days many people will turn from their faith. They will be fooled by evil spirits and by teachings that come from demons. 2They will also be fooled by the false claims of liars whose consciences have lost all feeling. These liars 3will forbid people to marry or to eat certain foods. But God created these foods to be eaten with thankful hearts by his followers who know the truth. 4Everything God created is good. And if you give thanks, you may eat anything. 5What God has said and your prayer will make it fit to eat.
Paul's Advice to Timothy
6If you teach these things to other followers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus. You will show you have grown up on the teachings about our faith and on the good instructions you have obeyed. 7Don't have anything to do with worthless, senseless stories. Work hard to be truly religious. 8-9As the saying goes,
‚ÄúExercise is good
for your body,
but religion helps you
in every way.
It promises life
now and forever.‚ÄĚ
These words are worthwhile and should not be forgotten. 10We have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of everyone, but especially of those who have faith. This is why we work and struggle so hard.
11Teach these things and tell everyone to do what you say. 12Don't let anyone make fun of you, just because you are young. Set an example for other followers by what you say and do, as well as by your love, faith, and purity.
13Until I arrive, be sure to keep on reading the Scriptures in worship, and don't stop preaching and teaching. 14Use the gift you were given when the prophets spoke and the group of church leaders blessed you by placing their hands on you. 15Remember these things and think about them, so everyone can see how well you are doing. 16Be careful about the way you live and about what you teach. Keep on doing this, and you will save not only yourself, but the people who hear you.
translation_text:
Ak…õr…õ…õ nombia wolala
1Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon yakow…Ē nideli nyiaa, t…õ…õle kei da benaa k…Ē …Ēto na balaŇč baale baa tina Wurubuar…õ kel…õ…õdi yela, na baa tei feliŇčkum…õ…õ na …Ēb…Ēnsam dekarate Ňčon nombia. 2Mena kawola k…õŇča lee balaŇč baŇč be d…ĒŇč…õ…õ b…õ…õ beo ba wose te bee dii ak…õr…õ…õ laŇč n…õ gyaŇč. Ba nyeeya ta yem Ňčgba nyeela te ba moow…Ē do boalaŇč man nyakee Ňča n…õ. 3B…õ…õ t…Ē balaŇč gb…õ…õ nyi be na yale ya, te b…õ…õ yako w…Ē bela nyi be na di ween…õ…õ Ňčaale ya. M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ y…õ…õ ween…õ…õ bo nyi balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ l…õ…õ e di te ba gyeŇč anokoare keŇč n…õ, baa fane fa e karaŇč na baa di. 4Nawolo nyi kpene kamas…õ keŇč Wurubuar…õ be y…õ…õ na ke dei, te akpaa nyi de fane fa e karaŇč na tekaboena nyi d…õ…õ b…õ…õ Ňča ya. 5Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan na kefane keŇč te desina Ňča.
6Akpaa n mo mena nombia k…õŇča wola Kristotena na, n ke y…õ…õ Yesu Kristo tobaale kpaakpaate Ňčon b…Ē…Ē mo kel…õ…õdi na kawola kpaakpaate keŇč n silana n…õ k…õ…õle e n…õ. 7Lese n wose lee Ňč…Ēla nombia na kawola Ňčan Ňč…Ē…Ē taŇč t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya n…õ man, na n ke sila Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom kpaakpaate keŇč gb…õ…õ nideli na ka ke male neŇč. 8Nawolo nyi wose kew…ĒŇčee dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē kyomii bo fa deniwalaŇč. M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē nombia pou man, nawolo nyi k…õ…õ fa nyeedoŇč gy…õŇč te k…õ…õ fa nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa debaŇč keŇč gyae ke k…ĒŇč n…õ man.
9Nombia k…õŇča y…õ…õ ampaŇč, te kaboena nyi d…õ…õ l…õ…õ Ňča di nideli. 10Kei d…Ē…Ē te d…õ…õ kyaŇč da wose y…õ…õ tom nideli debaŇč kamas…õ, nawolo nyi de loo gyakaa Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa kekpaakekpaa n…õ d…Ē…Ē. Ňäon y…õna balaŇč pou nyeel…õ…õre, tuturu na balaŇč baŇč pou b…õ…õ l…õ…õ e di n…õ.
11Wola w…Ē mena nombia k…õŇča, na n ke k…õ…õ nyi walaŇč kamas…õ te kasee Ňča nideli. 12Na yeli Ňčolo na Ňčolo ke k…õ…õ neŇč nyi n y…õ…õ bu e ya, m…Ēna yela n kekolosi na n din…Ē…Ē na n balaŇč kegyae na n kel…õ…õdi na n kekyaa keŇč ayim…õ…õ be doo n wose man ya n…õ, ka bese kolo kekasee fa balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di n…õ. 13P…õte maa k…ĒŇč na, m…õ…õ gyae nyi n ke y…õ…õ kakyeŇč mo n debaŇč na n doŇč kala Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan balaŇč man, na n ke yako w…Ē nyeekel…õ…õ nombia Ňčan, na n kawola w…Ē Ňče man nideli. 14Mo Wurubuar…õ kolo anefaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra be yako ke nombia, debaŇč keŇč kegyiise ba mo ba nyiŇčmaase gyakaa n d…Ē…Ē te n be nyim n…õ y…õ…õ tom nideli.
15Y…õ…õ kakyeŇč di mena nombia k…õŇča pou d…Ē…Ē debaŇč kamas…õ na n ka mo n wose fa Ňča, na balaŇč kenyiŇč naa n siaman kegyu na n nombia Ňčan n ne y…õ…õ n…õ. 16K…õ…õ n din…Ē…Ē na n kawola man nideli. Y…õ…õ kakyeŇč di Ňče d…Ē…Ē debaŇč kamas…õ. Akpaa n y…õ…õ mena na, n ke l…õ…õ …õm…õ…õ na baŇč bee tei neŇč n…õ pou nyee.

english_text:
How To Act toward Others
1Don't correct an older man. Encourage him, as you would your own father. Treat younger men as you would your own brother, 2and treat older women as you would your own mother. Show the same respect to younger women that you would to your sister.
3Take care of any widow who is really in need. 4But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should learn to serve God by taking care of her, as she once took care of them. This is what God wants them to do. 5 A widow who is really in need is one who doesn't have any relatives. She has faith in God, and she keeps praying to him night and day, asking for his help.
6A widow who thinks only about having a good time is already dead, even though she is still alive.
7Tell all this to everyone, so they will do the right thing. 8People who don't take care of their relatives, and especially their own families, have given up their faith. They are worse than someone who doesn't have faith in the Lord.
9For a widow to be put on the list of widows, she must be at least 60 years old, and she must have been faithful in marriage. 10She must also be well-known for doing all sorts of good things, such as raising children, giving food to strangers, welcoming God's people into her home, helping people in need, and always making herself useful.
11Don't put young widows on the list. They may later have a strong desire to get married. Then they will turn away from Christ 12and become guilty of breaking their promise to him. 13Besides, they will become lazy and get into the habit of going from house to house. Next, they will start gossiping and become busybodies, talking about things that are none of their business.
14I would prefer that young widows get married, have children, and look after their families. Then the enemy won't have any reason to say insulting things about us. 15Look what's already happened to some of the young widows! They have turned away to follow Satan.
16If a woman who is a follower has any widows in her family, she should help them. This will keep the church from having that burden, and then the church can help widows who are really in need.
Church Leaders
17Church leaders who do their job well deserve to be paid twice as much, especially if they work hard at preaching and teaching. 18 It is just as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúDon't muzzle an ox when you are using it to grind grain.‚ÄĚ You also know the saying, ‚ÄúWorkers are worth their pay.‚ÄĚ
19 Don't listen to any charge against a church leader, unless at least two or three people bring the same charges. 20But if any of the leaders should keep on sinning, they must be corrected in front of the whole group, as a warning to everyone else.
21In the presence of God and Christ Jesus and their chosen angels, I order you to follow my instructions! Be fair with everyone, and don't have any favorites.
22Don't be too quick to accept people into the service of the Lord by placing your hands on them.
Don't sin because others do, but stay close to God.
23Stop drinking only water. Take a little wine to help your stomach trouble and the other illnesses you often have.
24Some people get caught in their sins almost at once, even before the time of judgment. But other people's sins don't show up until later. 25It is the same with good deeds. Some are easily seen, but none of them can be hidden.
translation_text:

Be y…õ…õ mena n…õ, na b…õ…õ t…Ē be kya…Ē na be naana…Ē kom baa nawolo nyi mena te Wurubuar…õ ne gyae. 5Lialo Ňčon …Ē kyaa …Ē wose d…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē be dana walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne k…õ…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, …Ē na mo …Ē loo pou gyakaa Wurubuar…õ d…Ē…Ē bo. Mena d…Ē…Ē nelim na ka weese pou na …Ē gyakaa …Ē ne fane fa Wurubuar…õ nyi waa k…õ…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē. 6M…Ēna lialo Ňčon …Ē ne gyae t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyoŇč kedi n…õ d…Ē…Ē …Ē d…ĒŇč bo yakaa keŇč …Ē be dana nyeedoŇč ya. 7Kolosi nombia k…õŇča fa Kristotena baŇč, na Ňčolo be nyiŇč nombiikum daale yako lee ba wose man ya. 8Nyi akpaa Ňčolo b…õ…õ tale k…õ…õ …Ē loŇčtona d…Ē…Ē ya, tuturu na Ňčon gbagba …Ē denimantena d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ, na nawolo baa nyi …Ē te tina …Ē Wurubuar…õ kel…õ…õdi keŇč yela. Mena walaŇč ŇčonaŇč nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ gba kela walaŇč Ňčon …Ē b…õ…õ l…õ…õ Kristo di n…õ ya.
9Lialo Ňčon …Ē te dii ta kela kulutooneŇč s…ĒŇčotooro (60) ya na, be na kala e kpu liala dikpii keŇč man ya. Kaboena nyi waa y…õ…õ alo Ňčon …Ē be kyaa baale dokoloŇč gyaŇč, keŇč …Ē ta lee …Ē baale ŇčonaŇč w…Ēle ya. 10Waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon balaŇč na lese …Ē yele lee …Ē nombia kpaakpaa key…õ…õ man, na mena keŇč …Ē be tale k…õ…õle …Ē bia nideli te …Ē ne l…õ…õ Ňč…Ē…Ēla te …Ē ne k…õ…õ be d…Ē…Ē nideli m…Ē n…õ. Waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ balaŇč te …Ē ne k…õ…õ be d…Ē…Ē nideli, te …Ē ne y…õ…õ deeli fa baŇč ba wose be kyaŇč w…Ē m…Ē n…õ. Waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne nyiŇč debaŇč y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man.
11Liala baŇč ba te Ňčm…õ…õ ta ya n…õ, na kala w…Ē kpu mena liala dikpii kei man ya. Nawolo nyi yale de gy…Ē w…Ē loo kela ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč na, baa lee Yesu Kristo w…Ēle na be kaa yala bela. 12Nyi akpaa be y…õ…õ mena na, Wurubuar…õ waa bu w…Ē f…Ē…Ē nawolo nyi ba te yala ntam keŇč b…Ē…Ē taŇčgb…õ…õ kaŇč fa Kristo n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 13KenaŇč w…Ēle na, b…õ…õ w…Ēl…õ…õ ba debaŇč yakaa na be d…ĒŇč na akpaŇčalaŇč akpaŇčalaŇč. Kei w…Ēle na, b…õ…õ kolosi balaŇč wose man nombia na be dana ba wose b…õ…õ y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan tekaboena nyi baa y…õ…õ ya n…õ, te b…õ…õ kolosi nombia Ňčan tekaboena nyi baa kolosi ya n…õ. 14Lee mena d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ gyae nyi liala baŇč be y…õ…õ alebiaw…Ē n…õ, baa yala na baa lola bia na baa k…õ…õ ba akpaŇčalaŇčse d…Ē…Ē nideli. Be y…õ…õ mena na, de k…Ēlala baŇč be gyae baa nyiŇč nombiikum kamas…õ kolosi lee da wose man ya. 15Nawolo nyi liala baale taŇč lee Wurubuar…õ w…Ēle gyu be silana …Ēb…Ēnsam.
16Nyi akpaa Kristo alo Ňčolo dana liala …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇč man n…õ, kaboena nyi waa k…õ…õ be d…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē na mo w…Ē l…Ē Kristotena baŇč d…Ē…Ē ya. Na Kristotena baŇč ke nyiŇč k…õ…õ liala baŇč b…Ē…Ē taŇč baŇč baa k…õ…õ be d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ d…Ē…Ē.
17Kristotena kegyiise baŇč be y…õ…õ siamantenaw…Ē te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ ba tom nideli n…õ, kaboena nyi baa bu w…Ē nideli. Tuturu na kegyiise baŇč b…õ…õ kyaŇč ba wose kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan te b…õ…õ wola balaŇč Ňče man nideli n…õ. 18Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan ne yako daa nyiaa, ‚ÄúWonembu Ňčon …Ē ne tuku am…Ēla n…õ, b…Ē…Ē ne bake …Ē n…Ē…Ē ya. Mena m…Ē te walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne y…õ…õ tom na, kaboena nyi baa t…Ē e kom.‚ÄĚ 19Nyi akpaa Ňčolo da mo nombiikum daale gyakaa kegyia Ňčolo d…Ē…Ē n…õ, …õ na l…õ…õ ke do ya. Gyis…õ …õ be nyiŇč adans…õ…õtena bala yaa batooro p…õna …õ ke l…õ…õ ke do. 20BalaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ n…õ, …õ lese si w…Ē balaŇč nsana na gyakoloŇč ke nyiŇč gyoo balaŇč akaŇč baŇč m…Ē.
21M…õ…õ faree neŇč nideli lee Wurubuar…õ na Yesu Kristo na …Ē kpilala baŇč …Ē ba lese w…Ē n…õ siaman nyiaa, k…õ…õ nyi n te di mmaraase k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē keŇč nyi n be gyae Ňčolo kela Ňčolo ya.
22Na barase na n nyiŇčmaa ka mo gyakaa balaŇč d…Ē…Ē bileŇčbileŇč nyi n na lese w…Ē nyi baa y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ tom ya, te na mo n wose do balaŇč nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ man ya. Tela n wose nideli keŇč nyi n be y…õ…õ nombiikum ya.
23Lee n t…Ē…Ē kawee na n k…õ…õ wee bileŇčbileŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ, na tekaa ny…Ē…Ē loŇč kaageŇč ya m…Ēna ny…Ē…Ē soloŇč kyomii kyomii.
24BalaŇč baale wui nombiakum…õ…õ na lee deb…Ē…Ē bileŇč p…õna baa di be nombia. M…Ēna baale wui nombiakum…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē Ňča wees…õ…õ bo, w…Ēle w…Ēle p…õte na lee deb…Ē…Ē. 25Mena m…Ē te nombia kpaakpaa key…õ…õ b…õ…õ weese ya. Ňäan Ňča wees…õ…õ koraŇč n…õ, Ňče gyae kaa lee deb…Ē…Ē.

english_text:
1If you are a slave, you should respect and honor your owner. This will keep people from saying bad things about God and about our teaching. 2If any of you slaves have owners who are followers, you should show them respect. After all, they are also followers of Christ, and he loves them. So you should serve and help them the best you can.
False Teaching and True Wealth
These are the things you must teach and tell the people to do. 3Anyone who teaches something different disagrees with the correct and godly teaching of our Lord Jesus Christ. 4Those people who disagree are proud of themselves, but they don't really know a thing. Their minds are sick, and they like to argue over words. They cause jealousy, disagreements, unkind words, evil suspicions, 5and nasty quarrels. They have wicked minds and have missed out on the truth.
These people think religion is supposed to make you rich. 6And religion does make your life rich, by making you content with what you have. 7We didn't bring anything into this world, and we won't take anything with us when we leave. 8So we should be satisfied just to have food and clothes. 9People who want to be rich fall into all sorts of temptations and traps. They are caught by foolish and harmful desires that drag them down and destroy them. 10The love of money causes all kinds of trouble. Some people want money so much they have given up their faith and caused themselves a lot of pain.
Fighting a Good Fight for the Faith
11Timothy, you belong to God, so keep away from all these evil things. Try your best to please God and to be like him. Be faithful, loving, dependable, and gentle. 12Fight a good fight for the faith and claim eternal life. God offered it to you when you clearly told about your faith, while so many people listened. 13 Now I ask you to make a promise. Make it in the presence of God, who gives life to all, and in the presence of Christ Jesus, who openly told Pontius Pilate about his faith. 14Promise to obey completely and fully all that you have been told until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
15 The glorious God
is the only Ruler,
the King of kings
and Lord of lords.
At the time that God
has already decided,
he will send Jesus Christ
back again.
16 Only God lives forever!
And he lives in light
that no one can come near.
No human has ever seen God
or ever can see him.
God will be honored,
and his power
will last forever. Amen.
17Warn the rich people of this world not to be proud or to trust in wealth that is easily lost. Tell them to have faith in God, who is rich and blesses us with everything we need to enjoy life. 18Instruct them to do as many good deeds as they can and to help everyone. Remind the rich to be generous and share what they have. 19This will lay a solid foundation for the future, so they will know what true life is like.
20Timothy, guard what God has placed in your care! Don't pay any attention to godless and stupid talk that sounds smart but really isn't. 21Some people have even lost their faith by believing this talk.
I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
translation_text:
Nombia kpaakpaa wolala na nombiakum…õ…õ wolala
1∆źm…õ…õ baŇč …õ doo balaŇč t…õ…õ n…õ, …õ mo obuo fa …õ gbeŇčgyoona lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man. ∆ź y…õ…õ mena na Ňčolo be nyiŇč kolosi nombiikum lee Wurubuar…õ na de kawola keŇč wose man ya. 2Nyi akpaa Ňčolo …Ē gbeŇčgyoo y…õ…õ Kristo baale e n…õ, …Ē na k…õ…õ nyi keŇč be popou y…õ…õ Kristotenaw…Ē d…Ē…Ē …Ē be bu e nideli ya. KeŇč gba te kaboena nyi waa mo obuo fa e nideli nawolo nyi baŇč bee di ba tom t…Ēn…Ē…Ē n…õ, be y…õ…õ Kristotenaw…Ē te b…õ…õ gyae be nombia m…Ē. Mena nombia k…õŇča te kaboena nyi nya wola w…Ē na n ka do w…Ē kakyeŇč na baa di Ňče d…Ē…Ē.
3WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē na wola nombia kpoo Ňčan Ňč…Ē…Ē ne kyaa na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo kawola keŇč na Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom nombia ya n…õ, 4na mena walaŇč ŇčonaŇč y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne gyina …Ē wose adido te …Ē ba gyeŇč daale m…Ē ya. AŇčmaareŇč kum na kale nombia Ňčan Ňč…Ē…Ē taŇč t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya n…õ doo la …Ē kawola keŇč man. K…õŇča na moona nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčgba balaŇč kek…Ēla na aŇčmaareŇč kel…õ…õ na daaworo na …Ēmanso na balaŇč katee na nyeeman ditiŇčt…õ…õ na d…ĒŇča kek…Ēla n…õ k…ĒŇč. 5Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē nyeeya ta yekee, mena d…Ē…Ē te debaŇč kamas…õ na be dana nombia b…õ…õ w…ĒŇčee na d…ĒŇča. Anokoare be doo be man ya. BaŇč gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom y…õ…õ gb…õ…õ keŇč baa mo nyiŇč k…Ēba.
6Nyi akpaa kyomii keŇč n dana te n yela ke da gyoo n sia na, Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē nideli fa neŇč. 7Nawolo nyi de ta mo kolo k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ya, mena m…Ē te d…Ē…Ē gyae d…õ…õ mo kolo lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyu ya. 8M…Ēna nyi akpaa de dana ween…õ…õ na ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan d…õ…õ do na, kaboena nyi Ňče d…õ…õ gyoo de sia. 9BalaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ gyae k…Ēba beiŇč n…õ ne yale kek…õ…õsek…õ…õ na …Ēb…Ēnsam k…Ēta man, te ne yeli b…õ…õ y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē nkaraŇčkaraŇč na be ta yoo lee Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õneŇč man na ba te w…Ēl…õ…õ kekpaakekpaa. 10Nyi walaŇč de yase …Ē sia do k…Ēba kegyae d…Ē…Ē beiŇč man na, na mo e gyu dukum man. Baale te sila k…Ēba kegyae beiŇč kei te be tinaa ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč yela, te ba moo ba wose do kayey…õ…õ dinaa man.
P…Ē…Ēl kekolosi fa Timoti
11M…Ēna nyaŇč Wurubuar…õ walaŇč d…Ē…Ē yeu lee mena nombia k…õŇča pou man, na n ke sila nombia Ňčan Ňča ten…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ na Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom kpaakpaate, na nombia Ňčan n be l…õ…õ di n…õ na balaŇč kegyae na n ke y…õ…õ kakyeŇč seŇč keŇčkeŇč, na n ke bu n wose debaŇč kamas…õ. 12Yoo keŇč n ne yoo nyi keŇč n be l…õ…õ Kristo di d…Ē…Ē n…õ, seŇč keŇčkeŇč na n ke yoo ke nideli na n ke nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč. Kei d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ l…Ē…Ē yele baake neŇč, debaŇč keŇč n be gyuusu n n…Ē…Ē kolosi n kel…õ…õdi keŇč nombia balaŇč burum siaman n…õ. 13Ma seŇč…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē ne fa kpene kamas…õ nyeedoŇč n…õ, na Yesu Kristo Ňčon …Ē be di Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan adans…õ…õ kegyia P…Ēnti…Ēse Pilato gyaŇč nyi Ňče y…õ…õ anokoare n…õ siaman m…õ…õ yako neŇč nyiaa, 14k…õ…õ na n kedi mmaraase k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē. Na ky…õ…õkee Ňča ya, na n ke k…õ…õ nyi Ňčolo m…Ē be nyiŇč nombiikum daale yako lee n wose ya, kelii wee keŇč de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo waa bese k…ĒŇč. 15Wurubuar…õ waageŇč kaboela nyi d…õ…õ lese …Ē yele. Nawolo nyi Ňčon waageŇč y…õna doŇč pou nyee, na gyooneŇč pou Gyoo na gbeŇčgyoona pou GbeŇčgyoo. Ňäon ne gyae la waa yela Yesu Kristo kabese k…ĒŇč wee keŇč Ňčon gbagba ba see n…õ man. 16Ňäon waageŇč kyaa la kekpaakekpaa te …Ē b…õ…õ yem ya, te …Ē kyaa keŇčmaŇčee keŇč walaŇč b…õ…õ tale benaa ke ya n…õ man. Ňäon te walaŇč te naa ta e see ya, te Ňčolo m…Ē be gyae waa tale naa e ya. Ňäon danaana doŇč keŇč be taŇč …Ēto ya, te …Ē kaboena nyi baa lese …Ē yele debaŇč kamas…õ. Am…õn.
17Faree balaŇč baŇč be dana ba wose t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ nyi be na gyina ba wose adido ya te be na mo be loo gyakaa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan be dana n…õ d…Ē…Ē ya, nawolo nyi Ňč…Ē…Ē gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa ya. M…Ēna kaboena nyi baa mo be loo pou gyakaa Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē ne fa daa ab…Ē…Ē pou te ne y…õ…õ daa gyoŇč n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 18Faree w…Ē nyi baa y…õ…õ deeli, na baa yela be deeli key…õ…õ keŇč ka bo. Baa y…õ…õ w…Ēe na baa nyiŇč tale mo ba ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan be dana n…õ Ňčaale fa balaŇč. 19Nyi akpaa be y…õ…õ k…õŇča na, b…õ…õ y…õ…õ bo see ba wose wee daale. Kei man te baa tale mo nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč.
20Timoti, k…õ…õ kpene keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo do n nyiŇčmaa man n…õ d…Ē…Ē nideli. Lese n wose lee afa kum…õ…õ key…Ē…Ē na aŇčmaareŇč yakaa yakaa Ňčan balaŇč ne baake nyi nyansa nombia n…õ man. 21Nawolo nyi balaŇč baale kpa be dana mena nyansa kei, mena d…Ē…Ē te yeli be ta yoo lee ba kel…õ…õdi man. Wurubuar…õ waa mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele Ňčon. Am…õn.

english_text:
1From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus.
God himself chose me to be an apostle, and he gave me the promised life that Jesus Christ makes possible.
2 Timothy, you are like a dear child to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Christ Jesus will be kind and merciful to you and will bless you with peace!
Do Not Be Ashamed of the Lord
3Night and day I mention you in my prayers. I am always grateful for you, as I pray to the God my ancestors and I have served with a clear conscience. 4I remember how you cried, and I want to see you, because this would make me truly happy. 5 I also remember the genuine faith of your mother Eunice. Your grandmother Lois had the same sort of faith, and I am sure you have it as well. 6So I ask you to make full use of the gift God gave you when I placed my hands on you. Use it well. 7God's Spirit doesn't make cowards out of us. The Spirit gives us power, love, and self-control.
8Don't be ashamed to speak for our Lord. And don't be ashamed of me, just because I am in jail for serving him. Use the power that comes from God and join with me in suffering for telling the good news.
9God saved us and chose us
to be his holy people.
We did nothing
to deserve this,
but God planned it
because he is so kind.
Even before time began
God planned for Christ Jesus
to show kindness to us.
10Now Christ Jesus has come
to offer us God's gift
of undeserved grace.
Christ our Savior defeated death
and brought us
the good news.
It shines like a light
and offers life
that never ends.
11 My work is to be a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher. 12This is why I am suffering now. But I am not ashamed! I know the one I have faith in, and I am sure he can guard until the last day what he has trusted me with. 13Now follow the example of the correct teaching I gave you, and let the faith and love of Christ Jesus be your model. 14You have been trusted with a wonderful treasure. Guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit, who lives within us.
15You know that everyone in Asia has turned against me, especially Phygelus and Hermogenes.
16I pray that the Lord will be kind to the family of Onesiphorus. He often cheered me up and wasn't ashamed of me when I was put in jail. 17Then after he arrived in Rome, he searched everywhere until he found me. 18I pray that the Lord Jesus will ask God to show mercy to Onesiphorus on the day of judgment. You know how much he helped me in Ephesus.

Ňäon diina yeŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē nyeekel…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan d…Ē…Ē wola daa gb…õ…õ keŇč d…õ…õ mo nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč.

english_text:
A Good Soldier of Christ Jesus
1Timothy, my child, you must let Christ Jesus make you strong by his gift of undeserved grace. 2You have often heard me teach. Now I want you to tell these same things to followers who can be trusted to tell others.
3As a good soldier of Christ Jesus you must endure your share of suffering. 4Soldiers on duty don't work at outside jobs. They try only to please their commanding officer. 5No one wins an athletic contest without obeying the rules. 6And farmers who work hard are the first to eat what grows in their field. 7If you keep in mind what I have told you, the Lord will help you understand completely.
8Keep your mind on Jesus Christ! He was from the family of David and was raised from death, just as my good news says. 9And because of this message, I am locked up in jail and treated like a criminal. But God's good news isn't locked in jail, 10and so I am willing to put up with anything. Then God's special people will be saved and given eternal glory because they belong to Christ Jesus. 11Here is a true message:
‚ÄúIf we died with Christ,
we will live with him.
12 If we don't give up,
we will rule with him.
If we deny
that we know him,
he will deny
that he knows us.
13If we are not faithful,
he will still be faithful.
Christ cannot deny
who he is.‚ÄĚ
An Approved Worker
14Don't let anyone forget these things. And with God as your witness, you must warn them not to argue about words. These arguments don't help anyone. In fact, they ruin everyone who listens to them. 15Do your best to win God's approval as a worker who doesn't need to be ashamed and who teaches only the true message.
16Keep away from worthless and useless talk. It only leads people farther away from God. 17That sort of talk is like a sore that won't heal. And Hymenaeus and Philetus have been talking this way 18by teaching that the dead have already been raised to life. This is far from the truth, and it is destroying the faith of some people.
19But the foundation God has laid is solid. On it is written, ‚ÄúThe Lord knows who his people are. So everyone who worships the Lord must turn away from evil.‚ÄĚ
20In a large house some dishes are made of gold or silver, while others are made of wood or clay. Some of these are special, and others are not. 21This is also how it is with people. The ones who stop doing evil and make themselves pure will become special. Their lives will be holy and pleasing to their Master, and they will be able to do all kinds of good deeds.
22Run from temptations that capture young people. Always do the right thing. Be faithful, loving, and easy to get along with. Worship with people whose hearts are pure. 23Stay away from stupid and senseless arguments. These only lead to trouble, 24and God's servants must not be troublemakers. They must be kind to everyone, and they must be good teachers and very patient.
25Be humble when you correct people who oppose you. Maybe God will lead them to turn to him and learn the truth. 26They have been trapped by the devil, and he makes them obey him, but God may help them escape.
