Nawolo nyi Ňč…õ…õ desina bo w…Ēl…õ…õ walaŇč na Wurubuar…õ nsana. 17Ba afa Ňčan b…õ…õ y…Ē…Ē n…õ, ne yaasee bo Ňčgba kawee kum n…õ. BalaŇč benaŇč man baale y…õna Hameneyose na Filetuse. 18Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē ta yoo lee anokoare gb…õ…õ keŇč man. B…õ…õ wola balaŇč nyi debaŇč keŇč balaŇč baa foŇč lee yeŇč man n…õ taŇč ke k…ĒŇč laŇč, mena d…Ē…Ē ba te yeli baale ta lee w…Ēle ba kel…õ…õdi man w…Ēl…õ…õ. 19M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ anokoare keŇč seŇč…õ…õ keŇčkeŇč keŇč nyi kolo be tale wosi ke ya. Nombia k…õŇča te ba moow…Ē t…Ē ke n…Ē…Ē. Nombia ŇčenaŇč y…õna nyiaa, ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ gyeŇč balaŇč baŇč be y…õ…õ …Ē wui.‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúWalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē be l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di na, kaboena nyi waa lese …Ē wose lee nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ man.‚ÄĚ
20DekpaŇčalaŇč dinaa kamas…õ man na, be dana kuruwaase kpookpoo. K…Ēba yayam na k…Ēba wolom wui doo, te Ňčan b…Ē…Ē mo t…õ…õle na daase y…õ…õ Ňča m…Ē n…õ doo. B…õ…õ mo Ňčaale do ween…õ…õ debaŇč kamas…õ, te b…õ…õ mo Ňčaale bo see daa wee dinaa. 21Mena m…Ē te nyaŇč walaŇč Ňčon n lese n wose lee nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ man na, Wurubuar…õ waa mo neŇč y…õ…õ kolo kpaakpaa. Nawolo nyi nyaŇč walaŇč ŇčonaŇč wose taŇč ayim…õ…õ ya. N wose dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē fa n GbeŇčgyoo te n te desina n wose fa tom kpaakpaa kamas…õ key…õ…õ.
22Mena d…Ē…Ē lese n wose lee lookum keŇč ne yele alebia na tewul…õ…õ ne w…Ēl…õ…õ n…õ man. ∆źm…õ…õ na baŇč b…õ…õ mo kon…Ē…Ē kpaakpaate som Wurubuar…õ n…õ …õ mo …õ loo gyakaa nombia Ňčan ten…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē, na …õ ka seŇč nombia Ňčan …õ be l…õ…õ di n…õ d…Ē…Ē nideli na …õ ke gyae d…ĒŇča na …õ ke y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč. 23Lese n wose lee aŇčmaareŇč Ňčan Ňč…Ē…Ē taŇč t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya n…õ man, nawolo nyi n gyeŇč nyi Ňč…õ…õ mo kale bo k…ĒŇč. 24Tekaboena nyi de GbeŇčgyoo tobaale waa mo …Ē wose do kale nombia man korakora ya. M…Ēna kaboena bo nyi waa y…õ…õ deeli fa walaŇč kamas…õ, na waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia kawola, te …Ē ne nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē fa walaŇč kamas…õ. 25Kaboena nyi waa nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē na waa kolosi fa balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ l…õ…õ e aŇčmaareŇč n…õ. Na waa gyakaa loo nyi mberem Wurubuar…õ waa yela baa ky…õ…õkee be kon…Ē…Ēse, na baa gyeŇč anokoare keŇč. 26Kei gyae ke yela be sia ke ŇčmaŇčee w…Ē, na baa yeu lee …Ēb…Ēnsam Ňčon …Ē be di be d…Ē…Ē te …Ē ne yeli b…õ…õ y…õ…õ …Ē kegyaebii n…õ k…Ēta man.

english_text:
What People Will Be Like in the Last Days
1You can be certain that in the last days there will be some very hard times. 2People will love only themselves and money. They will be proud, stuck-up, rude, and disobedient to their parents. They will also be ungrateful, godless, 3heartless, and hateful. Their words will be cruel, and they will have no self-control or pity. These people will hate everything good. 4They will be sneaky, reckless, and puffed up with pride. Instead of loving God, they will love pleasure. 5Even though they will make a show of being religious, their religion won't be real. Don't have anything to do with such people.
6Some men fool whole families, just to get power over those women who are slaves of sin and are controlled by all sorts of desires. 7These women always want to learn something new, but they never can discover the truth. 8 Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, these people are enemies of the truth. Their minds are sick, and their faith isn't real. 9But they won't get very far with their foolishness. Soon everyone will know the truth about them, just as Jannes and Jambres were found out.
Paul's Last Instructions to Timothy
10Timothy, you know what I teach and how I live. You know what I want to do and what I believe. You have seen how patient and loving I am, and how in the past I put up with 11 trouble and suffering in the cities of Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. Yet the Lord rescued me from all those terrible troubles. 12In fact, anyone who belongs to Christ Jesus and wants to live right will have trouble from others. 13But evil people who pretend to be what they are not will become worse than ever, as they fool others and are fooled themselves.
14Keep on being faithful to what you were taught and to what you believed. After all, you know who taught you these things. 15Since childhood, you have known the Holy Scriptures that are able to make you wise enough to have faith in Christ Jesus and be saved. 16Everything in the Scriptures is God's Word. All of it is useful for teaching and helping people and for correcting them and showing them how to live. 17The Scriptures train God's servants to do all kinds of good deeds.
translation_text:
Kayey…õ…õ keŇč gyae ke k…ĒŇč debaŇč …Ētomante keŇč man n…õ
1Me bu Timoti, t…Ē…Ēse nyi t…õ…õle kei da benaa k…Ē …Ēto n…õ kayey…õ…õ gyae kek…ĒŇč. 2Nawolo nyi balaŇč baa gyae baŇč baageŇč wui bo na k…Ēba kegyae, na baa gyina ba wose adido na baa wola ba wose na baa tee balaŇč yakaa yakaa. Baa y…õ…õ deŇčele doŇč ba lolala d…Ē…Ē te baa y…õ…õ boniay…õtena te b…Ē…Ē gyae baa gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ ya. 3BalaŇč kegyae be gyae ke doo be man ya, te b…Ē…Ē gyae baa mo nombia ky…õ…õ ya. Baa tao balaŇč kelabere te b…Ē…Ē gyae baa tale di ba wose d…Ē…Ē ya. Baa y…õ…õ akpanyaŇčnyaŇč te b…Ē…Ē gyae baa gyae kolo kpaakpaa m…Ē ya. 4Baa lese d…ĒŇča fa na baa barase y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē. Baa gyina ba wose adido beiŇč na baa gyae t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyoŇč kedi kela Wurubuar…õ. 5Baa gyae Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom keŇč nombia na be sia, m…Ēna be kon…Ē…Ēse man na b…Ē…Ē ne l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ doŇč keŇč di ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē lese n wose lee mena balaŇč benaŇč man.
6Mena balaŇč benaŇč baale d…ĒŇč…õ…õ la na akpaŇčalaŇč akpaŇčalaŇč b…õ…õ beo ala baŇč b…Ē…Ē ne tale kyaŇč ba wose ya te nombiakum…õ…õ be wulu w…Ē, te lookum d…Ē…Ē bee sila gb…õ…õ kum kamas…õ n…õ. 7DebaŇč kamas…õ na mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē ne kasee nombia, m…Ēna b…Ē…Ē gyae baa gyeŇč anokoare keŇč ya. 8Mena keŇč Gyanese na Gyamberese ba seŇč tia Mosesi n…õ, mena ke yaa balaŇč k…õw…Ē m…Ē na sem tia anokoare nombia Ňčan. Be y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč ba nyeeya man nombia be w…Ēl…õ…õ te b…Ē…Ē dana daseŇčae kel…õ…õdi keŇč man ya. 9M…Ēna b…Ē…Ē gyae baa lii lem ya te be diyiŇč nombia Ňčan gyae ka lee deb…Ē…Ē, na walaŇč kamas…õ kena Ňčgba mena keŇč be y…õ…õ Gyanese na Gyamberese n…õ.
10M…Ēna nyaŇč d…Ē…Ē n gyeŇč me kawola na me din…Ē…Ē na nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē ma seŇč…õ…õ n…õ, na ma kel…õ…õdi na me kon…Ē…Ē kenyiŇč na me balaŇč kegyae na me kakyeŇč keŇč me dana, 11na mena keŇč b…Ē…Ē naase maŇč diyem, na diyem keŇč m…Ē…Ē naa Anti…Ēke na Ikonium na Listra don…Ē…Ēse man, na mena keŇč m…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ kakyeŇč seŇč keŇčkeŇč diyem keŇč b…Ē…Ē naase maŇč n…õ man. M…Ēna de GbeŇčgyoo lese maŇč lee nombia k…õŇča pou man. 12AmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako neŇč n ke nyii nyiaa, walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae nyi waa y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii lee de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo yele man n…õ, balaŇč baa naase e diyem. 13Te balaŇč kum…õ…õ na baŇč b…õ…õ beo balaŇč n…õ, baa bese y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ kpu. Baa beo balaŇč na balaŇč m…Ē ka beo w…Ē.
14M…Ēna nyaŇč d…Ē…Ē kyaŇč kpene keŇč n be kasee te n be l…õ…õdi n…õ man nideli, nawolo nyi n gyeŇč balaŇč baŇč gyaŇč n be kasee mena nombia ŇčenaŇč lee. 15Nawolo nyi leenaŇč n biat…õ…õ te n wulaa kasee Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan gyae ke yela n ka gyeŇč nyansa na n ke nyiŇč nyeedoŇč lee Yesu Kristo kel…õ…õdi man n…õ. 16Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan pou lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč, te Ňče dei fa nombia kawola na nombia kalese si walaŇč, na nombia kedesina na mena keŇč n ka wola walaŇč kolo lee Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii man. 17Kei gyae ke yela Wurubuar…õ walaŇč Ňčon ke desina …Ē wose nideli, na waa tale y…õ…õ tom kpaakpaate kamas…õ keŇč Wurubuar…õ ne gyae nyi waa y…õ…õ n…õ.

english_text:
1When Christ Jesus comes as king, he will be the judge of everyone, whether they are living or dead. So with God and Christ as witnesses, I command you 2to preach God's message. Do it willingly, even if it isn't the popular thing to do. You must correct people and point out their sins. But also cheer them up, and when you instruct them, always be patient. 3The time is coming when people won't listen to good teaching. Instead, they will look for teachers who will please them by telling them only what they are itching to hear. 4They will turn from the truth and eagerly listen to senseless stories. 5But you must stay calm and be willing to suffer. You must work hard, telling the good news and to do your job well.
6Now the time has come for me to die. My life is like a drink offering being poured out on the altar. 7I have fought well. I have finished the race, and I have been faithful. 8So a crown will be given to me for pleasing the Lord. He judges fairly, and on the day of judgment he will give a crown to me and to everyone else who wants him to appear with power.
Personal Instructions
9Come to see me as soon as you can. 10 Demas loves the things of this world so much that he left me and went to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia. 11 Only Luke has stayed with me.
Mark can be very helpful to me, so please find him and bring him with you. 12 I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
13 When you come, bring the coat I left at Troas with Carpus. Don't forget to bring the scrolls, especially the ones made of leather.
14 Alexander, the metalworker, has hurt me in many ways. But the Lord will pay him back for what he has done. 15Alexander opposes what we preach, so you had better watch out for him.
16When I was first put on trial, no one helped me. In fact, everyone deserted me. I hope it won't be held against them. 17But the Lord stood beside me. He gave me the strength to tell his full message, so that all Gentiles would hear it. And I was kept safe from hungry lions. 18 The Lord will always keep me from being harmed by evil, and he will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. Praise him forever and ever! Amen.
Final Greetings
19 Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and to the family of Onesiphorus.
20 Erastus stayed at Corinth.
Trophimus was sick when I left him at Miletus.
21Do your best to come before winter.
Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, and Claudia send you their greetings, and so do the rest of the Lord's followers.
22I pray that the Lord will bless your life and will be kind to you.
translation_text:
Kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia debaŇč kamas…õ
1Timoti, t…Ē…Ēse nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo waa bese k…ĒŇč …Ē gyoorobiiri man na …Ē kaa di balaŇč baŇč be kyaa na be sia, na baŇč b…Ē…Ē yekee n…õ pou nombia. Mena d…Ē…Ē m…õ…õ faree neŇč nideli lee Wurubuar…õ na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo siaman nyiaa, 2kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan fa balaŇč. Y…õ…õ kakyeŇč kolosi Ňča debaŇč kamas…õ. Kolosi fa w…Ē na n ka wola w…Ē gb…õ…õ keŇč kaboena nyi baa mo, na n ka lese nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan balaŇč ne y…õ…õ n…õ si w…Ē, na n ka do w…Ē kakyeŇč na n ke nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē lee n kawola keŇč man. 3Nawolo nyi debaŇč daale gyae kek…ĒŇč keŇč balaŇč baa b…õ…õ nyi b…Ē…Ē tei nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan ya. Te lee mena keŇč ba deŇčela man na toree w…Ē d…Ē…Ē n…õ, baa ta yilaa ak…õr…õ…õ wolala na be kaa kolosi nombia Ňčan baŇč gbagba ne gyae nyi baa nyii n…õ fa w…Ē. 4Baa b…õ…õ anokoare keŇč kenyii, na baa tei Ňč…Ēla nombia. 5M…Ēna nyaŇč Timoti d…Ē…Ē, yela n sia ke doo t…õ…õle man na n ke y…õ…õ kakyeŇč seŇč keŇčkeŇč diyem kamas…õ man. Y…õ…õ tom keŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia kolosire ne y…õ…õ n…õ, na n ke y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom tom keŇč nideli kelii …Ēto.
6Nawolo nyi maŇč P…Ē…Ēl d…Ē…Ē, ma debaŇč te lii nyi baa ky…õ…õkee me fatabo leki Ňčgba mena keŇč bee deesi te b…õ…õ toosi soloŇč woli n…õ. Ma debaŇč te lii nyi maa lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. 7Ma te yoo yoo kpaakpaa keŇč taŇč, te ma te yeu keyeu keŇč m…Ē kelii …Ēto. Ma te kyaŇč ma kel…õ…õdi keŇč man m…Ē kelii …Ēto. 8Mena d…Ē…Ē de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo Ňčon …Ē ne di nombia na Ňče gb…õ…õ n…õ, te desina gyooro kot…Ēt…Ē…Ē keŇč nawolo nyi me gb…õ…õneŇč ten…õ…õ n…õ see na waa mo t…Ē maŇč kom. Na maageŇč te waa y…õ…õ mena fa ya, m…Ēna baŇč pou bee gyeki …Ē kabese k…ĒŇč n…õ m…Ē kpu…õ.
9Timoti, y…õ…õ kakyeŇč na n kek…ĒŇč me gyaŇč bileŇč, 10nawolo nyi keŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē kegyae loo doo Demase man d…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ē te tina maŇč yela laŇč Tesalonika don…Ē…Ē man. Kires…õnse te lam Galatia don…Ē…Ē man, te Tituse m…Ē te lam Dalematia don…Ē…Ē man. 11Luka waageŇč kyaa la me gyaŇč. N ne k…ĒŇč na mo Maake kpu na n wose k…ĒŇč, nawolo nyi …Ē ne ky…Ē maŇč ma Wurubuar…õ tom key…õ…õ man. 12Me te kpila Tikikuse nyi waa gyu Efeso don…Ē…Ē man. 13Akpaa n ne k…ĒŇč na, mo ma kegba wurikyim keŇč m…Ē…Ē tina yela Kapuse gyaŇč Torase don…Ē…Ē man n…õ na me t…Ēna Ňčan k…Ēna maŇč. Tuturu na mo t…Ēna Ňčan b…Ē…Ē mo wonembu t…Ēne y…õ…õ Ňča n…õ k…Ēna maŇč.
14Al…õkesanda Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ luure e n…õ, te y…õ…õ maŇč nombiikum daale. De GbeŇčgyoo waa t…Ē e kom lee nombiikum keŇč …Ē be y…õ…õ maŇč n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 15Mena d…Ē…Ē k…õ…õ n wose man nideli na e, nawolo nyi …Ē b…õ…õ gyae de nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan d…õ…õ yako n…õ korakora ya.
16Me nombia kedi gyaŇčgbate keŇč man n…õ walaŇč na walaŇč ta sem me w…Ēle ya, be popou b…õ…õ maŇč bo. M…õ…õ sola Wurubuar…õ nyi …Ē na mo mena nombia k…õŇča t…Ē w…Ē kom ya. 17M…Ēna de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu seŇčaw…Ē me w…Ēle, te …Ē doo maŇč kakyeŇč te me kolosi …Ē nyeedoŇč nombia Ňčan fa baŇč pou ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ, te be nyiiw…Ē te Wurubuar…õ lese maŇč lee yeŇč man. 18Mena d…Ē…Ē de GbeŇčgyoo waa lese maŇč lee dukum kamas…õ man, na waa mo maŇč gyu …Ē gyoori keŇč man adido wosef…õ…õreŇč man. Ňäon te kaboena nyi baa lese …Ē yele kekpaakekpaa. Am…õn.
19M…õ…õ do Pirisila na Akuila na Onesiforuse na …Ē dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena pou n…Ē…Ē. 20Erasituse kyaa Korinto don…Ē…Ē man, te me te tina Tirofimuse Ňčon …Ē na wee n…õ yela Miletuse don…Ē…Ē man.
21Y…õ…õ kakyeŇč na n kek…ĒŇč p…õna waare debaŇč keŇč kelii. Ubuluse na Pudense na Linuse na Kl…Ē…Ēdia na de tebia Kristotena baŇč pou na do neŇč n…Ē…Ē.
22De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo waa kpu na neŇč, na Wurubuar…õ ka mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč boele Ňčon.

english_text:
1From Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ.
I encourage God's own people to have more faith and to understand the truth about religion. 2Then they will have the hope of eternal life God promised long ago. And God never tells a lie! 3So, at the proper time, God our Savior gave this message and told me to announce what he had said.
4 Titus, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and Christ Jesus our Savior will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
What Titus Was To Do in Crete
5I left you in Crete to do what had been left undone and to appoint leaders for the churches in each town. As I told you, 6 they must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. Their children must be followers of the Lord and not have a reputation for being wild and disobedient.
7Church officials are in charge of God's work, and so they must also have a good reputation. They must not be bossy, quick-tempered, heavy drinkers, bullies, or dishonest in business. 8Instead, they must be friendly to strangers and enjoy doing good things. They must also be sensible, fair, pure, and self-controlled. 9They must stick to the true message they were taught, so their good teaching can help others and correct everyone who opposes it.
10There are many who don't respect authority, and they fool others by talking nonsense. This is especially true of some Jewish followers. 11But you must make them be quiet. They are after money, and they upset whole families by teaching what they should not. 12It is like one of their own prophets once said,
‚ÄúThe people of Crete
always tell lies.
They are greedy and lazy
like wild animals.‚ÄĚ
13This surely is a true saying. And you should be hard on such people, so you can help them grow stronger in their faith. 14Don't pay any attention to any of those senseless Jewish stories and human commands. These are made up by people who won't obey the truth.
15Everything is pure for someone whose heart is pure. But nothing is pure for an unbeliever with a dirty mind. That person's mind and conscience are destroyed. 16Such people claim to know God, but their actions prove they really don't. They are disgusting. They won't obey God, and they are too worthless to do anything good.
translation_text:
1T…Ēne kei lee maŇč Wurubuar…õ tobaale P…Ē…Ēl Ňčon me y…õ…õ Yesu Kristo kpilale e n…õ gyaŇč. Wurubuar…õ lesena maŇč nyi me kaa ky…Ē balaŇč baŇč Ňčon Wurubuar…õ gbagba ba lese w…Ē see fa …Ē wose n…õ, na baa seŇč ba kel…õ…õdi man keŇčkeŇč na maa wola w…Ē Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ n…õ, na baa gyeŇč Ňča kpu nideli. Nombia k…õŇča kagyeŇč ne gyae la ke tale yela balaŇč ke y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii. 2Kei gyae ke yela baa nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaa keŇč d…Ē…Ē de loo gyakaa n…õ. Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē bee di dekara ya n…õ, wulaa yakow…Ē see leenaŇč kewalaŇč keŇč nyi dee nyiŇč mena nyeedoŇč kekpaa kei p…õte …Ē y…õ…õ kpene kamas…õ. 3Mena d…Ē…Ē debaŇč kpaakpaate keŇč Ňčon Wurubuar…õ gbagba ba see kelii n…õ, …Ē lese mena nombia k…õŇča deb…Ē…Ē. Da nyeel…õ…õre Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē ne fa balaŇč pou nyeedoŇč n…õ, Ňčon yako na mena nombia k…õŇča fa maŇč P…Ē…Ēl nyi me kaa yako fa balaŇč pou. 4M…õ…õ Ňčmarase t…Ēne kei mee yelee nyaŇč Tituse Ňčon m…Ē…Ē mo neŇč Ňčgba me bu gbagba n…õ. Nawolo nyi Kristo te daa na neŇč pou l…õ…õw…Ē di. De kya Wurubuar…õ na da nyeel…õ…õre Yesu Kristo baa mo be b…õr…õ…õ boele neŇč na baa fa neŇč wosef…õ…õreŇč.
Tituse key…õ…õ tom Kreti t…õ…õle man
5Kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē te m…Ē…Ē tina neŇč yela Kreti t…õ…õle man y…õna nyi, m…õ…õ gyae na n kenyiŇč desina ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan be kaa na kedesina n…õ nideli, na n ka lese kegyiise fa don…Ē…Ē kamas…õ Ňčgba mena keŇč m…Ē…Ē wola neŇč n…õ. 6Kaboena nyi Kristotena kegyia waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē wose man be dana nombiikum kamas…õ ya. ∆Ü w…õ…õle d…õ…õ y…õ…õ dokoloŇč. Kaboena nyi …Ē bia m…Ē baa y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ l…õ…õ Yesu di te bee bu balaŇč, te ba wose man be dana nombiikum daale keŇč balaŇč baa naa kolosi ya. 7Lee mena keŇč kegyia seŇč…õ…õ la Wurubuar…õ tom keŇč siaman d…Ē…Ē n…õ, tekaboena nyi baa nyiŇč nombiikum kamas…õ lee …Ē wose man ya. ∆Ü na y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē na wola …Ē wose yaa …Ē na gyeŇč baŇč bileŇčbileŇč ya. ∆Ü na y…õ…õ soloŇčny…Ē…Ēre yaa walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne gyae kale yaa walaŇč Ňčon …Ē sia ne y…õ…õ k…Ēba d…Ē…Ē beiŇč ya. 8M…Ēna kaboena nyi waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne l…õ…õ Ňč…Ē…Ēla, te …Ē ne gyae nombia kpaakpaa key…õ…õ. Kaboena nyi waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē gyeŇč …Ē wose katela, te …Ē nombia ay…õ…õsa m…Ē doo na ke gb…õ…õ. Kaboena nyi waa y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon ayim…õ…õ be doo …Ē man ya te …Ē ne tale di …Ē wose d…Ē…Ē. 9Kaboena nyi waa kyaŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ Ňčgba mena keŇč b…Ē…Ē wolo n…õ man nideli. Kaboena nyi waa kyaŇč Ňča nideli na waa nyiŇč tale mo nyeedoŇč nombia Ňčan wola balaŇč na ke gb…õ…õ, na waa lese nombiakum…õ…õ wolala baŇč nombiakum…õ…õ si w…Ē.
10Nawolo nyi balaŇč burum ta koro seŇč tia Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan, te be dana be nombia Ňčan Ňč…Ē…Ē taŇč t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya n…õ b…õ…õ beo balaŇč. Tuturu na Kristotena baŇč be y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē te be s…õ…õ be dana baala wose kekara mmaraa keŇč man n…õ, ne y…õ…õ na mena nombia k…õŇča. 11Kaboena nyi yaa t…Ē w…Ē mena nombia ŇčenaŇč kawola, nawolo nyi be s…õ…õ be dana nombia Ňčan tekaboena nyi baa wola ya n…õ man. Kei te yeli akpaŇčalaŇč akpaŇčalaŇč man balaŇč burum ta yoo gb…õ…õ lee kawola kpaakpaate keŇč man. Ke y…õ…õ des…õŇč nombii ke, nawolo nyi k…Ēba kegyae d…Ē…Ē te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ k…õŇča pou n…õ. 12BaŇč Kretitena benaŇč gbagba be kegyia Ňčolo yako ba wose man nombia nyiaa, ‚ÄúKretitena y…õ…õ ak…õr…õ…õtenaw…Ē. Be dana akpanyaŇčnyaŇč Ňčgba wonembu kum n…õ, te be y…õ…õ keb…ĒŇčaletena na diiraw…Ē.‚ÄĚ 13Mena nombii keŇč …Ē be yako n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč, mena d…Ē…Ē faree Kristotena baŇč be kyaa Kreti t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē n…õ nideli nyi baa kyaŇč ba Kristo kel…õ…õdi keŇč man nideli. 14Na baa tina Gyudatena beneŇč nombia Ňčan Ňča te y…õ…õ ampaŇč ya, na balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē ne l…õ…õ anokoare keŇč di ya n…õ mmaraase d…Ē…Ē kedi yela.
15BalaŇč baŇč ayim…õ…õ be doo be man ya n…õ, kpene kamas…õ dei fa w…Ē. M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč ayim…õ…õ doo be man te b…Ē…Ē ne l…õ…õ Kristo nombia Ňčan di ya n…õ d…Ē…Ē, kolo be kyaa keŇč ke dei fa w…Ē ya. Nawolo nyi nombiakum…õ…õ te di ba agyueŇč na be kon…Ē…Ē man nombia pou d…Ē…Ē, te nombiakum…õ…õ key…õ…õ m…Ē be dana w…Ē des…õŇč ya. 16B…õ…õ gy…Ē be kon…Ē…Ē yako nyi ba gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ, m…Ēna be nombia ay…õ…õsa b…õ…õ wolo nyi ba gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ ya. Be y…õ…õ balaŇč kum…õ…õw…Ē Wurubuar…õ siaman te b…Ē…Ē ne bu e ya. Ba tekaboena nombii kpaakpaa m…Ē key…õ…õ ya.

english_text:
Instructions for Different Groups of People
1Titus, you must teach only what is correct. 2Tell the older men to have self-control and to be serious and sensible. Their faith, love, and patience must never fail.
3Tell the older women to behave as those who love the Lord should. They must not gossip about others or be slaves of wine. They must teach what is proper, 4so the younger women will be loving wives and mothers. 5Each of the younger women must be sensible and kind, as well as a good homemaker, who puts her own husband first. Then no one can say insulting things about God's message.
6Tell the young men to have self-control in everything.
7Always set a good example for others. Be sincere and serious when you teach. 8Use clean language that no one can criticize. Do this, and your enemies will be too ashamed to say anything against you.
9Tell slaves always to please their owners by obeying them in everything. Slaves must not talk back to their owners 10or steal from them. They must be completely honest and trustworthy. Then everyone will show great respect for what is taught about God our Savior.
God's Kindness and the New Life
11God has shown us undeserved grace by coming to save all people. 12He taught us to give up our wicked ways and our worldly desires and to live decent and honest lives in this world. 13We are filled with hope, as we wait for the glorious return of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ. 14 He gave himself to rescue us from everything evil and to make our hearts pure. He wanted us to be his own people and to be eager to do right.
15Teach these things, as you use your full authority to encourage and correct people. Make sure you earn everyone's respect.
translation_text:
Nombia Ňčan kaboena nyi nya wola Kristotena
1NyaŇč d…Ē…Ē wola w…Ē anokoare nombia Ňčan ne kyaa na Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan n…õ. 2Wola baala kegyiise baŇč nyi baa yela be sia kedoo t…õ…õle man. Baa y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč obuo doo ba wose man, na baa gyeŇč ba wose katela. Wola w…Ē na baa l…õ…õ anokoare nombia Ňčan di, na baa gyae balaŇč lee be kon…Ē…Ēse man na baa nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē seŇč ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč man nideli.
3Mena m…Ē te wola ala kegyiise baŇč nyi baa kyaa Ňčgba balaŇč baŇč bee yee Wurubuar…õ n…õ. Be na y…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč bee kolosi balaŇč wose man nombia ya, te be na y…õ…õ soloŇčny…Ē…Ēra ya. M…Ēna kaboena bo nyi baa wola nombia kpaakpaa. 4Nyi akpaa be y…õ…õ mena na, baa tale wola alebia m…Ē na baa gyae be baala na be bia nombia, 5na baa gyeŇč ba wose katela keŇč b…Ē…Ē w…Ēl…õ…õ ba wose ya. Na baa y…õ…õ ala yalala kpaakpaa nyibaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ deeli, te b…õ…õ y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose fa be baala. Dek…ĒŇč mena na, Ňčolo be gyae waa nyiŇč nombiikum daale kolosi lee Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan wose man ya.
6Mena ke te do tewul…õ…õ m…Ē kakyeŇč na baa tale di ba wose d…Ē…Ē. 7Kaboena nyi nya y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa ab…Ē…Ē pou man na balaŇč kena na baa kasee neŇč. N nombia kawola man na yela balaŇč kena nya anokoare na n sibiiyam. 8Kolosi nombia Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ n…õ, na balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ koro seŇč tia neŇč n…õ be nyiŇč nombiikum daale yako lee n wose man ya. Na des…õŇč ke nyiŇč kyaŇč w…Ē nawolo nyi b…Ē…Ē gyae baa nyiŇč nombiikum daale kolosi lee da wose man ya.
9Wola tewul…õ…õ na alebia nyi baa y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose fa be gbeŇčgyoona lee kpene kamas…õ man, na baa y…õ…õ kpene keŇč gyae ka gyoo be gbeŇčgyoona baŇč sia. Be na kolosi na be gbeŇčgyoona baŇč ky…õ…õky…õ…õ ya. 10Be na Ňčm…õ…õle be gbeŇčgyoona baŇč kolo ya. M…Ēna baa y…õ…õ kpene keŇč gyae ke yela be gbeŇčgyoona baŇč ke l…õ…õ w…Ē di nideli. Na be nombia ay…õ…õsa pou ke yela da nyeel…õ…õre Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan b…õ…õ wolo n…õ kegy…Ē balaŇč loo.
11Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ ta lese …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč gyae ke yela balaŇč ke nyiŇč nyeedoŇč n…õ wola balaŇč pou. 12Wurubuar…õ b…õr…õ…õ kenaŇč na wola daa mena keŇč dee tina t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē nombia, na gb…õ…õneŇč Ňčan Ňča te y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õneŇč ya n…õ kesila yela. Na d…õ…õ tale di da wose d…Ē…Ē na de gb…õ…õneŇč m…Ē ka tenee Wurubuar…õ siaman, na d…õ…õ kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii man. 13∆ź yela d…õ…õ y…õ…õ mena debaŇč keŇč de kyaa d…õ…õ daa Wurubuar…õ na da nyeel…õ…õre Yesu Kristo kabese k…ĒŇč …Ē gyoorobiiri man keŇč d…Ē…Ē de loo gyakaa n…õ. 14Yesu moona …Ē wose fa nyi baa ko na waa mo t…Ē da nyeeman kom l…õ…õ daa lee de nombiakum…õ…õ pou man. Na d…õ…õ bese balaŇč baŇč de y…õ…õ Ňčon waageŇč wui, na waa desina daa na d…õ…õ tale y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa.
15Mo n doŇč pou wola w…Ē mena ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča. Do baŇč b…õ…õ y…Ēk…Ēse n…õ kakyeŇč, na n ka lese baŇč m…Ē b…õ…õ y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ n…õ nombiakum…õ…õ si w…Ē. Na yela be man Ňčolo ke k…õ…õ neŇč nyi n y…õ…õ bu e ya.

english_text:
Doing Helpful Things
1Remind your people to obey the rulers and authorities and not to be rebellious. They must always be ready to do something helpful 2and not say cruel things or argue. They should be gentle and kind to everyone. 3We used to be stupid, disobedient, and foolish, as well as slaves of all sorts of desires and pleasures. We were evil and jealous. Everyone hated us, and we hated everyone.
4God our Savior showed us
how good and kind he is.
5He saved us because
of his mercy,
and not because
of any good things
we have done.
God washed us by the power
of the Holy Spirit.
He gave us new birth
and a fresh beginning.
6God sent Jesus Christ
our Savior
to give us his Spirit.
7Jesus treated us much better
than we deserve.
He made us acceptable to God
and gave us the hope
of eternal life.
8This message is certainly true.
These teachings are useful and helpful for everyone. I want you to insist that the people follow them, so that all who have faith in God will be sure to do good deeds. 9But don't have anything to do with stupid arguments about ancestors. And stay away from disagreements and quarrels about the Law of Moses. Such arguments are useless and senseless.
10Warn troublemakers once or twice. Then don't have anything else to do with them. 11You know their minds are twisted, and their own sins show how guilty they are.
Personal Instructions and Greetings
12 I plan to send Artemas or Tychicus to you. After he arrives, please try your best to meet me at Nicopolis. I have decided to spend the winter there.
13 When Zenas the lawyer and Apollos get ready to leave, help them as much as you can, so they won't have need of anything.
14Our people should learn to spend their time doing something useful and worthwhile.
15Greetings to you from everyone here. Greet all of our friends who share in our faith.
I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
translation_text:
Mena keŇč kaboena nyi Kristotena baa kyaa
1T…Ē…Ēse Kristotena baŇč nyi baa y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose fa gyooneŇč na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē kegyiise, na baa di kegyiise baŇč mmaraase d…Ē…Ē, na baa doo ba wose d…Ē…Ē na ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa nyiŇčan key…õ…õ debaŇč kamas…õ. 2Kolosi fa w…Ē nyi be na mo nombiikum gyakaa Ňčolo d…Ē…Ē ya m…Ēna baa yela baŇč na balaŇč nsana ke y…õ…õ gyoŇč. Be na ŇčmanyaŇč balaŇč ya te debaŇč kamas…õ m…Ē na kaboena nyi baa y…Ēk…Ēse ba wose fa walaŇč kamas…õ.
3Nawolo nyi naf…Ē gyaŇčgba keŇč na daa m…Ē y…õ…õ ayiŇčtenaw…Ē te de y…õ…õ deŇčele doŇč te da yoo gb…õ…õ. Da ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan d…õ…õ gyae, na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ab…Ē…Ē kegyae dii da nyeeya. Da kekyaabii man na, de dana agyueŇč kum…õ…õ na sibii. BalaŇč k…Ēla daa te daa m…Ē k…Ēla w…Ē. 4M…Ēna debaŇč keŇč da nyeel…õ…õre Wurubuar…õ ba lese …Ē deeli na …Ē balaŇč kegyae keŇč deb…Ē…Ē n…õ, 5…Ē l…õ…õ da nyee. Kei te y…õ…õ nyi daa gbagba tom kpaakpaa keŇč d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē te …Ē l…õ…õ da nyee ya, m…Ēna …Ē wii de waraŇčase bo te …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon d…Ē…Ē saase de nombiakum…õ…õ pou leki, te …Ē yela de din…Ē…Ē ky…õ…õkeew…Ē Ňčgba ba bese yaa lola daa w…Ēle n…õ. 6Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē da nyeel…õ…õre Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē yela …Ē feliŇč Ňčon ka kyaaw…Ē de man nideli. 7Wurubuar…õ y…õ…õw…Ē mena na ke yela waa l…õ…õ daa do, na dee nyiŇč nyeedoŇč kekpaakekpaa keŇč de loo gyakaa ke d…Ē…Ē n…õ, lee …Ē b…õr…õ…õ keŇč d…Ē…Ē. 8Mena nombia k…õŇča y…õ…õ ampaŇč te m…õ…õ gyae nyi n ke yase n sia wola Ňče man nideli. Kei gyae ke yela baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di n…õ ke kyaŇč ba wose y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa. Nawolo nyi mena nombia k…õŇča y…õ…õ nombia kpaakpaa Ňča, te Ňče dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē fa walaŇč kamas…õ.
9∆ź tina diyiŇč na aŇčmaareŇč nombia yela. ∆ź tina …õ lolo kegyakaare d…Ē…Ē aŇčmaareŇč kel…õ…õ yela. ∆ź lese …õ wose lee kale na aŇčmaareŇč lee Gyudatena mmaraase Ňčan wose man. Nawolo nyi mena nombia k…õŇča pou y…õ…õ yakaa te Ňč…Ē…Ē taŇč t…Ēn…Ē…Ē daale ya. 10WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē doo …õ man te …Ē na koro seŇč tia Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan te …Ē na mo aŇčmaareŇč do balaŇč man na, kolosi na e dokoloŇč yaa bola. Akpaa …Ē te nyii neŇč ya na, lese n wose lee …Ē man. 11GyeŇč nyi mena walaŇč kei din…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õy…õ…õre e te …Ē b…õ…õ gyae anokoare ya. Ňäon gbagba nombia ay…õ…õsa ne buu na e f…Ē…Ē.
N…Ē…Ēkado …Ētomante
12Maa kpila Atemase yaa Tikikuse n gyaŇč. ∆Ü k…ĒŇč na y…õ…õ kakyeŇč na n kaa gyaŇčee na maŇč Nikopolise don…Ē…Ē man. Nawolo nyi ma te y…õ…õ ma agyueŇč nyi maa kyaa bot…Ē…Ē waare t…õ…õ. 13Y…õ…õ kakyeŇč na n ke ky…Ē Senase Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ mmaraa wolale e n…õ na Apolo be gb…õ…õ keta man, na n ke k…õ…õ nyi baa nyiŇč kpene kamas…õ keŇč b…õ…õ gyae. 14Kaboena nyi daa Kristotena d…õ…õ mo da debaŇč y…õ…õ kolo kpaakpaa, na d…õ…õ tale nyiŇč kolo ky…Ē balaŇč na baa lee ba diyem man. Tekaboena nyi d…õ…õ kyaa kehuhoori man ya.
15BalaŇč baŇč pou be kyaa me gyaŇč k…õbo n…õ na do neŇč n…Ē…Ē. Yako de gyoona baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Kristo di te b…õ…õ gyae daa n…õ nyi d…õ…õ do w…Ē n…Ē…Ē. Wurubuar…õ waa mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele …õ popou.

english_text:
1From Paul, who is in jail for serving Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is like a brother because of our faith.
Philemon, you work with us and are very dear to us. This letter is to you 2 and to the church that meets in your home. It is also to our dear friend Apphia and to Archippus, who serves the Lord as we do.
3I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
Philemon's Love and Faith
4Philemon, each time I mention you in my prayers, I thank God. 5I hear about your faith in our Lord Jesus and about your love for all God's people. 6As you share your faith with others, I pray they may come to know all the blessings Christ has given us. 7My friend, your love has made me happy and has greatly encouraged me. It has also cheered the hearts of God's people.
Paul Speaks to Philemon about Onesimus
8Christ gives me the courage to tell you what to do. 9But I would rather ask you to do it simply because of love. Yes, as someone in jail for Christ, 10 I beg you to help Onesimus! He is like a son to me because I led him to Christ here in jail. 11Before this, he was useless to you, but now he is useful both to you and to me.
12Sending Onesimus back to you makes me very sad. 13I would like to keep him here with me, where he could take your place in helping me while I am here in prison for preaching the good news. 14But I won't do anything unless you agree to it first. I want your act of kindness to come from your heart, and not be something you feel forced to do.
15Perhaps Onesimus was taken from you for a little while so you could have him back for good, 16but not as a slave. Onesimus is much more than a slave. To me he is a dear friend, but to you he is even more, both as a person and as a follower of the Lord.
17If you consider me a friend because of Christ, then welcome Onesimus as you would welcome me. 18If he has cheated you or owes you anything, charge it to my account. 19With my own hand I write: I, PAUL, WILL PAY YOU BACK. But don't forget you owe me your life. 20My dear friend and follower of Christ our Lord, please cheer me up by doing this for me.
21I am sure you will do all I have asked, and even more. 22Please get a room ready for me. I hope your prayers will be answered, and I can visit you.
23 Epaphras is also here in jail for being a follower of Christ Jesus. He sends his greetings, 24 and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, who work together with me.
25I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
translation_text:
1T…Ēne kei lee maŇč P…Ē…Ēl Ňčon me silana Yesu Kristo d…Ē…Ē te b…Ē…Ē kyaŇč maŇč t…Ē deni n…õ, na de gyoo Kristobaale Timoti gyaŇč. D…õ…õ Ňčmarase ke dee yelee nyaŇč de gyoo gyaagyi Filem…Ēn Ňčon daa na neŇč ne y…õ…õ tom dokoloŇč n…õ, 2na Kristotena baŇč b…õ…õ kyaa gyaŇčee n dekpaŇčalaŇč man n…õ, na de gyoo Kristo alo Afia, na de gyoo Akipuse Ňčon daa na e y…õ…õ yoon…Ē…Ētenaw…Ē Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan keyako man n…õ.
3De kya Wurubuar…õ na de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be b…õr…õ…õ boele Ňčon na baa fa Ňčon wosef…õ…õreŇč.
4Me d…Ē…Ē Filem…Ēn debaŇč kamas…õ keŇč m…õ…õ t…Ē…Ēse neŇč ma kefane man na, m…õ…õ fa me Wurubuar…õ karaŇč lee n d…Ē…Ē, 5nawolo nyi me nyii mena keŇč n ne gyae Wurubuar…õ balaŇč baŇč …Ē be baake w…Ē te …Ē be desina w…Ē see fa …Ē wose n…õ, na mena keŇč n be l…õ…õ de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu di n…õ. 6M…õ…õ fane nyi Wurubuar…õ waa ky…Ē neŇč na n ke nyiŇč tale kolosi Kristo nombia Ňčan n be l…õ…õ di n…õ nideli, na ke yela n ka gyeŇč ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa Ňčan pou Wurubuar…õ be fa daa lee keŇč daa na Yesu y…õ…õ dokoloŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ. 7Me d…Ē…Ē, n balaŇč kegyae keŇč na do maŇč kakyeŇč te k…õ…õ y…õ…õ maŇč gyoŇč nideli, nawolo nyi k…õ…õ do Wurubuar…õ balaŇč baŇč …Ē be baake w…Ē te …Ē be desina w…Ē see fa …Ē wose n…õ kakyeŇč nideli.
P…Ē…Ēl doo Filem…Ēn teree fa Onesimuse
8Kristo d…Ē…Ē naf…Ē me dana gb…õ…õ nyi maa Ňčere neŇč nyi nya y…õ…õ kpene keŇč kaboena nyi nya y…õ…õ n…õ. 9M…Ēna lee kegyae d…Ē…Ē m…Ē…Ē na Ňčere neŇč ya, m…õ…õ waase neŇč bo. MaŇč P…Ē…Ēl Ňčon ma seŇč…õ…õ Yesu naw…Ē…Ē man te n…õn…õ…õ me s…õ…õ me doo deni man lee Ňčon d…Ē…Ē n…õ ne waase na neŇč n…õ. 10Me bu Onesimuse nombia yaa m…õ…õ sola neŇč n…õ. Kristo d…Ē…Ē …Ē ta bese me bu nawolo nyi me d…Ē…Ē te …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē l…õ…õ Kristo di debaŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē kyaŇč maŇč t…Ē deni n…õ. 11GyaŇčgba keŇč na naf…Ē …Ē be dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē daale fa neŇč ya, m…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …Ē dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē fa daa na neŇč pou.
12N…õn…õ…õ m…õ…õ besena e baa nyi waa bese k…ĒŇč n gyaŇč. ∆Ü y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē nombia doo me kon…Ē…Ē man nideli. 13Naf…Ē me gyaew…Ē nyi waa kyaa n naw…Ē…Ē man me gyaŇč k…õbo, na waa ky…Ē maŇč debaŇč keŇč me doo deni man lee nyeedoŇč nombia Ňčan keyako d…Ē…Ē n…õ. 14M…Ēna m…Ē…Ē ne gyae nyi maa Ňčere neŇč nyi nya ky…Ē maŇč ya, m…õ…õ gyae bo nyi nya ky…Ē maŇč lee nyaŇč gbagba n kon…Ē…Ē man. Mena d…Ē…Ē nyi n te s…õ…õ ya na m…Ē…Ē gyae maa y…õ…õ kolo ya. 15Mberem na Onesimuse w…ĒŇča …Ē wose bo lee n gyaŇč debaŇč kyomii na ke yela waa nyiŇč kaa bese n wui kekpaakekpaa. 16N…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …Ē ta tekaa …Ē y…õ…õ n gbeŇči e kaageŇč ya, …Ē kela gbeŇči. ∆Ü y…õ…õ de naabu gyaagyi e. M…õ…õ gyae …Ē nombia nideli te ma gyeŇč nyi nyaŇč ne gyae …Ē nombia nideli gba kela maŇč. Nawolo nyi …Ē y…õ…õ n gbeŇči e te …Ē besew…Ē …Ē y…õ…õ n naabu e de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu d…Ē…Ē.
17Mena d…Ē…Ē nyi akpaa n gyeŇč nyi me y…õ…õ n d…Ē…Ē e na, l…õ…õ e do Ňčgba mena keŇč nyaŇč ke l…õ…õ maŇč do n…õ. 18Nyi akpaa …Ē te y…õ…õ neŇč nombiikum daale, yaa …Ē dana neŇč kolo kom na mo ke nyi maŇč danaana neŇč kom n…õ. 19MaŇč P…Ē…Ēl gbagba moona me nyiŇčmaa m…õ…õ Ňčmarase mena nombii kei n…õ. Maa t…Ē neŇč kom keŇč …Ē dana neŇč n…õ, m…Ēna na wolee nyi nyaŇč gbagba koraŇč dana maŇč kom ya. Nawolo nyi maŇč yele la te n nyiŇča nyeedoŇč w…Ēle Kristo man. Kei na wolo baa nyi n dibiliŇč koraŇč y…õ…õ me wui. 20Mena d…Ē…Ē me d…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa y…õ…õ nombii kei fa maŇč lee de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ yele man. Kristo d…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ ke fa maŇč, na n ke yela me w…Ēe ke f…õ…õ maŇč. 21M…õ…õ l…õ…õdi nyi me t…Ēne kei gyae ke yela n ke y…õ…õ kpene keŇč m…Ē…Ē yako neŇč n…õ, te ma gyeŇč nyi nyaŇč ke y…õ…õ gba kela mena.
22M…õ…õ Ňčmarase neŇč t…Ēne kei n…õ, m…õ…õ gyae nyi n ke desina dekyae see maŇč nawolo nyi ma gyeŇč nyi Wurubuar…õ waa nyii …õ kefane keŇč pou, na waa besena maŇč k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč.
23Epafirase Ňčon lee Kristo Yesu d…Ē…Ē te b…Ē…Ē kyaŇč daa na e t…Ē deni n…õ, na do neŇč n…Ē…Ē. 24Mena ke te Maake na Arisitakuse na Demase na Luka baŇč daa na w…Ē ne y…õ…õ tom dokoloŇč n…õ m…Ē na do neŇč n…Ē…Ē.
25De GbeŇčgyoo Yesu Kristo waa mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele …õ popou na waa seŇč …õ w…Ēle.

english_text:
1Long ago in many ways and at many times God's prophets spoke his message to our ancestors. 2 But now at last, God sent his Son to bring his message to us. God created the universe by his Son, and everything will someday belong to the Son. 3 God's Son has all the brightness of God's own glory and is like him in every way. By his own mighty word, he holds the universe together.
After the Son had washed away our sins, he sat down at the right side of the glorious God in heaven. 4He had become much greater than the angels, and the name he was given is far greater than any of theirs.
God's Son Is Greater than Angels
5 God has never said
to any of the angels,
‚ÄúYou are my Son, because today
I have become your Father!‚ÄĚ
Neither has God said
to any of them,
‚ÄúI will be his Father,
and he will be my Son!‚ÄĚ
6 When God brings his first-born Son into the world, he commands all of his angels to worship him.
7 And when God speaks about the angels, he says,
‚ÄúI change my angels into wind
and my servants
into flaming fire.‚ÄĚ
8 But God says about his Son,
‚ÄúYou are God,
and you will rule
as King forever!
Your royal power
brings about justice.
9You loved justice
and hated evil,
and so I, your God,
have chosen you.
I appointed you
and made you happier
than any of your friends.‚ÄĚ
10 The Scriptures also say,
‚ÄúIn the beginning, Lord,
you were the one
who laid the foundation
of the earth
and created the heavens.
11They will all disappear
and wear out like clothes,
but you will last forever.
12You will roll them up
like a robe
and change them
like a garment.
But you are always the same,
and you will live forever.‚ÄĚ
13 God never said to any
of the angels,
‚ÄúSit at my right side
until I make your enemies
into a footstool for you!‚ÄĚ
14 Angels are merely spirits sent to serve people who are going to be saved.
translation_text:
Wurubuar…õ keta …Ē bu d…Ē…Ē kolosi fa balaŇč
1GyaŇčgba gyaŇčgba keŇč na, Wurubuar…õ t…õ…õw…Ē wa akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra d…Ē…Ē kolosi agbaatee agbaatee fa de naana…Ē lee gb…õ…õneŇč kpookpoo man. 2M…Ēna debaŇč kei man keŇč t…õ…õle kei ne gyu k…Ē …Ēto n…õ, …Ē t…õe …Ē bu Yesu d…Ē…Ē kolosi fa daa. ∆Ü bu ŇčonaŇč d…Ē…Ē te …Ē t…õ…õw…Ē y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei pou. english_text:
This Great Way of Being Saved
1We must give our full attention to what we were told, so we won't drift away. 2The message spoken by angels proved to be true, and all who disobeyed or rejected it were punished as they deserved. 3So if we refuse this great way of being saved, how can we hope to escape? The Lord himself was the first to tell about it, and people who heard the message proved to us that it was true. 4God himself showed that his message was true by working all kinds of powerful miracles and wonders. He also gave his Holy Spirit to anyone he chose to.
The One Who Leads Us To Be Saved
5We know that God did not put the future world under the power of angels. 6 Somewhere in the Scriptures someone says to God,
‚ÄúWhat makes you care
about us humans?
Why are you concerned
for weaklings such as we?
7You made us lower
than the angels
for a while.
Yet you have crowned us
with glory and honor.
8And you have put everything
under our power!‚ÄĚ
God has put everything under our power and has not left anything out of our power. But we still don't see it all under our control. 9What we do see is Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels. Because of God's gift of undeserved grace, Jesus died for everyone. And now that Jesus has suffered and died, he is crowned with glory and honor!
10Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory. 11Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters. 12 He even said to God,
‚ÄúI will tell them your name
and sing your praises
when they come together
to worship.‚ÄĚ
13 He also said,
‚ÄúI will trust God.‚ÄĚ
Then he said,
‚ÄúHere I am with the children
God has given me.‚ÄĚ
14We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death. 15But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying. 16 Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants. 17He had to be one of us, so he could serve God as our merciful and faithful high priest and sacrifice himself for the forgiveness of our sins. 18And now that Jesus has suffered and was tempted, he can help anyone else who is tempted.

english_text:
Jesus Is Greater than Moses
1My friends, God has chosen you to be his holy people. So think about Jesus, the one we call our apostle and high priest! 2 Jesus was faithful to God, who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in serving all God's people. 3But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house. 4Of course, every house is built by someone, and God is really the one who built everything.
5Moses was a faithful servant and told God's people what would be said in the future. 6But Christ is the Son in charge of God's people. And we are those people, if we keep on being brave and don't lose hope.
A Rest for God's People
7 It is just as the Holy Spirit says,
‚ÄúIf you hear God's voice today,
8don't be stubborn!
Don't rebel like those people
who were tested
in the desert.
* 9 For forty years your ancestors
tested God and saw
the things he did.
10‚ÄúThen God got tired of them
and said,
‚ÄėYou people never
show good sense,
and you don't understand
what I want you to do.‚Äô
11God became angry
and told the people,
‚ÄėYou will never enter
my place of rest!‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
12My friends, watch out! Don't let evil thoughts or doubts make any of you turn from the living God. 13You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called ‚Äútoday.‚ÄĚ If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn. 14We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end. 15 The Scriptures say,
‚ÄúIf you hear his voice today,
don't be stubborn
like those who rebelled.‚ÄĚ
16 Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses? 17Who were the people that made God angry for 40 years? Weren't they the ones that sinned and died in the desert? 18And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him? 19We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.

english_text:
1The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out. 2We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good. 3 Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say,
‚ÄúGod became angry
and told the people,
‚ÄėYou will never enter
my place of rest!‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation. 4 In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested. 5 We also read that he later said, ‚ÄúYou people will never enter my place of rest!‚ÄĚ 6This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter. 7 Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said,
‚ÄúIf you hear his voice today,
don't be stubborn!‚ÄĚ
8 If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest. 9But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come. 10 On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
11We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did. 12God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts. 13 Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth.
Jesus Is the Great High Priest
14We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him. 15Jesus understands every weakness of ours, because he was tempted in every way that we are. But he did not sin! 16So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.

english_text:
1Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins. 2A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people. 3 This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others. 4 But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
5 This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him,
‚ÄúYou are my Son, because today
I have become your Father!‚ÄĚ
6 In another place, God says,
‚ÄúYou are a priest forever
just like Melchizedek.‚ÄĚ
7 God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers. 8Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God. 9Suffering made Jesus perfect, and now he can save forever all who obey him. 10This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek.
Warning against Turning Away
11Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand. 12 By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food. 13People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right. 14Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.

Na waa l…õ…õ ba kedeesa ab…Ē…Ē mo gyu ke deesi fa Wurubuar…õ, na Wurubuar…õ ke nyiŇč mo be nombiakum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ w…Ē. 2Ňäon gbagba m…Ē y…õ…õ deniwalaŇč e te …Ē ne tale y…õ…õ kpene keŇč be dei ya, mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē ne nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē fa balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč ya te b…õ…õ y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan be dei ya te ne w…ĒŇčee w…Ē lee Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õneŇč man n…õ. 3Mena d…Ē…Ē te kaboena nyi waa taŇčgb…õ…õ deesi fa Wurubuar…õ lee Ňčon gbagba wui nombiakum…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē, p…õna waa tale deesi fa …Ē balaŇč baŇč m…Ē wui nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ n…õ. 4Akpaa Wurubuar…õ te baake Ňčolo nyi …Ē kaa y…õ…õ …Ē sae kegyia Ňčgba mena keŇč …Ē be baake Aar…Ēn ya n…õ, walaŇč ŇčonaŇč gbagba be tale see …Ē wose Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia ya. 5Mena te Kristo m…Ē ta see …Ē wose Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia ya. english_text:
1We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God. 2And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment. 3Let's grow up, if God is willing.
4-6But what about people who turn away after they have already seen the light and have received the gift from heaven and have shared in the Holy Spirit? What about those who turn away after they have received the good message of God and the powers of the future world? There is no way to bring them back. What they are doing is the same as nailing the Son of God to a cross and insulting him in public!
7A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field. 8 But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
9My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved. 10God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people. 11We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is. 12Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them.
God's Promise Is Sure
13No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham, 14 ‚ÄúI, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!‚ÄĚ 15Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised. 16When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves. 17So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow. 18God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed.
We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us. 19 This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place. 20 Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.

4-5Nyi Wurubuar…õ da toro walaŇč agyueŇč te …Ētenate da gyeŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia, te Wurubuar…õ da mo …Ē b…õr…õ…õ boele e te …Ē mo …Ē feliŇč Ňčon do …Ē man te …Ē nyii Wurubuar…õ nombia te …Ē naa nyi Ňče dei te …Ē gyeŇč doŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ waa kaa lese wola debaŇč …Ētomante keŇč man n…õ, lee k…õŇča pou w…Ēle te …Ētenate da besena …Ē w…Ēle tee Wurubuar…õ na, kolo be kyaa keŇč deniwalaŇč waa y…õ…õ na kabesena …Ētenate k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč ya. 6Mena d…Ē…Ē nyi akpaa ba lee mena nombia k…õŇča w…Ēle n…õ, gyae ke y…õ…õ doŇč fa w…Ē nyi baa tina be nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan b…õ…õ y…õ…õ n…õ yela na baa k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč bela. Be nombia ay…õ…õsa ŇčenaŇč nawolo baa nyi, na y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba ba bese yaa b…õ…õ gy…Ē Wurubuar…õ bu Ňčon baa sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē fulu e balaŇč man n…õ.
7T…õ…õle keŇč …Ēga ne Ňč…õ…õ woli ke te ne yeli w…Ē…Ēfarale ne nyiŇč ween…õ…õ kpaakpaa lee ke man n…õ, Wurubuar…õ ne hiraa na ke. 8M…Ēna akpaa s…Ē…Ēse na Ňčalese ne tuu la ke d…Ē…Ē na, na mena t…õ…õle kenaŇč be dana t…Ēn…Ē…Ē ya. Te n te k…õ…õ ya na Wurubuar…õ waa tale falaa ke, na waa yela boalaŇč ke kpa ke.
9Me gyoona kpaakpaa, keŇč na de k…õ…õ yako kei koraŇč n…õ, da gyeŇč nyi …õm…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē nombia kpaakpaa Ňčaale doo …õ man, keŇč Ňč…õ…õ kyaa na nyeekel…õ…õ nombia Ňčan. 10Wurubuar…õ y…õ…õ walaŇč Ňčon …Ē ne di …Ē nombia ke gb…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē. Mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē be gyae waa wolee …õ tom keŇč …õ be y…õ…õ, na …õ balaŇč kegyae keŇč …õ na lese wola e n…õ ya. Nawolo nyi lee Ňčon d…Ē…Ē …õ te ky…Ē …õ tebia Kristotena, te …õ gyakaa …õ ne ky…Ē w…Ē. 11Da kegyaebii y…õna nyi, …õ man walaŇč kamas…õ waa kyaŇč nombia kpaakpaa k…õŇča man nideli kelii …Ēto. Kei gyae ke yela …õ ka gyeŇč nideli nyi …õ ke nyiŇč kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē …õ loo gyakaa n…õ. 12D…Ē…Ē ne gyae nyi …õ man Ňčolo waa y…õ…õ keb…ĒŇčale Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom keŇč man ya. M…Ēna d…õ…õ gyae bo nyi yaa kasee balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē l…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ di te b…Ē…Ē nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ kakyeŇč seŇč ba kel…õ…õdi keŇč man kelii …Ēto, te Wurubuar…õ be fa w…Ē kpene keŇč …Ē be yako see nyi waa fa w…Ē n…õ.
Wurubuar…õ nombii ayakoŇč see te k…ĒŇč mena
13DebaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ be yako …Ē nombii see fa de naana Aberaham n…õ, …Ē moo Ňčon Wurubuar…õ gbagba yele bo kaŇč ntam. Nawolo nyi na Ňčolo be kyaa keŇč …Ē kela Ňčon Wurubuar…õ keŇč waa mo walaŇč ŇčonaŇč yele kaŇč ntam ya. 14Wurubuar…õ yako de naana Aberaham nyiaa, ‚ÄúAmpaŇč yaa m…õ…õ yako neŇč nyi maa hiraa neŇč, na maa yela n duuluŇč man balaŇč ka bo nideli.‚ÄĚ 15Mena d…Ē…Ē de naana Aberaham m…Ē nyiŇča kon…Ē…Ē kyaa daa kpene keŇč Wurubuar…õ be yako see nyi waa fa e n…õ, te …Ē nyiŇčmaa kaŇča ke.
16Nyi akpaa Ňčolo ne gyae waa kaŇč ntam na, …Ē na mo walaŇč Ňčon …Ē kela e n…õ yele bo kaŇč ntam. Ntam kekaŇč kenaŇč na wola la nyi, nombia Ňčan …Ētenate be yako n…õ y…õ…õ ampaŇč keŇč aŇčmaareŇč kel…õ…õ be doo ke man ya. 17KeŇč Wurubuar…õ ne gyae nyi balaŇč baŇč baa nyiŇč kpene keŇč …Ē be yako see nyi waa fa w…Ē n…õ, ka gyeŇč nideli nyi …Ē be gyae waa ky…õ…õkee wa agyueŇč ya d…Ē…Ē n…õ, mena d…Ē…Ē te …Ē kaŇča ntam kpu na nombii keŇč …Ē be yako see n…õ. 18Nombii keŇč Wurubuar…õ be yako see na ntam keŇč …Ē be kam n…õ, …Ē be gyae waa ky…õ…õkee nombia ala k…õŇča man daale ya nawolo nyi …Ē bee di dekara ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē nombii kei na do daa baŇč d…Ē…Ē yeu kaa gyoo e n…õ kakyeŇč da kel…õ…õdi man, na d…õ…õ tale kyaŇč kpene keŇč d…Ē…Ē …Ē be yeli de loo gyakaa n…õ man nideli. 19Da nyeedoŇč pou seŇč…õe mena lookegyakaa kei d…Ē…Ē. KeŇč yele la te de nyiŇča gb…õ…õ gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč man, bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo kanyaŇč kpase ke keŇč naf…Ē gyaŇčgba na walaŇč yakaate bee nyiŇč gb…õ…õ gyoo bot…Ē…Ē ya n…õ. 20Yesu te kaa bese Wurubuar…õ saese pou kegyia kekpaakekpaa, Ňčgba mena keŇč M…õkes…õd…õke be di Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia kekpaakekpaa n…õ. Te …Ē te kyaŇč daa gb…õ…õ ka gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč man, na daa m…Ē ke sila e.

english_text:
The Priestly Family of Melchizedek
1 Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings. 2Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had.
The meaning of the name Melchizedek is ‚ÄúKing of Justice.‚ÄĚ But since Salem means ‚Äúpeace,‚ÄĚ he is also ‚ÄúKing of Peace.‚ÄĚ 3We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
4Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies. 5 The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests. 6Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise. 7Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
8Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive. 9Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth. 10This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
11Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron. 12And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
13The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar. 14Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
15All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest. 16That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end. 17 The Scriptures say about him,
‚ÄúYou are a priest forever,
just like Melchizedek.‚ÄĚ
18In this way a weak and useless command was put aside, 19because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
20-21 God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is,
‚ÄúI, the Lord, promise that you
will be a priest forever!
And I will never
change my mind!‚ÄĚ
22This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God. 23There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died. 24But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever! 25He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
26Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven, 27 and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself. 28The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
translation_text:

4∆źm…õ…õ gbagba …õ k…õ…õ na, …õ kena nyi …Ē y…õ…õ walaŇč dinaa e te …Ē dana yele m…Ē. De naana Aberaham Ňčon …Ē dana yele Wurubuar…õ siaman koraŇč n…õ kpase ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan …Ē be nyim lee yoo man n…õ akpeŇč kufu, te …Ē moo Ňče man dekpeŇč fa M…õkes…õd…õke ŇčonaŇč. 5Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan nawolo nyiaa, kaboena nyi Gyudatena baŇč be y…õ…õ de naana Aberaham …Ē naanasew…Ē n…õ, baa kpala ba ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan be dana n…õ akpeŇč kufu na baa mo Ňče man dekpeŇč dokoloŇč fa Lewitena baŇč be y…õ…õ saesew…Ē n…õ. DebaŇč kenaŇč na …Ē s…õ…õ …Ē doo …Ē naana Aberaham teŇča man.
11DebaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ be t…õ…õ de naana Mosesi d…Ē…Ē mo mmaraase Ňčan fa Gyudatena n…õ, …Ē lese balaŇč lee Lewitena man nyi baa di saere. M…Ēna …Ē ta lee Aar…Ēn Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ Lewi baale e n…õ duuluŇč man ya. 12Nyi walaŇč Ňčon …Ē te y…õ…õ Lewi baale e ya n…õ de kaa di Wurubuar…õ saere na, na kaboena nyi baa ky…õ…õkee mmaraa keŇč, nawolo nyi Lewitena baageŇč te kaboena nyi baa di Wurubuar…õ saere. 13Mena nombia k…õŇča d…õ…õ yako n…õ, na Yesu Kristo wose man nombia yaa d…õ…õ yako n…õ. Nawolo nyi …Ē ta lee Lewi dekpaŇčalaŇč man ya te …Ē kaa dii Wurubuar…õ saere. ∆Ü lee dekpaŇčalaŇč kpoo daale man, te Ňčolo m…Ē te dii ta Wurubuar…õ saere lee mena dekpaŇčalaŇč kenaŇč man see ya. 14De popou gyeŇč nyi de GbeŇčgyoo Yesu lee Gyuda dekpaŇčalaŇč man, te de naana Mosesi m…Ē te yako kolo lee Gyuda dekpaŇčalaŇčmantena benaŇč saere kedi wose man ya.
Yesu y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ sae e Ňčgba M…õkes…õd…õke n…õ
15N…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē nombii kei man kenyii be dana baŇč bela ya. Nawolo nyi walaŇč w…Ēle kpoo k…ĒŇčala kaa di Wurubuar…õ saere Ňčgba M…õkes…õd…õke n…õ. 16∆Ü saere keŇč …Ē be kaa dii n…õ, te y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē mmaraase yaa dekpaŇčalaŇč keŇč man …Ē ba lee n…õ d…Ē…Ē te …Ē dii ke ya. M…Ēna …Ē dii saere kenaŇč bo lee …Ē doŇč keŇč …Ē dana, keŇč yeŇč be tale di …Ē d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 17Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see yakow…Ē nyi, Wurubuar…õ yako Kristo nyiaa,
‚ÄúN y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ sae e kekpaakekpaa,
Ňčgba mena keŇč M…õkes…õd…õke m…Ē be di
Wurubuar…õ saere kekpaakekpaa n…õ.‚ÄĚ
18Mena d…Ē…Ē lee gb…õ…õ daale man na, d…õ…õ tale yako nyi Wurubuar…õ ta mo Mosesi mmaraase ben…õ…õ Ňčan see yenaŇč. Mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ te fa daa lookegyakaa kpaakpaate keŇč ka kela bene keŇč. Lee mena d…Ē…Ē n…õn…õ…õ d…õ…õ tale tuu benaa Wurubuar…õ.
20Wurubuar…õ kaŇča ntam p…õte …Ē see Yesu saere kekpaakekpaa, m…Ēna saese akaŇč baŇč d…Ē…Ē …Ē b…õ…õ kaŇč ntam p…õte b…õ…õ see w…Ē saere ya. 21Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč ne yako nyi, p…õ Wurubuar…õ ka see Yesu saere na …Ē kaŇča ntam nyiaa,

22Wurubuar…õ ntam keŇč …Ē be kam n…õ, keŇč yele la te Yesu kaa bese walaŇč Ňčon …Ē seŇč…õ…õ ntam kenaŇč w…Ēle, na ka wola nyi n…Ē…Ēkebakesee w…Ēle kei dei kela bene keŇč. 23GyaŇčgba keŇč na Wurubuar…õ saese baŇč boe, nawolo nyi yeŇč bee yeli baa y…õ…õ be saere tom keŇč kelii …Ēto ya. 24M…Ēna Yesu d…Ē…Ē …Ē saere kedi keŇč be gyae ka lee …Ē gyaŇč gyu Ňčolo gyaŇč ya, nawolo nyi Ňčon kyaa kekpaakekpaa. 25Lee mena d…Ē…Ē waa tale l…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč pou b…õ…õ t…õ…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč n…õ nyee, nawolo nyi …Ē kyaa kekpaakekpaa te …Ē ne fane fa Wurubuar…õ lee be d…Ē…Ē.

M…Ēna mmaraase Ňčan w…Ēle na Wurubuar…õ kaŇča ntam kpu na nombii keŇč …Ē be yako see n…õ, te …Ē lese …Ē bu Yesu Kristo Ňčon dukum kamas…õ be doo …Ē man ya n…õ nyi waa bese …Ē sae kegyia kekpaakekpaa.

english_text:
A Better Promise
1 What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven. 2He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
3Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer. 4If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices. 5 But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, ‚ÄúBe sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!‚ÄĚ 6Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
7If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one. 8 But the Lord found fault with it and said,
‚ÄúI tell you the time will come,
when I will make
a new agreement
with the people of Israel
and the people of Judah.
9It won't be like the agreement
that I made
with their ancestors,
when I took them by the hand
and led them out of Egypt.
They broke their agreement
with me,
and I stopped caring
about them!
10‚ÄúBut now I tell the people
of Israel
this is my new agreement:
‚ÄėThe time will come
when I, the Lord,
will write my laws
on their minds and hearts.
I will be their God,
and they will be
my people.
11Not one of them
will have to teach another
to know me, their Lord.‚Äô
‚ÄúAll of them will know me,
no matter who they are.
12I will treat them with kindness,
even though they are wicked.
I will forget their sins.‚ÄĚ
13When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.

10N…Ē…Ēkebakesee w…Ēle keŇč maa bake na me balaŇč Iseraetena y…õna nyiaa,
‚ÄėMaa mo me nombia Ňčan do ba agyueŇčse man,

yaa Ňčolo m…Ē ke yako …Ē loŇčto nyi
‚ÄėMaa wola neŇč Wurubuar…õ gb…õ…õneŇč ya!‚Äô
Nawolo nyi be popou baa gyeŇč maŇč lee
ba kemaasa d…Ē…Ē kelii be kegyia d…Ē…Ē.
12Maa mo be nombiakum…õ…õ pou ky…õ…õ w…Ē,
keŇč m…Ē…Ē gyae maa tekaa t…Ē…Ēse ab…Ē…Ē
Ňčan b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ tia maŇč n…õ bela ya.‚ÄĚ
13Wurubuar…õ ke baake n…Ē…Ēkebakesee kei nyi n…Ē…Ēkebakesee w…Ēle n…õ, na …Ē na wolo baa nyi gyaŇčgbate keŇč te y…õ…õ bene. Te kpene kamas…õ keŇč ka de y…õ…õ bene na, k…Ē…Ē doo kolo man bela ya. Nawolo nyi be te nyiŇč kolo w…Ēle see ke naw…Ē…Ē man.

english_text:
The Tent in Heaven
1The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth. 2 The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
3 Behind the curtain was the most holy place. 4 The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them. 5 On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy.
Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things. 6 But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties. 7 However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
8All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship. 9This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices. 10These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
11 Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world. 12Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
13 According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf. 14But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
15Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings! 16In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use. 17In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
18Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect. 19 Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water. 20He told the people, ‚ÄúWith this blood God makes his agreement with you.‚ÄĚ 21 Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship. 22 The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered.
Christ's Great Sacrifice
23These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better. 24This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
25Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal. 26If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
27We die only once, and then we are judged. 28 So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_text:
Gyudatena …Ēsom

Siamante keŇč man te ba moo kanea na tebulu keŇč d…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ mo bodobodo gyakaa fa Wurubuar…õ kedi n…õ see. Bot…Ē…Ē te b…õ…õ baake nyi Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa siamante. 3W…Ēlete keŇč m…Ē te b…õ…õ baake nyi Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante. 4Ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan doo bot…Ē…Ē y…õna kedeesa boe keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo k…Ēba yayam y…õ…õ ke te b…õ…õ too oliifi ke d…Ē…Ē n…õ, na adakaa keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo k…Ēba yayam mataa mataa ke pou kilisi n…õ. Kei y…õna kpene keŇč maa tale yako Ňčon debaŇč kei, nawolo nyi m…Ē…Ē gyae maa tale lese nombia k…õŇča man pou wola Ňčon debokeiman ya.
6Nyi akpaa ba see ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča pou deni keŇč man taŇč n…õ, Wurubuar…õ saese baŇč na gyoo deni siamante keŇč man ke y…õ…õ be saere tom wee kamas…õ. 7M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia Ňčon waageŇč te kaboena nyi waa gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč man. Kulutoo dokoloŇč man na, dokoloŇč kooŇč te …Ē na gyoo ke man. P…õte waa gyoo ke man m…Ē na, kaboena nyi waa nyiŇč wonembu fatabo keŇč waa mo gyoraa …Ē wose na …Ē balaŇč baŇč pou lee be nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan be ta gyem ya te b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ n…õ d…Ē…Ē. 8Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon d…ĒŇč nombia k…õŇča d…Ē…Ē …Ē na wola daa lee ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča man nideli nyiaa, debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ kanyaŇč …Ēsom deni dinaa kei nyiŇča ka seŇč…õ…õ n…õ, na gb…õ…õ ta toro ta fa balaŇč yakaa nyibaŇč nyi baa gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč man ya. 9Kei y…õ…õ nombia Ňčan doo fa daa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč, na d…õ…õ kasee kolo lee ke man. Nombia k…õŇča na wola daa baa nyi, wonembia na ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan balaŇč ba mo deesi pataa Wurubuar…õ n…õ, te tale gberaa ba ayim…õ…õ na baa gyeŇč be kon…Ē…Ēse man nyi Wurubuar…õ ta mo be nombiakum…õ…õ ky…õ…õ w…Ē ya. 10Nawolo nyi b…Ē …Ēsom pou y…õ…õ wosenaane man …Ēsom ke. 11N…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē Kristo y…õna de Wurubuar…õ sae kegyia, te …Ē ta mo ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa k…ĒŇč …Ēsom w…Ēle keŇč n…õn…õ…õ de dana n…õ man. Mena ab…Ē…Ē k…õŇča kela ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan naf…Ē Ňče doo …Ēsom bene keŇč man n…õ. Te Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man …Ē ne y…õ…õ …Ē tom n…õ, kela bene keŇč lee gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man. Ka te y…õ…õ …Ēsom deni keŇč denibalaŇč ba mo be nyiŇčmaase ma ke ya. Mena d…Ē…Ē ka te y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē …Ēsom deni ke ya. 12DebaŇč keŇč …Ē ba gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč man n…õ, na asoose na nunkyuese bia fatabo te …Ē moow…Ē gyoo bot…Ē…Ē ya. M…Ēna Ňčon gbagba fatabo te …Ē moow…Ē gyoo bot…Ē…Ē ke deesi karatetee dokoloŇč mo t…Ē nombiakum…õ…õ kom kekpaakekpaa. Nawolo nyi na ayim…õ…õ kamas…õ be doo …Ē man ya, te …Ē t…õe Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon …Ē kyaa kekpaakekpaa n…õ doŇč d…Ē…Ē mo …Ē wose deesi fa Wurubuar…õ lee de nombiakum…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē. ∆Ü fatabo keŇč ne faafo na de nombiakum…õ…õ lee de kon…Ē…Ēse man, na d…õ…õ tale som Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa kekpaakekpaa n…õ nideli.
15Lee kei d…Ē…Ē Kristo te kaa bese walaŇč Ňčon …Ē doo daa na Wurubuar…õ nsana lee n…Ē…Ēkebakesee w…Ēle keŇč man, na ke yela balaŇč baŇč Wurubuar…õ be baake w…Ē n…õ ke nyiŇč kpene keŇč …Ē be yako see nyi waa fa w…Ē kekpaakekpaa n…õ. Te lee Kristo yeŇč kei d…Ē…Ē, nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan balaŇč be y…õ…õ debaŇč keŇč be doo n…Ē…Ēkebakesee gyaŇčgbate keŇč man n…õ, Wurubuar…õ ta mo Ňče popou ky…õ…õ w…Ē.
16Nyi akpaa Ňčolo de y…õ…õ …Ē yeŇč w…Ēle ab…Ē…Ē kekpala t…Ēne see n…õ, kaboena nyi …Ētenate waa yeŇč p…õna Ňčolo ke di wa ab…Ē…Ē. Nawolo nyi walaŇč ta yeŇča ta ya na, b…Ē…Ē ne di wa ab…Ē…Ē ya. 17Nyi Ňčolo da yem na, p…õte …Ē yeŇč w…Ēle ab…Ē…Ē kekpala t…Ēne keŇč ne y…õ…õ tom. Nyi akpaa walaŇč Ňčon …Ē be y…õ…õ ke n…õ ta yeŇča ta ya na, t…Ēne kenaŇč b…õ…õ y…õ…õ tom ya. 18Lee kei d…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ moo fatabo t…Ē n…Ē…Ēkebakesee gyaŇčgbate keŇč n…Ē…Ē. 19Mena d…Ē…Ē te debaŇč keŇč Mosesi be yako balaŇč baŇč pou Wurubuar…õ mmaraase Ňčan taŇč n…õ, …Ē moo nunkyuese bia na asoose fatabo mo kyakale na loŇč. KenaŇč w…Ēle te …Ē moo wona Ňčaale Ňčgba kekuŇč n…õ te …Ē kpera daŇč daale tombii, te …Ē moo Ňča bom s…Ē…Ē loŇč na fatabo keŇč man te …Ē moow…Ē Ňčmatee mmaraa t…Ēne keŇč na balaŇč baŇč pou mo gyoraa w…Ē. 20KeŇčte Mosesi yako balaŇč baŇč nyiaa, kei y…õna fatabo keŇč Wurubuar…õ ba mo t…Ē nombia Ňčan …õm…õ…õ na e be bake see nyi yaa di Ňče d…Ē…Ē n…õ n…Ē…Ē. 21Mena m…Ē te Mosesi moo fatabo keŇč Ňčmatee Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč, na …Ēsom ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou. 22Ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč nyi Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan nawolo nyi, fatabo te b…õ…õ mo gberaa kpene kamas…õ lese ka ayim…õ…õ. Mena d…Ē…Ē nyi akpaa fatabo ta lee ya na, kenaŇč na dukum ka mo ky…õ…õ m…Ē be kyaa ya.
23Mena d…Ē…Ē kaboena nyi baa mo wonembia deesi, na baa mo gberaa ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan Ňče kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē te Ňča seŇč…õ…õ fa adido wui Ňčan n…õ. M…Ēna adido wui Ňčan gbagba d…Ē…Ē, kaboena nyi baa mo kpene keŇč k…õ…õ gyoo sibii kela wonembia fatabo n…õ deesi bo mo gyoraa Ňča. 24Nawolo nyi na …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč b…Ē…Ē kasee adido wui keŇč y…õ…õ n…õ t…Ē…Ēman te Kristo gyoow…Ē ya, m…Ēna …Ē gyoe adido gbagba bot…Ē…Ē keŇč Wurubuar…õ kyaa n…õ. Te n…õn…õ…õ …Ē seŇč…õ…õ de naw…Ē…Ē man Wurubuar…õ siaman …Ē na sola e fa daa. 25Kulutoo kamas…õ na, Gyudatena saese kegyia Ňčon na mo wonembu fatabo gyu Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč man ke deesi. M…Ēna Kristo d…Ē…Ē Ňčon ta mo …Ē wose gyu bot…Ē…Ē ke deesi fa Wurubuar…õ kulutoo na kulutoo ya. 26Nyi Kristo deŇč y…õ…õw…Ē mena n…õ, naf…Ē Ňčon naa diyem agbaatee agbaatee mo leenaŇč debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ be y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei kewalaŇč n…õ. M…Ēna t…õ…õle kei kee gyu k…Ē …Ēto n…õ, …Ē lese …Ē wose wola te …Ē moo …Ē wose deesi karatetee dokoloŇč na waa mo lese de nombiakum…õ…õ. 27Doo fa deniwalaŇč kamas…õ nyi yeŇč dokoloŇč te waa yeŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē. Lee kenaŇč w…Ēle na Wurubuar…õ waa di …Ē nombia. 28Mena yeŇč dokoloŇč kenaŇč ke te Kristo m…Ē yeŇčaw…Ē mo …Ē wose fa, na waa nyiŇč lese balaŇč burum lee ba dukum man. Yesu waa bese k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē bela. ∆Ü k…õ…õ k…ĒŇč kei d…Ē…Ē, na …Ē ne k…ĒŇč bo …Ē kaa lese balaŇč lee be nombiakum…õ…õ man ya. M…Ēna …Ē ne k…ĒŇč bo …Ē kaa l…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ kakyeŇč be kyaa be daa na e n…õ nyee.

english_text:
1The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year. 2If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices. 3-4But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
5 When Christ came into the world, he said to God,
‚ÄúSacrifices and offerings
are not what you want,
but you have given me
my body.
6No, you are not pleased
with animal sacrifices
and offerings for sin.‚ÄĚ
7Then Christ said,
‚ÄúAnd so, my God,
I have come to do
what you want,
as the Scriptures say.‚ÄĚ
8The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them? 9Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, ‚ÄúI have come to do what you want.‚ÄĚ 10So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
11 The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins. 12 But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side, 13and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power. 14By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
15The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
16 ‚ÄúWhen the time comes,
I will make an agreement
with them.
I will write my laws
on their minds and hearts.
17 Then I will forget
about their sins
and no longer remember
their evil deeds.‚ÄĚ
18When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices.
Encouragement and Warning
19My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place 20by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
21We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house. 22 So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water. 23We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us. 24We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things. 25Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
26No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth. 27 They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire. 28 If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death. 29 But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy. 30 We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people. 31It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
32Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light. 33Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others. 34You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
35Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards. 36Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised. 37 As the Scriptures say,
‚ÄúGod is coming soon!
It won't be very long.
38The people God accepts
will live because
of their faith.
But he isn't pleased
with anyone
who turns back.‚ÄĚ
39We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_text:
1Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan seŇč…õ…õ bo fa ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa Ňčan d…ĒŇč w…Ēle kaa k…ĒŇč n…õ, Ňča te y…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē kpaakpaa ŇčenaŇč gbagba ya. Lee kei d…Ē…Ē kedeesase Ňčan balaŇč ne deesi kulutoo kamas…õ Ňčgba mena keŇč mmaraase Ňčan ba wola w…Ē n…õ, be gyae ke tale yela walaŇč gb…õ…õneŇč ka tenee Wurubuar…õ siaman ya. 2Nyi ba kedeesase ŇčenaŇč deŇč talew…Ē yela be gb…õ…õneŇč deŇč teneew…Ē Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ, naf…Ē beŇč wulaa tinaa kedeesase ŇčenaŇč yela. Nyi Ňče deŇč talew…Ē l…õ…õ w…Ē n…õ, naf…Ē b…Ē…Ē tekaa w…Ēl…õ…õ nyi Wurubuar…õ waa kaa w…ĒŇč ba deŇčele lee be nombiakum…õ…õ ŇčenaŇč d…Ē…Ē ya. 3M…Ēna anokoare keŇč doo nombii kei man y…õna nyiaa, kulutoo kamas…õ na mena kedeesase Ňčan bee deesi agbaatee agbaatee n…õ, na bese bo yela b…õ…õ t…Ē…Ēse be nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ kaalaŇč n…õ. 4Nawolo nyi nunkyuese na asoose fatabo, be gyae ke tale faafo dukum lee deniwalaŇč wose man ya.
5-6Lee mena d…Ē…Ē debaŇč keŇč Kristo ne gyae waa k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ, …Ē yako …Ē kya Wurubuar…õ nyiaa,
‚ÄúNa kedeesa na ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan b…õ…õ mo sola neŇč n…õ na gyoo na n sia ya.
Wonembia katoo kedeesa,
na dukum kasola kedeesa koraŇč b…õ…õ gyoo n sia ya.
M…Ēna n te desina ma wosenaane na maa mo ke deesi fa neŇč.
7Mena d…Ē…Ē,
‚ÄėMe GbeŇčgyoo maŇč yaa.
Me k…ĒŇčae me kaa y…õ…õ n kegyaebii,
Ňčgba mena keŇč b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase ma wose man
nombia do n nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan man n…õ.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
8Kristo taŇč wulaa yako kaalaŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúWurubuar…õ b…õ…õ gyae kedeesa na ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan b…õ…õ mo sola e na wonembia katoo kedeesa na dukum kasola kedeesa ya, te Ňče b…õ…õ gyoo …Ē sia m…Ē ya.‚ÄĚ M…Ēna k…õŇča pou y…õ…õ kpene keŇč Mosesi mmaraase Ňčan na wolo nyi baa y…õ…õ. 9Bot…Ē…Ē te Kristo besew…Ē yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúMe GbeŇčgyoo maŇč yaa. 19Me tebia, lee Yesu yeŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē de te nyiŇč gb…õ…õ keŇč d…õ…õ tale gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč man yakaa. 20N…õn…õ…õ d…õ…õ tale gbaa kanyaŇč keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo kpase deni dinaa keŇč man n…õ.
