english_text:
The Two Witnesses
1 An angel gave me a measuring stick and said:
Measure around God's temple. Be sure to include the altar and everyone worshiping there. 2 But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for 42 months. 3My two witnesses will wear sackcloth, while I let them preach for 1,260 days.
4 These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth. 5Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths. 6 They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
7 After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them. 8 Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
9For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried. 10Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble. 11 But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
12 The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, ‚ÄúCome up here.‚ÄĚ And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud. 13 At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
14The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon.
The Seventh Trumpet
15 At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said,
‚ÄúNow the kingdom
of this world
belongs to our Lord
and to his Chosen One!
And he will rule
forever and ever!‚ÄĚ
16Then the 24 elders, who were seated on thrones in God's presence, knelt down and worshiped him. 17They said,
‚ÄúLord God All-Powerful,
you are and you were,
and we thank you.
You used your great power
and started ruling.
18 When the nations got angry,
you became angry too!
Now the time has come
for the dead
to be judged.
It is time for you to reward
your servants the prophets
and all your people
who honor your name,
no matter who they are.
It is time to destroy everyone
who has destroyed
the earth.‚ÄĚ
19 The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_text:
Wurubuar…õ adans…õ…õtena bala baŇč
1K…õŇča w…Ēle n…õ, te Ňčolo faa maŇč gbutu keŇč ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba daŇč keŇč b…õ…õ mo maa kolo n…õ. KeŇčte …Ē yako maŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúKoro gyu ke maa Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč na Wurubuar…õ kedeesa boe keŇč, na n ke kala balaŇč baŇč pou b…õ…õ som Wurubuar…õ bot…Ē…Ē n…õ. 2M…Ēna na maa t…õ…õle keŇč ke doo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč yenaase n…õ ya, nawolo nyi be ta mo bot…Ē…Ē fa balaŇč baŇč ba te y…õ…õ Gyudatenaw…Ē ya n…õ. BaŇč ne gyae la baa di doŇč Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē keŇč d…Ē…Ē gyaalaŇčna s…ĒŇčola na ala. 3Te maa kpila ma adans…õ…õtena bala, na baa do lia ab…Ē…Ē k…ĒŇč kaa kolosi me nombia Ňčan m…Ē…Ē lese wola w…Ē n…õ gyaalaŇčna s…ĒŇčola na ala.‚ÄĚ
4Adans…õ…õtena bala baŇč y…õna oliifi daase ala na kanease daase ala Ňčan seŇč…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei pou GbeŇčgyoo e n…õ siaman n…õ. 5Akpaa Ňčolo de k…ĒŇč nyi …Ē kaa y…õ…õ mena Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra bala k…õw…Ē nombiikum daale n…õ, boalaŇč na lee be n…Ē…Ēse man kaa ko be k…Ēlala benaŇč. Te walaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē ne gyae nyi waa y…õ…õ w…Ē kolo kum daale na, mena gb…õ…õ kenaŇč man te b…õ…õ mo ko …Ētenate. 6Mena Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra bala k…õw…Ē dana doŇč, keŇč baa tale t…Ē gb…õ…õ nyi debaŇč kamas…õ keŇč baa kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia na …Ēga na Ňč…õ…õ ya. Be dana doŇč keŇč baa tale yela loŇčse pou ka bese fatabo, te baa tale yela kayey…õ…õ kamas…õ m…Ē ke yala t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē debaŇč kamas…õ keŇč b…õ…õ gyae.
7Akpaa Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra bala baŇč de kolosi be nombia Ňčan taŇč n…õ, wonembukum Ňčon …Ē ne kyaa lee b…Ē…Ē kyilimkyilim keŇč ka be taŇč …Ēto ya man n…õ, waa kaa yokii na w…Ē na waa di be d…Ē…Ē na waa koe w…Ē. 8Te baa tina ba woya yela don…Ē…Ē dinaa keŇč ab…Ēntere man. Mena don…Ē…Ē kei man te be gy…Ē…Ē be GbeŇčgyoo sakaa …ĒpaŇčdaŇč d…Ē…Ē ko. BalaŇč ta do mena don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč yele nyi Sodom yaa Igyipite. 9BalaŇč baa lee don…Ē…Ēse kpookpoo, na deise kpookpoo na t…õ…õlese kpookpoo man k…ĒŇč kaa k…õ…õ woya benaŇč weeya atooro na yaale. M…Ēna balaŇč baa b…õ…õ nyi be na wuu w…Ē ya. 10Ba yeŇč gyae ke y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč gyoŇč. Baa di wee kenaŇč Ňčgba ba wee dinaa daale n…õ, na baa ky…õ…õ d…ĒŇča ab…Ē…Ē. Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra bala k…õw…Ē naase balaŇč diyem nideli t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē.
11M…Ēna weeya atooro na yaale kei w…Ēle n…õ, Wurubuar…õ yela …Ē wees…õ…õ kaa gyoo be man te be foŇčaw…Ē koro kyaa. Kei yela gyakoloŇč gyoo balaŇč baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē naa w…Ē n…õ nideli. 12Bot…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ kpilala bala baŇč nyii woya Ňčaale kolosiw…Ē na w…Ē keŇčkeŇč lee adido nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź gyeŇč k…ĒŇč adido k…õbo.‚ÄĚ KeŇčte ba moow…Ē Ňč…Ēma man gyeŇč gyu adido, na be k…Ēlala baŇč seŇč…õ…õ b…õ…õ k…õ…õ w…Ē. 13Mena debaŇč kenaŇč man n…õ, t…õ…õle wosiw…Ē nideli keŇč nyi don…Ē…Ē dinaa keŇč kekpase kufu man dekpeŇč dokoloŇč y…õl…õ…õw…Ē. T…õ…õle kewosi kenaŇč m…Ē koo balaŇč kakpoŇčse nyetooro (7,000). Te gyakoloŇč gyoo balaŇč akaŇč baŇč be ta yem ya n…õ, te ba lese Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa adido n…õ yele. 14Kayey…õ…õ nombia Ňčan alate kaalaŇča laa, te atoorote m…Ē ne k…ĒŇč.
Be kawola kaber…õ nyetoorote keŇč
15KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ kpilale nyetoorote Ňčon wola …Ē kaber…õ. ∆Ü kawola ke n…õ, te me nyii woya Ňčaale lee adido keŇčkeŇč nyiaa,
‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyoori
ta bese de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ
na …Ē bu nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon wui,
te de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ
ne gyae la waa di gyoori kekpaakekpaa.‚ÄĚ
16KeŇčte kegyiise sao banaara baŇč be kyaa be gyooro gbel…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ siaman n…õ, korow…Ē buŇčii som Wurubuar…õ. 17Te be yakow…Ē nyiaa,
‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ doŇč pou GbeŇčgyoo,
nyaŇč kyaa la kaalaŇč te nyaŇč s…õ…õ la n kyaa.
D…õ…õ do neŇč n…Ē…Ē nawolo nyi
n ta mo n doŇč keŇč ka dokoso be kyaa ya n…õ,
n ne di gyoori t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē.
18BalaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē gyeŇč neŇč ya n…õ ta gyeŇč baŇč n d…Ē…Ē,
m…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ te kaboena nyi nya lese n baŇč wola.
DebaŇč te lii keŇč nyaŇč ke di balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē yekee n…õ nombia,
na n ke t…Ē n tewul…õ…õ akpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēra
na n balaŇč baŇč pou b…õ…õ som neŇč n…õ kom.
N ke t…Ē balaŇč baŇč bee yee neŇč n…õ,
na balaŇč baŇč be dana yele,
na balaŇč yakaa nyibaŇč pou kom.
Te debaŇč te lii keŇč nyaŇč ke w…Ēl…õ…õ balaŇč baŇč pou
b…õ…õ w…Ēl…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei n…õ.‚ÄĚ
19Bot…Ē…Ē te be kyaŇča Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč ke kyaa adido n…õ toro, te me naa N…Ē…Ēkebakesee Adakaa keŇč. KeŇčte …Ēga Ňčmaalew…Ē te …Ē faew…Ē, te t…õ…õle wosiw…Ē te akpeleŇčkpeleŇčbia dinaana yalaw…Ē.

english_text:
The Woman and the Dragon
1Something important appeared in the sky. It was a woman whose clothes were the sun. The moon was under her feet, and a crown made of twelve stars was on her head. 2She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
3 Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads. 4 With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
5 The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne. 6The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for 1,260 days.
Michael Fights the Dragon
7 A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels. 8But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven 9 and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan. 10 Then I heard a voice from heaven shout,
‚ÄúOur God has shown
his saving power,
and his kingdom has come!
God's own Chosen One
has shown his authority.
Satan accused our people
in the presence of God
day and night.
Now he has been thrown out!
11‚ÄúOur people defeated Satan
because of the blood
of the Lamb
and the message of God.
They were willing
to give up their lives.
12‚ÄúThe heavens should rejoice,
together with everyone
who lives there.
But pity the earth
and the sea,
because the devil
was thrown down
to the earth.
He knows his time is short,
and he is very angry.‚ÄĚ
13When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son. 14 But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
15The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away. 16But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth. 17This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught. 18The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
translation_text:
Alo fulute Ňčolo na wonembukum dekpatakpere Ňčolo wose man nombia
1K…õŇča w…Ēle te gyakoloŇč nombia Ňčaale lese Ňča wose wola lee adido bela. Me naa alo Ňčolo na weese kilis…õ…õ na e Ňčgba keŇč te …Ē doona n…õ, te gyaalaŇč m…Ē doo …Ē naw…Ē…Ē t…õ…õ. Na …Ē wuuna gyooro kot…Ēt…Ē…Ē keŇč kyolobiise kufu ala mataa na ke. 2Na alo ŇčonaŇč dana fulu keŇč …Ē na doŇčee, te weeleŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē …Ē ne fae yeesa.
3KeŇčte ma besew…Ē naa gyakoloŇč nombia Ňčaale lee adido bela. Me naa wonembukum yayam dekpatakpere Ňčolo na …Ē y…õ…õ Ňčgba laŇčaŇč n…õ, na …Ē dana nyeeya nyetooro na woo kufu. Na gyooro kot…Ēt…Ē…Ēse nyetooro gyakaa …Ē nyeeya Ňčan d…Ē…Ē. 4Akpaa n kpala kyolobiise man akpeŇč atooro na, wonembu Ňčon moo …Ē gyuŇč gberaa Ňče man dekpeŇč dokoloŇč l…Ē t…õ…õle man. Na wonembu ŇčonaŇč seŇč…õ…õ alo Ňčon …Ē na doŇčee n…õ siaman, nyi akpaa …Ē lola …Ē bu Ňčon na waa mo e lila debokenaŇč man. 5Bot…Ē…Ē te alo Ňčon lola bu baale Ňčon waa di gyoori t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou d…Ē…Ē, keŇč …Ē be gyae waa wii be waraŇčase ya n…õ. ∆Ü ka lola bu Ňčon p…õ, te be kaa too e l…õo lee …Ē naa Ňčon nyiŇčmaa man mo e gyu kefa Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa …Ē gyooro gbeli d…Ē…Ē n…õ. 6KeŇčte alo Ňčon yeuw…Ē ka gyoo kefula man, bot…Ē…Ē keŇč Wurubuar…õ be desina see e nyi baa k…õ…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē gyaalaŇčna s…ĒŇčola na ala.
7KeŇčte yoo yalaw…Ē adido bot…Ē…Ē. Wurubuar…õ kpilale kegyia Maek…õle na …Ē tewul…õ…õ yoow…Ē na wonembukum dekpatakpere Ňčon na …Ē tewul…õ…õ. 8M…Ēna wonembukum Ňčon na …Ē tewul…õ…õ te y…õ…õ doŇč ya, mena d…Ē…Ē be te nyiŇč daseŇčae adido bot…Ē…Ē ya. 9KeŇčte be gegi wonembukum Ňčon lee adido bot…Ē…Ē. Ňäon y…õna beneŇč beneŇč dom Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi yele w…Ēl…õ…õre yaa …Ēb…Ēnsam, Ňčon …Ē na beo balaŇč pou t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ. Te be fuŇča Ňčon na …Ē tewul…õ…õ baŇč pou lee adido kaal…Ē t…õ…õle man.
10Bot…Ē…Ē te me nyii woya Ňčaale adido ne yako keŇčkeŇč nyiaa,
‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ
waa l…õ…õ …Ē balaŇč nyee n…õ te lii.
Wurubuar…õ ta lese …Ē doŇč na …Ē gyoori deb…Ē…Ē,
te Kristo nyeel…õ…õre Ňčon m…Ē ta lese wola nyi
…Ē dana doŇč t…õ…õle kei balaŇč pou d…Ē…Ē.
Nawolo nyi be te fuŇč …Ēb…Ēnsam Ňčon
…Ē na mo nombiakum…õ…õ gyakaa Kristotena d…Ē…Ē
Wurubuar…õ siaman nelim na ka weese n…õ ke l…Ē t…õ…õle man.
11Nambu Ňčon …Ē ba mo …Ē wose fa kedeesa n…õ fatabo,
na anokoare nombia Ňčan b…Ē…Ē kolosi n…õ,
Ňčan te ba moow…Ē di …Ēb…Ēnsam d…Ē…Ē.
Be te wii baŇč gbagba nyeedoŇč ya,
m…Ēna ba moo ba wose fa yeŇč lee ba kel…õ…õdi d…Ē…Ē.
12Mena d…Ē…Ē …õm…õ…õ baŇč pou …õ kyaa Wurubuar…õ man n…õ,
…õ yela ke y…õ…õ Ňčon gyoŇč.
M…Ē …õm…õ…õ baŇč pou …õ kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē
na …õpo man n…õ …õ laako,
nawolo nyi …Ēb…Ēnsam ta mo baŇč tisi
lee Wurubuar…õ man k…ĒŇč …õ gyaŇč.
∆Ü ta mo baŇč tisi k…ĒŇč,
nawolo nyi …Ē gyeŇč nyi …Ē weeya Ňčan
be kaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ ta boo ya!‚ÄĚ
13DebaŇč keŇč wonembukum Ňčon be naa nyi be te fuŇč e lee Wurubuar…õ man kaa l…Ē t…õ…õle man n…õ, te …Ē korow…Ē …Ē ne gegi alo Ňčon …Ē ba lola bu baale Ňčon n…õ. 14M…Ēna Wurubuar…õ faa alo Ňčon otoloku dinaa Ňčolo beŇčkel…õ…õ ala, na waa nyiŇč tale mo furufu yela wonembukum Ňčon gyu kefula man, bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…Ē…Ē desina see e nyi baa k…õ…õ …Ē d…Ē…Ē kulutooneŇč atooro na yale n…õ, na wonembukum Ňčon be nyiŇč gb…õ…õ ke y…õ…õ e kolo bot…Ē…Ē ya. 15Bot…Ē…Ē te wonembukum Ňčon puu loŇč lee …Ē n…Ē…Ēman Ňčgba boo kakpela n…õ, na ke gy…Ē alo Ňčon laŇč. 16M…Ēna t…õ…õle ky…Ē…Ē alo Ňčon. T…õ…õle b…õ…õw…Ē te ke ny…Ē…Ē loŇč keŇč pou wonembukum Ňčon be puu lee …Ē n…Ē…Ēman n…õ. 17Mena d…Ē…Ē wonembukum Ňčon gyeŇča baŇč alo Ňčon d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē laŇčaw…Ē yela e nyi …Ē kaa yoo na …Ē bia akaŇč baŇč. Alo ŇčonaŇč bia benaŇč y…õna balaŇč baŇč pou bee di Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan d…Ē…Ē, te bee di Kristo wose man adans…õ…õ n…õ. KeŇčte wonembukum Ňčon gyuuw…Ē ka seŇč …õpo n…Ē…Ēman.

english_text:
The Two Beasts
1 I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God. 2 The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast. 3One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast, 4and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, ‚ÄúNo one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.‚ÄĚ
5 The beast was allowed to brag and claim to be God, and for 42 months it was allowed to rule. 6The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God. 7 It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race. 8 The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
9If you have ears,
then listen!
10 If you are doomed
to be captured,
you will be captured.
If you are doomed
to be killed by a sword,
you will be killed
by a sword.
This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
11I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon. 12It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast. 13It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
14This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword. 15It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death. 16 All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves, 17they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
18You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is 666, and it stands for a person.
translation_text:
Wonembukum Ňčon …Ē ba lee …õpo man n…õ
1Bot…Ē…Ē te me naa wonembukum Ňčolo na …Ē ta lee …õpo man …Ē ne k…ĒŇč. Na …Ē dana woo kufu na nyeeya nyetooro, te gyooro kot…Ēt…Ē…Ēse wu…õ woo Ňčan pou d…Ē…Ē. Te be Ňčmarase mmusuo nombia Ňčaale gyakaa nyee kamas…õ d…Ē…Ē. 2Wonembukum Ňčon m…Ē…Ē naa n…õ y…õ…õ Ňčgba mok…Ē…Ēte n…õ. ∆Ü dana gelase Ňčgba …Ēsakuru n…õ, te …Ē n…Ē…Ē m…Ē y…õ…õ Ňčgba dinaate n…õ. KeŇčte wonembukum gyaŇčgbate Ňčon moo …Ē doŇč na …Ē gyoorobiiri na …Ē kegyiiri Ňčan pou …Ē dana n…õ fa wonembukum kei. 3Gyam dinaa daale kyaaw…Ē kyaa wonembukum Ňčon nyee keŇč daale d…Ē…Ē keŇč naf…Ē ke gyae ke tale ko e, m…Ēna gyam kenaŇč ta yem. Wonembukum ŇčonaŇč nombia dana gyakoloŇč, mena d…Ē…Ē t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou sila e. 4BalaŇč burum som wonembukum dekpatakpere Ňčon, nawolo nyi na …Ē ta mo …Ē doŇč fa wonembukum kei. Ba som wonembukum kei te be k…õl…õ…õ e nyiaa, ‚ÄúAm…Ēte kyaa la Ňčgba wonembukum kei n…õ, te am…Ēte m…Ē ne gyae la waa tale yoo na e?‚ÄĚ
5KeŇčte be feefuw…Ē do wonembukum Ňčon nyi waa y…õ…õ kegyira nombia, na waa kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuar…õ. Te be faa e doŇč nyi waa mo di t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyaalaŇčna s…ĒŇčola na ala. 6Te wonembukum Ňčon gyuusu …Ē n…Ē…Ē kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuar…õ w…Ēl…õ…õ …Ē yele, na …Ē deni dinaa keŇč na baŇč pou be kyaa Wurubuar…õ man n…õ. 7KeŇčte ba besew…Ē fa e doŇč nyi waa mo yoo na Wurubuar…õ balaŇč baŇč na waa di be d…Ē…Ē. Te be faa e doŇč bela nyi waa mo di akpaŇčalaŇčse, na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč na deise na loŇčtoro kamas…õ balaŇč d…Ē…Ē. 8T…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč burum baa som wonembukum kei. Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē y…õna balaŇč baŇč ba te Ňčmarase ba yela do nyeedoŇč t…Ēne keŇč man ya n…õ. NyeedoŇč t…Ēne kenaŇč y…õ…õ Nambu Ňčon …Ē ba mo …Ē wose fa kedeesa leenaŇč t…õ…õle kei kewalaŇč n…õ wui. 9WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē dana deŇčele anenyiiŇč dei n…õ, waa see deŇčele na waa tei. 10Akpaa y…õ…õ nyi baa kyaŇč Ňčolo yoo man na, yoo man te baa taŇč kyaŇč e.
Te akpaa y…õ…õ nyi Ňčolo waa yeŇč sila n…Ē…Ēman na,
sila n…Ē…Ēman te waa taŇč yeŇč.
‚ÄúKei nawolo baa nyi,
kaboena nyi Wurubuar…õ balaŇč baa nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē
na baa seŇč keŇčkeŇč ba kel…õ…õdi man.‚ÄĚ
Wonembukum Ňčon …Ē be b…õ…õ lee t…õ…õle man n…õ
11Bot…Ē…Ē te ma besew…Ē naa wonembukum Ňčolo te b…õ…õ lee t…õ…õle man bela. Na …Ē dana woo ala Ňčgba nam n…õ, m…Ēna …Ē kekolosi y…õ…õ Ňčgba wonembukum dekpatakpere Ňčon n…õ. 12DoŇč keŇč wonembukum gyaŇčgbate Ňčon dana n…õ, keŇč te balate kei m…Ē dana …Ē ne y…õ…õ …Ē tom …Ē d…Ē…Ē Ňčon yele man. Te …Ē yela t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou som wonembukum gyaŇčgbate Ňčon …Ē dana gyaŇčtare dinaa …Ē nyeeman n…õ. 13Wonembukum kei m…Ē y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia dinaana. ∆Ü yela boalaŇč gba koraŇč leew…Ē adido k…ĒŇč kaa yala t…õ…õle man te balaŇč pou naaw…Ē. 14Lee gyakoloŇč nombia Ňčan …Ē be y…õ…õ wonembukum gyaŇčgbate Ňčon yele man n…õ d…Ē…Ē, …Ē talew…Ē beo t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou. Te …Ē Ňčere w…Ē te be maa bane Ňčgba wonembukum gyaŇčgbate Ňčon, b…Ē…Ē mo yoosila gooli e …Ē nyeeman yeŇč yeŇč te …Ē ta yem ya n…õ. 15Be faa wonembukum balate kei doŇč nyi waa fa bane Ňčon b…Ē…Ē maa Ňčgba wonembukum gyaŇčgbate Ňčon n…õ nyeedoŇč na waa tale kolosi, na waa yela baa koe baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē b…õ…õ nyi b…Ē…Ē som bane ŇčonaŇč ya n…õ. 16Te mena wonembukum kei besew…Ē Ňčere walaŇč kamas…õ, balaŇč baŇč be dana yele na balaŇč yakaa nyibaŇč, na baŇč be dana k…Ēba na ayematena na gyoorobia na gbeŇča pou yela ba doo w…Ē nso be dunoluŇč nyiŇčmaase man na be tirin…Ē…Ēse d…Ē…Ē. 17WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon …Ē be dana wonembukum gyaŇčgbate Ňčon nso keŇč ya n…õ, …Ētenate be dana gb…õ…õ nyi waa y…Ē…Ē kolo yaa waa l…õ…õ kolo ya. Nso kei y…õna wonembukum Ňčon yele, yaa …Ē yele keŇč n…Ēmba.
18WalaŇč Ňčon Wurubuar…õ be fa e nyansa n…õ, Ňčon waageŇč ne gyae la waa tale nyii nombii kei man. WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē dana agyueŇč n…õ, Ňčon ne gyae la waa tale gyeŇč wonembukum Ňčon n…Ēmba kei yaan…Ē…Ēle, nawolo nyi n…Ēmba kei y…õ…õ deniwalaŇč Ňčolo yele ke. N…Ēmba kenaŇč y…õna s…ĒŇčonoŇčse looro na s…ĒŇčotooro na looro (666).

english_text:
The Lamb and His 144,000 Followers
1 I looked and saw the Lamb standing on Mount Zion! With him were 144,000, who had his name and his Father's name written on their foreheads. 2 Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps. 3And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the 144,000 who had been rescued from the earth. 4All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth. 5 They never tell lies, and they are innocent.
The Messages of the Three Angels
6I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth. 7The angel shouted, ‚ÄúWorship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.‚ÄĚ
8 A second angel followed and said, ‚ÄúThe great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.‚ÄĚ
9Finally, a third angel came and shouted:
Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead. 10 You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
11 If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
12God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
13Then I heard a voice from heaven say, ‚ÄúPut this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.‚ÄĚ
The Spirit answered, ‚ÄúYes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.‚ÄĚ
The Earth Is Harvested
14 I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand. 15 An angel came out of the temple and shouted, ‚ÄúStart cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.‚ÄĚ 16The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
17Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven. 18After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, ‚ÄúAll grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.‚ÄĚ 19The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger. 20 The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about 300 kilometers long and almost deep enough to cover a horse.
translation_text:
Nambu Ňčon na …Ē balaŇč
1KenaŇč w…Ēle ma besew…Ē g…õ…õse k…õ…õ na, te me naa Nambu Ňčon …Ē ba mo …Ē wose fa kedeesa n…õ seŇč…õ…õ Siy…Ēn bula keŇč d…Ē…Ē. Na balaŇč kakpoŇčse kufu bala akpeŇč kufu ala (144,000) seŇč…õ…õ Nambu ŇčonaŇč gyaŇč. Ba te Ňčmarase Nambu Ňčon yele na …Ē kya Wurubuar…õ yele gyakaa mena balaŇč benaŇč tirin…Ē…Ēse d…Ē…Ē. 2KeŇčte me nyii woya Ňčaale lee adido Ňčgba boo wuu la waale ne lam n…õ, te ka besew…Ē wii Ňčgba …Ēga fae la n…õ. Woya Ňčan m…Ē…Ē nyii n…õ, Ňče y…õ…õ Ňčgba baale ne gy…Ē…Ē na adol…õ…õse n…õ. 3Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē seŇčaw…Ē Wurubuar…õ gyooro gbeli keŇč, na wonise banaara baŇč na kegyiise baŇč siaman kpee n…Ē…ĒneŇč w…Ēle. Te Ňčolo te tale kasee n…Ē…ĒneŇč kenaŇč kpu na balaŇč kakpoŇčse kufu bala akpeŇč kufu ala (144,000) baŇč Wurubuar…õ be t…Ē ba nyeeman kom l…õ…õ w…Ē lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ ya. 4Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē y…õna balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē tela ba wose nideli, keŇč ba te doo na ala yaa baala see korakora ya n…õ. Bee sila Nambu Ňčon gyu yenaŇč kamas…õ keŇč …Ē ne gyu n…õ. BalaŇč k…õw…Ē y…õna balaŇč baŇč Wurubuar…õ be t…Ē ba nyee man kom l…õ…õ w…Ē lee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ. Be ta mo w…Ē fa Wurubuar…õ na …Ē Nambu Ňčon, Ňčgba mena keŇč walaŇč na mo …Ē w…Ē…Ēman ween…õ…õ gyaŇčgba nyiŇčan deesi fa Wurubuar…õ n…õ. 5Be te di dekara see korakora ya, te nombiikum kamas…õ m…Ē be mataa na w…Ē ya.
Wurubuar…õ kpilala batooro baŇč
6KenaŇč w…Ēle te ma besew…Ē naa Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo na …Ē ne furufu feliŇč man adido. Na …Ē dana Kristo nombia kpaakpaa Ňčan Ňče kyaa kekpaakekpaa n…õ waa kolosi fa deise, na akpaŇčalaŇčse na t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou. 7KeŇčte …Ē kolosiw…Ē keŇčkeŇč nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź yee Wurubuar…õ na …õ ka lese …Ē yele, nawolo nyi …Ē nombia kedi debaŇč keŇč te lii. ∆ź som Wurubuar…õ, nawolo nyi Ňčon y…õ…õna adido na ateta na …õpo na loŇčse pou.‚ÄĚ
8Bot…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ kpilale balate Ňčolo m…Ē k…ĒŇčaw…Ē kaa yako nyiaa, ‚ÄúBabil…Ēn don…Ē…Ē dinaa keŇč te y…õl…õ…õ. ∆Ü te yale, nawolo nyi …Ē ta mo …Ē kafoŇč nombia kyaŇčee t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou. ∆Ü kafoŇč kei y…õ…õ yaa Ňčgba soloŇč doŇčte te …Ē moow…Ē fa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč te be ny…Ē…Ēw…Ē d…õ…õse n…õ.‚ÄĚ
9KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ kpilale batoorote Ňčolo m…Ē kaa yakow…Ē keŇčkeŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúAkpaa Ňčolo da som wonembukum Ňčon, yaa bane Ňčon b…Ē…Ē maa Ňčgba Ňčon n…õ, yaa …Ē s…õ…õ te ba do e nso …Ē nyiŇčmaa man yaa …Ē tirin…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē n…õ, be gyae ke de fa e ya. 10Wurubuar…õ waa gyeŇč baŇč …Ētenate d…Ē…Ē na waa w…ĒŇč …Ē deŇčele nideli. Gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba Wurubuar…õ gbagba moona soloŇč doŇčte do leen…Ē…Ē keŇč nawolo nyi …Ē ta gyeŇč baŇč n…õ man fa e. Mena walaŇč ŇčonaŇč waa naa diyem nideli boalaŇč keŇč ne kpaa Ňčgba ba moo kpatakpela bo do ke man n…õ Wurubuar…õ kpilala na Nambu Ňčon siaman. 11BoalaŇč keŇč man mena walaŇč ŇčonaŇč waa yey…õ…õ n…õ, ke ny…Ē…Ēse gyae ka gyeŇč gyu adido kekpaakekpaa. Te balaŇč baŇč b…õ…õ som wonembukum Ňčon, na bane Ňčon b…Ē…Ē maa Ňčgba wonembukum ŇčonaŇč n…õ, na baŇč pou b…Ē…Ē do w…Ē wonembukum ŇčonaŇč nso n…õ, b…Ē…Ē gyae baa f…õ…õfo korakora ya. Baa naa diyem nideli nelim na ka weese.‚ÄĚ 12Mena d…Ē…Ē kaboena nyi Wurubuar…õ balaŇč baŇč bee di Wurubuar…õ mmaraase d…Ē…Ē, te be silana Yesu anokoare man n…õ, baa nyiŇč kon…Ē…Ē na baa seŇč keŇčkeŇč gb…õ…õ kamas…õ man. 13KeŇčte me nyii woya Ňčaale lee adido bela nyiaa, ‚ÄúŇämarase see nyiaa, ‚ÄėBalaŇč baŇč pou baa yeŇč de GbeŇčgyoo yele man lee gy…õŇč wee kei ne gyu n…õ, baŇč na nyeebam.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon m…Ē s…õ…õw…Ē nyiaa, ‚ÄúAyi, ke y…õ…õ ampaŇč. Baa f…õ…õfo lee be kaale pou man, nawolo nyi be nombia Ňčan b…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ n…õ ne gyae la ke sila w…Ē.‚ÄĚ
14Ma besew…Ē g…õ…õse k…õ…õ na, te me naa Ňč…Ēma wolom…õ…õ Ňčaale. Te me naa Ňčolo kyaa Ňč…Ēma ŇčenaŇč d…Ē…Ē Ňčgba ‚ÄúDeniwalaŇč‚ÄĚ n…õ. ∆Ü wuuna gyooro kot…Ēt…Ē…Ē …Ē nyee d…Ē…Ē, te …Ē dana sakawa keŇč k…õ…õ bee baŇčbaŇč n…õ …Ē nyiŇčmaa man. 15KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo m…Ē leew…Ē Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man adido k…ĒŇč, te …Ē kolosiw…Ē keŇčkeŇč yako walaŇč Ňčon …Ē kyaa Ňč…Ēma Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúMo n sakawa keŇč na n ke k…õr…õ…õ ween…õ…õ Ňčan. Nawolo nyi t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē ween…õ…õ Ňčan te y…õ…õ, te Ňča kek…õr…õ…õ m…Ē debaŇč te lii.‚ÄĚ 16Mena d…Ē…Ē walaŇč Ňčon …Ē kyaa Ňč…Ēma Ňčan d…Ē…Ē n…õ, fuŇča …Ē sakawa keŇč k…õr…õ…õ ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan pou Ňč…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ t…õ…õle d…Ē…Ē n…õ.
17KenaŇč w…Ēle te Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo m…Ē besew…Ē lee Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man adido bela. Na Ňčon m…Ē dana sakawa baŇčbaŇč daale. 18KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčolo m…Ē leew…Ē bot…Ē…Ē keŇč bee deesi fa Wurubuar…õ n…õ man k…ĒŇč. Wurubuar…õ kpilale kei ne k…õ…õ na kedeesa boalaŇč keŇč d…Ē…Ē. Te …Ē fae yeesa keŇčkeŇč yako walaŇč Ňčon …Ē dana sakawa baŇčbaŇč keŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúMo n sakawa keŇč na n ke k…Ē gerepo saase Ňčan pou Ňč…Ē…Ē y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ lee Ňče woŇčse Ňčan man.‚ÄĚ 19Mena d…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon m…Ē fuŇča …Ē sakawa keŇč k…Ē gerepo saase Ňčan pou lee Ňče woŇčse man, te …Ē fuŇča Ňča do b…Ē…Ē dinaa keŇč man b…õ…õ Ňčmaase bia Ňčan lese Ňča loŇč mo y…õ…õ soloŇč n…õ. Mena b…Ē…Ē kei seŇč…õ…õ fa Wurubuar…õ baŇč kum keŇč. 20KeŇčte be sakatee gerepo bia Ňčan bot…Ē…Ē Ňčmaase Ňča lese Ňča loŇč don…Ē…Ē keŇč n…Ē…Ēman. Te loŇč keŇč ba lee Ňče man n…õ bese fatabo te Ňče laŇčaw…Ē Ňčgba boo n…õ. Fatabo kenaŇč kelaŇč d…Ē…ĒloŇč man gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba maelese s…ĒŇčonoŇčse ala n…õ (200), te ke siiliŇč m…Ē gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba naw…Ē…Ēt…Ē…Ēse anoŇč n…õ.

english_text:
The Last of the Terrible Troubles
1After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
2Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps, 3 and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing,
‚ÄúLord God All-Powerful,
you have done great
and marvelous things.
You are the ruler
of all nations,
and you do what is
right and fair.
4 Lord, who doesn't honor
and praise your name?
You alone are holy,
and all nations will come
and worship you,
because you have shown
that you judge
with fairness.‚ÄĚ
5 After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open. 6And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold. 7One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever. 8 The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_text:
Wurubuar…õ kpilala baŇč be dana kayey…õ…õ nombia nyetooro n…õ
1KenaŇč w…Ēle te ma besew…Ē naa gyakoloŇč nombii dinaa daale Wurubuar…õ man adido bela. Me naa Wurubuar…õ kpilala nyetooro baale na be dana kayey…õ…õ nombia nyetooro. Kayey…õ…õ nombia k…õŇča y…õna Wurubuar…õ balaŇč deŇčele kew…ĒŇč …Ētomante keŇč. Nawolo nyi Ňča de kaa lam na, Wurubuar…õ m…Ē w…Ēe gyae ke f…õ…õ.
2KeŇčte me naa kolo gyooro gbeli keŇč siaman Ňčgba …õpo loŇč n…õ. Na ke y…õ…õ geleŇčgeleŇč te k…õ…õ Ňčalakee Ňčgba diiku n…õ, na boalaŇč m…Ē kyakal…õ…õ ke man. Te me naa balaŇč baale seŇč…õ…õ …õpo kenaŇč n…Ē…Ēman. BalaŇč benaŇč y…õna balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē yoo di wonembukum Ňčon na bane Ňčon b…Ē…Ē maa wonembukum ŇčonaŇč din…Ē…Ē man, te …Ē n…Ēmba seŇč…õ…õ fa …Ē yele d…Ē…Ē n…õ. Na be dana adol…õ…õse Ňčan Wurubuar…õ be fa w…Ē n…õ. 3Na bee kpee Wurubuar…õ tobaale Mosesi n…Ē…ĒneŇč, na Nambu Ňčon n…Ē…ĒneŇč nyiaa,
‚ÄúDe GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ doŇč pou nyee,
n nombia Ňčan n ne y…õ…õ n…õ y…õ…õ nombia dinaana Ňča,
te Ňče dana gyakoloŇč.
NyaŇč y…õna t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē pou gyoo.
N nombia doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ,
te Ňče y…õ…õ anokoare m…Ē.
4De GbeŇčgyoo,
am…Ēte kyaa la keŇč …Ē be gyae waa yee neŇč ya,
te am…Ēte m…Ē be gyae la waa lese n yele ya?
Nawolo nyi nyaŇč nyaageŇč kooŇč
y…õna walaŇč Ňčon ayim…õ…õ be doo n man ya.
T…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou baa k…ĒŇč
kaa buŇč neŇč na baa som neŇč.
Nawolo nyi n ta lese n nombia
Ňčan Ňče doo na Ňče gb…õ…õ n…õ deb…Ē…Ē.‚ÄĚ
5Kei w…Ēle te me k…õ…õ adido na, te me naaw…Ē na Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa t…Ē…Ēmante keŇč ne di adans…õ…õ nyi Wurubuar…õ kyaa bot…Ē…Ē n…õ ta toro. 6Bot…Ē…Ē te me naa Wurubuar…õ kpilala nyetooro baŇč be dana kayey…õ…õ nombia nyetooro Ňčan n…õ, ta lee Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man. Ba doona akaral…õ…õ kegbase wurikyim Ňčan Ňč…õ…õ Ňčalakee n…õ, te ba moo k…Ēba yayam ab…Ēs…Ē…Ēse kpaŇč karase be kon…Ē…Ēse man. 7KeŇčte wonise banaara baŇč man Ňčolo moo k…Ēba yayam nyefal…õ…õ nyetooro fa Wurubuar…õ kpilala nyetooro baŇč. Ab…Ē…Ē Ňčan doo nyefal…õ…õ Ňčan man n…õ, y…õ…õ kayey…õ…õ kum…õ…õ Ňčan Wurubuar…õ Ňčon …Ē kyaa kekpaakekpaa n…õ waa mo baŇč mo Ňča do t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč n…õ. 8Bot…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ gyoorobiiri na …Ē doŇč yela ny…Ē…Ēse kaa wulu Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man. Mena d…Ē…Ē Ňčolo te tale gyoo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man ya, kelii debaŇč keŇč kayey…õ…õ nombia nyetooro Ňčan Wurubuar…õ ba mo fa …Ē kpilala nyetooro baŇč n…õ taŇčaw…Ē.

english_text:
The Bowls of God's Anger
1From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, ‚ÄúGo and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.‚ÄĚ
2 The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
3The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
4 The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood. 5Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say,
‚ÄúYou have always been,
and you always will be
the holy God.
You had the right
to judge in this way.
6They poured out the blood
of your people
and your prophets.
So you gave them blood
to drink as they deserve!‚ÄĚ
7After this, I heard
the altar shout,
‚ÄúYes, Lord God All-Powerful,
your judgments are honest
and fair.‚ÄĚ
8The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire. 9Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
10 The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain. 11And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
12 The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east. 13An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet. 14These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
15 Remember Christ says, ‚ÄúWhen I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.‚ÄĚ
16 Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
17As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, ‚ÄúIt's done!‚ÄĚ 18 There were flashes of lightning, roars of thunder, and the worst earthquake in all history. 19 The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger. 20 Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared. 21 Hailstones, weighing about 50 kilograms each, fell from the sky on people. Finally, the people cursed God, because the hail was so terrible.
translation_text:

‚ÄúNyaŇč y…õna Wurubuar…õ Ňčon n wulaa n kyaa lee kewalaŇč keŇč,

Ňčgba mena keŇč kaboena w…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ

‚ÄúAmpaŇč, de GbeŇčgyoo Wurubuar…õ doŇč pou nyee,
n nombia kedi y…õ…õ anokoare te ke doo na ke gb…õ…õ.‚ÄĚ
8KenaŇč w…Ēle te Wurubuar…õ kpilale banaarate Ňčon m…Ē ky…õ…õkee kpene keŇč doo …Ē nyefale keŇč man n…õ woli weese d…Ē…Ē. 10KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ kpilale banoŇčte Ňčon m…Ē ky…õ…õkee kpene keŇč doo …Ē nyefale keŇč man n…õ woli wonembukum Ňčon gyooro gbeli keŇč d…Ē…Ē. 12KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ kpilale loorote Ňčon m…Ē ky…õ…õkee kpene keŇč doo …Ē nyefale keŇč man n…õ, do boo dinaa keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi Efurate n…õ man. Te loŇč keŇč pou yoow…Ē, na gb…õ…õ ke doo fa gyooneŇč baŇč baa lee weese d…õ…õlee man k…ĒŇč n…õ. 13Bot…Ē…Ē te me naa feliŇčkum…õ…õ atooro Ňčaale na Ňče y…õ…õ Ňčgba penlase n…õ. Ňäa lee wonembukum dekpatakpere Ňčon na wonembukum gyaŇčgbate Ňčon, na wonembukum balate dekarate Ňčon n…Ē…Ēse man. 14Mena feliŇčkum…õ…õ k…õw…Ē y…õ…õ …Ēb…Ēnsam tewul…õ…õw…Ē, te be dana doŇč keŇč b…õ…õ tale y…õ…õ gyakoloŇč nombia dinaana. 17KenaŇč w…Ēle te Wurubuar…õ kpilale nyetoorote Ňčon m…Ē ky…õ…õkee kpene keŇč doo …Ē nyefale keŇč man n…õ woli feliŇč man. Bot…Ē…Ē te me nyii yeesa keŇčkeŇč Ňčaale lee gyooro gbeli keŇč ke doo Wurubuar…õ …Ēsom deni dinaa keŇč man n…õ nyiaa, ‚ÄúN…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē te taŇč!‚ÄĚ 18Kei w…Ēle te …Ēga Ňčmaalew…Ē agbaatee agbaatee, te …Ē faew…Ē agbaatee agbaatee, te t…õ…õle wosiw…Ē nideli. LeenaŇč debaŇč keŇč balaŇč be kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē pou n…õ, t…õ…õle te wosi ta mena see ya. 19KeŇčte don…Ē…Ē dinaa keŇč kpasew…Ē akpeŇč atooro, te don…Ē…Ēse dinaana Ňčan pou Ňče kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ y…õl…õ…õw…Ē. Te Wurubuar…õ t…Ē…Ēse nombiakum…õ…õ Ňčan Babil…Ēntena be y…õ…õ n…õ, mena d…Ē…Ē …Ē gyeŇča baŇč kum do w…Ē nideli Ňčgba mena keŇč kaboena n…õ. 20Te don…Ē…Ēse Ňčan pou loŇč be kilisi Ňča gyaŇčee n…õ, na bulase pou Ňčaalaaw…Ē. 21Te akpeleŇčkpeleŇčbia dinaana Ňčan Ňče dana yuŇč nideli n…õ leew…Ē adido yala balaŇč d…Ē…Ē. english_text:
The Prostitute and the Beast
1 One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, ‚ÄúCome on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans. 2 Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.‚ÄĚ
3 With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns. 4 The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done. 5On her forehead a mysterious name was written:
I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
6I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me, 7and the angel said:
Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns. 8 The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
9Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings. 10Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
11You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
12 The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time. 13They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast. 14 These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
15The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages. 16The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire. 17God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
18The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_text:

Alo ŇčonaŇč y…õna don…Ē…Ē dinaa keŇč b…Ē…Ē baŇč ke booneŇč burum n…Ē…Ēman n…õ.‚ÄĚ 2T…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyooneŇč ta gyem tisi …Ē d…Ē…Ē ke y…õ…õ kafoŇč nombia na e. ∆Ü ta mo …Ē kafoŇč nombia w…Ēl…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou nyee, Ňčgba mena keŇč soloŇč ne w…Ēl…õ…õ soloŇč ny…Ē…Ēra nyeeya n…õ.
3KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon kaa gyoow…Ē me man nideli, te Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon moo maŇč gyu depampaa dinaa daale man. Bot…Ē…Ē te me naa alo Ňčolo na …Ē kyaa wonembukum yayam Ňčolo d…Ē…Ē. Ba te Ňčmarase mmusuo yela Ňčan Ňč…õ…õ tia Wurubuar…õ n…õ gyakaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē pou. Wonembukum ŇčonaŇč dana nyeeya nyetooro na woo kufu. 4Na alo ŇčonaŇč doona kanyaŇč yayam na agoo ab…Ē…Ē. Te …Ē moo k…Ēba yayam ab…Ē…Ē na kegyaŇčase kpookpoo kpaakpaa nyiŇčan Ňčan Ňč…õ…õ Ňčalakee te Ňče dana koyadoŇč n…õ mia desina …Ē wose. Te …Ē dana k…Ēba yayam kuruwaa keŇč …Ē nombia nkaraŇčkaraŇč na …Ē futooli nombia Ňčan be wulu ke man n…õ …Ē nyiŇčmaa man. 5Ba te Ňčmarase yele daale keŇč walaŇč na walaŇč ba gyeŇč ka as…õ…õ ya n…õ gyakaa …Ē tirin…Ē…Ē d…Ē…Ē nyiaa,
‚Äúbabil…Ēn don…Ē…Ē dinaa,
futooli ala pou be naa,
t…õ…õle kei nombia nkaraŇčkaraŇč pou nyee.‚ÄĚ
6KeŇčte me naaw…Ē na, alo Ňčon te ny…Ē…Ē Wurubuar…õ balaŇč fatabo baa d…õ…õse Ňčgba soloŇč te …Ē ny…Ē…Ēw…Ē n…õ. BalaŇč k…õw…Ē y…õna baŇč b…Ē…Ē koe w…Ē, keŇč b…Ē…Ē mo be kon…Ē…Ē pou sila Yesu n…õ d…Ē…Ē. Ma kena alo ŇčonaŇč n…õ, ma wose gbirisi maŇč nideli. 7Bot…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon b…Ē…Ēse maŇč nyiaa, ‚ÄúWoŇč d…Ē…Ē te n wose gbirisi neŇč? Yela maa lese alo Ňčon na wonembukum Ňčon …Ē dana nyeeya nyetooro na woo kufu, te alo Ňčon kyaa …Ē d…Ē…Ē n…õ nombia aweeseŇč Ňčan man wola neŇč. 8Wonembukum Ňčon n be naa n…õ naf…Ē na …Ē kyaa, m…Ēna n…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …Ē be kyaa bela ya. Waa lee b…Ē…Ē kyilimkyilim keŇč ke taŇč …Ēto ya n…õ man k…ĒŇč, na …Ē kaa yeŇč leki. Nyi akpaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč baŇč pou ba te Ňčmarase ba yela do Wurubuar…õ nyeedoŇč t…Ēne keŇč ke kyaa leenaŇč kewalaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ be y…õ…õ t…õ…õle kei ya n…õ de naa wonembukum Ňčon na, gyakoloŇč gyae ke kyaŇč w…Ē nideli. Nawolo nyi naf…Ē na wonembukum kei kyaa, te n…õn…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē …Ē be kyaa ya. M…Ēna waa bese kaa lese …Ē wose wola bela.
9WalaŇč Ňčon …Ē dana nyansa te …Ē gyeŇč nombia n…õ, Ňčon ne gyae la waa tale nyii nombia k…õŇča man nideli. Wonembukum Ňčon nyeeya nyetooro Ňčan seŇč…õ…õ fa bulasebia nyetooro, te bulasebia ŇčenaŇč d…Ē…Ē te alo Ňčon ne kyaa. Bulasebia ŇčenaŇč m…Ē seŇč…õ…õ fa gyooneŇč nyetooro. 10GyooneŇč k…õw…Ē man banoŇč ta yekee. Loorote Ňčon gyakaa …Ē ne di gyoori, te nyetoorote Ňčon te k…ĒŇča ta ya. Akpaa nyetoorote Ňčon de k…ĒŇč n…õ, waa di gyoori keŇč bo debaŇč kyomii na baa gegi e. 11Wonembukum Ňčon …Ē be kyaa kaalaŇč te n…õn…õ…õ …Ē be kyaa bela ya n…õ, Ňčon y…õna gyoo gyanaarate Ňčon te …Ē kpu…õ gyooneŇč nyetooro baŇč man. Te Ňčon d…Ē…Ē …Ē ne gyu baa …Ē kaa yeŇč leki.
12Woo kufu Ňčan n be naa n…õ, Ňčan m…Ē seŇč…õ…õ fa gyooneŇč kufu, m…Ēna be te dii ta gyoori ya. M…Ē baa fa gyooneŇč kufu benaŇč na wonembukum Ňčon doŇč, na baa di gyoori debaŇč dokoloŇč bo. 13Nombii dokoloŇč d…Ē…Ē te gyooneŇč kufu benaŇč pou seŇč…õ…õ. Baa y…õ…õ n…Ē…ĒdokoloŇč na baa mo be kegyiiri na be doŇč pou fa wonembukum Ňčon, na waa mo di gyoori. 14Baa yoo na Nambu Ňčon, m…Ēna Nambu Ňčon na …Ē balaŇč baŇč …Ē be baake w…Ē mo w…Ē y…õ…õ …Ē wui, te be silana e anokoare man n…õ baa di …Ē d…Ē…Ē. Nawolo nyi Ňčon y…õna gbeŇčgyoona pou GbeŇčgyoo na gyooneŇč pou Gyoo.‚ÄĚ
15Bot…Ē…Ē te Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon yako maŇč bela nyiaa, ‚ÄúBooneŇč Ňčan n be naa keŇč futooli alo Ňčon kyaa Ňče gyaŇč n…õ, seŇč…õ…õ fa balaŇč kpookpoo baŇč be kyaa t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē n…õ. 16Te wonembukum Ňčon na woo kufu Ňčan n be naa n…õ, baa k…Ēla futooli alo Ňčon nombia. Baa w…Ēl…õ…õ e n…õ kyekpakyekpakyekpa, na baa tina e yela diteŇčteli. Baa tao e, na baa too akaŇč keŇč. 17Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ yele la te gyooneŇč kufu baŇč s…õ…õw…Ē, te be y…õ…õ Ňčon Wurubuar…õ kegyaebii. Mena d…Ē…Ē te ba moo be doŇč pou fa wonembukum Ňčon nyi waa di gyoori kelii debaŇč keŇč Wurubuar…õ nombia aŇčmaraseŇč see Ňčan gyae ke k…ĒŇč mena n…õ. 18Alo Ňčon n be naa n…õ, Ňčon y…õna don…Ē…Ē dinaa keŇč ke gyoo ne di doŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyooneŇč pou d…Ē…Ē n…õ.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
The Fall of Babylon
1I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory. 2 The angel shouted,
‚ÄúFallen! Powerful Babylon
has fallen
and is now the home
of demons.
It is the den
of every filthy spirit
and of all unclean birds,
and every dirty
and hated animal.
3 Babylon's evil and immoral wine
has made all nations drunk.
Every king on earth
has slept with her,
and every merchant on earth
is rich because of
her evil desires.‚ÄĚ
4 Then I heard another voice
from heaven shout,
‚ÄúMy people, you must escape
from Babylon.
Don't take part in her sins
and share her punishment.
5 Her sins are piled
as high as heaven.
God has remembered the evil
she has done.
6 Treat her as she
has treated others.
Make her pay double
for what she has done.
Make her drink twice as much
of what she mixed
for others.
7 That woman honored herself
with a life of luxury.
Reward her now
with suffering and pain.
‚ÄúDeep in her heart
Babylon said,
‚ÄėI am the queen!
Never will I be a widow
or know what it means
to be sad.‚Äô
8And so, in a single day
she will suffer the pain
of sorrow, hunger, and death.
Fire will destroy
her dead body,
because her judge
is the powerful Lord God.‚ÄĚ
9 Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire. 10Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say,
‚ÄúPity that great
and powerful city!
Pity Babylon!
In a single hour
her judgment has come.‚ÄĚ
11 Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods. 12 There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble. 13No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
14Babylon, the things
your heart desired
have all escaped
from you.
Every luxury
and all your glory
will be lost forever.
You will never
get them back.
15 The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning. 16Then they shouted,
‚ÄúPity the great city
of Babylon!
She dressed in fine linen
and wore purple
and scarlet cloth.
She had jewelry
made of gold
and precious stones
and pearls.
17 Yet in a single hour
her riches disappeared.‚ÄĚ
Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea. 18 When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, ‚ÄúThis was the greatest city ever!‚ÄĚ
19 They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said,
‚ÄúPity the great city
of Babylon!
Everyone who sailed the seas
became rich
from her treasures.
But in a single hour
the city was destroyed.
20 The heavens should be happy
with God's people
and apostles and prophets.
God has punished her
for them.‚ÄĚ
21 A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said,
‚ÄúThis is how the great city
of Babylon
will be thrown down,
never to rise again.
22 The music of harps and singers
and of flutes and trumpets
will no longer be heard.
No workers will ever
set up shop in that city,
and the sound
of grinding grain
will be silenced forever.
23Lamps will no longer shine
anywhere in Babylon,
and couples will never again
say wedding vows there.
Her merchants ruled
the earth,
and by her witchcraft
she fooled all nations.
24 On the streets of Babylon
is found the blood
of God's people
and of his prophets,
and everyone else.‚ÄĚ

english_text:
1After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting,
‚ÄúPraise the Lord!
To our God belongs
the glorious power to save,
2 because his judgments
are honest and fair.
That filthy prostitute
ruined the earth
with shameful deeds.
But God has judged her
and made her pay
the price for murdering
his servants.‚ÄĚ
3 Then the crowd shouted,
‚ÄúPraise the Lord!
Smoke will never stop rising
from her burning body.‚ÄĚ
4After this, the 24 elders and the four living creatures all knelt before the throne of God and worshiped him. They said, ‚ÄúAmen! Praise the Lord!‚ÄĚ
The Marriage Supper of the Lamb
5 From the throne a voice said,
‚ÄúIf you worship
and fear our God,
give praise to him,
no matter who you are.‚ÄĚ
6 Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying,
‚ÄúPraise the Lord!
Our Lord God All-Powerful
now rules as king.
7So we will be glad and happy
and give him praise.
The wedding day of the Lamb
is here,
and his bride is ready.
8She will be given
a wedding dress
made of pure
and shining linen.
This linen stands for
the good things
God's people have done.‚ÄĚ
9 Then the angel told me, ‚ÄúPut this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.‚ÄĚ The angel also said, ‚ÄúThese things God has said are true.‚ÄĚ
10I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, ‚ÄúDon't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.‚ÄĚ
The Rider on the White Horse
11 I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war. 12 He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
13 The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as ‚ÄúThe Word of God.‚ÄĚ 14He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen. 15 From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made. 16On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, ‚ÄúKING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.‚ÄĚ
17 I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, ‚ÄúCome and join in God's great feast! 18You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.‚ÄĚ
19I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army. 20 But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur. 21But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.

KeŇčte be fuŇča wonembukum ŇčonaŇč, na …Ēb…Ēnsam dekara dekpeŇčkpeŇčgy…Ē…Ēre Ňčon na be sia fon…õ…õ do boalaŇč degyapaa keŇč k…õ…õ kpaa Ňčgba kpatakpela n…õ man. 21Yoosila keŇč doo walaŇč Ňčon …Ē kyaa d…õ…õky…ĒŇč wolom Ňčon d…Ē…Ē n…Ē…Ēman n…õ, keŇčte …Ē moow…Ē koe balaŇč akaŇč baŇč pou, te gyebuse kaa wuuw…Ē be naane d…Ē…Ē te be dii Ňča ba kegyaebii.

english_text:
The Thousand Years
1I saw an angel come down from heaven, carrying the key to the deep pit and a big chain. 2 He chained the dragon for 1,000 years. It is that old snake, who is also known as the devil and Satan. 3Then the angel threw the dragon into the pit. He locked and sealed it, so 1,000 years would go by before the dragon could fool the nations again. But after that, it would have to be set free for a little while.
4 I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for 1,000 years.
5-6These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for 1,000 years.
No other dead people were raised to life until 1,000 years later.
Satan Is Defeated
7At the end of the 1,000 years, Satan will be set free. 8 He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach, 9and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army. 10Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever.
The Judgment at the Great White Throne
11 I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go. 12 I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
13The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done. 14Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death. 15Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_text:

Ňäon y…õna beneŇč beneŇč dom Ňčon b…õ…õ baake e nyi yele w…Ēl…õ…õre yaa …Ēb…Ēnsam n…õ. KeŇčte …Ē bake e see nyi waa doo bot…Ē…Ē kulutooneŇč kakpoŇč (1,000). 3Te Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon fuŇča e do b…Ē…Ē keŇč ke taŇč …Ēto ya n…õ man, te …Ē t…Ē…Ē ke safowaa. KeŇčte …Ē moo kolo t…Ē ke n…Ē…Ē, na …Ē ba tekaa nyiŇč gb…õ…õ ta beo balaŇč t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē bela ya, kelii kulutooneŇč kakpoŇč (1,000) Ňčan …Ēto. KenaŇč w…Ēle na, baa bese lese e debaŇč kyomii daale bela.
4KenaŇč w…Ēle te me naa gyooro gbel…õ…õ Ňčaale bela. Na balaŇč baŇč be kyak…õ…õ Ňče d…Ē…Ē n…õ, y…õna balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē fa w…Ē doŇč nyi baa di balaŇč nombia n…õ. KeŇčte ma besew…Ē naa balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē kolosi Wurubuar…õ nombia Ňčan, te b…Ē…Ē di Yesu Kristo wose man adans…õ…õ d…Ē…Ē b…Ē…Ē k…õr…õ…õ ba nyeeya n…õ ba woya. Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē y…õna balaŇč baŇč be te buŇč som wonembukum Ňčon ya, yaa bane Ňčon b…Ē…Ē maa wonembukum Ňčon din…Ē…Ē man ya, te ba te s…õ…õ m…Ē nyi baa do w…Ē wonembukum Ňčon nso be tirin…Ē…Ēse d…Ē…Ē yaa be nyiŇčmaase d…Ē…Ē ya n…õ. Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē foŇčae lee yeŇč man, na baŇč na Kristo kedi gyoori kulutooneŇč kakpoŇč (1000). 5K…õw…Ē y…õna balaŇč gyaŇčgbatena baŇč b…Ē…Ē taŇčgb…õ…õ foŇč lee yeŇč man. Woya akaŇč baŇč d…Ē…Ē, b…Ē…Ē gyae baa foŇč ya kelii kulutooneŇč kakpoŇč (1000) Ňčan …Ēto p…õ. 6BalaŇč baŇč be kpu…õ balaŇč gyaŇčgbatena baŇč b…Ē…Ē taŇčgb…õ…õ foŇč lee yeŇč man n…õ, baŇč na nyeebam te Wurubuar…õ siaman na, ayim…õ…õ kamas…õ be doo be man ya. Mena balaŇč k…õw…Ē d…Ē…Ē, yeŇč bolate keŇč be dana doŇč be d…Ē…Ē bela ya. Baa bese Wurubuar…õ na Kristo saese, na baŇč na Kristo ke di gyoori kulutooneŇč kakpoŇč (1000).
Be te fuŇč …Ēb…Ēnsam do boalaŇč man
7KulutooneŇč kakpoŇč (1000) Ňčan w…Ēle n…õ, baa lese …Ēb…Ēnsam lee bot…Ē…Ē keŇč b…Ē…Ē t…Ē e do n…õ. 8Waa ta beo t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē yenaŇč kamas…õ balaŇč pou, na waa yilaa w…Ē fa yoo. Be yaan…Ē…Ēle y…õ…õ Ňčgba …õpo n…Ē…Ēman aŇčasa n…õ. T…õ…õlese k…õŇča te b…õ…õ baake nyi G…Ēge na Mag…Ēge. 9Ňäon na …Ē balaŇč yaaseew…Ē gyoo t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē pou, te be kilisi Wurubuar…õ balaŇč na …Ē don…Ē…Ē keŇč …Ē ne gyae ke n…õ. M…Ēna boalaŇč leew…Ē adido k…ĒŇč kaa kpa w…Ē pou. 10KeŇčte be fuŇča …Ēb…Ēnsam Ňčon …Ē ba beo w…Ē n…õ do boalaŇč degyapaa keŇč k…õ…õ kpaa Ňčgba kpatakpela n…õ man. Nombia kedi …Ētomante

Bot…Ē…Ē te be gyuusu t…Ēna Ňčaale man. KeŇčte ba besew…Ē gyuusu t…Ēne keŇč b…õ…õ baake ke nyi nyeedoŇč t…Ēne n…õ. Te be dii be man walaŇč kamas…õ nombia, Ňčgba mena keŇč b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase be nombia ay…õ…õsa do t…Ēna Ňčan man n…õ. 14KeŇčte be fuŇča yeŇč na woyad…õ…õ do boalaŇč degyapaa dinaa keŇč man. BoalaŇč degyapaa kei y…õna yeŇč bolate keŇč. 15WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon ba te naa …Ē yele nyeedoŇč t…Ēne keŇč man ya n…õ, bee fuŇč …Ētenate bo do boalaŇč degyapaa keŇč man.

english_text:
The New Heaven and the New Earth
1 I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea. 2 Then I saw New Jerusalem, the holy city, coming down from God in heaven. It was like a bride dressed in her wedding gown and ready to meet her husband.
3 I heard a loud voice shout from the throne:
God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people. 4 He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
5Then the one sitting on the throne said:
I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted. 6 Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty. 7 All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
8But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death.
The New Jerusalem
9I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, ‚ÄúCome on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.‚ÄĚ 10 Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
11The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone. 12 The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel. 13Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west. 14The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
15 The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls. 16The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about 2,400 kilometers high and 2,400 kilometers wide. 17Then the angel measured the wall, and by our measurements it was about 60 meters high.
18 The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal. 19Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald, 20the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst. 21Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
22I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple. 23 And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
24 Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there. 25 Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes. 26The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city. 27 But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_text:

Adido gyaŇčgbate na t…õ…õle gyaŇčgbate keŇč, na …õpo pou te Ňčaalaa. 2Bot…Ē…Ē te me naa Gyerusal…õm don…Ē…Ē w…Ēle keŇč ke y…õ…õ Wurubuar…õ don…Ē…Ē ke n…õ, te tisi lee Wurubuar…õ gyaŇč adido ne k…ĒŇč t…õ…õle man. Waa kyaa be man, na baŇč m…Ē ka bese …Ē balaŇč. Wurubuar…õ gbagba waa kyaa be man, na waa bese be Wurubuar…õ. 4Ňäon Wurubuar…õ ne gyae la waa feefo be siasaraŇč pou. 5Bot…Ē…Ē te walaŇč Ňčon …Ē kyaa gyooro gbeli keŇč d…Ē…Ē n…õ yakow…Ē nyiaa, ‚Äú∆ź k…õ…õ! Ňäon y…õna Nambu Ňčon w…õ…õle Ňčon.‚ÄĚ 10KeŇčte Wurubuar…õ feliŇč Ňčon kaa gyoow…Ē me man nideli, te Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon moo maŇč gyu bula dinaa dinaa daale keŇč ka kor…õ…õ n…õ nyee man. Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon moo …Ē daŇč keŇč maa don…Ē…Ē keŇč d…Ē…ĒloŇč man na, ke gyae ke y…õ…õ Ňčgba maelese kakpoŇč dokoloŇč na yaale n…õ (1,500). Don…Ē…Ē keŇč d…Ē…ĒloŇč na k…Ē …ĒfalaboŇč na k…Ē …ĒkooroŇč pou y…õ…õ ky…õ…õky…õ…õ. 17Wurubuar…õ kpilale Ňčon maa don…Ē…Ē keŇč dekyaŇč keŇč …ĒkeereŇč man na, na ke y…õ…õ naw…Ē…Ēt…Ē…Ēse s…ĒŇčonoŇčse akpeŇč ala na kufu looro.

Nawolo nyi Wurubuar…õ gyoorobiiri keŇč ne ŇčmaŇčee la bot…Ē…Ē, te Nambu Ňčon y…õna ke kanea. 24Mena keŇčmaŇčee kenaŇč man te t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē balaŇč pou baa naa ta, te t…õ…õle kei d…Ē…Ē gyooneŇč m…Ē baa mo ba wosekenyiŇč k…ĒŇč don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man. 25Baa toro don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč anaan…Ē…Ēse Ňčan see, keŇč b…Ē…Ē gyae baa t…Ē Ňča korakora ya. Nawolo nyi ditiŇčt…õ…õ be gyae ke kyaa don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man ya. 26Debiise pou balaŇč baa mo be gyoorobiiri na ba wosekenyiŇč k…ĒŇč don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man. 27M…Ēna kpene kamas…õ keŇč ke dana ayim…õ…õ n…õ be gyae ka gyoo don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man ya. WalaŇč kamas…õ Ňčon m…Ē …Ē ne y…õ…õ nombiakum…õ…õ, yaa des…õŇč nombia yaa Ňčon …Ē ne dii ak…õr…õ…õ n…õ, be man Ňčolo na Ňčolo be gyae waa gyoo don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man ya. M…Ēna balaŇč baŇč b…Ē…Ē Ňčmarase ba yela do Nambu Ňčon nyeedoŇč t…Ēne keŇč man n…õ, baŇč baageŇč ne gyae la baa gyoo don…Ē…Ē kenaŇč man.

english_text:
1 The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated. 2 Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
3 God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God 4 and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people. 5 Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever.
The Coming of Christ
6Then I was told:
These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon. 7Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
8My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
9But the angel said,
Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
10Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
11 Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
12 Then I was told:
I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done. 13 I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
14 God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city. 15But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
16 I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
17 The Spirit and the bride say, ‚ÄúCome!‚ÄĚ
Everyone who hears this should say, ‚ÄúCome!‚ÄĚ
If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
18 Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book:
If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book. 19If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
20The one who has spoken these things says, ‚ÄúI am coming soon!‚ÄĚ
So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
21I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
